TI Integrated solar oxygen enriched methane dry            reforming carbon dioxide combustion power generation            system, has high pressure turbine and low pressure            turbine connected with generator, and solar heliostat            connected to reflector.

AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 201942819H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide compressor                (17) formed with a carbon dioxide storage chamber                (18), where the carbon dioxide storage chamber is                connected with a first regenerator (12) and a                second regenerator (13). A left side heat return                pipe is connected with a right side heat exchange                pipe through a connection node. A multi-stream heat                exchanger (2) is connected with the first                regenerator. The right side heat return pipe and                the second regenerator are connected with a                low-pressure turbine (11). A low-pressure separator                (19) is connected with a mixer (1). A high pressure                turbine (10) and the low pressure turbine are                connected with a third regenerator (14). A solar                heliostat (20) is connected between a reflector                (21) and a solar reformer reactor (3).
   USE - Integrated solar oxygen enriched methane dry                reforming carbon dioxide combustion power                generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can reduce influence of power cycle                so as to directly heat supercritical carbon dioxide                combustion power circulation through enriched                oxygen, thus realizing zero discharge of                carbon-fuel power.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram                of an integrated solar oxygen enriched methane dry                reforming carbon dioxide combustion power                generation system. '(Drawing includes non-English                language text)'

   Three regenerators (12,13,14)
   Carbon dioxide compressor (17)
   Carbon dioxide storage chamber (18)
   Low-pressure separator (19)
   Solar heliostat (20)
   Reflector (21)

A support material (135) forms a                methanogenic flora bed and has a density less than                the density of water and movable in translation                along the longitudinal axis (101) of the enclosure,                to receive a methanogenic flora. Between support                material, outlet for methane and outlet for water,                a surface (140) for retaining the perforated                support material forming a stop for the                longitudinal movement of the support material at                the position of the surface, is formed.

TI Self-cooling carbon dioxide energy storage system            has air storage tank and liquid storage tank that are            suitable for storing gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid            carbon dioxide, and gasification components are            suitable to gasify carbon dioxide.

GA 2022C58936
AB    NOVELTY - The self-cooling carbon dioxide energy storage                system comprises an air storage tank and a liquid                storage tank (2), which are suitable for storing                gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid carbon dioxide.                An energy storage pipeline (3) and an energy                releasing pipeline (4) respectively communicate                with a gas storage tank. A liquefaction component                (5) and a gasification component (6) are                respectively set on the energy storing pipeline and                the energy-releasing pipeline, which is suitable                for liquefied carbon dioxide to store energy. The                gasification components are suitable to gasify                carbon dioxide for releasing energy. A cold heat                exchange medium and a heat exchanging medium are                respectively used to store the cold and heat                exchange materials.
   USE - Self-cooling carbon dioxide energy storage                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-cooling carbon dioxide energy storage                system completely liquefying the carbon dioxide in                the liquefying pipeline, the system does not need                to occupy large space, and the structure is                simple.

PN CN113620798-A; CN215403949-U
TI System useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize formic acid driven by renewable energy, comprises renewable energy power generation devices, carbon dioxide capture device, and hydrogen production device used to electrolyze water to produce hydrogen and oxygen.
AU ZHAO L
   FAN J
   LIU L
   GUO D
   LIU R
   WANG H
AE HUANENG POWER INT INC (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2021D6887A
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises renewable energy power generation devices, carbon dioxide capture device, which directly capture carbon dioxide in the air and hydrogen production device used to electrolyze water to produce hydrogen and oxygen. The formic acid synthesis device is respectively connected with the carbon dioxide capture device and the hydrogen production device, and synthesizes formic acid from the electric energy produced by the renewable energy power generation device, the carbon dioxide captured by the carbon dioxide capture device and the hydrogen produced by the hydrogen production device.
   USE - The system is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize formic acid driven by renewable energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high energy formic acid through the cooperation effect of each device, reduces the phenomenon that the renewable energy is wasted seriously in the power utilization low-valley phenomenon, solves the problem of power waste of renewable energy, reduces the content of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide in the air, realizes the resource utilization of the carbon dioxide gas, obtains the high energy compound formic acid, and saves the transportation cost of the raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing formic acid by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide driven by renewable energy, comprising synthesizing by carbon dioxide and hydrogen under the action of catalyst and an alkaline solution adopting the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic diagram of the system for synthesizing formic acid by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide driven by renewable energy (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a supported catalyst, including a carrier and an active center, the loading amount of active centers in the supported catalyst is less than 0.5 wt.%, the carrier is silica, carbon material, molecular sieve, hydrotalcite and/or mesoporous alumina, the active center is a noble metal single atom and the noble metal nitrogen atom is gold, palladium, ruthenium, and/or rhodium. The porous liquid includes zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8), and further includes ethylene glycol, 2-methylimidazole and/or polydimethylsiloxane, and the mass fraction of ZIF-8 in the porous liquid is 10-20%.

PT P
PN KR2021060202-A
TI Core-shell structured nanoparticle composite used in composition for detecting carbon dioxide, comprises porous core supported with pH indicator, and shell comprising amine group surrounding core and reacting by sensing carbon dioxide.
AU SUB H K
   PARK J J
   LEE C M
   YOUNG L J
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 202159139B
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-sensitive core-shell structured nanoparticle composite comprises a porous core supported with a pH indicator, and a shell comprising an amine group that surrounds the core and reacts by sensing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-sensitive core-shell structured nanoparticle composite used in composition for detecting carbon dioxide (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to home or industrial carbon dioxide detection sensor, textiles, clothes, electronic products, and automobiles.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-sensitive core-shell nanoparticle composite has excellent carbon dioxide detection sensitivity, supports the pH indicator, which is vulnerable to external environments such as heat, temperature, and humidity, and stably maintained for a long period of time even under poor conditions, and reduces the weight and size of instruments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of nanoparticle composite, which involves (i) adding an aromatic compound to the acid anhydride solution and performing oil-free polymerization to form a core of an aromatic-acid anhydride copolymer, (ii) preparing nanoparticles having a core-shell structure by adding an amine compound having at least one primary amine group on the core to form a shell, and (iii) permeating and supporting the pH indicator into the core of the prepared nanoparticles;
   (2) composition for detecting carbon dioxide, which comprises the nanoparticle complex as an active ingredient;
   (3) detecting carbon dioxide by using the composition, which involves monitoring carbon dioxide concentration or a change; and
   (4) kit for detecting carbon dioxide, which comprises the nanoparticle complex immobilized on a solid support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH indicator is methyl red, bromocresol purple or 4-nitrophenol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous core comprises an aromatic-acid anhydride copolymer. The shell comprises an amine compound containing at least one primary amine group. The shell is formed to a thickness adjusted to irreversibly maintain the color change reaction of the pH indicator. The aromatic-acid anhydride copolymer is poly(styrene-co-maleic anhydride). The amine compound containing at least one primary amine group is polyethyleneimine. The amine compound containing at least one primary amine group is a polymer having molecular weight of 600-4000. Preferred Composition: The aromatic-acid anhydride copolymer comprises an aromatic compound and an acid anhydride in a weight ratio of 70:30-90:10. The shell is formed using an amine compound containing one or more primary amine groups in an amount of 1-8 times based on the weight of the acid anhydride contained in the core. Preferred Properties: The core particles have an average diameter of 150-300 nm. The shell has an average thickness of 20-60 nm. The composition shows a color change within 30 seconds in contact with 0.5 ppm or more gas containing carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The step (i) is carried out by additionally adding potassium persulfide, which is a water-soluble polymerization initiator. In the step (ii), the amine compound containing at least one primary amine group is added in an aqueous solution state. The preparation method involves preparing a solution containing a pH indicator using dichloromethane as a solvent. Preferred Conditions: The step (i) is carried out by stirring at 250-350 rpm under a nitrogen atmosphere of 60-80 degrees C, after adding the polymerization initiator, for 2-10 hours, and cooling to 20-40 degrees C. The step (ii) is performed by stirring at 250-350 rpm for 6-48 hours.
DC A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); ; E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC A04-C04; A04-F05; A05-J07; A10-B04; A12-L04B; A12-W14; E10-A01; E10-A16B; E10-E02F1; E11-Q03J; E12-A15; E31-N05C; E31-U01; J04-C02A; J04-C04A; L03-X; S03-E09E; S03-E14P

PN US2020346165-A1; WO2020225689-A1; CA3138992-A1; AU2020269606-A1; KR2022005074-A; EP3962632-A1; US11285437-B2
TI System for calcination with carbon capture, has            carbon dioxide separation unit which is configured to            separate cooled gas stream including carbon dioxide            into carbon dioxide stream and carbon dioxide rich            stream.
AU LU X
   FORREST B A
   FETVEDT J E
   RAFATI N
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2020A7023V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reactor which is configured                to heat a carbonate-containing material in the                presence of an oxidant (6) to form decomposition                stream containing solids carbon dioxide gas. A                separator is configured to separate the                decomposition stream into a gas stream including                the carbon dioxide gas and a solids stream. A heat                exchange unit is configured to receive one or both                gas stream including the carbon dioxide gas and                solid stream and withdraw heat to provide cooled                gas stream including the carbon dioxide and cooled                solids stream. A carbon dioxide separation unit is                configured to separate the cooled gas stream                including the carbon dioxide into a carbon dioxide                stream and a carbon dioxide rich stream.
   USE - System for calcination with carbon                capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel efficiency is improved. The input gas                and solid streams can be preheated by the flue gas                and hot solids from the reactor in the heat                exchanger network before being injected into the                reactor to improve the heat utilization. The                overall cycle efficiency is improved significantly.                The synergy between three different processes can                be fully utilized to improve the economics of the                carbon capture system and reduce net capture costs                significantly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for calcination with carbon capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                components of systems useful in the capture of                carbon dioxide with simultaneous production of                industrially useful products.
   Raw material (1)
   Sorbent (2)
   Vent gas (3)
   Oxidant (6)
   Fuel (7)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Manufacture of carbon dioxide storage cement composition involves mixing Portland cement and dried byproduct obtained by drying carbon dioxide capture byproduct, carbon nanotubes and polyethylene glycol in microwave dryer.
AU KIM B H
   PARK S J
   KAGN H J
   KIM B
   PARK S
   KANG H
AE DAEWOO ENG & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   NOVIL (NOVI-Non-standard)
GA 2019000369
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbon dioxide storage cement composition involves supplying exhaust gas discharged from a cement manufacturing facility to a reaction tower containing an absorbing solution of calcium hydroxide and alkali admixture to produce a carbon dioxide capture byproduct, mixing the carbon dioxide capture byproduct with 1-5 wt.% powdered carbon nanotubes and 95-99 wt.% liquid polyethylene glycol, drying the mixed material using microwave in microwave dryer, and mixing 5-15 wt.% dried carbon dioxide capture byproduct with 85-95 wt.% Portland cement.
   USE - Manufacture of carbon dioxide storage cement composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared cement composition is capable of permanent storage of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The reaction tower further contains cocatalyst. Preferred Properties: The carbon nanotubes have specific surface area of 150-250 m2/g, purity of 95% or more, average diameter of 20 nm, length of 1-25 nm, metal oxide content of 5% or less, and bulk density of 0.03-0.05 g/cm3. The dried carbon dioxide capture byproduct has fineness of 6000 m2/g or more.

PT P
PN KR2001213-B1
TI Fuel cell system comprises hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit to generate hydrogen using electric energy as carbon dioxide as, methane-steam reformer to react methane and steam to generate hydrogen, and fuel cell.
AU GUNTAE K
   KIM C
   KIM J W
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 201983632T
AB    NOVELTY - Fuel cell system comprises a hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit (1100) for generating hydrogen using electric energy as carbon dioxide as a raw material; a methane-steam reformer (1300) which reacts methane with steam to generate hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; and a fuel cell (1400) supplied with hydrogen, carbon monoxide and unreacted methane from the methane-steam reformer as a fuel and discharging carbon dioxide as a product. The carbon dioxide generated from the methane-steam reformer and carbon dioxide discharged from the fuel cell are removed by being supplied as a raw material to the hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit. The hydrogen gas and the chlorine gas are generated and discharged together. The electrolyte solution in the entire reaction space is changed to a sodium hydrogen carbonate solution as the pH is increased and basified as the chlorine generation reaction proceeds.
   USE - Used as fuel cell system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fuel cell system comprises a hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit (1100) for generating hydrogen using electric energy as carbon dioxide as a raw material; a methane-steam reformer (1300) which reacts methane with steam to generate hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; and a fuel cell (1400) supplied with hydrogen, carbon monoxide and unreacted methane from the methane-steam reformer as a fuel and discharging carbon dioxide as a product. The carbon dioxide generated from the methane-steam reformer and carbon dioxide discharged from the fuel cell are removed by being supplied as a raw material to the hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit. The hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit comprises a reaction space (1111), an aqueous electrolyte solution contained in the reaction space and containing chlorine anion, a reduction electrode (1118) and an oxidation electrode arranged in the reaction space, and a hydrogen generator for generating hydrogen gas using electricity supplied from the outside and the methane-steam reformer and carbon dioxide gas supplied from the fuel cell. The electrons are separated from the chlorine anion at the anode to generate chlorine generation reaction in which chlorine gas is generated to maintain the amount of carbon dioxide dissolved in the electrolyte solution above a set value by adjusting the pH of the electrolyte solution above a set value. The hydrogen gas and the chlorine gas are generated and discharged together. The electrolyte solution in the entire reaction space is changed to a sodium hydrogen carbonate solution as the pH is increased and basified as the chlorine generation reaction proceeds.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the fuel cell system.
   Hydrogen generation and carbon dioxide removal unit (1100)
   Reaction space (1111)
   Reduction electrode (1118)
   Methane-steam reformer (1300)
   Fuel cell (1400)

PN CN114405227-A
TI Method for reducing carbon dioxide at the tail of            a cement kiln by an adsorption method, involves filling            the fixed bed reactor, loading formed carbon dioxide            adsorbent into fixed bed reactor, and carbon dioxide            adsorbent is modified high temperature resistant            calcium oxide carbon dioxide adsorbent.
AU ZHANG J
   SHU F
   ZHANG Y
   SHENG S
AE ZHEJIANG HONGSHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022650234
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing carbon dioxide at the tail                of a cement kiln by an adsorption method, involves                (1)filling the fixed bed reactor, loading formed                carbon dioxide adsorbent into fixed bed reactor,                and carbon dioxide adsorbent is modified high                temperature resistant calcium oxide carbon dioxide                adsorbent, (2)the tail gas passes through a cyclone                separator to remove solid dust, (3) directly                feeding the high-temperature flue gas into a fixed                bed reactor without cooling, firstly activating at                the temperature of 600&#8451;,(4) the carbon                dioxide adsorbent in fixed bed reactor with                saturated adsorption is calcined for 1-4h at                temperature of 850-1000 &#8451;, and then                recycling can be completed.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide at the tail                of a cement kiln by an adsorption method
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has large adsorption capacity,                easy recovery and treatment, good circulation                effect, no corrosion to equipment, low energy                consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The forming auxiliary                  agent is oleic acid or oleylamine or                  dodecylamine.The hydrotalcite nano material is a                  magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite nano material or                  zinc-aluminum hydrotalcite nano material. The                  mixed monomer is mixture of dimethyl diallyl                  ammonium chloride, 2-propylene boric acid and                  ferric acrylate, and mass ratio of 3 monomers is                  15-30:2-8: 1-5.The temperature programming rate                  in calcining furnace is 5-15                  &#8451;/min.Preferred Method:The process involves adding                  1.6-2.8 pts.wt. forming auxiliary agent into                  water according to the mass part, uniformly                  stirring and mixing, adding 160 pts.wt. calcium                  carbonate powder into the 100-one-charge water,                  uniformly stirring, transferring the material                  into a high-pressure reaction kettle, performing                  hydrothermal reaction at temperature of                  120-one-charge 180 &#8451; for 5-10 hours,                  filtering, dispersing obtained powder into 300                  pts.wt.200-one-charge water solution of 1-5%                  trihydroxypropionic acid, controlling the                  temperature to be 35-45 &#8451;, soaking for                  30-60 minutes, filtering, dispersing the obtained                  powder into 300 parts of 200-one-charge water                  solution of 2-7% aluminum nitrate for 10-30                  minutes, adding 10-18 parts of hydrotalcite nano                  material, uniformly stirring, filtering and                  separating, drying at the temperature of 80-100                  &#8451;, feeding into calcining furnace,                  raising the temperature to 850-one-charge 900                  &#8451;, after the heat preservation and                  calcination are carried out for 6-9h, the heating                  is stopped, and the material is discharged after                  material is naturally cooled to the room                  temperature, so as to obtain high-temperature                  resistant calcium oxide,(2)adding 50-100pts.wt.                  high-temperature-resistant calcium oxide and 500                  parts of hydrogen-containing silicone oil in a                  reaction kettle, heating to 50-60 &#8451;,                  and mechanically stirring and reacting for                  20-60min, filtering to remove silicone oil,                  adding 0.5-1.5 pts.wt. chloroplatinic acid                  isopropanol solution 2.2-5.8 pts.wt.mixed monomer                  and 500 pts.wt.toluene into solid product,                  uniformly mixing, controlling temperature to be                  60-70 &#8451;, reacting for 3-10h, filtering,                  washing with ethanol, and drying solid product in                  vacuum drying oven to obtain modified                  high-temperature-resistant calcium oxide carbon                  dioxide adsorbent.

PN WO2020176575-A1; CN113454269-A; US2022119966-A1
TI Electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide or carbonate ion by providing electrochemical cell comprising anode and cathode comprising copper/copper oxide particles, introducing aqueous medium, and reducing carbon dioxide or carbonate ion.
AU CHEN G
   RAO Y
   LI X
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   UNIV UTAH STATE (UTAS-C)
   UNIV UTAH STATE (UTAS-C)
   UNIV UTAH STATE (UTAS-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2020854867
AB    NOVELTY - Method for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide or carbonate ion, involves providing an electrochemical cell comprising an anode, and a cathode comprising copper/copper oxide particles with copper/copper oxide interfaces, introducing an aqueous medium into the cell, where the aqueous medium comprises carbon dioxide or carbonate ion, and reducing the carbon dioxide or carbonate ion by contacting the copper/copper oxide particles with the aqueous medium while supplying electricity to the cell.
   USE - The method is used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide or carbonate ion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exploits both copper and copper oxide for carbon dioxide reduction, where copper/copper oxide interfaces minimize their free energy by reconstruction and enhance molecular hydrogen adsorption and activation on carbon dioxide-covered atomically rough interfaces in the carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction. The method increases the Faradaic efficiency of carbon dioxide conversion by the electrochemical cell compared to electrochemical cells including cathodes not having copper/copper oxide particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrochemical cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises preparing the copper/copper oxide particles by providing copper oxide crystals, and reacting the copper oxide crystals with a reducing agent at a temperature for a time and forming the copper/copper oxide particles. The copper oxide crystals are provided by forming a solution including a copper ion contributor dissolved in a solvent, heating the solution to a temperature of 55-65 degrees C, adding a pH adjuster to the solution, where the solution has a pH of 2-12, adding a reducing agent to the solution to form a reaction mixture, and reacting the reaction mixture at the temperature of 55-65 degrees C to precipitate the copper oxide crystals from the reaction mixture. Preferred Components: The copper/copper oxide particles are nanosized. The copper/copper oxide particles comprise 18-facet polyhedral copper/copper oxide particles. The copper/copper oxide particles comprise twelve (110) facets and six (100) facets. The reducing agent comprises hydrazine hydrate, where the temperature is 50-70 degrees C, and reaction time is 1-10 minutes. Preferred Properties: The facets of the copper/copper oxide particles have rough surfaces and comprise the copper/copper oxide interfaces. The copper oxide crystals have an average particle size of 0.8 mu m. The copper/copper oxide particles have an atomic ratio of copper:oxygen of more than 2:1, preferably more than 10:1.

PN CN109589935-A
TI Carbon dioxide solid adsorbent comprises carrier gamma-alumina and amine-based compound.
AU HU X
   WANG Y
   TIAN L
   GENG X
   SU J
   LI W
   WANG H
AE CHINA HUADIAN ENG CO LTD (HUAD-C)
   HUADIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION SYSTEM (HUAD-C)
GA 201935077K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide solid adsorbent comprises carrier and supported active ingredient, where carrier is gamma -alumina, and active ingredient is an amine-based compound, where loading amount of active ingredient is 20-80 wt.% based on the total weight of the carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: has high specific surface area, large pore volume and high hydrothermal stability and excellent adsorption and regeneration performance; is economical; and preparation method has good adsorption rate for high-temperature and high-speed carbon dioxide-containing flue gas, is simple and economical and consumes less energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing a carbon dioxide solid adsorbent, comprising (i) soaking carrier in a solution of an amine compound and subjecting to suction filtration under stirring to obtain a filter cake, and (ii) dispersing filter cake in a solvent, stirring sequentially and drying under reflux; and
   (2) adsorbing carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide solid adsorbent, comprising filling the carbon dioxide solid adsorbent in a fixed bed reactor, passing flue gas into a fixed bed reactor and passing through the carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials used in the preparation of the gamma -alumina comprises aluminum salts, reactants and structural auxiliaries at molar ratio of 1-2:4-10:0.01-0.2 and reactant comprises at least one ammonium bicarbonate or ammonium carbonate. The aluminum salt comprises at least one aluminum nitrate or aluminum sulfate and structural auxiliaries comprises at least one polyethylene glycol (PEG)-200, PEG-400, PEG-600 or glycerol. The amine compound is polyethyleneimine, 3-aminopropyltriethyloxyalkane, aminoethylaminopropyl-trimethoxysilane, N-(3-(Trimethylsilyl)propyl)diethylenetriamine,ethyl-amino-aminopropyl-trimethoxyalkane or diethylsilyl-aminopropyl-trimethoxysilane. The solution of the amine compound has a mass concentration of 10-50%. Preferred Method: The preparation of gamma -alumina comprises adding a structural auxiliary agent into the powdery reaction product, mixing and grinding with the powdered aluminum salt to obtain a paste-like substance and aging and calcining paste-like substance sequentially. The aging is carried out at 60-90 degrees C for 6-48 hours and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 4-8 hours. The preparation of gamma -alumina comprises washing and drying the paste-like substance at 100-120 degrees C for 2-12 hours after aging before calcining. The reflux stirring is carried out under reflux in a constant temperature water bath at 50-80 degrees C for 6-12 hours. The reaction temperature of the fixed bed reactor is 40-100 degrees C, where flow rate of the flue gas is 10-100 ml/min and volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the flue gas is 10-20%.

The outlet provides fluid communication with                a borehole (105) in earth (103). The enrichment                unit is configured such that the flow of the                gaseous carbon dioxide provides a direct contact                within the flow of the brine solution. Another                borehole extends into a brine reservoir (104). PN CN114011212-A
TI Energy-saving method for recycling high-concentration carbon dioxide, involves collecting and recovering carbon dioxide from top of desorption tower, and entering solution into absorption tower to be continuously used after heat exchange.
AU BAI F
   LI H
AE ARBON NEW MATERIAL RES & DEV NANJING CO (ARBO-Non-standard)
   ARBON GREEN PROCESS & NEW MATERIALS RES (ARBO-Non-standard)
GA 202228459C
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling high-concentration carbon dioxide comprises introducing high concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas into a multi-stage membrane separation system, and extracting carbon dioxide with a mass concentration of 99.9% from an air outlet. The obtained carbon dioxide mixture is entered from the residual gas outlet of the multi-level membrane separation systems into the methyl diethanolamine liquid, where the bottom and the top of the tower are contacted through the heat exchanger. The residual carbon dioxide is captured, where the purified gas is discharged from the top surface of the absorption tower. The rich liquid of carbon dioxide is flown after the capture of the carbon dioxide from the bottom surface of an absorption tower and entered the tower top surface after the heat exchange with the tower of the desorption tower. The carbon dioxide is collected and recovered from the tops of the top surfaces of the absorber tower.
   USE - Method for recycling high-concentration carbon dioxide by film/methyl diethanolamine solution coupling used for capturing carbon dioxide from mixed gas containing carbon dioxide with high concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the film/MDEA solution coupling and recycling CO2 from the high-concentration CO2 mixed gas, through reasonable coupling technique, thus greatly improving the collecting rate of CO2 on the basis of saving energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the method for recycling high-concentration carbon dioxide by film/MDEA solution coupling.
   High-concentration (G1)
   Residual gas outlet mixed with (G2)
   Permeating outlet (G3)
   Purifying gas (G4)
   Mdea solution barren solution (L1)
   Mdea solution rich liquid (L2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas comprises 20-80% carbon dioxide, 0-20% water, 5-60% nitrogen and 5-60% methane. The methyl diethanolamine (MDEA) solution barren solution is a solution formed by alcohol amine, piperazine and water.

PN JP2021133304-A
TI Immobilizing carbon dioxide by contacting gas containing carbon dioxide and water in presence of compound having e.g., zinc, to obtain carbonic acid solution, and contacting with alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal containing compound.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 2021A3983W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is immobilized by (1) making gas containing carbon dioxide and water in the presence of compound which consists of zinc, copper, and ethylene amine contact to obtain carbonic acid solution, and (2) making carbonic acid solution contact with alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal containing compound.
   USE - Immobilization of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The immobilization method of carbon dioxide can reduce carbon dioxide in waste gas with low energy, and is very useful industrially.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Zinc is selected from organic salts, such as formic-acid zinc(II) and zinc(II) acetate, and/or mineral salt, such as zinc(II) chloride, zinc(II) nitrate, and zinc(II) sulfate. Copper is selected from organic salts, such as copper(II) formate and copper(II) acetate, and/or mineral salt, such as copper(II) chloride, copper(II) nitrate, and copper sulfate (II). Compound which consists of zinc, copper, and ethylene amine is lipophilic. Compound which consists of zinc, copper, and ethylene amine is supported on inorganic carriers, such as silica and alumina, or activated carbon. Gas containing carbon dioxide is waste gas which burned coal. Gas containing carbon dioxide is waste gas at the time of cement manufacture. Water is selected from purified water, river water, lake water, and seawater. Alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal containing compound are selected from carbonate mineral, silicate mineral, aluminate mineral, and/or waste concrete material. Carbonate mineral is selected from calcite containing alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal, aragonite, vaterite, dolomite, malachite, azurite, magnesite, rhodochrosite, and/or siderite. Silicate mineral is selected from clay minerals containing alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal, such as forsterite, monticellite olivine, montmorillonite, and/or talc. Aluminate mineral is selected from aluminate minerals, such as a gibbsite containing alkali and/or alkaline-earth metal, Boehmite, mullite, kaolinite, illite, feldspar, zeolite, and/or aluminosilicate salt mineral.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Ethylene amine is selected from alkylated body of ethylene amines, such as ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylene hexamine, piperazine, amino ethyl piperazine, ethylene amines, ethylene-oxide adduct to ethylene amines, and/or propylene-oxide adduct to ethylene amines.

PN CN113899161-A
TI Method for extracting ethane from natural gas            involves pressurizing feed gas, using activated            methyldiethanolamine solution to absorb carbon dioxide,            dehydrating and removing mercury, recovering ethane,            and condensing.
AU SONG S
   SHI S
   YIN Y
   DING F
   WANG Y
   SUN J
   GAO J
AE SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN PETROLEUM EXPLORATION (SNPC-C)
GA 202213625A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting ethane from natural gas                involves (1) pressurizing feed gas to 4.4 MPa and                48degreesC by feed gas compressor, obtaining                pressurized feed gas, (2) using activated                methyldiethanolamine solution to absorb carbon                dioxide in the pressurized feed gas, removing                carbon dioxide to below 50 ppm, obtaining raw                material gas after decarburization, (3) feeding the                raw material gas after the decarburization obtained                in step (2), using three-tower isobaric molecular                sieve dehydration and mercury removal process for                dehydration and sulfur-leaching activated carbon to                remove mercury, using molecular sieve dehydration                and mercury removal device in the molecular sieve                dehydration and mercury removal process, (4)                pre-cooling the purified gas in step (3) to                -64degreesC to -67degreesC through main cold box                (E4), feeding into low temperature section for                gas-liquid separation, separating the liquid phase                by the cryogenic separator, and separating the gas                phase.
   USE - Method for extracting ethane from natural                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product with high carbon                dioxide removing efficiency, anti-carbon dioxide                anti-freezing block demethanizer, carbon recovery                rate reaches more than 95% (preferably more than                99%) recovery rate, and high product yield. The                method consumes less energy, saves energy up to                12.5%, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting ethane from natural gas                involves (1) pressurizing feed gas to 4.4 MPa and                48degreesC by feed gas compressor, obtaining                pressurized feed gas, (2) using activated                methyldiethanolamine solution to absorb carbon                dioxide in the pressurized feed gas, removing                carbon dioxide to below 50 ppm, obtaining raw                material gas after decarburization, (3) feeding the                raw material gas after the decarburization obtained                in step (2), using three-tower isobaric molecular                sieve dehydration and mercury removal process for                dehydration and sulfur-leaching activated carbon to                remove mercury, using molecular sieve dehydration                and mercury removal device in the molecular sieve                dehydration and mercury removal process to adopt                three drying towers (T3) and a small tower, (4)                pre-cooling the purified gas in step (3) to                -64degreesC to -67degreesC through main cold box                (E4), feeding into low temperature section for                gas-liquid separation, separating the liquid phase                by the cryogenic separator, feeding to upper                portion of the demethanizer (T4), separating the                gas phase by the cryogenic separator which is                divided into two strands, cooling the gas volume of                14-16 vol.% to -104degreesC to -106degreesC by the                main cold box (E4) and throttling into upper                portion of the demethanizer (T4), expanding the                strand to 2.0 MPag by the expansion/compressor (C1)                and feeding into upper portion of the demethanizer                (T4), reheating the gas phase at the top of the                demethanizer (T4) to 16-18degreesC through the main                cold box (E4) and enters the booster end of the                expansion/compressor (C1) to be pressurized to 2.0                MPag, feeding to external dry gas booster unit                (J1), using 9-12% gas volume of the dry gas booster                unit (J1) as circulating dry gas, cooling the                refluxed dry gas to -104degreesC to -106degreesC                through main cold box (E4) and feeding to the top                of the demethanizer (T4), so that the refluxed dry                gas can conduct mass and heat transfer with the                rising gas phase in the demethanizer (T4),                pressurizing the condensate at the bottom of the                demethanizer (T4) by the bottom pump (P4) and                feeding out, (5) feeding the condensate from bottom                of demethanizer (T4) to middle part of deethanizer                (T5) for fractionation, feeding top gas phase                ethane of the deethanizer (T5) into the ethane cold                box for condensation and feeding into deethanizer                top reflux tank (D51), pressurizing by deethanizer                top reflux pump (P51), feeding a portion to ethane                storage tank (S1) as ethane product, and refluxing                another portion to the top of the deethanizer (T5).                The two drying towers (T3) enter and exit the raw                gas up and down during dehydration, and the third                drying tower (T3) enters and exits under the                regeneration gas during regeneration, and the small                tower is used for the drying of the regeneration                gas. The control pressure difference between raw                gas and regeneration gas is controlled by flow                control of 70 kPa or more. The amount of                regeneration gas is about 10% of the amount of raw                gas. The dry gas from the tower is filtered by the                third dust filter (F33). The obtained purified gas                with water content of 1 ppm or less and mercury                content of less than 0.01 mug/m3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The activated                  methyldiethanolamine solution is prepared by                  using 38.5 wt.% activated methyldiethanolamine                  solution, 56.64 wt.% water and 4.86 wt.%                  piperazine.

PN WO2021055074-A1; US2021086131-A1; US11083994-B2
TI Facility used for processing hydrocarbon gas stream, contains acid gas removal facility for separating hydrocarbon gas stream into sweetened gas stream and acid gas stream comprising primarily hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
AU NORTHROP P S
   AIKINS T R
AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2021297520
AB    NOVELTY - A gas processing facility (400) comprises an acid gas removal facility (420) for separating hydrocarbon gas stream into a sweetened gas stream (422) and an acid gas stream (423) comprising primarily hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, a Claus sulfur recovery unit (SRU) (430) that receives the acid gas stream and air stream which is enriched with oxygen such that the air stream comprises 22-100% oxygen, and a tail gas treating unit (440) including a reducing gas generator (RGG) that combusts fuel gas and the tail gas stream (434) with an air flow (426), a catalytic bed configured to receive and hydrogenate the RGG outlet stream, thereby converting sulfur dioxide, carbonyl sulfide, mercaptans, and other sulfur species to a gaseous catalytic output stream comprising hydrogen sulfide, a dehydration unit and a compressor station for receiving the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream and providing pressure to the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream for injection into subsurface reservoir.
   USE - Facility used for processing hydrocarbon gas stream of petroleum refinery for removing acid gas, such as carbon dioxide and sulfurous components such as hydrogen disulfides, for forming finished products and chemical intermediates (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facility includes the SRU in which oxygen enrichment reduces the nitrogen content of the tail gas, thereby reducing the volume of the stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas processing facility comprises an acid gas removal facility for separating the hydrocarbon gas stream into a sweetened gas stream and an acid gas stream comprising primarily hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, a Claus sulfur recovery unit (SRU) that receives the acid gas stream and an air stream which is enriched with oxygen such that the air stream comprises 22-100% oxygen, and a tail gas treating unit including a reducing gas generator (RGG) that combusts fuel gas and the tail gas stream with an air flow, a catalytic bed configured to receive and hydrogenate the RGG outlet stream, thereby converting sulfur dioxide, carbonyl sulfide, mercaptans, and other sulfur species to a gaseous catalytic output stream comprising hydrogen sulfide, a dehydration unit that removes water from the gaseous catalytic output stream to produce a partially-dehydrated acid gas stream and a compressor station for receiving the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream, and providing pressure to the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream for injection into a subsurface reservoir. The SRU combusts the acid gas stream and the atmospheric air to thereby separate the acid gas stream into a liquid stream of elemental sulfur, and a tail gas stream comprising acid gas impurities. The RGG stoichiometrically combusts the fuel gas and tail gas stream with the air flow to produce RGG outlet stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon monoxide. The gas processing facility for processing a hydrocarbon gas stream including sulfurous components and carbon dioxide. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) processing of hydrocarbon gas stream in gas processing facility, which involves separating the hydrocarbon gas stream into sweetened gas stream, and acid gas stream comprising primarily of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, receiving the acid gas stream at sulfur recovery unit, receiving air stream at the SRU, combusting the acid gas stream and the air stream in the SRU to thereby separate the acid gas stream into liquid stream of elemental sulfur, and tail gas stream comprising acid gas impurities, with an air flow, sub-stoichiometrically combusting the tail gas stream and fuel gas to produce the outlet stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon monoxide, hydrogenating the outlet stream in a catalytic bed, thereby converting sulfur dioxide, carbonyl sulfide, mercaptans, and other sulfur species to a gaseous catalytic output stream comprising hydrogen sulfide, removing water from the gaseous catalytic output stream to produce a partially-dehydrated acid gas stream and pressurizing the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream to produce compressed dehydrated acid gas stream and injecting the compressed, partially- dehydrated acid gas stream into a subsurface reservoir; and
   (2) petroleum refinery, in which petroleum is processed into finished products and chemical intermediates, in which hydrocarbon gas and liquid streams are processed by using the facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas processing facility.
   Facility (400)
   Acid gas removal facility (420)
   Sweetened gas stream (422)
   Acid gas stream (423)
   Air flow (426)
   Carbon dioxide rich stream (427)
   Enriched acid gas stream (429)
   Claus sulfur recovery facility (430)
   Tail gas stream (434)
   Tail gas treating unit (440)
   Hydrogen disulfide (442)
   Line (444)
   Incinerator (450)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The catalytic bed reduces oxidized sulfur species in the RGG outlet stream to hydrogen sulfide. The gas processing facility further consists of an absorber vessel and a regenerator vessel, in which the dehydration unit using an amine that absorbs both carbon dioxide and sulfurous components such that a majority of the carbon dioxide entering the tail gas treating unit is absorbed in the absorber vessel and released from the absorber vessel to the regenerator vessel in a rich solvent stream, a condenser vessel for separating residual amine and condensed water from carbon dioxide and sulfurous components in an overhead gas stream of the regenerator and a line (444) for directing the residual amine and condensed water back to the regenerator vessel, several injection wells for transmitting the pressurized partially-dehydrated acid gas stream from the compressor station to the subsurface reservoir, in which the overhead acid gas stream from the regenerator vessel is taken through the condenser vessel for removal of residual amine and some water vapor before the overhead gas stream is delivered to the compressor station, an acid gas enrichment facility for receiving the acid gas stream from the acid gas removal facility, and separating the acid gas stream into an overhead carbon dioxide stream (427), and hydrogen disulfide-rich acid gas stream. The acid gas stream received by the Claus sulfur recovery unit is the hydrogen sulfide-rich acid gas stream. The overhead carbon dioxide stream is directed from the acid gas enrichment facility to the compressor station and placed under pressure for injection into the subsurface reservoir along with the partially-dehydrated acid gas stream. The overhead gas stream of the regenerator is used for enhanced oil recovery operations in the subsurface reservoir. The compressor station comprises one of a pump or a compressor. The dehydration unit comprises a quench tower, and further comprises a cooler that cools at least portion of the water removed from the gaseous catalytic output stream and returns at least portion of the water to the quench tower.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine comprises diethanolamine (DEA), diisopropanolamine, and/or monoethanol. The amine comprises methyl diethanol amine (MDEA) which is activated to facilitate carbon dioxide absorption. The MDEA is activated with piperazine. The hydrocarbon gas stream comprises raw natural gas from a hydrocarbon production operation. The air stream comprises 100% oxygen. The air flow is enriched with oxygen such that the air stream comprises 22-100% oxygen. Preferred Processes: The removal of acid gas from the gaseous catalytic output stream is accomplished using an absorber vessel and a regenerator vessel. The method further involves absorbing both carbon dioxide and sulfurous components such that majority of the carbon dioxide entering the tail gas treating unit in the absorber vessel, using the amine, and releasing from the absorber vessel to regenerator vessel along with sulfurous components in rich solvent stream, providing several acid gas injection wells for transmitting the pressurized partially-dehydrated acid gas stream from the regenerator vessel in the tail gas treating unit to the subsurface reservoir for enhanced oil recovery operations, separating residual amine and condensed water from carbon dioxide and sulfurous components in the overhead gas stream of the regenerator vessel, and returning residual amine and condensed water to the regenerator vessel, and incinerating an overhead by-products stream from the absorber vessel and venting the incinerated by-products stream into the atmosphere.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AB    NOVELTY - Gas processing equipment (P1) comprises oxygen                remover (2) that removes oxygen contained in                exhaust gas; and gas processing device (3) that                processes pretreated exhaust gas, from which oxygen                has been removed by oxygen remover, with treatment                agent.
   USE - Gas processing equipment used in carbon                dioxide capture system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas processing equipment is capable of                suppressing mixture of oxygen to a treatment agent                (absorbent solvent, etc.) that performs a treatment                such as separation of carbon dioxide from exhaust                gas, to thereby maintain suitable performance of                the treatment agent. When iron particles are used                as the deoxidizer, they can effectively and                efficiently remove oxygen because of their                significantly high affinity to oxygen and reduce                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a gas                processing method comprising oxygen removal step                that removes oxygen contained in exhaust gas, and a                gas processing step that processes pretreated                exhaust gas, from which the oxygen has been removed                by the oxygen removal step, with a treatment                agent;
   (2) a carbon dioxide capture system                comprising: an oxygen remover that removes oxygen                contained in exhaust gas; an absorber that                separates carbon dioxide from pretreated exhaust                gas, from which the oxygen has been removed by the                oxygen remover, by bringing a carbon dioxide                absorbent solvent and the pretreated exhaust gas                into contact with each other so that the carbon                dioxide contained in the pretreated exhaust gas is                absorbed in the carbon dioxide absorbent solvent,                and a regenerator that diffuses the carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide absorbent solvent                discharged from the absorber, and discharges the                carbon dioxide absorbent solvent, which has                diffused the carbon dioxide, and a regenerator                discharged gas containing the carbon dioxide;                and
   (3) a carbon dioxide capture method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of carbon                dioxide capture system.

The oxygen remover removes the oxygen by                  passing the exhaust gas through a non-liquid                  deoxidizer. The deoxidizer contains at least one                  of iron, copper, lead, silver, zeolite, and                  activated carbon. The deoxidizer has a granular                  shape, a honeycomb shape, a porous shape, or a                  combination of at least two of these                  shapes.

PT P
PN EP3369322-A1; NL2018448-B1; EP3369322-B1
TI Method for gassing poultry, involves converting liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) into gaseous carbon dioxide, bringing gaseous carbon dioxide to desired temperature, and subsequently introducing gaseous carbon dioxide into shed space.
AU VAN ECK P A C
AE VAN ECK BEHEER BV P (VECK-Non-standard)
   VAN ECK BEHEER BV P (VECK-Non-standard)
GA 2018693974
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide is converted into the gaseous carbon dioxide. The gaseous carbon dioxide is brought to a desired temperature. The desired temperature is between 10 degrees C and 35 degrees C. The gaseous carbon dioxide is subsequently introduced into the shed space. The desired temperature is adapted to an average animal shed temperature. The gaseous carbon dioxide is introduced into the shed space at pressure in the range from 2 bar to 4 bar.
   USE - Method for gassing poultry.
   ADVANTAGE - The gaseous carbon dioxide is introduced into the shed space at relatively low pressure to prevent the noise which accompanies introducing gaseous carbon dioxide into the shed space under pressure, and to prevent birds from trying to escape or greatly reduce the number of birds exhibiting escape behaviour. The gaseous carbon dioxide is distributed over the volume of the shed space in the shed space evenly to gas the birds relatively quickly and with relatively limited suffering. The even distribution of the gaseous carbon dioxide is ensured, so that the birds die uniformly on each level or at each height above the shed floor. The gaseous carbon dioxide is removed from the shed space to prevent any risks to workers when entering the shed space. The drop in temperature in the shed space is ensured during and after the introduction of gaseous CO2 is not more than 10 degrees C and thus helps to prevent discomfort for the animals to be killed. The space can be ventilated freely more quickly after the killing, as the introduced CO2 is in gas form.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for arrangement for gassing poultry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for gassing poultry.
   Supply (33)
   Energy source (51)
   Buffer vessel (52)
   Heat-exchanging sections (53,54)
   Pump unit (55)

PT P
PN CN114504934-A
TI Reducing carbon dioxide emissions comprises            removing impurities from residue gas, pre-processing            residue, performing membrane separation, and reducing            emissions.
AU DONG M
   DU W
   WU C
   ZHANG H
AE ZHEJIANG HONGSHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022721201
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide emissions comprises                reducing pressure of the residue gas to 0.5-1.8MPa                through a pressure reducing valve, and then                increasing water vapor content to 10-15% through a                humidifier, and then passing humidified residue gas                through a cyclone separator to remove solid dust                and water droplets. The impurity-removed residue                gas is pressurized to 3-6MPa through a booster                pump, then passed through a precision filtration                system to remove solid dust, and then passed                through a heat exchanger to adjust the exhaust gas                dimension to 40-60degrees Celsius. The pressure of                the pretreated residue gas is controlled to                150-260kPa, and passed into the membrane separation                system. The permeate gas of the membrane separation                system is carbon dioxide, where the trapped gas is                the directly discharged residue gas. The membrane                separation system includes a high-efficiency carbon                dioxide selective permeation membrane.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide emissions                from cement kiln residue based on a membrane                separation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can achieve a very good goal of                reducing emissions, which is conducive to the                country's goal of carbon neutrality, where the                high-efficiency carbon dioxide selective permeation                membrane has high solubility and high diffusivity                for carbon dioxide compared with other gases, so                that the membrane has both high carbon dioxide                selectivity and permeability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The high-efficiency                  carbon dioxide selective permeation membrane is                  sprayed on the membrane substrate by using                  polyvinylamine and modified hydrotalcite                  nanomaterials. The modified hydrotalcite                  nanomaterial is prepared by using aminosilane to                  treat the surface of hydrotalcite, and then                  performing cross-linking reaction with                  (2-mercaptoethyl)-guanidine sulfate and                  formaldehyde. The high-efficiency carbon dioxide                  selective permeation membrane is prepared by                  drying base film at 50-75degrees Celsius for                  30-60 minutes, spraying a layer of composite                  surface modifier prepared by polyvinylamine and                  modified hydrotalcite nanomaterials, drying at                  50-75degrees Celsius for 30-60 minutes, and then                  demolding to obtain carbon dioxide selective                  permeable membrane. The hydrotalcite                  nanomaterials are magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite                  nanomaterials or zinc-aluminum hydrotalcite                  nanomaterials. The membrane separator is a flat                  roll membrane. The membrane material of the base                  membrane is polyethersulfone, polyvinylidene                  fluoride or mixed cellulose ester. The modified                  hydrotalcite nanomaterial is prepared by passing                  nitrogen in a closed autoclave, adding 50-70 pts.                  wt. hydrotalcite nanomaterials, 100-230 pts. wt.                  ethanol, and 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. aminosilane under                  heating and stirring to 50-60degrees Celsius, and                  mechanically stirring for 20-60 minutes, adding                  0.5-2.8 pts. wt. (2-mercaptoethyl)-guanidine                  sulfate and 3-10 pts. wt. 25-40% formaldehyde                  solution under heating and stirring to                  55-65degrees Celsius for 15-30 hours, and then                  filtering, washing with ethanol, and drying the                  solid product in a vacuum drying oven to obtain                  modified hydrotalcite nanomaterials.Preferred Components: The aminosilane is                  selected from gamma-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane,                  gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  N-(beta-aminoethyl)-gamma-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane,                  N-                  (beta-aminoethyl)-gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  N-(beta-aminoethyl)-gamma-aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane,                  N-(beta-aminoethyl                  )-gamma-aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane,                  gamma-Aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane,                  gamma-aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, and                  gamma-aminopropylethoxydimethylsilane.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a water sample from a carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well. Initial pH value of the water sample and initial ion concentration of each ion in the water sample are detected. First SAI value is calculated according to a Ryznar stability index theory. Different bicarbonate-concentration values are determined when the first SAI value is greater than or equal to 6. Scaling trend of a carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well is obtained through an iterative algorithm. Relationship between the fouling critical concentration values of the bicarbonate-concentration value and carbonate Ion-concentration value are established. The carbonate Ion-concentration value and the bicarbonate-concentration value of wellbore water samples are monitored. Fouling tendency of the carbon dioxide gas flooding oil well is determined corresponding to the bicarbonate-concentration value.
   USE - Method for predicting scaling trend of carbon dioxide gas drive wellbore based on stability index.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables correctly predicting carbon dioxide gas-driven oil-gas well fouling tendency so as to prepare reasonable cleaning anti-scaling solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for predicting scaling trend of carbon dioxide gas drive wellbore based on stability index (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Calcium oxide compound for absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas, comprises calcium oxide and humate in specific weight parts.
AU SUN Z
   CHEN L
   XU J
   ZHANG L
   XIE H
AE UNIV SHANGHAI POLYTECHNIC (USSP-C)
GA 2019A3061P
AB    NOVELTY - A calcium oxide compound comprises 95-99 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 1-5 pts. wt. humate.
   USE - Calcium oxide compound for absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium oxide compound has prolonged service life and high absorption efficiency, in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a calcium oxide compound, which involves fully stirring the calcium-based solid waste, humate, and water, drying and calcinating in a fluidized bed calciner at high temperature to obtain the finished product, where the calcium-based solid waste is an industrial solid waste that generates calcium oxide after heating
   (2) a method for absorbing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas by a calcium oxide compound, which involves adding the calcium oxide compound to a fluidized bed carbonation furnace, and passing in the exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide to make the calcium oxide compound in the gas absorbs carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas to form calcium carbonate complex, returning the calcium carbonate composite to a fluidized bed calciner, and heating and calcining to obtain a calcium oxide composite and pure carbon dioxide, and discharging the pure carbon dioxide from the fluidized bed calciner, returning the calcium oxide compound to the fluidized bed carbonation furnace and reusing to absorb carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The humate is sodium humate and/or potassium humate. The calcium-based solid waste is a calcium carbide slag, steel slag, and fly ash. The reaction between the calcium oxide and water to form calcium hydroxide and the reaction between the calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate and water.

AU HE D
AE HE D (HEDD-Individual)
GA 201935081R
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing carbon dioxide by using salt comprises (i) preparing saturated salt water, (ii) introducing ammonia gas into the saturated salt water, introducing the mixed gas of carbon dioxide, and fully reacting, (iii) filtering the reaction product, roasting the filtered precipitate, cooling the solid product, packing, recycling the gas produced during roasting, (iv) cooling the filtrate and adding salt powder, (v) filtering the product in the step (iv), collecting the solid product, returning the filtrate to the step (ii), (vi) heating the solid product collected in step (v), collecting the decomposition product, and returning the ammonia decomposition product to step (ii).
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing carbon dioxide by using salt.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for absorbing carbon dioxide by using salt (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the environment temperature of the saturated salt solution prepared is 30-35 degrees C. In the step (ii), the mixed gas containing carbon dioxide is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide produced by industrial combustion. In the step (ii), the reaction time is 25-35 minutes. In the step (iii), the roasting temperature is 85-150 degrees C. In the step (iv), the cooling temperature is 5-10 degrees C. In the step (vi), the heating of the solid product is carried out by adding the catalyst to the stepwise heating. In the step (vi), the stepwise heating comprises first stage heating and second stage heating, and the heating temperature of second stage heating is higher than the heating temperature of first stage heating. In the step (vi), the heating temperature of the first stage heating is 110-120 degrees C. In the step (vi), the heating temperature of the second stage heating is 290-300 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve material efficiently adsorbs carbon dioxide in environment to achieve the purpose of purifying air.

The weight ratio of molecular sieve to organic amine solution is 1:(1-100). Preferred Conditions: The stirring temperature of mixture is room temperature and stirring time is 2-4 hours. The ultrasonically immersing time is 1 hour. The drying temperature is 100-130 degrees C, and the drying time is 8-12 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - A membrane-based process involves providing a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, compressing the gas mixture, transferring the compressed gas mixture to a heat exchanger, transferring the cooled gas mixture to membrane module with selective permeability to carbon dioxide-lean first retention flow and carbon dioxide rich forming first permeate flow, transferring first retained flow to the heat exchanger under reduced pressure by an expander to heat by heat exchange, compressing first permeate flow to the heat exchanger in a pressurized state to heat exchange, transferring the cooled first permeate stream to a distillation column to obtain a high purity carbon dioxide by gas/liquid separation, transferring low purity carbon dioxide into liquid flow, and separating top flow into a containing gaseous mixture, utilizing the high purity carbon dioxide to recover the liquid stream, where the process has a separate closed external cooling cycle with a single or mixed refrigerant.
   USE - Membrane-based process for capturing and separating carbon dioxide from gas mixtures.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane-based process has increased separation efficiency and heat exchanger capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A membrane-based process involves providing a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, compressing the gas mixture to 1.2-5 bara, transferring the compressed gas mixture to a heat exchanger for cooling, transferring the cooled gas mixture to membrane module with selective permeability to carbon dioxide-lean first retention flow and carbon dioxide rich forming first permeate flow, transferring first retained flow to the heat exchanger under reduced pressure by an expander to heat by heat exchange, compressing first permeate flow to the heat exchanger in a pressurized state to heat exchange, transferring the cooled first permeate stream to a distillation column to obtain a high purity carbon dioxide by gas/liquid separation, transferring low purity carbon dioxide into liquid flow, and separating top flow into a containing gaseous mixture, utilizing the high purity carbon dioxide to recover the liquid stream, where the process has a separate closed external cooling cycle with a single or mixed refrigerant, that the closed external cooling cycle provides the necessary cooling for the process by heat exchange in the heat exchanger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The single refrigerant is propane. Preferred Composition: The mixed refrigerant is 10-25 mol.% ethane, 25-50 mol.% propane and 30-60 mol.% butane.

TI Method for extracting carbon dioxide gas from e.g. aqueous amine, for e.g. motor vehicle, involves separating carbon dioxide from organic phase, where aqueous solution and organic phase are in direct contact with each other.
AU HAAS T
AE EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 202279152G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting an aqueous solution with dissolved carbon dioxide with an organic phase i.e. alkane. The aqueous solution and the organic phase are heated to temperature of 50 degree Celsius such that the carbon dioxide is migrated from the aqueous solution to the organic phase. The carbon dioxide is separated from the organic phase, where the aqueous solution comprises water by weight at temperature of 10 degree Celsius to 40 degree Celsius and a base, where the aqueous solution and the organic phase are in direct contact with each other and maintained in separate phases, and the base is selected from a group consisting of amine and hydroxide.
   USE - Method for extracting carbon dioxide gas from an aqueous solution i.e. aqueous amine, for different applications e.g. fossil fuel-based power plant and motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables capturing the carbon dioxide from ambient air to convert the captured carbon dioxide to pure carbon dioxide gas stream, thus reducing carbon dioxide concentration in air.

PN KR2020120344-A; KR2217311-B1
TI Catalyst useful in electrode for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises hollow polyhedron containing alloy between at least two active metals active against carbon dioxide reduction.
AU JUN S H
   CHOI U
   PARK J W
   AQIL J
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020A3408F
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises hollow polyhedron containing alloy between at least two active metals active against carbon dioxide reduction.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in electrode for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has improved catalytic ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrode comprising the catalyst;
   (2) reducing carbon dioxide, comprising contacting the working electrode and the fluid containing carbon dioxide, and applying voltage between the working electrode and the counter electrode to generate carbon monoxide using the electrode containing the catalyst as a working electrode; and
   (3) device for reducing carbon dioxide comprising a working electrode which is an electrode containing the catalyst, a counter electrode of the working electrode, a separator positioned between the working electrode and the counter electrode, and carbon dioxide-containing electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alloy includes gold, silver (both are preferred in atomic ratio of 1:1.5-1.5:1), zinc, palladium, gallium, cadmium, copper, indium, and/or tin. The thickness of the shell of the hollow polyhedron is 2-10 nm. The length of the longest side of the polygon forming the plane of the hollow polyhedron is 20-50 nm. The hollow polyhedron is a hollow cube. The reduction is electrochemical reduction. The catalyst has maximum CO Faraday efficiency of -0.50 to -0.75 V (V vs. RHE). The catalyst has voltage of 1 mA/cm2 at CO production current density of -0.4 to -0.55 V (V vs. RHE). The catalyst has mass activity of greater than or equal to 75 A/g per unit mass of alloy of -0.6V (V vs. RHE).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
