PN US2019185389-A1; WO2019115044-A1; EP3687664-A1
TI Binding hazardous agricultural ammonia using organic carbon dioxide involves pressurizing primary reaction vessel with organic carbon dioxide, mixing organic carbon dioxide with ammonia solution and concentrating ammonia-bound solution.
AU VAN WAKEREN P A P
   GRISKONIS E
   NAKAS T
AE VAN WAKEREN P A P (VWAK-Individual)
   GRISKONIS E (GRIS-Individual)
   NAKAS T (NAKA-Individual)
   VAN WAKEREN P A P (VWAK-Individual)
   NAKAS T (NAKA-Individual)
   GRISKONIS E (GRIS-Individual)
   FRIEDMAN M J (FRIE-Individual)
   FRIEDMAN M J (FRIE-Individual)
GA 201951682P
AB    NOVELTY - Binding hazardous agricultural ammonia using organic carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) providing an ammonia solution, organic carbon dioxide, and a primary reaction vessel; (b) pressurizing the primary reaction vessel with the organic carbon dioxide; (c) mixing the organic carbon dioxide with the ammonia solution, within the primary reaction vessel to produce an ammonia-bound solution; and (d) concentrating the ammonia-bound solution by removing a portion of a solvent of the ammonia-bound solution.
   USE - The method is useful for binding hazardous agricultural ammonia using organic carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces ammonia emissions from farming operations producing livestock e.g. cows, pigs and poultry; and allows for the storage and transport of ammonia for future applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises: providing compressed air, solvent, and agricultural waste, where agricultural waste comprises agricultural ammonia; aerating the agricultural waste with the compressed air to extract the agricultural ammonia from the agricultural waste, prior to step (a); and dissolving the agricultural ammonia into the solvent to produce the ammonia solution. The method comprises: providing pH adjusting reagents; and homogenously mixing the pH adjusting reagents into the agricultural waste, prior to the aeration of the agricultural waste. The method comprises: providing a centrifuge; separating the agricultural waste into a liquid waste fraction and a solid waste fraction using the centrifuge; and aerating the liquid waste fraction with compressed air to extract the agricultural ammonia. The method comprises: providing pH adjusting reagents; and homogenously mixing the pH adjusting reagents into the liquid waste fraction, prior to the aeration of the liquid waste fraction. The method comprises: providing a micro-sieve membrane; sifting the quantity of agricultural waste using the micro-sieve membrane to drain a liquid waste fraction from a solid waste fraction; and aerating the liquid waste fraction with the compressed air to extract the agricultural ammonia. The method comprises: providing pH adjusting reagents; and homogenously mixing the pH adjusting reagents into the liquid waste fraction, prior to the aeration of the liquid waste fraction. The method comprises: providing a gas scrubber; dissolving the agricultural ammonia into the solvent to form an ammonia solution using the gas scrubber; and transporting the ammonia solution into the primary reaction vessel. The method comprises: reducing the temperature within the primary reaction vessel, during step (c); diffusing the ammonia solution into the primary reaction vessel as a mist, during step (c); or diffusing the organic carbon dioxide into the primary reaction vessel through the ammonia solution during step (c). Preferred Components: A solute of the ammonia-bound solution is ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium carbamate. The ammonia solution comprises aqueous ammonia, ammonium ions, and water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Manufacturing renewable feedstock comprises            preparing carbon dioxide derived lactone, and            processing the carbon dioxide derived lactone to form            the renewable feedstock.
AU DASTGIR S
   FERRETTI F
   SHARIF M
   JACKSTELL R
   BELLER M
   STEPHAN D W
   FAN L
   JACHSTELL R
AE QATAR FOUND (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A27579
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing (P1) a renewable feedstock                comprises: preparing a carbon dioxide derived                lactone; and processing the carbon dioxide derived                lactone to form the renewable feedstock.
   USE - The process is useful for manufacturing                renewable feedstock for polymer production                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing (P2) the renewable feedstock for                polymer production, comprising preparing carbon                dioxide derived lactone and alkoxycarbonylating the                carbon dioxide derived lactone to form a                diester.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (P1), the                  processing the carbon dioxide derived lactone                  comprises hydrogenating the carbon dioxide                  derived lactone to form a hydrogenated carbon                  dioxide derived lactone. The hydrogenated carbon                  dioxide derived lactone is processed to form                  2-ethylideneheptanoic acid using at least one of                  a homogeneous hydrogenation system and a                  heterogeneous hydrogenation system. The                  hydrogenated carbon dioxide derived lactone is                  processed by reacting delta -lactone in presence                  of metal/phosphine ligand under hydrogen in                  methanol at 90 degrees C to give                  (Z)-2-ethylideneheptanoic acid, this is reacting                  with hydrogen catalyst or palladium carbon                  catalyst to give 2-ethylheptanoic acid, and then                  undergo catalytic hydrogenation in presence of                  molybdenum or rhodium to give 2-ethylheptan-1-ol.                  The hydrogenated carbon dioxide derived lactone                  is processed using a rhodium/phosphine biphasic                  hydrogenation system. The hydrogenated carbon                  dioxide derived lactone is processed by using a                  thiol click reaction to produce a regioselective                  polymer. The hydrogenated carbon dioxide derived                  lactone is processed by reacting delta -lactone                  with dithio compounds of formula (HS-R-SH) (I) to                  give 6-ethyl-tetrahydro-pyran-2-one derivatives                  of formula (II), or reacting                  (-2-ethylideneheptanoic acid with (I) to give                  carboxylic acid derivatives of formula (III). The                  process includes a metal catalyzed reaction e.g.                  reacting delta -lactone primary amine of formula                  (RNH2) (IV) to give amine derivatives of formula                  (V)-(VII).R = H, CH2, CH3, alkyl, cycloalkyl, ethers,                  substituted siloxane, substituted silylethers,                  phenyl or substituted phenyl.The hydrogenated carbon dioxide derived                  lactone is processed using an alkene metathesis.                  The alkene metathesis includes e.g. a                  self-metathesis reaction including                  self-metathesis of                  3-eth-(E)-ylidene-6-vinyl-tetrahydro-pyran-2-one                  to give                  (Z)-6,6'-bis-eth-(E)-ylidene-tetrahdro-(3,3')bipyranylidene-2,2'-dione,                  (3E,3'E)-6,6'-((E)-ethene-1,2-diyl)bis(3-ethylidenetetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-one)                  and                  (E)-3-ethylidene-6-((E)-((E)-6-ethylidene-2-oxo-2H-pyran-3(4H,5H,6H)-ylidene)methyl)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-one,                  or self-metathesis of                  6-ethyl-3-eth-(E)-ylidene-tetrahydro-pyran-2-one                  to give                  (Z)-6,6'-diethyl-tetrahydro-(3,3')bipyranylidene-2,2'-dione.                  The process (P2) further comprises hydrogenating                  the diester to form the renewable feedstock. The                  alkoxycarbonylating the carbon dioxide derived                  lactone includes a methoxycarbonylation reaction                  including reacting delta -lactone with alcohol                  (ROH) and water under heating to give ester                  derivatives of formulae                  (R-O-C(=O)-C(=CHCH3)-CH2CH2-C(OR)-CH=CH2) (Xa)                  and (R-O-C(=O)-C(=CH-CH3)-CH2CH2CH=CH-O-R) (Xb),                  followed by reacting palladium compound catalyst                  and carbon monoxide to give diester compound of                  formula                  (R-O-C(=O)-C(=CH-CH3)-CH2CH2CH=CH-CH2C(=O)-O-R)                  (Xc). Preferred Components: The                  alkoxycarbonylating the carbon dioxide derived                  lactone includes a palladium compound catalyst.                  The palladium compound catalyst includes                  palladium(II) acetate, palladium(II) chloride, or                  bis(dibenzylideneacetone)palladium(0). The                  alkoxycarbonylating includes a phosphine ligand,                  alcohol, solvent or acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202258185T
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide recovery system (10) comprises an electrochemical cell comprising a working electrode containing a carbon dioxide adsorbent, and a counter electrode. Application of a voltage between the working and counter electrodes causes electrons to be supplied from the counter electrode to the working electrode, and enables the carbon dioxide absorbent to bind to carbon dioxide as electrons are supplied. The carbon dioxide absorbent is a crystalline porous body, and has a molecular structure in which a functional group that exchanges electrons and binds to carbon is regularly arranged in a bridging portion of a crystal lattice.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system for separating carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system is capable of increasing the density of carbon dioxide adsorption sites in an electrochemical cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working electrode used in a carbon dioxide recovery system that separates carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing gas containing carbon dioxide by an electrochemical reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide recovery system.
   Carbon dioxide recovery system (10)
   Compressor (11)
   Flow path switching valve (12)
   Utilization device (13)
   Control device (14)
   Recovery device (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystalline porous body is at least one of a metal-organic framework, a covalent organic framework, and a carbonaceous material. The crystalline porous body is a metal-organic framework and the functional group is a ketone group. The metal-organic framework has an aluminum ion coordinated with benzophenone dicarboxylate having a ketone group.

TI Mobile carbon dioxide gas grain storage device comprises bellows connection port, where bellows connection port is sequentially connected to shut-off valve and gasifier through pipe, and gasifier sequentially connects filter.
AU WANG X

   HONG W

   ZHANG Z
   ZOU H
AE CHANGZHOU FEIYUN ENERGY EQUIP TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
   SINOGRAIN TONGLING DIRECT DEPOT CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 201934015P
AB    NOVELTY - Mobile carbon dioxide gas grain storage device comprises a bellows connection port (1), where the bellows connection port is sequentially connected to the shut-off valve (3) and the gasifier (2) through a pipe. The gasifier sequentially connects the filter (5), ball valve, pressure regulating valve (8), ball valve (7) and flow control mechanism through a pipeline. The flow control mechanism includes a flow meter and a flow control valve (9). One end of the flow control valve is connected to the flow meter, and the other end of the flow control valve is connected to the shutoff control valve (11) and the bellows connection port.
   USE - Mobile carbon dioxide gas grain storage device.
   ADVANTAGE - The mobile carbon dioxide gas grain storage device is environmental friendly, and has low cost and convenient application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using mobile carbon dioxide gas storage grain device, which involves:
   (A) storing liquid carbon dioxide in a carbon dioxide tank truck;
   (B) moving the carbon dioxide tank truck to the granary;
   (C) connecting the carbon dioxide tank truck to the granary through the mobile carbon dioxide gas storage grain storage device;
   (D) connecting one bellows connection port of the mobile carbon dioxide gas storage grain storage device with the air outlet of the carbon dioxide tank truck, and connecting the other bellows connection port with the air return port of the carbon dioxide tank truck;
   (E) opening the shut-off valve to allow liquid carbon dioxide to flow into the gasifier;
   (F) passing the carbon dioxide vaporized by the gasifier into the metering control mechanism through the filter, ball valve and pressure regulating valve, such that the pressure regulating valve reduces the gas pressure of the vaporized carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.4 megapascal;
   (G) sending the accurately quantified gasified carbon dioxide to the granary through the shut-off valve and the bellows connection port by the metering control mechanism; and
   (H) connecting the metering control mechanism to the PLC control system, and connecting the monitoring devices installed at the same time to the PLC control system to achieve the purpose of automatically controlling the total outlet temperature and flow rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of mobile carbon dioxide gas grain storage device.
   Bellows connection port (1)
   Gasifier (2)
   Shut-off valve (3)

   Pressure regulating valve (8)
   Flow control valve (9)
   Shutoff control valve (11)

PT P
PN US2021387139-A1; WO2021252713-A1
TI Patterned electrode for producing electroswing adsorption cell utilized in gas separation system to remove target gas e.g. carbon dioxide, comprises electrolyte regions, gas regions and conductive scaffold including electroactive species.
AU POPERE B C
   BURNS E G
   THOMAS-ALYEA K
   VOSKIAN S
   BURNS E
   POPERE B
AE VERDOX INC (VERD-Non-standard)
   VERDOX INC (VERD-Non-standard)
GA 2021E36064
AB    NOVELTY - Patterned electrode 110 comprises many electrolyte regions 140, many gas regions, and a conductive scaffold 120 extending into the electrolyte regions and including an electroactive species 130 capable of bonding with a target gas when the electroactive species is in a reduced state and releasing the target gas when the electroactive species is in an oxidized state.
   USE - Patterned electrode for producing electroswing adsorption cell that is utilized in gas separation system (all claimed) for removing a target gas, preferably carbon dioxide from ambient air and enclosed spaces, such as airtight building and car cabins, for reducing heating cost of incoming air for ventilation, submarines and space capsules, for capturing carbon dioxide post combustion at varying concentrations in electrical power industry, for separating target gases from oil and gas industrial flue gas or process gas, and for capturing sulfur dioxide and other gases from flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The patterned electrode provides gas separation system with improved efficiency and productivity for capturing or removing target gas from gaseous mixture by improving the speed at which gas species are able to diffuse into a sorbent electrode and diffusion time constant of the gas species into the electrode. The gas separation system containing patterned electrode, captures carbon dioxide and other gases from various processes and diverts them for downstream compression or processing, and captures carbon dioxide from burning natural gas used to heat the greenhouses in mild and cold climates and diverts the captured dioxide into the greenhouse for the plants to use in photosynthesis, preferably to feed the plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. an electroswing adsorption cell 100, which comprises the patterned electrode , a second electrode comprising a complementary electroactive composite layer and a separator between the patterned electrode and the second electrode;
   2. a method for producing the patterned electrode, which involves:
   a. depositing a composite material onto a separator, where the composite material comprises an electrolyte and the conductive scaffold coated with the electroactive species;
   b. forming the patterned electrode comprising electrolyte regions and gas regions by laser ablation, lithography, mechanical impression, machining, etching and/or removal of a porogen.
   3. a method for producing electroswing adsorption cell, which involves:
   i. depositing composite material onto a separator; ii. forming the patterned first electrode by removing a portion of the composite material to form electrolyte regions and gas regions;
   iii. coupling a gas flow field and second electrode to the patterned first electrode to provide the electroswing adsorption cell; and
   4. a gas separation system, which comprises many electroswing adsorption cells in fluid communication with a gas inlet and a gas outlet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of an asymmetric electrochemical swing adsorption cell with a patterned electrode.
   Electroswing adsorption cell (100)
   Patterned first electrode (110)
   Conductive scaffold (120)
   Electroactive species (130)
   Electrolyte region (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first electrode has a thickness of 15-500 microm, and a pleated or folded configuration. The gas regions comprise zeolite particles having an average diameter of 1-100 microm. The electrode comprises two or more layers. The gas regions have a ratio of average diameter to the average distance is 0.01-0.5. The patterned electrode, second electrode, and separator are folded or pleated together.Preferred Method: The method for producing electroswing adsorption cell, comprises providing a composite layer comprising electrolyte and first conductive scaffold coated with electroactive species, providing second conductive scaffold comprising gas-filled pores, pleating the composite layer with the second composite layer to provide a patterned first electrode and coupling gas flowfield and a second electrode to the patterned first electrode to provide the electroswing adsorption cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The electrolyte region comprises an electrolyte comprising ionic liquid, gel electrolyte, gel polymer electrolyte or a polymer ionic liquid. The electroactive species comprise an electroactive polymer, electroactive oligomer and/or electroactive organic compound.

PN CN112586566-A
TI Processing peanut blended oil by performing embryo processing, frying, drying peanuts, extracting, distilling, refining, adding olive oil, sunflower oil and Camellia oil and dripping traditional Chinese medicinal liquid and emulsifier.
AU WEI Z
AE HUNAN SHANGYOU FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202135975Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing peanut blended oil involves (a) screening out impurities and dirt adhering to the surface of the peanuts and adding the peanut kernel raw materials into embryo rolling machine for embryo processing, (b) frying the peanut embryos into a drum-type wok and drying the peanuts, (c) adding peanut embryo into extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting, passing the carbon dioxide and peanut oil mixture into the separation kettle for vacuum separation, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle and collecting peanut oil crude oil, (d) adding crude peanut oil into a molecular distillation tank for distillation and refining, (e) mixing peanut oil, olive oil, sunflower oil, and Camellia oil, then dripping traditional Chinese medicinal liquid and emulsifier while stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain peanut blended oil.
   USE - The method is useful for processing peanut blended oil for regulating physical fitness, improving microcirculation system and enhancing disease resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares peanut blended oil which is green and healthy, can improves production quality and efficiency of peanut oil and lowers residual oil content in peanut meal, hence reduces waste and production costs and ensures nutrition and safety of peanut oil products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for processing peanut blended oil involves (a) screening out impurities and dirt adhering to the surface of the peanuts, then removing the shells of the treated peanuts to obtain peanut kernel raw materials, adding the peanut kernel raw materials into embryo rolling machine for embryo processing and collecting the peanut embryo slices, (b) adding the peanut embryos into a drum-type wok for frying, sun drying the peanuts, drying the dried peanuts in a drying box until the moisture content is 3.2-3.8% and then collecting the peanut embryo, (c) adding peanut embryo into extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, controlling the flow of carbon dioxide in the extraction kettle at 28-30 l/hour, then adjusting the extraction process conditions, when the extraction is completed, passing the carbon dioxide and peanut oil mixture into the separation kettle for vacuum separation, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, and collecting peanut oil crude oil from the separation kettle, (d) adding crude peanut oil into a molecular distillation tank, adjusting the molecular distillation process parameters, controlling the scraping speed at 100-300 revolutions per minute (rpm) and feed rate at 15-50 ml/minute, refining and collecting the refined peanut oil, (e) weighing and adding 180-240 pts. wt. peanut oil, 60-100 pts. wt. olive oil, 40-60 pts. wt. sunflower oil, and 30-50 pts. wt. Camellia oil into a mixing container, stirring to form a blended oil, then dripping 5-10 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicinal liquid and 4-8 pts. wt. emulsifier to the blended oil while stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain peanut blended oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In step (a), the rotation speed of the embryo rolling machine is 1800-2400 rpm and the thickness of the prepared peanut embryo is 0.30-0.35 mm. The step (b) involves frying the peanut for 40-50 minutes at 150-170 degrees C and air-drying for 10-18 hours. The step (b) involves drying at 90-95 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The step (c) involves extracting at 45-55 degrees C and 30-35 MPa for 2.5-3.5 hours. The step (c) involves controlling the temperature in the separation kettle at 30-50 degrees C and pressure in the separation kettle at 10-20 MPa. The step (d) involves controlling vacuum degree inside the molecular distillation tank at 3-8 Pa, distillation temperature at 120-150 degrees C and condensation temperature at 30-50 degrees C. In step (e), the traditional Chinese medicinal liquid comprises Acorus calamus, bamboo leaves, licorice and Asparagus. In step (e), the emulsifier is at least one of polyglycerin fatty acid ester, monoglycerin fatty acid ester, and sorbitan fatty acid ester.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN109627333-A
TI Preparing monoclonal antibodies against Bc48 protein of Babesia in Yili, Xinjiang, comprises e.g. purification of HIS-Bc48 recombinant protein from Escherichia coli containing Bc48 recombinant plasmid of Xinjiang Ili, Babesia,.
AU GERIC B
   WANG P
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG W
   SONG J
   LIU S
   SONG R
   WEN X
   ZHANG M
   WANG L
AE UNIV XINJIANG AGRIC (UYXA-C)
GA 201938597C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monoclonal antibodies against Bc48 protein of Babesia in Yili, Xinjiang, comprises e.g. (i) purification of HIS-Bc48 recombinant protein from Escherichia coli containing Bc48 recombinant plasmid of Xinjiang Ili, Babesia, and determination of protein concentration by protein content analyzer, and storing at -80 degrees C, (ii) injecting many healthy BALB/c mice with immunopurified HIS-Bc48 recombinant protein, dosing recombinant protein of HIBBC48, immunizing mice, taking mice and intraperitoneally injecting carbon dioxide titer, recombinant protein enhances immunity, (iii) preparing Aseptic extraction of the spleen of the mouse, fresh spleen samples from the spleen cells, and mixing the spleen cells and myeloma cells, adding polyethylene glycol for fusion, resuspending the cells with 1640 medium containing fetal bovine serum, adding to 96-well cell culture plate, and culture, placing plate in carbon dioxide incubator on the second day of fusion.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monoclonal antibodies against Bc48 protein of Babesia in Yili, Xinjiang.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing monoclonal antibodies against Bc48 protein of Babesia in Yili, Xinjiang, comprises (i) purification of HIS-Bc48 recombinant protein from Escherichia coli containing Bc48 recombinant plasmid of Xinjiang Ili, Babesia, and determination of protein concentration by protein content analyzer, and storing at -80 degrees C, (ii) injecting many healthy BALB/c mice aged 6-8 weeks old with immunopurified HIS-Bc48 recombinant protein in the back of the back, dosing recombinant protein of HIBBC48 at 100 mu g/n and was free of immunization, the same dose of antigen, immunizing the mice at intervals of 2 weeks, and 10 days after the third immunization, the tail titer was used to test the antibody titer, taking mice at 37 degrees C and intraperitoneally injecting 5% carbon dioxide titer above 105 100 mu g 3 days before the fusion, recombinant protein enhances immunity, (iii) preparing Aseptic extraction of the spleen of the mouse, fresh spleen samples from the spleen cells, and mixing the spleen cells and myeloma cells in a mass ratio of 5:1-10:1, in a centrifuge tube, adding 1 ml of 50% polyethylene glycol for fusion, resuspending the cells with 1640 medium containing 15% fetal bovine serum, adding to 96-well cell culture plate, 100 mu l/well, and culture, placing plate in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, on the second day of fusion, adding 1% HAT medium, 100 mu l per well, and placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, (iv) After 5-7 days of culture, completely changing the HAT medium once, and after 4-5 days, aspirating the culture solution, and screening the indirect ELISA with HIS-Bc48 protein and Bc48 transfected 293T cells by indirect immunofluorescence, taking positive strains for subcloning, and obtaining 4-5 subclones to obtain hybridoma cell line stably secreting monoclonal antibodies against Babesia Bc48 protein.

PN WO2022015516-A1
TI Method used for oil production, offshore power generation, ammonia production, and carbon dioxide injection for enhanced oil recovery, by providing offshore facility comprising gas turbine, producing produced hydrocarbon, combusting, reforming, combining and compressing and injecting into reservoir.
AU WANG R
   AUSBORN M C
   LI Z
   CHANG-TANG E M
AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202213789N
AB    NOVELTY - Method involves providing an offshore facility comprising a gas turbine, a first gas separator, a reformer, a second gas separator, and an ammonia reactor; producing a produced hydrocarbon from a gas reservoir to the offshore facility; separating the produced hydrocarbon to yield a produced gas and a produced oil; combusting the produced gas in a gas turbine to produce power and a flue gas; at least partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas in the first gas separator to produce a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; reforming a portion of the produced gas in the reformer to produce a stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide; reacting the hydrogen stream and the nitrogen-enriched flue gas in the ammonia reactor to yield ammonia; combining and compressing the carbon dioxide stream and the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas in a gas compressor to produce a compressed gas; and injecting the compressed gas from the gas compressor into the gas reservoir.
   USE - The method is useful for oil production, offshore power generation, ammonia production, and carbon dioxide injection for enhanced oil recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the offshore facilities that produce oil, ammonia, and power as the primary or only product exports and readily available power that may allow for placing production and transportation facilities further from shore and tap resources that are not currently economically viable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method involves providing an offshore facility comprising a gas turbine, a first gas separator, a reformer, a second gas separator, and an ammonia reactor; producing a produced hydrocarbon from a gas reservoir to the offshore facility; separating the produced hydrocarbon to yield a produced gas and a produced oil; combusting the produced gas in a gas turbine to produce power and a flue gas; at least partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas in the first gas separator to produce a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; reforming a portion of the produced gas in the reformer to produce a stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide; at least partially separating the carbon dioxide in the second gas separator from the stream to yield a carbon dioxide stream and a hydrogen stream; reacting the hydrogen stream and the nitrogen-enriched flue gas in the ammonia reactor to yield ammonia; combining and compressing the carbon dioxide stream and the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas in a gas compressor to produce a compressed gas; and injecting the compressed gas from the gas compressor into the gas reservoir, where greater than or equal to 80 mol% hydrocarbon in the produced gas is combusted, reformed, and/or injected into the gas reservoir. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an offshore facility comprising an offshore production unit configured for producing a produced hydrocarbon from a reservoir; an oil and gas separator to receive the produced hydrocarbon for separating the produced hydrocarbon into a produced gas and a produced oil; a gas turbine configured to receive the produced gas for producing power and a flue gas; a first gas separator configured to receive the flue gas for at least partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas and for producing a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; a reformer configured to receive a portion of the produced gas from upstream of the gas turbine for producing a stream comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen; a second gas separator configured to receive the stream for at least partially removing carbon dioxide from the stream and for producing a carbon dioxide stream and a hydrogen stream; an ammonia reactor configured to receive the hydrogen stream and the nitrogen-enriched flue gas for producing ammonia; a gas compressor configured to receive the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and the carbon dioxide stream for producing a compressed gas; and an injection unit configured for injecting the compressed gas from the gas compressor into the gas reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon-derived exports from the offshore facility consists essentially of the produced oil, the power, and ammonia. Preferred Method: The method further involves purifying the produced gas upstream of the gas turbine to at least partially remove of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, a heavy metal, and hydrogen sulfide, where purifying at least partially removes carbon dioxide, and the method further involves combining carbon dioxide with the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and the carbon dioxide stream before compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas. The method further involves diverting a portion of the produced gas upstream of the gas turbine and adding the portion of the produced gas to the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas before compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas, where the produced gas comprises a gas chosen from steam, hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, 1-4C hydrocarbons, metal-containing compounds, and their combination. The method further involves condensing a water vapor from the flue gas to produce a liquid water and injecting the liquid water with the compressed gas into the gas reservoir, where the power is 500 megawatts to 3 gigawatts; and the power is stored, consumed either on board or by nearby subsea equipment, transmitted via cables to onshore grid or transported by battery ship, or their combination. The method further involves providing the power to production platforms within operating range, where the production platforms are floating structures, where the power is comprised of a high voltage alternative current, a high voltage direct current or their combination; and the offshore facility is a low motion floater chosen from a semi-submersible, a ship-shaped monohull, and their combination. The method further involves controlling a position of the low motion floater by a computer-controlled system. The method further involves cooling the flue gas with sea water. Preferred Composition: The produced gas comprises a combined carbon dioxide and nitrogen content of greater than or equal to 25 mol%. The produced gas has a gas volume of 1 million standard cubic feet per day (MMSCFD) or greater. The produced gas has a gas volume of 50-500 MMSCFD.

PN CN213294870-U; CN113620437-A
TI Processing system for reducing hardness of carbon dioxide waste water, has tubular precise filter whose bottom is connected with waste water removing reaction tank, where electromagnetic valve is provided between reaction tank and filter.
AU WANG Z
   GU T
   JIN Z
   JIANG J
AE WUXI PERFECTECH ENG EQUIP CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202161146N
AB    (CN213294870-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide waste water reducing hardness processing system, comprising a waste water inlet, the waste water inlet is connected with the top of the mixer, the waste water inlet and the mixer is provided with a first electromagnetic valve, the bottom of the mixer and the top of the carbon dioxide container tank are connected with each other; between the carbon dioxide container tank and the mixer is provided with a second electromagnetic valve; the top part of the mixer is connected with the carbon dioxide inlet; the fourth electromagnetic valve is provided with a second pH meter; the bottom of the tubular precise filter is connected with the waste water removing reaction tank; a fifth electromagnetic valve is arranged between the waste water removing reaction tank and the tubular precise filter; The utility model through the reaction of carbon dioxide and waste water, can form calcium carbonate particles, so that the hardness of the waste water can be reduced, and the tubular precise filter can filter the calcium carbonate particles in the waste water, so as to greatly reduce the hardness of the waste water.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a salt compound containing a cation and an anion in a molecule, where the anion includes at least one cyanuric anions of formula (I)-(III). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   (2) carbon dioxide conversion catalyst, comprising either: amine compounds having triple bonds, carbon dioxide, and salt compound containing ammonium and cyanuric acid of formula (IVa)-(IVd) as a catalyst for carbon dioxide conversion reaction in which carbon atom of carbon dioxide is bonded to nitrogen atom derived from the amine group by reaction with nitrogen atom and oxygen atom as heteroatom to obtain a 5-membered heterocyclic compound, or aminobenzonitrile, carbon dioxide, and compound-IV as catalyst for carbon dioxide conversion reaction in which 6-membered heterocyclic compound containing nitrogen atom and oxygen atom is obtained as heteroatom, and carbon atom derived from carbon dioxide is bonded between the nitrogen atom derived from the amino group and the nitrogen atom derived from the nitrile group in the aminobenzonitrile.

AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)

The reactive                metal nucleation substrate comprises Group II                element or alloy or Group I element or alloy or                Group XIII element or alloy.

Preferred                  Process: The method involves introducing at least                  one of an additive gas or an additive liquid into                  the liquid contacting the reactive metal                  nucleation substrate. The additive gas or                  additive liquid comprises one or more of promoter                  for hydrate formation, surfactant, or enzyme. The                  clathrate hydrate nucleation condition comprises                  pressure of greater than 150 psig or from 150                  psig to 4500 psig. The multiple of gas bubble                  comprises reaction product gas, and reaction                  product gas comprises hydrogen gas. The method                  involves forming multiple of gas bubbles,                  contacting liquid with reactive metal nucleation                  substrate. The reactive metal nucleation                  substrate comprises Group II element or alloy or                  group I element or alloy or Group XIII element or                  alloy.

TI Conversion of carbon dioxide used for producing e.g. alcohol, involves exposing cathode in electrolysis device, comprising electroconductive support to carbon dioxide-containing aqueous catholyte solution, and applying electrical current.
AU FONTECAVE M
   MOUGEL V
   TRAN NGOC H
   WAKERLEY D
   LAMAISON S
   TRAN N H
AE PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
GA 2020561347
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon dioxide involves (a) providing an electrolysis device comprising an anode and a cathode, (b) exposing cathode to a carbon dioxide-containing aqueous catholyte solution, (c) applying an electrical current between electrodes, to reduce the carbon dioxide into syngas which is a mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The cathode comprises an electroconductive support of which at least a portion of the surface is covered by a metal (m1) deposit of zinc and of a metal (m2) chosen from copper, silver, and gold, and preferably copper. The metal deposit comprising at least 1 wt.% alloy phase(s) of zinc and metal (m2).
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide into syngas used for producing unsaturated hydrocarbon, alcohol and/or aldehyde, by Fischer-Tropsch reaction, methanol synthesis, methanation reaction, hydroformylation, or syngas fermentation (all claimed) for producing fossil fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables conversion of carbon dioxide using the metal deposit having excellent porous structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrode;
   (2) preparation of electrode; and
   (3) electrolysis device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catholyte solution comprises a salt of bicarbonate chosen from alkali-metal bicarbonate and quaternary ammonium bicarbonate, preferably cesium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, tetramethylammonium bicarbonate, or tetraethylammonium bicarbonate, preferably cesium bicarbonate, which is formed in-situ by reaction of a hydroxide salt, preferably potassium hydroxide with carbon dioxide contained in the catholyte solution. The electroconductive support comprises an electroconductive material chosen from copper, steel, aluminum, zinc or titanium, a metal oxide from fluorine-doped titanium oxide, or indium tin oxide, a metal sulfide from cadmium sulfide or zinc sulfide, carbon in particular in the form of carbon felt, graphite, vitreous carbon, or boron-doped diamond, and a semiconductor such as silicon. The preparation of electrode is performed by immersing the support in an acidic aqueous solution containing ions of zinc and ions of metal (m2). Te zinc salts are chosen from zinc sulfate, zinc chloride, zinc perchlorate, zinc nitrate, zinc bromide, or zinc phosphate, and oxidized zinc salt chosen from zinc oxide, zinc hydroxide-based salt, or zinc oxide-based salt. The salt of metal (m2) is chosen from copper sulfate, copper chloride, a salt based on copper hydroxide, silver chloride, silver nitrate, silver chlorate, silver carbonate, silver phosphate, silver perchlorate, silver sulfate, silver fluoride, silver nitrite, gold (III) bromide, gold (I) chloride, gold (III) chloride, gold monoiodide, potassium gold (III) chloride, chloroauric acid, and oxidized salt of metal (m2), preferably silver oxide or silver hydroxide. Preferred Properties: The metal deposit has specific surface area of at least 0.1 m2.g-1, preferably 1-50 m2.g-1, and thickness of 1-250 mu m, preferably 5-200 mu m. Preferred Composition: The metal deposit preferably comprises at least 30 wt.% alloy phase(s) of zinc and metal(m2), and a porous structure having average pore size of 1-500 mu m, preferably 20-100 mu m. The weight ratio of zinc and metal (m2) is 99/1-35/65, preferably less than 35/6.

PT P
PN CN108785166-A; CN108785166-B
TI Edible healthcare cosmetic comprises purple peony seeds, purple peony petals, purple peony pistil and fresh roses.
AU LU Y
   RAN T
   DOU Y
AE GANSU CHANGYUAN ECOLOGY AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
   GANSU CHANGYUAN ECOLOGY AGRIC TECHNOLOGY (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 2018933611
AB    NOVELTY - Edible healthcare cosmetic comprises 87-91 wt.% purple peony seeds, 6-8 wt.% purple peony petals, 2-5 wt.% purple peony pistil and 1-2 wt.% fresh roses.
   USE - As edible healthcare cosmetic.
   ADVANTAGE - The healthcare cosmetic is environmentally-friendly has high quality, comprehensive nutrition, no harmful substances, recycles waste residue and its production process is simple, cost-effective and lowers energy consumption, retains the natural original active ingredients of the raw materials, improves yield and saves resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing edible healthcare cosmetic, which involves (a) (i) selecting full-bodied purple peony seeds having low-maturation, sieving the purple peony seeds into a rotating sieve to remove sand and other impurities, feeding the purple peony seeds into the storage hopper, packing the purple peony seeds at the opening of the hopper in a bag, freezing in a refrigerator at -10 degrees C for 40 hours, storing the frozen purple peony seeds in the warehouse for 40 hours and adding into a sheller for shelling, (ii) feeding the purple peony seed kernel from the sheller into the multi-stage screening machine using hoist, removing the discolored peony seed kernel, gradually adding the selected purple peony seed kernels into a drum-type rotating long-flow airflow dryer with the feeding machine, heating raw material using natural gas to ignite the hot blast stove, heating the combustion exhaust gas to heat the hot air furnace tube so that the hot combustion gas passes through the filter to remove the air in the dust, blowing the heated hot air into the drum-type rotating long tube airflow dryer by the fan and drying the purple peony seed kernel until the moisture content is 2-3% and (iii) feeding the dried purple peony kernels into storage tank, naturally cooling to 35 degrees C, feeding into pulverizer by feeder, crushing into powder having particle size of 30-40 mesh and separating the pulverized peony seed kernel powder by a cyclone separator, (b) (i) selecting pest-free purple peony petals, washing with water, evenly adding into the drum-type rotating long tube airflow dryer, heating raw material using natural gas to ignite the hot blast stove, heating the combustion exhaust gas to heat the hot air furnace tube so that the hot combustion gas passes through the filter to remove the air in the dust and drying the purple peony petal at 50 plus minus 2 degrees C until the moisture content is 4-5% and (ii) naturally cooling to 32 degrees C, crushing into powder having particle size of 30-40 mesh and separating the pulverized peony petal powder by a cyclone separator and (c) (i) cleaning the entire production unit clean and free from any form of oil and grease, connecting the cylinder with carbon dioxide to the system, closing the valve A, opening the valve B and the valve C, feeding carbon dioxide into the low temperature liquefier, cooling carbon dioxide to -5 degrees C using freezing liquid at -25 degrees C for the low temperature liquefier and feeding cooled carbon dioxide to the buffer, (ii) opening the valve D, feeding the carbon dioxide into the presser, feeding the pressurized carbon dioxide in the press machine to the heater and heating the heater heating pipe by hot water of 60 degrees C, mixing the purple peony seed powder, purple peony petal powder, rose flower powder and purple peony pistil powder in a multi-chromatography slant plate gas-solid phase reverse flow extraction tower, (iii) opening the valve E, feeding the carbon dioxide into the a multi-chromatography slant plate gas-solid phase reverse flow extraction tower and controlling the pressure at 20-25 MPa and 38-50 degrees C, (iv) opening the top of the extraction tower valve F, filtering the extract through the filter and cooling to 15 degrees C in aftercooler, (v) collecting the carbon dioxide gas phase, citronellol, nerol, phenylethyl alcohol, decyl alcohol, linalool, red pigment and carotene liquid phase, opening the mixture of the extracted product components to the valve G, separating, controlling the temperature at 30-40 degrees C, vaporizing the vaporized in the mixture and feeding the semi-finished product into storage slot, (vi) feeding the exhaust gas from the top of the pre-separator 30 into the aftercooler, cooling the exhaust gas with 15 degrees C cooling water, opening the valve H, feeding the exhaust gas into the rear separator, separating the waste in the exhaust gas, opening the valve K, feeding into the semi-finished storage tank, closing valve B and opening valves C and A and (vii) feeding the exhaust gas at the top of the rear separator to the low temperature liquefier, lowering the pressure, opening the residue discharge port at the bottom, recovering the residue, opening the front separator, draining the bottom, opening the rear separator, draining the bottom, opening the semi-finished storage tank valve L, feeding semi-finished product into the multi-layer superfine packing pressure filter, filtering the impurities in the product, opening the valve M, feeding the product storage tank, opening the valve N and packing the product.

TI Preparing carbon dioxide based polylactic acid            copolymer comprises e.g. reacting lactic acid and            ethylsulfonyl chloride, carrying out esterification            reaction with vinyl alcohol, carrying out oxidation            reaction with oxidizing agent and carrying out            copolymerization reaction with carbon dioxide.
AU LI L
   MA S
   TANG H
   ZHANG T
AE OUSHANGYUAN TIANJIN CO LTD (OUSH-Non-standard)
GA 202290916B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide based polylactic acid                copolymer comprises (1) reacting lactic acid and                ethylsulfonyl chloride to obtain                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoic acid (I), (2)                carrying out esterification reaction of (I) and                vinyl alcohol to obtain vinyl                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoate (II), (3) carrying                out oxidation reaction of (II) and oxidizing agent                to obtain oxiran-2-yl                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoate (III), (4) carrying                out copolymerization reaction of (III) and carbon                dioxide to obtain carbon dioxide-based polylactic                acid copolymer (IV).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide based polylactic acid copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polylactic acid                copolymer has good physical and chemical                properties, thermal stability and                biodegradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide based polylactic acid                copolymer comprises (1) reacting lactic acid and                ethylsulfonyl chloride to obtain                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoic acid of formula (I),                (2) carrying out esterification reaction of (I) and                vinyl alcohol to obtain vinyl                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoate of formula (II), (3)                carrying out oxidation reaction of (II) and                oxidizing agent to obtain oxiran-2-yl                2-(ethylsulfonyloxy)propanoate of formula (III),                (4) carrying out copolymerization reaction of (III)                and carbon dioxide to obtain carbon dioxide-based                polylactic acid copolymer of formula (IV).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide-based polylactic acid copolymer, prepared                by the above method.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the reaction of step                  (1), the reaction temperature is -10&#8451;                  to 50&#8451;, and the reaction time is 8-24                  hours. In the esterification reaction of step                  (2), sulfuric acid is used as catalyst, the                  reaction temperature is -10&#8451; to                  80&#8451;, and the reaction time is 2-24                  hours. In the oxidation reaction of step (3),                  acetylacetone peroxide is used as the oxidant,                  the reaction temperature is 50-100&#8451;,                  and the reaction time is 3-8 hours. In the                  copolymerization reaction of step (4), ethyl zinc                  and water are used as catalysts, the reaction                  temperature is 50-150&#8451;, the reaction                  pressure is 2-4 MPa, and the reaction time is                  3-48 hours.Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  lactic acid to ethylsulfonyl chloride is 1:1. In                  step (2), the molar ratio of vinyl alcohol to                  lactic acid in step (1) is 1:1; and the molar                  ratio of vinyl alcohol to sulfuric acid is                  (50-100):1. In step (3), the molar ratio of the                  acetylacetone peroxide to the vinyl alcohol in                  step (2) is (1-5):1. In step (4), the value of n                  ranges from 50 to 100; the molar ratio of (III)                  to ethyl zinc and water is                  (100-1000):1:0.1.

PT P
PN US2018243681-A1; EP3563923-A1; US10493397-B2
TI System enables recovery of carbon dioxide from flue gas by scrubbing flue gas in absorption column packed with aqueous solution of ammonia and salt, and heating carbon dioxide enriched solution taken from bottom of column.
AU STAUFFER J E
AE STAUFFER J E (STAU-Individual)
   STAUFFER J E (STAU-Individual)
GA 201867510M
AB    NOVELTY - A system enables recovery of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing flue gas by burning a fossil fuel to create a supply of flue gas containing carbon dioxide at a fossil site, continuously transporting the flue gas supply to the top of an absorption column (10) packed with an aqueous solution of ammonia, and salt chosen from sodium chloride and potassium chloride while replenishing the solution to the top of the column to scrub the flue gas by a downward co-current flow of the gas and the solution though the column at 15-50 degrees C to saturate the solution with carbon dioxide to produce a bicarbonate and ammonia chloride, heating the carbon dioxide enriched solution taken from the bottom of the column to release the absorbed carbon dioxide, and recycling the regenerated aqueous solution to the top of the column to replenish the solution.
   USE - System is used for recovering carbon dioxide from flue gas generated during burning of fossil fuel in electric power generation plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is capable of efficiently removing carbon dioxide from flue gas and easily regenerating the solvent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide-recovering system.
   Burner (2)
   Heater (4)
   Absorption column (10)
   Conduit (11)

PN CN110486902-A; CN110486902-B
TI Method for measuring carbon-dioxide concentration of new fan, involves correcting current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value according to preset correction algorithm, and outputting corrected value as carbon-dioxide display value.
AU LV Y
   SUN D
AE HISENSE SHANDONG AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (HISG-C)
GA 2019A03318
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S10) carbon-dioxide concentration detection value in real time through the sensor of the new fan. A judgment is made (S20) whether the current carbon-dioxide concentration change rate corresponding to current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value satisfies a preset concentration deviation condition. The current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value is output (S30) as a carbon-dioxide display value, if the carbon-dioxide concentration change rate corresponding to current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value does not satisfy preset concentration deviation condition. The current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value is corrected (S40) according to a preset correction algorithm, and corrected value is output as a carbon-dioxide display value, if the carbon-dioxide concentration change rate corresponding to the current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value satisfies the preset concentration deviation condition.
   USE - Method for measuring carbon-dioxide concentration of new fan.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the accuracy of carbon-dioxide concentration detection, and correctly guide the users to use a new fan through a highly accurate carbon-dioxide concentration display.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a CO2 concentration measuring device for a new fan; and
   (2) a new fan.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for measuring carbon-dioxide concentration of new fan. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining carbon-dioxide concentration detection value (S10)
   Step for judging whether the current carbon-dioxide concentration change rate corresponding to current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value satisfies a preset concentration deviation condition (S20)
   Step for outputting current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value as a carbon-dioxide display value (S30)
   Step for correcting current carbon-dioxide concentration detection value according to preset correction algorithm (S40)

PN CN107576778-A
TI Quantitative characterization of carbon dioxide            fleeing degree comprises carrying out carbon dioxide            flooding experiment, collecting, recording, drawing,            determining, subjecting regression equation and            calculating.
AU YANG H
   ZHAO X
   JIANG S
   JIN Z
   PANG Z
   WANG H
   YAO Z
   KANG Y
   LIANG K
AE SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
GA 2018060620
AB    NOVELTY - Quantitative characterization of carbon                dioxide fleeing degree comprises (i) carrying out                carbon dioxide flooding experiment, collecting and                recording end of oil and carbon dioxide gas, (ii)                drawing carbon dioxide gas containing rate curve,                (iii) determining corresponding injection amount                and carbon dioxide injection amount corresponding                to each inflection point, (iv) subjecting                regression equation of each section of the                corresponding gas content curve with regression of                the data of the gas content curve of carbon dioxide                and (v) calculating carbon dioxide channeling                comprehensive index.
   USE - The method is useful for quantitative                characterization of carbon dioxide fleeing                degree.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more accurate and uniform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quantitative characterization of carbon                dioxide fleeing degree comprises (i) carrying out                carbon dioxide flooding experiment under reservoir                pressure, collecting and recording the end of oil                and carbon dioxide gas till the test injection end                carbon dioxide quantity is 1 PV, (ii) drawing the                carbon dioxide gas containing rate curve, where the                ordinate is carbon dioxide rate and the abscissa is                carbon dioxide injection, (iii) determining the                corresponding injection amount and the carbon                dioxide injection amount corresponding to each                inflection point when the carbon dioxide gas                migration occurs according to the carbon dioxide                gas rate curve, (iv) subjecting the regression                equation of each section of the corresponding gas                content curve with regression of the data of the                gas content curve of carbon dioxide and (v)                calculating the carbon dioxide channeling                comprehensive index using the formula, where the                formula is given the specification.

PT P
PN WO2020092053-A1; CA3117998-A1; US2022002327-A1; US11414445-B2; US2022348598-A1
TI Reducing carbon dioxide involves obtaining carbon            dioxide dioxaphosphetane composition, placing carbon            dioxide dioxaphosphetane composition in solution,            combining sodium borohydride with solution comprises            carbon dioxide dioxaphosphetane.
AU OTOO B
AE UNIV OAKLAND FOUNDED GEN BAPTISTS INC (UYOA-Non-standard)
   UNIV OAKLAND FOUNDED GEN BAPTIST INC (UYOA-Non-standard)
   UNIV OAKLAND FOUNDED GEN BAPTISTS INC (UYOA-Non-standard)
GA 202037623P
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide involves obtaining                carbon dioxide dioxaphosphetane composition,                placing the carbon dioxide dioxaphosphetane                composition in a solution and combining sodium                borohydride with solution comprises carbon dioxide                dioxaphosphetane to form formate composition and                reducing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce carbon dioxide                that is cost effective and energy efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for reducing carbonate, which                involves dissolving the carbonate in water,                combining carbonate solution with boric acid,                adding sodium borohydride to combination to reduce                carbonate to formate;
   (2) a solid carbon dioxide dioxaphosphetane                composition, which is represented by formula                (I);
   (3) a method for making carbon dioxide                dioxaphosphetane composition, which involves                combining phosphate and water in container,                flushing the combination of dibasic sodium                phosphate and water with carbon dioxide, stirring                the resultant combination and cooling the resultant                combination to form carbon dioxide dioxaphosphetane                composition; and
   (4) a method for capturing carbon dioxide,                which involves capturing carbon dioxide in carbon                dioxide dioxaphosphetane composition, which is                represented by formula (I).
   M=cation and/or alkyl group, H, Na, K, aryl,                and alkyl.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The formate                  composition is sodium formate. The phosphate is                  precipitated via combination of sodium                  borohydride with solution comprises carbon                  dioxide. The carbonate is a water-soluble                  carbonate. The carbonate is metal carbonate. The                  metal carbonate is selected from ammonium                  carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate,                  rubidium carbonate, and cesium carbonate. The                  metal carbonate is alkali metal carbonate. The                  formate is sodium formate. The boric acid is                  added at about 1 molar equivalent of boric acid                  to carbonate. The composition is crystalline. The                  phosphate is selected from ammonium phosphate,                  sodium phosphate, potassium phosphate, and                  dialkyl phosphate. The carbon dioxide                  dioxaphosphetane composition is crystalline                  composition. Preferred Conditions: The boric acid                  is added at more than 1 molar equivalent of boric                  acid to carbonate. The carbonate is reduced to a                  formate at an efficacy of at least 90%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN EP4043576-A1; FR3119847-A1
TI Producing gaseous hydrogen and solid carbon            comprises supplying extracted and recovered carbon            dioxide into bioreactor tank containing nutrient medium            for rapidly growing biomass culture and separating            digestate and biogas.
AU HUBERT K
AE ENERKA CONSEIL (ENER-Non-standard)
   ENERKA CONSEIL (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5344U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing gaseous hydrogen and solid carbon                comprises (a) performing heat recovery and carbon                dioxide extraction from captured gases and fumes of                industrial origin or at the outlet of a heating                network, (b) supplying recovered carbon dioxide                into bioreactor tank containing a nutrient medium                to be enriched with carbon dioxide for a rapidly                growing biomass culture composed of strains of                microalgae, or macroalgae, (c) transforming by                anaerobic fermentation or by hydrothermal                gasification of the cultivated biomass for                production of a recoverable digestate and biogas                comprising methane and carbon dioxide, (d)                separating digestate and biogas, (e) separating at                least methane and carbon dioxide from produced                biogas, and (f) carrying out thermal decomposition                of methane to produce carbon in solid state and                gaseous hydrogen, where methane from natural gas                distribution network is mixed with methane                separated in step (e) and methane mixture that                underwent thermal decomposition in step (f).
   USE - The method is useful for producing gaseous                hydrogen and solid carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and                environmentally-friendly, recovered calories from                gases and fumes can be reused in the installation                to optimize the biomass culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                installation for producing gaseous hydrogen and                solid carbon, comprising a means of sampling gases                and fumes from a source emitting gases and fumes. A                rapid growth biomass culture device composed of                strains of microalgae, or macroalgae. An anaerobic                fermentation device for production of biogas                composed of methane and carbon dioxide by anaerobic                fermentation of the fast growing biomass culture or                a hydrothermal gasification device for production                of biogas composed of methane, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen by thermochemical process on fast growing                biomass culture. A methane separator of biogas                composed of methane and carbon dioxide generated by                anaerobic fermentation device or biogas composed of                methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen generated by                hydrothermal gasification device. A methane                decomposition reactor for the production of                hydrogen and solid carbon from methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (c) biogas                  produced during transformation by hydrothermal                  gasification of biomass grown in step (b)                  comprises methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen.                  In step (e) hydrogen is separated from methane                  and carbon dioxide from biogas. In the step (f),                  thermal decomposition of methane is carried out                  by plasma torch, or by microwaves, or by thermal                  process with metal catalyst or by high                  temperature process. The heat recovered from                  captured fumes can also be used in step (c)                  during conversion by anaerobic fermentation or by                  hydrothermal gasification of biomass and/or can                  be used in step (e) during thermal decomposition                  of methane. The carbon dioxide separated in step                  (e) is returned to bioreactor. The digestate                  separated in step (d) is returned to                  bioreactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The installation                  further comprises at least one thermal heat                  exchanger for recovering heat from flue gases or                  that generated by means of forming gaseous                  hydrogen and solid carbon to give it further to                  culture device of biomass and/or anaerobic                  fermentation device or hydrothermal gasification                  device.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113883958-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide gas gun device for small carrier rocket emission, has heating device for heating liquid carbon dioxide in main gas chamber to form carbon dioxide, and bottom support for supporting main air chamber and gun tube.
AU ZHANG B
   LI J
   ZHAO L
   ZHANG H
   CHEN A
   NIE W
   ZHI Y
   ZHU Y
   LIN W
AE UNIV SPACE ENG PLA STRATEGIC SUPPORT FOR (UYSP-Non-standard)
GA 2022112494
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a main air chamber coaxially sleeved on a gun tube outside an air guide hole. A CO2 gas storage tank fills liquid carbon dioxide to the main gas chamber. A vacuum pumping device vacuumizes an inner cavity of a rear end of the gun tube. A heating device heats the liquid CO2 in the main chamber to form supercritical carbon dioxide. A bullet releasing mechanism enters a gun pipe rear end inner cavity through a charging hole and an air guiding hole to push a bullet to emit forward. A bottom support supports the main and the gun pipe back end inner cavities.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide gas gun device for small carrier rocket emission. Can also be used in emergency rescue, emergency rescue and army daily training applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses phase state transition characteristic of carbon dioxide from liquid to supercritical state, so as to realize energy storage with larger power density, short emitting period, stable property, low requirement for storage and transportation environment. The transmitting tube and the main air chamber adopt coaxial parallel structure to save space and more convenient to use. The device is in the low temperature environment, and carbon dioxide gas structure is stable, not chemical reaction with the gun body material, the gun case will not be damaged due to emission, and has long service life. The size of the device is adjusted, it can be used for boosting the unmanned aerial vehicle, increasing the initial speed to save energy, skilfully transmits the supercritical carbon dioxide into the gun tube to push the elastic support to emit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a supercritical carbon dioxide gas gun device for small carrier rocket emission.
   Vacuum pump (41)
   Vacuum tube (42)
   Vacuum valve (43)
   Critical gas-guide tube (61)
   Electronic vacuum valve (62)

TI Changing mode of operation of device, comprising e.g. converting carbon dioxide-containing electrolysis insert in electrolyzer into electrolysis product containing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in normal operation of device.
AU CICHY T
   HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
   ZICH T
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LIND-Non-standard)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202045574C
AB    NOVELTY - Changing mode of operation of device having an electrolyzer, a compressor and a membrane separation device between normal and standby mode, comprising converting a carbon dioxide-containing electrolysis insert (3) in the electrolyzer into an electrolysis product (4) containing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in normal operation of the device, passing at least part of which via the compressor and supplying to the membrane separation device at increased pressure to obtain a retentate (8) which is enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide compared to the electrolysis product. The electrolyser is completely fluidically isolated from the membrane separation device and then shut down to switch from normal to standby operation, and the pressure conditions in the membrane separation device are largely maintained.
   USE - The method is useful for changing mode of operation of devic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and the device produces amount of retentate depleted in carbon dioxide more economically than in the prior art with high flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device comprising a compressor, a membrane separation device and an electrolyser with which an electrolysis insert comprising carbon dioxide can be converted into an electrolysis product containing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, at least part of which can be conducted via the compressor and can be fed to the membrane separation device at elevated pressure in order to to obtain a retentate that is enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide compared to the electrolysis product, during normal operation of the device, where the device has an isolating device with at least one valve, with which the electrolyser flows from the membrane separating device in terms of flow technology when changing from normal to standby mode while largely maintaining the pressure conditions in the membrane separating device can be completely isolated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Insert (1)
   Carbon dioxide-containing electrolysis insert (3)
   Electrolysis product (4)
   Retentate (8)
   First line (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The compressor is connected to the membrane separation device to form a fluidically closed system in which the suction side of the compressor is connected via a first line to the permeate side of the membrane separation device, and is connected to the pressure side of the compressor or the retentate side of the membrane separation device via a second line to maintain the pressure conditions in the membrane separation device, where the differential pressure between the retentate and permeate side is controlled via a control valve arranged in the second line. The electrolyzer is started up and then its fluidic insulation is completely removed from the membrane separation device while largely maintaining the pressure conditions in the membrane separation device to switch from standby to normal operation. The system, which is sealed off from the outside in terms of flow technology, and which comprises the compressor and the membrane separation device, is connected to the electrolyzer which has already started up to switch from standby to normal operation, where the path for the retentate downstream of the membrane separation device is opened at the same time and the direct connections between the suction side of the compressor and the permeate side of the membrane separation device and the pressure side of the compressor or the retentate side of the membrane separation device are interrupted. The isolating device comprises multiple valves and as a first (10) and a second line for connecting the membrane separating device to the compressor to form a closed system in which the suction side of the compressor is connected via the first line to the permeate side of the membrane separation device and via the second line to the pressure side of the compressor or the retentate side of the membrane separation device is connected, where a control element is arranged in the second line by which the differential pressure between the retentate and permeate sides of the membrane separation device can be controlled when the operating mode changes. The device further comprises a mixing device arranged upstream of the electrolyser and connected to the permeate side of the membrane separation device in terms of flow technology, in which an insert (1) containing carbon dioxide with at least part of the permeate obtained in membrane separation device for use in electrolysis can be mixed, where the fluidic connection existing between the permeate side of the membrane separation device and the mixing device comprises a valve belonging to the isolation device, which is open during normal operation of the device and closed in standby mode. The electrolyzer is a high or a low temperature electrolyzer which is designed to electrochemically form carbon dioxide alone or together with water to form hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide to implement.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Container for carbon dioxide absorbent, used in electronic device, has carbon dioxide absorption device that is provided with carbon dioxide concentration section that serves as supply section for supplying gas.
AU NAKA T
   DOE T
   KISHIRA K
   YAMADA H
   YANASE I
   SATO K
AE SHARP KK (SHAF-C)
   UNIV SAITAMA NAT CORP (SAIT-C)
   SENJU METAL IND CO (SENJ-C)
GA 201874064G
AB    NOVELTY - The container has a carbon dioxide absorber (10) that is provided for absorbing carbon dioxide contained in a gas containing water and carbon dioxide from the gas contains a tetravalent lithium silicate. A carbon dioxide absorption device (1) is provided with a carbon dioxide concentration section (11) that serves as a supply section for supplying a gas (G0-G2) having a CO2 concentration of 1% or more and a relative humidity of 80% or more to a CO2 absorber, and a humidification section (12).
   USE - Container for carbon dioxide absorbent, used in electronic device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorption ratio in a carbon dioxide absorber is improved. The reliability of a detection of the carbon dioxide concentration in the detection unit is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The condensed unit is provided with supply unit supplies the gas after making carbon dioxide levels increase to the carbon-dioxide absorber. The concentration detection unit detects the carbon dioxide levels of the gas. The concentration control unit controls the condensed unit, based on the detection result of concentration detection unit. The concentration control unit makes the carbon dioxide levels of the gas increase, when the carbon dioxide levels which the concentration detection unit detected are lower than setting value. The humidification unit is provided with supply unit for supplying the gas after making humidity increase to the carbon-dioxide absorber. The humidity detection unit detects the humidity of the gas. The humidification control unit controls the humidification unit, based on the detection result of humidity detection unit. The supply switching control unit performs switching to the feed route in the feed route switch unit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon-dioxide absorption method involves absorbing the carbon dioxide contained in the gas out of the gas containing moisture content and carbon dioxide. The said carbon-dioxide absorber contains tetravalent lithium silicate, where carbon dioxide levels are 1 % or more. The gas which is 80% or more of a relative humidity is supplied to the carbon-dioxide absorber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the functional block diagram of the structure of carbon dioxide absorber.
   Carbon dioxide absorption device (1)
   Carbon dioxide absorber (10)
   Carbon dioxide concentration section (11)
   Humidification section (12)
   Gas (G0-G2)
