TI Apparatus for carbon dioxide capture, sequestration and utilization, comprises substrates (having surfaces coated with layer of carbon-dioxide capture coating composition) each arranged and supported in spaced-apart relationship.
AU MCDANIEL C S
AE THRI LLC (THRI-Non-standard)
GA 202199472Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture apparatus (415, 416) comprises substrates each arranged and supported in a spaced-apart relationship with each adjacent one of the substrates. The spaced-apart substrates jointly define an occupied plan view area. Each of the substrates has one or more surfaces coated with a layer of carbon-dioxide capture coating composition. The substrate inhibits the transmission of water through a thickness and along a length and width.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for carbon dioxide capture, sequestration and utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can capture carbon dioxide from a gaseous environment such as, for example, the earth's atmosphere to enable long-term storage, utilization or atmospheric balancing of such captured carbon dioxide (i.e. carbon dioxide sequestration, and/or captured carbon utilization). On a unit-volume basis, a carbon dioxide capture facility provides a total area of photosynthetic organism-coated vertical surfaces magnitudes of order greater than the atmosphere-exposed surface of a corresponding volume of ocean or forest. In this manner, the apparatus enable a marked increase in the amount of photosynthetic conversion of carbon dioxide for a given volume of space as compared to ocean-bound algae or terrestrial plants and, thus, advantageously overcome shortcomings associated with other negative emission technologies.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a diagrammatic view of attributes associated with implementation of carbon dioxide capture and sequestration.
   Cap (402)
   Carbon sequestration system (403)
   Carbon dioxide capture devices (415, 416)
   Encasement sleeves (417)
   Gas device (475)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The coatable surfaces of each of the substrates define a respective coatable surface area; a total coatable surface area of the carbon dioxide capture apparatus is equal to an aggregation of the coatable surface area of all of the substrates; and the total coatable surface area of the carbon dioxide capture apparatus is at least 40 times the plan view area. The substrates extend vertically in the plan view. The substrate is made from a material that is light transmissive. The substrate of each of the carbon dioxide capture devices is generally flat. The carbon-dioxide capture coating composition is adhered to the surface respectively coated on it. The substrate of each of the carbon dioxide capture devices is in the form of an elongated tubular body; each elongated tubular body has a different cross-sectional size than each other elongated tubular body; and a particular one of the elongated tubular bodies has nested in all of the other elongated tubular bodies having a cross-sectional size smaller than the cross-sectional size of the particular one of the elongated tubular bodies.

PN IN202141039273-A
TI Quantifying carbon dioxide in concrete cured with carbon dioxide by detecting presence of absorbed carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide detection test, identifying pH using indicator test and estimating amount of carbon dioxide absorbed.
AU SANTHOSH K T
   SOWJANYA L
   BALAJI K
   CHANDAN K P
   RAMESH B
   SETH D
AE SANTHOSH K T (SANT-Individual)
   SOWJANYA L (SOWJ-Individual)
   BALAJI K (BALA-Individual)
   CHANDAN K P (CHAN-Individual)
   RAMESH B (RAME-Individual)
   SETH D (SETH-Individual)
GA 2021B1110R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for quantification of carbon dioxide in concrete cured with carbon dioxide involves detecting presence of absorbed carbon dioxide in a concrete sample using at least one carbon dioxide detection test, identifying pH level of the concrete samples using at least one indicator test and utilizing the obtained pH levels to estimate amount of carbon dioxide absorbed in the concrete sample.
   USE - The method is useful for quantifying carbon dioxide in concrete cured with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The at least one carbon dioxide detection test comprises either lime water test or liquid ammonia test. The lime water test utilizes lime water which is colorless that turns to milky white when it reacts with carbon dioxide cured concrete and the liquid ammonia test utilizes liquid ammonia that forms a crystalline white precipitate when reacts with carbon dioxide gas thus indicating presence of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide cured concrete. The at least one indicator test comprises either bromothymol blue indicator test or hydrogen carbonate indicator test. The bromothymol blue indicator test allows determination of a change in pH of 6-7.6 while changing its color from blue to yellow through green colors and the hydrogen carbonate indicator test utilizes hydrogen carbonate as indicator which is red in color and turns to orange when it reacts with concrete cured with carbon dioxide and turns to yellow color if more amount of carbon dioxide is absorbed by the concrete. The at least one indicator test utilizes specific color indications that correspond to specific carbon dioxide concentrations.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids.    ADVANTAGE - The method has good light transmittance, improves molecular weight and distribution index of the copolymer and mechanical strength of the copolymer, reduces melt index compared with the un-added branching agent copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The branching agent is diphenyl methane diisocyanate, itaconic anhydride, maleic anhydride, dicumyl peroxide, benzoyl peroxide, pyromellitic dianhydride, diisopropylbenzene peroxide and diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine or dimethylaminopropylamine. The catalyst is the mixture of Lewis acid and Lewis base, the Lewis acid is triethyl boron, tripropyl boron, tributyl boron, tribenzyl boron, trimethylamine boron or dimethyl amine boron, the Lewis base is tetramethyl ammonium chloride, tetraethyl ammonium chloride, tetrabutyl ammonium chloride, triethyl methyl ammonium chloride, benzyl trimethyl ammonium chloride or benzyl triethyl ammonium chloride. Preferred Conditions: The mol ratio of Lewis base to Lewis acid is 1:2-20, the mol ratio of Lewis base to phthalic anhydride is 1:400-2000, the mol feeding ratio of propylene oxide and phthalic anhydride is 4-16:1, the mol feeding ratio of diphenyl methane diisocyanate and phthalic anhydride is 0.5-10:1. The mol ratio of Lewis base to phthalic anhydride is 1:400-800, the mol ratio of propylene oxide to phthalic anhydride is 4-10:1 and the mol ratio of diphenyl methane diisocyanate to phthalic anhydride is 0.5-4:1. The carbon dioxide pressure of the ring-opening copolymerization reaction is 0.5-3 megaPascal, the reaction temperature is 60-90degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 5-8 hours.

PN DE102019120641-A1
TI Production of synthesis gas such as carbon dioxide and hydrogen from waste such as household waste, involves sorting inorganic components from waste, and thermally using remaining organic components to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU VIETEN J
   ROEB M
AE DEUT ZENT LUFT & RAUMFAHRT EV (DELF-C)
GA 202112749K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthesis gas involves sorting inorganic components from the waste, and thermally using the remaining organic components to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is controlled by units of at least one metal oxide.
   USE - Production of synthesis gas such as carbon dioxide and hydrogen from waste such as household waste, plastic waste and/or composite materials (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy production of synthesis gas having excellent recyclability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the production of synthesis gas. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves (a) supplying waste, (b) optionally separating the inorganic components from the waste, (c) contacting the waste with at least one metal oxide, (d) heating the waste metal oxide mixture to 500 degrees C or more, by a partial oxidizing and forming carbon dioxide and hydrogen and reducing the metal oxide, and (e) optionally purifying and separating of carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The method further involves (f) deoxidizing the reduced metal oxide, so that a metal oxide is obtained which can be used again in the process, (g) storing the synthesis gas formed in the step (e), and (h) synthesizing hydrocarbons from the synthesis gas. The energy released in the step (f) and (h) used to generate electricity. Preferred Components: The metal oxide is chosen from platinum, palladium, gold, silver, nickel and copper, preferably copper. The energy required for the process is provided by concentrated solar radiation unit.

TI Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon dioxide            adsorbent involves adding amino acid and pore-forming            agent into reaction pot, mixing uniformly, mixing            mixture in high temperature furnace heating, cooling            product after washing and drying.
AU WANG L
   HU X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 201957352F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon dioxide                adsorbent involves adding amino acid and                pore-forming agent into reaction pot, mixing                uniformly, mixing mixture in high temperature                furnace heating, cooling product after washing and                drying to obtain nitrogen-doped porous carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped porous                carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nitrogen-doped                porous carbon dioxide adsorbent that has simple                preparation process, low cost, high yield, large                specific surface area, and strong adsorption                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed solution of                  amino acid includes glycine, alanine, valine,                  leucine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, proline,                  tryptophan, serine, tyrosine, cysteine,                  methionine, asparagine, glutamine, threonine,                  aspartic acid, glutamic acid, lysine, arginine                  and histidine, pore-forming agent is alkaline                  substance, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate, amino                  acid and pore forming agent according to mass                  ratio of 1:0.1-100. The microscopic feature is                  higher specific surface area and carbon dioxide                  adsorption. Preferred Conditions: The high                  temperature furnace comprises intermittent                  continuous high-temperature furnace, batch-type                  high temperature furnace, tubular furnace,                  continuous high temperature furnace. Preferred                  Process: The method involves subjecting mixing                  mode is mechanical agitation mixing and chemical                  mixing, chemical mixing refers to process in                  which material is completely dissolve in certain                  solvent, stir uniformly, and then solvent is                  remove by process such as evaporation to obtain                  uniform mixture. The method further involves                  heating mixture in high temperature furnace,                  furnace working atmosphere comprises hydrogen                  gas, nitrogen gas and argon gas, pressurizing to                  1-5 atmospheric pressure, heating to 300-1500                  degrees C and reacting for 1-72 hours to obtain                  nitrogen-doped porous carbondioxide                  adsorbent.

TI Removing benzene in carbon dioxide gas comprises mixing benzene carbon dioxide gas with ambient gas to obtain mixed gas, passing mixed gas into catalytic reaction device, reacting, passing the intermediate gas into an adsorption device.
AU CUI S
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201822186C
AB    NOVELTY - Removing benzene in carbon dioxide gas comprises mixing benzene carbon dioxide gas with ambient gas to obtain a mixed gas, passing the mixed gas into a catalytic reaction device, reacting in presence of a catalyst to obtain intermediate gas, passing the intermediate gas into an adsorption device to carry out adsorption removal treatment in presence of an adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for removing benzene in carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the energy consumption, satisfies high purifying requirement, high efficiency, achieves removal of benzene and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ambient gas comprises 20-30 vol.%, preferably 25-30 wt.% oxygen and carbon dioxide (balance amount). The mixed gas comprises 50-5000 mg/l, preferably 500-3000 mg/l benzene gas. The concentration benzene in the intermediate gas is less than or equal to 300 mg/l, preferably less than or equal to 20 mg/l. The reaction removal treatment catalyst comprises alumina (preferred), silicon oxide, titanium oxide, zinc oxide or calcium oxide as a carrier, and loaded with noble metal or non-noble metal. The noble metal is platinum or palladium. The non-noble metal is copper and at least one of manganese, cerium and zirconium. The catalytic reaction device is a tubular reactor or a fixed-bed tubular reactor packed with the catalyst. The adsorption device includes at least one adsorption processor, connected in series. The adsorbent is activated carbon, modified activated carbon, carbon molecular sieve or activated carbon fiber. Preferred Method: The catalytic reaction is carried out at 250-300 degrees C with mixed gas space velocity of 1000-20000/hour. The adsorption removal treatment is carried out at 25-30 degrees C for 30-60 minutes.

AE SHENZHEN GAS GROUP CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201980530M
AB    NOVELTY - Methane/carbon dioxide separating device                comprises gas source and many stages of separation                mechanisms, the gas source is connected to the                first-stage separation mechanism of many stages of                separation mechanisms, the water source is                connected to each of the separation mechanisms of                the stages, the reaction temperature of the                hydration reactor of each separation mechanism is                higher than 283 K, the raw material gas is                separated by methane/carbon dioxide separating                device of hydrate method to obtain methane hydrate                and liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The methane/carbon dioxide separating device                is useful for hydrate method to separate methane                hydrate and liquid carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                methane/carbon dioxide separation process of                hydrate method by using methane/carbon dioxide                separating device comprising subjecting raw                material gas to pressure condensation through                compressor and condenser of first stage separation                mechanism; mixing raw material gas with water,                passing to the hydration reactor of first stage                separation mechanism for hydration reaction, where                the reaction temperature of the hydration reaction                is higher than 283 K, and the reaction pressure is                higher than 7 MPa; separating the product obtained                by the hydration reaction; obtaining the                concentration of carbon dioxide in the hydrate                phase of the separated first-stage separation                mechanism; if the concentration of carbon dioxide                satisfies the first predetermined condition,                passing separated solid methane hydrate into                storage tank for storage and transportation,                otherwise, heating and decomposing methane hydrate                into the second-stage methane separation mechanism,                and orderly analogy until the concentration of                hydrate phase carbon dioxide satisfies the first                predetermined condition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                methane/carbon dioxide separating device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction pressure                  of the hydration reactor in the separation                  mechanism of each stage is higher than 7 MPa. The                  many stages of separation mechanisms include                  three-stage methane separation mechanism, and the                  gas source and the water source are both                  connected to the first stage methane separation                  mechanism, the first stage methane separation                  mechanism is respectively connected to the second                  stage methane separation mechanism, and the                  second stage methane separation mechanism and the                  third stage methane separation mechanism. The                  third stage methane separation mechanism is                  connected to the carbon dioxide separation                  mechanism, the liquid carbon dioxide partially                  separated by the third methane separation                  mechanism flows into the carbon dioxide                  separation mechanism. Preferred Composition: The                  methane/carbon dioxide separating device further                  comprises carbon dioxide separation mechanism,                  the carbon dioxide separation mechanism is                  connected to the first stage methane separation                  mechanism, and the carbon dioxide separation                  mechanism is connected to the second methane                  separation mechanism to form a loop, so that the                  carbon dioxide separation mechanism separates the                  gas methane portion into the second methane                  separation mechanism, the portion of the liquid                  carbon dioxide separated by the second methane                  separation mechanism flows into the carbon                  dioxide separation mechanism. Preferred Method:                  The method further comprises obtaining the                  concentration of methane dissolved in the                  separated liquid carbon dioxide; if the                  concentration of methane satisfies the second                  predetermined condition, introducing the                  separated liquid carbon dioxide into the storage                  tank for storage and transportation, otherwise,                  heating and decomposing the liquid carbon dioxide                  into the second-stage carbon dioxide separation                  mechanism, orderly analogy until the                  concentration of the methane satisfies the second                  predetermined condition. In the method, the                  separation of the product obtained by the                  hydration reaction specifically comprises                  detecting the density of solid methane hydrate                  and the liquid carbon dioxide density in the                  product, and obtaining a preset requirement of                  the solid water hydrate body methane density,                  liquid carbon dioxide density, and liquid water                  density; determining a separation process                  according to the preset requirement, and                  transmitting the product to a separation device                  corresponding to the separation process for                  separation.

PN US2018169569-A1; WO2018108588-A1
TI System for removing chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide in combustion of fuel in plant, has water wash station fixed downstream of and connected to carbon dioxide absorber to absorb ammonia slip from flue gas via ammonia-based solution.
AU DUBE S K
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
GA 201848602Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a direct contact cooler (12) for cooling flue gas that includes gaseous carbon dioxide. A carbon dioxide absorber is fixed downstream of and fluidly connected to the direct contact cooler to absorb the gaseous carbon dioxide from the flue gas via ammonia-based solution to form a carbon dioxide-rich stream and an ammoniated, carbon dioxide-lean/depleted stream. A water wash station (16) is fixed downstream of and fluidly connected to the carbon dioxide absorber to absorb an ammonia slip from the flue gas through washing solution and form molecular ammonia stream, where the direct contact cooler is fluidly connected to the water wash station and receives the molecular ammonia stream.
   USE - System for removing chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide in combustion of fuel such as coal, oil, peat and waste in combustion plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures residual molecular ammonia within washing solution is converted to ionic ammonia so as to reduce and/or prevent ammonia slip from occurring in a water wash station. The system reduces capital and operating costs by utilizing flue gas within a direct contact cooler to strip/recover ammonia captured by the water wash station via the washing solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a direct contact cooler for an ammonia-based carbon dioxide removal system
   (2) a method for recovering absorbed ammonia from a water wash station in a chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide removal system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a system for removing chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide in combustion of fuel.
   Carbon dioxide removing system (10)
   Direct contact cooler (12)
   Water wash station (16)
   Regenerator (24)
   Reboiler (25)

PT P
PN IN201721025093-A; IN368509-B
TI Integrated synergistic process to produce propylene, comprises e.g. dehydrogenating propane in presence of carbon dioxide and first catalyst to produce gaseous mixture of propylene, unconverted propane, unconverted carbon dioxide and syngas.
AU BHATT U B
   KALANTRI P B
   JASRA R V
   SREEDHARAN U
   JINKA K M
   SRIVASTAVA V K
AE RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
   RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
GA 2019615302
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated synergistic process for producing propylene, comprises: e.g. (a) dehydrogenating propane in the presence of carbon dioxide and at least one first catalyst, at 500-700 degrees C and at a pressure of 0.1-1 kg/cm2, to produce a gaseous mixture comprising propylene, unconverted propane, unconverted carbon dioxide and syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where the ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas is 1:1; (b) feeding the gaseous mixture to a first separator to obtain a first stream containing syngas and unconverted carbon dioxide and a second stream containing propylene and unconverted propane; (c) feeding the first stream to a second separator to obtain a syngas stream and a carbon dioxide stream; and (d) contacting the syngas stream with at least one second catalyst, at 100-400 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-60 kg/cm2, to produce an intermediate stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME), unconverted carbon dioxide, hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   USE - The process is useful for producing propylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: increases the production of propylene with reduced capital expenditure (CAPEX) and operational expenditure (OPEX); enhances the life of a catalyst used in dehydrogenation by reducing the deposition of carbon on the catalyst; provides efficient separation of streams e.g. carbon dioxide, water, DME and propane divided wall columns; utilizes the divided wall columns utilize significantly less energy for efficient separation of the streams, thus reducing CAPEX and OPEX of the entire process; and utilizes the quantity of raw material required for producing propylene that is reduced since the separated portion of the unconverted propane is recycled to the propane dehydrogenation (PDH) reactor for producing propylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated synergistic process for producing propylene, comprises: (a) dehydrogenating propane in the presence of carbon dioxide and at least one first catalyst, at 500-700 degrees C and at a pressure of 0.1-1 kg/cm2, to produce a gaseous mixture comprising propylene, unconverted propane, unconverted carbon dioxide and syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where the ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas is 1:1; (b) feeding the gaseous mixture to a first separator to obtain a first stream containing syngas and unconverted carbon dioxide and a second stream containing propylene and unconverted propane; (c) feeding the first stream to a second separator to obtain a syngas stream and a carbon dioxide stream; (d) contacting the syngas stream with at least one second catalyst, at 100-400 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-60 kg/cm2, to produce an intermediate stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME), unconverted carbon dioxide, hydrogen and carbon monoxide; (e) feeding the intermediate stream to a third separator for separating at least a portion of unconverted carbon dioxide, hydrogen and carbon monoxide from the intermediate stream separately and recycling the separated portion of unconverted carbon dioxide to the process step (a) and recycling the separated portion of hydrogen and carbon monoxide to the process step (d); (f) contacting the intermediate stream after removal of the unconverted carbon dioxide, hydrogen and carbon monoxide with at least one third catalyst, at 200-600 degrees C and at a pressure of 0.5-10 kg/cm2, to produce a resultant stream comprising propylene, water and unreacted DME; and (g) feeding the resultant stream to a fourth separator along with the second stream to obtain a product stream containing propylene and three residual streams, where a first residual stream comprises separated unconverted propane which is recycled to the process step (a), a second residual stream contains separated unreacted DME which is recycled to the process step (f) and a third residual stream comprises water which is separated from the fourth separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first catalyst comprises chromium or platinum-tin. The second catalyst comprises copper oxide, chromium oxide, zinc oxide or aluminum oxide. The third catalyst comprises zeolites, alumino-phosphate molecular sieves, silicoalumino phosphate molecular sieves or their substituted forms.
   EXAMPLE - 1 mole Propane was dehydrogenated in the presence of 5 mole of carbon dioxide and platinum group noble metal or metal oxide supported catalyst (for dehydrogenation of propane to propylene) at 600 degrees C and at a pressure of 1 kg/cm2 , to produce a gaseous mixture comprising propylene, unconverted propane, unconverted carbon dioxide and syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide. Gaseous mixture was fed to a first separator to obtain first stream containing syngas and -unconverted carbon dioxide and second stream containing propylene and unconverted propane. First stream was fed to a second separator to obtain syngas stream and carbon dioxide stream. Syngas stream was contacted with copper-zinc/alumina or copper based mixed metal oxide supported on various supports like alumina, zeolite (for the conversion of syngas to DME) at a 300 degrees C and at a pressure of 50 kg/cm2, to produce the intermediate stream. Intermediate stream was further fed to a third separator for separating the unconverted carbon dioxide and hydrogen and carbon monoxide and DME. Mixture of unconverted carbon dioxide and hydrogen and carbon monoxide were fed to the second separator. Separated portion of the unconverted carbon dioxide from the second separator was recycled to the dehydrogenation step and the separated portion of hydrogen and carbon monoxide was recycled to the intermediate stream producing step. Intermediate stream after removal of the unconverted carbon dioxide and hydrogen and carbon monoxide was contacted with MFI or CHA (Chabazite) based zeolite (for the conversion of DME to propylene) at 500 degrees C and at a pressure of 5 kg/cm2, to produce a resultant stream comprising propylene, water and unreacted DME. Resultant stream was fed to a fourth separator along with the second stream to obtain a product stream containing 92% propylene.

PN CN113388425-A
TI Device for preparing full spectrum carbon dioxide            hydrocarbon fuel using solar energy, has photo-thermal            high temperature waste heat recovery unit that is            connected to outlet of carbon dioxide capture            conversion unit.
AU XUAN Y
   HE J
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 2021A8651G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a solar collecting mirror (1)                and a frequency divider (2). The solar collecting                mirror is used for polymerizing the solar beam. The                frequency divider is used for separating the solar                beam after polymerization. A photovoltaic cell (3)                is connected to a photovoltaic low-temperature                waste heat recovery unit (5) and a water                electrolysis device (6), and outputs electrical                energy to the water electrolysis device. The water                electrolysis device outputs hydrogen, oxygen and                water. The hydrogen is allowed to enter into a                carbon dioxide capture conversion unit (4). The                oxygen is released to the outside along with the                water circulation. The water is connected with the                photovoltaic low-temperature waste heat recovery                unit to take away the heat of the photovoltaic                cell. A photo-thermal high temperature waste heat                recovery unit is connected to the outlet of the                carbon dioxide capture conversion unit.

PN KR2018041783-A
TI Low temperature reservoir with automatic ventilation device, has controller which drives actuator to close first and second windows and stop ventilation fan when carbon dioxide concentration falls below predetermined reference value.
AU KIM J R
   LEE J Y
   LEE B J
   SONG K J
AE PURGOFARM CO LTD (PURG-Non-standard)
GA 201834098K
AB    NOVELTY - The reservoir has a ventilation fan which is provided in each of first and second windows (130,132). A carbon dioxide sensor (140) measures the concentration of carbon dioxide in storage unit (110). When the carbon dioxide concentration measured by carbon dioxide sensor exceeds a preset reference value, actuator is driven to open the first and second windows and operate ventilation fan. A controller (160) drives actuator to close first and second windows and stop the ventilation fan when carbon dioxide concentration falls below a predetermined reference value.
   USE - Low temperature reservoir with automatic ventilation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The ventilation is smoothly performed, and the opening and closing of each window is automatically performed in correspondence with the concentration of carbon dioxide in the low-temperature storage. The inconvenience of opening windows is solved. The windows are formed in the walls facing each other so that the ventilation is smoothly performed, thus the occurrence of condensation is reduced. The occurrence of the condensation phenomenon is actively suppressed by lowering the temperature and humidity difference between the indoor and the outdoor to below the dew point by using the ventilation fan.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the low temperature reservoir with automatic ventilation device.
   Storage unit (110)
   First window (130)
   Second window (132)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (140)
   Controller (160)

AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 2018390885
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a fuel cell reformer (20) which is provided for converting methanol and oxygen into carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen gas. A carbon dioxide liquefier (50) is provided for changing the temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide so as to liquefy the carbon dioxide gas extracted from the fuel cell reformer with liquid carbon dioxide. A storage tank is provided for storing the liquid carbon dioxide in the storage tank.
   USE - Carbon dioxide exhausting device for submarine equipped with fuel cell reformer.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the carbon dioxide gas produced after the reforming process is dissolved in seawater and stored in the liquid state as carbon dioxide in the compartment without being discharged to the outside, the gas can be vaporized in the snorkel stage, and the carbon dioxide gas can be treated in an environmentally friendly manner. The need of a separate weight-compensating tank in the cabin is avoided, such that the procedure for treating the carbon dioxide gas is very simple. Since the carbon dioxide gas produced during the operation period is also stored in the form of liquid carbon dioxide and processed later, the phenomenon in which an operation is obstructed by carbon dioxide gas in advance is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for discharging carbon dioxide submarine equipped with fuel cell reformer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram showing the overall structure of carbon dioxide exhausting apparatus equipped with fuel cell reformer. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide exhausting device (1)
   Fuel cell reformer (20)
   Status detection sensor (25)
   Fuel cell module (40)
   Carbon dioxide liquefier (50)
DC J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

TI Carbon dioxide electrolysis/carbon fuel cell            integrated apparatus used in system for electric power            generation device, contains electrolytic bath tank for            accommodating molten salt, carbon deposition/combustion            electrode, and power supply.
AU YASUHIKO I
   TOKUJIRO N
   HIROYUKI W
   WATABE H
   ITO Y
   NISHIKIORI T
   WATANABE H
AE IMSEP CO LTD (IMSE-Non-standard)
   IMSEP CO LTD (IMSE-Non-standard)
   IMSEP CO LTD (IMSE-Non-standard)
GA 201919681C
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide electrolysis/carbon fuel cell                integrated apparatus (1) comprises an electrolytic                bath tank (2) for accommodating molten salt (20)                containing an oxide ion, a carbon                deposition/combustion electrode (3) in which                portion(s) is immersed in molten salt, an oxygen                gas-evolution electrode (4) in which portion(s) is                immersed in molten salt, electrically connected to                carbon deposition/combustion electrode, an oxygen                gaseous reduction electrode (5) in which portion(s)                is immersed in salt, a carbon dioxide gas supply                portion (6) for supplying carbon dioxide-containing                gas to molten salt, and producing carbonate ion, a                power supply (7) for applying voltage in which                carbonate ion is reduced on carbon                deposition/combustion electrode, and carbon                precipitates between carbon deposition/combustion                electrode and oxygen gas-evolution electrode, and                an oxygen gas supply portion (8) for supplying                oxygen-containing gas to oxygen gaseous reduction                electrode, and producing oxide ion in salt.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolysis/carbon fuel cell                integrated apparatus is used in system (claimed)                for electric power generation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The system using the carbon dioxide                electrolysis/carbon fuel cell integrated apparatus                enables mutual conversion with electrical energy                and chemical energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) system, which comprises the integrated                apparatus and a carbon dioxide gas storage portion                (9) for storing the carbon dioxide gas emitted in                carbon deposition/combustion electrode; and
   (2) usage method of the integrated apparatus,                which involves supplying carbon dioxide-containing                gas to the molten salt from the carbon dioxide gas                supply portion, carrying out carbon                electrodeposition process by applying voltage,                supplying oxygen-containing gas to the oxygen                gaseous reduction electrode from the oxygen gas                supply part, and producing oxide ion in the molten                salt, and carrying out electric power generation                process by generating carbon dioxide gas on carbon                which is precipitated on the carbon                deposition/combustion electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide electrolysis/carbon fuel cell                integrated apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide electrolysis/carbon fuel cell                integrated apparatus (1)
   Electrolytic bath tank (2)

   Oxygen gaseous reduction electrode (5)
   Carbon dioxide gas supply portion (6)
   Power supply (7)
   Oxygen gas supply portion (8)
   Carbon dioxide gas storage portion (9)
   Carbon storage portion (10)
   Molten salt (20)
   Carbon dioxide gas collection portion                (30)
   Oxygen gas-gathering portion (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The apparatus comprises                  an oxygen gas-gathering portion (40) for                  collecting oxygen gas emitted in the oxygen                  gas-evolution electrode, and a carbon dioxide gas                  collection portion (30) for collecting carbon                  dioxide gas emitted in the carbon                  deposition/combustion electrode. The carbon                  deposition/combustion electrode comprises                  portion(s) of inner wall of the electrolytic bath                  tank. The system comprises a carbon storage                  portion (10) for storing carbon generated in                  carbon deposition/combustion electrode. Preferred                  Composition: The molten salt contains alkali                  metal halide and/or alkaline-earth metal halide.                  The molten salt contains alkali metal carbonate                  and/or alkaline-earth metal carbonate.

PT P
PN CN107904175-A; CN107904175-B
TI Incubator useful for induced amplification of immune cells comprises e.g. oxygen source connected with box body to supply oxygen into culture space, carbon dioxide source connected to box body to supply carbon dioxide into culture space.
AU XU Z
   ZHANG X
AE GUANGZHOU SHAAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201830051Q
AB    NOVELTY - Incubator comprises a box body, which is a defined culture space, and an oxygen source connected with the box body for supplying oxygen into the culture space, a carbon dioxide source connected to the box body for supplying carbon dioxide into the culture space, an oxygen concentration sensor arranged on the inner wall of the box body for detecting the concentration of oxygen in the culture space, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor set on the inner wall of the box body for detecting carbon dioxide concentration in the culture space, and a controller set on the box body.
   USE - The incubator is useful for induced amplification of immune cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Incubator comprises a box body, which is a defined culture space, and an oxygen source connected with the box body for supplying oxygen into the culture space, a carbon dioxide source connected to the box body for supplying carbon dioxide into the culture space, an oxygen concentration sensor arranged on the inner wall of the box body for detecting the concentration of oxygen in the culture space, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor set on the inner wall of the box body for detecting carbon dioxide concentration in the culture space, and a controller set on the box body and connected with the oxygen source. The carbon dioxide source is connected with the carbon dioxide concentration sensor. The oxygen source and the carbon dioxide source are controlled based on the data of the oxygen concentration sensor and the carbon dioxide concentration sensor. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) induced amplification of immune cells by using the incubator, comprising inoculating mononuclear cells into a bioreactor from day 1 to day 9, where the controller controls the concentration of carbon dioxide in the culture space to be 2-7%, preferably 5%, the controller controls the concentration of oxygen in the culture space to be 8-10%, inoculating mononuclear cells into the bioreactor from day 10 to day 22, where the controller controls the concentration of oxygen in the culture space to be 12-18%, preferably 15%, the controller controls the concentration of carbon dioxide in the culture space to be 2-7%; and
   (2) immune cell, obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises inoculating mononuclear cells into a first culture medium comprising 10% autologous plasma, 1000 IU/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 0.01 KE/ml T-cell expansion culture medium on the first day of culturing, culturing in a bioreactor for 2-9 days, inoculating mononuclear cells into a second culture medium comprising 10% autologous plasma, 1000 IU/ml interleukin (IL)-2 T-cell expansion medium or IL-2 containing 1000IU/ml T-cell expansion culture medium and inoculating mononuclear cells into a third culture medium comprising SuperCulture L500 (RTM: Lymphocyte serum-free medium) culture medium containing 1000 IU/ml IL-2, and culturing for 10-22 days. The method also comprises inoculating mononuclear cells into the bioreactor, incubating for 13-15 days to obtain immune cells, recovering 14 days cultured mononuclear cells, and re-suspending in physiological saline containing 5% human serum albumin on the 15th day of culturing to obtain immune cells. The separation of peripheral blood mononuclear cells comprises (i) first centrifugal treatment of quickly detected peripheral blood at 2500 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes, (ii) performing second centrifugation to the obtained upper plasma at 3500 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes to obtain supernatant plasma and blood cell pellets, (iii) inactivating the supernatant plasma, performing third centrifugation treatment at 3500 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes to remove complement, where the inactivation treatment is carried out at 56 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, (iv) re-suspending the blood cell pellet into 50% saline and adding hydroxyethyl starch (1/3 of the total volume) to the re-suspension, (v) re-suspending the re-suspension to sediment red blood cells, (vi) subjecting the resuspension to fourth centrifugation at 1800 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes for white blood cell sedimentation, (vii) precipitating the white blood cells using 50% physiological saline to obtain a re-suspension, (viii) adding equal volume of human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation fluid, centrifuging the resulting mixture at 2000 revolutions/minute for 25 minutes to obtain middle cloud layer of white blood cells, and (ix) washing the middle cloud white blood cells with physiological saline, which are the mononuclear cells, preferably the human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation fluid includes dextran and meglumine diatrizoate. Preferred Components: The T-cell expansion culture medium is OpTmizer CTS (RTM: Growth and expansion of human T lymphocytes) T-cell exhibiting serum-free medium (SFM) culture medium. The IL-2 is from Pepro Tech Company. The mononuclear cells are derived from peripheral blood.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); T01 (Digital Computers)

PN GB2600013-A; WO2022079407-A1
TI Synthesizing hydrocarbons involves making synthesis gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide from feedstock in synthesis gas generation unit, removing carbon dioxide, synthesizing mixture of hydrocarbons from purified gas.
AU TICEHURST P R
   MCKENNA M J
   COE A J
   CLAXTON H A
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
GA 2022524554
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing hydrocarbons involves making synthesis gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide from feedstock in synthesis gas generation unit; removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas in carbon dioxide removal unit to produce carbon dioxide stream and purified synthesis gas comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide; synthesizing mixture of hydrocarbons from purified synthesis gas in Fischer-Tropsch (FT) hydrocarbon synthesis unit, with co-production of FT water stream, where at least portion of FT water stream is fed to electrolysis unit to provide oxygen stream, which is fed to synthesis gas generation unit, and hydrogen stream, at least portion of carbon dioxide stream recovered from carbon dioxide removal unit and portion of hydrogen stream produced by electrolysis unit are fed to reverse water-gas shift unit to produce carbon monoxide stream, and portion of carbon monoxide stream from reverse water-gas shift unit is fed to Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit.
   USE - Method for synthesizing hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for synthesizing hydrocarbons enables to avoid need to carbon capture and storage; maximizes production of liquid fuels from feed stock and helps to reduce carbon dioxide emissions; improves efficiencies; eliminates or reduces size of air separation unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for performing process, which comprises synthesis gas generation unit for making synthesis gas; carbon dioxide removal unit coupled to synthesis gas generation unit for removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas to produce carbon dioxide stream and purified synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit coupled to carbon dioxide removal unit for synthesizing mixture of hydrocarbons from purified synthesis gas, with co- production of FT water stream, where electrolysis unit which is coupled to Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit, configured to fed with at least portion of FT water to provide oxygen stream, which is configured to fed to synthesis gas generation unit, and hydrogen stream, reverse water-gas shift unit which is coupled to carbon dioxide removal unit, and electrolysis unit and configured to fed with at least portion of carbon dioxide stream from carbon dioxide removal unit and portion of hydrogen stream produced by electrolysis unit, to produce carbon monoxide stream, and Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit which is coupled to reverse water-gas shift unit to receive at least portion of carbon monoxide stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowsheet of methods for synthesizing hydrocarbons by using system.
   Line (10)
   Electrolysis unit (16)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (20)
   Water gas shift unit (24)
   Line (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feedstock comprises natural gas, associated gas, coal, biomass or municipal solid waste or equivalent contains non- biogenic carbon. The feedstock is natural gas and synthesis gas generation unit comprises catalytic partial oxidation unit, non- catalytic partial oxidation unit or autothermal reformer. The feedstock is coal, biomass or municipal solid waste or equivalent contains non-biogenic carbon and synthesis gas generation unit comprises gasifier, with one or more downstream processing units selected from partial oxidation unit, tar reforming unit and purification reactors contains purification material. The carbon dioxide removal unit comprises physical wash system or reactive wash system. The Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit comprises tubular reactor in which catalyst carriers contains Fischer-Tropsch catalyst are arranged in one or more tubes cooled by cooling medium. The hydrotreating unit comprises one or more vessels contains catalyst selected from hydroisomerization catalyst, hydrogenation catalyst, hydrodeoxygenation catalyst, and hydrocracking catalyst. The portion of hydrogen stream from electrolysis unit is fed to hydrotreating unit. The water stream produced by reverse water gas shift unit is fed to electrolysis unit. The portion of hydrogen stream from electrolysis unit is fed to Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit. The oxygen stream provided by electrolysis unit is provided to combust portion of feed gas comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen fed to reverse water-gas shift unit to raise temperature of feed gas. The water formed in reverse water-gas shift unit is fed to electrolysis unit. The tail gas comprises one or more of methane, ethane, propane, butane and C5-C10 hydrocarbons, is recovered from Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit, and is fed to synthesis gas generation unit. The tail gas comprises one or more of methane, ethane, propane, butane and C5-C10 hydrocarbons, is recovered from Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit, and is subjected to separate reforming step to form reformed tail gas contains hydrogen, which is fed to Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit and reverse water-gas shift unit. The hydrocarbon products recovered from hydrotreating unit are fed to separation unit to recover C1-C4 gases, naphtha fraction, at least one kerosene and gas oil fraction and heavy fraction. The hydrotreating unit coupled to Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit for upgrading mixture of hydrocarbons to produce hydrocarbon products.Preferred Process: The process for synthesizing hydrocarbons involves upgrading mixture of hydrocarbons synthesized in Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis unit in hydrotreating unit to produce hydrocarbon products.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN111732560-A
TI Preparing epoxides for carbon dioxide recovery by hydrogen chloride and chlorohydrin method comprises e.g. passing chlorine gas, water and sodium bicarbonate from the electrolysis alkali production unit to hypochlorous acid preparation unit.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 202099268S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing epoxides for carbon dioxide recovery by hydrogen chloride and chlorohydrin method comprises (i) passing chlorine gas, water and sodium bicarbonate from the electrolysis alkali production unit to hypochlorous acid preparation unit, reacting water and chlorine to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride and reacting sodium bicarbonate and hydrogen chloride to form sodium chloride and carbon dioxide, (ii) passing carbon dioxide, sodium carbonate, water or aqueous solution of carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate from the hypochlorous acid preparation unit to the carbon dioxide recovery unit, reacting carbon dioxide, water, sodium carbonate or carbon dioxide, sodium carbonate aqueous solution to generating sodium bicarbonate and entering to the hypochlorous acid production unit or produces commercial sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing epoxides for carbon dioxide recovery by hydrogen chloride and chlorohydrin method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has no waste water, waste residue and waste gas in the production process; has many target products, economic and environmental benefits; which can replaces the traditional chlorohydrin epoxide production technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing epoxides for carbon dioxide recovery by hydrogen chloride and chlorohydrin method comprises (i) passing chlorine gas, water and sodium bicarbonate from the electrolysis alkali production unit to hypochlorous acid preparation unit, reacting water and chlorine to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride and reacting sodium bicarbonate and hydrogen chloride to form sodium chloride and carbon dioxide, (ii) passing carbon dioxide, sodium carbonate, water or aqueous solution of carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate from the hypochlorous acid preparation unit to the carbon dioxide recovery unit, reacting carbon dioxide, water, sodium carbonate or carbon dioxide, sodium carbonate aqueous solution to generating sodium bicarbonate and entering to the hypochlorous acid production unit or produces commercial sodium bicarbonate, (iii) passing mixture of hypochlorous acid and sodium chloride from hypochlorous acid preparation unit and raw materials to be chlorohydrin to the chlorohydrin reaction unit, reacting the raw material to be chlorohydrin with hypochlorous acid to generate chlorohydrin, (d) entering the mixture of chlorohydrin and sodium chloride from chlorohydrin reaction unit and sodium hydroxide to the saponification reaction unit, reacting chlorohydrin with sodium hydroxide to produce epoxide and sodium chloride, separating the target product epoxide and sodium chloride solution of saponification liquid and electrolyzing the sodium chloride solution to the alkali production device, where the method includes a hypochlorous acid preparation unit, a carbon dioxide recovery unit, a chlorohydrin reaction unit, a saponification reaction unit. The aforementioned unit is used in conjunction with an electrolytic alkali production device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide from the hypochlorous acid preparation unit or the sodium hydroxide lye from the electrolysis alkali production unit enters the carbon dioxide recovery unit, where sodium hydroxide reacts with carbon dioxide to produce sodium carbonate, sodium carbonate further reacts with carbon dioxide to generate sodium bicarbonate, and the generated sodium bicarbonate enters the hypochlorous acid preparation unit or prepares commercial sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Components: The chlorohydrination raw material is ethylene, propylene, chloroethylene, chloropropene and the corresponding epoxide is epoxy ethane, propylene oxide, epoxy chloroethane, epoxy chloropropane.

PN KR2395977-B1
TI Eco-friendly block composition comprises cement, carbon dioxide capture material and nano-silica, which is collected by filtering gas generated in production process of silicon metal, and reacting carbon dioxide collector with gas.
AU BYEONG-YONG L
   CHO C
   YUM J H
AE NATURE & ENVIRONMENT CO LTD (NATU-Non-standard)
   HANLIMECO TECH CO LTD (HANL-Non-standard)
GA 2022652196
AB    NOVELTY - An environmentally-friendly block composition comprises cement, a carbon dioxide trap and nanosilica. The nanosilica is particles collected by filtering a gas generated in a production process of silicon metal. The nanosilica is coated on the surface of the carbon dioxide trap by reacting the carbon dioxide trap with the gas generated in the production process of silicon metal, and is added as a carbon dioxide trap surface-modified with nanosilica.
   USE - Composition for manufacturing environmentally-friendly block (claimed) e.g. concrete block used for construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides carbon dioxide in cement without deterioration of physical properties such as compressive strength and workability, and increases amount of trap replacement, and improves permanent storage of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of environmentally-friendly block.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide trap is obtained by mixing and pulverizing a carbon dioxide trap raw material and adipic acid.

TI Extracting vanadium by blowing carbon dioxide at            bottom of rotary furnace by adding vanadium-containing            molten iron into converter, blowing carbon dioxide,            blowing mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide, and            adding coolant in oxygen lance.
AU LI L
   WU G
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 2021347343
AB    NOVELTY - A vanadium is extracted by adding                vanadium-containing molten iron into converter,                blowing carbon dioxide at bottom of converter,                blowing mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide by                oxygen lance, adding coolant in 2 minutes of oxygen                lance, and blowing to end point.
   USE - Method for extracting vanadium by blowing                carbon dioxide at bottom of rotary furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recycles the carbon dioxide gas                discharged by the steel production; saves energy                and reduces emission; uses carbon dioxide to                replace part of oxygen or nitrogen to carry out                converter vanadium extraction; inhibits molten pool                heating; is good for extracting vanadium and                protecting carbon; and reduces the residual                vanadium content.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the partitions at the bottom of the                converter.

PT P
PN CN112129889-A
TI Calculation of carbon dioxide release rate of dead plant involves collecting and culturing plant vertical blight, collecting gas sample, determining carbon dioxide of collected sample, dry dead tissue weighing and calculating release rate.
AU ZHANG X
   JIANG W
   MAO R
   WANG G
   LIU J
AE UNIV NANJING NORMAL (UNNO-C)
GA 202101540J
AB    NOVELTY - Calculation of carbon dioxide release rate of dead plant involves (i) collecting and culturing plant vertical blight and placing plants collected in the wild into a culture bottle and sealing, collecting (ii) gas sample by using syringe to collect gas samples in the culture bottle during the closed culture period, determining (iii) carbon dioxide of the collected gas sample by analyzing carbon dioxide concentration of the gas sample through the detector, and dry dead tissue weighing (iv) after taking out and packaging the plant vertical cake tissue in the culture bottle, placing in the oven to dry to constant weight, and calculating the carbon dioxide release rate.
   USE - Calculation of carbon dioxide release rate of dead plant in field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculation of carbon dioxide release rate of dead plant with high accuracy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The carbon dioxide gas release rate (YT) is calculated using slope (dc/dt) of linear regression of carbon dioxide concentration of collected gas with time, dry weight of dead tissue (m), volume of the culture flask (V), atmospheric pressure (P), absolute temperature during the sampling period (T), molar volume of gas in the standard state (V0), air pressure in the standard state (P0), and absolute temperature under standard conditions (T0), and satisfy the relation: YT = (dc/dt)x (12x Vx Px T0)/(mx V0x P0x T) (I). Preferred Composition: The method is performed by using 2-5 g dead plants.

AB    NOVELTY - Method of fixing carbon dioxide involves                contacting a gas comprising carbon dioxide with a                mixed solution comprising sodium hydroxide having a                concentration of 0.2 N or less and at least one of                a chloride of a group-II element and chloride of a                divalent metal element.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:

PN CN109028628-A; CN109028628-B
TI Carbon dioxide heat pump device, has carbon dioxide heat pump device comprising ice-melting circulation pipeline, where carbon dioxide heat pump device is returned to compressor through gas-liquid separator to form ice-melting pipeline.
AU PAN Z
   WANG X
   CHEN S
AE ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTRO-MECHANICAL TECHNOL (DUNN-C)
   ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTRO-MECHANICAL TECHNO (DUNN-C)
GA 2018A4994P
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide heat pump device comprising an ice-melting circulation pipeline. A compressor is connected to the ice-melting circulation pipeline, a filter, an electro-magnetic valve, an evaporator and a gas-liquid separator. The evaporator is provided with a lower part of an independent pipeline. The carbon dioxide heat pump device is returned to the compressor through a gas-liquid separator to form the ice-melting circulation pipeline. A throttle device is connected with an outlet pipe of the evaporator when the carbon dioxide heat pump device operates low ambient temperatures.
   USE - Carbon dioxide heat pump device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has system stable operation and low ambient temperature operation, and realizes rapid melting ice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide heat pump device.

PN WO2018088783-A1; KR2018052504-A; KR1913099-B1; US2019270047-A1; US11406930-B2
TI Continuous removal of carbon dioxide used in e.g.            industrial plant, involves reacting gas containing            carbon dioxide with solution containing amine-based            compound and acidic calcium compound and removing            obtained calcium carbonate.
AU JEONG S
   YOUN M H
   PARK K
   KANG S
   LEE W H
   KIM H
   KIM Y
   KANG J M
   MURNANDARI A
   JEONG S K
   PARK K T
   KANG S P
   KIM H J
   KIM Y E
   LEE W
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
   KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
   KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2018386886
AB    NOVELTY - Method for continuous removal of carbon                dioxide involves (a) preparing an aqueous solution                containing an amine-based compound and an acidic                calcium compound, (b) contacting a gas containing                carbon dioxide to be treated with the aqueous                solution to prepare a calcium carbonate                precipitate, and (c) recovering the calcium                carbonate and then adding a basic calcium compound                to the residual aqueous solution. The steps (b) and                step (c) are repeatedly performed after performing                step (c).
   USE - Method for continuous removal of carbon                dioxide using apparatus in industrial plant and                internal combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method requires low energy and efficiently                removes carbon dioxide. The method is capable of                mineralizing and removing carbon dioxide at a fast                rate without a separate time for induction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                continuous removal apparatus of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic calcium                  compound comprises a chlorine atom. The basic                  calcium compound comprises an oxygen atom or a                  hydroxyl group. The gas is an exhaust gas stream.                  Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide in step                  (b) is dissolved in the aqueous solution in an                  amount of 0.7 mol or more per 1 mol amine-based                  compound. Preferred Property: The calcium                  carbonate recovered in step (c) has an average                  crystal size of 10-50 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine-based                  compound is one or more chosen from primary                  amine, a secondary amine, a tertiary amine, and a                  sterically hindered amine compound.

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Equipment for recovering electrical energy, has container for carbon dioxide that is incorporated into system, and turbine that is driven by carbon dioxide to generate energy.
AU 
AE BUCHMANN A (BUCH-Individual)
GA 201802788J
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment has a container for carbon dioxide can be incorporated into the system. A turbine (12) is driven by carbon dioxide to generate energy. The turbine is designed only for pressure to work. The carbon dioxide is supplied in liquid form to an evaporator (8) and is heated. The gaseous carbon dioxide is fed to steam inlet of turbine. AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate electrolyte                containing an abb structure, comprises 10-95 wt.%                carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate containing abb                structure and 5-90 wt.% lithium salt.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                electrolyte containing abb structure is useful in                lithium-ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                electrolyte: is prepared by selectively regulating                the composition of polycarbonate by changing the                conditions of the copolymerization reaction, thus                achieves the regulation of polymer electrolyte                properties; has wide electrochemical window and                high lithium-ion conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   Preparing carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                electrolyte containing abb structure, comprising                mixing and dissolving carbon dioxide-based                polycarbonate containing abb structure with an                organic solvent to obtain a uniform polycarbonate                solution; adding lithium salt to the polycarbonate                solution, and optionally adding an auxiliary agent,                continuously stirring and mixing to obtain mixed                solution; pouring the the above mixed solution into                a flat mold, vacuum drying to obtain final                product;
   Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate electrolyte                containing multiple abb structures, comprising                10-95 wt.% carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                containing multiple abb structures and 5-90 wt.%                lithium salts; the carbon dioxide-based                polycarbonate containing multiple abb structures is                prepared by copolymerization of carbon dioxide and                at least two alkylene oxides to obtain final                product, specifically carrying out copolymerization                of carbon dioxide and n kinds of different alkylene                oxides of (I) under the action of bifunctional                organoboron catalyst using carbon dioxide as                structural unit a, and multiple alkylene oxides are                respectively ring-opened as structural units b-b                inserted into the polymer chain and performing                copolymerization to form carbon dioxide-based                polycarbonate containing various abb structures;                the product include carbonate unit, abb structural                unit and ether unit, carbonate unit includes ab-ab                units formed by the ring-opening copolymerization                of carbon dioxide and/or an alkylene oxide; the abb                unit includes abb-abb-abb units formed by the                ring-opening copolymerization of carbon dioxide                and/or two alkylene oxides; the ether unit includes                bbb-bbb-bbb units formed by ring-opening                self-polymerization of alkylene oxide. The number                of repeating units of abb structural units abb-abb                units is 0-100000, but cannot be 0 at the same                time; the number of repeating units of each ether                unit bbb- bbb-bbb is an integer from 0-100000. b-b                = units after ring-opening of n kinds of alkylene                oxide raw materials; n = raw material species of                alkylene oxide participating in the                copolymerization reaction, n is an integer from 2                to 5; p and q = 1 to n; r, t, and s = 1 to n; ab-ab                = x-x; x-x =                0-100000;1n1n11pqnn111rtsnnn11nn111rtsnnn1n1n1n1n

PN CN110331972-A
TI Mixed fracturing process of liquid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide foam system in low pressure tight gas reservoir comprises e.g. pre-pad stage including injecting e.g. carrying proppant, sand-carrying stage, and displacing liquid stage.
AU CHEN T
   NIU Z
   MA Y
   LIU J
   SU W
   LIU Y
   WANG H
   LI N
AE CNPC BOHAI DRILLING ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019867988
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed-fracturing process for liquid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide foam systems in a low-pressure tight gas reservoir comprises e.g. (a) pre-pad stage: using the pure liquid carbon dioxide and the pure liquid carbon dioxide carrying a small amount of proppant, alternately injecting into the wellbore fracturing stratum seam, and the pure liquid carbon dioxide reaches the supercritical state under the action of temperature and pressure after entering the formation; (b) sand-carrying stage: injecting the cross-linking low-concentration low residue thickener solution carrying the proppant and the liquid carbon dioxide into the wellbore through three-way mixing into pit-shaft, forming foam fracturing fluid due to variations in borehole temperature, and using high-viscosity gas-liquid two-phase under the condition of adding sand, and fracturing, and (c) displacing liquid stage: after finishing adding, base liquid for the foam in the well fracturing fluid displacement into the formation.
   USE - The method is useful for mixed fracturing process of liquid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide foam system in low pressure tight gas reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the extremely strong fluidity and penetrating power of the supercritical carbon dioxide to make a complex joint, and then utilizes the high-viscosity property of the carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid under the gas-liquid two-phase condition to carry out sand fracturing, thus ensures the construction scale, and reduces the amount of liquid entering the ground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mixed-fracturing process for liquid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide foam systems in a low-pressure tight gas reservoir comprises (a) pre-pad stage: using the pure liquid carbon dioxide and the pure liquid carbon dioxide carrying a small amount of proppant, alternately injecting into the wellbore fracturing stratum seam, and the pure liquid carbon dioxide reaches the supercritical state under the action of temperature and pressure after entering the formation; (b) sand-carrying stage: injecting the cross-linking low-concentration low residue thickener solution carrying the proppant and the liquid carbon dioxide into the wellbore through the three-way mixing, into the pit-shaft, forming foam fracturing fluid due to variations in borehole temperature, and using high-viscosity gas-liquid two-phase under the condition of adding sand, and fracturing, and (c) displacing liquid stage: after finishing adding, base liquid for the foam in the well fracturing fluid displacement into the formation, fracturing construction after finishing, closing the pressure drop for 2 hours, when the pressure is stable, starting spraying liquid discharging; and does not randomly shut the well until there is no pressure at the wellhead.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The base liquid comprises 0.25% low residue thickener, 0.5% foaming agent, 0.2% pH adjuster and water, and the above percentage is mass percentage based on water; low residue thickener is composed of N,N-dimethylacrylamide, terminally carboxylated aliphatic polylactic acid and cetyltrimethyl quaternary ammonium salt at a mass ratio of 8:5:1, using azobisisobutyryldimethyl dimethyl ester as initiator , and the product obtained at a reaction temperature of 65 degrees C and a reaction time of 3 hours; the pH adjusting agent is sodium citrate; the foaming agent is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate; the crosslinked low concentration low residue thickener solution is obtained by mixing a base liquid with 0.025% crosslinking agent, and the crosslinking agent the percentage content is mass percentage content of base liquid as reference; where the crosslinking agent is synthesized by uniformly mixing water, triethanolamine, phthalic acid, ferric chloride and aluminum chloride according to a mass ratio of 70:5:5:3:2, and heating the mixture to a temperature of 65 degrees C, further, adding 15 pts. wt. zirconium oxynitrate, carrying out the reaction for 6-7 hours, and finally cooling the product obtained by the reaction to room temperature. In the method, before the fracturing stage further comprises through washing well, test stage: reverse to outlet water quality, full wellbore casing 25 MPa, 30 minutes pressure drop less than 0.5MPa is qualified, and blowout preventer installation, stable test 35MPa, 10min is qualified, leakage-free and preparing oil pipe blowout preventing gate. The mixed fracturing well fracturing technology is suitable for KQ65-70. The unblocking step construction limiting is 20 MPa, furthest improve the injection capacity of the liquid carbon dioxide under the condition of ensuring no overpressure in the construction process. The fluid phase carbon dioxide injection amount along with the support agent ratio of increase and decrease, and limiting is 55 MPa laden stage for construction. After fracturing spraying liquid discharging system is as follows: spraying oil nozzle control, displacement control at 200-300 l/minute; when the wellhead pressure is below 15 MPa, the 8 mm nozzle discharging; when 10 MPa the wellhead pressure is lower than, by 10 mm nozzle discharging; and when the 1 MPa wellhead pressure below a liquid discharging opening. The fluid phase foam fracturing liquid pH is 5.5-6.5. Preferred Components: The proppant is at least one of high-strength ceramsite particles of 40-70 meshes and 30-50 meshes particle size.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PT P
PN US2022290315-A1; CN115125545-A; JP2022139003-A
TI Carbon dioxide treatment device comprises recovery            device that recovers carbon dioxide, electrochemical            reaction device that electrochemically reduces carbon            dioxide recovered, and homologation reaction device            that increases number of carbons by multimerizing            ethylene generated when carbon dioxide.
AU OIKAWA H
   HIROSHI O
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022C0170Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide treatment device comprises a                recovery device (1) that recovers carbon dioxide,                an electrochemical reaction device that                electrochemically reduces carbon dioxide recovered                by the recovery device, and a homologation reaction                device that increases the number of carbons by                multimerizing ethylene generated when carbon                dioxide is reduced in the electrochemical reaction                device (2), where the recovery device includes an                absorption unit that brings carbon dioxide gas into                contact with an absorption liquid and absorbs                carbon dioxide into the absorption liquid and a                discharge unit that heats the absorption liquid                that has absorbed carbon dioxide and discharges                carbon dioxide gas, where the discharge unit heats                the absorption liquid using heat generated in a                multimerization reaction of the homologation                reaction device.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment device                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high energy efficiency for                recovering and reducing carbon dioxide. It has                excellent separation performance.
