GA 2022A16373

PN CN114150164-A
TI Method for separating lithium-ion from mixed solution used in gas separation application, involves reacting carbon dioxide and alkaline solution, separating lithium carbonate precipitate, reacting with e.g. alcohol, washing, and converting lithium carbonate into lithium chloride and carbonate.
AU WANG D
   WANG K
   DING H
   MAO L
AE SHANGHAI YILAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU ZHONGKE BAIAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022446498
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating lithium ion from mixed solution, involves (1) introducing sufficient carbon dioxide into alkaline solution containing lithium ions, and controlling reaction pressure of 0.1-7 MPa, controlling temperature at 30-320degreesC, pH greater than 8.5, and stirring, where lithium ion is converted into lithium carbonate precipitate, (2) separating lithium carbonate precipitate, adding alcohol, ketone and ester mixture to separated clear liquid, where adding amount is 0.1-50 vol% of content of solution, continuously introducing carbon dioxide, maintaining reaction pressure of 0.1-7 MPa, controlling temperature of 30-320degreesC, precipitating and separating residual sparingly soluble lithium carbonate in clear liquid, and (3) washing lithium carbonate of first-level and multi-level separated precipitate, adding hydrochloric acid, converting lithium carbonate into lithium chloride and carbonate, where lithium chloride utilized after drying is utilized as pure product.
   USE - Method for separating lithium ion from mixed solution used in gas separation application e.g. low silicon aluminum X type zeolite molecular sieve of lithium exchange, and as nitrogen and oxygen separating agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate lithium-ion from mixed solution, which has high yield, purity, simple operation, and low production cost, and ensures high recovery rate of lithium-ion concentration in low condition, and continuous processing efficiency, and high processing efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram illustrating the method for separating lithium ion from mixed solution. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (3), the separation method involves separating lithium chloride solution containing organic solvent into rectifying tower, separating organic solvent, and recycling to multi-level separation. The step (3) involves finally separating lithium carbonate recycled to level and multi-level separation for precipitating lithium ion. For one-stage and multi-stage separation, carbonate is not necessary and unique, and is replaced by water-soluble basic carbonate such as sodium carbonate. The precipitation separation stage is according to lithium ion solubility in initial solution and final recovery of lithium depth needs to increase precipitation reaction level.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (1), the solution of calcium ion and magnesium ion forms carbonate precipitation of metal ion concentration within 100 microg/ml. The step (2) involves utilizing at least one or more of alcohol, ketone and ester, where the alcohol includes at least one or more of methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol, glycerol, polyvinyl alcohol and normal propyl alcohol. The ketones include at least one or more of acetone, butanone, cyclopentanone, pentanone and propiophenone. The ester includes at least one or more of ethyl acetate, methyl acetate and butyl acetate, preferably, the step (2) involves utilizing ethanol, acetone and methyl acetate at mass ratio of 1:0.3:0.1.

GA 2022189411
AB    NOVELTY - Normal temperature carbonated beverage comprises fermented milk, food additive and carbon dioxide, where the fermented milk comprises 50-100 pts. wt. skim milk powder, 10-40 pts. wt. protein powder, 0.03-0.05 pts. wt. strain, the final acidity of the fermented milk is 4.2-4.4, the content of protein in the normal temperature carbonated beverage is 2-7 g/kg, the average particle diameter of the particles in the normal temperature carbonated beverage is less than 1 microm.
   USE - Used as normal temperature carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage: has protein content of 2-7 g/kg, and has good system stability in 6 months normal temperature shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the normal temperature carbonated beverage, comprising adding water in 4-6 times of the raw material weight in 50-100 pts. wt. degreasing milk powder and 10-40 pts. wt. protein powder, after the first homogenizing and sterilizing for the first time, cooling to the fermentation temperature configured to recover milk, adding 0.03-0.05 pts. wt. strain in reconstituted milk, fermenting, fermenting until the pH value reaches 4.2-4.4, demulsifying and cooling, homogenizing for the second time to obtain fermented milk, mixing the food additive with the fermented milk and then preparing into material liquid, homogenizing the material liquid for three times and sterilizing for the second time, making the average grain diameter of the particles in the material liquid less than 1 microm, filling the carbon dioxide into the material liquid to obtain the finished product; the content of the protein in the finished product is 2-7 g/kg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mass of the protein in the skim milk powder is greater than 32%, and the mass of the protein in the protein powder is greater than 70%, the mass ratio of the degreased milk powder to the protein in the protein powder is 1-2:1, the average particle diameter of the particles in the normal temperature carbonated beverage is less than 0.6 microm, and the average particle diameter of the particles in the fermented milk is less than 15 microm. The food additive comprises 50-90 pts. wt. sweetening agent, 0.3-2 pts. wt. acidity regulator, 1-3 pts. wt. protein stabilizer, 1-10 pts. wt. fruit juice, 60-180 pts. wt. fermented milk and is 6-8 pts. wt. carbon dioxide. The sweetening agent comprises xylitol, erythritol, stevioside, glucosyl stevioside, mogroside, sucralose, acesulfame potassium, the acidity regulator comprises citric acid, lactic acid, sodium citrate, sodium tripolyphosphate, sodium bicarbonate, the protein stabilizing agent is soluble soybean polysaccharide and juice is clear concentrated juice. The strain comprises Lactobacillus bulgaricus, Streptococcus thermophilus, Lactococcus lactis subsp lactis, Lactococcus lactis subsp lactis, Lactococcus lactis diacetyl, Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus plantarum, and/or Lactobacillus helveticus. The strain comprises Lactobacillus bulgaricus, Streptococcus thermophilus and Bifidobacterium at the mass ratio of 1:4:2.Preferred Method: The temperature of the first time of homogenizing is 62-65degrees Celsius under 160-180 bar, the pressure of the first time of homogenizing is the temperature of the second homogenizing is 34-36degrees Celsius under 120-140 bar, the pressure of the second homogenizing is the temperature of the third homogenizing is 62-65degrees Celsius, and pressure of the third homogenizing is 200-220 bar. The first sterilization is carried out by pasteurization and the second sterilization is carried out by high temperature sterilization at 121plus minus2degrees Celsius for 4 seconds. In the aeration and fusion step, the feed liquid is first cooled to 0-4degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide is pressurized to 3-3.5 bar, the feed liquid and carbon dioxide are mixed through a mixing pipeline of 30-50 cm, and after mixing, pass through for 15-30 minutes and remaining into the finished product. The finished product is aseptically filled, the filling temperature during filling is 4-6degrees Celsius, the filling speed is 20-30 ml/s, and the pressure is released at 10-15 seconds.

PN KR2022005662-A; KR2369336-B1
TI Device used for reducing exhaust pollutants, consists of exhaust pipe for discharging exhaust gas generated from combustion engine that generates power by burning liquefied natural gas, methane oxidation catalyst reactor including methane oxidation catalyst and selective catalytic reduction reactor.
AU LEE S J
AE SAMSUNG HEAVY IND CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 2022118523
AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust pollutant reduction device consists of an exhaust pipe for discharging exhaust gas generated from a combustion engine that generates power by burning liquefied natural gas, a methane oxidation catalyst reactor installed in the exhaust pipe to oxidize unburned methane contained in the exhaust gas to carbon dioxide, including a methane oxidation catalyst, and a selective catalytic reduction reactor installed in the exhaust pipe to reduce nitrogen oxides contained in the exhaust gas.
   USE - Device used for reducing exhaust pollutants such as unburned methane and nitrogen oxides contained in exhaust gas generated from combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The device includes the methane oxidation catalyst reactor in the exhaust pipe, so that the unburned methane contained in the exhaust gas can be oxidized to carbon dioxide. As unburned methane is oxidized to carbon dioxide, it can minimize the emission of methane, greenhouse gas, into the atmosphere, thereby reducing the aggravation of global warming. As the methane oxidation catalytic reactor is installed inside the selective catalytic reduction reactor, the space inside the ship can be utilized more efficiently, and the accompanying equipment such as the burner unit and air injection unit can be shared, thereby reducing costs. As the methane oxidation catalytic reactor is arranged close to the combustion engine, some of the waste heat generated in the combustion engine can be utilized for the catalytic reaction, thereby reducing costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The methane oxidation catalytic reactor includes a porous support. The selective catalytic reduction reactor includes a catalyst layer. The porous support is installed in front end of the catalyst layer. The device further consists of an air injection unit for desorbing impurities by injecting compressed air into the methane oxidation catalytic reactor and the selective catalytic reduction reactor, respectively, a burner unit that receives the exhaust gas from the exhaust pipe at the rear end of the selective catalytic reduction reactor, heats, and supplies gas to the exhaust pipe at the front end of the methane oxidation catalyst reactor, and urea water decomposition unit installed at the rear end of the burner unit to generate ammonia by heating aqueous urea with the heat of the exhaust gas supplied from the burner unit, and supplying the ammonia to the front end of the methane oxidation catalyst reactor. The heat generated by heating the exhaust gas in the burner unit is used to regenerate the catalyst of the methane oxidation catalytic reactor or the selective catalytic reduction reactor, or to react in the methane oxidation catalytic reactor or the selective catalytic reduction reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous support is a ceramic structure in which a methane oxidation catalyst made of one or more metals chosen from platinum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, and cobalt is supported on a support made of one or more oxides chosen from magnesium, aluminum, silica, and zirconia.

   ADVANTAGE - The binding agent has good thermal stability, high mechanical strength, low production cost; ensures thermal explosion crushing which is not easy to occur during gasification; and can meet the requirements of coal gasification for granular raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) Coal tar slag-humic acid salt composite gasification coal forming raw material comprises 100 pts. wt. slaked lime, 1-10 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 3-5 pts. wt. composite binder, where the coal tar residue is 1-15 5 pts. wt., preferably 8-10 5 pts. wt. humic acid salt, where consumption of the above raw materials is the consumption after deducting the water content; and
   (2) Producing coal tar slag-humic acid salt composite gasification moulded coal raw material comprising (i) mixing coal powder, coal tar residue raw material, slaked lime, and humic acid salt and reacting to obtain a mixture to be compressed, where the mixture is retorted for 24 hours; (ii)extruding the retorted material to obtain a molding mixture; (iii) reacting forming mixture with carbon dioxide for carbonization to obtain a coal tar residue-humate compound gasification coal forming raw material product and reacting forming mixture with carbon dioxide for carbonization, and the reaction comprises carbon dioxide reacting with calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate and water and alkyl acetate reacting with sodium hydroxide to obtain alkyl sodium acetate and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The humic acid salt is prepared by humic acid, alkali and water. The humic acid salt is prepared by 100 pts. wt. humic acid, 0-35 pts. wt. ammonia and 5-25 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, where the amount of the above raw materials is the amount after deducting the water, and the mass percentage of the humic acid salt solution is greater than or equal to 12%, the pH range is 11-13.5, the mass fraction of sodium hydroxide solution is 25-45%, and the mass fraction of ammonia water is 10-25 %.Preferred Method: The R-COOH represents humic acid during the preparation process of the humic acid salt and when sodium hydroxide is added, the reaction comprises acetic acid reacting with sodium hydroxide to produce alkyl sodium acetate and water. The production method further comprises pretreatment of raw coal powder, crushing raw coal powder and sieving to make the particle size less than or equal to 2mm. The production method further comprises crushing and sieving the quicklime (80-100 mesh), adding water or dilute ammonia, mixing and sintering to obtain slaked lime, the slaked lime is produced during the preparation process and reaction comprises re acting calcium oxide and water to obtain calcium hydroxide and reacting calcium oxide and carbon dioxide to obtain calcium carbonate. The production method further comprises mixing humic acid and alkali to obtain humic acid salt, where alkali includes sodium hydroxide and/or ammonia. In the step (iii), the method comprises performing a drying treatment to make the moisture content of the product meet the requirements of coal tar residue gasification after the carbonization of the molding mixture.

PN IN202141038798-A
TI Method for quantification of CO2 in concrete cured with carbon dioxide, involves detecting presence of absorbed CO2 in concrete sample using one CO2 detection test and pH level of concrete samples is identified using indicator test.
AU KUMAR S T
   SOWJANYA L
   BALAJI K
   RAMESH B
AE KUMAR S T (KUMA-Individual)
   SOWJANYA L (SOWJ-Individual)
   BALAJI K (BALA-Individual)
   RAMESH B (RAME-Individual)
GA 2021B1115K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting the presence of absorbed carbon dioxide (CO2) in a concrete sample using one CO2 detection test (101). The pH level of the concrete samples is identified using an indicator test (102). The obtained pH levels are utilized to estimate the amount of CO2 absorbed in the concrete sample (103). The CO2 detection test is provided with either lime water test or liquid ammonia test. The lime water test is used to utilize lime water which is colorless that turns to milky white when it reacts with CO2 cured concrete. The liquid ammonia test is used to utilize liquid ammonia that forms a crystalline white precipitate when reacts with CO2 gas.
   USE - Method for quantification of CO2 in concrete cured with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The bromothymol blue indicator test allows determining a change in pH values while changing its color from blue to yellow through green colors and the hydrogen carbonate indicator test utilizes hydrogen carbonate as an indicator which is red and turns to orange when it reacts with concrete cured with CO2 and turns to yellow color if more amount of CO2 is absorbed by the concrete.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the method.
   Detecting the presence of absorbed CO2 in a concrete sample using one CO2 detection test (101)
   Identifying the pH level of the concrete samples using an indicator test (102)
   Utilizing the obtained pH levels to estimate the amount of CO2 absorbed in the concrete sample (103)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tobacco flavors comprises                introducing pulverized smoke powder of 10 wt.%                Zimbabwe tobacco leaves, 10 wt.% Brazilian tobacco                leaves, Dali Nanjian Hongda tobacco leaves 80 wt.%                into the barrel of the extraction kettle, setting                certain temperature, turning on carbon dioxide                cylinder, the pressurizing system through the                high-pressure pump, and turning on cosolvent pump                at the same time to inject the cosolvent, making                mixed fluid with carbon dioxide, and maintaining                the temperature in the extraction kettle at 70                degrees C, the pressure is 25 Mpa in the                supercritical state of the mixed fluid of carbon                dioxide and cosolvent, and fully contacting with                the smoke powder for 2.5 hours, dissolving oily                components in the smoke powder in the supercritical                fluid, and collecting the fluid from the outlet of                the separation kettle to obtain tobacco                flavor.

PT P
PN CN113234012-A
TI Preparing 3-methylaminopyridine, by filling            reactor with catalyst, passing carrier gas nitrogen,            switching to hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas, heating raw            material 3-aminopyridine to complete melting, switching            gas flow to gas of hydrogen and carbon monoxide or            carbon dioxide, reacting, and cooling.
AU MU X
   WANG X
   LI H
AE QINGDAO HUAHE PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202194910U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing 3-methylaminopyridine,                involves (i) filling reactor with catalyst (I),                passing carrier gas nitrogen and programming the                temperature to 220-250 degrees C, switching to a                hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas, where the hydrogen                content is 10 vol.%, increasing the temperature to                400-450 degrees C within 200-300 minutes to reduce                the catalyst for 3-5 hours, switching back to                nitrogen, and reducing the temperature to 200-230                degrees C in a nitrogen stream, (ii) heating the                raw material 3-aminopyridine to complete melting,                passing to the reactor of step (i), switching the                gas flow in the reactor to a synthesis gas of both                hydrogen and carbon monoxide, or a mixed gas of                both hydrogen and carbon dioxide, or a mixture of                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and                controlling the feed rate so that the material                space velocity is 0.1-10000 hour-1, and (iii)                reacting, and cooling the materials discharged from                the reactor to obtain a crude product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing                3-methylaminopyridine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high activity and selectivity,                simple operation, low cost of raw materials and                catalysts, without other reaction steps, is                suitable for large-scale industrial production,                requires no precious metals and can be carried out                continuously, uses no other organic solvents or                reducing agents in the process, avoids large amount                of organic waste liquid and solid waste, and                ensures simple collection of process                products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst (I) used                  in step (i) is a heterogeneous supported                  catalyst, the active component comprises copper,                  zinc, rhenium, chromium, vanadium, manganese                  metal and/or their oxides, preferably copper,                  zinc metal and/or oxides, and the support                  comprises activated carbon, alumina, silica,                  titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, zinc oxide,                  magnesium oxide, silicon carbide, and/or zeolite.                  The catalyst support is aluminum oxide, zirconium                  oxide, zeolite, or their binary composites. The                  catalyst (II) used in step (iv) is a conventional                  hydrogenation catalyst for pyridyl hydrogenation,                  e.g. Raney nickel or a supported catalyst, and                  the active component in the supported catalyst                  comprises nickel, cobalt, ruthenium, palladium,                  copper and/or their oxides, and the support                  comprises activated carbon, alumina, silica,                  titanium dioxide, and zirconium dioxide, zinc                  oxide, magnesium oxide, silicon carbide, zeolite                  and/or their mixtures. Preferred Composition:                  Based on the total weight of the catalyst, the                  content of active components is 5-40 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves (iv) gasifying or not gasifying the                  crude product obtained in step (iii), feeding to                  the reactor loaded with catalyst (II) under the                  action of the carrier gas, feeding hydrogen to                  the reactor, and reacting at 80-250 degrees C to                  obtain 3-methylaminopiperidine. The step (iv)                  involves adding methylamine gas, where the molar                  ratio of methylamine and 3-aminopyridine as the                  raw material is 1:1-8:1, preferably 3:1.                  Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the raw                  material 3-aminopyridine is heated at 65-70                  degrees C to ensure that the raw material                  3-aminopyridine is completely melted, the molar                  ratio of synthesis gas of both hydrogen and                  carbon monoxide, or a mixed gas of both hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide, or a mixture of hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, the mixture                  of carbon and hydrogen is controlled to be                  1:2-1:30, preferably 1:4-1:8, and the feed rate                  is controlled so that the material space velocity                  is 0.1-100 hour-1, preferably 0.1-1 hour-1. The                  crude product obtained in step (iii) used in step                  (iv) does not need to be purified, and a small                  amount of impurities such as water and methanol                  is removed, and is directly used in step (iv). In                  step (iv), the hydrogen pressure in the reactor                  is maintained at 2-12 MPa, preferably 4-8 MPa,                  the feed rate is controlled so that the material                  space velocity is 0.1-100 hour-1, preferably                  0.3-1 hour-1, and the reaction temperature is                  maintained at 120-200 degrees C. Preferred                  Devices: The reactor used in step (i) is a                  reactor, a fixed bed reactor, a fluidized bed                  reactor, a simulated moving bed reactor, and a                  fixed bed reactor. The reactor in step (iv) is a                  reactor or a trickle bed reactor, preferably a                  trickle bed reactor. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of hydrogen and 3-methylaminopyridine                  in step (iv) is 40:1-15:1.

PT P
PN WO2021119020-A1; AU2020402768-A1; CA3164188-A1; KR2022112276-A; CN115052592-A; US2022296666-A1; EP4072544-A1
TI Preparing de-flavored peppercorn used in dietary            supplement, food ingredient or additive, medical food,            or nutraceutical, by contacting peppercorn with            supercritical carbon dioxide at specified temperature            and pressure.
AU HERRMANN A M
   MAHADEVAN S
   HERMANN A M
AE BRIGHTSEED INC (BRIG-Non-standard)
   BRIGHTSEED INC (BRIG-Non-standard)
   BRIGHTSEED INC (BRIG-Non-standard)
   BRIGHTSEED INC (BRIG-Non-standard)
GA 202165594M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing de-flavored peppercorn                involves (a) contacting a peppercorn with                supercritical carbon dioxide at 30-90 degrees C and                a pressure of 5-80 MPa, (b) contacting a peppercorn                with one or more oils at 50-190 degrees C and                pressure of 10-30 psi for 5-120 minutes, or (c)                pretreating a peppercorn by applying pressure to                the peppercorn, increasing temperature applied to                peppercorn, irradiating peppercorn, applying                solvent to the peppercorn, or their                combination.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing de-flavored                peppercorn which is used in dietary supplement,                food ingredient or additive, a medical food, or                nutraceutical (claimed) for improving, maintaining,                and/or promoting good metabolism, digestion and                insulin response.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes volatile oils imparting                aroma, and retains most of the naturally occurring                metabolic micronutrients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a dietary supplement, food ingredient or                additive, a medical food, or nutraceutical,                comprising a de-flavored peppercorn and a carrier,                where the de-flavored peppercorn comprises 0.01-0.6                weight/weight% piperine, 60 weight/weight% or more                carbohydrates, 1 weight/weight% or less fat, 10                weight/weight% or less ash, 10-15 weight/weight%                protein and 0.05 weight/weight% or more metabolic                micronutrients;
   (2) a method for extracting piperine from a                peppercorn involves contacting a peppercorn with                supercritical carbon dioxide, organic solvent, high                intensity light or UV radiation; and
   (3) a de-flavored peppercorn comprising                0.01-0.6 weight/weight% piperine, 5-25                weight/weight% moisture content, 0.005-0.2                weight/weight% metabolic micronutrients and                carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves                  heating peppercorn at 30-60 degrees C and                  pressure of 7.4-36 MPa. The method further                  involves contacting the peppercorn with a solvent                  chosen from water, ethanol or their combination.                  The method involves using 0-60 weight/weight%                  de-flavored peppercorn piperine with respect to                  original piperine content of the peppercorn,                  where the de-flavored peppercorn comprises 0.01-6                  weight/weight% piperine, 5-25 weight/weight%                  moisture content, and 0.005-0.2 weight/weight%                  metabolic micronutrients. The method further                  involves milling the peppercorn, contacting the                  peppercorn with supercritical carbon dioxide for                  15 minutes to 4 hours. The method further                  involves agitating the peppercorn for 5-120                  minutes. The method further involves separating                  the vegetable oil from the peppercorn, washing                  the peppercorn with an aqueous solution, removing                  residual vegetable oil, drying the peppercorn and                  milling the peppercorn. The UV radiation is                  performed at 150-350 nm. Preferred Product: The                  peppercorn has a moisture content of 5-25%. The                  de-flavored peppercorn comprises 0.01-0.6                  weight/weight% piperine, 60 weight/weight% or                  more carbohydrates, 1 weight/weight% or less fat,                  10 weight/weight% or less ash, 10-15                  weight/weight% protein, and 0.05 weight/weight%                  or more metabolic micronutrients. The de-flavored                  peppercorn comprises 0.3 weight/weight% piperine.                  The metabolic micronutrients comprise at least                  one N-trans-caffeoyl tyramine,                  N-trans-feruloyltyramine and p-coumaroyltyramine.                  The nutraceutical further comprises a                  preservative. The preservative is chosen from                  sodium benzoate, methyl paraben, propylparaben,                  sodium nitrite, sulphur dioxide, sodium sorbate,                  potassium sorbate or salts of edetate. The                  metabolic micronutrients comprise at least one                  N-trans-caffeoyl tyramine,                  N-trans-femloyltyramine and p-coumaroyltyramine.                  The nutraceutical further comprises a secondary                  agent. The secondary agent is chosen from                  phytonutrient, flavoring agent, coating layer,                  stabilizer, colorant, diluent, filler, alkaloid,                  vitamin or tyramine containing hydroxycinnamic                  acid amides. The de-flavored peppercorn is                  formulated to provide increased metabolic                  nutrients. The de-flavored peppercorn further                  comprises 5-25 weight/weight% moisture content.                  The de-flavored peppercorn comprises 0.01-0.3%                  piperine, preferably 0.3% piperine, and has 25%                  moisture content. Preferred Components: The                  peppercorn is chosen from a whole peppercorn, a                  ground peppercorn, and a crushed peppercorn. The                  carrier is chosen from at least one sugar,                  starch, cellulose, powdered tragacanth, malt,                  gelatin, talc, excipient, oil, glycol, polyol,                  ester, agar, buffering agent, alginic acid,                  isotonic saline, ethyl alcohol, phospholipid, pH                  buffered solutions or polyesters. The peppercorn                  is chosen from a whole peppercorn, a ground                  peppercorn, and a crushed peppercorn. The oil is                  a vegetable oil chosen from sunflower oil,                  safflower oil, olive oil, soybean oil, canola oil                  and a peanut oil. The oil has a temperature of                  50-135 degrees C, pressure of 14.5-30 psi and pH                  of 3-8. The peppercorn is fried, baked or                  pressure cooked. The de-flavored peppercorn                  comprises 100 mg/kg or more metabolic                  micronutrients, 0-60% original piperine                  levels.
   ACTIVITY - Anabolic; Metabolic; Gastrointestinal-Gen.;                Antidiabetic. Test details are described but no                results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The dietary supplement is administered by oral                route. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Energy-saving valve for heating pipeline, has gas storage cylinder internally provided with piston cylinder that is connected with lower bottom surface of gas storage cylinder through tension spring, where cylinder is provided with carbon dioxide gas.
AU DONG K
   XU G
AE LIAONING HONGDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 202142838A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an energy-saving valve for heating pipeline, comprising a connecting piece, a valve body, a handle and a valve core; the valve body is square; the inner part is fixedly connected with a clapboard; the valve core is provided with a screw rod; the handle is provided with a threaded hole; the lower part of the valve body is provided with a gas storage cylinder; the gas storage cylinder is provided with a piston cylinder; the piston cylinder is connected with the lower bottom surface of the gas storage cylinder through a tension spring; the gas storage cylinder is provided with carbon dioxide gas; under the cooperation of the handle and the screw rod can realize the control of the total opening of the valve; the carbon dioxide gas in the gas storage cylinder, when the indoor temperature rises, the volume is increased, the piston cylinder is moved upwards, reducing the valve opening degree, reducing the volume flow of the heat fluid in the heating pipeline; when the temperature is reduced, the volume shrinks; the piston cylinder moves downwards under the action of the stretching spring; the opening of the valve is increased; the volume flow of the hot fluid is increased; The device has simple structure, sensitive temperature reaction, good adjusting effect; it can reduce the supply of hot fluid after rising temperature of heating point, which has the effect of saving energy.

Several uprights are                arranged in a gas accommodating cavity, and several                uprights (51) make the air flow circulate in a                serpentine shape. The infrared heating device is                used to heat the VOCs exhaust gas treated by a                plasma generating device (4), such that the VOCs in                the exhaust gas absorb infrared energy and further                interact with the plasma remaining in the airflow,                and decompose to form carbon dioxide and                water.

PT P
PN US2020238258-A1
TI Solid solution catalyst for production of high quality syngas from carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbons used for producing fuels (e.g. diesel fuel) and chemicals, comprises a single transition metal, preferably nickel.
AU SCHUETZLE R
   SCHETZLE D
AE GREYROCK TECHNOLOGY LLC (GREY-Non-standard)
GA 202071045H
AB    NOVELTY - A solid solution catalyst comprises a single transition metal, preferably nickel, where catalyst is in contact with carbon dioxide (CO2), and catalyst is capable of producing syngas with a pre-selected ratio of hydrogen/carbon monoxide (H2/CO), and where H2/CO ratio is capable of being varied based on the production process used to produce the syngas.
   USE - A solid solution catalyst for production of high quality syngas from carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbons used for producing fuels (e.g. diesel fuel) and chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is stable up to 1100 degrees C; is highly versatile; efficiently produces high-quality syngas under dry reforming, combination dry/steam reforming, or tri-reforming; does not form carbon (coking); and has good resistance to contaminants that may be present in captured CO2 streams, natural gas, biogas, or other gas feedstock sources. The catalyst is also capable of reforming complex and higher molecular weight hydrocarbons without coking or other deactivation that occurs on traditional steam methane reforming (SMR) and other reforming catalyst systems. This catalyst exhibits high activity at low nickel concentrations (5-20 wt.%), compared to other catalysts that require greater than or equal to 30 wt.% nickel. Furthermore, the use of expensive precious metals to enhance catalyst performance is not necessary. High conversion efficiencies of light hydrocarbons in the feed stream of 90-100% are achieved when the catalyst is operated under the recommended space velocities and temperature conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Catalyst does not include a precious metal. Catalyst contains 5-20 wt.% nickel. Catalyst is capable of producing syngas with a H2/CO ratio of 1.5-2.5. Catalyst is capable of producing syngas with a H2/CO ratio of 0.7-1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN KR2020083098-A; KR2204932-B1
TI Cosmetic composition production involves adding supercritical carbon dioxide to acerola fruit powder to perform supercritical extraction, and then recovering pellet from which fat-soluble components are removed.
AU KWONSUNGPIL
   YOU K H
   KIM H
   LEE H S
AE CHUNGDAM CDCJN PHARM LLC (CHUN-Non-standard)
GA 202063331V
AB    NOVELTY - Cosmetic composition production involves adding supercritical carbon dioxide to acerola fruit powder to perform supercritical extraction, and then recovering a pellet from which fat-soluble components are removed. The purified water is added to the recovered pellet under nitrogen atmosphere to extract acerola extract. The obtained acerola extract is centrifuged to recover supernatant, which is freeze-dried to obtain acerola extract powder. The obtained acerola extract powder is added to culture medium for lactic acid bacteria growth to obtain culture solution by inoculating and culturing the lactic acid bacteria. The obtained culture solution is centrifuged to get supernatant containing.
   USE - Method for producing cosmetic composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the cosmetic composition in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with excellent skin whitening effect, wrinkle improvement effect, antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing acerola extract, which involves adding supercritical carbon dioxide to acerola fruit powder to perform supercritical extraction to recover pellet from which fat-soluble components are removed, adding purified water to the pellet recovered under nitrogen atmosphere, centrifuging the obtained acerola extract to recover supernatant to obtain acerola extract powder, and then adding purified water to the acerola extract powder followed by standing to induce precipitation; and
   (2) a method for producing liposome, which involves dissolving lecithin in an organic solvent containing manuka oil, adding an aqueous solution containing the acerola extract, concentrating under reduced pressure to form lipid film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Microorganisms: The lactic acid bacteria are Lactobacillus rhamnosus.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

GA 202024250Y

The rotating shaft is vertically arranged in the inner cavity. The top of the shaft is connected to the motor output shaft. A rotating element is arranged in the inner cavity and sequentially distributed on the rotating shaft. The air inlet is located on the top of the casing and on the right side of the motor, the air inlet is connected to the inner cavity, the air outlet is provided above the outer right side of the casing, the air outlet is connected to the outer cavity, the filter outlet is provided on the left side of the compartment, and the filter outlet communicates with the inner cavity and the outer cavity.

   Air outlet (1)

   Motor (3)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inner core layer is made of zeolite powder and carbonized pine powder. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the zeolite powder composite carbonized pine powder involves (i) uniformly mixing zeolite powder and diatomite according to a mass ratio of 10:(2-3), adding into a rare earth chloride solution, heating and stirring for 2-3 minutes, filtering, washing, drying to constant weight, and sieving through a 40-mesh sieve to obtain a mixture, (b) subjecting the obtained mixed powder to heat preservation treatment for 40-45 minutes under a nitrogen atmosphere at 700-800 degrees C, and naturally cooled to room temperature to obtain a heat treatment material, (c) carrying out acidification treatment on pine powder, adding pine powder into a phosphoric acid solution with the mass of 3 times, stirring for 40 minutes at the temperature of 70-80 degrees C, filtering, washing with clean water to neutral, and drying to constant weight to obtain acidified pine powder, (d) carbonizing the obtained acidified pine powder, adding the acidified pine powder into a carbonization furnace, heating at 200-230 degrees C, keeping the temperature for 40 minutes, then continuing to raise the temperature to 380-400 degrees C, keeping the temperature for 1 hour, and then cooling to room temperature by using water to obtain the carbonized material, (e) adding the obtained carbonized material into an acetic acid solution with the mass of 3 times, stirring and reacting for 2 hours, then filtering, washing with clear water to neutral, and carrying out microwave drying to constant weight to obtain carbonized pine powder, and (f) uniformly mixing the heat treatment material and the carbonized pine powder according to the mass ratio of 3:(10-12), and then pressing the heat treatment material and the carbonized pine powder under the pressure of 110 degrees C and 30-35 MPa to obtain 0074he composite material. In the method, the chlorinated rare earth solution is a lanthanum chloride solution with a mass fraction of 0.013-0.015%. The concentration of the phosphoric acid solution is 1.5 mol/l. The microwave power is 500 W.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

The hydrogen absorption tube (33) performs hydrogen absorption separation on the mixed gas in the hydrogen separation chamber. The separated pure hydrogen is output from the pure hydrogen outlet of the hydrogen absorption tube, and separated carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is output from the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet. PN GB2574208-A; WO2019229424-A1; CA3101921-A1; IN202047053560-A; AU2019279392-A1; SE202051523-A1; DE212019000291-U1; US2021213220-A1; ES1278229-U; JP3234614-U; ES1278229-Y; PL437561-A1; CN217662389-U; GB2574208-B
TI Extraction of xenon gas bound to a filter material            involves using supercritical carbon dioxide to form a            mixture in which both carbon dioxide and xenon are in a            supercritical state.
AU BROWN S M
   BROWN S
AE SAGETECH MEDICAL EQUIP LTD (SAGE-Non-standard)
   SAGETECH MEDICAL EQUIP LTD (SAGE-Non-standard)
   SAGETECH MEDICAL EQUIP LTD (SAGE-Non-standard)
   SAGETECH MEDICAL EQUIP LTD (SAGE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A1303L
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of xenon gas bound to a filter                material involves using supercritical carbon                dioxide (CO2) to form a mixture in which both CO2,                and xenon are in a supercritical state.
   USE - Extraction of xenon gas bound to filter                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical extraction                conditions enables a much higher throughput than                gas chromatography due to high density of                supercritical solutions. The use of vortex tube                allows small, zero maintenance component to achieve                gas separation. The pressure drop used to drive                separation is already available from the                depressurisation of supercritical mixture that                makes it an efficient process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an apparatus to perform the method, which                comprises a module housing filter material and into                which anesthetic gas can pass so that xenon                anesthetic agent binds to the filter material, and                a supercritical fluid source, where the module is                resistant to supercritical CO2 and able to                withstand supercritical CO2 pressure and                temperature so as to enable captured xenon to be                reclaimed by exposure to supercritical CO2;
   (2) a xenon closed circle breathing system                with capture, extraction and re-delivery of xenon                into the circuit; and
   (3) a method for re-administration of xenon                gas to same patient by the capture of xenon onto a                filter material, extraction of xenon in                supercritical CO2, separation and removal of CO2                from xenon gas and redelivery of xenon to breathing                circuit within the medical device for administering                xenon to the patient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Xenon is captured from                  the exhaust of a medical device delivering xenon                  by binding it to a filter material that includes                  silica gel, zeolites, metal organic frameworks,                  or metal doped silica/zeolite. The method for                  capturing xenon from the exhaust of a medical                  device involves delivering xenon by binding it to                  a filter material formed of a silver or lithium                  doped aerogel. The method to capture xenon from                  exhausted anesthetic gas involves processing gas                  containing xenon with filter material. The method                  also involves step of releasing xenon from the                  filter using a supercritical fluid. The method                  further involves passing gas derived from a                  patient in a medical environment through a filter                  so that xenon anesthetic agent bound to it,                  subjecting the filter material to a supercritical                  fluid to form a supercritical solution, and                  removing contaminants from the supercritical                  solution. The xenon anesthetic agent is collected                  from the supercritical solution, and the xenon                  anesthetic agent is reintroduced to a patient.                  The step of separating xenon gas derived from a                  medical device and CO2 involves using a vortex                  tube. The method of producing medical grade xenon                  from contaminated xenon is derived from the                  exhaust of a xenon delivery medical device by                  using liquid CO2 chromatography followed by                  separation of xenon from CO2. Liquid CO2 is used                  as the mobile phase for chromatographic                  purification of xenon from gaseous contaminants                  derived from the patient or breathing                  systems.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

TI Carbon dioxide reduction-type air cleaning device for being installed between indoor and outdoor, has straight line provided in exhaust path in direction, and heat exchanger provided on indoor side to form air flow to indoor side of fan.
AU EUN S K
AE EUN S K (EUNS-Individual)
GA 2019A2441K
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a straight line provided in an exhaust path in direction from an outdoor to an indoor to allow outdoor air to be taken into an inner surface. The straight line blocks one of an exhaust passage (20) and an intake passage, and transmits heat provided to block another exhaust passage and intake passage. A septum is formed in a heat exchanger (24) to allow one of air streams to pass, and provide inlets and outlets on one side divided by a partition wall (23) to allow another air to pass. The heat exchanger is provided on an indoor side to form air flow to the indoor side of an intake fan (32).
   USE - Carbon dioxide reduction-type air cleaning device for being installed between an indoor and an outdoor.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can reliably distinguish the flow path through which a bet flows from the flow path and which outer side air flows, so that the bet passing through a bet flow path of the heat exchanger is surely discharged to an outer side during ventilation, and outdoor air passing through the outer side air flow path of the heat exchanger is indoors, thus ensuring reliable inflow to a furnace, and greatly improving ventilation efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a window frame when a carbon dioxide reduction-type air cleaning device is installed. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Holders (11)
   Exhaust passage (20)
   Partition wall (23)
   Heat exchanger (24)
   Intake fan (32)

PN JP3223507-U
TI Free carbonic acid feeder for bathtubs, has fastening cap which strongly pushes open end of compression cylinder in direction of protrusion, and partition portion provided at open end of compression cylinder is destroyed.
AU NAKAJIMA H
AE NAKAJIMA KOGYO YG (NAKA-Non-standard)
GA 201986398K
AB    NOVELTY - The feeder has a protrusion which is provided with ventilation portion formed at a position where comes into contact with the open end of a compression cylinder. A fastening cap strongly pushes the open end of the compression cylinder in the direction of the protrusion, and partition portion provided at the open end of the compression cylinder is destroyed. The carbon dioxide filled in the compression cylinder is crazed to a gas permeable material arranged on the outer peripheral surface of the ultrafine bubble generating nozzle by regulator, and ultra fine bubbles are released to the bathtub and footbath from a bubble generation portion composed of film.
   USE - Free carbonic acid feeder for bathtubs.
   ADVANTAGE - The bubbles can be refined focusing on the proportionality between the bubble size and the ascending speed, and the fine bubbles have extremely small buoyancy, so that the stagnation in the liquid is prolonged and the dissolved amount is increased. The pressurized fine bubbles are easily fused in the liquid and enriched in the amount of dissolved gas. The prolonged stagnation in the liquid and the increase in the dissolved amount lead to the use of a small amount of carbon dioxide gas. The adoption of compression cylinder filled with carbon dioxide gas, which is generally marketed, provides a household with a carbonic acid bath that can achieve the same effect as a simple carbonated spring.

GA 2019728332
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption tower (11) provided with a tower first inlet (11A1) and a second inlet (11A2). A barren and heat exchanger (13) is provided with a barren and heat exchanger first inlet (13A1) and a barren and heat exchanger second inlet (13A2). One side of a pump (12) is connected to a tower outlet (11B1). Another side of the pump is connected to the barren and heat exchanger first inlet. A low temperature and low pressure steam in a boiling device (19) is heated and cooled to become condensed water. The boiling device passes the condensed water to a coal-fired steam injection boiler system (17) through an outlet of the boiler (19B1), which is used as feed water for the coal injection boiler system.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system based on coal-fired steam injection boiler system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system generates electric energy by a steam turbine generator for supplying power to rich liquid pump and lean liquid pump, saves extra power consumed by the rich liquid pump and the lean liquid pump and reduces integration cost.

TI Production of biomethane used as fuel in filling            station for vehicles, involves introducing feed gas            stream into pre-treatment unit, separating carbon            dioxide and oxygen, compressing, subjecting to            cryogenic separation, and purifying.
AU TERRIEN P
   CHANTANT N
   PAUL T
   NICOLAS C
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2019539746
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biomethane involves introducing                feed gas stream into a pre-treatment unit,                separating carbon dioxide and oxygen contained in                the feed gas stream, compressing to pressure (P1)                of more than 25 bar, subjecting resultant gas                stream to cryogenic separation in a distillation                column containing 8-100 trays to separate nitrogen                from gas stream, recovering methane-enriched stream                by pumping bottom product from distillation column                (26) at pressure (P2) of more than 25 bar, and                purifying resultant product. When the molar                concentration of nitrogen in gas stream is less                than predetermined value, and nitrogen is injected                into gas stream, such that the molar concentration                of nitrogen equal to predetermined threshold.
   USE - Production of biomethane used as fuel in                filling station for vehicles, and natural gas                network.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                production of biomethane with reduced energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                installation for production of bio methane by                purification of biogas from non-hazardous waste                storage facilities, which comprises a biogas                source, a nitrogen source, a pre-treatment unit for                removing total or portion of volatile organic                compound, water and sulfur compound from                to-be-treated gas stream, 2 or more separating                membranes for partially separating carbon dioxide                and nitrogen oxide from gas stream, a compressor                capable of compressing gas stream at pressure of                25-100 bar, heat exchanger for cooling gas stream                and distillation column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The predetermined                  threshold is 5 mol%. The pressure (P1) is more                  than 50 bar. The pressure (P2) is more than 40                  bar. Preferred Composition: The gas stream                  contains 0.3-2 mol% carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Process: The separation process is performed by a                  unit comprising 2 or more separating membranes.                  The gas stream is at least partially condensed in                  heat exchanger.

AU MIN W H
   JEONG L H
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 201925501G
AB    NOVELTY - Transgenic Synechococcus elongatus strain comprises a pyruvate decarboxylase enzyme gene encoding a pyruvate decarboxylase expressed by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 1), an aldehyde dehydrogenase enzyme (ald6) gene encoding an aldehyde dehydrogenase represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 2), an acetyl-CoA acetyltransferase (AtoB) gene encoding an enzyme that produces acetoacetyl-CoA from acetyl-CoA represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 3), an acetotacetate CoA transferase (AtoDA) gene encoding an enzyme that produces acetoacetate from acetoacetyl-CoA represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 4), and an acetoacetate decarboxylase (Adc) gene encoding an enzyme producing acetone from acetoacetate represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 5), given in the specification.
   USE - Transgenic Synechococcus elongatus strains for producing acetone from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The transgenic Synechococcus elongatus strains leads to mass-production of acetone using carbon dioxide as a carbon source, and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The transgenic Synechococcus elongatus strain further comprises a Salmonella enterica acetyl-CoA synthetase variant (acsL641P) gene encoding the acetate-coA ligase enzyme represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 6), an acetyl-CoA carboxylase (accBC) gene encoding the acetyl-CoA carboxylating enzyme represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 7), and an acetyl-CoA carboxylase beta-subunit (accD1) gene encoding the acetyl-CoA carboxylating enzyme represented by the sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 8), given in the specification. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing acetone, which involves culturing the strain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The strain is transformed with a second vector, where second vector comprises a gene encoding pyruvate decarboxylase (pdc) enzyme represented by sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 1) and an aldehyde dehydrogenase enzyme (ald6) gene encoding an aldehyde dehydrogenase represented by sequence comprising (SEQ ID NO: 2). The strain is transformed with a first vector, where the first vector comprises a gene encoding acetyl-CoA acetyltransferase that produces acetotacetate CoA transferase, and a acetoacetate decarboxylase. The strain is transformed with a third vector, where the third vector comprises the acsL641P, accBC, and accD1. The second vector comprises kanamycin resistance gene, lacI repressor, trc promoter, pdc gene, and an aldehyde dehydrogenase (ald6) gene. The first vector comprises spectinomycin resistance gene, lacI repressor, trc promoter, AtoB gene, AtoDA gene, and an Adc gene. The third vector comprises chloramphenicol resistance gene, lacI repressor, trc promoter, acsL641P gene, accBC gene, and accD1 gene. The strain is one where second vector is transformed into the parent strain Synechococcus elongatus PCC7942 preserved at American Type Culture Collection under (ATCC 33912). The strain is preserved under KCCM 12179P.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109293973-A
TI Polymer micro-foamed material prepared by e.g. adding raw material thermoplastic polyurethane particles into high pressure reaction kettle, and filling the reaction kettle with mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
AU QIN L
   ZHANG C
   MA W
   LIANG Y
   XU Y
   SHI Z
AE NINGBO GMF NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201915538P
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer micro-foamed material is claimed. The polymer micro-foamed material is processed from thermoplastic polyurethane particles, and a large cell and a small cell are formed by foaming distribution inside the thermoplastic polyurethane particle, where the thermoplastic polyurethane expanded particles have a diameter of 5-8 mm, the thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles have a density of 0.15-0.28 g/cm3, the thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles have a large cell diameter of 100-200 mu m, and the small cell diameter is 8-20 mu m.
   USE - Used as polymer micro-foamed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer micro-foamed material has ultra-low density and excellent particle density consistency; and not only ensures the overall size of the particles, reduces the particle density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polymer micro-foamed material, comprising (i) adding raw material thermoplastic polyurethane particles into the high pressure reaction kettle, and filling the reaction kettle with a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and controlling the volume content of the nitrogen in the mixed gas to less than 60%, (ii) gradually increasing the pressure and temperature in the reaction kettle until the carbon dioxide gas in the mixed gas reaches a supercritical state, maintaining the pressure for a period of time, and then quickly relieving pressure, and (iii) quickly placing infiltrated thermoplastic polyurethane particles in a foaming device for heating and foaming to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic polyurethane particles have a spherical or ellipsoidal shape. The volume ratio between carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the mixed gas charged in the reactor is 7:3. Preferred Method: The temperature of the heat foaming is 80-140 degrees C, and the time is 10-180 seconds. The duration of the rapid pressure relief process is 10-60 seconds. In step (iii), the heating and foaming is heating by a water bath or an oven. In step (ii): maintaining the pressure for 0.5-5 hours. When the carbon dioxide gas reaches the supercritical state, the state in the reactor is: the pressure is higher than 7.38 MPa, and the temperature is higher than 31.1 degrees C.

PN CN109266598-A
TI Establishing a hypoxic injury cell model involves            culturing cells in a 5 percent carbon dioxide            incubator, and formulating them into a cell suspension            when the cells are grown in a culture flask and            fused.
AU WANG R
   JIA Z
   YUE X
   XIE H
   LI W
   LU H
AE LANZHOU MILITARY HOSPITAL (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019118818
AB    NOVELTY - Establishing a hypoxic injury cell model                involves culturing cells in a 5% carbon dioxide                incubator, and formulating them into a cell                suspension when the cells are grown in a culture                flask and fused to a density of 80-90%. The cell                suspension is added to the culture vessel and the                cells are plated until the cells grow again to a                density of 80-90%. The medium that has been                equilibrated in an anoxic environment is prepared,                and the medium is replaced in the culture vessel                with the above-mentioned medium that has been                equilibrated in an anoxic environment for                anti-hypoxic drug screening. The different                concentrations of the drug is added at the same                time. The culture vessel is covered with a gas                permeable membrane, and then the culture vessel is                placed in a modular culture chamber having a                certain gas component, and then the culture chamber                is placed in an incubator at room temperature of                37&#176; C and a 5% carbon dioxide                content.
   USE - Method for establishing a hypoxic injury cell                model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to establish a hypoxic                injury cell model that does not require the                participation of experimental animals, has low                modeling cost, is more sensitive to hypoxic stress                response, and is capable of investigating hypoxic                damage at the molecular biology level, and can be                used for simulating hypoxia experiments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The cells are cultured according to the                required hypoxia time, and after the completion of                the culture, the cell culture vessel is taken out                to detect the oxidative damage index.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The cells may be                  adherent cells or suspension growth cells. The                  medium is a medium in which dissolved oxygen and                  an oxygen partial pressure in an anoxic                  environment are balanced. The anoxic environment                  is an anoxic environment having an oxygen content                  of less than 21%. The modular culture chamber is                  a modular culture chamber capable of maintaining                  a fixed gas component and have good air                  tightness. The gas permeable membrane is a                  semipermeable membrane in which water molecules                  cannot pass and gas molecules can be exchanged                  freely. The oxidative damage index is the level                  of change of genes, proteins, and metabolites                  produced by the cells in an anoxic environment.                  Preferred Components: The culture vessel                  comprises a culture dish, a culture plate, and a                  culture bottle which can be used for culturing                  the cells. The semipermeable membrane may be a                  microporous polyethylene film, a fluorocarbon                  film or a polyethylene terephthalate film.

PN CN109225179-A; CN109225179-B
TI Recycling of waste activated carbon for adsorbing            saturated chlorine-containing organic compounds and            treatment method, involves catalytically reforming            reactor to convert chlorine-containing organic waste            gas in exhaust gas into mixed gas.
AU LI H
   LI L
   ZHOU K
   WANG X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   KAIFENG YONGHE NONFERROUS METAL CO LTD (KAIF-Non-standard)
GA 201912618N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a rotary kiln as an                activation device for waste activated carbon, and                introducing a gas from the kiln head of the rotary                kiln for low-oxygen combustion to provide a                gradient temperature field in the rotary kiln. The                waste activated carbon enters from the kiln tail of                the rotary kiln, and is sequentially dried,                pyrolyzed and activated by carbon dioxide and                steam. The activated carbon is discharged from the                kiln head of the rotary kiln and cooled. The                activated carbon is treated by oxidation with an                oxidizing acid solution, followed by washing and                drying to obtain regenerated activated carbon. The                exhaust gas in the rotary kiln is discharged from                the kiln tail. The catalytic reforming reactor is                catalytically reformed to convert the                chlorine-containing organic waste gas in the                exhaust gas into a mixed gas containing hydrogen,                carbon dioxide, hydrogen chloride, and a mixed gas,                after dedusting and is returned to the kiln head as                gas, after alkali washing.
   USE - Recycling of waste activated carbon for                adsorbing saturated chlorine-containing organic                compounds and treatment method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes rotary kiln to                continuously dry, regenerate, and activate carbon                dioxide and steam activation of waste activated                carbon, and then activates through acidic                oxidation. The process has short regeneration time,                high recovery rate and excellent adsorption                performance of regenerated activated carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method                for recycling waste activated carbon. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)

PN CN108276279-A
TI Synthesizing asymmetric carbonate ester involves reacting oxirane, carbon-dioxide, metyl aclcohal, and any other alcohal to obtain ethylene glycoland asymmetric alkyl methyl carbonate, where raw materials are ethylene oxide.
AU SHI L
   YU Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201856095P
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing asymmetric carbonate ester involves reacting oxirane, carbon-dioxide, metyl aclcohal, and any other alcohal (ROH) to obtain ethylene glycoland asymmetric alkyl methyl carbonate (CH3OCOOR), where raw materials are respectively ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ROH, where ROH is linear alcohol, isomeric alcohol, an aromatic alcohol, phenol, glycol such as ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol and polyol. The product has a molar ratio of ethylene glycol and carbonate is 1:1. The catalyst which is used for catalyzing one-step reaction is a composite basic catalyst having mesoporous and microporous structure. The preparation of microporous molecular sieve carrier involves taking certain mass of MS carrier which is separately added to a certain temperature, volume, concentration of acidic stirring, and then filtered, washed, and dried to obtain a de-aluminated MS-DAl carrier.
   USE - Method for synthesizing asymmetric carbonate ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesized asymmetric carbonate ester which is simple distillation, has clean whole reaction process, high efficiency, eco-friendly, and no any by-product of low value is generated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The desiliconization involves adding prepared MS-DAl carrier to a certain temperature, concentration, and a certain volume of an alkaline solution for stirring, and subjected to suction filtration, washing, and drying to obtain multi-stage pore MS-DAl-DSi carrier. The DSi carrier is calcined in a muffle furnace to obtain a carrier labeled (M-meso-S), where asymmetric ester of carbonic acid is prepared by continuous reaction in a fixed bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is represented by general formula X/S, where X is lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium , barium, lanthanum, cerium, yttrium, zirconium, chromium, tungsten, manganese, iron, ruthenium, cobalt, rhodium, iridium, palladium, platinum, copper, silver, zinc, boron, aluminum, and gallium and their oxides. The composite basic catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure. The S is a carrier of different topologies includes topological structure of electrical metal tubing (EMT), zeolite framework type (MOR), MWW, faujasite (FAU) zeolite, silicalite-1 (structure type MFI), zeolite framework type Ferrierite(FER) , aluminum-beta zeolite (BEA) molecular sieves and one or more oxides of conventional silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, magnesium oxide and zinc oxide carriers. The S molecular sieve carrier is first subjected to ion exchange, and bronsted acid is poisoned by metal ion, where ion exchangers of poisoned molecular sieves are hydrochlorides, sulfates or nitrates of metals. Preferred Condition: The acidic solution is added in minimum amount to submerge all molecular sieves which is 10-20 times of volume of molecular sieve, preferably 15 times. The alkaline solution comprises one or more of sodium carbonate, preferably sodium hydroxide. The concentration of alkaline solution is 0.05-2.0 mole per liter. The amount of the alkaline solution is added in molecular sieves are submerged, which is 5-15 times, preferably 10 times volume of the molecular sieve. The preparation of microporous molecular sieve carrier has mesoporous pore size distribution of 4-34 nanometer which is obtained by the M-meso-S carrier. The specific pore size is related to concentration of the alkaline solution used. The supported alkali metal oxide catalyst having mesoporous and microporous structure carrier is coprecipitation or impregnation, where alkaline solutions for coprecipitation includes alkali metal and ammonium hydroxides such as lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and ammonium hydroxide, alkali metal carbonates, hydrogencarbonates, and formic acid as a salt and an acetate salt, such as a solution of lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate is also mixture of two or more of the above alkaline solutions. The liquid medium is used for alkaline substance solution is preferably. The amount of alkaline solution is added to submerge all the carriers, which is 10-20 times or more, preferably 15 times volume of the carrier, and pH of the control solution is 10-14, preferably pH 11 at time of co-precipitation. The room temperature is (-)99 degrees C, preferably 70 degrees C, aging is performed at (-)80 degrees C, aging time is 1 hour - 20 days, and preferably 5 days. The active component precursor of supported catalyst of the composite pore structure is preferably aluminum nitrate, potassium nitrate, cesium nitrate, magnesium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, barium hydroxide, strontium nitrate, lanthanum nitrate, ferric nitrate. The molar ratio of ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol, other alcohol is 1:1:3:2, space velocity is 0.5-5 per hour, and reaction temperature is 78-130 degrees C. The reaction pressure of the reactor is 0.1-10 mega Pascal, molar ratio of ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, and methanol and other alcohols is 0.1:1-10:1. The molar ratio of two methanol and other alcohols is 1:5-5:1. The asymmetric ester of carbonic acid is prepared by reaction in presence of a catalyst. The reaction is sealed at 25-150 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours. The catalyst is a basic catalyst, and amount of the catalyst is quality of raw material 0.1-10 wt.%.

GA 201857613P

   USE - The H.marmoreus strain WZ-29 is useful for producing mycelium, spore and fruiting body of H.marmoreus. The method is useful for cultivating H.marmoreus strain WZ-29 (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The H.marmoreus strain WZ-29 has short cultivation period. The method improves yield of H.marmoreus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) method for cultivating H.marmoreus strain WZ-29, which involves (a) inoculating H.marmoreus strain WZ-29 into culture medium, culturing at 20-25 degrees C for 30-38 days, where the air relative humidity is controlled to 70-80%, and the concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled to 2000-3000 parts per million, and obtaining mycelium, (b) inoculating the mycelium obtained in the step (a) into fruit body culture medium, and culturing at 20-28 degrees C for 70-78 days, where the relative humidity is controlled to 70-80%, and the concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled to 2500-4000 parts per million, (c) placing the cultivated mycelium obtained in the step (b) in a breeding room for fertility regulation to obtain a fruit body, and culturing at 13-15 degrees C to obtain fruit body, where the air relative humidity is controlled to 92-97%, the concentration of carbon dioxide is controlled to 1000-2500 parts per million, and the shelf light is turned on every day, and (d) culturing fruit body using culture medium, harvesting mushroom and storing after harvesting; (2) H.marmoreus obtained by performing the above-mentioned method; and (3) mycelium, spore and fruiting body of H.marmoreus obtained using H.marmoreus strain WZ-29.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves setting shelf lamp for providing light intensity of 50-100 Lx during first 7 days of cultivation, and light intensity of 200-300 Lx. Preferred Component: The fruit body culture medium comprises 16-25% red corn cob, 3-6% wood meals, 8-15% soybean hulls, 20-35% rice bran, 10-20% bran, 3-10% corn flour, 6-13% cottonseed hulls, 1-3% mycelial active agent and 20-28% water.

PN CN107857286-A
TI Preparing cryolite involves adding fluosilicic acid waste liquid into steel lined plastic reaction kettle, adding pure alkali (sodium carbonate), stirring material liquid and heating to obtain silicon oxide, carbon dioxide and water.
AU LIU Z
AE DAZHOU LIZHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TE (DAZH-Non-standard)
GA 201826429J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cryolite involves adding the fluosilicic acid waste liquid into the steel lined plastic reaction kettle, adding 5-10% pure alkali (sodium carbonate), stirring the material liquid and heating to 70 degrees C until the pH value reaches 8-9 to obtain silicon oxide, carbon dioxide and water, filtering by silica gel and heating, where filtrate is sodium fluoride solution. The fluosilicic acid waste liquid is added into the steel lined plastic reaction kettle, added aluminum oxide to form material solution, stirred and heated to 80 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing cryolite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cryolite improves the fluorosilicic acid waste liquid utilization rate, quality and yield of cryolite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cryolite involves adding the fluosilicic acid waste liquid into the steel lined plastic reaction kettle, adding 5-10% pure alkali (sodium carbonate), stirring the material liquid and heating to 70 degrees C until the pH value reaches 8-9 to obtain silicon oxide, carbon dioxide and water, filtering by silica gel and heating, where filtrate is sodium fluoride solution. The fluosilicic acid waste liquid is added into the steel lined plastic reaction kettle, added aluminum oxide to form material solution, stirred and heated to 80 degrees C. The obtained product is filtered by silica gel to obtain aluminum fluoride solution. The sodium fluoride solution is added into the synthesis kettle, added 9-11% aluminum fluoride solution, heated to 95-100 degrees C, filtered, washed with water and dried to obtain cryolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of fluosilicic acid waste liquid is 12%, calcined soda purity is not greater than 96%, and purity of the aluminum oxide is not greater than 95%. The obtained product is heated until the material liquid pH value is 9. The added aluminum fluoride solution excess value is 10%.
