PN CN107720821-A
TI Hydrothermal phase transformation of bismuth oxide at atmospheric pressure involves taking high purity bismuth oxide, adding to bismuth nitrate solution, heating, and adding carbon dioxide-containing aqueous sodium hydroxide solution.
AU WU S
   HUANG Z
   TU J
   ZHOU L
   SHAO Q
AE GUIXI SANYUAN METAL CO LTD (GUIX-Non-standard)
GA 2018171925
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrothermal phase transformation of bismuth oxide at atmospheric pressure involves taking high purity bismuth oxide, adding to bismuth nitrate solution, heating at 80-90 degrees C, adding carbon dioxide-containing aqueous sodium hydroxide solution dropwise, mixing, precipitating, expanding bismuth oxide hydrate, heating, stirring, dehydrating, washing, decanting, filtering, drying, adding ammonium vanadate solution, reacting at 60 degrees C and pH of 9-10, removing precipitate, filtering, washing with distilled water and acetone, drying at 120 degrees C, calcining at 550 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling, and grinding.
   USE - Method for hydrothermal phase transforming bismuth oxide at atmospheric pressure (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and practical, and has high product quality and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrothermal phase transformation of bismuth oxide at atmospheric pressure comprises taking high purity bismuth oxide, adding to bismuth nitrate solution, heating at 80-90 degrees C, adding carbon dioxide-containing aqueous sodium hydroxide solution dropwise, mixing, precipitating, expanding bismuth oxide hydrate, heating, stirring, dehydrating, washing, decanting, filtering, drying, adding ammonium vanadate solution, reacting at 60 degrees C and pH of 9-10, removing precipitate, filtering, washing with distilled water and acetone, drying at 120 degrees C, calcining at 550 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling, grinding, calcining at 650-700 degrees C for 15 hours, cooling, configuring inversion solution, adding processed product, mixing for 3.5 hours, centrifuging for 15 minutes, filtering, solid-liquid separating, and drying at 60-85 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inversion solution comprises 85 wt.% primary solution, 10 wt.% dispersant and 5 wt.% catalytic reagent. The dispersant comprises sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and potassium bicarbonate at ratio of 1:1.5:1.25:0.8. The primary solution comprises 324 g/mL sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. The primary solution comprises taking 1000 g sodium aluminate, crushing, drying, sieving at 60-100 mesh, adding to 200 g purified water, mixing, sealing, injecting pure hydrogen gas, reflecting, and filtering. Preferred Method: Centrifuging is done at 45-55 degrees C and 15-45 revolutions/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Anti-corrosion agent comprises 10-20 pts. wt. sodium propionate, 30-45 pts. wt. nickel nitrate and 20-35 pts. wt. organic phosphoric acid ester.

PN CN107593891-A
TI Preserving Russula vinosa, by washing fresh Russula vinosa, coating plant preservative containing clove oil and beeswax, packaging in fresh-keeping bag containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide and placing in cold storage.
AU SHE Y
AE SHE Y (SHEY-Individual)
GA 201807602C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving Russula vinosa, involves (a) picking fresh and non-damaged R.vinosa, and washing dirt on the surface using a soft hair brush, (b) preparing a plant preservative, and coating the preservative on the outer surface of the R.vinosa, (c) packaging the R.vinosa in a microporous fresh-keeping bag, filling the bag with mixed gas containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and (d) placing the packages in cold storage, where the plant preservative comprises clove oil, beeswax, allicin and water.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving R.vinosa.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits pathogenic fungus on surfaces of R.vinosa, retains nutritional content and moisture content of R.vinosa, is environmentally-friendly, and nontoxic and harmless and preserves R.vinosa with extended shelf-life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preserving Russula vinosa, involves (a) picking fresh and non-damaged R.vinosa, and washing dirt on the surface using a soft hair brush, (b) preparing a plant preservative, and coating the preservative on the outer surface of R.vinosa, (c) packaging R.vinosa obtained in step (b) in a microporous fresh-keeping bag, filling the bag with mixed gas containing 20-30% nitrogen and 15-25% carbon dioxide, and (d) after packing, placing the package in a cold storage at -20 to -35 degrees C for storage, where the plant preservative comprises 3-8.5 wt.% clove oil, 8-15 wt.% beeswax, 10-16 wt.% allicin and 60.5-79 wt.% water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The soft hair brush is made of pig bristles. The plant preservative is prepared by heating water to 80-85 degrees C, adding beeswax, after complete dissolution, stopping heating, cooling down to 30-35 degrees C, adding clove oil, and allicin, and stirring mixture for 5-10 minutes. The plant preservative comprises 6.5 wt.% clove oil, 12 wt.% beeswax, 15 wt.% allicin and water (remainder). In step (c), the mixed gas in the microporous fresh-keeping bag containing 25% nitrogen and 22% carbon dioxide.

TI Extracting chromosome of induced pluripotent stem cell, comprises e.g. culturing stem cell in carbon dioxide incubator, adding fluorouracil deoxyribonucleoside, incubating, extracting cell suspensions, fixing on glass slide and dyeing.
AU WANG H
   WU L
   LIANG P
AE HANGZHOU MATERNITY HOSPITAL (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2018071019
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting chromosome of induced pluripotent stem cell, comprises (i) placing stem cells in 5% carbon dioxide incubator and open-type culturing at 37 degrees C, (ii) replacing fresh culture medium, adding fluorouracil deoxyribonucleoside to the culture, incubating, adding thymidine to the culture, incubating, adding colchicines to the culture, uniformly mixing, incubating and taking out the culture flask, (iii) extracting stem cells from the culture flask to obtain cell suspensions, (iv) dropping the cell suspension on a glass slide and fixing, and (v) dyeing the fixed chromosome.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting chromosome of induced pluripotent stem cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high chromosome resolution in the karyotype identification of stem cells, large number cells and high success rate of karyotype analysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting chromosome of induced pluripotent stem cell, comprises (i) placing stem cells in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, open-type culturing at 37 degrees C and changing culture medium every day, (ii) replacing fresh culture medium at 8 in the morning before the day of stem cell harvesting, adding 100 mu l fluorouracil deoxyribonucleoside to the culture flask at 10 a.m., continuing to incubate the culture, adding 200 mu l thymidine the next day at 8 a.m., continuing to incubate the culture, adding 100 mu l colchicines at 11 a.m. to the culture flask, uniformly mixing, incubating for 15 minutes and taking out the culture flask, (iii) extracting stem cells from the culture flask to obtain cell suspensions, (iv) dropping the cell suspension on a glass slide and fixing, and (v) dyeing the fixed chromosome.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (iii), specifically comprises (A) pipetting the culture medium into centrifugal tube, rinsing the culture flask using 2 ml of physiological saline and washing liquid also pipetted into the centrifuge tube, (B) carrying out digestion using 0.25% EDTA-trypsin solution into the culture flask for 5 minutes, transferring the cells into the centrifuge tube, rinsing the culture flask using physiological saline for 3-5 times and rinsing the flask, (C) centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes, removing the supernatant, adding 0.5 ml of 0.075 moles/l potassium chloride hypotonic solution to the centrifugal tube, preheating at room temperature or 37 degrees C, placing on the vortex 10-15 seconds, uniformly mixing, continuously adding 0.075 mol/L of KCl hypotonic solution to 10 ml scale, covering the lid, reverse mixing uniformly and placing in water bath of low permeability at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes, (D) slowly adding 0.5 ml fixative agent along the tube wall, covering the lid, reverse mixing uniformly, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 8 min, pipetting the supernatant, leaving the height of 3-5 mm, placing on the vortex 10-15 seconds, uniformly mixing, adding the fixative agent to 8 ml scale, covering the lid , reverse mixing uniformly and centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes, (E) pipetting out fixative agent, leaving the height of 3-5 mm, adding fixative agent to 8 ml scale, covering the lid, reverse mixing uniformly, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes and repeating step twice and (F) absorbing the fixative agent from the centrifuge tube, leaving a 1-1.5 ml fixative agent at bottom of the tube and pipetting to another centrifuge tube. The step (iv) specifically comprises writing number on the glass slide, immersing the glass slide into distilled water for 1 s of depth 5 cm. immediately taking out and placing it on vertical absorbent paper, absorbing excessive water, covering the glass slide surface with a layer of water film, immediately placing in the drawer, closing the drawer, uniformly mixing cell suspension using pipette and remove cell suspension, opening the drawer, adding 30 mu l suspension to the middle of the upper end of the slide for even flow, closing the drawer, drying the slides, removing from the drawer and placed on the slide holder, placing in the oven at 65 degrees C for overnight or 75 degrees C for 3 hours. The step (5) comprises placing fixing sheet on the staining rack, placing staining rack in the first cylinder and digesting time is noted, digesting for 1 minute 50 seconds, taking out immediately, immersing in the second cylinder for 3 times each time for 1-2 seconds, removing the excess water along the edges of cylinder for 3-5 times, immersing in the third cylinder for 3 times each time for 1-2 seconds, immersing in the fourth cylinder for 3-5 times, dipping and dyeing time begins at the same time, dyeing for 2.5 minutes, dyeing in the fifth cylinder, scrubbing, rinsing for 3 times for 1-2 seconds, removing the excess water along the edges of cylinder for 3-5 times or immersed into the large beaker for 3 times, drying moisture, blow drying using hair drier and drying in the oven at 55 degrees C. The first cylinder contains trypsin digestion solution, which is preapared by adding 240 ml physiological saline or phosphoric acid buffer solution of pH 7.2 in the dyeing tank , 10 ml pancreatic enzyme stock solution and adjusting the pH to 7.2- 7.4; the second cylinder contains saline solution, which is prepared by adding 250 ml physiological saline in the dyeing tank and adding or not adding 2 ml calf serum; the third cylinder contains physiological saline, which is prepared by adding 250 ml physiological saline to the dyeing tank; the fourth cylinder contains giemsa dye solution, which is prepared by adding 180 ml distilled water, pH 6.8 phosphate buffer stock solution of 10x 20 ml, 10-12 ml giemsa stain stock solution, 190 ml water, 10-12 ml giemsa stain stock solution, completely stirring and mixing uniformly using plastic pipette; and the fifth cylinder contains 250 ml tap water or taking large beaker filled with 1 l tap water. Preferred Components: In the (ii), the concentration of fluorouracil deoxyribonucleoside is 1 mu g/ml. The concentration of thymidine is 2.4 mg/ml. The concentration of colchicine is 20 mu g/ml. In the (D), the fixing solution is methanol and glacial acetic acid mixture and the volume ratio is 3:1.

PN CN107537569-A; CN107537569-B
TI Ionized covalent organic framework material load metallic catalyst is prepared by taking amino pyrene monomer PyTTA and aldehyde pyrene monomer 1,3,6,8-tetrakis(p-formylphenyl)pyrene (TFPPy) as raw material and using Schiff base reaction.
AU GAO Y
   YING X
   LENG W
   FENG L
   JING X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201803586B
AB    NOVELTY - Ionized covalent organic framework material load metallic catalyst is claimed. The preparation of ionized covalent organic framework material load metallic catalyst comprises taking amino pyrene monomer PyTTA and aldehyde pyrene monomer 1,3,6,8-tetrakis(p-formylphenyl)pyrene (TFPPy) as raw material, using Schiff base reaction between amino and aldehyde, synthesizing TF-Py COF frame using solvent thermal reaction under vacuum condition, grafting sulfonic acid group by ion exchange in the frame and different metal load in the frame.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for generating ring carbonic ester compound of carbon dioxide and epoxy compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts carbon dioxide to cyclocarbonate, expands reactive substrate species, has high reaction rate, recycles catalyst and easy to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing ionized covalent organic framework material load metallic catalyst comprising taking amino pyrene monomer PyTTA and aldehyde pyrene monomer 1,3,6,8-tetrakis(p-formylphenyl)pyrene (TFPPy) as raw material, using Schiff base reaction between amino and aldehyde, synthesizing TF-Py COF frame using solvent thermal reaction under vacuum condition, reducing imine bond frame to secondary amine bond, grafting sulfonic acid group by ion exchange in the frame and different metal load in the frame, where the metal is cobalt, nickel, copper and zinc and the loading amount of metal element in the product is 3-7 wt.%; and
   (2) using catalyst for generating ring carbonic ester compound of carbon dioxide and epoxy compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of amino pyrene monomer PyTTA and aldehyde pyrene monomer TFPPy is 0.5-1:0.5-1. The solvent is a mixed solvent and the mixed solvent includes 1,4-dioxane, trimethylbenzene, N, N-dimethyl acetamide and 3 mol/l acetic acid solution in a volume ratio of 0.5-0.7ml/0.5-0.7ml/0.5-0.7ml/0-0.1ml. The ion exchange material is cobalt(II) acetate, nickel(II) acetate, copper(II) acetate and zinc(II) acetate. Preferred Method: The vacuum condition is 20-200 Pa, the reaction time is 24-168 hours and the reaction temperature is 120-140 degrees C. The method includes using sodium borohydride as reducing agent, reducing the molar ratio of TF-Py, COF and sodium borohydride is 1:1-1:2000, reaction temperature of 20-40 degrees C, the reaction time of 15-60 minutes; using grafting sulfonic acid group of raw material is 1,3-propane sultone and 1,4-butane sultone, where the molar ratio after reduction of COF material and grafted sulfonic acid group is 1:1-1:20, the reaction temperature is 110-150 degrees C and the reaction time is 2-15 hours. The method comprises mixing epoxy compound, catalyst tetrabutylammonium iodide and M(II)TF-Py COF in presence of carbon dioxide, mixing the reaction product, separating the catalyst by filtering or extracting, where the liquid phase is catalytic target product cyclic carbonate compound and epoxy compound has the molecular formula, where the R is substituted group, epoxy compound R is methyl, chloromethyl, butyl, butoxy, phenyl or hexyl; the catalyst tetrabutyl ammonium iodide is tetrabutyl ammonium bromide or ammonium tetrabutyl; the mass ratio of epoxy compound, cocatalyst and M (II) @ TF-Py COF is 500-2000:0.5-4:1; the reaction temperature is 80-100 degrees C and carbon dioxide pressure is 1-5 MPa; the reaction system allows the volume ratio of 0-10% in presence of air and the reaction time is 12-72 hours.

TI Production of hydrogen for producing methanol,            involves endothermically reforming feed gas stream            comprising hydrocarbon component and steam to form            synthesis gas stream containing hydrogen and carbon            dioxide, obtaining hydrogen-rich stream, and separating            carbon dioxide from residual gas stream.
AU SCHMIDT S
   MCGUINESS SCHMID T
   SCHMID M T
   SOPHIA S
   TEJA S M
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
GA 202222984S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen involves providing a                feed gas stream comprising a hydrocarbon component                comprising at least methane, and a steam,                endothermically reforming at least one portion of                the feed gas stream over a reforming catalyst to                form a synthesis gas stream (S1) comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                unreacted methane, autothermally reforming a                portion of the feed gas stream to form a synthesis                gas stream (S2) and combining streams (S1) and (S2)                to form a synthesis gas stream (S3), converting                carbon monoxide contained in stream (S3) with a                steam to hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a synthesis                gas stream (S4), separating hydrogen from stream                (S4), obtaining a hydrogen-rich stream and a                residual gas stream, and separating carbon dioxide                from the residual gas stream by cryogenic carbon                dioxide separation. Streams (S2) and (S3) comprise                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                unreacted methane.
   USE - Production of hydrogen used as synthesis gas                for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method modifies the known processes so                that the amount of carbon dioxide formed per unit                weight of hydrogen produced is reduced and reduces                carbon dioxide emissions from processes which                combine autothermal reforming of methane with                cryogenic carbon dioxide removal, and produces                hydrogen with high yield and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrogen involves providing a                feed gas stream comprising a hydrocarbon component                comprising at least methane, and a steam,                endothermically reforming at least one portion of                the feed gas stream over a reforming catalyst to                form a synthesis gas stream (S1) comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                unreacted methane, autothermally reforming a                portion of the feed gas stream to form a synthesis                gas stream (S2) and combining streams (S1) and (S2)                to form a synthesis gas stream (S3), or                autothermally reforming stream (S1) by exothermic                partial oxidizing and endothermic reforming with a                steam over a reforming catalyst to form stream                (S3), converting carbon monoxide contained in                stream (S3) with a steam to hydrogen and carbon                dioxide to a synthesis gas stream (S4) comprising                hydrogen, carbon dioxide, unreacted methane, and                carbon monoxide, separating hydrogen from stream                (S4) by pressure swing adsorption, obtaining a                hydrogen-rich stream and a residual gas stream                comprising carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide,                hydrogen not removed in the above process, and                unreacted methane, and separating carbon dioxide                from the residual gas stream by cryogenic carbon                dioxide separation to obtain a carbon dioxide-rich                stream and a residual gas stream comprising                unconverted carbon monoxide, hydrogen not separated                from the above process, carbon dioxide, and                unreacted methane. Streams (S2) and (S3) comprise                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                unreacted methane. The heat generated by                autothermal reforming is used for heating in the                endothermic reforming. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a hydrogen production plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-rich stream comprises unreacted                  methane.

PT P
PN CN113929806-A; CN113929806-B
TI Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide displacement oil displacement agent useful in e.g. puff oil production comprises reacting two monomer to generate first intermediate and reacting with third monomer to generate second intermediate, reacting fourth and fifth monomer and polymerizing.
AU ZHANG R
   ZHANG F
   HOU Y
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022174389
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide displacement oil displacement agent comprises reacting first monomer and second monomer to generate a first intermediate, reacting the first intermediate with the third monomer to generate the second intermediate, reacting the fourth monomer with the fifth monomer to obtain the third intermediate and polymerizing the second intermediate and the third intermediate to obtain oil- displacement agent, where the first monomer comprises ethylene oxide and/or propylene oxide, the second monomer comprises diethylamine, dipropylamine and/or dibutylamine, the third monomer comprises 3-pentenoic acid, 4-pentenoic acid, 2-methyl-4-pentenoic acid and/or 4-phenyl-4-pentenoic acid, the fourth monomer comprises 4-phenyl-1-butanol, 5-phenyl-1-pentanol and/or 6-phenyl-1-hexanol and the fifth monomer comprises 5-chloro-1-pentene, 6-chloro-1-hexene and/or 7-chloro-1-heptene.
   USE - The supercritical carbon dioxide displacement oil displacement agent is useful in downhole periodic huff and puff oil production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method significantly increases the swept volume of supercritical carbon dioxide; improves the utilization efficiency of supercritical carbon dioxide and oil displacement agent and greatly improves the recovery factor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a oil displacing agent prepared by the above method; and #
   (2) use of oil displacing agent comprising mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and the oil displacement agent, injecting in oil well underground, stewing well, extracting oil-gas mixture, separating the carbon dioxide and crude oil in the oil-gas mixture, collecting the separated gaseous carbon dioxide to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide and performing the oil extraction operation of the next period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first monomer is ethylene oxide. The second monomer is dipropylamine. The third monomer is 4-pentenoic acid. The fourth monomer is 5-phenyl-1-pentene alcohol and the fifth monomer is 6-chloro-1-hexene. The ratio of the first monomer and the second monomer is 3-5: 12-16. The ratio of the first intermediate and the third monomer is 10-20: 8-12. The ratio of the fourth monomer and the fifth monomer is 25-30: 10-14. The ratio of the second intermediate and the third intermediate is 20-28:30-40.Preferred Method: The method comprises adding the first monomer and the second monomer into the first solvent and reacting at 60-90degrees Celsius for not less than 3 hours to obtain the first intermediate, where the first solvent comprises toluene, diethyl ether, dichloromethane, dimethylformamide and/or dimethyl sulfoxide. The method comprises adding the first intermediate and the third monomer to the second solvent, adding 98% concentrated sulfuric acid and reacting at 100-140degrees Celsius for 1-5 hours to obtain second intermediate, where the second solvent comprises toluene, xylene, dichloromethane and/or tetrahydrofuran. The method comprises adding the fourth monomer to ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, adding sodium hydroxide solution at 50-90degrees Celsius, adding the fifth monomer drop wise and reacting at 60-100degrees Celsius for 20-28 hours to obtain third intermediate. The method comprises adding the second intermediate and the third intermediate into a third solvent under the protection of non-active gas, adding initiator, reacting at 50-90degrees Celsius for 1-5 hours to obtain the oil displacement agent, where the third solvent comprises hexane, benzene, toluene, xylene, ether and/or acetone. The method comprises mixing the supercritical carbon dioxide and the oil displacement agent in a ratio of (100-1000): 1, injecting into the oil production well at an injection rate of 3-7 t/hour, stewing well for 24 hours and producing the oil and gas mixture.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. PN KR2064612-B1
TI Method for controlling water content during carbon dioxide purification process, involves performing water removal process, and controlling internal temperature of phase separator by refrigerant flow rate and/or refrigerant temperature.
AU BAE H
AE YUJIN CHEM CO LTD (YUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202004790X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing a water removal process. A dew point is raised by compressing carbon dioxide source gas containing water. Moisture is condensed by cooling the carbon dioxide source gas containing water. The compressed and cooled carbon dioxide source gas is flash-expanded into a phase separator (30) to separate condensed water into carbon dioxide feed gas. Pressure in an inner side of the phase separator is controlled by a rear pressure control valve (40) that is installed in a supply gas outlet of the phase separator. Internal temperature of the phase separator is controlled by refrigerant flow rate and/or refrigerant temperature.
   USE - Method for controlling a water content during a carbon dioxide purification process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling the internal temperature of the phase separator by refrigerant flow rate and/or refrigerant temperature so as to improve water removal efficiency, provide characteristic to stably and constantly control a water content contained in the purified carbon dioxide, provide pressure control effect during a primary water removal process, improve moisture removal efficiency. The method enables maintaining homeostasis of the moisture content in exhaust gas under pressure and temperature control so as to maintain operation replacement cycle of a filling adsorption dryer in a constant manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for controlling a water content during a carbon dioxide purification process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating a method for controlling a water content during a carbon dioxide purification process. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Compressor (10)
   Cooler (20)
   Phase separator (30)
   Rear pressure control valve (40)
   Adsorption dryer (50)

PN JP2019035102-A
TI Carbon monoxide manufacturing system has reactor to perform reverse shift reaction to produce carbon monoxide and water from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, by introducing cathode off gas exhausted from cathode of electrolyzer.
AU KUSHI T
   DOKO T
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 2019229006
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon monoxide manufacturing system (10A) has an electrolyzer (24) which is provided with an anode (24B) and a cathode (24A) to perform the electrolysis process. A carbon-dioxide supply unit (12) supplies a carbon dioxide to the cathode. A water supply unit (P2) supplies water to the cathode. A reactor (26) performs a reverse shift reaction to produce carbon monoxide and water from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, by introducing the cathode off gas (G1) exhausted from the cathode of the electrolyzer. A separation unit separates the components other than carbon monoxide containing water from the reverse shift off gas (G2) discharged from the reactor. A water circulation path (P8) supplies the water separated in the separation unit to the cathode. The separation unit further separates carbon dioxide from reverse shift off gas. A carbon dioxide supply path (P1) supplies separated carbon dioxide to the cathode.
   USE - Carbon monoxide manufacturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon deposition on the stack of electrolyzer is suppressed. The generation of the carbon monoxide is improved efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon monoxide manufacturing system has the separation unit that separates components other than carbon monoxide including water from the reverse shift-off gas in a gas phase state by a separation membrane. A heat exchange unit performs the heat exchange between water supplied to the cathode and reverse shift off gas upstream of the separation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon monoxide manufacturing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon monoxide manufacturing system (10A)
   Carbon-dioxide supply unit (12)
   Electrolyzer (24)
   Cathode (24A)
   Anode (24B)
   Reactor (26)
   Cathode off gas (G1)
   Reverse shift off gas (G2)
   Carbon dioxide supply (P1)
   Water circulation path (P8)

PN US9914644-B1
TI Separating carbon dioxide from seawater comprises e.g. increasing concentration of dissolved inorganic carbon in volume of seawater, and acidifying formed modified seawater by flowing modified seawater into acidified solution compartment.
AU EISAMAN M
AE X DEV LLC (XDEV-C)
GA 201819758N
AB    NOVELTY - Method-m1 for separating carbon dioxide from seawater comprises: increasing a concentration of dissolved inorganic carbon in a volume of seawater to produce modified seawater; acidifying modified seawater by flowing modified seawater into an acidified solution compartment in an electrodialysis unit (110); flowing seawater from a second volume of seawater into a basified solution compartment in the unit at the same time as flowing the modified seawater into acidified solution compartment, where the second volume is unmodified; and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide from acidified seawater.
   USE - The methods are useful for separating carbon dioxide from seawater (claimed), where the carbon dioxide is useful as a fuel source.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods are energy efficient, and reduce a concentration of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method-m2 for separating carbon dioxide from seawater comprising removing a portion of water molecules from a volume of seawater comprising an amount of dissolved inorganic carbon to form modified seawater, after removing a portion of water molecules from the volume of seawater, introducing the modified seawater into an acidified solution compartment in an electrodialysis unit, acidifying the seawater in the acidified solution compartment, at the same time as introducing the modified seawater into the acidified solution compartment, introducing the seawater from a second volume of the seawater into a basified solution compartment in the electrodialysis unit, where the second volume of the seawater is unmodified, and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide from the acidified seawater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic side view of a system operable to extract or otherwise remove carbon dioxide from seawater.
   System (100)
   Electrodialysis unit (110)
   Vessel (120)
   Cooling vessel (121)
   Conduit (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method-m1: increasing a concentration of dissolved inorganic carbon in the seawater comprises heating the seawater; heating the seawater comprises exposing the seawater to low grade heat and exposing the seawater to solar thermal energy; prior to acidifying the modified seawater, the method comprises cooling the modified seawater; after acidifying the modified seawater, the method comprises transferring the acidified seawater to a tank, and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide from the acidified seawater comprises collecting the carbon dioxide that escapes from the acidified seawater in the tank; prior to transferring the acidified seawater to the tank, the method comprises evacuating the tank of air; and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide comprises vacuum stripping the carbon dioxide from the acidified seawater. In the method-m2: removing a portion of water molecules from a volume of seawater comprises heating the seawater; heating the seawater comprises exposing the seawater to low grade heat and exposing the seawater to solar thermal energy; prior to introducing the seawater into an electrodialysis unit, the method comprises cooling the modified seawater; after acidifying the modified seawater, the method comprises transferring the acidified seawater to a tank, and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide from the acidified seawater comprises collecting the carbon dioxide that escapes from the acidified seawater in the tank; prior to transferring the acidified seawater to the tank, the method comprises evacuating the tank of air; and collecting an amount of carbon dioxide comprises vacuum stripping the carbon dioxide from the acidified seawater.

PT P
PN CN111665218-A; CN111665218-B
TI Method for increasing inversion precision of carbon dioxide differential absorption laser radar, involves establishing conditional adjustment model to obtain optimal carbon dioxide optical thickness value for each layer.
AU SHI T
   MA X
   HAN G
   PEI Z
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202092414D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inverting optical thickness of each layer of carbon dioxide according to measured signal of a differential absorption laser radar. The inverted optical thickness of each layer of carbon dioxide is optimized according to Chebyshev fitting. Total carbon dioxide optical thickness value is calculated between lower signal receiving position and a boundary layer as limiting condition. A conditional adjustment model is established by utilizing signal-to-noise ratio of each layer as weight to obtain optimal carbon dioxide optical thickness value for each layer.
   USE - Method for increasing inversion precision of a carbon dioxide differential absorption laser radar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring high increasing stability, reducing limitations of carbon dioxide inversion accuracy to hardware parameters so as to obtain accurate data for research of carbon circulation and climate change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for increasing inversion precision of carbon dioxide differential absorption laser radar. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN112176360-A; CN112176360-B
TI Preparing synthesis gas by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, involves loading carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst on gas diffusion electrode main body.
AU MAO S
   FU J
   LI H
   LI Z
   WANG D
   LV X
   CHEN X
   GUO B
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202107368N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises using H-type double-electrochemical cell reactor, separating middle of H-type double-electrochemical cell reactor into cathode chamber and anode chamber by proton exchange membrane; introducing carbon dioxide gas into cathode chamber before reaction; using three-electrode system; using gas diffusion electrode as working electrode; platinum electrode as auxiliary electrode; silver/silver chloride electrode as reference electrode; working electrode opposite to auxiliary electrode; reference electrode close to working electrode; adding electrolyte; cathode chamber of working electrode is magnetically stirred; applying working voltage; reaction is carried out at room temperature; gas diffusion electrode containing gas diffusion electrode main body; and carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst loaded on gas diffusion electrode main body.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis gas by electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improves Faraday efficiency of product synthesis gas, and accurately controls the ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst comprises graphite-phase carbon nitride carrying gold-based double metal. The gold-based double metal comprises nickel, silver, iron, cobalt, copper, zinc, indium or metal of two metals. The gas diffusion electrode body comprises carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst is specifically prepared by using sodium borohydride as reducing agent, trisodium citrate as stabilizer, tetrahydrate chloroauric acid as precursor of gold, respectively using nickel acetate tetrahydrate, silver nitrate, ferrous acetate, cobalt acetylacetonate, copper acetylacetonate, zinc acetate dihydrate, indium chloride as second metal element nickel, silver, iron, cobalt, copper, zinc, and indium, precursor of indium, graphite-phase carbon nitride as carrier, and adopting liquid phase reduction method to obtain a gold-based bimetallic catalyst supported by graphite phase carbon nitride. The gas diffusion electrode is specifically prepared by (i) dispersing the electrochemical reduction catalyst of carbon dioxide into a mixture of isopropanol and deionized water, and adding Nafion (RTM: Tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoro-3,6-dioxa-4-methyl-7- octenesulfonic acid copolymer) solution to obtain a mixed solution, and (ii) coating the mixed solution on a gas diffusion electrode main body, and drying to obtain the gas diffusion electrode loaded with carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst.

GA 202230699Q

   (2) manufacture of the metal oxide composite, which involves preparing a precursor aqueous solution containing a solution of steel slag dissolved in an acidic aqueous solution and an organic structure defining agent corresponding to mesopores, forming a precipitate in the precursor aqueous solution, and forming the oxide composite; and

Preferred Conditions: The precursor aqueous solution is heated at room temperature-200 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic aqueous solution is an aqueous solution of an organic acid. The organic structure defining agent is a nonionic surfactant.

TI Method for fireproof sealing detection of cable shaft at room temperature, involves releasing carbon dioxide to certain concentration through gas cylinders on floor to be detected, and detecting carbon dioxide concentration in floor.
AU LIU Y
   LI W
   DENG Y
AE UNIV CHINA THREE GORGES (UYCR-C)
GA 202142490G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a fire model of cable shaft, and selecting carbon dioxide as the main standard for the fireproof sealing detection under constrained conditions. The standard is obtained for the fireproof sealing detection at room temperature according to the carbon dioxide concentration diffusion law at room temperature. The carbon dioxide is released to a certain concentration through gas cylinders on the floor to be detected. The carbon dioxide concentration is detected in the floor where it is located through the carbon dioxide detection extension, and the data is sent to the monitoring host. The monitoring host is configured to analyze and process the received data, the change curve of the current carbon dioxide concentration is recorded with time, and the data is analyzed through the analysis software to judge whether qualified.
   USE - Method for fireproof sealing detection of cable shaft at room temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The interval average value method is adopted to reduce the error, when the above monitoring host records the carbon dioxide concentration detection value sent by each carbon dioxide detection extension.

AU DICENZO A M
   BRUSCHI J P
   PARLETT J G
   GALASSO D A
   CROOKS T H
AE BOEING CO (BOEI-C)
GA 2019115656
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (400) has a set of air moving devices (416) generating a flow of atmospheric air into the apparatus. A condenser (402) removes water from the flow of atmospheric air. A desiccant (404) removes additional water from the flow of atmospheric air to produce dry air. A contactor chamber (406) adsorbs carbon dioxide from the dry air to a molecular sieve material in the contactor chamber. A vacuum chamber (408) evacuates the adsorbed carbon dioxide from the contactor chamber and transitions the evacuated carbon dioxide from a gas to a solid. The vacuum chamber comprises a cold finger (424) cooled to a temperature sufficient to transition the evacuated carbon dioxide from the gas to the solid, where the cold finger is cooled by flowing a coolant through the cold finger.
   USE - Apparatus for collecting carbon dioxide atmospheric air for enhanced oil recovery (EOR) application.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can be implemented or located at or near an existing pipeline, thus reducing transportation and pipeline costs.

PN CN113175687-A; CN214700775-U
TI Flue gas carbon dioxide trapping and purifying            system, has condenser used for sublimation and            separation of carbon dioxide gas and impurity gas            remaining in mixed gas, where condenser returns            impurity gas in mixed gas in gaseous form.

   WANG X
   DU Q
   MENG X

GA 2021882666
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide separator (4)                used to separate carbon dioxide in flue gas, and                output liquid carbon dioxide and mixed gas, where                liquid carbon dioxide output from the carbon                dioxide separator is pumped into a carbon dioxide                storage tank (10) through a booster pump (9-1), and                liquid carbon dioxide output from the carbon                dioxide storage tank is cooled by a refrigerant                heat exchanger (3-2). A rectification tower (11) is                used for rectifying and purifying liquid carbon                dioxide to generate purified liquid carbon dioxide.                A pressure regulating valve (8-2) is provided on a                pipeline between the carbon dioxide storage tank                and the refrigerant heat exchanger. A condenser (6)                is used for sublimation and separation of carbon                dioxide gas and impurity gas remaining in the mixed                gas. The condenser returns the impurity gas in the                mixed gas in a gaseous form to a gas heat exchanger                (5) and discharges the mixed gas after cooling                down.
   USE - Flue gas carbon dioxide trapping and purifying                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system avoids the problems of high energy                consumption when the physical separation operation                is used for capturing carbon dioxide in                low-concentration flue gas and environmental                pollution caused by the chemical absorption                operation. The low-concentration carbon dioxide                flue gas is physically separated from carbon and                carbon dioxide at a low temperature through a                carbon dioxide separator after the flue gas is                cooled down twice through the cold water heat                exchanger group and the second refrigerant heat                exchanger in turn. The liquid carbon dioxide and                the mixed gas are separated, and the residual                carbon dioxide gas in the mixed gas is purified so                as to realize the purification of low-concentration                carbon dioxide flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a flue                gas carbon dioxide trapping and purifying                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a flue                gas carbon dioxide trapping and purifying system.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is electrochemically reduced by (a) contacting carbon-dioxide-containing gas stream with capture solvent to absorb carbon dioxide from carbon-dioxide-containing gas stream to form carbon-dioxide-rich capture solvent; (b) introducing a portion of carbon-dioxide-rich capture solvent into cathode compartment of electrochemical cell; (c) applying electrical potential between anode and cathode in electrochemical cell sufficient for cathode to reduce carbon dioxide into reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture in the carbon-dioxide-rich capture solvent to provide carbon-dioxide-poor capture solvent; (d) collecting reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture; and (e) optionally, recirculating a portion of carbon-dioxide-poor capture solvent to an absorber. Anode is separated from cathode by semi-permeable separator; absolute pressure in electrochemical cell is preferably 20-138 bar; and temperature in electrochemical cell is preferably greater than or equal to 0 degrees C.
   USE - Electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The capital investments of the method are reduced without sacrificing production rates. The capture and electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide may be performed with the same fluid medium. Carbon dioxide may be electrochemically converted to valuable chemical compounds. Separation and collection of the valuable chemical compounds is more easily achieved than from water. The capture and reduction of carbon dioxide requires less operational units, where lower capital investment is required. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream does not need to be purified. Carbon dioxide capture and reduction at elevated absolute pressures allow for high current density, which results in lower capital investments required. The capture of carbon dioxide by a capture solvent and electrochemical reduction of the absorbed carbon dioxide advantageously results in a cost, energy, and resource efficient process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprising absorber unit and electrochemical cell connected to absorber unit, where electrochemical cell is operative to reduce carbon dioxide from capture solvent and comprises greater than or equal to 2 compartments separated by a semi-permeable separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Carbon-dioxide-containing gas stream is introduced in absorber unit under an absolute pressure of greater than or equal to 1 bar, and carbon dioxide is selectively absorbed by capture solvent. At least one salt in a non-aqueous solution or at least one salt in an aqueous solution is added to the cathodic compartment and/or anodic compartment to improve electrical conductivity. Preferred Conditions: Contacting of carbon-dioxide-containing gas stream with capture solvent is performed at greater than or equal to 0 degrees C. Each of steps (a)-(d) is performed at an absolute pressure of 1-200 bar, preferably 20-138 bar. Preferred Devices: Electrochemical cell comprises a cathodic compartment and an anodic compartment. Cathode comprises an electronically conducting metal electrocatalyst. Apparatus further compries stripper unit and/or separation unit connected to the electrochemical cell. Electrochemical cell comprises greater than or equal to 1 electrodes that are heated.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preferred Components: Capture solvent comprises greater than or equal to 1 physical solvent, greater than or equal to 1 aqueous physical solvent, greater than or equal to 1 chemical solvent, or a mixture of greater than or equal to 1 physical solvent and greater than or equal to 1 chemical solvent. Physical solvent comprises dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, methanol, ammonia, and/or propylene carbonate, preferably mixture of various dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol. Chemical solvent comprises aqueous solution of 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, tert.-amine methyldiethanolamine, and/or ammonia. Reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture comprises carbon monoxide, alkanes, alkenes, alcohols, carboxylic acids, and their salts, such as formates, oxalates, and acetates, aldehydes, and/or ketones.

AE CHINA CHEM HUALU NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (CHCH-Non-standard)
GA 202168827W
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving method for extracting                supercritical carbon dioxide involves performing                heat exchange reaction of liquid carbon dioxide in                extraction heat exchanger (005) through compressed                high-temperature liquid refrigerant (020), heating                liquid carbon dioxide, introducing obtained                supercritical carbon dioxide into extracting kettle                (006), performing heat exchange reaction of                supercritical carbon dioxide to form gaseous carbon                dioxide (018), introducing gaseous refrigerant into                condenser (010), introducing gaseous carbon dioxide                into separating kettle (008), performing heat                exchange condensation with gaseous refrigerant,                condensing into liquid carbon dioxide (019),                performing extraction circulation under carbon                dioxide booster pump through bypass pipeline (003),                absorbing heat of carbon dioxide by gaseous                refrigerant and entering into compressor (012), and                adjusting flow by regulating valve group (013) to                enter into extraction heat exchanger and separation                heat exchanger.
   USE - Energy-saving method for extracting                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing more than                20% device investment, and can reduce energy                consumption of more than 40%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy-saving method for extracting                supercritical carbon dioxide involves storing                liquid carbon dioxide in indoor storage tank (001),                pressurizing liquid carbon dioxide by carbon                dioxide booster pump (002), measuring by flow meter                (004), transporting to extraction heat exchanger,                performing heat exchange reaction of liquid carbon                dioxide in the extraction heat exchanger through                compressed high-temperature liquid refrigerant,                heating liquid carbon dioxide to undergo phase                change to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide,                cooling high-temperature liquid refrigerant,                vaporizing liquid refrigerant under the action of                throttle expansion valve (011), passing gaseous                refrigerant into condenser, introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide into extracting kettle                (006), extracting extract in the extracting kettle                by supercritical carbon dioxide, decompressing and                expanding supercritical carbon dioxide, entering                into separating heat exchanger (007), performing                heat exchange reaction of supercritical carbon                dioxide carrying the extract and high-temperature                liquid refrigerant to form gaseous carbon dioxide,                cooling liquid refrigerant by separating heat                exchanger, vaporizing under the action of throttle                expansion valve, introducing gaseous refrigerant                into the condenser, introducing gaseous carbon                dioxide containing the extract into separating                kettle for gas-liquid separation, collecting the                extract at bottom of the separating kettle and                discharging through extract discharging valve (009)                at bottom of the separating kettle, introducing the                carbon dioxide separated into the condenser,                performing heat exchange condensation with the                gaseous refrigerant, condensing into liquid carbon                dioxide, performing extraction circulation again                under the push of the carbon dioxide booster pump                through bypass pipeline, absorbing heat of the                carbon dioxide by gaseous refrigerant and entering                into compressor, compressing by compressor to                obtain high-temperature liquid refrigerant, and                adjusting flow by regulating valve group to enter                into the extraction heat exchanger and the                separation heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the                energy-saving method for extracting supercritical                carbon dioxide.
   Indoor storage tank (001)
   Carbon dioxide booster pump (002)
   Bypass pipeline (003)
   Flow meter (004)
   Extraction heat exchanger (005)
   Extracting kettle (006)
   Separation heat exchanger (007)
   Separating kettle (008)
   Extract discharging valve (009)
   Condenser (010)
   Throttle expansion valve (011)
   Compressor (012)
   Regulating valve group (013)
   Gaseous carbon dioxide (018)

PN BR102016024561-A2
TI Carbon dioxide separation and injection system comprises mixture of aqueous solution with carbon dioxide and source of gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide, and heat exchanger module.
AU RIBEIRO LIMA P C
AE RIBEIRO L P C (RIBE-Individual)
GA 202045756H
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide separation and injection system (100) comprises a mixture of aqueous solution with carbon dioxide and a source of gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide (110), and heat exchanger module (125). A hull section (120) is provided with carbon dioxide gas, liquid and hydrates. Another hull section is provided with predominantly unreacted gases. A third hull section is provided with predominantly saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution. A fourth section is provided with saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution and carbon dioxide hydrates. Multiple tubes are provided for heat exchange. A rear head expansion valve is provided for reducing pressure of a mixture of carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution. A pipeline is provided to transport non-reactive gasses (130) from the top of the hull to a processing or transport unit for natural gas.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation and injection system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation and injection system improves production or underground geological formation to sequester carbon dioxide from the rear head, and reduces risks of plumbing and eruptions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide separation and injection system (100) comprises a mixture of aqueous solution with carbon dioxide and a source of gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide (110), and heat exchanger module (125). A hull section (120) is provided with carbon dioxide gas, liquid and hydrates. Another hull section is provided with predominantly unreacted gases. A third hull section is provided with predominantly saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution. A fourth section is provided with saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution and carbon dioxide hydrates. Multiple tubes are provided for heat exchange. A rear head expansion valve is provided for reducing pressure of a mixture of carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution. A pipeline is provided to transport non-reactive gasses (130) from the top of the hull to a processing or transport unit for natural gas. A high-pressure solution line is provided to transport saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution from the third section to a hydrocarbon reservoir or to an underground geological formation. A low-pressure solution line is provided to transport aqueous solution from the rear head to a pumping or processing unit in hydraulic communication with a hydrocarbon reservoir or an underground geological formation. A carbon dioxide line is provided to transport a carbon dioxide stream from the rear head to a hydrocarbon reservoir. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for separating and injecting carbon dioxide, which involves:
   (A) using heat exchanger having hull, tubes, front and rear heads;
   (B) injecting a mixture of aqueous solution at high pressure with carbon dioxide and a gas stream containing carbon dioxide;
   (C) forming carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution in the hull section;
   (D) segregating mixture of carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution, saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution and non-reactive gasses in the hull;
   (E) creating another section above the former section predominantly with unreacted gases;
   (F) creating a third section near and below the gas-liquid interface on the right side of the hull;
   (G) transporting the unreacted gasses from the latter hull section to the gas pipeline;
   (H) transporting the mixture of carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution from the fourth hull section to an expansion valve;
   (I) reducing the pressure of the mixture of carbon dioxide hydrates and saturated aqueous carbon dioxide solution;
   (J) transporting the low pressure carbon dioxide stream from the top of the bottom section of the rear head to a compression system in hydraulic communication with a hydrocarbon reservoir or with an underground geological formation; and
   (K) transporting the aqueous solution with low pressure carbon dioxide from the bottom of the bottom section of the rear head to a pumping system in hydraulic communication with a hydrocarbon reservoir.
   The jet pump or ejector is used to provide mixture of water and carbon dioxide with the gas stream containing carbon dioxide for the former section. The gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide and methane from a primary processing plant is configured to receive fluids from a hydrocarbon reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the heat exchanger.
   Carbon dioxide separation and injection system (100)
   Gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide (110)
   Hull section (120)
   Heat exchanger module (125)
   Non-reactive gases (130)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2018219992-A1; TW201902827-A
TI Hydrogenating carbon dioxide, comprises reacting carbon dioxide with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst containing nickel- and magnesium-containing spinels.
AU LIZANDARA C
   KAUFMAN RECHULSKI M D
   SCHUNK S A
   MILANOV A
   WASSERSCHAFF G
   HOFFMANN M
   KAUFMAN R M D
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 201896870E
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenating carbon dioxide, comprises reacting carbon dioxide with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst containing nickel- and magnesium-containing spinels, where reactant gas has a molar fraction of carbon dioxide of 10-60%, a molar fraction of hydrogen of 10-90% and a molar fraction of water vapor of 0-15%, and the molar ratio of methane in the educt gas is 0-10%.
   USE - The method is useful for hydrogenating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes sing catalysts which can be prepared easily with cost-effective starting materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises reacting the reactant gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst containing nickel and magnesium containing spinels, where the pressure of the reactant gas in contact with the catalyst is 1-100 bar(abs), preferably 10-30 bar(abs) and the temperature of the reactant gas when in contact with the catalyst is 20-1400 degrees C, preferably 850-1200 degrees C, the gas hourly space velocity of the method has a value of 1000-1000000 h-1, preferably 10000-300000 h-1 and the synthesis gas produced has hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of 0.1-10, preferably 1.5:3. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 5-30 mol.%, preferably 10-20 mol.% a nickel, 10-40 mol.%, preferably 20-35 mol.% magnesium and 40-70 mol.%, preferably 50-65 mol% aluminum. The catalyst comprises nickel crystallites smaller than 200 nm and magnesium spinel crystallites smaller than 200 nm, which are dispersed in a highly dispersed and homogeneous manner on the carrier. The catalyst has an intensity ratio of the diffraction reflectances I(43.15 degrees )/I(44.83 degrees C), measured with a copper-K( alpha ) radiation of 0.3-1, preferably 0.7-0.92. The catalyst has Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of 10-200 m2/g, preferably 10-70 m2/g, an at least bimodal pore structure comprising mesopores having a pore diameter of 1-100 nm and macropores having a pore diameter of 1-300 mu m. The volume of the macropores is 0.05-0.5 ml/g and the volume of the mesopores is 0.1-0.4 ml/g.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109441573-A; US2020141282-A1; US10830107-B2; CN109441573-B
TI Processing zero carbon emission natural gas combined power generation useful for peak shaving comprises e.g. drawing air from the outside atmosphere by compressor and pressurizing through axial compressor, and preheating liquid oxygen.
AU TIAN Y
   QIAO Y
   XIE K
   SUN L
   LIU R
   HU L
   YANG Z
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2019248118
AB    NOVELTY - Processing zero carbon emission natural gas combined power generation comprises e.g. drawing air from the outside atmosphere by compressor and pressurizing to 0.4-0.8 MPa through axial compressor, increases air temperature for liquid oxygen preheating, 0.4-0.8 MPa pressurized air enters air separation unit performs air separation to obtain liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen, using pumped pressurized liquid oxygen for air and supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchange cooling and power generation, and using the pumped pressurized liquid nitrogen as the flue gas coolant, storing the liquid nitrogen and partial liquid oxygen at night, used for peak daytime use; exchanging high-pressure vaporized oxygen and high-pressure circulating carbon dioxide by supercritical carbon dioxide cooler, mixing with injected natural gas, then high temperature and high pressure combustion flue gas enters turbine to expand and work, and pushing the turbine to drive the compressor and the generator to rotate.
   USE - The method is useful for peak shaving (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing zero carbon emission natural gas combined power generation comprises drawing air from the outside atmosphere by compressor and pressurizing to 0.4-0.8 MPa through an axial compressor, increases the air temperature for liquid oxygen preheating; 0.4-0.8 MPa pressurized air enters air separation unit performs air separation to obtain liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen, using the pumped pressurized liquid oxygen for air and supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchange cooling and power generation, and using the pumped pressurized liquid nitrogen as the flue gas coolant after heat exchange vaporized power generation, storing the liquid nitrogen and partial liquid oxygen at night and used for peak daytime use; exchanging the high-pressure vaporized oxygen and high-pressure circulating carbon dioxide by supercritical carbon dioxide cooler, mixing with injected natural gas, then high temperature and high pressure combustion flue gas enters the turbine to expand and work, and pushing the turbine to drive the compressor and the generator to rotate at a high speed, partially converting the chemical energy of the natural gas into mechanical work and the electric work is output; passing the high temperature combustion flue gas through the carbon dioxide, the waste heat boiler heat exchanger performs supercritical carbon dioxide power generation, and the coolant is pressurized liquid oxygen, after cooling, circulating the supercritical carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide compressor; after the exchange, exchanging the flue gas with the pressurized liquid nitrogen, and the liquid nitrogen vaporization is promoted nitrogen the turbine generator generates electricity, and the liquid flue gas is separated and recovered by the distillation tower after the flue gas is dehydrated, returning the carbon dioxide to the gas turbine feed for cyclic temperature control, and using portion of carbon dioxide for the supercritical carbon dioxide power generation, partially sold as liquid carbon dioxide product; during the peak period of power grid operation in the daytime, storing the night and day separated pumping pressurized by liquid nitrogen heat exchange evaporation generating storage, night and day separated liquid oxygen pumping pressure and heat after being vaporized by the change in load of the gas turbine for peak power.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The air separation is one of a stepped air separation, a step separation air separation of a pressure swing adsorption separation and a cryogenic separation, and a cascade air separation of a membrane separation and/or cryogenic separation. The supercritical carbon dioxide power generation is one of supercritical carbon dioxide power generation modes of recompression cycle, sectional expansion cycle, pre-pressure cycle and partial cooling cycle. The supercritical carbon dioxide pressure is 7-40 MPa. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of oxygen to recycled carbon dioxide is 1:2-12.

PT P
PN CN114031811-A
TI Desulfurization of waste tires by supercritical            carbon dioxide comprises using supercritical carbon            dioxide as reaction medium, under specific temperature            and pressure conditions, swelling tire and bringing            desulfurizer into tire, reacting with backbone or            cross-links to remove sulfur element.
AU WANG N
   XU R
   CHEN B
   DAI C
   YU G
   WANG X
   LIU N
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202229341F
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization of waste tires by                supercritical carbon dioxide comprises using                supercritical carbon dioxide as the reaction                medium, under specific temperature and pressure                conditions, swelling the tire and bringing the                desulfurizer into the tire utilizing the solubility                and permeability, reacting with the backbone or                cross-links in the cross-linked network structure                to remove the sulfur element from the interior of                the tire.
   USE - The method is useful for desulfurization of                waste tires by supercritical carbon dioxide
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique process,                strong process controllability and simple                operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The desulfurizing                  agent is diphenyl disulfide,                  2,2'-dibenzoylaminodiphenyl disulfide,                  tetramethylthiuram disulfide or                  N-cyclohexyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenamide.Preferred Method: The reaction is carried                  out at 150-350&#8451;, under pressure                  5-25MPa, for 1-5 hours, and the amount of the                  desulfurizer is 5-20% of the mass of the waste                  tire when using diphenyl disulfide as the                  desulfurizer. The reaction is carried out at                  150-350&#8451;, under pressure 10MPa, for 4                  hours, and the amount of the desulfurizer is 100%                  of the mass of the waste tire when using,                  2'-dibenzoylaminodiphenyl disulfide as the                  desulfurizer.The reaction is carried out at                  150-350 oC, under pressure 10MPa, for 4 hours,                  and the amount of the desulfurizer is 10% of the                  mass of the waste tire when using,                  tetramethylthiuram disulfide as the desulfurizer.                  The reaction is carried out at                  150-350&#8451;, under pressure 10MPa, for 4                  hours, and the amount of the desulfurizer is 10%                  of the mass of the waste tire when using                  -cyclohexyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenamide as the                  desulfurizer. The waste tire desulfurization                  method, comprises (i) cutting tread rubber from                  waste tires, washing off the surface dust and                  cutting the tread rubber into small pieces,                  placing them in a crusher and pulverize them,                  sieving them after pulverization, and selecting                  tire particles with a particle size of 0.55-1 mm;                  (ii) stirring and mixing the tire particles and                  the desulfurizer uniformly; (iii) adding mixture                  into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor,                  pumping out the air in the reactor with a vacuum                  pump, and closing the pressure relief valve after                  draining; (iv) feeding carbon dioxide into the                  reactor afterwards, adjust the pressure in the                  reactor to be 5-20 MPa, pushing carbon dioxide                  into the reactor through a pressurized pump to                  reach a predetermined pressure value, and                  simultaneously heating up, and the setting                  temperature is 150-350&#8451;, by adjusting                  the temperature and pressure in the reactor to                  make the carbon dioxide in the reactor in a                  supercritical state; (v) fully reacting for 1-5                  hours after the reaction temperature and pressure                  reach the set value; and (vi) sampling the gas in                  the reactor after the reaction finishes and                  completing the sampling to depressurize and cool                  down.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.)

PT P
PN CN111718432-A; CN111718432-B; AU2020223672-B1
TI Coagulating natural rubber latex using high-pressure carbon dioxide by feeding carbon dioxide under airtight conditions, and coagulating natural rubber latex to obtain rubber clot.
AU LIN H
   DENG D
   SUN Z
   LI G
   WANG B
   ZHANG F
   XU K
   ZHONG J
   LIAO L
   PENG Z
AE AGRIC PROD PROCESSING RES INST CHINESE A (CTAG-C)
   AGRIC PROD PROCESSING RES INST CHINESE A (CTAG-C)
GA 202098395Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for coagulating natural rubber latex using high-pressure carbon dioxide, involves feeding carbon dioxide until the pressure is 10-30 MPa under airtight conditions, and coagulating the natural rubber latex to obtain a rubber clot.
   USE - The method is useful for coagulating natural rubber latex using high-pressure carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts fast coagulation speed, and is carried out in closed reactor, and retains non-water-soluble and non-gelatinous components in the latex such as protein and phosphate to the greatest extent, maintains the high performance of the product, does not damage the components in natural rubber latex, and maintains the original state, and the prepared natural rubber product has excellent quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The natural rubber latex is a natural rubber latex with an ammonia content of 0-0.45 %mass and a dry rubber content greater than or equal to 25 %mass. Preferred Conditions: The solidification is carried out under a constant temperature of 25-40 degrees C, where the constant temperature is achieved through a water bath environment. The sealing conditions are provided by the reactor. The pressure is maintained for 5-10 minutes. Preferred Method: The solidification is performed under static conditions, and leaving still for 1-5 hours. The method involves sequentially crushing the rubber agglomerate, after obtaining the rubber agglomerate, crimping, dehydrating, granulating, and drying at 60-100 degrees C to obtain the natural rubber product.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).)

PN CN110241153-A
TI Enzymolysis of cellulose based on supercritical            carbon dioxide system by stirring microcrystalline            cellulose, dextran modified cellulase and buffer            solution, and homogeneous enzymolysis reacting in            supercritical carbon dioxide system.
AU LI L
   YU S
   LI H
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201991746Y
AB    NOVELTY - Enzymolysis of cellulose based on                supercritical carbon dioxide system involves using                dextran modified cellulase to reduce amino group to                form dextran modified cellulase, uniformly stirring                microcrystalline cellulose, dextran modified                cellulase and buffer solution, and exposing to                supercritical carbon dioxide system for homogeneous                enzymolysis reacting at 50 degrees C for 24 hours                at 8.2 MPa of cellulose to directly obtain reducing                sugars.
   USE - The method us used for enzymolyzing cellulose                based on supercritical carbon dioxide system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can directly obtain reducing sugar                with high content, keeps modified cellulase high                catalytic activity and stability in reaction                process, and promotes degradation of                cellulose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular weight                  of dextran is 2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, or 40000.                  The microcrystalline cellulose has molecular                  weight of 2.5-5, polymerization degree of                  153-300, ash content of not less than 0.6% and                  grain diameter of 25-190 mu m. The buffer                  solution is citric acid-disodium hydrogen                  phosphate buffer solution, and has PH value of                  4.8 Preferred Method: The dextran modified                  cellulase is prepared by adding natural cellulose                  in buffer solution, completely dissolving, adding                  dextran, mixing, adding reducing agent sodium                  cyanoborohydride, reacting at 30-35 degrees C for                  24 hours, adding glycine to stop reaction, and                  dialyzing and purifying.

TI Recovering alkali metal catalyst from carbon dioxide-water-alcohol system comprises preparing mixed solution containing alkali metal-catalyzed gasification residue and water, and alcohol, soaking, and filtering.

AB    NOVELTY - Recovering alkali metal catalyst from carbon dioxide-water-alcohol system comprises preparing mixed solution containing alkali metal-catalyzed gasification residue and water, and alcohol, soaking at 40-220 degrees C and at 0.1-5 MPa for 5-120 minutes, and filtering.
   USE - Method for recovering alkali metal catalyst from carbon dioxide-water-alcohol system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly, increases solubility of carbon dioxide in water by adding alcohol to hydrothermal reaction system, increases acidity of carbon dioxide in hydrothermal dissociation process, accelerates rate and extent of insoluble alkali metal leaching in gasification residue, and has high alkali metal recovery rate and mild leaching condition.
