TI Carbon dioxide removal method involves using            carbon dioxide-promoting transport membrane with carbon            dioxide/oxygen selectivity and carbon dioxide/nitrogen            selectivity configured with porous membrane carrying            hydrophilic polymer layer.
AU OKADA O
   TERAMOTO M
   NONOUCHI T
   HANAI N
   MIYATA J
   KIYOHARA Y
   SAKURAI M
   FURUKAWA S
AE RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   JAPAN AEROSPACE EXPLORATION AGENCY (NIAE-C)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   JAPAN AEROSPACE EXPLORATION AGENCY (NIAE-C)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   JAPAN AEROSPACE EXPLORATION AGENCY (NIAE-C)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
   JAPAN AEROSPACE EXPLORATION AGENCY (NIAE-C)
   JAPAN AEROSPACE EXPLORATION AGENCY (NIAE-C)
   RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
GA 201957846K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying a processing                target gas containing carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                oxygen to supply side of carbon dioxide selective                permeable membrane. The water vapor is generated                and supplied to carbon dioxide selective permeable                membrane. The carbon dioxide in the gas to be                treated is selectively permeated to the oxygen and                nitrogen in the gas to be treated from the supply                side to the permeation side of the carbon dioxide                selective permeable membrane. The concentration of                carbon dioxide in the gas to be treated is 3 mol%                or less on a dry basis. The hydrophilic polymer                layer is provided with an amino acid and a                deprotonating agent to prevent the protonation of                the amino group of amino acid. The carbon                dioxide-promoting transport membrane (1) is                utilized with carbon dioxide/oxygen selectivity and                carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity configured with                a porous membrane carrying the hydrophilic polymer                layer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide removal method.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is selectively removed from                nitrogen and oxygen from the gas to be treated                containing nitrogen, oxygen and trace amounts of                carbon dioxide of 3 mol% or less. Thus, the carbon                dioxide concentration in the gas to be treated is                reduced. The loss of oxygen is suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide removal apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the test                equipment for pressure-reducing method to evaluate                the membrane performance of carbon                dioxide-promoting transport membrane. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide-promoting transport membrane                (1)
   Gas permeable cell (2)
   Supply side chamber (3)
   Bubbler (5)
   Demister (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The amino acid is selected from glycine,                  2,3-diaminopropionic acid, alanine, arginine,                  asparagine, serine, ornithine, creatine,                  threonine, and 2-aminobutyric acid.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide pressurization system for production of polyolefin foamed resin, includes a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank and a pneumatic piston booster pump booster connected to the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank.
AU ZHAO K
   CHEN Q
   ZHANG S
   LIU Q
   LIU F
AE NEW ZIYUAN SHANGHAI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (NEWZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020639934
AB    NOVELTY - The upercritical carbon dioxide pressurization system includes a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1) and a pneumatic piston booster pump booster connected to the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The carbon dioxide connected to the booster device and heater (3). A buffer tank (4) connected to the carbon dioxide heater and a second carbon dioxide heater (5) connected to the buffer tank. A control valve group (6) connected with a heater and a connecting pipeline of the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The supercharging device is provided with a first pipeline cooling layer and carbon dioxide filter. The liquid carbon dioxide storage tank has at least one safety valve, carbon dioxide gas outlet and carbon dioxide liquid outlet.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide pressurization system for production of polyolefin foamed resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts a specific pneumatic piston booster pump type booster device and the setting of the pipeline cooling layer. The piston is driven by compressed air to deliver carbon dioxide liquid which can be started and stopped at any time and the control pressure is stable which ensures the safety.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide pressurization system for production of polyolefin foamed resin.

   Heater (3)
   Buffer tank (4)
   Second carbon dioxide heater (5)
   Control valve group (6)

AE WISDRI ENG & RES INC LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 202285628Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model claims an electric furnace flue gas carbon dioxide capturing system, comprising a flue gas pre-processing device, a carbon dioxide capturing device, a carbon dioxide recovery device, a flue gas pre-processing device comprises a coarse dust removing device connected in turn, waste heat recycling device and a fine dust removing device, the coarse dust removing device is connected with the electric furnace through the flue, the carbon dioxide capturing device comprises a first combustion reaction device, a second combustion reaction device, an air inlet pipeline and an air discharge pipeline, two combustion reaction devices are connected with a carbon dioxide recycling device through the second reversing valve. The utility model model can effectively remove large particle dust and other small particle impurities in the flue gas, greatly reduce the impurity of the flue gas before entering the combustion reaction device, reduce the secondary reaction in the flue gas combustion, also reduce the by-product generation of the flue gas, ensure the purity of the carbon dioxide gas in the flue gas, at the same time, the service life of the oxygen carrier can be prolonged.

PT P
PN WO2022115923-A1; BR102020024670-A2
TI Separating carbon dioxide from a gas stream for            offshore oil production fields, comprises e.g. passing            gas stream containing carbon dioxide and methane,            adding carbonic anhydrase enzyme to the liquid, and            adjusting pH by sodium hydroxide.
AU CASTRO A M D
   MACHADO DE CASTRO A
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 2022791520
AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from a gas stream,                comprises (a) passing a gas stream in continuous                flow, containing carbon dioxide and methane in a                contactor module containing membranes, (b) adding                carbonic anhydrase enzyme to the absorbent liquid,                (c) passing the absorber liquid solution from step                (b) in the contactor module through a loop                recirculation system, in which the absorber liquid                solution and the gaseous stream operate in a                counter-current direction, and (d) adjusting pH of                the liquid absorbent solution using a solution of                sodium hydroxide to maintain the alkaline                environment when the pH is less than 9.
   USE - The method is useful for separating carbon                dioxide from a gas stream, which is useful in                offshore oil production fields and in onshore                natural gas or biogas processing units                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: promotes the efficient capture of                carbon dioxide and avoids its emission into the                atmosphere; converts carbonates also represents a                form of environmental contribution, as it consists                of a more permanent sequestration of carbon                dioxide; and leads to a reduction in the salinity                of the stream, representing a form of treatment                that can reduce impacts of its possible disposal on                the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process: the                  gas stream is natural gas or biogas; the gas                  stream contains 2-70% carbon dioxide; the                  absorber liquid is industrial water, sea water or                  production water; the production water is                  synthetic or natural; the absorber liquid is used                  with or without pre-treatment and conditioning,                  with or without the addition of promoter, amine,                  hydroxide or inorganic carbonate; the absorbent                  liquid solution in step (c) passes in continuous                  mode. Preferred Process: In the process: the pH                  is adjusted to 9.5-12; the liquid stream                  containing absorbed carbon dioxide is directed to                  a second unit, for recovery of carbon dioxide in                  gaseous form; and the carbon dioxide recovered in                  gaseous form is destined for processes of                  converting this gas into other molecules, or                  being directed to geological storage.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The process contains                  two contactor modules in series.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the process, the                  contactor membrane is PTFE, polyvinylidene                  fluoride, polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS),                  poly(tetrafluoroethylene-co-alkyl vinyl ether                  perfluorinated) ) (PFA), polypropylene (PP) or                  ceramic.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                capture of carbon dioxide from mixing with methane,                using 10 mM sodium hydroxide solution as absorber                that do not support the claims.

PN CN113149827-A
TI Preparing alkyne acid i.e. propiolic acid by using terminal alkyne and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting ethyne compound with carbon dioxide in presence of alkali and solvent, and then reacting with hydrochloric acid.
AU HU J
   XIANG G
   QI C
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021914090
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkyne acid i.e. propiolic acid (I) by using terminal alkyne and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting ethyne compound (II) with carbon dioxide in presence of alkali and solvent to obtain reaction liquid, and then reacting reaction liquid with hydrochloric acid to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alkyne acid by using terminal alkyne and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is carried out under low temperature and normal pressure conditions; does not requires metal catalyst; produces single product, which is convenient to separate; and has safe and simple operation, and excellent economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alkyne acid i.e. propiolic acid of formula (I) by using terminal alkyne and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting ethyne compound of formula (II) with carbon dioxide in presence of alkali and solvent to obtain reaction liquid, and then reacting reaction liquid with hydrochloric acid to obtain final product.
   R = phenyl, p-tolyl, p-ethylphenyl, p-propylphenyl, p-pentylphenyl, m-tolyl, m-fluorophenyl, m-chlorophenyl, m-bromophenyl, o-bromophenyl, p-bromophenyl, p-methoxyphenyl, p-ethoxyphenyl, p-pentoxyphenyl, 3,5-dimethoxyphenyl, p-cyanophenyl, 3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl, phenoxy, 1-naphthyl, 2-naphthyl, 4-biphenyl, 6-methoxy-2-naphthyl, 3-thienyl or p-ethynylphenyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding alkyne, alkali and organic solvent into a reaction vessel, mixing uniformly, heating and stirring under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and cooling to room temperature to obtain reaction liquid, and (ii) extracting the reaction liquid obtained in step (i), separating the liquids, taking the aqueous layer, acidifying, and separating and purifying to obtain (I). In step (i), the heating and stirring process is carried out at a stirring speed of 600-800 revolutions/minute at 40-70 degrees C for 6-36 hours. In step (ii): the extraction step includes adding water into the reaction liquid obtained in step (i) and extracting with ethyl acetate, separating the liquids and taking the aqueous layer; the acidification includes adjusting the pH of the aqueous layer to be acidic to obtain acidified aqueous layer; and the separation and purification includes: extracting the acidified aqueous layer with ethyl acetate again, separating the liquids, taking the organic layer, washing the layer with saturated salt water, drying, filtering the filtrate and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain alkyne acid. Preferred Components: In step (i), the organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone or acetonitrile; the alkali is cesium carbonate, potassium tert-butoxide, 4-dimethylaminopyridine, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, potassium carbonate or sodium hydroxide; and the molar ratio between alkali and (II) is 1.5-2.5:1. The pH value of the acidified aqueous layer is 1.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by a circulating jet type gas-liquid contact process comprises e.g. providing circulation spraying reactor by electrophilic-nucleophilic functional catalyst as catalyst and taking carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide.
AU REN W
   LI M
   LV X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019632221
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by a circulating jet type gas-liquid contact process comprises e.g. providing circulation spraying reactor by using electrophilic-nucleophilic functional catalyst as catalyst, taking carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw material and carrying out coupling reaction for preparing cyclic carbonic ester, where the specific reaction process is: adding catalyst in the circulation spraying reactor cyclic carbonate, heating the starting material to a reaction temperature by heat exchanger, filling carbon dioxide to reaction system pressure under reaction pressure, then introducing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in circulation spraying reactor, and maintaining the reaction pressure, then continuously reacting completely consumed to alkylene, spraying circulation of reaction material in reactor and transferring to flash tank, discharging carbon dioxide, vacuum extracting and distilling, and obtaining residual liquid of cyclic carbonate-containing catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by a circulating jet type gas-liquid contact process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the defect that reaction rate shows a sharp drop after the conversion rate of alkylene oxide reaches a level in the prior art; improves the efficiency of coupling carbon dioxide with alkylene oxide to prepare cyclic carbonate; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate by a circulating jet type gas-liquid contact process comprises providing circulation spraying reactor by using electrophilic-nucleophilic functional catalyst as catalyst at 0.2-5 MPa of the reaction pressure and reaction temperature of 25-180 degrees C, taking carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw material and carrying out coupling reaction for preparing cyclic carbonic ester, where the specific reaction process is: adding catalyst in the circulation spraying reactor cyclic carbonate, heating the starting material to a reaction temperature by heat exchanger, filling carbon dioxide to reaction system pressure under reaction pressure, then introducing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in circulation spraying reactor, and maintaining the reaction pressure, then continuously reacting completely consumed to alkylene, then spraying the circulation of reaction material in the reactor and transferring to the flash tank, discharging carbon dioxide, vacuum extracting and distilling, obtaining residual liquid of cyclic carbonate-containing catalyst as next reaction starting material to recycle, and the mass of alkylene oxide and catalyst is 50:1-50000:1, and the molar ratio of alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 1:1.

AU GAO X

GA 2020B7276T
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner (1) has a second carbon dioxide sensor that is configured to detect the concentration of carbon dioxide at different preset detection positions in the indoor space. The controller is configured to includes acquiring the carbon dioxide concentration set sent by the second carbon dioxide sensor when the air conditioner is first operated and the indoor space is closed. A reference compensation value for the carbon dioxide concentration is determined based on the carbon dioxide concentration set. The carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor space is determined based on the reference compensation value and the carbon dioxide concentration sampling value of the first carbon dioxide sensor. PN US2020306688-A1; WO2020205231-A2; WO2020205231-A3; US11266947-B2
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream, involves contacting gas stream comprising carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide-lean solvent stream to form treated gas stream and carbon dioxide-rich solvent stream, and heating solvent stream.
AU MALHOTRA D
   KOECH P K
   HELDEBRANT D J
   GLEZAKOU V
   ROUSSEAU R J
   NGUYEN M T
   PERRY R
   PAGE J P
   CANTU D C
AE BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
   BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
   BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
GA 202097084T
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide involves contacting a gas stream comprising an amount (a1) of carbon dioxide with a carbon dioxide-lean solvent stream comprising a diamine compound (I) to form a treated gas stream comprising an amount (a2) of carbon dioxide that is less than the amount (a1), and to form a carbon dioxide-rich solvent stream, and heating the carbon dioxide-rich solvent stream to form a carbon dioxide stream and regenerating the carbon dioxide-lean solvent, where compound (I) do not comprises compound (II) or (III).
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream e.g. exhaust gas discharged from single subcritical coal-fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables removal of carbon dioxide having excellent fluids resistance, and gradual deformation by shear stress or tensile stress.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removal of carbon dioxide involves contacting a gas stream comprising an amount (a1) of carbon dioxide with a carbon dioxide-lean solvent stream comprising a diamine compound of formula: R1(R2)N-L1-NH-R3 (I) to form a treated gas stream comprising an amount (a2) of carbon dioxide that is less than the amount (a1), and to form a carbon dioxide-rich solvent stream, and heating the carbon dioxide-rich solvent stream to form a carbon dioxide stream and regenerating the carbon dioxide-lean solvent, where compound (I) do not comprises compound of formula (II) or (III).
   R1,R2 = aliphatic group or cycloaliphatic group;
   R1+R2+N = heterocyclyl;
   L1 = R1;
   L1+R1+N = heterocyclyl;and
   R3 = R1, cycloalkylalkyl, or alkoxyalkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon-dioxide removing system, which comprises an absorption unit comprising a gas inlet, a solvent inlet (i1) and a solvent outlet (O1), a regeneration unit comprising a solvent inlet (i2) fluidly coupled to the solvent outlet (O2), and a solvent outlet (O2) fluidly coupled to the solvent inlet (i1), and a solvent comprising the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves contacting the gas stream with the carbon dioxide-lean solvent at a temperature (T1) of 30-80 degrees C, heating the carbon dioxide-rich solvent to a temperature (T2) of 80-160 degrees C, cooling the regenerated carbon dioxide-lean solvent to a temperature of 30-60 degrees C. Preferred Apparatus: The system further comprises a heat exchange unit fluidly coupled to the regeneration unit, and the absorption unit such that the heat exchange unit facilitates transfer of heat energy from the solvent stream leaving the regeneration unit and to the solvent stream entering the regeneration unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) comprises 16 compounds for e.g. compounds of formulae (IV)-(VIII). Preferred Composition: The solvent has a water content of 0.1 wt.% or less. Preferred Properties: The solvent has a vapor pressure of 1 Torr or less at 40 degrees C, and a viscosity of 75 cP or less at carbon dioxide-loading of 40 mol% and at a temperature of 40 degrees C.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1,R2 = 1-6C alkyl;
   R3 = 3-6C alkyl;and
   L1 = 2-4C alkyl.

TI Capturing method of carbon dioxide used for preparing hydrogen comprises injecting electrolyte into cathode and anode electrolytic cells, absorbing carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide, blowing air, and mixing alkaline and acidic solutions.
AU YUAN Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG T
   DUAN W
   ZHOU A
   SUN W
   OUYANG Z
   WANG Y
AE QINGDAO UPC ENVIRONMENT & SECURITY TECHN (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2022592274
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing method of carbon dioxide comprises injecting the same electrolyte into the cathode electrolytic cell and the anode electrolysis cell, connecting the two are connected through the salt bridge, connecting cathode and anode are connected with the power supply to electrolyze, generating hydrogen, at the same time, the solution is changed into alkaline solution, generating oxygen in the cathodic cell; introducing the flue gas after dust removing, reacting and absorbing carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide in the gas, until the adsorption is saturated, stopping introducing flue air; blowing air into the electrolytic tank, using oxygen to oxidize sulfur dioxide to dissolve sulfur dioxide-dissolved in the electrolyte, so that the sulfur dioxide is completely absorbed in the fluent gas; and mixing the alkaline liquid and the acidic liquid in the anodic cell to produce a neutralization reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide by electrolyzing water to prepare hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the oxidation effect of the air to solve the influence of sulfur dioxide on the carbon dioxide capturing process, the capturing process of carbon dioxide in the flue gas is not influenced by the particulate matter dioxide; uses the electrolytic water to generate oxygen and green energy hydrogen, rationally using the energy in the solvent regeneration process; and the electrolysis process can use the green electricity (solar power generation/wind energy to generate electricity), which avoids the indirect carbon dioxide emission in the regeneration process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for realizing the method.

PN WO2022074002-A1
TI Preparation of beer concentrate involves introducing beer into closed system while leaving headspace, and producing decarbonated beer by contacting gas phase that is present in headspace with carbon dioxide capturing material.
AU BROUWER E R
   BEKKERS A
AE HEINEKEN SUPPLY CHAIN BV (HETB-C)
GA 202250937G
AB    NOVELTY - A method of preparing beer concentrate involves (a) introducing beer into a closed system while leaving a headspace, (b) producing a decarbonated beer by contacting the gas phase that is present in the headspace with a carbon dioxide capturing (CDC) material to remove dissolved carbon dioxide from the beer and (c) producing a beer concentrate by removing water from the decarbonated beer by freeze concentration and/or membrane filtration. The beer contains 80-99 wt.% water, 0-16 wt% ethanol, and 0.5-8 mg/g dissolved carbon dioxide. The CDC material comprises a metal hydroxide comprising sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide and/or magnesium hydroxide. The gas phase is contacted with the CDC material by continuously removing gas phase from the headspace, transporting the removed gas phase to a chamber comprising the CDC material, and returning decarbonated gas phase from the chamber to the headspace.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a beer concentrate (claimed), where the beer is a universally popular beverage, consumed worldwide. The beer is used as a ready-to-drink beer by mixing the beer concentrate with carbonated water in a dispensing device and dispensing the beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of beer concentrates by minimizing the detrimental effect of carbon dioxide on the concentration step. Both the rate at which carbon dioxide is removed from the gas phase by the carbon dioxide capturing (CDC) material and the rate of replenishment from the beer are so high that carbon dioxide that remains in the beer after fermentation can be removed very rapidly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CDC material is an aqueous solution of the metal hydroxide. The CDC material is a granulate containing calcium hydroxide. The granulate comprises metal hydroxide selected from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and their combinations.

PN US2022007963-A1
TI Method for determining volume of exhaled carbon dioxide as function of time using side-stream capnography, involves synchronizing carbon dioxide concentration measurement with flow dynamics measurement in time.
AU RONEN M
   COLEMAN J L
AE COVIDIEN LP (COVI-C)
GA 202207364V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining flow dynamics measurements of a subject from a flow sensor (240). Carbon dioxide concentration measurements of the subject are obtained from a side-stream carbon dioxide monitor. Duration of time for a sample of gas to flow from a reference point (225) to the monitor (210) is determined based on a time until a notch in the concentration measurement. The concentration measurement is synchronized with the flow dynamics measurement based on the determined duration of time. A volume of carbon dioxide exhaled is determined as a function of time based on flow dynamics and synchronized CO2 concentration measurements in time.
   USE - Method for determining volume of exhaled carbon dioxide as a function of time using side-stream capnography for non-intubated populations.
   ADVANTAGE - The accurate side-stream volumetric capnography is enabled by calculating the time lag of the carbon dioxide concentration measurement, such that an accurate synchronization in time between flow dynamics and carbon dioxide concentrations is achieved. The accurate and/or sensitivity of the volume capnograph and its ability to provide insights into the ventilation physiology, such as dead space breathing, shunt and EtCO 2 to PaCO 2 gradients are enhanced, since performing the actual volume measurements at the carina removes a major part of the anatomical dead space and airway tubing dead space, and thus the accuracy and sensitivity are enhanced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for determining a volume of exhaled CO as a function of time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system.
   System (200)
   Patient (201)
   Monitor (210)
   Reference Point (225)
   Flow Sensor (240)

AU ZHANG Z
   YAN B
   ZHONG J
   LI X
   WANG X
   DONG S
   HUANG W
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202213559N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing double-functional mixed substrate                film with high carbon dioxide selectivity involves                preparing amino functionalized bentonite, taking                1-7 pts. wt. amino functionalized bentonite, 70-80                pts. wt. film mother agent in the microwave oven,                stirring and mixing uniformly, orderly adding 1-3                pts. wt. pore-forming agent and 15-20 pts. wt.                homogeneous polyvinylidene fluoride, under the                microwave radiation auxiliary mechanical stirring                for 0.5-2 hours, vacuum defoaming to obtain the                homogeneous casting solution, uniformly coating the                obtained casting solution on the clean glass plate                and putting into the coagulation water bath for                curing and forming, after the film is naturally                dropped from the glass plate, heat processing in                the vacuum oven to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing double-functional mixed                substrate film with high carbon dioxide selectivity                used in carbon dioxide/nitrogen dioxide, carbon                monoxide/carbon dioxide/chlorinated natural gas                separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a simple technique, low                cost, short time, greatly increasing gas permeation                selectivity, high efficient collecting CO2 with                double-functional mixed matrix film preparation                method, and the application in gas separation. The                bentonite is organically modified, cation in the                quaternary ammonium salt and the bentonite                interlayer cation generate exchange, the layered                crystal lattice is cracked, the crystal interlayer                distance is enlarged, the pore canal is dredged,                the cross-linking of bentonite and fluorine group                is increased, the carbon dioxide selectivity is                greatly increased and the performance is excellent,                which is suitable for CO 2/N2, CO 2 /CH 4                separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The amino functional                  silane is placed in the ethanol solution, fully                  stirred and dispersed to form solution A, the                  concentration is 0.5-2 mole/L, added organic                  bentonite into the ethanol, fully stirred and                  dispersed to form dispersion liquid B, mixed the                  solution A and dispersion B, mechanically stirred                  for a certain time, standing and centrifuged, and                  washed with ethanol, placed the washed product in                  blast drying box to dry to obtain amino                  functionalized bentonite, the amino functional                  silane is 3-amino propyl trimethoxy silane,                  3-amino propyl triethoxy silane or N-(small sharp                  s-aminoethyl)-aminopropyl triethoxy silane. The                  heat treatment temperature of the mixed matrix                  film in the vacuum oven is 40-60degrees Celsius                  and the processing time is 4-7 hours. The power                  of the microwave oven is 200-350W under the                  assistance of microwave radiation, The frequency                  range of the microwave is from 300-3000 KMHz                  Preferred Components: The film preparing mother                  agent is tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N-methyl pyrrolidone, N, N-dimethyl formamide or                  N, N-dimethyl acetamide. The pore-forming agent                  is one of polyvinyl pyrrolidone, isopropanol or                  polyethylene glycol.

TI Decarburization system of liquefied natural gas-powered ship using fuel cold energy includes LNG supply system, carbon dioxide capture, separation system, carbon dioxide liquefying system, dry ice synthesis system, and refutation pump.
AU LI B
   LI Y
   LIU C
   YANG F
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2021605846
AB    NOVELTY - A decarburization system of liquefied natural gas (LNG)-powered ship using fuel cold energy comprises LNG supply system, carbon dioxide capture and separation system, carbon dioxide liquefying system, and dry ice synthesis system. The LNG supply system comprises refutation pump, booster pump, refrigerant A heat exchanger, refrigerant B heat exchanger, and first cylinder sleeve water heater. The carbon dioxide capture and separation system comprises carbon dioxide capturing and separating unit, cooling tower, absorption tower, seawater heat exchanger, absorption liquid circulating pump, discharge valve, separating tower and second cylinder water heater. The carbon dioxide liquefaction system comprises compressor, liquefying tank, liquefying heat exchanger, and cooling medium B circulating pump. The dry ice synthesis system comprises cooling medium A circulating pump, dry ice heat exchanger, reactor, and valve.
   USE - Decarburization system of liquefied natural gas-powered ship using fuel cold energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can use the cold energy of the fuel on LNG-powered ship to liquefy the carbon dioxide gas produced by the combustion fuel of the main engine of the ship to make dry ice and put it into the seabed for storage. The dry ice can also be transported to land for industrial alkali production or other needs. The system can achieve zero carbon emissions of LNG-powered ships which increase the number of LNG-powered ships. Based on the enhancement of human environmental protection awareness and the increasing requirements of international maritime conventions on carbon emissions, zero carbon emission has gradually become a development trend. TI Production of carbonaceous product used as e.g. fuel, involves providing oxygen and fuel, burning fuel with oxygen to provide energy, cleaning generated flue gas using obtained energy in form of heat, and separating carbon dioxide.
AU HANNEMANN F
   ZIMMERMANN G
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 202012748D
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbonaceous product (P) involves providing oxygen, preferably from electrolysis, and a fuel, burning the fuel with the oxygen via an oxyfuel process to provide energy (EE), cleaning a flue gas generated by oxyfuel process, and separating carbon dioxide from the flue gas generated by oxyfuel process. The energy provided by oxyfuel process comprises heat which is used as process heat (C) for cleaning and/or synthesizing or preparing carbonaceous product.
   USE - Production of carbonaceous product e.g. methane, methanol, methyl tert-butyl ether, dimethoxyethane, o-methoxyethane, kerosene, gasoline, diesel and/or waxes is used as secondary energy source, fuel, solvent, chemical raw materials or intermediate products (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, reliable and energy-saving production of carbonaceous product with reduced degree of contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant (100) for producing carbonaceous product, which comprises a combustion device (101), a gas cleaning device (102), a carbon dioxide separation device (103), and, a device (104) for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the carbonaceous product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of process flow of production of carbonaceous product.
   Plant (100)
   Combustion device (101)
   Gas cleaning device (102)
   Carbon dioxide separation (103)
   Device for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen to carbonaceous product (104)
   Biomass (BM)
   Process heat (C)
   Energy (EE)
   Carbon dioxide purification (GR)
   Carbonaceous product (P)
   Renewable energy (RES)
   Carbon dioxide separation (Sep)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves providing hydrogen from electrolysis and reacting deposited carbon dioxide with the hydrogen to form carbonaceous product. The method comprises an inverse water gas shift reaction. The step of providing oxygen and/or hydrogen is carried out using fluctuating renewable energy (RES) by electrolysis. The step of providing energy is not fluctuating. The electrolysis is carried out under increased pressure, such that the electrolysis products, oxygen and hydrogen are also under increased pressure, or oxygen and hydrogen are provided in compressed form after their electrolytic production. The remaining oxygen portion is converted with hydrogen to water for the carbon dioxide separation (Sep) or a carbon dioxide purification (GR). The energy provided by the oxyfuel process comprises electrical energy which is obtained as non-fluctuating energy, via a steam power process. The oxyfuel process is carried out without flue gas recirculation. A fluidized bed is used for the combustion of the fuel. Preferred Composition: The fuel is biomass (BM) or biomass-based, such as standardized fuel present in compressed form or granulate form.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Picosecond carbon dioxide laser amplifier pumping device, has laser pump module and electric pumping module pumping carbon dioxide molecule to laser resonant cavity for broadening mixing gain spectral line to realize laser output.
AU XIAO Y
   XIA R
   TANG X
   LI X
   QIN Y
   HU C
   SONG Y
   CHEN H
   LONG S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
   UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201925795W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a laser pump module, an electric pumping module and a laser resonator connected with each other. The laser pump module pumps a first carbon dioxide molecule band. The electric pumping module pumps a second carbon dioxide molecule band. The laser pump module and the electric pumping module pump carbon dioxide molecule to a laser resonant cavity for broadening a mixing gain spectral line to realize laser output. The laser pumping module is orderly arranged with a semiconductor pumping solid laser (3). A collimator (4) is fixed with the semiconductor pumping solid laser for generating pump light collimation.
   USE - Picosecond carbon dioxide laser amplifier pumping device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device encrypts a gain spectrum by performing optical pumping while discharging excitation, and encrypts the gain spectrum by using a characteristic that a sequence gain spectrum line overlaps with a conventional band gain line, so that the mixed gain spectrum is obtained to achieve continuous spectrum picosecond carbon dioxide pulse amplification, thus reducing spectrum splitting phenomenon after pulse passes. The device can be avoid that the laser energy can be distributed to the input single pulse modulated and split into a series of discrete ps pulses to cause a single ps pulse, thus increasing amplification efficiency of ultra-short pulses.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a picosecond carbon dioxide laser amplifier pumping device.
   Semiconductor pumping solid laser (3)
   Collimator (4)
   Cathode (10)
   Pump cylinder (12)

PT P
PN US2019030485-A1; US10744457-B2
TI Electrochemical separator for separating carbon            dioxide from mixed gas stream, comprises reactor having            cathode, and aqueous working liquid comprising water            and electrochemically active organic molecules            functionalized with ionic groups.
AU PERRY M L
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 201911286B
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical separator (20) comprises an                electrochemical reactor (22) having an anode (24)                and a cathode (26), and an aqueous working liquid                (34) for circulation through the reactor. The                aqueous working liquid comprises water and                electrochemically active organic molecules                dissolved in water, where the organic molecules are                functionalized with one or more ionic groups.
   USE - Electrochemical separator used for separating                carbon dioxide from mixed gas stream (claimed).                Uses include but are not limited to space structure                applications, submarine applications, airplane                applications, and compression cycle in refrigerant                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical separator has excellent                separation efficiency as compared to the existing                carbon dioxide gas cycles, which are relatively                energy-intensive in pressurizing gaseous carbon                dioxide. The cathode of the reactor is operable                with an input of electric power via electrical                circuit, to electrochemically reduce the quinone of                the aqueous working liquid, thereby                electrochemically activating the quinone for carbon                dioxide capture in the carbon dioxide collector.                The aqueous working liquid is relatively                non-volatile, non-flammable, and non-corrosive in                comparison to other working fluids often used in                electrolytic cells. Since the aqueous working                liquid is water-based, it has relatively low                viscosity, which facilitates pumping and flow                management. The aqueous working liquid adsorbs more                carbon dioxide per liter and enhances diffusion of                quinone in the reactor, thus increasing reaction                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of carbon dioxide from mixed gas                stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrochemical separator.
   Electrochemical separator (20)
   Electrochemical reactor (22)
   Anode (24)
   Cathode (26)
   Aqueous working liquid (34)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic molecules                  are quinone molecules chosen from benzoquinone,                  naphthoquinone and anthraquinone, preferably                  benzoquinone-disulfonic acid. The ionic groups                  include sulfonic acid. Preferred Composition: The                  aqueous working liquid comprises 1-4 mol/L,                  preferably 1.5-4 mol/L organic molecules.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The reactor comprises                  an ion-conducting membrane comprising                  ion-exchange polymer.

A lithium hydroxide supply unit (36) is coupled to the treatment unit and configured to feed lithium hydroxide into the treatment unit such that reaction between the lithium hydroxide and the carbon dioxide occurs inside the treatment unit to convert carbon dioxide into lithium carbonate and water. A treated exhaust outlet (20) is coupled to the treatment unit and configured to discharge the treated exhaust gases.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The lithium hydroxide supply unit is designed as a storage vessel, supply line, a screw-type feeder and/or conveying device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for treating exhaust gas from a turbine system in a gas turbine engine of a vehicle.

PT P
PN WO2021062130-A1; CA3152358-A1; EP4034285-A1; US2022331737-A1
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide, involves            expanding hot carbon dioxide-depleted stream over            stages of expansion to drive compression of cooled            exhaust and feeding moist carbon dioxide-depleted            stream to interstage of stages of expansion.
AU DIPIETRO J
   MOATE J
   GUNER U
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
   AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
   AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 202133040B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling an exhaust stream                containing carbon dioxide by stages of indirect                heat exchange to produce a cooled exhaust stream.                The cooled exhaust stream is compressed over stages                of compression to produce a compressed exhaust                stream. The compressed exhaust stream is seperated                to produce a carbon dioxide-enriched stream and a                carbon dioxide- depleted stream. The carbon                dioxide-enriched stream is dehydrated by contacting                the carbon dioxide-enriched stream with regenerated                desiccant to produce a carbon dioxide product                stream and a spent desiccant. The warm carbon                dioxide-depleted stream with the spent desiccant is                contacted to produce a moist carbon dioxide-                depleted stream and the regenerated desiccant. The                hot carbon dioxide-depleted stream is expanded over                stages of expansion to drive the compression of the                cooled exhaust, to produce an expanded carbon                dioxide-depleted stream. The moist carbon                dioxide-depleted stream is fed to an interstage of                the stages of expansion.
   USE - Process for capturing carbon dioxide and for                initiating separation of carbon dioxide from fluid                stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Different fluids pass through the finned tube                portions of stages in order to optimize the heat                integration of the overall system. The start-up                method precludes or at least oil loss from the                bearings of turbochargers and improves the                operational life of turbochargers. The heat                captured by multi-stage heat exchanger provides for                regeneration of dehydration unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an apparatus for capturing carbon                dioxide;
   (2) a process for capturing carbon dioxide;                and
   (3) a method for initiating separation of                carbon dioxide from a fluid stream.

PT P
PN WO2020109295-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide into carbon containing products comprises capturing carbon dioxide from air or flue gases and electrochemically converting carbon dioxide.
AU NEGRO E
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202049222G
AB    NOVELTY - Process for converting carbon dioxide into carbon containing products, comprises capturing carbon dioxide from air or flue gases and electrochemically converting the carbon dioxide. The method involves (a) introducing the carbon dioxide into a vessel comprising a liquid sorbent to capture the carbon dioxide in the liquid sorbent, (b) pressurizing the liquid sorbent comprising the captured carbon dioxide by introducing the liquid sorbent into a pump, to provide a pressurized carbon dioxide comprising liquid sorbent, and (c) feeding the pressurized carbon dioxide comprising liquid sorbent of step (b) to a cathode compartment of an electrolyzer comprising a catalyst to produce carbon containing gaseous products.
   USE - The process is used for converting carbon dioxide into carbon containing products, preferably gaseous products chosen from carbon monoxide, methane, dimethyl ether, ethylene and propylene. The process is used for producing chemicals and/or fuels from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is more efficient and less energy demanding process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide to produce useful chemicals and/or fuels.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure in steps (b) and (c) is 1-5 MPa. The sorbent is regenerated and recycled back into the process. Preferred Components: The liquid sorbent is a chemical sorbent. The sorbent is a metal hydroxide or amines. The cathode of the electrolyzer comprises a heterogeneous catalyst comprising a metal. The metal is chosen from gold, silver, copper, palladium, lead, tin, or their combinations. The electrolyzer comprises a membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The products from the cathode reaction of carbon dioxide, water and electrons, are gaseous products chosen from carbon monoxide, methane, dimethyl ether, ethylene and propylene.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN WO2020206148-A1; US2022203292-A1
TI System for collecting carbon dioxide, has conduit wall that comprises membrane material which is hydrophobic trapping liquid sorbent, and porous allows gaseous carbon dioxide transfers through conduit wall from outside conduit.
AU LACKNER K
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   LACKNER K (LACK-Individual)
   LACKNER K (LACK-Individual)
GA 202098120L
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a liquid sorbent (102) that is a carbon dioxide sorbent. Multiple conduits (104) comprises a hollow interior (106) enclosed by a conduit wall (108). The conduit wall comprises a membrane material (110) that is hydrophobic trapping the liquid sorbent inside the hollow interior, and porous allows gaseous carbon dioxide (122) to transfer the conduit through the conduit wall, and into the liquid sorbent within the hollow interior. A flow of the liquid sorbent from the first manifold (112) to the second manifold (114) through the riser (116). A decreasing density of the liquid sorbent caused by addition of replacement water to the liquid sorbent after the liquid sorbent flows through the conduits. The liquid sorbent has a first density in the first manifold. The first density is chosen such that the passive convection creates a flow rate that results in liquid sorbent that is regenerated when leaving the first manifold is saturated when reaching the second manifold.
   USE - System for collecting carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid sorbent from escaping is prevented. The hydrophobic material avoids pushing fluid through the membrane, which prevents liquid flow through the pores due to a high capillary pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multiple conduits comprises 100 conduits, and each conduit has a radius less than 8mm. The asorbent regeneration assembly is in fluid communication with the second manifold, and the sorbent regeneration assembly configured to receive the liquid sorbent and release the carbon dioxide captured within the liquid sorbent using one of an electrolytical process and an electrochemical process, producing a product stream of carbon dioxide. A liquid pump is in fluid communication with one of the first manifold and the second manifold, and the flow of the liquid sorbent from the first manifold through the conduits to the second manifold through the riser to the first manifold, is driven by the liquid pump at a flow rate. A sorbent regeneration assembly is in fluid communication with the second manifold, and the sorbent regeneration assembly receives the liquid sorbent and releases the carbon dioxide captured within the liquid sorbent, producing a product stream of carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for collecting carbon dioxide, involves circulating a liquid sorbent within a carbon dioxide collection system comprising a first manifold, a second manifold, a conduit, and a riser. The liquid sorbent is passed from the first manifold to the second manifold through conduits and from the second manifold to the first manifold through the riser. The first manifold and second manifold are in fluid communication through the riser and each conduit comprises a hollow interior enclosed by a conduit wall. The conduit wall comprises a membrane material that is hydrophobic and porous, and the liquid sorbent passes through the hollow interior of each conduit. The gaseous carbon dioxide is received and captured as the gaseous carbon dioxide transfers, for each conduit from outside the conduit, through the conduit wall, and into the liquid sorbent flowing through the hollow interior of the conduit as the liquid sorbent flows from the first manifold to the second manifold. Each conduit has a radial cross- section that is circular. The liquid sorbent is regenerated using a sorbent regeneration assembly in fluid communication with the second manifold, and the liquid sorbent is received and the carbon dioxide captured within the liquid sorbent is released, and a product stream of carbon dioxide is produced. The liquid sorbent has a first density in the first manifold, and the first density is chosen such that the passive convection creates a flow rate that results in liquid sorbent that is regenerated when leaving the first manifold is saturated when reaching the second manifold. The liquid pump is configured to drive the liquid sorbent at a flow rate that results in liquid sorbent that is regenerated when leaving the first manifold is substantially saturated when reaching the second manifold. An air-side boundary layer having a boundary layer thickness that is equal to a radius of the conduit is comprised for each conduit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for collecting carbon dioxide using membrane conduits.

PN CN110817800-A; CN211998805-U
TI Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system useful for methanol steam reforming and separating hydrogen comprises a carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe connected with three-phase heat exchange and reforming separating device.
AU YUE X
   HAN D
   ZHAO J
   LI J
   CHEN F
   ZHANG Y
   YUE Y
AE ZHANGJIAGANG IND TECHNOLOGY INST CO LTD (CACP-C)
GA 202018156E
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system comprises a reforming separating device, a steam separating device, a three-phase heat exchanger, a steam trap, a water-cooling heat exchanger, a carbon dioxide separation device and a water gas reforming device. The reforming separating device comprises a methanol steam inlet pipe, a hydrogen outlet pipe and carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe. The carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with a three-phase heat exchanger, the steam trap, the water cooling heat exchanger, the carbon dioxide separating device and water gas reforming device. An air pump is provided on the reforming mixed gas outlet pipe for lifting the reformed mixed gas delivery pressure in the pipe. A methanol water vapor inlet pipe and the hydrogen outlet pipe are both connected to a three-phase heat exchange device and the pure hydrogen in the hydrogen outlet pipe is sent to a hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of a liquid pump.
   USE - The system is useful for methanol steam reforming and separating hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves recycling purification of the gas in the system; reach the theoretical yield of 100% and achieve hydrogen yield of greater than or equal to 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system comprises a reforming separating device, a steam separating device, a three-phase heat exchanger, a steam trap, a water-cooling heat exchanger, a carbon dioxide separation device and a water gas reforming device. The reforming separating device comprises a methanol steam inlet pipe, a hydrogen outlet pipe and carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe. The carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with a three-phase heat exchanger, the steam trap, the water cooling heat exchanger, the carbon dioxide separating device and water gas reforming device. The operating temperature of the water-cooling heat exchanger is less than or equal to 30.8 degrees C. An outlet of the water gas reforming device is connected to a reforming mixed gas outlet pipe and is connected to an inlet of the reforming separating device. An air pump is provided on the reforming mixed gas outlet pipe for lifting the reformed mixed gas delivery pressure in the pipe. A methanol water vapor inlet pipe and the hydrogen outlet pipe are both connected to a three-phase heat exchange device. The methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a liquid pump. A pump pressure of the liquid pump is 40-100 MPa and the pure hydrogen in the hydrogen outlet pipe is sent to a hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of a liquid pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing hydrogen from methanol water under high pressure comprising (a) allowing the liquid pump to send methanol water into the methanol water vapor tube at a pump pressure of 40-100MPa, heating the methanol water to vaporize into methanol water vapor, entering the reaction chamber of the reforming separation device, decomposing the methanol water vapor into a mixed gas of 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, (b) allowing the hydrogen absorption tube separating the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where the separated hydrogen is output from the hydrogen absorption tube, sending the pure hydrogen to the hydrogen storage tank under the pressure of the liquid pump, controlling the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas using water-cooled heat exchanger, where the remaining carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is output from the reaction chamber, sending the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into the carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation, where the gas phase of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas including 25-45% hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0-3% water and 0.3-5% carbon monoxide, indirectly controlling the pressure of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas in the carbon dioxide separation device by a liquid pump, where the pressure is 40-100 MPa and the operating temperature of the water-cooled heat exchanger is less than or equal to 30.8 degrees C, (c) making the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into a liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in a carbon dioxide separator, collecting the output of liquid carbon dioxide, where the hydrogen mixed residual gas including 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide and 3-9% carbon monoxide, (d) sending the hydrogen mixed gas is to the water gas reforming unit for reforming, mixing the water to make the reforming mixed gas, distributing water to the amount of carbon monoxide in a ratio of 1: 1-2, reforming the hydrogen mixed waste gas into reformed mixed gas by water gas reforming reaction device, where the gaseous components of the reformed mixed gas including 62-77% hydrogen, 22-27% carbon dioxide and 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide and the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the reformed mixed gas is close to the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide and (e) mixing the reformed mixed gas with a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide and sending the reformed mixed gas into the reaction chamber again with the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to separate hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reforming and separating device comprises a reaction chamber including a methanol water vapor inlet and a carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet. The reaction chamber is filled with catalyst filler. The methanol water vapor is introduced into the reaction chamber from the air inlet for catalytic reaction to generate a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. A hydrogen absorption tube is inserted into the catalyst filler of the reaction chamber. The hydrogen absorption tube is suitable for separating the generated mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide into a mixed gas of pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The pure hydrogen is collected from the output of the hydrogen absorption tube. The carbon dioxide mixed waste gas is output from the carbon dioxide mixed waste gas outlet. The hydrogen absorption tube is a niobium tube. The catalyst filler including a inner-layer catalytic filler and an outer-layer catalytic filler. The niobium tube is inserted into the inner layer of catalytic filler. The outer-layer catalytic filler wraps the inner-layer catalytic filler. The inner layer filler is a copper-based filler and the outer layer filler is a zirconium-based filler. The temperature at which the methanol water vapor catalyzes the reaction is 200-500 degrees C and the temperature at which the niobium tube separates hydrogen is 200-500 degrees C. The hydrogen absorption tube is a palladium film tube or a palladium alloy film tube. The temperature when the palladium membrane tube or the palladium alloy membrane tube separates hydrogen is 250-550 degrees C. The heating chamber supplying working heat to the reaction chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber and the heat of the heating chamber is provided by the heat generated by the combustion of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas. The output pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are all output after heat exchange and temperature reduction in a three-phase heat exchange device. The methanol water is vaporized into methanol water vapor through a three-phase heat exchange device. The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).)

PN US2018274856-A1; US11090711-B2
TI Preserving material in contact with fluid involves            replacing fluid with non-gaseous fluid comprising            primarily of carbon dioxide, cooling non-gaseous fluid            to cause non-gaseous fluid to freeze forming solid, and            cooling.
AU GOLFETTO M
AE GOLFETTO M (GOLF-Individual)
   META DRY LLC (META-Non-standard)
GA 202195457W
AB    NOVELTY - Method of preserving a material in contact                with a first fluid involves (i) replacing the first                fluid with a first non-gaseous fluid comprising                primarily of carbon dioxide, (ii) cooling the first                non-gaseous fluid to cause the first non-gaseous                fluid to freeze forming a solid, and (iii) cooling                at a rate of at least 0.2 degrees C/minute.
   USE - The method is used for preserving a material                in contact with a first fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows highly delicate materials,                minimizes or avoids damage due to gas-liquid                interfaces and pore collapse, allows material to be                freeze-dried fast enough to be economically viable,                and allows the freeze-drying processing times to be                comparable to other economically viable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of drying and sterilizing and preserving and                impregnating a material, comprising (a) flushing an                original liquid from a material having pores and                non-tertiary amines with an intermediate liquid                having higher solubility with carbon dioxide than                the original liquid to replace the original liquid                with the intermediate liquid in the pores and                around the material to protect the material from                chemical reactions and expedite eventual                replacement of the intermediate liquid with                non-gaseous carbon dioxide, where the intermediate                liquid is chosen from acetone, acetonitrile, acetic                acid, amyl alcohol, benzene, carbon tetrachloride,                chlorobenzene, chloroform, cyclo-cresylic acid,                hexane, isopropyl alcohol, dimethylformamide,                ethanol, ethyl acetate, furfural, furfural alcohol,                methanol, N-butane, N-heptane, N-hexane, pyridine,                and their combinations, the non-tertiary amines                comprise of one of primary amines, secondary                amines, and a combination of primary and secondary                amines, (b) evaporating the intermediate liquid to                form a saturated vapor, (c) surrounding the                material and the intermediate liquid with the                saturated vapor to impede evaporation of the                intermediate liquid, (d) heating the material and                the saturated vapor and the intermediate liquid to                at least the critical temperature of carbon                dioxide, (e) applying gaseous carbon dioxide at a                rate that prevents turbulent fluid motion around                the material to increase the pressure around the                material to just below the critical pressure of                carbon dioxide, (f) maintaining pressure around the                material at just below the critical pressure of                carbon dioxide for at least ten minutes, (g)                applying non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide and a                co-solvent to the material, (h) submerging the                material in the non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide                and the co-solvent thereby causing the non-tertiary                amines of the material to reversibly react with the                non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide to form carbamate                groups which protect the material from surface                tension, (i) flushing the material with the                non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide and the co-solvent                to remove the intermediate liquid surrounding the                material and any gas from around the material to                avoid moisture in the gas rehydrating the material                while avoiding gas-liquid interfaces, (j)                subjecting the material to an impregnating agent                with the impregnating agent being non-gaseous at                the temperature and pressure conditions at which                carbon dioxide is sublimed during part (p) and with                the impregnating agent being soluble in liquid and                supercritical carbon dioxide and insoluble in                gaseous carbon dioxide, (k) flushing the material                with the non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide and the                co-solvent and the impregnating agent while                avoiding gas-liquid interfaces, (l) simultaneously                with step (k) agitating the material and the                non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide and the co-solvent                with ultrasonic irradiation, (m) simultaneously                with steps (l) and (k) mixing the non-gaseous fluid                carbon dioxide and the co-solvent and the                impregnating agent to establish shear forces to                extract the intermediate liquid from the pores of                the material, (n) cooling the material and the                non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide and the co-solvent                and the impregnating agent to below the freezing                point of carbon dioxide to cause the non-gaseous                fluid carbon dioxide and the co-solvent to freeze                by transferring heat with a cryogenic liquid having                a freezing point below the freezing point of carbon                dioxide, (o) simultaneously with step (n) expanding                the non-gaseous carbon dioxide thereby reforming                gaseous carbon dioxide to decrease pressure about                the material at a rate greater than or equal to                0.001 MPa/minute and less than or equal to 2                MPa/minute to precipitate the impregnating agent                into the pores and to produce a matrix of the                material encased in solid carbon dioxide and the                co-solvent and the impregnating agent, (p)                subliming solid carbon dioxide in the solid phase                from the matrix and forming the non-tertiary amines                by changing the environment of the matrix to one of                little humidity and a temperature between room                temperature and of 400 degrees C and a pressure                above ambient pressure and at which carbon dioxide                sublimes thereby producing a dried and sterilized                material that contains the impregnating agent, and                characterized by (q) prior to step (o) cooling the                non-gaseous fluid carbon dioxide around the                material to form a solid layer primarily of solid                carbon dioxide on and surrounding the material by                transferring heat with a cryogenic liquid to                protect the material from gas-liquid interfaces and                surface tension before forming gaseous carbon                dioxide in step (o), and (r) cooling at a rate of                at least 0.2 degrees C/minutes in steps (n) and (q)                to limit the size of crystals formed when carbon                dioxide freezes and for avoiding the formation of                gas bubbles and for avoiding damage to the pores                and exposure of the material to gas-liquid                interfaces.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves: (iv)                  subliming the solid from the material; reacting                  amines of the material comprising of one of                  primary amines, secondary amines, and a                  combination of primary and secondary amines with                  carbon dioxide to form carbamate groups, and                  forming amines by subliming carbon dioxide from                  the material; (v) preserving a material                  containing at least one pore comprises,                  subjecting the material to an impregnating agent                  with the impregnating agent being non-gaseous at                  the temperature and pressure conditions at which                  carbon dioxide is sublimed and with the                  impregnating agent being soluble in at least one                  of liquid and supercritical carbon dioxide, and                  depositing the impregnating agent into at least                  one pore in the material; and (vi) forming a                  solid layer of the solid on and surrounding the                  material, decreasing pressure about the material                  by expanding the first non-gaseous fluid to form                  a gas. The step (i) involves flushing the                  material with the first non-gaseous fluid. The                  step (ii) involves transferring heat with a                  liquid circulating about the material. The step                  (vii) involves transferring heat with a liquid                  circulating about the material. Preferred                  Conditions: The rate of cooling is at least 0.5                  degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: The first                  fluid is a non-gaseous fluid. The first                  non-gaseous fluid is carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN EP4043089-A1; GB2605263-A
TI Method for capturing and sequestering biogenic            carbon dioxide, invovles generating biofuel by            fermentation of biomass, and seperating biogenic carbon            dioxide stream from biofuel, and liquifying biogenic            carbon dioxide stream.
AU LUKAS P H
AE FUTURE BIOGAS LTD (FUTU-Non-standard)
   FUTURE BIOGAS LTD (FUTU-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5346D
AB    NOVELTY - The method invovles generating a biofuel by                fermentation of a biomass. A biogenic carbon                dioxide stream is seperated from the biofuel. The                biogenic carbon dioxide stream is liquefied. The                liquefied biogenic carbon dioxide streams from the                biofuel upgraders is combined. The liquefied                biogenic carbon dioxide is sequestered at a                sequestration site, where the biofuel is generated                by anaerobic digestion of the biomass and the                biofuel comprises biomethane and biogenic carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for capturing and sequestering biogenic                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively capturing and                sequestering the biogenic carbon dioxide in an                economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for capturing biogenic carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for capturing biogenic carbon                dioxide.
   Bioreactor installation (1)
   Biogenic carbon dioxide stream (14)
   Central storage facility (17)
   Transport system (18)

PN DE102017214456-A1
TI Carbon dioxide electrolyzer arrangement for plant complex, has gas separation device that is coupled to combustion chamber, and connecting line that is provided between exhaust device of combustion chamber and supply device.
AU STARK K
   BALDAUF M
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 201916904S
AB    NOVELTY - The arrangement has a supply device (16) of feed gas, and a circulation device is provided for generating an electrolyte circuit. The circulation device is provided with a gas separation device (20). The gas separation device is coupled to a combustion chamber (22). A connecting line is provided between an exhaust device (23) of the combustion chamber and the supply device. An electrolysis cell (3) is provided with a gas space (4) which adjoins a cathode (6) which is designed as a gas diffusion electrode (7) and which separates the gas space from a cathode space (8). The electrolysis cell comprises an anode and an anode space (12).
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolyzer arrangement for plant complex (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide can be recycled from the anolyte energy-efficiently in the electrolyzer or in the gas space of the electrolyzer. The gas that accumulates in the electrolyte containing carbon dioxide and oxygen is not released to the environment. The carbon dioxide electrolyzer can be operated very energy efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The cathode space and anode space are separated by a membrane (13). The cathode space receives a catholyte (14) and the anode space receives an anolyte (15). A supply device supplies carbon dioxide containing educt gas (18) to gas space. The gas separation device in the form of an anode gas separation device is in communication with the anode space. An electrical connection line exists between a power generator of combined heat and power plant and a power supply of electrolysis cell. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for operating carbon dioxide electrolyzer arrangement involves passing carbon dioxide containing educt gas by gas space in an electrolyte. The present oxygen from the liquid electrolyte is separated and the gas mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen in a combustion chamber is fed. The combustion chamber is further fed by combustible fluid. The combustible fluid is converted with the oxygen to carbon dioxide and water and carbon dioxide as portion of the carbon dioxide-containing educt gas. The educt gas containing carbon dioxide is conducted into the gas space and is converted at a gas diffusion electrode. The gas diffusion electrode is connected to a liquid electrolyte in the form of a catholyte. The cathode space is separated by a membrane from an anode space in which there is a liquid electrolyte in the form of an anolyte. The carbon dioxide passes into the anode space and thus into the anolyte, after which the carbon dioxide is deposited in the gas separation device in the form of an anode gas separation device. The combustible fluid is a methane-containing gas. The combustion chamber is a portion of a combined heat and power plant. The electrical energy generated by the combined heat and power plant is partially used for the operation of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer. The biogas source is provided and the combustion chamber is fed by a methane-containing biogas; and
   (2) a plant complex comprises gas separation device provided with the carbon dioxide electrolysis cell and the fuel source. The combustion chamber of the cogeneration unit is in communication with exhaust device of the combined heat and power plant with a supply device for reactant gas of the carbon dioxide electrolysis cell. The power generator of the cogeneration plant is in communication with power supply of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer arrangement.
   Electrolysis cell (3)
   Gas space (4)
   Cathode (6)
   Gas diffusion electrode (7)
   Cathode space (8)
   Anode space (12)
   Membrane (13)
   Catholyte (14)
   Anolyte (15)
   Supply device (16)
   Carbon dioxide containing educt gas (18)
   Gas separation device (20)
   Combustion chamber (22)
   Exhaust device (23)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC E11-N; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; J03-B01; J03-B02; J03-B05; X25-R01A; X25-R01D
