PT P
PN WO2022178323-A1; US2022267914-A1
TI Integrated direct air capture of carbon dioxide            for aqueous electrochemical reduction of carbon            dioxide, involves contacting input air stream            comprising carbon dioxide with electrolyte solution,            and reducing subset of carbon dioxide using electrolyte            solution to generate reduced carbon products.
AU MCGINNIS R
   HERRMANN-NOWOSIELSKI M
AE PROMETHEUS FUELS INC (PROM-Non-standard)
   PROMETHEUS FUELS INC (PROM-Non-standard)
GA 2022A85642
AB    NOVELTY - Method for integrated direct air capture of                carbon dioxide for aqueous electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide, involves (a)                contacting input air stream with an electrolyte                solution, where the input air stream comprises                carbon dioxide to capture at least a subset of                carbon dioxide from the input air stream to the                carbon dioxide electrolyte solution, and (b)                reducing at least the subset of carbon dioxide                using the electrolyte solution to generate reduced                carbon products, where during or subsequent to (b),                the electrolyte solution is regenerated to capture                additional carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is used for integrated direct air                capture of carbon dioxide for aqueous                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a composition comprising a hydrocarbon mixture                having carbon at an isotopic distribution of                greater than -25% &#916; 13carbon; and
   a system for integrated direct air capture of                carbon dioxide for aqueous electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide comprising a housing                configured to contain an electrolyte solution, and                computer processors that are individually or                collectively programmed to (a) bring the                electrolyte solution in contact with an input air                stream comprising carbon dioxide to capture at                least a subset of the carbon dioxide from the input                air stream to the electrolyte solution, and (b)                reduce the at least the subset of the carbon                dioxide using the electrolyte solution to generate                reduced carbon products, where during or subsequent                to (b), the electrolyte solution is regenerated to                capture additional carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The capture of the subset                  of carbon dioxide involves performing absorption                  or adsorption by the electrolyte solution. The                  method further involves controlling a temperature                  of the electrolyte solution to facilitate capture                  of water. The step (a) involves subjecting the                  electrolyte solution to flow from a first                  electrolyte reservoir to a contactor, where the                  input air stream and the electrolyte solution is                  contacted at the contactor. The method further                  involves directing the electrolyte solution to a                  second electrolyte reservoir, and controlling a                  pH or the electrolyte solution (i) prior to or                  (ii) subsequent to the contacting of the input                  air stream and the electrolyte solution. The                  method further involves prior to (a), contacting                  a first electrolyte solution with a carbon                  dioxide containing liquid material sufficient to                  capture carbon dioxide to output the electrolyte                  solution, and subsequent to (b), contacting the                  electrolyte solution with the first electrolyte                  solution. Preferred Conditions: The reducing in                  step (b) is carried out in the absence of an                  independent hydrogen feed to the electrolyte                  solution. The controlling is carried out by                  adjusting or maintaining the pH of the                  electrolyte solution to 9-15, preferably 7-10.                  The first electrolyte solution of the liquid                  material sufficient to capture carbon dioxide is                  contacted at a bipolar membrane stack containing                  an anion exchange membrane or a cation exchange                  membrane stack or both, where the bipolar                  membrane stack or the cation exchange membrane                  stack is configured to facilitate transport of                  carbon containing species from the liquid                  material sufficient to capture carbon dioxide to                  the first electrolyte solution. The reducing of                  the subset of carbon dioxide using the                  electrolyte solution generates the reduced carbon                  products. Preferred Composition: The input air                  stream has a carbon dioxide concentration of at                  most 1000 parts per million (ppm), preferably at                  most 420 ppm. Preferred Components: The input air                  stream comprises water, where subsequent to (a),                  at least a subset of water is absorbed by the                  electrolyte solution. The first electrolyte                  reservoir is different from the second                  electrolyte reservoir. The first electrolyte                  reservoir is the same as the second electrolyte                  reservoir. The contactor comprises an adsorbent                  to facilitate adsorption of the subset of carbon                  dioxide from the input air stream. The adsorbent                  comprises water. The adsorbent comprises a solid                  substrate comprising reactive chemical                  adsorbents, preferably a polystyrene bead                  functionalized with amines, or activated or                  nanostructured carbon materials. The activated or                  nanostructured carbon material comprises carbon                  nanotubes (CNTs), Buckminster fullerene, and                  graphene. The contactor comprises members                  comprising a membrane contactor, random or                  structured gas-liquid contacting packing, film                  fill, splash packing, packed falling film device,                  cooling tower, fluidized bed, liquid shower in                  contact with gases, and/or nanostructured or                  activated carbon material, preferably carbon                  nanotube membrane, where several nanotubes of the                  carbon nanotube membrane function as pores, and                  several openings of the several nanotubes are                  functionalized with adsorbing functional groups.                  The liquid material sufficient to capture carbon                  dioxide comprises members comprising an aqueous                  hydroxide solution, an amine solution, and/or an                  ionic liquid. The reduced carbon products                  comprise fuel. The hydrocarbon mixture has carbon                  at an isotopic distribution of -6 to -9%                  &#916; 13carbon, no detectable sulfur, no                  detectable aromatic compounds, and no detectable                  metal compounds.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E05-U03; E05-U05C; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; J01-X; J03-B05; A04-C02E; A10-E01; A12-W11; A12-W11D; A12-W11F
IP B01J-041/14; C25B-003/25; C25B-009/19; C25B-003/26

PN JP2020063206-A; JP7117971-B2
TI Preparing methane involves generating stream from            cooling water, using generated steam as thermal energy            for separating carbon dioxide, and condensing steam and            using as cooling water, and absorbing carbon dioxide            having carbon dioxide.
AU INAGAKI R
   KANNO S
   SATO K
   OHATA H
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   DENGEN KAIHATSU KK (DNGK-C)
   HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   ELECTRIC POWER DEV CO LTD (DNGK-C)
GA 202032554W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methane involves generating stream                from cooling water. The generated steam is used as                the thermal energy for separating carbon dioxide,                and the used steam is condensed and used as the                cooling water. The carbon dioxide is absorbed and                adsorbing carbon dioxide in an absorbing material                or adsorbing carbon dioxide in an adsorbing                material. The carbon dioxide is separated from the                absorbing material or the adsorbing material by                using thermal energy. The methanation reaction is                carried out for producing methane by using a                catalytic reaction from carbon dioxide and hydrogen                separated and recovered in the carbon dioxide                separation and recovery, and used reaction heat                generated by the catalytic reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare methane improves                the system efficiency in methane production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide comprises an absorbing material that                absorbs carbon dioxide or an adsorbing material                that adsorbs carbon dioxide, and a carbon dioxide                separating unit that separates carbon dioxide using                thermal energy from the absorbing material or                adsorbing material of the carbon dioxide absorbing                and adsorbing unit, and an methanation reaction                unit includes a catalyst packed bed for producing                methane by using a catalytic reaction from carbon                dioxide and hydrogen separated and recovered by the                carbon dioxide separation and recovery unit, and                using heat of reaction generated by the reaction,                steam generated from cooling water for cooling the                catalyst packed bed is used as thermal energy for                separating carbon dioxide, and the steam is used as                cooling water for cooling the catalyst packed                bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The separated carbon                  dioxide is circulated, and the steam generated by                  the heat of reaction is used to heat the                  circulated carbon dioxide. The heated carbon                  dioxide is used as thermal energy for separating                  carbon dioxide from the adsorbent. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature of the steam                  generated by the heat of reaction is monitored,                  and when the temperature of the steam rises, the                  amount of steam generated is increased. The                  carbon dioxide separation / recovery device uses                  a thermal energy to separate carbon dioxide from                  the adsorbent, and the carbon dioxide adsorption                  device separates the carbon dioxide from the                  adsorbent. The carbon dioxide uses heater that                  circulates portion and uses the steam generated                  by the heat of reaction to heat the circulating                  carbon dioxide to supply the thermal                  energy.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-D02; J01-E03C; J01-E03F; J04-E01; N06-D; N07-B02

PN US2021163873-A1; WO2021113179-A1
TI System for inoculating and transporting Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism, comprises carbon dioxide element arranged in second depression, and carbon dioxide element configured to release carbon dioxide gas.
AU ANTIABONG J F
   FONT B K
AE BIOMED DIAGNOSTICS INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
   BIOMED DIAGNOSTICS INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
GA 202159680F
AB    NOVELTY - System 100 comprises tray 102 comprising floor wall 104 defining first depression 108 and second depression 110. The tray further comprises multiple sidewalls extending up to perimeter edge forming opening. The medium is set in first depression. The carbon dioxide element 114 is arranged in second depression. The carbon dioxide element is configured to release carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide seal covers second depression. The inner panel overlays first and second depressions. The inner panel 116 is configured to be removed and reapplied over first and second depressions. The outer panel overlays inner panel. The outer panel is configured to be removed and reapplied over inner panel.
   USE - System used for inoculating and transporting Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has advanced antibiotic susceptibility testing capabilities, increases sensitivity, and avoids over-estimation viability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for inoculating and transporting specimen containing Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism, which involves:
   a. providing tray comprising floor wall defining first and second depression, tray further comprises multiple sidewalls extending up to perimeter edge forming opening;
   b. detaching portion of outer panel from perimeter edge of tray;
   c. detaching portion of inner panel from perimeter edge of tray;
   d. identifying first depression in floor wall, first depression containing Gonococcal medium;
   e. introducing Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism into Gonococcal medium;
   f. identifying second depression in floor wall, second depression containing carbon dioxide element, second depression is sealed with carbon dioxide seal, carbon dioxide element is configured to release carbon dioxide gas;
   g. puncturing carbon dioxide seal, where carbon dioxide gas saturates Gonococcal medium;
   h. inoculating Gonococcal medium with Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism;
   i. reattaching inner panel to perimeter edge of tray, where inner panel seals first and second depressions;
   j. reattaching outer panel to perimeter edge of tray, where outer panel overlays inner panel;
   k. incubating tray at about 35degreesC for 24 hours.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows top view of system.
   System (100)
   Tray (102)
   Floor wall (104)
   First depression (108)
   Second depression (110)
   Carbon dioxide element (114)
   Inner panel (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The medium comprises Gonococcal medium configured to grow Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism. The Gonococcal medium is defined by chocolate color. The tray comprises polypropylene cassette having 12-month shelf life.Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas inhibits degradation of Neisseria gonorrhoeae or microbial organism.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114659316-A
TI Static magnetic field assisted liquefied carbon            dioxide pulse jet quick freezing device comprises            freezing chamber provided with closed refrigerating            chamber, freezing cavity provided with material placing            area in vertical middle area, liquid carbon dioxide            injection device.
AU FAN D
   JIANG Q
   ZHANG M
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 202287098S
AB    NOVELTY - Static magnetic field assisted liquefied                carbon dioxide pulse jet quick freezing device                comprises a freezing chamber, the freezing chamber                is provided with a closed refrigerating chamber,                the freezing cavity can achieve less than 1.5 MPa                pressure after pre-pressurizing carbon dioxide,                freezing cavity is provided with a material placing                area in the vertical middle area, liquid carbon                dioxide injection device, the spray device of                liquid combined fluid nozzle structure is arranged                on the top surface of the freezing cavity, and the                inlet of the combined fluid nozzle structure                connected with the first carbon dioxide input heat                preservation soft pipe, the first carbon dioxide                input heat preservation soft pipe provided with a                feeding interface exposed to freezing chamber, the                jet orifice of combined fluid nozzle structure is                suspended above the material placing area,                injection port of combined fluid nozzle structure                can uniformly spray the carbon dioxide liquid the                material placing area.
   USE - Used as static magnetic field assisted                liquefied carbon dioxide pulse jet quick freezing                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device obviously reduces the freezing                time, realizes ice nucleation process is controlled                and frozen uniformly, shortens maximum ice crystal                generating belt time by more than 20%, reduces the                product ice crystal size by more than 20% and                reduces the loss rate of the juice liquid by more                than 4%, achieves carbon dioxide pre-treated and                static magnetic field synergetic effect, and has                strong liquefied carbon dioxide permeability, fast                heat exchange, fast freezing speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                pressurizing and high-efficient freezing method                based on quick-freezing device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the static magnetic field assisted                liquefied carbon dioxide pulse jet quick freezing                device.

PN US2022226770-A1; JP2022109656-A; CN114762790-A; GB2605245-A
TI Carbon dioxide capture system for use in thermal            power plant, has absorbing liquid line supplying            absorbing liquid regenerated in liquid regenerator as            cleaning liquid to spray, where liquid sprayed by spray            is supplied as absorbing liquid.
AU SAITO S
   HANDA Y
   KATO Y
   MURAOKA D
   FUJITA K
   QI T
AE TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 202293453K
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a carbon dioxide Capturer                (20) configured for absorbing carbon dioxide                contained in a combustion exhaust gas (2) into an                absorbing liquid containing an amine. An absorbing                liquid regenerator releases the carbon dioxide from                the absorbing liquid discharged from the capturer.                A washer (21) cleans the exhaust gas with a mist of                a cleaning liquid sprayed by a spray to capture the                amine accompanying the gas. The second washer (22)                is provided to wash the gas with another cleaning                liquid. A cleaning liquid line supplies the liquid                regenerated in the regenerator as the former                cleaning liquid to the spray, where the former                liquid is supplied as absorbing liquid.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system for use in                thermal power plant. Can also be used in other                types of thermal power plants such as hydroelectric                power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively reduces the amine                discharged from the carbon dioxide capture system.                The system suppresses the discharge of carbon                dioxide contained in the combustion exhaust gas                generated by burning fossil fuel into the                atmosphere. The carbon dioxide-containing gas                containing carbon dioxide can be prevented from                accompanying an amine when discharged to the                atmosphere from the absorption column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                operating a carbon dioxide capture system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide capture system.
   1Carbon dioxide capture system
   2combustion exhaust gas
   20carbon dioxide Capturer
   21washer
   22second washer

PN CN113842749-A
TI Alkanolamine-ionic liquid composite absorbent used for absorbing carbon dioxide in mixed gas, comprises water, and ionic liquid and ethanolamine in preset molar ratio.
AU WANG W
   LV R
   MA J
   YANG L
   QIN Y
   WANG C
   KONG J
   WANG T
   MU X
   WU Z
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 202209479S
AB    NOVELTY - An alkanolamine-ionic liquid composite carbon dioxide absorbent comprises ionic liquid, ethanolamine and water. The total concentration of ionic liquid and ethanolamine is 0.2-2.5 mol/L, and the molar ratio of ionic liquid and ethanolamine is 1:9-9:1.
   USE - Alkanolamine-ionic liquid composite absorbent is used for absorbing carbon dioxide in mixed gas e.g. pure carbon dioxide gas, fossil fuel power plant flue gas, automobile exhaust gas, chemical synthesis gas, coal-fired power plant flue gas and lime kiln gas (all claimed) in waste gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent has low toxicity, serious volatilization, regeneration temperature and regeneration energy consumption. The absorption rate of the absorbent, and the absorption capacity is equivalent to the same concentration of monoethanolamine (MEA), and which avoids the environment pollution and reduces the use cost. The absorbent does not only have low desorption temperature, but also has fast and high carbon dioxide absorption rate and solubility. The adsorbent can obviously improve the collecting rate of carbon dioxide, quickly and efficiently capture carbon dioxide. The regeneration process is simple, the operation condition is easy to control, even after multiple circulation absorption-desorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the composite carbon dioxide absorbent, which involves uniformly mixing the ionic liquid, ethanolamine and water according to the stoichiometric ratio; and
   (2) method for absorbing carbon dioxide in mixed gas, which involves using the composite carbon dioxide absorbent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide absorbing device.
   Gas phase control valve (4)
   Rotor flow meter (6)
   Transmission device (10)
   Liquid phase control valve (14)
   Super constant temperature water bath (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The total concentration of ionic liquid and ethanolamine is preferably 1.0 mol/L, and the molar ratio of ionic liquid to ethanolamine is preferably 1:9. The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 3-90 wt.%.Preferred Components: The ethanolamine is chosen from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine and N-methyldiethanolamine. The ionic liquid is chosen from 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium glycinate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium glycinate and 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110591124-A
TI Preparing polyamide master batch by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. mixing nano-sized toner particles, coupling agent and functional component, adding supercritical carbon dioxide, and wet grinding.
AU YAN Y
   YU K
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2019A75824
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyamide master batch by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) mixing 70-75 pts. wt. nano-sized toner particles, 5-10 pts. wt. coupling agent and 5-10 pts. wt. functional component, adding 5-10 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide as dispersant , and wet grinding to obtain color paste containing supercritical carbon dioxide, and (ii) adding 80-85 pts. wt. polyamide monomer and 5-10 pts. wt. deionized water agent into the polymerization kettle, controlling the polymerization pressure of the polymerization kettle at 1-1.2 MPa, carrying out polymerization reaction, when the relative viscosity of the polymerization reaches 1.1-2, reducing the temperature to 30-40 degrees C, adding 10 pts. wt. color paste containing supercritical carbon dioxide, heating to 60-70 degrees C, stirring and dispersing, continuing the in situ polymerization, completing the reaction, discharging through the polymerization kettle, washing, drying and granulating to obtain product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyamide master batch by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes physical dispersion of toner under a viscous liquid using supercritical carbon dioxide as a dispersant, uses high solubility toner molecule in supercritical carbon dioxide and high diffusivity of supercritical carbon dioxide to promote the diffusion of toner for dyeing, achieves uniformity of dyeing, and reduces energy consumption and pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polyamide masterbatch dispersed with supercritical carbon dioxide, comprising the polyamide master batch color fixation rate is 85-95%, and the color fastness is greater than or equal to grade 4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the particle size of the nano-sized toner particles is 10-100 nm, and the nano-sized toner particles is nano ferric oxide toner particles or nano copper oxide toner particles. In the step (i), the coupling agent is vinyltrichlorosilane, vinyltrimethoxysilane and/or vinyltriethoxysilane. In the step (i), the functional component is carbon nanotubes, graphene, tetrapod-shaped zinc oxide, needle-like calcium carbonate, nano silver powder and/or nano nickel powder. In the step (ii), the polyamide monomer is polyamide 6 monomer, polyamide 66 monomer or polyamide 612 monomer. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the controlling of the polymerization pressure in the polymerization kettle at 1.0-1.2 MPa is achieved through a pressure sensor.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.)
MC A05-F01C; A08-E01; A08-M01D; A08-M10; A08-S01; A11-A03; A11-A04; A12-S09A

PN WO2019187231-A1; AU2018413755-A1; US2020011576-A1; JP2019515686-X; JP6733814-B2; EP3779322-A1; AU2018413755-B2; EP3779322-A4; US11466903-B2; US2022357080-A1
TI Cooling system for pre-cooling natural gas in            normal temperature state, has specific heat exchanger            that is provided to cool pressurized carbon dioxide or            mixed refrigerant by vaporized carbon dioxide or mixed            refrigerant.
AU KIMIHIRO S
   HIROMICHI N
   TAIGA Y
   SAWA K
   NOMA H
   YAMAMOTO T
AE IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
   IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
   IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
   IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
GA 2019833650
AB    NOVELTY - The cooling system (1A) has a compressor (2)                which increases the pressure of carbon dioxide to                form pressurized carbon dioxide. A mixer (6) is                provided to generate a mixed refrigerant obtained                by mixing the pressurized carbon dioxide and liquid                state solvent. A decompression device (7) is                provided in a downstream side of the mixer for                reducing the pressure of the mixed refrigerant. A                separation apparatus (8) is provided to separate                the gaseous carbon dioxide from the mixed                refrigerant. A plate fin type heat exchanger is                provided to heat-exchange between the mixed                refrigerant cooled by reduced pressure, and cooling                object fluid. A second heat exchanger is provided                to cool the pressurized carbon dioxide or mixed                refrigerant by the vaporized carbon dioxide or                mixed refrigerant.
   USE - Cooling system for pre-cooling natural gas in                normal temperature state. Can also be used for                cooling foodstuff in freezing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - By including the second heat exchanger, the                pressurized carbon dioxide, the mixed refrigerant,                or solvent can be cooled by low-temperature carbon                dioxide or vaporized mixed refrigerant. Thus, the                energy efficiency of the cooling system is improved                by reducing the pressure after decreasing the                temperature of pressurized carbon dioxide and                solvent. Since the mixed refrigerant is used, the                occurrence of blocking in interior of piping due to                the generation of dry ice is reduced, and the                operation and the reliability of the cooling system                are improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                cooling system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
   Cooling system (1A)
   Compressor (2)
   Mixer (6)
   Decompression device (7)
   Separation apparatus (8)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC E31-N05C; Q75-A02A; Q75-T99

PN KR2018125288-A
TI Carbon-dioxide chamber used in control system for treating fruits with carbon-dioxide to ripen fruits, comprises main portion having transparent window, control unit, sensing unit, and gas inlet.
AU JUNG K S
   BAE N J
   SHIN E J
AE FOUND TECH COMMERCIALIZATION & TRANSFER (TECO-Non-standard)
GA 201896298A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-dioxide chamber for fruit comprises (a) main portion (10) having a transparent window (11) which is visually recognized inside a frame by forming a space in which fruit is loaded, (b) a control unit (20) located on one side of the main portion to control amount of carbon dioxide, and for sensing temperature, humidity, and carbon dioxide concentration of a sensing unit (40) provided in it and for performing communication with an external computer network, (c) sensing unit provided inside the main portion and having a temperature and humidity sensor capable of sensing temperature and humidity inside the chamber and a sensor capable of sensing the concentration of carbon dioxide, and (d) gas inlet (50) provided at one side of the main portion for supplying carbon dioxide supplied from the outside to the inside of the chamber.
   USE - The carbon-dioxide chamber is used in control system (claimed) for treating fruits with carbon-dioxide to ripen the fruits.
   ADVANTAGE - The chamber increases taste and aroma of the fruits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for control system , which comprises the carbon-dioxide chamber, personal digital assistant capable of communicating with the chamber and being an external device so as to be controlled from the outside, and server capable of performing a large amount of data processing and comprehensive control, and personnel computer (PC) for allowing a farmer or an administrator to connect to the server.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the carbon-dioxide chamber.
   Main portion (10)
   Transparent window (11)
   Control unit (20)
   Sensing unit (40)
   Gas inlet (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The chamber further comprises a notification unit interlocked with the control unit and for informing operator of the completion of the work and emergency situation, and a ventilation device for circulating air inside the chamber to the outside which is provided at one side of the main portion. The control unit comprises a display module, a control button, and a control valve for controlling the opening and closing of the gas supply pipe connecting carbon dioxide supply unit and main portion.

GA 2022D67209
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a dry ice generator                and carbon dioxide capture system. the dry ice                generator comprises a cavity, a feeding pipeline, a                gas phase carbon dioxide outlet and a dry ice                outlet; the feeding pipeline horizontally passes                through the wall surface of the cavity; one end of                the feeding pipeline is a liquid phase carbon                dioxide inlet, the feeding pipeline in the cavity                is provided with a sprayer, the sprayer is provided                with a liquid outlet hole horizontally upward, the                liquid outlet hole is used for spraying carbon                dioxide liquid drop; the gas phase carbon dioxide                outlet is set on the top of the cavity; the dry ice                outlet is set at the bottom of the cavity. The                utility model can process carbon dioxide gas with                different concentrations, preparing dry ice with                large density and high purity, reducing storage and                transportation cost; using dry ice generator and                carbon dioxide capture system can avoid using                expensive chemical absorbent, reducing the                collecting cost; firstly condensing into liquid and                then spraying and granulating to form dry ice                process, it can realize continuous operation on the                engineering, which is good for production in large                scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide capturing system.

PN CN215690801-U
TI Mask for correcting excessive ventilation, has carbon dioxide detecting component that is set on function interface for detecting carbon dioxide concentration in mask main portion and air bag.
AU WANG J
   XUE J
   YANG L
   MEI S
AE SHANGHAI SIXTH PEOPLES HOSPITAL (USJT-C)
GA 202225353V
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a mask for correcting excessive ventilation, comprising a mask main body, an air suction hole, an air storage bag, a carbon dioxide detection component and an exhaust hole, the front end of the mask main body is provided with a functional interface; the air suction holes are set at two sides of the mask main body; the air storage airbag is connected with the mask main body through the functional interface; the carbon dioxide detecting component is set on the function interface for detecting the carbon dioxide concentration in the mask main body and the air bag; the exhaust hole is set on the air storage bag; The suction hole and the exhaust hole can be opened or closed. The mask provided by the utility model can make the patient repeatedly inhale the gas with high carbon dioxide concentration, relieving and treating excessive ventilation syndrome; and the mask main body is further provided with a carbon dioxide detecting component, the concentration of carbon dioxide in the mask main body and air bag can be detected, avoiding excessive correction caused by excessive carbon dioxide concentration; when the concentration of carbon dioxide is high, the suction hole and the exhaust hole can be opened, so that the patient breath air.

TI Liquefied carbon dioxide energy storage system,            has regenerator whose first outlet is connected with            inlet of liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, where            outlet of carbon dioxide storage tank is connected with            second inlet of regenerator.
AU LIU Z
   SUN W
   YANG X
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202115156L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooler(20) whose first outlet                is connected with an inlet of an expander(11). An                outlet of the expander is connected with a first                inlet of a regenerator(15). A first outlet of the                regenerator is connected with an inlet of a liquid                carbon dioxide storage tank(17). An outlet of the                liquid carbon dioxide storage tank is connected                with a second inlet of the regenerator through a                throttle valve(16). An outlet of a cold tank(19) is                connected with a second inlet of the cooler. A                second outlet of the cooler is connected with an                inlet of a hot tank(23).
   USE - Liquefied carbon dioxide energy storage                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is flexible to use an artificial                container, and utilizes carbon dioxide as energy                storage medium, improvs energy density, forms a                closed loop, so as to avoid releasing greenhouse                gas to environment, realizes power grid frequency                modulation, peak clipping and valley filling, and                increases renewable energy utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                liquefied carbon dioxide energy storage system                using method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the liquefied carbon dioxide energy storage                system.

   Regenerator (15)
   Throttle valve (16)
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank                (17)
   Cold tank (19)
   Cooler (20)
   Hot tank. (23)

PN CN209516301-U
TI Carbon dioxide laser tube, has carbon dioxide laser tube main body whose inner part is provided with spiral pipe, where top part of carbon dioxide laser tube main body is fixed with fixing plate and bottom part of main body is provided with vent.
AU GAO Y
AE BEIJING RUISHU LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201989919J
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide laser tube can fast heat dissipation, comprising a carbon dioxide laser tube body, the carbon dioxide laser tube main body is provided with a spiral pipe, the top part of the carbon dioxide laser tube body is fixedly installed with a fixing plate; the fixed plate is fixedly mounted with a fan, fixed on the top of the fixing plate is mounted with a connecting plate, the top of the connecting plate is fixed with a water tank, the water tank is at one side of the top part of connecting plate is fixedly mounted with a water pump, the bottom of the carbon dioxide laser tube main body is provided with a vent. The utility model is provided with a water pump, a fan, a water tank, a first connecting pipe, a second connecting pipe, ventilation window, the ventilating port and spiral tube, solving the problem that the existing carbon dioxide laser tube when using heat dissipation slowly, resulting in carbon dioxide laser tube cannot be used for a long time and the heat dissipation, without any circulation function, problem is waste of resource.

PN KR2024753-B1; WO2020139055-A1
TI Apparatus for separating carbon dioxide contained in mixed gas, has controller for maintaining pressure of gas flowing into rich gas vortex tube within predetermined range, and lean gas collection chamber for storing carbon dioxide lean gas.
AU YEON I
   YU S S
   IM S Y
AE UNIV TONGMYONG IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UTIA-C)
   UNIV IND & ACADEMIC COOP IN CHUNGNAM NAT (UCHU-C)
GA 201986373F
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a condenser (100) for condensing mixed gas before the mixed gas enters into a compressor (120). An oxygen removal catalyst (110) is fixed to the compressor after removing oxygen from the mixed gas. A lean gas condenser condenses gas collected in a lean gas collection chamber (140). A lean chamber pressure sensor senses internal pressures of the lean gas collection chamber. A lean gas compressor compresses the condensed gas. A controller maintains pressure of the gas flowing into a rich gas vortex tube within a predetermined range. The lean gas collection chamber stores carbon dioxide lean gas.
   USE - Apparatus for separating carbon dioxide contained in mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The controller maintains pressure of the gas flowing into the rich gas vortex tube within the predetermined range so as to separate the carbon dioxide without using a separate carbon dioxide absorbent, so that carbon dioxide separation can be repeated in multiple stages to separate the carbon dioxide in an effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide separation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an apparatus for separating carbon dioxide contained in mixed gas.
   Condenser (100)
   Oxygen removal catalyst (110)
   Compressor (120)
   Vortex tube (130)
   Lean gas collection chamber (140)

PN KR2019073915-A; KR2012480-B1
TI Hermetic supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system has control unit that is configured to perform first stage injection mode for controlling carbon dioxide from pump to enter cooler through bypass passage.
AU CHO J H
   RA H S
   BAIK Y J
   SHIN H K
   LEE G B
   LEE B J
   ROH C W
   CHO J J
   CHOI B S
   JO J H
   BAEK Y J
   SHIN H
   BONG L K
   SU C
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019566826
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide injection unit which is configured for injecting the carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide tank (101) to a suction side of a pump (10). A cooler (40) is configured to cool the carbon dioxide from one of a turbine (30) and the pump by heat exchange with cooling water. A first expansion valve (111) is configured for opening and closing a first bypass flow passage (110), and for expanding carbon dioxide from the pump. A heater inlet valve (25) is installed in a flow path connecting the pump and the heater to regulate carbon dioxide from the pump to flow into the heater. The first expansion valve is opened and the heater inlet valve is shielded, when the injection of carbon dioxide is started from the carbon dioxide injection unit. A control unit is configured to perform a first stage injection mode for controlling carbon dioxide from the pump to enter the cooler through the first bypass passage, to be cooled, and to be circulated back to the pump.
   USE - Hermetic supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature of the carbon dioxide and the saturation pressure is lowered by cooling the carbon dioxide from the pump at the initial stage of filling the carbon dioxide by the cooler. The carbon dioxide can be smoothly injected without operating the carbon dioxide supply pump separately by securing the pressure difference between the pressure of carbon dioxide flowing into the pump and the supply pressure supplied from the carbon dioxide tank. The carbon dioxide filling can be effectively performed as the carbon dioxide is charged, even if the pressure inside the system is changed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling hermetic supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system.

PT P
PN US2019168185-A1; US10953384-B2
TI Capturing and separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture, involves exposing carbon dioxide sorbent to gas mixture that contains carbon dioxide and treating sorbent that contains adsorbed carbon dioxide under conditions sufficient.
AU GOEPPERT A
   ZHANG H
   OLAH G A
   PRAKASH G K S
AE UNIV SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA (USCA-C)
GA 201949383Q
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and separating carbon dioxide from a gas mixture, comprises: exposing a carbon dioxide sorbent to a gas mixture that contains carbon dioxide to effect adsorption of the carbon dioxide by the sorbent; and treating the sorbent that contains adsorbed carbon dioxide under conditions sufficient to release the adsorbed carbon dioxide either at a higher carbon dioxide concentration or as purified carbon dioxide, where the sorbent has sufficiently high surface area for solid-gas contact and sufficient structural integrity for adsorbing carbon dioxide from gas mixture without degrading, the sorbent comprises modified polyamine which is supported upon and within solid support, with the modified polyamine formed as reaction product that includes amine functionalities from reaction of excess of amine and epoxide, and with the solid support.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing and separating carbon dioxide from a gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes sorbent that provides structural integrity, as well as high selectivity and increased capacity for efficiently capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixtures, including the air, and is regenerative, and can be used through multiple cycles of adsorption-desorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing and separating carbon dioxide from a gas mixture, comprises: exposing a carbon dioxide sorbent to a gas mixture that contains carbon dioxide to effect adsorption of the carbon dioxide by the sorbent; and treating the sorbent that contains adsorbed carbon dioxide under conditions sufficient to release the adsorbed carbon dioxide either at a higher carbon dioxide concentration or as purified carbon dioxide, where the sorbent has a sufficiently high surface area for solid-gas contact and sufficient structural integrity for adsorbing carbon dioxide from the gas mixture without degrading, the sorbent comprises a modified polyamine which is supported upon and within a solid support, with the modified polyamine formed as a reaction product that includes amine functionalities from reaction of an excess of amine and an epoxide, and with the solid support is (a) a nano-structured support of silica, silica-alumina, alumina, titanium oxide, calcium silicate, carbon nanotubes, and/or carbon, and having a primary particle size of less than 100 nm, or (b) a natural and/or synthetic clay.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sorbent is provided in a fixed, moving, or fluidized bed and the gas and bed are in contact for a sufficient time to trap the carbon dioxide in the sorbent, where the sorbent is treated with sufficient heat, reduced pressure, vacuum and/or gas purge to release a substantial amount or all of the adsorbed carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is captured by the sorbent and separated from ambient air at low carbon dioxide concentrations of 200-5000 ppm. The carbon dioxide is captured by the sorbent and separated from ambient air at moderate temperatures of less than 55 degrees C. The carbon dioxide sorbent is regenerative for capturing and separating carbon dioxide for at least one adsorption/regeneration cycle. The carbon dioxide is captured from air having a carbon dioxide concentration of 200-5000 ppm. The adsorbent regeneration temperature is less than 130 degrees C. The sorbent is prepared by combining the amine, epoxide, support and a solvent in a mixture, mixing and heating the mixture for a sufficient time to allow the amine and epoxide to combine as the reaction product and to be provided upon and within the support, followed by removal of the solvent to obtain the sorbent as a solid material. The sorbent is prepared by dispersing the support in a solvent to form a suspension, dissolving the amine in the same solvent to form an amine solution, providing the epoxide in a solution with the same solvent or in a concentrated form, and combining the suspension, the amine solution, and the epoxide or epoxide solution in a mixture with agitation or stirring for a period of time to form a reaction product between the amine and the epoxide, with the solvent removed by heating the liquid reaction product, if necessary under vacuum, to obtain the sorbent as a solid. The sorbent is prepared by dissolving the amine in solvent to form an amine solution, adding the epoxide, in a concentrated form or in the form of a solution having the same solvent, to the amine solution to form a mixture, mixing the mixture at a temperature of 15-30 degrees C for 0.1-50 hours, then heating the mixture to allow for complete reaction between the amine and epoxide, followed by heating for 30 seconds to 300 minutes to remove part or all of the solvent, with any remaining solvent removed by heating, if necessary, under vacuum, to obtain the modified amine, dispersing the support in the same solvent to form a suspension, and forming the sorbent by adding the modified amine to the dispersion of the support with stirring to disperse the modified polyamine onto the support. The method further comprises: adding a polyol or polyether to the mixture before removing of the solvent; reacting the released carbon dioxide to form methanol, dimethyl ether, formic acid or carbon monoxide; reacting the released carbon dioxide to form methanol, and dehydrating the methanol under conditions sufficient to produce dimethyl ether; heating the dimethyl ether in the presence of an acidic-basic or zeolitic catalyst under conditions sufficient to form ethylene or propylene; and converting the ethylene and/or propylene under conditions sufficient to higher olefins, synthetic hydrocarbons, aromatics, or a product produced from its, for use as a feedstock for chemicals or as transportation fuel; or hydrating the ethylene or propylene under conditions sufficient to form ethanol or propanol. Preferred Components: The amine is a primary, secondary or tertiary alkyl- or alkanolamine, an aromatic amine, a mixed amine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine, triethylenetetramine, diethylenetriamine, ethylenediamine, hexaethyleneheptamine, a polyethylenimine, a polyvinylamine, polyallylamine, and/or other polymeric amine compounds, where the epoxide is a simple epoxide, diepoxide, triepoxide, a polyepoxide compound, polymeric epoxide, ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-epoxybutane, 2,3-epoxybutane, glycidol, butyl glycidyl ether, t-butyl glycidyl ether, dodecyl and tetradecyl glycidyl ethers, octyl/decyl glycidyl ether, 1,2-epoxycyclohexane, epichlorohydrin, glycerol diglycidyl ether, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol diglycidyl ether, neopentyl glycol diglycidyl ether, poly(ethylene glycol) diglycidyl ether, resorcinol diglycidyl ether, poly(propylene glycol) diglycidyl ether, 4,4'-isopropylidenediphenol diglycidyl ether, 1,2,5,6-diepoxycyclooctane, trimethylolpropane triglycidyl ether, N,N-diglycidyl-4-glycidyloxyaniline and/or 4,4'-methylenebis(N,N-diglycidylaniline). The modified polyamine is present in an amount of 1-90 wt.% of the sorbent, or in an approximately equal amount by weight as the support. The sorbent further comprises a polyethylene oxide and/or polyol in an amount of 1-25 wt.% of the sorbent, where the polyol is glycerol, oligomers of ethylene glycol or polyethylene glycol, and the polyethylene oxides may be present as their corresponding ethers.
   EXAMPLE - Supported polyamine adsorbent was first placed in round bottom flask and evacuated (~30 mTorr) at 85 degrees C for 3 hours to desorb carbon dioxide and water present on the adsorbent. After this pretreatment, 1 g of the adsorbent was placed in a straight glass tube between two glass wool plugs thermostated at 25 degrees C. The adsorbent weight (1 g) after pretreatment was used for the later calculation of the carbon dioxide adsorption capacities. For the adsorption measurements a Horiba VIA-S 10 carbon dioxide analyzer equipped with an infrared detector specifically intended for carbon dioxide measurements was placed in-line with the adsorption setup. Before the experiment, the analyzer was calibrated with reference gases, carbon dioxide in air and ultra zero grade air for the zero. An air mixture containing 1000 ppm carbon dioxide and 50% moisture (dew point of 14 degrees C) was used for the adsorption measurements. The air flow (~335 ml/minutes) was then opened on the adsorbent bed. Almost immediately the carbon dioxide concentration in the gas outlet fell to a value lower than 10 ppm, signaling essentially complete carbon dioxide adsorption from the air. The carbon dioxide concentration was recorded as a function of time via lab-view 8.6. After an initial period close to 0 ppm carbon dioxide, the concentration in the outlet gas started to increase. After saturation of the adsorbent, when the carbon dioxide concentration reached a value close to the inlet value (1000 ppm), the gas flow was stopped. The total adsorption capacity was determined to be 106 mg carbon dioxide/g adsorbent (2.4 mmol carbon dioxide/g adsorbent) after 5 hours of adsorption. The desorption of the carbon dioxide on the adsorbent was performed by heating the adsorbent containing glass tube to 50 degrees C with a heating tape and then passing a flow of air containing 400 ppm carbon dioxide and 13% humidity (dew point of 14 degrees C) (335 ml/minutes) through it for 1 hour. The carbon dioxide concentration was recorded as a function of time via lab-view 8.6. Heating resulted in an increase of the carbon dioxide concentration to values above 5000 ppm followed by a decrease until a carbon dioxide concentration close to the inlet concentration (400 ppm carbon dioxide) was reached. This initial adsorption/desorption cycles was followed by 10 additional adsorption/desorption cycles under the same conditions except for the adsorption time which was reduced to 3 hours (adsorption at 25 degrees C for 3 hours, 1000 ppm carbon dioxide in air, 50% humidity (dew point of 14 degrees C), 335 ml/minutes and desorption at 50 degrees C for 1 hour, 400 ppm carbon dioxide in air, 13% humidity (dew point of 14 degrees C), 335 ml/minutes). The adsorption capacity remained stable at around 96-98 mg carbon dioxide/g adsorbent.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

The volume ratio of the isopropyl alcohol to the deionized water is 1:10 to 10:1; and the volume ratio of the Nafion solution to the mixed solution is 1 :1000-1:100. PN US2018290892-A1; US10570018-B2
TI Method for production of carbon dioxide, involves mixing lime with water to obtain lime slurry, pouring slurry into designed conveyor pans followed by placing chains into pans, preparing lime and carbon dioxide, removing and storing.
AU MISIRIAN H
   MISIRIAN H J
AE MISIRIAN H (MISI-Individual)
   MISIRIAN H J (MISI-Individual)
GA 2018782547
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide production method involves mixing lime with water to obtain a lime slurry. The lime slurry is poured into specially designed conveyor pans. Chains are placed into pans, prior to pouring the lime slurry, in which chains act as an enforcement for the lime panels and to use magnetic, moving hardened slurry panels from conveyor to cable hangers, moving cable hangers to provide fresh air contact with the hardened lime panels, reacting lime panels and saturating with carbon dioxide, converting lime to limestone within 24 hours exposure to the air, crushing limestone panels, after grinding, filling into the containers or preparing lime and carbon dioxide from limestone, removing carbon dioxide and storing, and repeating continuously, using the same lime.

PN CN112742349-A
TI Magnetic ionic liquid nano composite adsorbent material for capturing carbon dioxide is composite material containing amine-based ionic liquid loaded on surface of nano magnetic material carrier.
AU JIANG Y
   CHEN X
   HUANG Z
   KONG J
   WANG D
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202150876C
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic ionic liquid nano composite adsorbent material for capturing carbon dioxide is a composite material containing an amine-based ionic liquid loaded on the surface of a nano magnetic material carrier.
   USE - The adsorbent material is useful for capturing carbon dioxide, where the adsorbent material is suitable for a gas source with a carbon dioxide partial pressure of 5 kPa to 1 MPa and the carbon dioxide adsorption temperature is 30-130 degrees C (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent material has high adsorption capacity, strong stability, no loss of ionic liquid, large carbon dioxide capacity at higher temperature and fast and efficient recovery and excellent prospect in the application of carbon dioxide capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The loaded ionic liquid content is 20-90 wt.%, preferably 40-70 wt.%, where the nano-magnetic material is ferroso ferric oxide-silica. Preferred Method: The adsorption material is prepared by (1) adding iron(III)chloride hexahydrate and ferrous chloride tetrahydrate to 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride under nitrogen protection, stirring and mixing thoroughly and gradually dripping concentrated aqueous ammonia to the system to react, where the aqueous ammonia reacts with the reactants in the solution layer to generate ferroso ferric oxide, washing, magnetically separating and drying and obtaining powdery ferroso ferric oxide particles, (2) dissolving the surfactant in hydrochloric acid at room temperature, where the pH of the solution is 1-5, adding trimethylbenzene and ammonium fluoride, heating at 37-38 degrees C with stirring for more than 30 minutes, adding powdered ferroso ferric oxide particles and a silicon source compound, heating at 100-120 degrees C for 20-22 hours, cooling the mixture to room temperature, magnetically separating to obtain a solid product, washed, magnetically separating, drying and calcining at 400-450 degrees C for greater than or equal to 8 hours and (3) dissolving the ionic liquid in ethanol, dispersing the carrier obtained in step (2) in the ethanol solution of the ionic liquid, stirring at 20-50 degrees C for 8-10 hours, washing, magnetically separating and drying to obtain powdery particles, removing ethanol and drying at 50-60 degrees C, where the mass ratio of the surfactant to trimethylbenzene is (2:1)-(1:4) and the mass ratio of the silicon source compound to the surfactant is (3:1)-(1:3). Preferred Components: The silicon source compound is ethyl silicate, sodium silicate and sodium silicate of formula: NaHSi2O5.3H2O.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid has cation which is organic amine salt cations and organic alcohol amine cations and anion having an amino acid structure of formula: H2N-R-COOH. The surfactants are non-ionic surfactants pan80 and tween series. The cationic surfactant is sodium bis(2-ethylhexyl) succinate sulfonate, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, and cetyltrimethylamine. The anionic surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate.R = CnH2n;andn = 1-5.

TI Producing gaseous drink by providing thermally decomposable material capable of releasing carbon dioxide in heating chamber and energy to heat material, releasing carbon dioxide from material and collecting and infusing carbon dioxide.
AU SHALEV P
AE SO SPARK LTD (SOSP-Non-standard)
   SO SPARK LTD (SOSP-Non-standard)
GA 201848505T
AB    NOVELTY - Method of producing a gaseous drink involves (i) providing a material capable of releasing carbon dioxide upon thermal decomposition, into a heating chamber, (ii) providing energy to the heating chamber to heat the material, (iii) releasing carbon dioxide from thermal decomposition of the material and (iv) collecting and infusing the released carbon dioxide into a liquid reservoir to create the gaseous drink.
   USE - The method is used for producing a gaseous drink, preferably beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is efficient and quickly extracts the carbon dioxide, enables an efficient transfer of heat between the water and powder and reduces water loss substantially, minimizes time consumption, and prevents the thermally decomposable material particles from being expelled from capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material comprises sodium bicarbonate and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises providing radio frequency energy. The method further involves circulating liquid through carbon dioxide released from thermal decomposition of the material. The step (i) involves enclosing the material in a capsule provided into the heating chamber and the step (iii) involves releasing carbon dioxide in the capsule. The method further involves collecting the released carbon dioxide from the capsule, filtering the material from the released carbon dioxide generated in the capsule, and disposing the capsule and reusing the heating chamber. The method involves controlling pressure in the heating chamber during the step (ii), where carbon dioxide is released in the heating chamber under pressure. The capsule comprises a first portion made of metal and a second portion made of material substantially transparent to radio frequency energy and the method involving adding different materials to the first portion and to the second portion. The method further involves monitoring the pressure evolved during the release of carbon dioxide from the material with a pressure meter, providing a control circuit operatively connected to the pressure meter and an energy generator for providing the energy and changing at least the power of the energy generated by the energy generator responsive to a signal from the pressure meter. The energy generator is a radio frequency energy generator and the method further involves changing the frequency of the radio frequency energy generator in response to the signal from the pressure meter.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   YAN Y
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Solid oxide electrolytic cell electrolysis                low-cost carbon dioxide synthesis gas system                comprises direct collecting carbon dioxide unit (A)                and solid oxide electrolytic cell unit (B). Direct                collecting carbon dioxide unit includes dryer (1-2)                for filtering and drying air or flue gas (1). Air                inlet of the dryer communicates with air or flue                gas. Air outlet is connected with air inlet on top                of carbon dioxide absorbing chamber (1-6). Pipeline                connecting top air inlet of carbon dioxide                absorbing chamber with dryer is sequentially                provided with second flow adjusting valve. Pipeline                connecting top air inlet of carbon dioxide                absorbing chamber with dryer is provided with                second flow adjusting valve, first air pump and                first carbon dioxide concentration detector. Bottom                air outlet of carbon dioxide absorbing chamber is                divided into two paths through first three-way                valve. Branch road is provided with second carbon                dioxide concentration detector.
   USE - Used as solid oxide electrolytic cell                electrolysis low-cost carbon dioxide synthesis gas                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is economical and environmental                friendly; and realizes the negative emission of                carbon dioxide, and the resource utilization of                greenhouse gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solid oxide electrolytic cell electrolysis                low-cost carbon dioxide synthesis gas system                comprises a direct collecting carbon dioxide unit                and a solid oxide electrolytic cell unit. The                direct collecting carbon dioxide unit includes a                dryer (1-2) for filtering and drying air or flue                gas (1). An air inlet of the dryer communicates                with the air or flue gas. An air outlet is                connected with the air inlet on the top of a carbon                dioxide absorbing chamber (1-6). A pipeline                connecting the top air inlet of the carbon dioxide                absorbing chamber with the dryer is sequentially                provided with a second flow adjusting valve. A                pipeline connecting the top air inlet of the carbon                dioxide absorbing chamber with the dryer is                provided with the second flow adjusting valve                (1-3), a first air pump (1-4) and a first carbon                dioxide concentration detector. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for producing synthesis gas                by electrolysis of low-cost carbon dioxide in a                solid oxide electrolytic cell comprising adopting                directly capturing carbon dioxide and solid oxide                electrolysis cell to co-producing synthesis gas by                involving (i) directly capturing of carbon dioxide                including (a) drying the air or flue gas, filtering                by the dryer, entering into the carbon dioxide                absorbing chamber, adsorbing the carbon dioxide in                the air or flue gas by the carbon dioxide adsorbent                in the carbon dioxide absorbing chamber, passing                the air or flue gas through the first three-way                valve on a branch of the outlet of the carbon                dioxide absorbing chamber, and discharging the                second carbon dioxide concentration detector and                the third flow adjusting valve into the atmosphere,                and (b) processing carbon dioxide desorption and                storage including humidifying the carbon dioxide                adsorbent in the carbon dioxide absorbing chamber                by using atomized water vapor, desorbing carbon                dioxide by the saturated carbon dioxide adsorbent                because of high humidity, carrying water vapor by                desorbed carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide                absorbing chamber, separating waste water and a                mixture of carbon dioxide and air by the first                three-way valve, condenser and gas-liquid                separator, storing the mixture of carbon dioxide                and air in the gas tank through the second air pump                for specific period of time, reaching the adsorbed                carbon dioxide to desorption limit, and realizing                regeneration of carbon dioxide adsorbent, and (ii)                producing synthesis gas by solid oxide electrolytic                cell including heating the heating furnace to the                reaction temperature by argon gas protection,                connecting the external voltage on both sides of                the cathode and anode of the electrolytic cell, (a)                performing cathode reaction including discharging                the mixture of carbon dioxide and air from the gas                tank in the directly capturing carbon dioxide unit,                passing through the steam generator, carrying water                through the third three-way valve into the cathode                ceramic tube at the cathode to obtain electron to                generating synthesis gas and oxygen ion ions with a                hydrogen-carbon according to the ratio of                1:1-1.1:1, and entering oxygen ion the anode                through the electrolyte layer, and (b) performing                anode reaction including carrying out an electron                loss reaction by oxygen ion to generating oxygen,                and carrying out the partial oxidation reaction                with fuel methane to generating synthesis gas with                a hydrogen-carbon according to the ratio of                1.9:1-2.1:1.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the solid                oxide electrolytic cell electrolysis low-cost                carbon dioxide synthesis gas system.
   Direct collecting carbon dioxide unit                (A)
   Solid oxide electrolytic cell unit (B)
   Air or flue gas (1)
   Dryer (1-2)
   Carbon dioxide absorbing chamber (1-6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A drying agent in the                  drier comprises anhydrous calcium chloride,                  allochroic silica gel or molecular sieve. A                  carbon dioxide adsorbent in the carbon dioxide                  absorbing chamber comprises active carbon loaded                  with sodium carbonate, molecular sieve or anion                  exchange resin nano-porous material. In the step                  (i), the gas tank in directly collecting carbon                  dioxide unit comprises mixture of carbon dioxide                  and air, and the concentration of carbon dioxide                  is 30-90 vol.%. In the step (ii), the external                  voltage in the solid oxide electrolytic cell unit                  is 0.8-1.5 V. The material of the anode in the                  solid oxide electrolytic cell unit comprises                  perovskite or hexaaluminate. The material of the                  cathode in the solid oxide electrolytic cell unit                  comprises metal ceramic or perovskite. The                  material of the electrolyte in the solid oxide                  electrolytic cell unit comprises doped zirconium                  oxide, cerium oxide, yttrium oxide, perovskite or                  bismuth oxide complex oxide. Preferred Method: In                  the step (i), directly capturing of carbon                  dioxide through the indicating of the first                  carbon dioxide concentration detector and the                  second carbon dioxide concentration detector                  connecting to the inlet and outlet of the carbon                  dioxide absorbing chamber, determining whether                  the carbon dioxide adsorbent is saturating, and                  indicating that the adsorbent is saturating, when                  the indications of the two carbon dioxide                  concentration detectors are the same. The                  directly collecting carbon dioxide unit in the                  gas-liquid separator (1-12) separating from the                  waste water and adding to the atomized water                  vapor for humidifying the carbon dioxide                  adsorbent. In the step (ii), the temperature of                  the heating furnace in the solid oxide                  electrolytic cell unit at 600-1000 degrees C and                  maintaining the high temperature requiring for                  the reaction by using focused solar energy as a                  heat source. PN CN113070094-A
TI Catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene aryl alkylation, is prepared by uniformly mixing copper-based catalyst with HZSM-5 molecular sieve, where copper-based catalyst comprises mixed oxide comprising copper main portion and modified metal.
AU XIAO Z
   YANG Z
   XU J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202185128H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst is prepared by uniformly mixing copper-based catalyst with HZSM-5 molecular sieve, where copper-based catalyst comprises mixed oxide comprising copper main portion and modified metal, the mass ratio of Cu-based catalyst and surface HZSM-5 molecular sieve is 1:3-3:1, the modified metal comprises zirconium, zinc, cerium and/or titanium, the content of copper oxide is 50-95 wt.%, and the content of modified metal is 5-50 wt.%.
   USE - The catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene aryl alkylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst reduces the side reaction of methanol to olefins caused by improper methanol/toluene feeding ratio, improves the production efficiency of xylene, inhibits the isomerization reaction of xylene, improves the selectivity of p-xylene in product, and has excellent stability at high temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing catalyst, involving (a) taking copper nitrate and zirconium nitrate solid to stoichiometric number, and dissolving in ethanol to obtain ethanol solution to obtain solution (I), (b) configuring precipitant solution, dripping obtained solution (I) to precipitant solution, uniformly stirring and reacting to obtain solution (II), (c) performing suction filtration on solution (II), drying to obtain precursor (I), and calcining obtained precursor (I) to obtain component (I), and (d) uniformly mixing component (I) with HZSM-5 molecular sieve modified by surface silicon coating method, grinding and tableting to obtain catalyst; and
   (2) a use of catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene aryl alkylation by above-mentioned method, involving performing activation process by loading catalyst to fixed bed reactor, using hydrogen/nitrogen mixture gas with concentration of 0-100% or hydrogen/argon mixture gas with concentration of 0-100% for performing pretreatment at 250-500 degrees C under pressure of 0-2 MPa, and performing activation process for 1-50 hours, after completing activation process, reducing the pressure in fixed bed reactor to normal pressure, adjusting the temperature in fixed bed reactor to 300-500 degrees C, passing fixed bed reactor with molar ratio of 0.5:1-1:5, using carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas for reacting with toluene, where the reaction pressure is 0.5-8 MPa, the gas phase space velocity of reaction feed is 1000-50000/hour, the liquid phase space velocity is 0.1-3/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified metal comprises zirconium and/or zinc. The precipitating agent in step (b) comprises oxalic acid, sodium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonia and its hydrate, which are soluble in water Preferred Composition: The total concentration of copper nitrate and zirconium nitrate in ethanol solution in step (a) is 1 M, and the molar ratio of metal ions of copper and zirconium is 0.5:0.5-0.95:0.05.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The HZSM-5 molecular sieve is HZSM-5 molecular sieve modified by surface silicon coating method. The SAR of the HZSM-5 molecular sieve is 12.5-125. Preferred Method: The process of HZSM-5 molecular sieve modified by silicon coating method involves adding HZSM-5 to tetraethylorthosilicate cyclohexane solution and soaking for 4 hours, rotating for removing cyclohexane at 80 degrees C, drying and calcining at 550 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute under air for 4 hours, and performing above-mentioned process for 1-5 times to obtain HZSM-5 molecular sieve modified by silicon coating method. Preferred Composition: The concentration of precipitant in step (b) is 0.5 M. Preferred Conditions: The method involves stirring reaction for 0.5 hour, and calcining at 400 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/min under air for 3 hours. The application involves performing activation process by loading catalyst to fixed bed reactor, and pretreating nitrogen concentration, where the pretreatment temperature is 300 degrees C, under pressure of 0 MPa for 2 hours, and reacting by adjusting the temperature in fixed bed reactor to 360-420 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide-hydrogen mixed gas with molar ratio of 1:3 to fixed bed reactor for reacting with toluene, where the reaction pressure is 3-5 MPa, the gas-phase space velocity of reaction feed is 12000/hour, and the liquid-phase space velocity is 1/hour.
