PT P
PN CN111138392-A
TI Preparing furandicarboxylic acid using furfural involves preparing transition metal monoatomic catalyst by light method, dispersing graphene in water to obtain graphene dispersion liquid, adding transition metal salt aqueous solution.
AU QIAN C
   JIN H
   ZHOU S
   RUAN J
   WANG S
   CHEN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020424657
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing furandicarboxylic acid using furfural involves preparing transition metal monoatomic catalyst by light method, dispersing graphene in water to obtain graphene dispersion liquid as catalyst load. The ratio of transition metal and graphene is 1.0-1.5. The transition metal salt aqueous solution is added to the graphene dispersion liquid, placed under light source to irradiate stirring reaction for 3-6 hours. The reaction product is frozen until solid is formed, and then freeze-dried in vaccum to obtain transition metal monoatomic catalyst. The transition metal monoatomic catalyst is added to the autoclave equipped with furfural, and carbon dioxide and oxygen are introduced for catalytic oxidation reaction. The ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide is 2:1. The mass ratio of transition metal monoatomic catalyst to furfural is 5-10%. The reaction temperature is 90-120 degrees C, pressure is 0.1-1.0 megapascal, and reaction is stirred for 15-20 hours, after reaction is completed.
   USE - Method for preparing furandicarboxylic acid using furfural.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare furandicarboxylic acid using furfural that has characteristics of simple process, mild conditions, environmental friendliness, high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing furandicarboxylic acid using furfural involves preparing transition metal monoatomic catalyst by light method, dispersing graphene in water to obtain graphene dispersion liquid as catalyst load. The ratio of transition metal and graphene is 1.0-1.5. The transition metal salt aqueous solution is added to the graphene dispersion liquid, placed under light source to irradiate stirring reaction for 3-6 hours. The reaction product is frozen until solid is formed, and then freeze-dried in vaccum to obtain transition metal monoatomic catalyst. The transition metal monoatomic catalyst is added to the autoclave equipped with furfural, and carbon dioxide and oxygen are introduced for catalytic oxidation reaction. The ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide is 2:1. The mass ratio of transition metal monoatomic catalyst to furfural is 5-10%. The reaction temperature is 90-120 degrees C, pressure is 0.1-1.0 megapascal, and reaction is stirred for 15-20 hours, after reaction is completed. The solvent is added to dissolve the reaction product. The resulting reaction solution is filtered, and filtrate is rectified under reduced pressure to obtain furandicarboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metals are cobalt, nickel, copper, rhodium, palladium and silver. The solvent is ethyl acetate.

TI Direct reduction process for producing sponge iron            by using converter gas and coke oven gas comprises            passing high-temperature converter gas, purifying,            desulfurizing and passing gas-based direct reduction            shaft furnace to reduce iron ore.
AU CHEN L
AE UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYSS-C)
GA 201917720D
AB    NOVELTY - Direct reduction process for producing sponge                iron by using converter gas and coke oven gas                comprises passing the high-temperature converter                gas discharged from the converter to the converter                gas flue through the hood, purifying and                desulfurizing the coke oven gas, adding into the                converter gas flue using gas injection and                reforming endothermic reaction of methane in the                coke oven gas and carbon dioxide in the converter                gas, rapidly reducing the temperature of the gas in                the converter gas flue to 850-900 degrees C,                subjecting the high-temperature gas at 850-900                degrees C from the converter gas flue to hot                cyclone dust removal, passing the gas-based direct                reduction shaft furnace to reduce iron ore.
   USE - The direct reduction method is useful for                producing sponge iron by using converter gas and                coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and has low                energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Direct reduction process for producing sponge                iron by using converter gas and coke oven gas                comprises passing the high-temperature converter                gas discharged from the converter to the converter                gas flue through the hood, purifying and                desulfurizing the coke oven gas, adding into the                converter gas flue using gas injection and                reforming endothermic reaction of methane in the                coke oven gas and carbon dioxide in the converter                gas, rapidly reducing the temperature of the gas in                the converter gas flue to 850-900 degrees C,                subjecting the high-temperature gas at 850-900                degrees C from the converter gas flue to hot                cyclone dust removal, passing the gas-based direct                reduction shaft furnace to reduce iron ore and                generating a shaft furnace top gas, where the high                temperature converter gas discharged from the                converter has a temperature of 1400-1650 degrees C,                the purified and desulfurized coke oven gas has a                temperature of 25-50 degrees C, hydrogen sulfide                content of less than 20 ppm and the purified and                desulfurized coke is added into the converter gas                flue and the gas volume of the added coke oven gas                is 22.5-32.5% the amount of high temperature                converter gas discharged from the converter and the                reforming endothermic reaction using methane in                coke oven gas and carbon dioxide in converter gas                is carried out by reacting methane and carbon                dioxide to generate carbon monoxide and                hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The gas-based direct                  reduction shaft furnace has a working pressure of                  6-10 kPa (G). The coarse gas is removed from the                  top gas of the shaft furnace, cooling to 150                  degrees C through the heat exchanger and                  discharging as a gas output by the dust removal                  and cooling device. The dust-removal and cooled                  shaft furnace top gas is output as output gas and                  the gas low-calorific value is 6500-7500                  kJ/Nm3.

PN CN108866331-A
TI Production of zinc oxide using zinc-containing ore includes mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first leaching agent, filtering, purifying, calcining, pressurizing, depressurizing, rinsing and drying.
AU YUE H
   LI S
   LONG Z
   JIANG T
   LIU Q
AE CHONGQING DONGQUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201895437M
AB    NOVELTY - Production of zinc oxide using zinc-containing ore comprises mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first leaching agent, filtering to obtain first leaching solution, purifying first leachate, adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to first leachate, mixing, filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to first filtrate, mixing, filtering to obtain second solid and second filtrate, calcining at 150-1050 degrees C, adding second leaching agent to calcined product, mixing, filtering to obtain second leaching solution, pressurizing carbon dioxide in second leachate to obtain slurry containing crystals, filtering slurry in sealed environment to obtain third solid and third filtrate, depressurizing third filtrate to normal pressure, decomposing ammonium carbonate into ammonia and carbon dioxide, rinsing third solid with water, drying and calcining at 450-900 degrees C.
   USE - Method of producing zinc oxide using zinc-containing ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate, economical and environment-friendly, does not require steaming, reduces energy consumption and avoids evaporation of ammonia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of zinc oxide using zinc-containing ore comprises mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first leaching agent, filtering to obtain first leaching solution, purifying first leachate, adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to first leachate, mixing, filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to first filtrate, mixing, filtering to obtain second solid and second filtrate, calcining at 150-1050 degrees C, adding second leaching agent to calcined product, mixing, filtering to obtain second leaching solution, pressurizing carbon dioxide in second leachate to obtain slurry containing crystals, filtering slurry in sealed environment to obtain third solid and third filtrate, depressurizing third filtrate to normal pressure, decomposing ammonium carbonate into ammonia and carbon dioxide, rinsing third solid with water, drying and calcining at 450-900 degrees C. The first leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium hydrogencarbonate, mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate. The second leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate, mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and carbonic acid or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate.

GA 2018704658
AB    NOVELTY - A high molecular weight polyether carbonate is prepared by reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of double metal cyanide catalyst and organometallic catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of high molecular weight polyether carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combination of catalyst (I) and a DMC does not need a starter compound when used to prepare high molecular weight polyether carbonates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high molecular weight polyether carbonate is prepared by reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst and catalyst of formula (I).
   M1, M2=Zn(II), Cr(II), Co(II), Cu(II), Mn(II), Mg(II), Ni(II), Fe(II), Ti(II), V(II), Cr(III)-X, Co(III)-X, Mn(III)-X, Ni(III)-X, Fe(III)-X, Ca(II), Ge(II), Al(III)-X, Ti(III)-X, V(III)-X, Ge(IV)-(X)2, or Ti(IV)-(X)2;
   R1, R2=H, halide, nitro, nitrile, imine, amine, ether, silyl, silyl ether, sulfoxide, sulfonyl, sulfinate, or acetylide, or optionally substituted alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, haloalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, aryloxy, alkylthio, arylthio, alicyclic group, or heteroalicyclic group;
   R3=optionally substituted alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene, arylene, heteroarylene, or cycloalkylene, each may optionally be interrupted by aryl, heteroaryl, alicyclic group, or heteroalicyclic group;
   R5=H, or optionally substituted aliphatic group, heteroaliphatic group, alicyclic group, heteroalicyclic group, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl, or alkylaryl;
   E1=(i) C provided that E2 is O, S, or NH, or (ii) N provided that E2 is O;
   E3-E6=N, NR4, O, or S, provided that when E3-E6 are N, then the solid line/jagged line is a double bond, or when E3-E6 are NR4, O, or S, then the solid line/jagged line is a single bond;
   R4=H or optionally substituted aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl, -alkylC(O)OR19, or -alkylC=N, or alkylaryl;
   X=OC(O)Rx, OSO2Rx, OSORx, OSO(Rx)2, S(O)Rx, ORx, phosphinate, halide, nitrate, OH, carbonate, amino, amido, or optionally substituted aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, or heteroaryl in which M1-X-M2 is possible;
   Rx=H, or optionally substituted aliphatic, haloaliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, alkylaryl, or heteroaryl; and
   G=absent or neutral or anionic donor ligand which is a Lewis base.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) polymerization system for copolymerization of carbon dioxide and an epoxide comprising catalyst (I) and the DMC catalyst; and
   (2) high molecular weight polyether carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Reaction pressure is 1-60 bar carbon dioxide, preferably 1-10 bar carbon dioxide. Reaction temperature is 50-110 degrees C, preferably 55-80 degrees C. No starter or initiator compound is present in the reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst (I) has a symmetric macrocyclic ligand or an asymmetric macrocyclic ligand. The catalyst is represented by 23 compounds including, but not limited to, formulae (L1M1M2(X)2(G)2), (L2M1M2(X)2(G)2), or (L3M1M2(X)2(G)2). DMC catalyst comprises metal centers and cyanide ligands. DMC catalyst additionally comprises complexing agents, water, metal salt, and/or an acid, optionally in non-stoichiometric amounts. N.B.: L1-L3 are not defined. DMC catalyst comprises formula M'd(M''e(CN)f)g.d=3;e=1;f=6; andg=2.Preferred Process: DMC catalyst is prepared by treating a solution of metal salt with solution of metal cyanide salt in the presence of complexing agent, water, and/or acid, where metal salt has structure of formula M'(X'); metal cyanide salt has structure of formula (Y1)qM''(CN)b(A)c; complexing agent is selected from (poly)ether, polyether carbonate, polycarbonate, a poly(tetramethylene ether diol), ketone, ester, amide, alcohol, and/or urea, such as propylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, (m)ethoxy ethylene glycol, dimethoxyethane, tert.-butyl alcohol, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme, triglyme, methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol and sec.-butyl alcohol, 3-buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol, and/or 3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol; and the acid, if present, has structure of formula HrX'''.M'=Zn(II), Ru(II), Ru(III), Fe(II), Ni(II), Mn(II), Co(II), Sn(II), Pb(II), Fe(III), Mo(IV), Mo(VI), Al(III), V(V), V(VI), Sr(II), W(IV), W(VI), Cu(II), or Cr(III);X'=halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate, or nitrate;p= greater than or equal to 1 in which charge on anion multiplied by p satisfies valency of M';M''=Fe(II), Fe(III), Co(II), Co(III), Cr(II), Cr(III), Mn(II), Mn(III), Ir(III), Ni(II), Rh(III), Ru(II), V(IV), or V(V);Y1=proton or alkali metal ion or alkaline earth metal ion, e.g. K;A=halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate, or nitrate;q, b= greater than or equal to 1;c= greater than or equal to 0;(Yx q +CNx b+Ax c)=valency of M'';X'''=halide, sulfate, phosphate, borate, chlorate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate, or nitrate; andr=integer corresponding to charge on X'''.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: High molecular weight polyether carbonate has molecular weight of greater than or equal to 40000 Daltons, preferably greater than or equal to 100000 Daltons. The polyether carbonate has polydispersity index (PDI) of greater than or equal to 1, preferably greater than or equal to 3. Preferred Component: Polyether carbonate has structure of formula (IV).m/(n+m)=0-1; andRe1, Re2=not defined and depend on nature of epoxide used in the method.

PN CN108207435-A
TI Temperature control device for insectivorous plant cultivation room for cultivating vermivorous plant e.g. carnivorous plant, has water tank with inner cavity whose bottom portion is fixedly provided with transducer sheet.
AU LV B
AE LV B (LVBB-Individual)
GA 201853619U
AB    NOVELTY - The control device has a supporting frame (2) fixed at a top portion of a fan-shaped structured greenhouse (1). A solar film (3) is fixed on a top portion of the frame. A surface of the solar film is fixed with an exhaust fan (10). An end of the greenhouse is provided with a heat-insulating cloth roll (4). An end of the heat-insulating cloth roll is fixedly connected with a cross rod (5). A side at a top end of the greenhouse is fixedly provided with a motor (7). An end of the motor is fixedly connected with a rotating shaft (8). An end of the rotating shaft is fixedly connected with a bearing seat (9). A wall surface of a cavity of the greenhouse is provided with a heating pipe. An end of the cavity of the greenhouse is provided with a carbon dioxide generator. A side of the generator is provided with a water tank. A bottom portion of an inner cavity of the tank is fixedly provided with a transducer sheet. The rotating shaft is connected with the cross rod by a cotton rope (6).
   USE - Temperature control device for an insectivorous plant cultivation room for cultivating vermivorous plant e.g. carnivorous plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The control device uses the fan-shaped structured greenhouse with large illumination area and dissipates temperature indoor heat in the greenhouse by the heating pipe so as to maintain the temperature in the greenhouse while injecting carbon dioxide into the greenhouse by the carbon dioxide generator, thus effectively increasing concentration of indoor carbon dioxide and accelerating respiration effect of the plant to effectively increase growth rate and speed of the plant. The greenhouse is ventilated by the exhaust fan when the indoor temperature is high so as to control the temperature in the greenhouse. The control device has simple structure, low production cost, low power consumption and wide popularization and use effects.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the temperature control device.
   Greenhouse (1)
   Supporting frame (2)
   Solar film (3)
   Heat-insulating cloth roll (4)
   Cross rod (5)
   Cotton rope (6)
   Motor (7)
   Rotating shaft (8)
   Bearing seat (9)
   Exhaust fan (10)

PT P
PN KR1848788-B1
TI Packing fresh cut fruit, comprises e.g. selecting fresh fruit according to fruit type and grades, disinfecting the prepared fruit, cutting, removing faeces, and immersing the pretreated fruit in immersion liquid for washing.
AU KIM K M
AE FOOD FACTORY (FOOD-Non-standard)
GA 2018333096
AB    NOVELTY - Packing fresh cut fruit, comprises (a) selecting fresh fruit according to the fruit type and grades, (b) disinfecting the prepared fruit, (c) cutting, removing faeces or removing the skin according to the type and intake type of fruit according to the disinfected fruits, (d) immersing the pretreated fruit in immersion liquid for washing, (e) dehydrating the fruit, (f) cooling the dehydrated fruit to 0-4 degrees C, (g) packing the cooled fruit at refrigeration temperature of 0-10 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for packing fresh cut fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents browning of fruits, improves shelf life of fruits, and maintains stability, texture, flavor and nutrients of fruits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Packing fresh cut fruit, comprises (a) selecting fresh fruit according to the fruit type and grades, (b) disinfecting the prepared fruit, (c) cutting, removing faeces or removing the skin according to the type and intake type of fruit according to the disinfected fruits, (d) immersing the pretreated fruit in immersion liquid for washing, (e) dehydrating the fruit, (f) cooling the dehydrated fruit to 0-4 degrees C, (g) packing the cooled fruit at refrigeration temperature of 0-10 degrees C. The immersion liquid includes vitamin C and sodium hydrogen carbonate. The vitamin C is L-ascorbic acid. The immersion liquid is prepared by dissolving L-ascorbic acid having concentration of 1.5-10% and sodium hydrogen carbonate in chiller water maintained at 3-5 degrees C, and adjusting concentration to 1-5%, and immersing for 1-10 minutes. The method is carried out by sterilizing by immersing for 5-7 minutes at chlorine concentration of 200 ppm. The method is carried out by adjusting chiller water temperature at 0-10 degrees C, contacting L-ascorbic acid and sodium bicarbonate, and preventing chemical reaction using deionized water. The packaging is carried out by packaging fruit under modified atmosphere, filling inside of the package with 10.6% oxygen and 3.3% carbon dioxide, when the fruit is orange; filling inside of the package with 3.65% oxygen and 2.6% carbon dioxide, when the fruit is pineapple; filling inside of the package with 7.4% oxygen and 4.7% carbon dioxide, when the fruit is a wreath; and filling inside of the package with 6.6% oxygen and 5.3% carbon dioxide, when the fruit is apple.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Increasing coke oven gas comprises supplying reactant gas containing carbon dioxide and/or water to coke oven gas stream in carbonization chamber of coke oven to react reactant gas with carbon in carbonization chamber and gasifying.
AU PARK J H
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
   RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
GA 201823378W
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing coke oven gas comprises supplying reactant gas containing carbon dioxide and/or water to coke oven gas stream in a carbonization chamber of coke oven to react the reactant gas with carbon in carbonization chamber and gasifying. The reactant gas is supplied through nozzle in the 1/4 section where the entire section of the gas-way on the carbonization chamber is divided into four parts from the coke side to the pusher side where the uprising pipe is located, when the coke oven gas flows.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases carbon dioxide conversion rate, reduces investment burden on coke oven gas treatment facility, is economical, and minimizes manpower and equipment required for stable operation of the coke plant and removal of adhered carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Increasing coke oven gas comprises supplying reactant gas containing carbon dioxide and/or water to coke oven gas stream in a carbonization chamber of coke oven to react the reactant gas with carbon in carbonization chamber and gasifying. The reactant gas is supplied through nozzle in the 1/4 section where the entire section of the gas-way on the carbonization chamber is divided into four parts from the coke side to the pusher side where the uprising pipe is located, when the coke oven gas flows. Temperature of the 1/4 section where the reactant gas is supplied is greater than or equal to 75% of the average temperature of the carbonization chamber gas-way. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coke oven comprising carbonization chamber provided with coal for carbonizing, and an uprising pipe disposed at upper end of one side of the carbonization chamber for discharging coke oven gas generated during the coal carbonization. The carbonization chamber includes at least one reactant gas supply nozzle for supplying the reactant gas in a direction parallel to the inner wall of the carbonization chamber, where the reactant gas supply nozzle is located in the 1/4 section when it is divided into quarters in the direction of the pusher side on the side where the uprising pipe is located on the side of the coke side opposite to the rising pipe of the carbonization chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the coke oven having carbonized gas inlet pipe.

PN CN110614440-A; CN110614440-B
TI Performing laser remelting and gasifying composite            polishing of carbon dioxide optical elements involves            cleaning and drying optical elements, carbon dioxide            laser remelting polishing, and carbon dioxide laser            gasification polishing.
AU LI X
   LU G
   ZHI X
   WANG D
   WANG K
   YUAN S
   ZHOU Q
   PENG Y
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2020035058
AB    NOVELTY - Performing laser remelting and gasifying                composite polishing of carbon dioxide (CO2) optical                elements involves cleaning and drying optical                elements, CO2 laser remelting polishing, CO2 laser                gasification polishing, CO2 laser alternate                layer-by-layer remelting polishing and gasification                polishing on the surface of optical                components.
   USE - Method for performing laser remelting and                gasifying composite polishing of carbon dioxide                optical elements.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform laser remelting                and gasifying composite polishing of carbon dioxide                optical elements, which is suitable for efficient                and high-precision polishing of various optical                elements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The CO2 laser repair                  is performed by cleaning sample in an ultrasonic                  cleaning device in a class 10000 clean room, and                  then removing the sample to dry the sample in a                  normal temperature mode with a blower. The sample                  is placed on the workbench and fixed so that the                  polished surface to be remelted is perpendicular                  to the laser direction, turned on the CO2 laser                  system, drawn a graph of the scanning trajectory,                  where area of the polishing area is 1.01-1.1                  times the surface of the sample to be polished,                  and pattern is imported into the laser system.                  The laser power, frequency, duty cycle, spot                  diameter, defocus amount, and scanning speed                  during remelting and polishing are adjusted. The                  sample is placed on the workbench and fixed. The                  placement should ensure that the parallel                  trajectory of vaporization polishing is                  perpendicular to the parallel trajectory of the                  last remelting polishing. The laser power,                  frequency, duty cycle, spot diameter, defocus                  amount, and scanning speed during gasification                  polishing are adjusted. The remelting and                  gasification polishing is completed alternately.                  The laser power is reduced to 4-3-5 Watt (W) and                  scan speed 3-5mm/seconds, the polishing track                  direction is increased by 10-20 degrees from the                  previous track direction, and the remaining                  parameters are unchanged. The optical glass                  comprises fused silica glass, KDP or K9. The                  angle between the incident light of the                  gasification polishing and the normal direction                  of the optical element is 45-80 degrees . The                  ultrasonic cleaning solution refers to absolute                  ethanol or acetone. The CO2 laser-related                  parameter includes laser power is 10-50 Watt, and                  the frequency is 1-20 kilohertz (kHz), duty ratio                  1-10%, spot diameter 1-5mm, defocus amount                  5-50mm, scanning speed 5-50mm/seconds. The                  clamping between the normal line of the surface                  to be vaporized and polished and the direction of                  the laser beam. The angle is 45-80 degrees . The                  CO2 laser-related parameter includes laser power                  is 50-100 Watt, and frequency is 1-20kHz, duty                  ratio 1-10%, spot diameter is 0.01-0.1mm, defocus                  amount is 1-10mm, scanning speed is                  50-1000mm/seconds.

GA 2019A3722V
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide adsorbent comprises amine compound which is supported on carbon-type adsorbent with hydrophobic surface.
   USE - A carbon-dioxide adsorbent for office, commercial, establishment, movie theater, thermal power station, and factories.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high carbon-dioxide adsorption capability and adsorbs carbon dioxide effectively where relative humidity is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing carbon-dioxide adsorbent which involves hydrophobing process of preparing carbon-type adsorbent by heat-processing raw material activated carbon in non-oxidizing atmosphere, and carrying process of carrying out the impregnation support of amine compound at carbon-type adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound has as a main component chosen from ethylenediamine and/or diethylene tetramine. The atomic ratio of surface oxygen amount and surface carbon amount in the surface of carbon-type adsorbent is less than or equal to 0.05. Preferred Process: The heat-processing is performed at 600-1200 degrees C in nitrogen gas atmosphere for 0.5-2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon-type adsorbent is activated carbon fiber. The activated carbon fiber is polyacrylonitrile (PAN) system activated carbon fiber or pitch-type activated carbon fiber. Preferred Parameters: The carbon-type adsorbent has Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of 1500-2000 m2/g.

PT P
PN CN113898432-A
TI Carbon dioxide cross-critical Rankine cycle method in coal-fired power plant, involves heating gaseous carbon dioxide working medium temperature of gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after heating and expanding working substance.
AU LIANG Y
   YANG Z
   CHEN J
   CHEN Y
   CHEN P
   LUO X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 202214381C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide cross-critical Rankine cycle method involves heating the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium with the pressure of 7.378-24 megaPascal, the temperature of the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after heating is 250-800degrees Celsius, expanding the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance to do work treatment, the temperature of the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after expansion working treatment is higher than the supercritical temperature, the pressure is 4.8-7.377 megaPascal, performing a cooling treatment to the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after the expansion working treatment, so that the temperature of the carbon dioxide working medium is reduced to 50-120degrees Celsius, performing two-stage cooling treatment to the carbon dioxide working medium after one section of cooling treatment.
   USE - Carbon dioxide cross-critical Rankine cycle method used in coal-fired power plant and for other types of power generation cycles such as carbon monoxide cycle, carbon dioxide cycle, nitrogen oxide cycle and nitrogen dioxide cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to condense all the gaseous CO2 working medium to all of the liquid CO 2 working medium, thus reducing the cooling load of the condenser, and reducing the heat exchange area of condenser. It can fully utilize the advantage of low temperature heat source, and improves the circulation efficiency. The average dryness is improved in the condensing process, the average heat exchange coefficient of the process is improved, and the heat exchanger area is reduced and the economical efficiency is improved. The method uses a partial condensation to reduce cooling load in condenser at the same cold source temperature and cold sources temperature rise, thus it can reduce the coolant load, condensing temperature, and circulation efficiency of CO2 transcritical circulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide cross-critical Rankine cycle method involves heating the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium with the pressure of 7.378-24 megaPascal, the temperature of the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after heating is 250-800degrees Celsius, expanding the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance to do work treatment, the temperature of the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after expansion working treatment is higher than the supercritical temperature, the pressure is 4.8-7.377 megaPascal, performing a cooling treatment to the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after the expansion working treatment, so that the temperature of the carbon dioxide working medium is reduced to 50-120degrees Celsius, performing two-stage cooling treatment to the carbon dioxide working medium after one section of cooling treatment, so that the temperature of the carbon dioxide working medium is reduced to 37-49degrees Celsius, condensing the carbon dioxide working medium, obtaining the gas-liquid two-phase mixed carbon dioxide working medium, the dryness of the gas-liquid two-phase mixed carbon dioxide working medium is 0.1-0.9, performing gas-liquid separation to the gas-liquid two-phase mixed carbon dioxide working medium, obtaining saturated liquid carbon dioxide working medium and saturated gaseous carbon dioxide working medium, compressing and processing the saturated liquid carbon dioxide after gas-liquid separation and the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide working medium in the compression processing is lower than the critical temperature, the pressure of the liquid carbon dioxide working medium after compression processing is 7.378-24 megaPascal, the saturated gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after gas-liquid separation is compressed, the pressure of the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after compression treatment is 7.378-24 megapascal after compressing, the pressure of the liquid carbon dioxide working substance is the same as that of the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after compressing. The heat phase of the liquid carbon dioxide working substance absorption after compression treatment is changed into gaseous carbon dioxide working medium, the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after compression treatment is mixed. The mixed gaseous carbon dioxide working substance absorbs the heat of the cooling treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The dry degree of the gas-liquid two-phase mixed carbon dioxide working medium is 0.47-0.76. The temperature of the gaseous carbon dioxide working medium after heating is 500-800degrees Celsius. The pressure of the gaseous carbon dioxide working substance after the expansion working treatment is 6.1-7.377 megaPascal. The temperature of the carbon dioxide working medium is reduced to 79-94degrees Celsius. The method involves condensing the carbon dioxide working substance by cooling water, the temperature of the cooling water is 6-20.9degrees Celsius.

TI Obtaining target molecule from source material by            bringing source material into contact with molecularly            imprinted polymer to associate target molecule present            in source material with molecularly imprinted polymer,            and eluting target molecule off molecularly imprinted            polymer using carbon dioxide.
AU LOW A W K
   JAMIESON B W
AE LIGAR LP (LIGA-Non-standard)
GA 202286064B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for obtaining at least one target                molecule from a source material, involves (a)                bringing a source material into contact with a                molecularly imprinted polymer to associate at least                one target molecule present in the source material                with the molecularly imprinted polymer, and (b)                eluting the at least one target molecule off the                molecularly imprinted polymer using liquid or fluid                carbon dioxide as an elution solvent, where the                liquid or fluid carbon dioxide used as an elution                solvent is a pressure of above 7 MPa and a                temperature of above 31 degrees C, or the liquid or                fluid carbon dioxide used as an elution solvent is                a pressure of 0.5-41 MPa and a temperature of -50                degrees C to 30 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining at least                one target molecule from source material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the industry/public with a                useful choice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for associating at least one                target molecule with a molecularly imprinted                polymer, involving (i) solubilizing a source                material in liquid or fluid carbon dioxide and (ii)                bringing the solubilized source material in the                liquid or fluid carbon dioxide into contact with                molecularly imprinted polymer to associate at least                one target molecule present in the source material                with the molecularly imprinted polymer, where the                liquid or fluid carbon dioxide is at a first                pressure and temperature combination when                solubilizing the source material and at a second                pressure and temperature combination when bringing                the solubilised source material into contact with                the molecularly imprinted polymer;
   (2) a system for obtaining at least one target                molecule from a source material, comprising a                molecularly imprinted polymer, a device for                providing a flow of liquid or fluid carbon dioxide,                where the system is adapted to bring a source                material into contact with a molecularly imprinted                polymer to associate at least one target molecule                present in the source material with the molecularly                imprinted polymer, and the system is adapted to                elute the target molecule that is associated with                the molecularly imprinted polymer using the flow of                liquid or fluid carbon dioxide as an elution                solvent, and the carbon dioxide is at a pressure of                above 7 MPa and temperature of above 31 degrees C,                or the system is adapted to elute the target                molecule that is associated with the molecularly                imprinted polymer using the flow of liquid or fluid                carbon dioxide as an elution solvent, and wherein                the carbon dioxide is at a pressure of 0.5-41 MPa                and a temperature of -50 degrees C to 30 degrees C;                and
   (3) a system for associating at least one                target molecule with a molecularly imprinted                polymer, comprising a molecularly imprinted                polymer, at least one device for providing a flow                of liquid or fluid carbon dioxide at a first                pressure and temperature combination and a second                pressure and temperature combination, and the                system is adapted to solubilize a source material                in the liquid or fluid carbon dioxide, and the                system is adapted to bring the solubilized source                material into contact with a molecularly imprinted                polymer to associate at least one target molecule                present in the source material with the molecularly                imprinted polymer, and the liquid or fluid carbon                dioxide is at the first pressure and temperature                combination when solubilizing the source material                and at the second pressure and temperature                combination when bringing the solubilised source                material into contact with the molecularly                imprinted polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The liquid or fluid                  carbon dioxide used as an elution solvent is a                  pressure of 7-100 MPa and a 31-70 degrees C. The                  liquid or fluid carbon dioxide is at a pressure                  of 5-30 MPa and temperature of -10 degrees C to                  25 degrees C, or the liquid or fluid carbon                  dioxide is at a pressure of 7-15 MPa and                  temperature of about 10-25 degrees C. The liquid                  or fluid carbon dioxide is used as an elution                  solvent is supercritical carbon dioxide. The                  liquid or fluid carbon dioxide used as an elution                  solvent has a density of 0.4-1 g/cm3, 0.6-1 g/cm3                  or 0.8-1 g/cm3, or 0.1-1.1 g/cm3, 0.2-1.1 g/cm3,                  0.2-1 g/cm3, 0.5-1 g/cm3 or 0.7-1 g/cm3. The                  source material is solubilized in at least one                  solvent prior to being brought into contact with                  the molecularly imprinted polymer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The elution solvent                  comprises co-solvent comprising water, ethanol,                  methanol, ethyl acetate, isopropyl alcohol,                  acetonitrile, acetone and/or tetrahydrofuran. The                  source material comprises a liquid, a wax, an                  oil, a solid and/or an extract of an animal,                  plant or synthetic source material. The source                  material is in a binding solvent when brought                  into contact with the molecularly imprinted                  polymer and the binding solvent comprising liquid                  or fluid carbon dioxide, water, ethanol,                  methanol, ethyl acetate, isopropyl alcohol,                  acetonitrile, acetone and/or tetrahydrofuran,                  where the binding solvent comprises supercritical                  carbon dioxide, and the binding solvent comprises                  liquid or fluid carbon dioxide at a density that                  is lower than the density of the liquid or fluid                  carbon dioxide used as the elution solvent. The                  binding solvent comprises liquid or fluid carbon                  dioxide and co-solvent. The source material                  comprises solubilized in one or more of water,                  ethanol, methanol, ethyl acetate, isopropyl                  alcohol, acetonitrile, acetone, tetrahydrofuran,                  liquid or fluid carbon dioxide, where the source                  material is solubilized in supercritical carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Composition: The co-solvent is                  greater than 0-75 vol.%, 0-70 vol.%, 0-60 vol.%,                  0-50 vol.%, 0-50 vol.%, 0-40 vol.%, 0-30 vol.%                  liquid or fluid carbon dioxide elution solvent or                  0-20 %mass flow of the liquid or fluid carbon                  dioxide elution solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The first pressure and                  temperature combination is 7.4-35 MPa and about                  31-80 degrees C or 0.5-41 MPa and -50-30 degrees                  C. The liquid or fluid carbon dioxide at the                  first pressure and temperature combination is                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The liquid or fluid                  carbon dioxide at the first pressure and                  temperature combination is subcritical carbon                  dioxide. The liquid or fluid carbon dioxide at                  the second pressure and temperature combination                  is supercritical carbon dioxide. The liquid or                  fluid carbon dioxide at the first pressure and                  temperature has a higher density than the liquid                  or fluid carbon dioxide at the second pressure                  and temperature combination. The source material                  is solubilized in the carbon dioxide by                  extracting the source material from a solid                  material using supercritical carbon dioxide at                  pressure of 7.4-35 MPa and 31-80 degrees C and at                  density of 0.4-1 g/cm3, where the source material                  is solubilized in the carbon dioxide by                  extracting the source material from a solid                  material using subcritical carbon dioxide at                  pressure of 0.5-41 MPa and temperature of -50                  degrees C to 30 degrees C at density of 0.01-1.3                  g/cm3.

PT P
PN CN110357102-A; CN110357102-B
TI Method for purifying carbon dioxide, involves passing carbon dioxide gas processed by pre-cooler into liquefaction device for liquefaction treatment, and changing carbon dioxide gas into liquid carbon dioxide.
AU YU J
   ZHANG G
   RU G
AE GUANGDONG HUATE GAS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201988847Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending liquid raw material carbon dioxide in a raw material tank (11) to a vaporization heating device (13) through a low-temperature liquid pump. The liquid carbon dioxide is vaporized and the vaporized carbon dioxide gas is heated to a set temperature. The heated carbon dioxide gas is passed to the raw material tank to perform heat exchange with liquid carbon dioxide gas in the raw material tank. The light components separated from the carbon dioxide of the liquid raw material are continuously discharged through the drain pipe of the raw material tank. The liquid raw material carbon dioxide in the raw material tank is again sent to vaporization heating device. The carbon dioxide gas processed by the pre-cooler is passed into a liquefaction device for liquefaction treatment, and carbon dioxide gas is changed into liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide treated by liquefaction device is passed into a product tank for storage.
   USE - Method for purifying carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has of simple process and convenient operation, which can achieve the purification effect and reduce the investment cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view showing the method for purifying carbon dioxide.
   Raw material tank (11)
   Cryogenic liquid pump (12)
   Vaporization heating device (13)
   Filter set (23)
   Dry valve (201)

PN CN115218465-A
TI Method for controlling fresh air in control device            of air conditioner i.e. side-by-side type            air-conditioner, involves automatically closing fresh            air function when preset condition is satisfied, and            determining whether fresh air function is turned-on            according to user.
AU YAN J
   QI G
AE AUX AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 2022D4329J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting and obtaining a                carbon dioxide concentration in a lower room (S1).                Judgment is made to check whether the carbon                dioxide concentration is less than a threshold                (S2). Judgment is made to check whether a delta is                less than the carbon dioxide concentration when the                carbon dioxide concentration is greater than the                threshold (S3). Judgment is made to check whether a                first preset condition is satisfied when the delta                is greater than the carbon dioxide concentration                (S4). A fresh air function is automatically                turned-on when the first preset condition is                satisfied. Judgment is made to check whether a                second preset condition is satisfied (S5). A fresh                air function is automatically closed (S6) when the                second preset condition is satisfied. Determination                is made to check whether the fresh air function is                turned-on according to a user.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh air in a control                device of an air conditioner (claimed) i.e.                side-by-side type air-conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the user left a room for a                period of time, and automatically turns-on the                fresh air. The method ensures air circulation in a                room, thus providing a comfortable indoor                environment for the user. The method increases air                quality, and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                fresh air control device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                method for controlling fresh air in a control                device of an air conditioner i.e. side-by-side type                air-conditioner. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   S1Step for detecting and obtaining a carbon                dioxide concentration in a lower room
   S2Step for judging whether the carbon dioxide                concentration is less than a threshold
   S3Step for judging whether a delta is less                than the carbon dioxide concentration when the                carbon dioxide concentration is greater than the                threshold
   S4Step for judging whether a first preset                condition is satisfied when the delta is greater                than the carbon dioxide concentration
   S5Step for judging whether a second preset                condition is satisfied
   S6Step for automatically closing fresh air                function when the second preset condition is                satisfied

PN WO2022069800-A1; FI202005961-A
TI Producing product gas from feed comprising carbon            dioxide, hydrogen and hydrocarbons, by supplying feed            to reactor comprising catalyst, heating catalyst            electrically, supplying feed through catalyst and            performing reaction, and forming product gas comprising            carbon monoxide and hydrogen in reactor.
AU SIMELL P
   HANNULA I
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 202248353N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a product gas from a feed                comprising at least carbon dioxide, hydrogen and                hydrocarbons, comprises supplying the feed to a                reactor comprising a catalyst, heating the catalyst                electrically, supplying the feed through the                catalyst and performing a reaction at least between                carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of the                catalyst in the reactor, and forming the product                gas comprising at least carbon monoxide and                hydrogen in the reactor.
   USE - The method is used in the production of                hydrocarbons, Fischer-Tropsch (FT) process,                treatment of carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide capture                process, catalytic partial oxidation (CPOX)                process, reforming off-gases, cracking process of                naphtha and other hydrocarbon feedstocks,                methanation process, and/or production of methanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats and converts carbon dioxide                based feeds easily and effectively. The method uses                a reactor that can be heated effectively using                electrical heating, and also brings the necessary                heat for the reaction in which carbon dioxide is                converted to carbon monoxide using the partial                oxidation. The method prevents reaction of the                oxygen and the burning such that the reactions do                not take place too quickly, but the reactions take                place with the catalyst in the reactor when the                oxygen is fed to carbon dioxide stream. The method                uses carbon dioxide as a feed for a FT process. The                method recirculates and uses undesired products or                streams comprising hydrocarbons, such as off-gases                from FT processes in the feed, which improves the                yield of oils and waxes in the FT process. The                method increases the yield of synthesis products                from power compared to conventional solutions. The                method helps controlling the carbon or coke                formation. The method and apparatus offer a                possibility to treat carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide easily, and energy- and cost-effectively.                The method provides an industrially applicable,                simple and affordable way to produce carbon                monoxide, and further to produce desired                hydrocarbons using the FT reaction. The method and                apparatus are easy and simple to realize in                connection with production processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing a product gas from a                feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Hydrogen is fed to the                  reactor. Oxygen is fed to the reactor. Oxygen is                  added to a carbon dioxide stream, the carbon                  dioxide stream is combined with a hydrogen based                  stream to form the feed and a hydrocarbon                  containing stream is supplied to the hydrogen                  based stream before combining with the carbon                  dioxide stream. A partial oxidation is carried                  out in the reactor. The catalyst is heated                  resistively or inductively. An off-gas stream                  from a Fischer-Tropsch process is recirculated as                  hydrocarbons and is added to the feed. Preferred                  Components: The feed comprises at least carbon                  dioxide, hydrogen, oxygen and hydrocarbons. The                  catalyst is a porous material structure with a                  catalytic material. The catalytic material is                  arranged on surfaces of pores of porous material                  in the porous material structure. The catalyst is                  rhodium on alumina catalyst.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a phase change storage tank (1) that is capable of storing liquid carbon dioxide, and by exchanging heat with the environment to make the carbon dioxide generate gas-liquid phase change and liquid-gas phase change in the corresponding working stage. A high-temperature storage tank (2) is used for storing high temperature and high pressure carbon dioxide gas obtained by compressing a multi-stage compression module. The high temperature storage tank is provided with a heat exchange function for exchanging heat of the high temperature high-pressure carbon dioxide to heat supply circulating water. A turbine generating device uses the high temperature and high pressure carbon dioxide output by the high temperature storage tank as power to generate electricity to the power grid (6), and sends the low parameter exhaust gas after working to the phase change storage tank.

The heat and power cogeneration system has long service life, simple system, mature key technology, the heat device and technology with mature application experience. The heat and power cogeneration system energy supply process can flexibly convert power supply and supply heat according to demand, so as to flexibly adapt to the actual requirement.

PN CN113969329-A
TI Metal reduction smelting method for reducing            emission of carbon dioxide, involves pressurizing            circulating gas and adding oxygen, where combustible            component in circulating gas burns and releases heat to            form hot air.
AU XU Y
   ZHANG R
   YU X
   YAN Z
   CHEN J
   TIAN M
   HE X
   WANG Y
   QIU J
AE BERIS ENG & RES CORP (CMEG-C)
GA 2022226272
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing a blast furnace                tail gas into relaxation gas and circulating gas.                The oxygen is added to make the combustible                components in the circulating gas burn and release                heat to form hot air, after pressurizing the                circulating gas. The hot air is passed into the                blast furnace (1) to participate in smelting. One                or more of a reducing medium, a combustible medium,                and water vapor are added to the circulating gas.                The combustible medium comprises one or more of                natural gas, producer gas, synthesis gas, and                chemical exhaust gas. The reducing medium comprises                hydrogen or carbon monoxide. The oxygen content in                the oxygen is greater than 75%. The carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide are separated after the residual                pressure energy is recovered in the relaxation gas,                and the carbon monoxide is returned to the                circulating gas for reuse.
   USE - Metal reduction smelting method for reducing                emission of carbon dioxide in a blast furnace                iron-making process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing emission of carbon                dioxide and system need not be modified, using the                existing blast furnace, avoiding large construction                waste resource, in addition, the by-product tail                gas de-dusting and purifying facility can utilize                the blast furnace dust removing and purification                facility, reducing the investment cost. The method                reduces carbon dioxide emission of the metal                reduction smelting system. The by-products tail gas                nitrogen content is small, is recycled and is good                for separating and using carbon dioxide, the carbon                dioxide concentration in the purge gas is higher,                it is easier to separate and utilize. The main flow                of the circulating gas is not provided with a                carbon dioxide removing device, and the circulating                process does not raise and reduce the temperature                and lift pressure process, which saves                energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a metal                reduction smelting system for reducing emission of                carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                metal reduction smelting system for reducing                emission of carbon dioxide.

TI Exhaust-gas circulation non-equilibrium reformation system provided in internal combustion engine of vehicle, makes ratio of amount of exhaust recirculation gas with respect to amount of total gases burned within engine to be low, when carbon-dioxide-storage rate is high.

AE NISSAN MOTOR CO LTD (NSMO-C)
GA 2021A6522B
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an exhaust-gas-recirculation (EGR) path e.g. EGR piping (30) which recirculates exhaust-gas from upstream side of exhaust gas purification catalyst to intake passage e.g. air-intake piping (12) of internal combustion engine (10). A reformation fuel injection unit (33) injects reformation fuel to EGR path. An EGR valve (8) adjusts the amount of exhaust recirculation gas refluxing to the intake passage based on the driving state of engine. The ratio of amount of exhaust recirculation gas with respect to amount of total gases burned within engine is made relatively high, when carbon-dioxide-storage rate in carbon-dioxide absorption discharge material (5) is low. The ratio of amount of exhaust recirculation gas with respect to amount of total gases burned within engine is made relatively low as compared with case where carbon-dioxide-storage rate is low, when carbon-dioxide-storage rate in carbon-dioxide absorption discharge material is high.
   USE - Exhaust-gas circulation non-equilibrium reformation system provided in internal combustion engine e.g. four-cycle reciprocating engine of vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The expected improvement in fuel efficiency can be realized. The responsiveness of the carbon-dioxide absorption discharge material with respect to EGR gas can be improved. The rapid temperature fall by local heat-generation and thermal absorption at the time of the carbon-dioxide absorption discharge by the carbon-dioxide absorption discharge material can be suppressed. The EGR rate can be heightened irrespective of the operating condition in start-up of the internal combustion engine. The advantage of restoring the performance of the modification catalyst can be acquired.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the vehicle provided with the exhaust-gas circulation non-equilibrium reformation system in the internal combustion engine. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN WO2018038683-A1; CN109715567-A; JP2019526440-W; JP7129706-B2
TI Removal of contaminants e.g. dye from wastewater            discharged from e.g. printing industry, involves            contacting wastewater with adsorbent containing carbon            dioxide-responsive group and having preset base            dissociation constant, and separating.
AU HU X
   BAI Y
   LIANG Y N
   LIANG Y
AE UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
   UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
GA 201817618D
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of contaminants (120) from wastewater                involves contacting wastewater comprising at least                one contaminant with a polymeric adsorbent material                containing carbon dioxide-responsive functional                groups (110) capable of complexing the contaminant,                to form a mixture (A), separating the mixture (A)                to provide a portion (A1) free of contaminant and                adsorbent material, and a portion (A2) comprising                contaminant complexed to functional groups,                bubbling carbon dioxide into portion (A2) to form a                mixture (B), and separating. The adsorbent material                has base dissociation constant (pKb) of                7.5-9.
   USE - Removal of contaminants e.g. heavy metal ions,                organic molecules, dyes, solvent, and pesticides                from wastewater (all claimed) discharged from                electronics, paint, wiring, printing, mining and                other industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The heavy metal ions contained as contaminants                in wastewater are removed effectively by carbon                dioxide-switchable adsorption and desorption                process. The recovery and regeneration of the                adsorbent material are enabled after removal of                contaminants from wastewater, without requiring                heat and acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removal of contaminants from wastewater                involves contacting wastewater comprising at least                one contaminant with a polymeric adsorbent material                containing carbon dioxide-responsive functional                groups capable of complexing the contaminant, to                form a mixture (A), separating the mixture (A) to                provide a portion (A1) substantially free of                contaminant and the adsorbent material, and a                portion (A2) comprising the contaminant complexed                to the carbon dioxide-responsive functional groups                of the adsorbent material, bubbling carbon dioxide                into the portion (A2) to release the contaminant to                form a mixture (B) comprising the contaminant in an                uncomplexed state and the polymeric adsorbent                material, in which functional groups are in carbon                dioxide-replete form, and separating the mixture                (B) to provide a preconcentrate solution portion                (B1) comprising the contaminant in water and                polymeric adsorbent material portion (B2)                comprising the adsorbent material, in which                functional groups are in the carbon dioxide-replete                form. The polymeric adsorbent material has base                dissociation constant (pKb) value of 7.5-9.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the contaminant adsorption and regeneration of the                adsorbent material.
   Nano-substrate material (100)
   Carbon dioxide-responsive groups (110)
   Contaminant (120)
   Proton (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The polymeric adsorbent                  material portion (B2) is separately contacted                  with an inert gas or air to regenerate the carbon                  dioxide-responsive functional groups of the                  polymeric adsorbent material from the carbon                  dioxide-replete form to the form, in which                  functional groups are capable of complexing the                  contaminant. The regenerated polymeric adsorbent                  material comprising the functional groups in a                  form capable of complexing the contaminant is                  recycled to initial contacting process.                  Alternately, the portion (B2) is contacted with                  inert gas or air at the same time during initial                  contacting process, such that the adsorbent                  material is initially provided in the form, in                  which functional groups are in the carbon                  dioxide-replete state and the functional groups                  are converted into the form capable of complexing                  the contaminant. The inert gas contacting process                  is carried out at 0-45 degrees C. The separation                  is carried out by filtration, sedimentation,                  magnetic separation or centrifugation, preferably                  ultrafiltration. Preferred Conditions: Each                  separation is carried out at 10-40 degrees C,                  preferably 20-35 degrees C. The pH maintained                  during initial contacting process of wastewater                  and adsorbent is 5-6, preferably 5.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymeric                  adsorbent material comprises carbon                  dioxide-responsive functional groups chosen from                  amino, carboxylic acid, amidinyl, guanidinyl,                  pyridinyl, hydroxyl and ether groups. When the                  functional group contains ether group, at least                  one other functional group chosen from amino,                  carboxylic acid, amidinyl, guanidinyl, pyridinyl,                  and hydroxyl is also contained in the adsorbent.                  The polymeric material is poly(dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate), polyacrylic acid, polymethacrylic                  acid or their homopolymer or copolymer,                  preferably poly(dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate).                  The polymeric material is provided in a high                  chain-density form or in the form of a branched                  polymeric structure, a star-shaped polymeric                  structure or a dendritic polymeric structure. The                  linear or branched polymeric chains attached to a                  nano-substrate material (100), which is a                  molecular cage or nanoparticles. The                  nano-substrate material is silsesquioxane, and                  several linear or branched polymeric chains                  attached to the silsesquioxane are polymer chains                  chosen from poly(dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate), polyacrylic acid and                  polymethacrylic acid. The silsesquioxane is                  optionally a polyhedral oligomeric                  silsesquioxane, and linear or branched polymeric                  chains attached to the silsesquioxane are                  poly(dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate) polymer                  chains. The polymeric material is attached to a                  micro- or macro-substrate material, which is                  chosen from porous and non-porous structures e.g.                  gels, particles, membranes, mesh and fibers.                  Preferred Properties: The polymeric material is                  provided in the form of star-shaped                  poly(dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate) structure                  having molecular weight of 50000-200000 Daltons,                  preferably 124000 Daltons, a linear                  poly(dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate) structure                  having molecular weight of 5000-20000 Daltons,                  preferably 11000 Daltons. The base dissociation                  constant of the polymeric adsorbent material is                  preferably 8-8.9, more preferably 8.75.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN DE102021200387-A1
TI Device useful for introducing carbon dioxide into            drinking water for domestic use, comprises carbon            dioxide source and metering line connected to carbon            dioxide source and having metering valve unit.
AU FREUDIGMANN H
   EBERLE V
   SCHMIDT H
   FEHSE J
   SCHLEPER V
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 202293671X
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a carbon dioxide source (12)                and a metering line (16) connected to carbon                dioxide source and having a metering valve unit                (14) for metered introduction of carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide source into drinking water                (18), where the carbon dioxide source has a carbon                dioxide separator which is designed to filter                carbon dioxide to be introduced into the drinking                water from ambient air to introduce into drinking                water by metering line.
   USE - The device is useful as a table-top drinking                water system, preferably table-top drinking water                bubbler, or an undersink drinking water system,                preferably undersink drinking water bubbler for                controlling and/or regulating a carbon dioxide                filter process from ambient air, and introducing                carbon dioxide into drinking water for domestic use                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device ensures excellent carbon                dioxide-binding property during introduction of                carbon dioxide into drinking water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling and/or regulating a carbon dioxide                filter process from ambient air for introduction                into drinking water for domestic use.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                device.
   Carbon dioxide source (12)
   Metering valve unit (14)
   Metering line (16)
   Drinking water (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The carbon dioxide                  separating device is designed as a carbon dioxide                  adsorber device which is designed for filtering                  carbon dioxide to be introduced, chemically or                  physically binding the carbon dioxide from the                  ambient air by adsorption. The carbon dioxide                  adsorber device for adsorption has a heating unit                  for heating a correspondingly functionalized                  adsorber material. The carbon dioxide source                  further has a fan unit which is designed to                  supply the ambient air for filtering carbon                  dioxide to carbon dioxide separating device. The                  fan unit has an air supply channel having an air                  supply valve unit for supplying the ambient air.                  The ambient air filtered by carbon dioxide                  separating device is discharged from the carbon                  dioxide separating device by an air discharge                  channel having an air discharge valve unit. The                  carbon dioxide source further has a pump unit                  which is designed to generate a negative pressure                  in carbon dioxide separating device and/or the                  carbon dioxide filtered by carbon dioxide                  separating device under pressure indirectly or                  forwarded directly to the dosing line for                  introduction into the drinking water. The carbon                  dioxide source further has a gas pressure                  reservoir, which is designed to temporarily store                  carbon dioxide filtered by the carbon dioxide                  separating device and directly or indirectly to                  the dosing line for introduction into the                  drinking water to deliver. The carbon dioxide                  separation device is connected to the gas                  pressure reservoir by a gas line. The pump unit                  is arranged in the gas line between carbon                  dioxide separating device and gas pressure                  accumulator to conduct filtered carbon dioxide                  under pressure into gas pressure accumulator. The                  device comprises a pressure sensor which is                  designed to detect a gas pressure in the gas                  pressure accumulator, and a control unit which is                  designed to control and/or regulate carbon                  dioxide filter process, preferably carbon dioxide                  volume variable filtered in the process, as a                  function of the detected gas pressure. The                  control unit for controlling and/or regulating                  filtered carbon dioxide is designed to control                  and/or regulate at least one of carbon dioxide                  separation device, fan unit, air discharge valve                  unit, and pump unit. The device comprises a                  vessel receiving unit for releasably receiving a                  drinking water having drinking water container.                  The device is connected or is connected to a                  water line, in particular a domestic water                  line.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC D03-H02E; D04-A01F1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01P1; E31-N05C; Q42-D

PT P
PN WO2020078688-A1; AU2019359938-A1; CA3116193-A1; CN112752726-A; KR2021075093-A; BR112021007108-A2; EP3867195-A1; US2021269307-A1; IN202117014345-A
TI Carbon utilization increment involves passing dry            reformed stream to carbon dioxide removal unit to            separate into purified carbon dioxide stream and carbon            dioxide-scrubbed stream having lower carbon dioxide            content.
AU MORTENSEN P M
   SAHAI A
   SAHAY A
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2020324942
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon utilization increment involves passing                dry reformed stream (106) to carbon dioxide removal                unit (20) to separate into a purified carbon                dioxide stream (22) and carbon dioxide-scrubbed                stream (23) having a lower carbon dioxide content                than the purified carbon dioxide stream. The carbon                dioxide-scrubbed stream is passed from the carbon                dioxide removal unit to a cold box (30) to separate                into a cold box off-gas (32) comprising methane,                hydrogen and carbon monoxide, hydrogen-rich stream                and a high-purity carbon monoxide stream. The                purified carbon dioxide stream is combined from the                carbon dioxide. The combined carbon-rich stream is                compressed, recycled and reformed.
   USE - Method for increasing carbon utilization of                synthesis gas plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the carbon utilization of                synthesis gas plant in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with increased carbon economy,                reduced consumption of reformer feed with little                extra capital investment, reduced waste and reduced                energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                synthesis gas plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                synthesis gas plant.
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (20)
   Purified carbon dioxide stream (22)
   Carbon dioxide-scrubbed stream (23)
   Cold box (30)
   Cold box off-gas (32)
   Dry reformed stream (106)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an improved nasal oxygen tube, comprising an oxygen, carbon dioxide pipe, connecting pipe and a nose plug, connecting pipe is set with diaphragm, diaphragm lumen in the adapter is an oxygen pipe section of the separating tube and carbon dioxide pipe section; the oxygen pipe section connected with the oxygen, the carbon dioxide pipe section with pipe, nasal carbon dioxide has oxygen inlet plug and outlet carbon dioxide nasal oxygen nasal feeding pipe section connected with the oxygen, the carbon dioxide nasal and carbon dioxide pipe section connected with the end part of carbon dioxide pipe is set with adapter. The utility model improves the structure design of the oxygen, oxygen from the oxygen delivering pipe entering the oxygen pipe section into the patient ' s nasal cavity through the nasal oxygen, carbon dioxide exhaled patient nasal carbon dioxide enters the carbon dioxide pipe section , then carbon dioxide conduit into a carbon dioxide concentration monitoring instrument, the exhaled carbon dioxide concentration monitoring patient such that it simultaneously has the function of input carbon dioxide, oxygen and output in real time.
