PT P
PN CN112326001-A
TI Detecting carbon dioxide weight, involves checking color change condition of heat-sensitive adhesive tape and calculating carbon dioxide weight according to liquid level conversion curve of corresponding standard carbon dioxide gas cylinder.
AU LI Z
   ZHANG Y
   XIAO Z
   KE Z
   ZHANG R
AE JIUJIANG ZHONGCHUAN FIRE FIGHTING APP CO (CHSB-C)
GA 202115257V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring temperature of a carbon dioxide gas bottle, and obtaining a corresponding liquid level height value according to temperature information and theoretical carbon dioxide weight. The liquid level height on the carbon dioxide gas bottle is adhered with a heat-sensitive adhesive tape. Color change condition of the heat-sensitive adhesive tape is checked. Height from bottom of the carbon dioxide gas bottle is measured to a color changing point. Carbon dioxide weight is calculated according to a liquid level conversion curve of a standard carbon dioxide gas cylinder.
   USE - Method for detecting carbon dioxide weight in gas bottle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing the heat-sensitive adhesive tape to obtain carbon dioxide liquid level height in the carbon dioxide gas bottle according to the corresponding standard carbon dioxide gas bottle liquid level conversion curve to obtain the carbon dioxide weight in the gas bottle. The method enables detecting the carbon dioxide weight in a convenient and quick manner with better application effect and low cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the liquid level conversion curve corresponds to the 68.1L carbon dioxide cylinder drawn.
DC S02 (Engineering Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC S02-C06; S02-D02; T01-J04

TI Polystyrene ground leg carbon dioxide physical            foaming technique involves mixing polystyrene resin and            talc to obtain mixed material, extruding and melting,            injecting carbon dioxide, cooling, and injecting with            mold release agent.
AU GUO X
   ZHANG J
AE NANJING JINGGE NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022260975
AB    NOVELTY - A polystyrene ground leg carbon dioxide                physical foaming technique comprises respectively                absorbing polystyrene resin and talc using                automatic suction system, mixing to obtain mixed                material, adding into the double-screw extruder by                the automatic feeding system for extruding and                melting, injecting carbon dioxide, cooling to                obtain extruded material, injecting with mold                release agent, cooling and forming, cooling via                water cooling system, pulling and cutting, and                packing.
   USE - Polystyrene ground leg carbon dioxide physical                foaming technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes mechanized production and                has high production efficiency. The product is                smooth, has no cracking and deformation, light and                beautiful, has small thermal insulation, easy to                handle and transport, recyclable, and                environment-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Methods: The double-screw extruder                  temperature is 200-230degrees Celsius, preferably                  210-220degrees Celsius. After cooling, accurately                  controlling the extruding temperature by a heat                  exchanger and an oil temperature machine. The                  injection carbon dioxide control pressure is 15                  MPa.

PN CN113599973-A
TI Composite absorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide from mixed gas, comprises main absorbent, activator, buffer, corrosion inhibitor, antioxidant and water.
AU YU X
   ZHU L
   GU J
AE ANHUI PUFAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021D02924
AB    NOVELTY - A composite absorbent comprises 21-56 %mass main absorbent, 1-10 %mass activator, 0.1-10 %mass buffer, 0.1-10 %mass corrosion inhibitor, 0.05-0.5 %mass antioxidant and 40-70 %mass water.
   USE - Composite absorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide from mixed gas, in recovery and removal of carbon dioxide in chemical tail gas, power plant gas tail gas and cement calcination flue gas (all claimed), in chemical, cement, steel, and natural gas and coal industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite absorbent has large absorption amount, good purifying effect, low corrosion, low regeneration energy consumption, and can directly improve the carbon dioxide pressure, and reduces device investment and operation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for recycling carbon dioxide in mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The main absorbent includes 5-40 %mass N-methyldiethanolamine, 5-20 %mass benzylamine, 5-20 %mass N-methylcyclohexylamine, 3-15 %mass hydroxyethyl ethylenediamine and 3-15 %mass diethylenetriamine. The composite absorbent also includes 0.02-1 %mass surfactant.Preferred Components: The activator is one or two of 1-dimethylamino-2-propanol and 2-amino-2-methyl-propanol. The buffer is a mixture of boric acid and sodium citrate in a mass ratio of 1:1. The corrosion inhibitor is one of cycloalkylimidazoline, ethanolamine phosphate, urotropin, and molybdate. The antioxidant is one of sodium sulfite, acetaldoxime, and dimethyl ketoxime. The surfactant is one of sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, alkyl dimethyl amine caprolactone, and sodium sulfate.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN NL2026669-B1; WO2022079081-A1
TI Process for converting carbon dioxide to methane, involves contacting aqueous solution comprising dissolved carbon dioxide in form of bicarbonate and/or carbonate ion, with electron-charged packed bed consisting of support and biofilm.
AU LIU D
   DE RINK F
   MARIA KLOK J B
   KLOK J B M
AE PAQELL BV (PAQE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1553W
AB    NOVELTY - Process for converting carbon dioxide to methane involves contacting an aqueous solution comprising dissolved carbon dioxide in the form of bicarbonate and/or carbonate ion, with an electron-charged packed bed (8) consisting of a support and a biofilm of microorganisms under anaerobic conditions.
   USE - Process for converting carbon dioxide to methane.
   ADVANTAGE - Methane can be produced with excellent energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for activating or deactivating bioelectrochemical system containing anode (9) and biocathode (8a).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow sheet explaining production of methane.
   Absorption column (3)
   Reactor (4)
   Heat exchanger (7)
   Packed bed (8)
   Biocathode (8a)
   Anode (9)
   Ion exchange membrane (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The support consists of granules of activated carbon. Power is not supplied to electron-charged packed bed. The anode is a titanium grid coated with iridium and/or tantalum. The supplied power is generated by a chemical reaction at the anode. The packed bed of activated carbon granules has an activated specific surface area of 500-1500 m2/g. Microorganisms are present in the form of biofilm on the surface of the activated surface. The pH of the aqueous solution is more than 8. The aqueous solution comprises 0.3-4 M sodium cations or sodium and potassium cations. Preferred Apparatus: The electron-charged packed bed is a portion of a biocathode in a bioelectrochemical system, which comprises an anode, an ion exchange membrane (10), and a cathode. The packed bed is charged by applying a potential to the bioelectrochemical system, and current is passed between the biocathode and the anode for a specified time. Recirculation is performed between aqueous solution, preferably anolyte of anode, and aqueous solution, preferably catholyte of cathode. A portion of the catholyte is fed to the anode to become portion of the anolyte, and portion of the anolyte is fed to the cathode to become portion of the catholyte. The process is performed for 0.03-12 hours when no power is transferred to the electron-charged packed bed. The packed beds are charged with electrons when process is not performed. The power supply consists of electricity generated using sunlight and/or wind. The packed bed is charged by applying a cathode potential of -0.5 V to -0.6 V to the cathode electrode, or by applying current density of 5-200 A/m2. The aqueous solution comprising dissolved carbon dioxide is obtained by contacting a gas comprising carbon dioxide with an aqueous solution having pH of more than 8. The gas comprising carbon dioxide is countercurrently connected to the aqueous solution having pH of greater than 8, and gas strips the methane from the aqueous solution to yield a gas comprising methane.
DC X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC X27-A03
IP C12M-001/00; C12M-001/107; C12M-001/12; C12M-001/42; C12N-013/00; C12P-039/00; C12P-005/02; C25B-001/04; C25B-011/037; C25B-011/043; C25B-011/067; C25B-015/02; C25B-015/08; C25B-003/25; C25B-009/00

PN CN113299934-A
TI New carbon deposition and carbon dioxide resistant fuel pole material prepared by e.g. dissolving citric acid and glycine in double distilled water, adding the oxide of antimony, adjusting the pH, stirring, heating and calcining.
AU XIA C
   ZHANG S
   HU X
   XIA Y
   ZHANG L
   HUAN D
   HENG Z
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021A22176
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon deposition and carbon dioxide resistant fuel pole material of (I).
   USE - Used as carbon deposition and carbon dioxide resistant fuel pole material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has no carbon deposit after long-term operation, excellent performance and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon deposition and carbon dioxide resistant fuel pole material of formula (Sr2Fe1.5Mo(0.5-x)SbxO6- delta ) (I), is new.
   x = doping amount of Sb i.e. less than 0-0.2; and
   delta = not defined.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for preparation of (I); #solid oxide battery, comprising carbon deposition resistant and carbon dioxide resistant fuel electrode material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (a) dissolving citric acid and glycine in double distilled water to obtain solution A, (b) adding the oxide of antimony to the solution A, and adding dilute acid dropwise until the oxide of antimony is completely dissolved to obtain solution B, (c) adding strontium salt, iron salt and molybdenum salt to the solution B, adjusting the pH to 6-7, and stirring to obtain solution C, (d) heating the solution C until a spontaneous combustion reaction occurs to obtain powder A, and(e) calcining the powder A at high temperature to obtain fuel electrode material resistant to carbon deposition and carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The particle size of the fuel electrode material is 100-500 nm. The strontium salt is strontium nitrate. The iron salt is iron nitrate and/or iron acetate. The molybdenum salt is ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate. Preferred Method: In the solution C, the total amount of metal ions and the molar ratio of glycine and citric acid are 1:(2-4):(0.1-0.5). In the step (d), the heating temperature is 200-400 degrees C. In the step (e), the high-temperature calcination temperature is 500-1200 degrees C, and the high-temperature calcination time is 1-10 hours. The high-temperature calcination is performed in an air atmosphere.

TI Removing plasticizing agent in pepper comprises            e.g. taking pepper volatile oil without plasticizer            residue as cosolvent, and removing plasticizing agent            in the pepper by supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction method.
AU QI L
   ZHANG J
   GAO W
   WU Y
   JIANG Y
AE CHENGUANG BIOTECH GROUP CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202134427A
AB    NOVELTY - Removing plasticizing agent in pepper                comprises taking pepper volatile oil without                plasticizer residue as cosolvent, removing                plasticizing agent in the pepper by supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction method, in the process of                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,                controlling the pressure in the carbon dioxide                extraction kettle is 9-13.9 MPA, and the                temperature is 32-40 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for removing plasticizing                agent in pepper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the ideal removal of                plasticizing agent from pepper by adjusting the                proper extraction process for the first time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of pepper oil resin, comprising                removing plasticizing agent, and continuously                extracting pepper oil resin in the pepper by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flow rate of the                  cosolvent is 1-10 wt.%/hour of the weight of the                  pepper. The plasticizer di(2-ethylhexyl) phthlate                  (DEHP) is less than 0.3 ppm and dibutyl phthalate                  (DBP) is less than 0.3 ppm. Preferred Method: The                  pressure in the separation kettle is controlled                  to be 4-6 MPA, and the temperature is 25-35                  degrees C, and the volatile oil of pepper is                  obtained by separating to obtain the plasticizing                  agent. In the process of extracting the pepper                  oil resin in the pepper by supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction, the pressure in the carbon                  dioxide extraction kettle is controlled to be                  25-35 MPA and the temperature is 50-60 degrees C.                  The preparation method comprises after                  extracting, controlling the temperature in the                  rectifying column is 35-45 degrees C, the                  pressure is 12-16 MPA, vacuum extracting and                  separating to obtain the plasticizer is not                  detected pepper oil resin, and/or controlling the                  temperature of the separating kettle at 30-40                  degrees C, and the pressure is 5-8 MPA, before                  extracting the pepper, the water content in the                  pepper is 6-12%, and the pepper is crushed until                  the granularity is 10-30 meshes.

AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of oil from drilling waste involves adding drilling waste to a blast drying box, drying, then crushing with a crusher, mixing an entrainer with the crushed drilling waste, uniformly stirring, adding obtained mixture to a supercritical extraction reactor, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, generating solid product and liquid mixture of drilling waste, sequentially discharging the liquid mixture into a separation tank (S1) and a separation tank (S2) for separation, and collecting oil from the bottom of the separation tank.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of oil from drilling waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves oil extraction rate of drilling waste, recovers maximum amount of residual oil, removes heavy metal pollutants in the drilling waste, and treats drilling waste by safe process.

TI Improved beer drink and method for improving beer drink.
AU MIAO J
   BEIBUTIAN V
   LIN C
   KUO F
   MIAO J C
   LIN C C
   KUO F C
AE GEN OPTICS CORP (GEOP-Non-standard)
   GOC INT TECHNOLOGY CORP (GOCI-Non-standard)
GA 201983056A
AB    NOVELTY - The instant invention provides an improved beer drink and the method for improving a beer drink. The improved beer drink contains carbon dioxide and hydrogen dissolved therein, and has a negative Reduction-Oxidation potential. In addition, in the method for improving beer drink, a beer drink with an initial concentration of carbon dioxide is provided, and carbon dioxide and hydrogen are added to a closed mixing tank; then the beer drink is fed into the closed mixing tank to make the beer contact with hydrogen for dissolving hydrogen in the beer drink, thereby forming an improved beer drink. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the improved beer drink is maintained at the initial concentration by the content of the carbon dioxide in the closed mixing tank. The instant invention can provide beer drinks with improved foam quality and enhance the taste of the beer drink.

PN JP2019088240-A; JP6943733-B2
TI Manufacturing organic substance by supplying synthesis gas which contains carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen in a fermenter containing microorganisms, and carrying out microbial fermentation of synthesis gas within the fermenter.
AU HAMACHI S
   NATSUYAMA K
AE SEKISUI CHEM IND CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 201951363S
AB    NOVELTY - An organic substance is manufactured by supplying synthesis gas which contains carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen in a fermenter containing microorganisms, and carrying out microbial fermentation of synthesis gas within the fermenter. Carbon dioxide levels in the synthesis gas supplied to the fermentation layer are set to 0.1-9 vol.% with respect to the total concentration of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and nitrogen in the synthesis gas.
   USE - Manufacture of organic substance.
   ADVANTAGE - By setting the carbon dioxide in a synthesis gas into a specific concentration range, the synthesis gas can be highly efficiently converted into organic substance, especially ethanol, improving productivity of ethanol obtained by microbial fermentation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: Microorganisms contain Clostridium genus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method includes source gas production process of gasifying carbon source and producing source gas, and pretreatment process which adjusts carbon dioxide levels in the source gas to obtain synthesis gas. Pretreatment process includes processing source gas with an adsorption-and-desorption apparatus. Preferred Components: Carbon source is a waste material. Organic substance contains ethanol.

PN CN108269989-A; CN108269989-B
TI Preparation of carbon-coated porous silicon used for preparing lithium ion battery cathode material, by reacting magnesium silicide in atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, and subjecting to pickling treatment.
AU HUO K
   GAO B
   XIANG B
   AN W
   FU J
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 2018574251
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a carbon-coated porous silicon comprises reacting magnesium silicide in an atmosphere containing carbon dioxide at 400-800 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain crude product containing magnesium oxide and carbon-encapsulated silicon, where the atmosphere containing carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide atmosphere or mixed atmosphere of carbon dioxide and inert gas; and subjecting the crude product to pickling treatment to remove magnesium oxide and residual magnesium silicide material.
   USE - Method for preparing a carbon-coated porous silicon used for preparing a lithium ion battery cathode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon-coated porous silicon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The magnesium silicide has a particle diameter of 0.2-10 mu m. The magnesium silicide is prepared by reacting commercial silica particles and magnesium powder at mass ratio of 1:1.5-2, under the protection of inert gas, for 4-12 hours at 400-700 degrees C, cooling, and ball-milling. The flow rate of the gas in the atmosphere containing carbon dioxide is 30-100 mL/minute. The concentration of the carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is not less than 10 vol.%. The porous silicon has a particle size of 1-3 mu m. The thickness of the carbon layer is 10-30 nm. The carbon-coated porous silicon has a specific surface area of 20-100 m2/g, and comprises 10-20 wt.% carbon, and balance of silicon.

AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Method for anhydrous dyeing of para-aramid                fiber involves dyeing para-aramid fiber using                2-(3-hydroxy-1,2-dihydroquinolin-2-ylidene)-2,3-dihydro-1H-indene-1,3-dione                (solvent yellow 114) and/or                14H-anthra[2,1,9-mna]thioxanthen-14-one (solvent                orange 63) as dye, and supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid as dyeing medium.
   USE - Method for anhydrous dyeing of para-aramid                fiber.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Process: The method involves                  adding 1-7% dye to dyeing kettle, placing                  para-aramid fiber in kettle, sealing, feeding                  carbon dioxide and discharging the air in the                  kettle, condensing carbon dioxide stored in                  storage tank to liquid state through a condenser,                  pressurizing through high-pressure pump, heating                  to a supercritical state under the action of a                  preheater, so that the supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid flows into the dyeing kettle to                  dissolve the dye, dyeing the para-aramid fiber,                  releasing the supercritical carbon dioxide to                  cool down, separating dyed fiber from non-dyed                  material in separation kettle, and condensing the                  carbon dioxide through condenser and returning to                  the storage tank to complete the dyeing of                  para-aramid.Preferred Component: The para-aramid fiber                  is chosen from para-aramid fiber, para-aramid                  yarn and para-aramid fabric.Preferred Condition: The dyeing is carried                  out at 100-250&#176;C under pressure of 10-30                  MPa for 10-200 minutes.

PN WO2022104232-A1
TI Composition used for treating nausea and emesis, comprises extract of ginger rhizome, where ginger rhizome has starting mass, and extract has 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, 10-gingerol, and 6-shogaol.
AU CASTOR T P
AE CASTOR T P (CAST-Individual)
GA 202268922K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises an extract of ginger rhizome, where the ginger rhizome has a starting mass, and the extract has a 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, 10-gingerol, and 6-shogaol in a mass ratio of 90-100%.
   USE - The composition is used for treating nausea and emesis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has improved treatment efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a treatment for nausea and emesis in humans comprising administering an effective amount of an extract or composition of matter; and
   (2) a method for forming the composition, comprising forming a dried powdered biomass of ginger rhizome, placing the dried powdered ginger rhizome in a vessel with carbon dioxide under supercritical, near critical or critical conditions to form a saturated biomass powder, and separating the carbon dioxide from the biomass to form a fluid extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide has a modifier.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The extract comprises 66-72 wt.% 6-gingerol, 8-9 wt.% 8-gingerol, 12-14 wt.% 10-gingerol, and/or 4-5 wt.% 6-shogaol. The composition of matter comprises: a composition with an absolute purity of 28.78% total gingerols and shogaol (20.78 wt.% 6-gingerol, 2.50 wt.% 8-gingerol, 4 wt.% 10-gingerol and 1.50 wt.% 6-shogaol), where the product is chromatographed on solid phase columns to produce a product with an absolute purity greater than 90% total gingerols and shogaol; and an extract of ginger rhizome for the treatment of nausea in humans comprising the extract in an oil base. The oil base is held in a capsule or gelcap. The capsule has 5-20 mg of the extract of ginger rhizome. The extract has 4.50-18 mg of combined gingerols and shogaol. The oil has an antioxidant. The antioxidant is tocopherol. The oil has one or more emulsifying agents chosen from lecithin, and short chain, medium chain and long chain triglycerides. The oil is olive oil. The modifier is ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step of administering is: (a) initiated three days before the start of chemotherapy, (b) performed on the day of chemotherapy, and (c) performed through three days after the end of chemotherapy. The step of administering is at the moment experiencing the nausea and emesis. The nausea and emesis are induced in cancer patients undergoing chemotherapy. The nausea and emesis occur during pregnancy. The method involves forming a dried powdered biomass of ginger rhizome, placing the dried powdered ginger rhizome in a vessel with carbon dioxide under supercritical, near critical or critical conditions to form a saturated biomass powder, and separating the carbon dioxide from the biomass to form a fluid extract, where the product is further purified using chromatography. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is held at a temperature of 20-50 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is held at a pressure of 1000-4000 psi. Preferred Components: The effective amount is administered every 3-4 hours. The nausea and emesis occur after elective surgery using anesthesia. The nausea and emesis are caused by motion in cars, planes, boats and elevators. The nausea and emesis are caused by medication.
   ACTIVITY - Antiemetic. Test details are described but no result given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - The example describes fractionation of ginger rhizome, purification of gingerols, and formulation of purified gingerols that does not support claim.

TI Preparing mixed alcohols involves obtaining an exhaust-gas stream from a methane-to-syngas unit, compressing exhaust-gas stream, and separating exhaust-gas stream into syngas stream, carbon dioxide-rich stream, and methane-rich stream.
AU PARDUN M E

   PARDUN M
AE STANDARD ALCOHOL CO AMERICA INC (STAL-Non-standard)

GA 2022666896
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mixed alcohols involves obtaining an exhaust-gas stream from a methane-to-syngas unit, where exhaust-gas stream comprises carbon dioxide (CO2), carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen (H2), and methane (CH4), compressing exhaust-gas stream, and separating exhaust-gas stream into at least a syngas stream, a CO2-rich stream, and a CH4-rich stream. The syngas stream is introduced into a mixed-alcohol reactor operated at effective alcohol synthesis conditions and in the presence of an alcohol-synthesis catalyst, generated mixed alcohols and a reactor off-gas, and purified mixed alcohols to generate a mixed-alcohol product.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed alcohols
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the environmental footprint of an existing or novel hydrogen production facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for producing mixed alcohols.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The CO2-rich stream is compressed and used in enhanced oil recovery. The CO2-rich stream is sequestered in a geological formation. The CH4-rich stream is utilized as a fuel for heating said methane-to-syngas unit.The process further generates a H2-rich stream. The H2-rich stream is combined with hydrogen produced from methane-to-syngas unit. The H2-rich stream is utilized as a fuel for heating methane-to-syngas unit. The process utilizes an amine-based system, a cryogenic system, a membrane-separation system, or a second pressure-swing adsorption unit. The process is conducted and includes of sulfur removal, dehydration or distillation. Preferred Components: The alcohol-synthesis catalyst is a metal sulfide catalyst. The mixed-alcohol reaction sub-system contains a metal sulfide catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN GB2596830-A; WO2022008915-A1
TI Producing fertilizer composition used to increase nutrient content of plant growing medium comprises providing composition comprising ammonia, and contacting composition comprising carbon dioxide, and source of sulfate ion.
AU HAMMOND P
AE CCM TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CCMT-Non-standard)
GA 202207431C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a fertilizer composition comprises: (a) providing a composition comprising ammonia; and (b) contacting the composition provided in step (a) with (i) a composition comprising carbon dioxide, and (ii) a source of sulfate ion.
   USE - The methods are useful for: producing a fertilizer composition; and increasing the nutrient content of a plant growing medium (all claimed).
No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides improved nutrient composition; and is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a fertilizer composition obtained by the method; and
   (2) increasing the nutrient content of a plant growing medium comprising (a)-(b) as per se, and optionally (iii) cellulosic fibers, (c) optionally pelletizing the material obtained after steps (b), and (d) mixing the mixture obtained after steps (b) and/or (c) with the plant growing medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition provided in step (a) comprises: an anaerobic digestate; an anaerobic digestate and 30-40 wt.% ammonia. The composition comprises: carbon dioxide used in step (b) (i) i.e. a gaseous composition; carbon dioxide comprising the exhaust gases from the combustion of a fossil fuel; and carbon dioxide comprising biogas. The source of sulfate ion comprises gypsum.Preferred Method: The method further involves: adding a source of nitrate ions; adding a source of phosphorus; and a step (c) of pelletizing the material obtained after steps (b). In step (b) (iii) is carried out before step (b) ((i) and step (b) (ii). The step (b) (iii) is carried out after step (b) (i) and step(b) (ii).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (b) further involves contacting the composition provided in step (a) with (iii) cellulosic fibers. The step (b) (iii) involves adding a compost material comprising cellulosic fibers to the mixture obtained in step (a).
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Sample of 640 ml water comprised of 360 g ammonium bicarbonate was taken from a stock solution of ammonium bicarbonate formed by the bubbling carbon dioxide through ammonia solution. The temperature of the solution was 13degrees Celsius and the pH of solution was ca. 8 (tested with pH paper). 392.6 g Calcium sulfate dehydrate was slowly added into the ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution portion by portion over 1 hour and 25 minutes with vigorous stirring. In addition, of calcium sulfate dehydrate into the solution, a lot of bubbles was produced which was carbon dioxide gas. The pH and temperature were monitored time by time through the reaction process. After calcium sulfate dehydrate addition was complete, the temperature had increased to 15.8degrees Celsius and the pH had dropped to 6-7 (tested with pH paper). The reaction mixture was stirred overnight (24 hours) until no bubbles were observed. The solid collected by filtration and dried in air for a week to obtain 258.6 white caked solid.

AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a support layer, and a                selective polymer layer arranged on the support                layer. The selective polymer layer comprises a                selective polymer matrix comprising hydrophilic                polymer and an aminosilane cross-linking                agent.
   USE - Membrane e.g. gas permeable membrane used for                separating gas such as carbon dioxide, hydrogen                sulfide and hydrogen chloride (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) separation of gas (G1) from a feed gas                stream, which involves contacting a membrane with                the feed gas stream comprising gas (G1) under                conditions effective to afford transmembrane                permeation of the gas (G1); and
   (2) manufacture of membrane, which involves                depositing a selective polymer layer on a support                layer.

The selective                  polymer layer further comprises carbon nanotubes                  dispersed in the selective polymer matrix. The                  membrane is configured in a flat sheet,                  spiral-wound, hollow fiber, or plate-and-frame                  configuration. The membrane is selectively                  permeable to an acidic gas, or fluid comprising                  carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide,                  sulfur trioxide, nitrogen oxide, hydrogen                  chloride, water, and their combinations. The feed                  gas comprises hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen                  sulfide, hydrogen chloride, carbon monoxide,                  nitrogen, methane, steam, sulfur oxides, nitrogen                  oxides, syngas and their combinations. The membrane has a gas (G1)/(G2) selectivity of                  20-300 at 107 degrees C and feed pressure of 31.7                  bar. Preferred Composition: The selective polymer                  layer comprises 0.01-5 wt.% graphene oxide with                  respect to the total dry weight of the selective                  polymer layer.

PN WO2021165514-A1
TI Production of particle coating on articles involves mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with solution comprising dissolved material for forming particles, spraying mixture into chamber which is at pressure below supercritical pressure of supercritical fluid, and capturing particles on articles.
AU PADRELA L
   VERMA V
   RYAN K
AE UNIV LIMERICK (UYLI-Non-standard)
GA 2021990029
AB    NOVELTY - Production of particle coating on one or more articles (100) involves (102) mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with a solution comprising dissolved material for forming particles, (104) spraying the mixture into a precipitation chamber to precipitate particles, and the chamber is at a pressure below a supercritical pressure of the supercritical fluid, and capturing the precipitated particles on one or more articles located within the chamber.
   USE - Production of particle coating on article(s).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces cost and complexity of the process and equipment, and increases throughput and/or process efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart for production of particle coating on articles.
   Production of particle coating (100)
   Mixing process (102)
   Spraying process (104)
   Coating process (106)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution comprises an active pharmaceutical ingredient dissolved in an organic solvent, and optionally (i) the active pharmaceutical ingredient comprises one of carbamazepine, risperidone, ketoprofen and hydrochlorothiazide, and/or (ii) the organic solvent comprises an alcohol, preferably methanol. The solution comprises a saturated solution. Preferred Property: The distance between an entry location of the spray and the one or more articles are 10-20 cm, preferably 15 cm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrier particles comprise microcrystalline cellulose. Preferred Process: The method further involves introducing a flow of drying gas into the chamber, orienting a flow direction of drying gas and a spray direction of the mixture in the same direction, directing the flow of drying gas from a top portion of the chamber towards the one or more articles, spraying the mixture through a nozzle having an orifice size of 150 mu m or smaller, into the chamber to precipitate particles, capturing the precipitated particles on carrier particles in a fluidized bed located within the chamber, fluidizing the bed of carrier particles using the flow of drying gas, controlling a concentration of the solution to control particle loading on the one or more articles, supporting the one or more articles on the surface comprising a mesh, configured to allow fluid to pass through, selecting or controlling an area of the surface configured to allow fluid to pass through, and optionally by controlling movement of a cover arranged over the surface, and mixing the supercritical carbon dioxide with the solution in the nozzle comprising a coaxial nozzle.

PN CN113186565-A
TI Preparation of nitrogen doped carbon material used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide includes dissolving carrier carbon tube or metal oxide with aniline monomer in acid solution, performing suction filtration and drying.
AU KANG P
   SHI H
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202190053W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nitrogen doped carbon material comprises weighing carrier carbon tube or metal oxide, dissolving with aniline monomer in acid solution to obtain precursor solution A, dissolving oxidant in acid solution to obtain precursor solution B, mixing precursor B solution to precursor solution A, placing precursor solution in ice water bath at 0-10 degrees C for 2-8 hours, centrifuging, performing suction filtration, drying in oven to obtain sample and carbonizing dried sample in tubular furnace at 700-900 degrees c for 2-4 hours.
   USE - The preparation of nitrogen doped carbon material is used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has not greater than 95% selectivity of carbon monoxide and reduces flue gas produced in daily life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst electrode is prepared by weighing nitrogen doped carbon material catalyst, dispersing in volatile solvent, adding Nafion solution to obtain uniform electrode dispersion by ultrasound, taking 5-10 mu l electrode dispersed droplets, applying to glassy carbon electrode and performing electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in 0.5 M potassium bicarbonate solution with calomel electrode as reference electrode and platinum wire as counter electrode. Preferred Components: The carrier carbon nanotubes or metal oxide is multi walled carbon nanotubes, zirconium dioxide and/or cerium (IV) oxide. The ratio of carrier added to precursor solution A to aniline is 1:2-2:1. The concentration of aniline in precursor solution A is 0.2-0.6 mol/l. The oxidant is ammonium persulfate, ferric chloride and/or potassium dichromate. The concentration of oxidant in precursor solution B is 0.05-0.15 mol/l. The molar ratio of aniline in precursor A to oxidant in precursor B is 4-8:1. The carbonization atmosphere of tubular furnace is ammonia and/or argon. The volatile solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide, ethanol and/or isopropanol. The catalyst concentration in electrode dispersion is 10 mg/ml. The Nafion mass concentration is 0.25 wt.%.

PT P
PN CN112899722-A; CN112899722-B
TI Preparing metal-organic framework-derived            indium/zinc oxide doped carbon hollow nano-cage            electrocatalytic material used in electrocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, involves dissolving            cetrimonium bromide in water, and drying            precursor.
AU CHEN Z
   TENG X
   NIU Y
   GONG S
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202166967N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing metal-organic                framework-derived indium/zinc oxide doped carbon                hollow nano-cage electrocatalytic material,                involves (a) dissolving soluble zinc salt, soluble                indium salt and cetrimonium bromide in water to                obtain solution (A), (b) dissolving                2-methylimidazole in water to obtain solution (B),                and (c) pouring obtained solution (A) to solution                (B), uniformly stirring, separating, washing and                drying obtained precursor product, and calcining to                obtain target product.
   USE - The method for preparing metal-organic                framework-derived indium/zinc oxide doped carbon                hollow nano-cage electrocatalytic material used in                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the application cost of                indium, solves the problem of low catalytic current                density of indium electrocatalyst to certain                extent, expands zinc at same time, and is suitable                for base catalyst to formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the                  soluble zinc salt is zinc nitrate, and the                  soluble indium salt is indium nitrate. Preferred                  Composition: In step (a), the ratio of addition                  amount of soluble zinc salt, soluble indium salt,                  cetrimonium bromide and water is 0.16 g:0.08                  g:0.004 g:8 ml. Preferred Conditions: The step                  (c) involves uniformly stirring for 0.5 hour, and                  calcining at 600 degrees C for 2 hours under                  argon atmosphere
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (b), the                  ratio of the addition amount of 2-methylimidazole                  and water is 3.632 g:56 ml. In step (c), the                  addition amount of solution (A) to solution (B)                  meets the mass ratio of soluble zinc salt and                  2-methylimidazole is 0.16 g:3.632 g.

TI Producing activated carbon for adsorbing dioxin and butane by pulverizing rice bran, malt debris, and/or silver grass, carbonizing, and activating carbonized raw material powder while injecting superheated steam, carbon dioxide, or air.
AU MIN S D
AE POSCO CHEM (POSC-C)
GA 2021557467
AB    NOVELTY - An activated carbon is produced using plant-based raw materials by pulverizing rice bran, malt debris, and/or silver grass to average particle size of 5-1000 mu m based on D50 to prepare a raw material powder; carbonizing pulverized raw material powder at 300-700 degrees C for 1 minute to 10 hours; and activating carbonized raw material powder at 500-950 degrees C for 30 minutes to 6 hours while injecting superheated steam at 150-700 degrees C, carbon dioxide, or air.
   USE - Production of activated carbon using plant-based raw materials for adsorption of harmful gas, such as butane and dioxin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon has good adsorption and high toxic gas removal efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Activated carbon is a porous material with developed meso- and macropores.

PN CN112779086-A
TI Removing peroxidation value of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises e.g. placing the broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles in the carbon dioxide extraction kettle, adding cosolvent and performing supercritical extraction.
AU CHEN Y
AE JIANGSU ZIBUTANG PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202155012C
AB    NOVELTY - Removing peroxidation value of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises placing the broken Ganoderma lucidumspore powder particles in a carbon dioxide extraction kettle, adding cosolvent and performing supercritical extraction, taking out the extract from the extraction kettle and removing the water and other impurities by molecular distillation to obtain refined Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, maintaining the activity of the Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and avoid oxidation, carrying out the extraction and refining under the protection of nitrogen and encapsulating the refined Ganoderma lucidum spore oil by capsules.
   USE - The method is useful for removing peroxidation value of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and oxygen and other external gas; controls the peroxide value and avoids the component from oxidize.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed, under the protection of nitrogen, water is used as entrainment, the extraction and separation operations are performed. In the extraction kettle, the extraction pressure is 15-25MPa, the temperature is 35-45 degrees C, and the extraction time is 2-3 hours, then add water as an cosolvent and continue the extraction for 1-2 hours, then the separation is carried out in two steps. In the step (i), the separation is carried out through the first separation kettle, the separation pressure is 5-7MPa and the temperature is 40-45 degrees C. In the step (ii) separation is carried out through the second separation kettle, the separation pressure is 3-5MPa, and the temperature is 30-40 degrees C, the extraction kettle, the first separating kettle and the second separating kettle are all under nitrogen protection. The purification is further purified by molecular distillation, the control indicators includes distillation temperature of 190-210 degrees C, scraper speed at 120-160 rotations/minute (rpm), the sampling rate of 2-3 ml/minutes and refined Ganoderma lucidumspores are obtained.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN112608556-A
TI Vehicle polypropylene composite material with soft tactility comprises e.g. pre-drying polypropylene, thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride and calcium carbonate and irradiating blend particles.
AU WANG L
   WENG Y
   CHEN S
   XU C
   XU K
AE SUZHOU RUNJIA POLYMER MATERIALS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202138205Y
AB    NOVELTY - Vehicle polypropylene composite material with soft tactility comprises (i) pre-drying polypropylene, thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride, and calcium carbonate in an oven for 12-24 hours; (ii) passing the dried raw materials in step (i) through a twin-screw extruder through a melt extrusion process and melt blending at 210-230 degrees C with screw speed of 20-30 revolutions/minute; (iii) extruding and pelletizing the blend in a carbon dioxide foamed plastic extrusion granulation molding equipment, and using carbon dioxide as a physical foaming agent to obtain blend particles; and (iv) irradiating the blend particles with high-energy rays in an air atmosphere.
   USE - Used as vehicle polypropylene composite material with soft tactility.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good compatibility, uniform surface appearance, no cracks and no flow marks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the component comprises 50-60 pts. wt., preferably 55 pts. wt. polypropylene, 15-18 pts. wt., preferably 17 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride, and 5-8 pts. wt., preferably 6 pts. wt. calcium carbonate. The melt flow rate of the polypropylene is 15-18 g/10 minutes, the melt flow rate of the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer is 12-15 g/10 minutes and the melt flow rate of the polyethylene grafted maleic anhydride is 20-25 g/10 minutes. The polypropylene is a mixture of atactic polypropylene and isotactic polypropylene and the crystallinity of the isotactic polypropylene is more than 80%, and the isotacticity is 90-95%. The calcium carbonate is ball milled by a ball mill before use, and a particle size sieve is used to make the average particle size of the calcium carbonate less than 100 mu m. In the step (iii), the pressure of carbon dioxide is 10-15 Bar. In the step (iv), the high-energy rays are gamma rays, and the irradiation dose is 100-150 kGy and the irradiation source of high-energy rays is cobalt 60. The vehicle polypropylene composite material with soft tactility is further added with other additives, pigments, antistatic agents, flame retardants, antioxidants, light stabilizers, and/or plasticizers.

PN CN112473712-A
TI Preparation of cerium oxide/graphitized carbonitride heterojunction material for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves calcining dispersion mixture of cerium oxide and graphitized carbonitride, in air, nitrogen and hydrogen.
AU DONG L
   LI W
   PU Y
   ZOU W
   WEI X
   ZHU C
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 202130666A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cerium oxide/graphitized carbonitride heterojunction material involves adding solid cerium oxide into ultra-pure water, ultrasonically dispersing to form dispersion (A), ultrasonically dispersing solid graphitized carbonitride in ultra-pure water to obtain dispersion (B), dripping dispersion (B) into dispersion (A), uniformly mixing, heat-stirring to remove water to obtain solid, uniformly grinding the solid material, calcining in air, nitrogen and hydrogen atmosphere, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain cerium carbonitride, cerium-carbonitride-nitrogen and cerium carbonitride-hydrogen heterojunction material.
   USE - Preparation of cerium oxide/graphitized carbonitride heterojunction material for processing in different atmosphere e.g. photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, uses inexpensive raw materials, and produces heterojunction material having excellent environmental stability and strong practicability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Cerium oxide is prepared by calcining cerium nitrate hexahydrate at 550-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours. Solid graphitized carbonitride is prepared by calcining urea in a muffle furnace at 550-600 degrees C in presence of air. The reaction temperature of dispersion (A) and dispersion (B) is 100-200 degrees C. The calcination temperature is 450-550 degrees C and gas flow rate is 150-250 mL/minute. Preferred Composition: The concentration of dispersion (A) is 0.5-1 g/L and concentration of dispersion (B) is 9-9.5 g/L and ultrasonic dispersing time is 0.5-1 hour. The mass ratio of cerium oxide and graphitized carbonitride is 1:10-1:15.

   USE - The method is for preparation of two-dimensional (2D) carbon nitride photocatalyst containing D-A structure used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare hydrocarbon fuels (claimed).

AU ZHANG T
   YANG W
   LIU Z
   GAO H
   YANG H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202109817A
AB    NOVELTY - Metal supported catalyst comprises 80-99.9 pts. wt. carrier and 0.1-20 pts. wt. active component. The active component includes iron, cobalt, nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium, and/or platinum; and the carrier includes cerium oxide and main group element oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in the reaction of dry reforming of low-carbon alkanes and carbon dioxide into syngas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high catalytic activity and selectivity for dry reforming of ethane at lower reaction temperature, and excellent resistance to carbon deposition. The method: is simple and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing metal supported catalyst, comprising preparing carrier containing cerium oxide and main group element oxide, and loading active component, including iron element and cobalt, nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium, and/or platinum on the carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active component includes iron, cobalt, nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium, and/or platinum in a molar ratio of 1:9-5:1. The main group element oxide includes magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide, silicon oxide, calcium oxide, gallium oxide or tin oxide. The catalyst includes 0.1-20 pts. wt. active components, 15-85 pts. wt. cerium oxide, and 10-80 pts. wt. main group element oxides; and the molar percentage of trivalent cerium in carrier cerium oxide based on the mole percentage of cerium atoms in the cerium oxide is 13-55%. The soluble cerium salt includes cerium nitrate (III), cerium nitrate (IV), and/or cerium ammonium nitrate (IV); the surfactant includes polyoxyethylene-polyoxypropylene-polyoxyethylene triblock polymer, polyethylene glycol, polypropylene glycol; and the solvent includes methanol, ethanol, and water. Preferred Method: The sol-gel method is used to synthesize carrier containing cerium oxide and main group element oxide, and the method specifically comprises mixing surfactant, solvent, soluble cerium salt, and soluble salt of another main group element, heating, and calcining. The heating temperature is 30-120 degrees C, and the heating time is 12-72 hours; and the calcination temperature in the air is 400-800 degrees C, and the calcination time is 4-8 hours. The method comprises loading active components on carrier, and the method specifically comprises mixing soluble salt of active component, solvent, and carrier.

The heating is infrared heating and electron beam heating. Preferred Components: The slipping agent comprises one or more of low-density polyethylene resin, erucamide and oleamide.

PN CN111808066-A; HK40032090-A0
TI Extracting schisandrin B comprises e.g.            pulverizing Schisandra chinensis, subjecting to            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, eluting,            separating by silica gel column chromatography and            carrying out low-temperature crystallization.
AU ZHOU Y
AE HEILONGJIANG AOLIDA PHARM CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
   HEILONGJIANG AOLIDA PHARM CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5111W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting schisandrin B comprises (i)                pulverizing Schisandra chinensis to obtain                Schisandra chinensis powder, (ii) subjecting the                Schisandra chinensis powder to supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction to obtain supercritical extract,                (iii) dissolving the supercritical extract in                organic solvent, eluting and separating by silica                gel column chromatography and concentrating to                obtain concentrated fraction of schisandrin B, (iv)                dissolving the concentrated fraction of schisandrin                B in a mixed solvent of hexane and isopropanol, and                carrying out low-temperature crystallization to                obtain crude schisandrin B, and (v) purifying the                crude schisandrin B by hexane to obtain schisandrin                B.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                schisandrin B (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has content measured by                high-performance liquid chromatography method of                25-95%; and has low cost, and complete process; and                is suitable for industrial promotion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mesh number of the                  Schisandra chinensis powder is 30-50 mesh. The                  extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction is 20-30 MPa; the extraction                  temperature is 30-50 degrees C, and the                  extraction time is 1.5-2.5 hours. Preferred                  Method: The step (iii) comprises (a) dissolving                  the supercritical extract in organic solvent,                  passing through silica gel column chromatography,                  eluting and separating with first eluent, and                  concentrating to obtain concentrated fraction of                  lignan, where the organic solvent is petroleum                  ether, the first eluent is petroleum ether, (b)                  dissolving the lignan concentrated fraction with                  second organic solvent, separating by silica gel                  column chromatography with second eluent elution,                  and concentrating to obtain schisandrin B                  concentrated fraction, where the second organic                  solvent is a mixed solvent of petroleum ether and                  acetone, and the volume ratio between petroleum                  ether and acetone in the second organic solvent                  is 90-97:10-3, the second eluent is a mixed                  solvent of petroleum ether and acetone, and the                  volume ratio between petroleum ether and acetone                  in the second eluent is 90-97:10-3. In the step                  (a), the flow rate of the first eluent is 3-7                  ml/minute, and in the step (b), the flow rate of                  the second eluent is 3-7 ml/minute. In the step                  (iv), the volume ratio between hexane and                  isopropanol is 1:1.5-2.5; and the temperature of                  low-temperature recrystallization is -10 degrees                  C to -30 degrees C. In the step (iv), the volume                  ratio between hexane and isopropanol is 1:2, and                  the temperature of low-temperature                  recrystallization is -20 degrees C. In the step                  (iv), the mass-volume ratio between concentrated                  fraction of schisandrin B and mixed solvent is                  1:1-3 (g:ml). The crude schisandrin B is purified                  by elution with hexane at 0-8 degrees C. The                  schisandrin B extract uses schisandrin B as                  indicator component, and its content is 25-95                  wt.%.

PN CN111747815-A
TI Separating methane oxidation coupling reaction            product gas comprises providing methane oxidation            coupling reaction product gas contains hydrogen,            methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ethylene,            ethane, and carbon.
AU SUN L
   LIU G
   ZHAO B
   SHENG Z
   WANG Z
   NIE Y
   LI S
   DING L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A1202E
AB    NOVELTY - Separating methane oxidation coupling reaction                product gas comprises providing methane oxidation                coupling reaction product gas contains hydrogen,                methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ethylene,                ethane, carbon and more than three hydrocarbons,                and preferably acetylene, sulfur oxide, nitrogen                oxide, where the separating method comprises                quenching and cooling product gas, primary                boosting, amine washing, alkali washing, secondary                boosting, secondary cooling, rectifying to remove                light component, expander refrigerating and heat                exchange with the product gas, pressure swing                adsorption separating methane and carbon monoxide,                carbon two and carbon three and more components                separating, carbon two component refining.
   USE - The method is useful for separating methane                oxidation coupling reaction product gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures simple process flow,                reduces the number of cold boxes, avoids the                complicated heat exchange network, and reduces the                energy consumption and equipment investment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The light component                  comprises hydrogen, methane and carbon monoxide.                  The expander is a gas expander. Preferred Method:                  The separating method comprises (1) cooling the                  product gas by quenching unit, the product gas                  generating steam and cooling to 30-50 degrees C                  gas phase; (2) the gas phase from the quenching                  unit is boosted to 1-2.5 MPaG, and the compressor                  for one time; (3) removing CO2 and sulfur oxide                  in the product gas according to the sequence of                  alkali washing after the gas phase after the step                  of preamine washing, obtaining the gas phase with                  the concentration of CO2 and sulfur oxide in the                  rate of 1-20 ppm; (4) the gas phase obtained in                  the step (3) is boosted to 3-3.0 4.5 MPaG, the                  compressor for the second time, (v) gas phase                  after boosting enters the cold separating unit                  for secondary cooling, the cold separating unit                  realizes hydrogen, methane, separating CO and                  carbon two and heavier components, the operation                  in the cold separating unit comprises gradually                  reducing the gas phase temperature by using the                  cold box or/and heat exchanger; removing hydrogen                  from the tower top in the demethane tower after                  cooling, after methane and CO, using expander for                  refrigeration, the obtained cold energy is used                  for heat exchange with the product gas to reduce                  the product gas temperature, then performing                  pressure swing adsorption operation to realize                  the separation of methane and hydrogen, and                  carbon monoxide; and (6) carbon two and carbon                  three and heavier components obtained in the cold                  separation unit, separating carbon two and carbon                  three and heavier components through the                  deethanitizing tower, after the carbon two                  components are selectively hydrogenated to remove                  acetylene hydrocarbon, obtaining polymerized                  ethylene by rectifying operation. The quenching                  and cooling process uses the product gas heat to                  generate 0.2-5 MPaG steam, and the steam                  generator is one-stage or multi-stage. The                  methane obtained by pressure swing adsorption                  separation is further recycled to the upstream                  oxidation coupling reaction system to participate                  in the reaction. The operation pressure of the                  demethanizer is 3-4.5 MPaG, and the temperature                  of the tower top is -70 to -102 degrees C. The                  benzene removal process is provided during the                  primary and/or secondary pressure increase of the                  product gas to control the benzene content                  entering the downstream process. The                  refrigeration outlet pressure of the expander is                  0.05-1 MPaG. The ethane obtained in the carbon                  two component refining process is sent back to                  the upstream oxidative coupling reaction system                  to participate in the reaction.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) the catalyst used in the synthesis method, which comprises metal atoms and/or metal nanoparticles supported on the reactant carbonate, where the metal atoms and/or the metal in the metal nanoparticles are platinum, ruthenium, iridium, rhodium, osmium, palladium and its alloy; and

The metal in the metal atom and/or metal nanoparticles is platinum, ruthenium, iridium, rhodium, palladium and/or its alloy. The carrier is metal oxide, metal hydroxide, metal carbonate and/or metal phosphate. The metal in the metal oxide, metal hydroxide, metal carbonate and/or metal phosphate as the carrier is cerium, iron, cobalt, nickel, zinc, manganese, tin, indium, chromium, potassium, sodium, calcium, magnesium, strontium, barium, aluminum and/or silicon.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst and carbonate are dispersed in a solution, where the solvent of the solution is alkane, water and/or alcohol. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the gas in the reaction system is 0.1-25 MPa, preferably 2-8 MPa. Preferred Components: The organic compound is a 2-35C, preferably 5-26C polycarbon compound, which is polycarbon hydrocarbon and/or polycarbon alcohol. The hydrocarbon is alkane and/or alkene. AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of lithium involves (1) mixing a                carbon dioxide gas and/or adding a water-soluble                carbonate to a to-be-treated liquid in which at                least dissolving a lithium, (2) separating a                precipitate containing crystals of lithium                carbonate precipitated by the step (1) from the                to-be-treated liquid, and (3) separating and                recovering an alkali containing at least lithium                hydroxide from the to-be-treated liquid by                performing a bipolar membrane electrodialysis with                respect to the to-be-treated liquid after the step                (2).

PN CN111139108-A; CN111139108-B
TI Reacting carbon monoxide water vapor conversion            using platinum-based catalyst comprises e.g. using            platinum-based catalyst to catalyze reaction of carbon            monoxide with water to produce carbon dioxide and            hydrogen.
AU ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
   HUANG Y
   HUANG C
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202042449G
AB    NOVELTY - Reacting carbon monoxide water vapor                conversion using platinum-based catalyst comprises                using the platinum-based catalyst to catalyze the                reaction of carbon monoxide with water to produce                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the structural                formula of the platinum-based catalyst is                platinum/-zirconium dioxide/santa barbara amorphous                (SBA)-15. The platinum-based catalyst comprises a                composition carrier formed by zirconium dioxide                dispersed on SBA-15 and active metal platinum                supported on the composition carrier. The content                of zirconium dioxide in the composition carrier is                8.5-11 wt.%, and the content of active metal                platinum in the catalyst is 1-2 wt.%.
   USE - The method is useful for reacting carbon                monoxide water vapor conversion using                platinum-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent catalytic activity,                synergistic catalytic effect and excellent                industrial application prospects; and obtains high                catalytic efficiency at relatively low                temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction                  temperature of the water vapor conversion                  reaction is 300 degrees C or higher, preferably                  300-400 degrees C. The alkaline solution is                  ammonium carbonate or ammonia water during the                  preparation of the composition carrier, and the                  pH value of the solution is adjusted and                  maintained at 9.5-10.5. The solid-liquid                  separation is filtration, and the solvent used in                  the photocatalytic reduction method is a mixed                  liquid of methanol and water. The platinum-based                  catalyst is pretreated with a mixed gas                  containing hydrogen before being used in the                  catalytic reaction. The temperature of the                  pretreatment of the platinum-based catalyst is                  280-320 degrees C for 1-4 hours, preferably 1.5-2                  hours, and the content of hydrogen in the                  pre-treated mixed gas is 10-30 vol.%. The mixed                  gas for pretreatment of the catalyst is composed                  of hydrogen and an inert gas, and the volume                  content of the hydrogen is 15-25%, preferably the                  inert gas is argon. Preferred Method: The                  platinum-based catalyst method comprises using a                  dipping method to load zirconium dioxide on                  SBA-15 in a single-layer or multi-layer                  dispersion form to prepare the composition                  carrier, and anchoring the nano- platinum and/or                  supported on the above composition carrier by a                  photocatalytic reduction method. The composition                  carrier method comprises mixing zirconyl chloride                  octahydrate and SBA-15 molecular sieve in a                  liquid, adjusting the pH value using an alkaline                  solution, separating, washing, drying and                  calcining the resulting solid-liquid at 400-600                  degrees C. The photocatalytic reduction method                  comprises adding a chloroplatinic acid solution                  to a solvent containing the composition carrier                  to obtain a mixed solution, which is reduced by a                  photocatalyst of the mixed solution under                  irradiation of a ultraviolet (UV) lamp in a dark                  environment, and subjecting the solid formed to                  solid-liquid separation, washing and                  drying.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   ASAHI YUKIZAI KOGYO KK (ASAY-C)
GA 202037579X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyurethane foam involves mixing composition (A) comprising polyol as a main component and composition (B) comprising polyisocyanate as a main component, reacting, foaming, curing, adding a foaming agent (A1) comprising subcritical or liquid carbon dioxide to the compositions (A) and/or (B), and reacting. The polyol is an aromatic polyester polyol. The compositions (A) and/or (B) comprise flame retardant (F1) comprising red phosphorus.
   USE - Manufacture of polyurethane foam used as heat insulating material. Uses include but are not limited to interior and exterior wall materials, panels for construction, metal siding, electric refrigerator, insulation of building walls, ceilings, roofs, condominiums, and frozen warehouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces polyurethane foam having excellent flame retardance, uniform density distribution, suppresses the occurrence of curvature and the occurrence of the difference in shrinkability between the layers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compositions (A) and/or (B) comprise flame retardant (F2) comprising at least one of a phosphate ester, phosphate-containing flame retardant, stannate-containing flame retardant, bromine-containing flame retardant, boron-containing flame retardant and metal hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The polyol comprises 30 %mass or more aromatic polyester polyol. The compositions (A) and/or (B) comprise 1-70 parts mass flame retardant (F1) comprising red phosphorus and 3-90 parts mass flame retardant (F2) with respect to 100 parts mass polyol in the composition (A). Preferred Components: The composition (A) further comprises polyether polyol as polyol. The composition (A), composition (B) and foaming agent (F1) comprises foaming agent (A2) comprising at least one of water, hydrocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon and halogenated olefin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polyurethane foam has a difference between the overall density and the core density of 0.1-12 kg/m3, and a total calorific value of 8 MJ/m2 or less when heated with radioactive heat intensity of 50 kW/m2 for 10 minutes, preferably 8 minutes according to the test method specified in ISO-5660. The aromatic polyester polyol has a number average molecular weight of 200-1000.

Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) taking the above raw materials, (ii) crushing and passing through a 60 mesh sieve to obtain mixture, placing in the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction kettle, (iii) adding ethanol as cosolvent and extracting, (iv) increasing the temperature for recycling ethanol to obtain extract, adding purified water until the density is not less than 1.04 and adjusting the pH value to 4.3-6 by using pH adjusting agent including citric acid or dilute hydrochloric acid and filtering, and (v) filling and sterilizing. PN CN110496651-A; CN110496651-B
TI Preparing catalyst used for preparing methanol from syngas, by reacting cation solutions with alkaline solution, controlling pH of precipitate, roasting, precipitating silicon and aluminum compounds on powder and treating powder.
AU YU Y
   CHEN H
   YIN Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2019A4113U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing catalyst, involves (i) mixing and reacting cation solutions containing copper, zinc and aluminum with an alkaline solution to obtain a precipitate, controlling the pH value of the precipitate to 7-8, aging, washing, drying and roasting the precipitates to obtain a powder (a), (ii) precipitating silicon and aluminum compounds onto powder (a) by adopting a chemical method, drying, roasting, washing, drying and roasting to obtain a powder (b), and (iii) treating powder (b) by using reagent and carrying out molding to obtain the catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst for preparing methanol from syngas with high carbon dioxide content.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent hydrothermal stability and maintains active specific surface area and methanol yield, in the reaction of high carbon dioxide content syngas to methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The chemical method is carried out by reacting a mixed solution containing a soluble silicon source and an aluminum source with alkaline solution to obtain a precipitate; or co-currently subjecting the mixed solution containing soluble silicon source and aluminum source to a double hydrolysis reaction to obtain a precipitate and adjusting the pH value of the precipitate to 7-8 with an alkaline solution or a nitric acid solution. The reagent treatment in step (iii) is carried out by soaking the powder (b) with a reagent at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C for 20-100 minutes, washing, drying and roasting. The step of roasting is carried out at a temperature of 250-500 degrees C. Preferred Components: The alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide solution, sodium carbonate solution or sodium bicarbonate solution. The reagent is a phosphoric acid solution containing ethylene glycol. Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of the alkaline solution is 0.5-2 mol/l. The mass ratio of silicon to aluminum contained in the silicon source and the aluminum source is 1:4-4:1. The ratio of the total mass of the silicon and aluminum elements to the total surface area of the powder (a) is 1x 10-4-5x 10-3 g/m2. The mass concentration of phosphoric acid in the phosphoric acid solution is 70-85%. The mass concentration of ethylene glycol is 1-5%.

PT P
PN CN110305070-A; CN110305070-B
TI Preparing tetrazolium acetate by hydrated            formaldehyde method comprises e.g. adding triethyl            orthoformate and glycine into solvent, setting the            temperature reaction, and adding hydrated carbon            dioxide into reaction system.
AU FENG W
   YUE T
   XU T
   YANG S
   DI Y
   XING L
   LIU Q
AE CHEM TECHNOLOGY ACAD (CHTE-Non-standard)
   CHEM TECHNOLOGY ACAD (CHTE-Non-standard)
GA 2019961918
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tetrazolium acetate by hydrated                formaldehyde method comprises (i) adding triethyl                orthoformate and glycine into the solvent and                setting the temperature reaction for 1-3 hours;                (ii) adding hydrated carbon dioxide into the                reaction system obtained in step (i), and setting                the temperature reaction for 3-5 hours; (iii)                adding sodium nitrite solid into the system                obtained in the step (ii), setting the temperature                for 2 hours, adding 98% concentrated sulfuric acid                solution, and continuing to continue the heat                preservation reaction for 2-4 hours after the                dropwise addition, subjecting to filtration,                chromatography, steaming, crystal precipitation and                drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tetrazolium                acetate by hydrated formaldehyde method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple synthetic route, high                product purity, strong operability and                repeatability and is replaced by industrial                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i) the molar                  ratio of glycine to orthoformate toluene is                  1:0.3-0.5. The solvent is glacial acetic acid and                  the molar ratio of glycine to glacial acetic acid                  is 1:5-8. In step (ii) the molar ratio of                  hydration incorporation to glycine is 0.5-0.8:1.                  In step (ii) the concentration of the hydrated                  blend is 85%. In step (iii) the molar ratio of                  sodium nitrite to glycine is 0.6-0.8:1. In step                  (iii) the ratio of 98% of concentrated sulfuric                  acid to glycine is 0.25-0.5:1. Preferred Method:                  In step (i) the temperature is 25-45 degrees C.                  In step (ii) the reaction temperature is 55-75                  degrees C.
