PT P
PN CN110091034-A
TI Welding 921A steel comprises preheating the whole workpiece to be welded, performing gas metal arc welding on the workpiece, using a mixed gas of argon gas and carbon dioxide as shielding gas.
AU CHEN B
   WANG Q
   WANG F
   ZHAO J
   YANG X
AE WUHAN MARINE MACHINERY PLANT CO LTD (CSHI-C)
GA 201969892H
AB    NOVELTY - Welding 921A steel comprises preheating the whole workpiece to be welded, performing gas metal arc welding on the workpiece, using a mixed gas of argon gas and carbon dioxide as shielding gas, the welding wire used for welding comprises 0-0.08 wt.% carbon, 0.20-0.55 wt.% silicon, 0.70-1.65 manganese, 0-0.01 wt.% sulfur, 0-0.015 wt.% phosphorus, 0-0.50 wt.% molybdenum , 1.50-3.50 wt.% nickel, 0-3 wt.% chromium, 0-0.10 wt.% vanadium, 0-0.10 wt.% copper, and iron, and annealing the workpiece.
   USE - The method is useful for welding 921A steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the quality of the weld; has excellent quality and meet the welding requirements of 921A steel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preheating of the entire workpiece to be welded includes measuring a thickness of the workpiece to be welded. If the thickness of the area to be welded does not exceed 30 mm, preheat the workpiece to 80-100 degrees C. If the thickness of the area to be welded is greater than 30 mm, the workpiece is preheated to between 100-150 degrees C. The volume ratio of argon to carbon dioxide in the shielding gas is 80%:20%. The flow rate of the protective gas is from 15-35 l/minutes. When the workpiece is subjected to gas metal arc welding, the welding current is controlled to be 220-240 A, the welding voltage is 24-28 V, and the welding speed is 28-35 cm/minutes. The wire has a dry elongation of 10-14 mm during welding. The temperature between the layers is controlled to be 80-150 degrees C during welding. The annealing temperature is lower than the phase transition temperature of 921A steel. The annealing the workpiece comprises: gradually heating the workpiece to 380-420 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The workpiece is gradually heated to 510-530 degrees C and held for 5-6 hours. If the temperature of the workpiece exceeds 400 degrees C, the rate at which the temperature rises is controlled to not exceed 200 degrees C/hours.

TI Supercritical preparation of foam aluminum            material with controllable aperture by ultrasonically            processing ethyl cellulose to anhydrous ethanol, adding            aluminum scraps and performing microwave reaction with            supercritical carbon dioxide.

AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 2019249925
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical preparation of foam aluminum                material with controllable aperture comprises                adding ethyl cellulose to anhydrous ethanol,                ultrasonically processing for 5-10 minutes to                obtain dispersed alcohol solution, adding crushed                aluminum scraps to dispersed alcohol solution,                stirring for 20-50 minutes to obtain suspension                alcohol solution, performing constant-pressure                microwave reaction with supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid for 1-3 hours, performing                sedimentation to obtain mixed solution, putting                mixed solution in metal mold, constant-pressure                heating for 1-3 hours, constant-pressure reacting                for 2-4 hours, blowing by nitrogen, cooling to                obtain foam aluminum precursor, illuminating and                decomposing for 2-4 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The material has simple preparation, low                production cost and uniform pore structure. The                method is suitable for mass production.

TI Preparation of monodisperse cubic precipitated            calcium carbonate includes calcining limestone,            digesting quicklime, introducing carbon dioxide,            reacting, mixing, adjusting pH, aging, drying,            pulverizing and sieving.
AU YANG A
   ZHU Y
   WEN Q
   LI Y
   MAN Z
AE GUANGXI HESHAN HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019249161
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of monodisperse cubic precipitated                calcium carbonate comprises calcining limestone at                1000-1300 degrees C to obtain quicklime, digesting                quicklime to obtain calcium hydroxide to obtained                calcium hydroxide suspension, introducing carbon                dioxide to calcium hydroxide suspension, reacting                at 20-30 degrees C, mixing at 500-800 revolutions                per minute (rpm), adjusting pH to 9, adding calcium                hydroxide suspension to reaction tower to obtain                calcium hydroxide suspension, adding dispersant                Span-20 to calcium hydroxide suspension, mixing at                600 rpm for 30 minutes, aging at 60-80 degrees C                for 24 hours, dehydrating aged calcium hydroxide                suspension, drying, pulverizing and sieving.
   USE - Method of preparing monodisperse cubic                precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has good dispersibility,                uniform particle shape and regular shape. The                method has simple operation and low cost.

PN CN109339883-A
TI Heat-pump condensed industrial waste heat recycling system, has electrically-driven heat pump for cooling condensed water in condenser to form main power generation circulation, and heat source connected with heat pump to heat.
AU WANG Y
AE DAQING YUANSHI ELECTROMECHANICAL TECHNOL (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 201917675S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an industrial waste heat part connected with an evaporator that is connected with a working substance generator i.e. carbon dioxide generator set. A condenser is connected with the working substance generator and a working substance pump. An electrically-driven heat pump cools condensed water in the condenser to form main power generation circulation. A heat source is connected with the electrically-driven heat pump to heat, where the electrically-driven heat pump inputs industrial waste heat into the evaporator to reduce temperature of the industrial waste heat. TI Carbon dioxide injection-production well system optimization design method, involves calculating equivalent diameter of annular tube flow space, and obtaining injection parameters of different leakage loss values.
AU PANG Z
   WANG G
   ZHOU W
   YAO H
   WANG X
   LIU X
   DOU S
   LI G
   LIU K
   LI J
   WANG H
   HAN C
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   DAQING OILFIELD CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2018833111
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating equivalent diameter of an annular tube flow space. Relative roughness of a tube wall is calculated. Turbulent flow coefficient is calculated. Flow rate is calculated. Head loss is calculated when the turbulent flow is affected by the roughness of the tube wall. Converted pressure loss generated by friction resistance of a ring part in an upper turbulent state is calculated. Maximum leakage differential pressure is determined as differential pressure during injection process when pressing liquid is reaches a downhole of a well. Injection parameters of different leakage loss values are obtained.
   USE - Carbon dioxide injection-production well system optimization design method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables directing a site for timely adjustment according to real-time monitoring data, realizing strong operability, providing theory reference for field work and reducing operation cost.

TI Preparation of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid involves reacting epoxycyclohexane, magnesium powder and trimethylsilyl chloride, obtaining hydroxycyclohexanecarboxylic acid, dissolving in ethanol and hydrogenating using palladium/carbon catalyst.
AU YANG H
   WANG Y
   CUI J
   ZHANG Y
AE HUBEI JINGHONG CHEM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2018647411
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid (I) involves dissolving epoxycyclohexane (II) in an organic solvent, adding magnesium powder under stirring, cooling to less than 0 degrees C, adding trimethylsilyl chloride while introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, obtaining 2-hydroxycyclohexanecarboxylic acid (III), dissolving the compound (III) in ethanol and hydrogenating using palladium/carbon catalyst in the presence of an acid.
   USE - Preparation of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables safe, simple and convenient preparation of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclohexanecarboxylic acid of formula (I) involves dissolving epoxycyclohexane of formula (II) in an organic solvent, adding magnesium powder under stirring, cooling to less than 0 degrees C, adding trimethylsilyl chloride while introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, obtaining 2-hydroxycyclohexanecarboxylic acid of formula (III), dissolving the compound (III) in ethanol and hydrogenating using palladium/carbon catalyst in the presence of an acid.

TI Preparing dandelion compound extract useful for preparing anti-aging medicine, comprises mixing dandelion, endive root, and perillae leaves, adding borax aqueous solution, soaking and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU LIU X
   WANG X
   HUO Z
   LIU S
AE YANGZHOU POLYTECHNIC INST (YZPI-C)
GA 2018593440
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dandelion compound extract comprises (i) mixing dandelion, endive root, and perillae leaves, crushing, adding borax aqueous solution, soaking for 1-2 hours, adding 20-30% ethanol solution into refluxing solution for 5-6 hours, filtering, adjusting the filtrate pH to 5-6, and concentrating to obtain crude extract; and (ii) adding the crude extract into a extraction vessel, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and obtaining dandelion compound extract, where the raw materials includes 3-5 pts. wt. dandelion, 1-2 pts. wt. endive root, and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. perillae leaves.
   USE - The extract is useful for preparing anti-aging medicine or anti-aging health care product (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a composition, comprising the dandelion compound extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the crushing is preferably crushed to 20-40 meshes. In the step (ii), the extraction is carried out at 26-28 MPa, at 45-48 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 50-55 l/hours, the extraction time is 2-3 hours, separation kettle I temperature is 40-42 degrees C, the pressure is 8-10 MPa, the separation kettle II has temperature of 35-38 degrees C and pressure of 5-6 MPa. Preferred Components: The amount of borax aqueous solution is 2-2.5 times mass of the dandelion, endive root, and perillae leaves and the borax aqueous solution has mass concentration of 2-4%. The composition also includes other anti-aging active ingredients. The anti-aging active ingredient includes anti-aging medicine or anti-aging health care product.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

AU WANG L
   ZHOU M
   LIU D
   CAO Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201845555U
AB    NOVELTY - Polyimide gas separation membrane material i.e. 5,5'-(perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)bis(hexahydro-1H-isoindole-1,3(2H)-dione) polymer (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for separation of variety of gases e.g. separation of oxygen/nitrogen gas in air, hydrogen/nitrogen separation, separation of carbon dioxide from methane during natural gas purification (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has higher gas permeability and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyimide gas separation membrane material i.e. 5,5'-(perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)bis(hexahydro-1H-isoindole-1,3(2H)-dione) polymer of formula (I) is new.
   R = MPDSAM structure i.e. 2,4-diaminobenzenesulfonate metal complex of formula (II);
   M = Li, Na or K; and
   n = 50-1000.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) carrying out polycondensation of diamine monomer and dianhydride monomer in non-protonic polar solvent at 0-50 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain polyamic acid solution containing (II) having a solid content of 5-25%, and (ii) carrying out chemical imidization reaction using acetic anhydride as dehydrating agent and tertiary amine as catalyst for 2-24 hours to obtain product, then precipitating in alcohol or acetone medium, washing, filtering, and drying under vacuum at 100-250 degrees C to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The non-protonic polar solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone and/or dimethylsulfoxide. The dehydrating agent is acetic anhydride, the catalyst is triethylamine or pyridine, and the precipitator is methanol, ethanol or acetone. The molar ratio of diamine monomer to dianhydride monomer is 1:1, the ratio of acetic anhydride to diamine monomer in the dehydrating agent acetic anhydride is 2-9:1, the catalyst is triethylamine and/or pyridine, and the molar ratio of catalyst to diamine monomer is 2-9:1.

TI Catalyst useful for synthesizing poly(cyclohexene carbonate), comprises 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate zinc complex.
AU FAN L
   LIU Z
   QIN G
   WANG L
   SHEN Z
   CHEN J
   WANG C
   WANG Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
   UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 2018114961
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate zinc complex (I).
   USE - Zinc complex catalyst is useful for synthesizing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The zinc complex catalyst has excellent catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate zinc complex of formula (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for synthesizing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) using the catalyst, which involves adding 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate zinc complex catalyst to a high-pressure kettle, vacuumizing at 100 degrees C for 1 hour, replacing carbon dioxide several times, cooling to room temperature, adding cyclohexene oxide to the kettle, filling carbon dioxide, heating in an oil bath at a temperature of 80-130 degrees C and a pressure of 2-5 MPa, stirring for 12-36 hours, cooling to room temperature, dissolving polymer in dichloromethane, taking methanol as a precipitant, dripping dichloromethane solution of polymer into methanol, filtering, collecting solids, and vacuum drying to obtain the poly(cyclohexene carbonate).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by dissolving 6-amino-2-methylphenol in methanol, adding acetylacetone to 6-amino-2-methylphenol solution, stirring, reflux heating under nitrogen protection for 2 hours to obtain 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate ligand, adding 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate ligand to a container, adding toluene solution of diethyl zinc to ice-salt bath under the protection of nitrogen, cooling, stirring for 1-2 hours, removing ice-salt bath, stirring at normal temperature for 1 hour and carrying out reduced pressure pumping to remove toluene and unreacted diethyl zinc to obtain 6-amino-2-methylphenol-acetylacetone zinc complex catalyst. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 6-amino-2-methylphenol and acetylacetone is 2:1. The molar ratio of 6-amino-2-methylphenol acetylacetonate ligand and diethyl zinc is 1:1-1.2. The mass ratio of the zinc complex catalyst to cyclohexene oxide is 1:100-500. The amount of methanol is 2-5 times the mass of the dichloromethane solution of polymer.

The control module controls the driving module to drive the carbon dioxide collecting module to leave the air inlet and return to the initial position to stop the carbon dioxide in the collecting chamber when a second preset condition are satisfied such that the carbon dioxide in the chamber is collected to reduce the carbon dioxide concentration.

AU DREISER C
   WACHSEN O
   BRAND S
   HUNGSBERG M
   DROCHNER A
   ETZOLD B
AE CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
GA 2021A65584
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a synthesis gas involves feeding a solid and/or liquid feedstock comprising at least one carbon-containing material and a gasification agent into a gasifier and supplying external energy to obtain a product stream comprising raw synthesis gas, optionally cooling the product stream, separating the solid ingredients from the gaseous ingredients in the raw synthesis gas product stream, feeding the product stream into a water-gas shift reactor to obtain a modified synthesis gas product stream, removing and separating carbon dioxide at least partially from the modified product stream to obtain an exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide and a separated synthesis gas product stream, and at least partially recycling back the exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide into the gasifier. The gasification agent comprises 2-70 mol% carbon dioxide and 30-98 mol% of at least one gas chosen from steam, hydrogen and methane.
   USE - Production of synthesis gas used in downstream catalytic synthesis reaction for producing synthesis gas downstream product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to implement and uses readily-available apparatus, and produces synthesis gas by flexibly using different carbon-containing raw materials without the need of changes in the technical equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of a synthesis gas involves feeding a solid and/or liquid feedstock comprising at least one carbon-containing material and a gasification agent comprising at least 2 mol% carbon dioxide and optionally at least one gas selected from steam, oxygen, hydrogen, methane, and air as a feed stream into a gasifier and supplying external energy obtained from electric power to obtain a product stream comprising raw synthesis gas, removing the raw synthesis gas as product stream from the gasifier and optionally cooling the product stream, separating the solid ingredients from the gaseous ingredients in the raw synthesis gas product stream, feeding the product stream into a water-gas shift reactor in which a portion of carbon monoxide of the synthesis gas is transformed together with steam into hydrogen and carbon dioxide and obtaining a modified synthesis gas product stream, optionally removing sulfur compounds from the modified product stream by desulfurizing, removing and separating carbon dioxide at least partially from the modified product stream to obtain an exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide and a separated synthesis gas product stream, optionally further purifying the separated synthesis gas product stream, and at least partially recycling back the exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide into the gasifier. The gasification agent preferably comprises 2-70 mol% carbon dioxide and 30-98 mol% of at least one gas chosen from steam, hydrogen and methane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of a synthesis gas downstream product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The feedstock comprises at least one carbon-containing material chosen from plastic solid waste, non-recyclable waste material, and waste material from chemical industry processes, preferably plastic solid waste. The feedstock has a carbon/oxygen atomic ratio of 0-0.7, preferably 1.8-2.2. The gasification agent preferably comprises 20-60 mol% carbon dioxide, 40-80 mol% steam, and 0-1 mol% further gaseous components. The synthesis gas product stream has a hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of 1:1-2.5:1.

PT P
PN WO2021103023-A1
TI Purifying and recycling carbon dioxide from            chemical industrial waste gas comprises cooling waste            gas, pressurizing, passing into sodium bicarbonate            solution, removing particulate impurities, cooling,            condensing, and heat exchanging.
AU LI D
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202159809L
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises cooling the waste gas by tail                gas discharging chimney, using the fan to pump and                pressurize the waste gas, passing the supercharged                waste gas into a waste gas pre-cooler, cooling the                waste gas, passing the waste gas into a solution                with sodium bicarbonate, removing the particulate                impurities contained in the waste gas, absorbing a                small amount of acidic impurities in the waste gas,                cooling, passing the clean waste to an air                pre-cooler for pre-treatment, cooling, condensing                water vapor, reducing the moisture content,                cooling, using liquid ammonia to exchange heat and                cooling the heat exchanger to prepare liquid waste                gas, using a low-temperature liquid-liquid                separator, which retains the carbon dioxide in the                liquid to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, and storing                liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying and                recycling carbon dioxide from chemical industrial                waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method comprises (a) cooling the waste gas to                less than 100 degrees C by tail gas discharging                chimney, using the fan to pump and pressurize the                waste gas to 0.1-0.2 MPa, (b) passing the                supercharged waste gas into a waste gas pre-cooler,                by passing cold water at 20-35 degrees C into the                waste gas pre-cooler, and cooling the waste gas to                50-60 degrees C, (c) passing the waste gas into a                solution with 1-2% sodium bicarbonate, using a                mixer to stir the solution to fully contact the                waste gas, removing the particulate impurities                contained in the waste gas, and absorbing a small                amount of acidic impurities in the waste gas,                cooling the waste gas to 30-40 degrees C to obtain                clean waste gas, (d) using a gas compressor to                compress the waste gas to obtain waste gas with a                pressure of 2.25-2.65 MPa, (e) passing the clean                waste to an air pre-cooler for pre-treatment,                cooling, passing the pre-cooled waste gas into an                air cooler to further cool the waste gas and                condense the water vapor in the waste gas, reducing                the moisture content of the waste gas to 3-5%, and                cooling the waste gas to 1-2 degrees C, (f) passing                the waste gas into a condenser, using the liquid                ammonia output from the ice machine to exchange                heat and cooling the heat exchanger to prepare                liquid waste gas, (g) using a low-temperature                liquid-liquid separator, which retains the carbon                dioxide in the liquid to obtain liquid carbon                dioxide with a purity of 98.3-99.5%, and (h)                passing the obtained liquid carbon dioxide into the                liquid carbon dioxide storage tank through a liquid                carbon dioxide booster pump for the storage of                liquid carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  fan adopts the GPF series high temperature                  explosion-proof fan, and the inlet end of the fan                  is installed with a filter with a hole diameter                  of 1-2 mm. In the step (b) the waste gas                  pre-cooler adopts a plate-type waste gas                  pre-cooler. The outer wall of the waste gas                  pre-cooler is coated with a high temperature                  resistant coating. In the step (c), the speed of                  the mixer is 800-1200 revolutions/minute, and the                  mixing blades and mixing shaft of the mixer are                  respectively made of stainless steel. In the step                  (d), the compressor uses a reciprocating gas                  compressor, and the piston speed of the                  compressor is 540-660 revolutions/minute and the                  travel of the piston is 4.5-5.5 m. In the step                  (e), the best cooling temperature of the waste                  gas is 2 degrees C, and the humidity of the waste                  gas is 3%. In the step (f), the radiating of the                  ice machine adopts air cooling. In the step (g),                  the outer liquid of the carbon dioxide storage                  tank comprises a heat insulation cotton layer,                  and the liquid resistance of the carbon dioxide                  storage tank is not less than 4.3 MPa.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

The target block is used according to the principle that the target block has the same injection-production well working system and the carbon dioxide cycle injection volume is different. The three-dimensional geological model and digital simulation software to determine the oil increase corresponding to different carbon dioxide cycle injections in the target block, according to the increase of the oil increase corresponding to the increase of the two adjacent carbon dioxide cycle injections, determined the adjacent two incremental oil change rate under the carbon dioxide cycle injection rate by establishing the relationship between the carbon dioxide cycle injection volume and the incremental oil change rate, the optimal carbon dioxide cycle injection volume in the target block is optimized.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing stable lithium silicate nanosponges for capturing carbon dioxide, involving dissolving lithium nitrate in water to form a solution, adding aqueous ammonia solution to the solution to hydrolyze and maintain pH at 8, dispersing dendritic fibrous nanosilica (DFNS) in water by sonication, adding DFNS solution to lithium nitrate solution at room temperature with stirring, storing the solution for 24 hours without stirring, subjecting the solution to heating at 60-150 degrees C under stirring, evaporating the solvent to produce dry white powder, grinding the powder and heating at 400-950 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide is captured at a capacity of 10-33/gg and kinetics of 12-15.6/gg minute at a temperature of 500-700 degrees C. The DFNS is slowly added to lithium nitrate solution under stirring for 1 hour. The grounded powder is heated in a furnace at 650 degrees C at a rate of 10 degrees C/minute. The white powder is maintained at 650 degrees C for 6 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 2018459731
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide energy storage system (104)                comprises a storage tank configured to store a                carbon dioxide slurry including dry ice and liquid                carbon dioxide, where the storage tank stores the                slurry at the carbon dioxide triple point                temperature and pressure. A first pump is coupled                in flow communication with the storage tank, where                the first pump is configured to receive the carbon                dioxide slurry from the storage tank and to                increase a pressure of the carbon dioxide slurry to                a pressure above the carbon dioxide triple point                pressure. A contactor is coupled in flow                communication with the first pump. The contactor is                configured to receive the high pressure carbon                dioxide slurry from the pump and receive a first                flow of gaseous carbon dioxide at a pressure above                the carbon dioxide triple point pressure.
   USE - Carbon dioxide energy storage system for a                power generation system (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide energy storage system                comprises a storage tank configured to store a                carbon dioxide slurry including dry ice and liquid                carbon dioxide, where the storage tank stores the                slurry, and hence enhances the power plant                efficiency, and electricity generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a power generation system; and
   (2) a method for operating a power generation                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a power                generation system.
   Power generation system (100)
   Power generation cycle (102)
   Carbon dioxide energy storage system                (104)
   Turbine (106)
   Feed pump (108)

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a polyhydroxylamine-based polymer (I) or (II).
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent for absorbing and separating carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent has excellent carbon dioxide absorption ability, absorption rate, and reproducibility, absorbs and separates carbon dioxide with reduced overall energy consumption, in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a polyhydroxylamine-based polymer of formula (I) or (II).
   halide = halogen element; and
   n = repeating unit.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing a carbon dioxide absorbent comprising reacting polyhydroxyamine-based polymer with an amine compound, an aldehyde precursor and an ammonium halide
   (2) a method for absorbing and separating carbon dioxide
   (3) a catalyst for regenerating a carbon dioxide absorbent
   (4) a method for regenerating an amine-based carbon dioxide absorbent.

PN US2018231307-A1; US10537823-B2
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from carrier liquid in cryogenic applications, involves compressing carbon dioxide gas from carrier liquid using heat exchanger at preset conditions, where pressure-temperature curve is plotted for carbon dioxide.
AU BAXTER L
   HOEGER C
   MANSFIELD E
   STITT K
   BURT S
   DAVIS N
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   HOEGER C (HOEG-Individual)
   MANSFIELD E (MANS-Individual)
   STITT K (STIT-Individual)
   BURT S (BURT-Individual)
   DAVIS N (DAVI-Individual)
   HALL LABS LLC (HALL-Non-standard)
GA 201863420K
AB    NOVELTY - Removal (100) of carbon dioxide from carrier liquid (102) involves using heat exchanger (106) to conduct heat exchange between a carrier liquid and compressed carbon dioxide gas, conveying the carrier liquid at a temperature (T1) and a pressure (P1), heating to a temperature (T2), compressing carbon dioxide gas by raising to a pressure (P2), and cooling compressed carbon dioxide gas (120) to a temperature (T3). When a pressure-temperature curve is plotted for carbon dioxide, the temperature (T1) and the pressure (P1) are located in a liquid phase area, the temperature (T2) is shifted from the temperature (T1) to (T2), the temperature (T2) is higher than the temperature (T1), the pressure (P1) and temperature (T2) are located in a gas phase area that allows for compression from the pressure (P1) to the pressure (P2) to keep the carbon dioxide gas as a gas, the temperature (T3) is lower than the temperature (T2), and the temperature (T3) and the pressure (P2) are in a liquid phase area.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from carrier liquid in cryogenic applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently removes carbon dioxide from carrier liquid using heat exchanger by compression, avoids inflection region that occurs between a heat-temperature curve of the cooling side and heating side, of the heat exchanger, and requires less energy input to accomplish latent heats of vaporization and condensation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves providing a heat exchanger with a cooling side comprising an input port (128) and an output port (126) and a heating side comprising an input port and an output port (124), to conduct heat exchange process between the carrier liquid and compressed carbon dioxide gas, conveying the carrier liquid at a temperature (T1) and a pressure (P1) to the heating input port, where the carbon dioxide is a carbon dioxide liquid, heating the carrier liquid through the heat exchanger, causing carbon dioxide and a portion of the carrier liquid to vaporize by subjecting the carrier liquid and carbon dioxide to a temperature (T2) where the carbon dioxide vaporizes to a carbon dioxide gas, passing the carrier liquid and carbon dioxide gas through the heating output port to a feed inlet of a liquid removal vessel (108), removing a carbon dioxide depleted carrier liquid (116) from the bottom of the liquid removal vessel, compressing the carbon dioxide gas from the liquid removal vessel through compression by raising the carbon dioxide gas to a pressure (P2) to form the compressed carbon dioxide gas, sending the compressed carbon dioxide gas to the cooling input port, cooling the compressed carbon dioxide gas by the heat exchange process with the carrier liquid through the heat exchanger, and bringing the compressed carbon dioxide gas to a temperature (T3) to cool the carbon dioxide gas to form a pure carbon dioxide liquid (104). The compression is sized to allow the heat exchange process to be efficient and to avoid an inflection region that occurs between a heat-temperature curve of the cooling side and the heating side, of the heat exchanger, to remove carbon dioxide from the carrier liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the process for removing carbon dioxide from carrier liquid.
   Removal of carbon dioxide from carrier liquid (100)
   Carrier liquid (102)
   Pure carbon dioxide liquid (104)
   Heat exchanger (106)
   Liquid removal vessel (108)
   Compressor (110)
   Carbon dioxide depleted carrier liquid (116)
   Compressed carbon dioxide gas (120)
   Heating output port (124)
   Cooling outlet (126)
   Cooling inlet (128)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface of the heat exchanger and the liquid removal vessel exposed to the carrier liquid and the carbon dioxide gas, comprises aluminum, stainless steel, polymer, carbon steel, ceramic, PTFE, polychlorotrifluoroethylene, natural diamond, man-made diamond, chemical-vapor deposition diamond, and/or polycrystalline diamond. The liquid removal vessel comprises packing materials comprising stainless steel, hastelloy, titanium, nickel, copper, carbon steel, alumina, ceramics, polypropylene, polyethylene, PVC, polyvinylidene fluoride, polyfluoroethylene propylene, ethylene chlorotrifluoroethylene, PTFE, polychlorotrifluoroethylene, natural diamond, man-made diamond, chemical-vapor deposition diamond, and/or polycrystalline diamond. The surface of a compressor (110) exposed to the carbon dioxide gas comprises stainless steel, ceramics, cast aluminum, wrought aluminum, bronze, and/or graphite resin. The carbon dioxide in the carrier liquid comprises dissolved carbon dioxide, entrained carbon dioxide, and/or solid carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier liquid comprises 1,1,3-trimethylcyclopentane, 1,4-pentadiene, 1,5-hexadiene, 1-butene, 1-methyl-1-ethylcyclopentane, 1-pentene, 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, 2,3-dimethyl-1-butene, 2-chloro-1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 2-methylpentane, 3-methyl-1,4-pentadiene, 3-methyl-1-butene, 3-methyl-1-pentene, 3-methylpentane, 4-methyl-1-hexene, 4-methyl-1-pentene, 4-methylcyclopentene, 4-methyl-trans-2-pentene, bromochlorodifluoromethane, bromodifluoromethane, bromotrifluoroethylene, chlorotrifluoroethylene, cis-2-hexene, cis-1,3-pentadiene, cis-2-hexene, cis-2-pentene, dichlorodifluoromethane, difluoromethyl ether, trifluoromethyl ether, dimethyl ether, ethyl fluoride, ethyl mercaptan, hexafluoropropylene, isobutane, isobutene, isobutyl mercaptan, isopentane, isoprene, methyl isopropyl ether, methylcyclohexane, methylcyclopentane, methylcyclopropane, N,N-diethylmethylamine, octafluoropropane, pentafluoroethyl trifluorovinyl ether, propane, sec-butyl mercaptan, trans-2-pentene, trifluoromethyl trifluorovinyl ether, vinyl chloride, bromotrifluoromethane, chlorodifluoromethane, dimethylsilane, ketene, methylsilane, perchloryl fluoride, propylene, and/or vinyl fluoride.

AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2022C41005
AB    NOVELTY - A reducing agent comprises a perovskite type                crystal structure (I) and an oxygen carrier having                oxygen ion conductivity.
   USE - Reducing agent used for reducing carbon                dioxide for producing gas containing carbon                monoxide (claimed) such as greenhouse gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The reducing agent has high efficiency of                conversion of carbon dioxide into a valuable carbon                substance with high yield and high efficiency of                reduction by hydrogen, and that sufficiently cuts                downs on carbon dioxide by setting, in a perovskite                oxide, and is economically manufactured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reducing agent comprises a perovskite type                crystal structure of formula: AB'Ox(I) and an                oxygen carrier having oxygen ion                conductivity.
   x=2-4;
   A=elements containing at least one metal                element of groups (1)-(3);and
   B'=elements containing at least one metal                element differing from the elements (A).
   (i) The electronegativity (Ax) of the elements                (A), an electronegativity (Bx) of the elements                (B'), and a temperature (T) at which carbon dioxide                is brought into contact with the reducing agent,                satisfy relation: 104x [(Bx-Ax)/T] = less than 8.31                (IA). The (Ax) is less than the (Bx). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of                gas, which involves bringing the reducing agent                into contact with a raw material gas containing                carbon dioxide, and reducing the carbon dioxide to                produce a generated gas containing carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The electronegativity                  (Ax) of the elements (A), the electronegativity                  (Bx) of the elements (B'), and the temperature                  (T) satisfy relation: 104x [(Bx-Ax)/T] =                  preferably 7.47 or less. The difference between                  the electronegativity (Ax) of the element (A) and                  the electronegativity (Bx) of the element (B') is                  0.90 or less, preferably 0.75 or less. The                  electronegativity (Ax) of the element (A) is                  0.93-1.3, preferably 1-1.2. The electronegativity                  (Bx) of the element (B) is 1.40-1.88. The element                  (A) includes a metal element (e1) having an                  electronegativity of more than 1 and a metal                  element (e2) having an electronegativity of 1 or                  less. The element (B') contains at least one                  metal element having an electronegativity of 1.83                  or less, preferably 1.81 or less. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of the metal element                  (e1) with respect to the metal element (e2) is                  2.5 or less. The reducing agent comprises more                  than 90 parts mass oxygen carrier. Preferred                  Components: The element (B') is at least one of                  magnesium, scandium, chromium, manganese, iron,                  cobalt, nickel and gallium. The reducing agent is                  used to reduce the carbon dioxide by contact with                  the carbon dioxide-containing feed gas to produce                  a product gas containing carbon monoxide as the                  carbon value. The reducing agent is reduced by                  contact with a reducing gas containing hydrogen,                  where the amount of hydrogen brought into contact                  with the reducing agent is 0.01-50 mmol,                  preferably 1-50 mmol with respect to 1 g of the                  reducing agent, and the amount of carbon dioxide                  brought into contact with the reducing agent is                  0.01-50 mmol, preferably 1-50 mmol with respect                  to 1 g of the reducing agent. The reducing agent                  is used in separate reaction steps for the                  reduction reaction of carbon dioxide and the                  reduction reaction of the reducing agent.

GA 202276376S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a chimney that is provided with                an inlet and an outlet for discharging flue gas.                The outlet is arranged on the top of the chimney.                The inlet is arranged at the lower part of the                chimney. The flue is connected to the industrial                facility of the power plant, and used to receive                the flue gas from the outlet to the outside.
   USE - The system is used for effectively collecting                carbon dioxide component in flue gas of a power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively collects carbon dioxide                component in power plant flue gas. The carbon                dioxide collecting device is installed at the                downstream side of the chimney and sucked by the                blower, and the gas flow sensor is set near the                outlet side in the flue, thus increasing the amount                of smoke discharged to the carbon dioxide                collection device. The blower increases the smoke                amount discharged to carbon dioxide collector                through the flower, until the gas temperature or                gas type concentration in the sensor is changed,                and stops absorbing any gas exceeding the amount,                when the gas type changes, thus collecting carbon                dioxide in the gas while the gas is sucked in a                constant amount.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a nucleotide sequence for coding the formate                dehydrogenase comprising (a) nucleotide sequence                encoding the formate dehydrogenase, (b) a                nucleotide sequence encoding the formate                dehydrogenase comprising an amino acid sequence of                SEQ ID NOs: 1-3 (not fully defined in the                specification), and (c) nucleotide sequence                comprising a base pair sequence of SEQ ID NOs: 4-6                (not fully defined in the specification); and
   a gene expression vector for formate                dehydrogenase comprising nucleotide sequence of the                formate dehydrogenase or the nucleotide, where the                gene expression vector is pET plasmid, preferably                pETDuet plasmid, and gene expression vector is                prepared by inserting the nucleotide sequence of                the formate dehydrogenase or the nucleotide into                plasmid into the restriction enzyme cutting site of                the plasmid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The amino                  functionalized ion liquid comprises choline ion                  liquid and tetramethyl guanidine ion liquid. The                  choline ion liquid comprises choline serine,                  choline isoleucine, choline tryptophan, choline                  tyrosine, choline histidine, choline oleic acid,                  choline glutamic acid, choline, choline arginine,                  choline lysine, choline cysteine, choline valine,                  choline phenylalanine, choline leucine, choline                  alanine, choline glycine and choline dihydrogen                  phosphate ion liquid. The ionic liquid comprises choline                  proline, choline arginine, choline lysine,                  choline glutamate ion liquid and tetramethyl                  guanidine phenol salt ion liquid. The formate                  dehydrogenase is prepared by placing the gene                  engineered strain in lysogeny broth (LB) culture                  medium containing ampicillin antibiotic to                  culture, obtaining seed liquid, inoculating the                  seed liquid in LB culture medium containing                  ampicillin antibiotic to ferment and culture,                  adding inducer for inducing culture when optical                  density (OD)600 is 0.6-1, centrifuging and                  collecting thalli, crushing the cell by                  ultrasonic crushing device, centrifuging,                  filtering supernatant by filter membrane of 0.22                  m, using 5 ml Hiptin purification column to                  purify the protein on the AKTA protein                  purification device, replacing the preservation                  solution with 5 ml HiTrap Desalting column,                  obtaining the purified formate dehydrogenase,                  using the BCA protein quantitative method to                  measure concentration of the formic acid                  dehydrogenase obtained by purification, where the                  Escherichia coli strain is BL21 (DE3) strain, and                  the inoculating amount of the seed liquid is                  0.5-5%, the inducer comprises                  isopropyl-&#946;-D-thiogalactopyranoside, the                  inducing culture time is 5-30 hours, the                  temperature of the inducing culture is                  15-25&#176;C, the ultrasonic power is 40-60%,                  the centrifuging condition is 8000 revolutions                  per minute (rpm) and 4&#176;C for 20                  minutes.Preferred Composition: The mass volume                  concentration of the ion liquid the mixed                  solution is 0.0001-20 %mass%, preferably 0.01-5                  %mass. The substrate is carbon dioxide. The                  concentration of NADH in the mixed solution is                  1-5 mM. The concentration of formate                  dehydrogenase is 0.20 mg/ml. The buffer solution                  is phosphoric acid buffer solution, and pH is 7.                  The temperature of the catalytic reaction is                  25&#176;C, and the catalytic reaction time is                  1 hour. The catalytic reaction is performed under                  condition. The formate dehydrogenase comprises                  (a) an amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NOs: 1-3,                  (b) an amino acid sequence with homology of                  &#8805; 75% with the amino acid sequence of                  SEQ ID NOs: 1-3, and (c) an amino acid sequence                  in which one or several amino acid residues are                  modified, substituted, deleted or added in amino                  acid sequence of SEQ ID NOs: 1-3. The formate                  dehydrogenase comprises formic acid dehydrogenase                  derived from microorganism and its mutant. The                  microorganism comprises fungi, Paracoccusand                  Thiobacillus.Preferred Method: The method involves                  obtaining ion liquid NADH, formate dehydrogenase                  and buffer solution, and introducing carbon                  dioxide into the mixed solution for catalytic                  reaction, where the concentration of the ion                  liquid in mixed solution is 0.0001-20%, the                  concentration of NADH is 0-10 mM, the adding                  amount of formate dehydrogenase is 2.20 mg/ml,                  the addition amount of formate dehydrogenase is                  0-2.20 mg/ml, and the mixed solution is catalyzed                  and reacted at 25&#176;C under the condition                  for 0.5-5 hours.

PN KR2020118601-A; KR2249885-B1
TI Controlling properties of magnesium carbonate, comprises absorbing carbon dioxide in alkanolamine-based absorbent solution, and preparing magnesium carbonate by reacting alkanolamine-based absorbent solution.
AU PARK J W
   WOO K
   YOO Y
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 2020A2715P
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling properties of magnesium carbonate, comprises (a) absorbing carbon dioxide in an alkanolamine-based absorbent solution, and (b) preparing magnesium carbonate by reacting the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution absorbing carbon dioxide with a magnesium hydroxide solution. The properties of the prepared magnesium carbonate are kind of the alkanolamine-based absorbent. The concentration of the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution is determined according to reaction temperature and ratio of number of moles of sodium ions to the number of moles of magnesium ions present in the magnesium hydroxide solution (number of moles of (Mg2+) ions and number of moles of (Na+) ions).
   USE - The method is useful for controlling properties of magnesium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can predict and control the properties of magnesium carbonate produced without an additional post-treatment process, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing magnesium carbonate comprising (a) absorbing carbon dioxide in an alkanolamine-based absorbent solution, and (b) preparing magnesium carbonate by reacting the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution absorbing carbon dioxide with a magnesium hydroxide solution; and #recycling industrial wastewater, comprising absorbing carbon dioxide in an alkanolamine-based absorbent solution, and preparing magnesium carbonate by reacting the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution absorbing carbon dioxide with a magnesium hydroxide solution, where the magnesium hydroxide is precipitated by adjusting the pH of industrial wastewater to 7-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium hydroxide is precipitated by adjusting pH of industrial wastewater to 7-10. The alkanolamine-based absorbent is a primary amine. Types of the alkanolamine absorbent include monoethanolamine, 3-amino-1-propanol, 4-amino-1-butanol, 5-amino-1-pentanol, and/or 6-amino-1-hexanol, preferably 3-amino-1-propanol or 4-amino-1-butanol. The concentration of the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution is adjusted in 5-30 wt.%. The temperature of the reaction is controlled at 20-100 degrees C. The ratio of the number of moles of sodium ions to the number of moles of magnesium ions present in the magnesium hydroxide solution is controlled by adding sodium chloride to the magnesium hydroxide solution, and is adjusted to 0.2-1:1. The magnesium hydroxide solution contain 10-30 wt.% magnesium hydroxide concentration. The magnesium hydroxide is precipitated by adjusting the pH of industrial wastewater to 9-10. The concentration of the alkanolamine-based absorbent solution is adjusted to 13-17 wt.%. The temperature of the reaction is controlled at 65-75 degrees C. The ratio of the number of moles of magnesium ions and the number of moles of sodium ions present in the magnesium hydroxide solution is adjusted by adding Sodium chloride to the magnesium hydroxide solution, and is adjusted in the range of 0.8-1:1. The magnesium hydroxide concentration of the magnesium hydroxide solution is 10-30 wt.%. The magnesium carbonate is nesquehonite. The magnesium carbonate is nesquehonite when temperature of the reaction is 20-50 degrees C. The magnesium carbonate is hydromagnesite when temperature of the reaction is 65-100 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN110711601-A
TI New triazole-based ionic liquid catalysts useful in preparing quinazoline-2,4 (1H,3H)-dione and their derivatives by in situ conversion of carbon dioxide.
AU LIU M
   PING R
   GU Y
   ZHAO P
   LIU F
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202010851P
AB    NOVELTY - Triazole-based ionic liquid catalysts (A) are new.
   USE - (A) are useful in preparing quinazoline-2,4 (1H,3H)-dione and their derivatives by in situ conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation, mild reaction conditions and excellent catalytic performance; and produces easily separable and recyclable product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Triazole-based ionic liquid catalysts i.e. of formulae (I)-(V) (A) are new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing quinazoline-2,4 (1H,3H)-dione and their derivatives by in situ conversion of carbon dioxide comprising (i) adding triazolium ionic liquid into a reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas slowly to remove the air in the reactor, closing the reactor outlet valve and adjusting the carbon dioxide inlet valve, then filling the reactor with carbon dioxide at 0.1-0.5 MPa, and absorbing carbon dioxide at 25-50 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours, (ii) adding anthranilonitrile as substrate into the reactor, where the molar ratio between anthranilonitrile and triazolium ionic liquid is 1:4-8, (iii) heating the reaction system at 40-100 degrees C for 2-48 hours, (iv) cooling the reactor to room temperature, (v) adding distilled water into the reactor to elute ionic liquid, washing the crude product three times with methyl tert-butyl ether and vacuum drying to obtain final product with high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is (I). In step (ii), the anthranilonitrile substrate includes 2-aminobenzonitrile, 2-amino-5-chlorobenzonitrile, 2-amino-5-bromobenzonitrile, 2-amino-4-chlorobenzonitrile, 2-amino-4-methylbenzonitrile, 2-amino-5-nitrobenzonitrile and 2-amino-3-chloro-5-nitrobenzonitrile. The molar ratio between anthranilonitrile substrate and triazolium ionic liquid is 1:6. Preferred Method: In step (i), the carbon dioxide absorption temperature is 40 degrees C, and the absorption time is 1 hour. In step (i), the reaction temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical method (I) comprises reducing carbon dioxide or reacting formic acid, formaldehyde, methanol, and/or carbon monoxide with use of an electrochemical device, where the device comprises at least one cathode, and at least one anode, and at least one electrolyte between the cathode and the anode, and the electrolyte comprises at least one lanthanide and/or actinide.
   USE - The electrochemical method is useful for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is performed with no major competitive side reactions, high carbon dioxide solubility in acetonitrile and high carbon dioxide permeability and solubility of Nafion and possibility of lanthanide to participate as a metal catalyst and/or mediator; avoids or decreases passivation by reduction products such as carbon monoxide; and utilize the device and generates value added products, carbon sequestration, increases efficiency of liquid organic fuels in electrochemical devices for generation and storage, and opportunities for closed system energy generation devices.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrochemical method (II) comprising electrochemically reacting carbon dioxide, formic acid, formaldehyde, methanol, carbon monoxide, and/or any other carbon compound in the presence of at least one lanthanide and/or at least one actinide; and
   (2) the device adapted for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide or reacting formic acid, formaldehyde, methanol and/or carbon monoxide or any other one carbon compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises: reduction of carbon dioxide; and reaction of formic acid, formaldehyde, methanol and/or carbon monoxide. Preferred Components: The lanthanide and/or actinide comprises fluorosulfonate preferably trifluoromethanesulfonate. The cathode and/or anode is modified with a polymeric film. The cathode and/or anode is modified with an ionically conducting or ionically permeable film, optionally comprising a magnetic material. The cathode and anode are not magnetically modified. The cathode and/or anode is magnetically modified. The cathode and/or anode is magnetically modified with a film comprising magnetic particles and a fluorosulfonic acid polymer. The electrolyte is dissolved in a solvent system comprising as primary solvent a solvent which has a dielectric constant of at least 5. The carbon compound is a C-1 carbon compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device is part of a fuel cell, a battery, an electrosynthetic device, or an electrolyzer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113860992-A
TI Comprehensively producing methanol, compressed            natural gas and liquefied ammonia by utilizing methanol            purge gas comprises e.g. mixing methanol, nitrogen,            carbon dioxide to form mixed gas, introducing into            mixed gas compressor, compressing, reacting,            synthesizing, recycling and vacuum extracting.
AU ZHAO Y
   PENG J
   WANG H
   XIE F
   ZHANG A
   DI K
   YANG Q
AE XINXIANG GUANGSHUO CHEM CO LTD (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 202214806G
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively producing methanol, compressed                natural gas and liquefied ammonia by utilizing                methanol purge gas comprises (i) mixing methanol,                nitrogen, carbon dioxide to form a mixed gas,                introducing into the mixed gas compressor by                pipeline A, compressing, reacting hydrogen in the                methanol relaxation gas with carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide, cooling and separating to generate                methanol and unreacted gas, and collecting                methanol; (ii) introducing unreacted gas after the                methanol is separated out by the first gas-liquid                separator to the methanation preheater through the                pipeline D, preheating, and introducing the methane                gas to the city gas pipeline network for fuel use;                and (iii) introducing unreacted gas after methane                production into synthetic ammonia heat exchanger                through pipeline F, exchanging heat at                250-275degrees Celsius, and introducing into                ammonia synthesis tower through pipeline G,                synthesizing liquid ammonia, separating product,                recycling, vacuum extracting.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively                producing methanol, compressed natural gas and                liquefied ammonia by utilizing methanol purge                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate and                environmentally friendly; and saves resources and                energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensively producing methanol, compressed                natural gas and liquefied ammonia by utilizing                methanol purge gas comprises (i) mixing 60 wt.%                methanol (3.8-4.2 MPa), 25% wt.% nitrogen with a                mass purity of 99.9%, 15 wt.% carbon dioxide with a                mass purity of 89% to form a mixed gas, introducing                the mixed gas into the mixed gas compressor by                pipeline A, compressing to 15 MPa, introducing into                the methanol pre-heater by the pipeline B to                preheat to 200-210degrees Celsius, introducing into                the methanol synthesis tower through pipeline C,                reacting hydrogen in the methanol relaxation gas                with carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to generate                methanol under the catalysis of copper-based                catalyst to generate methanol, introducing into the                first gas-liquid separator through pipeline,                cooling and separating to generate methanol and                unreacted gas, and collecting methanol; (ii)                introducing unreacted gas after the methanol is                separated out by the first gas-liquid separator to                the methanation preheater through the pipeline D,                preheating to 275-285degrees Celsius, introducing                preheated gas into the methane reactor with nickel                catalyst through pipeline E, reacting CO2 and H2 at                350-370degrees Celsius to form methane and water,                cooling and separating second gas-liquid separator,                discharging effluent water, and introducing the                methane gas to the city gas pipeline network for                fuel use; and (iii) introducing unreacted gas after                methane production into the synthetic ammonia heat                exchanger through the pipeline F, exchanging heat                at 250-275degrees Celsius, and introducing into                ammonia synthesis tower through pipeline G,                synthesizing liquid ammonia with hydrogen and                nitrogen at a temperature of 350-370degrees Celsius                under the active iron catalysis of iron-based                catalysts, separating product after two-stage                cooling of water cooling and ammonia cooling,                recycling unreacted gas for use in the previous                section after hydrogen extraction, vacuum                extracting rest of the gas, introducing to the                boiler for use as fuel, so as to realize the                comprehensive production of methanol, methane and                liquid ammonia using methanol purge gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Methane reactor (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  mixing 60 wt.% methanol (4 MPa), 25% wt.%                  nitrogen with a mass purity of 99.9%, 15 wt.%                  carbon dioxide with a mass purity of 89% to form                  a mixed gas, introducing the mixed gas into the                  mixed gas compressor by pipeline A, compressing                  to 15 MPa, introducing into the methanol                  pre-heater by the pipeline B to preheat to                  205degrees Celsius, introducing into the methanol                  synthesis tower through pipeline C, reacting                  hydrogen in the methanol relaxation gas with                  carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to generate                  methanol under the catalysis of copper-based                  catalyst to generate methanol, introducing into                  the first gas-liquid separator through pipeline,                  cooling and separating to generate 17 wt.%                  methanol and unreacted gas, and collecting                  methanol; (ii) introducing unreacted gas after                  the methanol is separated out by the first                  gas-liquid separator to the methanation preheater                  through the pipeline D, preheating to 280degrees                  Celsius, introducing preheated gas into the                  methane reactor with nickel catalyst through                  pipeline E, reacting CO2 and H2 at 360degrees                  Celsius to form methane and water, cooling and                  separating second gas-liquid separator,                  discharging effluent water, and introducing the                  methane gas to the city gas pipeline network for                  fuel use; and (iii) introducing unreacted gas                  after methane production into the synthetic                  ammonia heat exchanger through the pipeline F,                  exchanging heat at 260degrees Celsius, and                  introducing into ammonia synthesis tower through                  pipeline G, synthesizing liquid ammonia with                  hydrogen and nitrogen at a temperature of                  360degrees Celsius under the active iron                  catalysis of iron-based catalysts, separating                  product after two-stage cooling of water cooling                  and ammonia cooling, recycling unreacted gas for                  use in the previous section after hydrogen                  extraction, vacuum extracting rest of the gas,                  introducing to the boiler for use as fuel, so as                  to realize the comprehensive production of                  methanol, methane and liquid ammonia using                  methanol purge gas.

PN CN113310978-A
TI Preparing smart labels for detecting sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide content change in fruit and vegetable packaging environment, involves preparing indicator solution by dissolving natural pigment followed by coating on film.
AU YAN R
   YUAN L
   XIANG H
   CHENG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 2021A2621R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing smart labels, involves preparing an indicator solution by dissolving natural pigments in distilled water and preparing a pigment attachment layer by mixing indicator solution with chitosan solution, pouring trehalose, stirring uniformly, adding starch into the mixed solution, stirring, adding glycerin dropwise, stirring, mixing, subjecting the mixed solution into a water bath, heating and stirring to prepare pigment attachment liquid. The smart label is prepared by cooling the mixed solution, uniformly coating the solution on polylactic acid (PLA) film, drying, maintaining the film at constant temperature, drying at 30 degrees C and allowing the film to stand and dry for 24 hours at room temperature to become a smart label, where the natural pigments are beet red, rose red, lac red, Llithospermum, purple cabbage, Niuhuahong or purple sweet potato, the natural pigments are used as color developing agent and biodegradable material PLA is used as base material.
   USE - Method for preparing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide sensitive smart labels, which is used as indicator label for detecting sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide content change in fruit and vegetable packaging environment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables timely and rapid detection of sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide content in fruit and vegetable packaging environment thus predicting the commodity and shelf life of the fruit and vegetable, using wide range of raw materials, and providing low-cost, compact and easy to use smart labels with full-component degradability and environmental safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing smart labels, involves preparing an indicator solution by dissolving natural pigments in distilled water at a mass ratio of 0.8-3%, and preparing a pigment attachment layer by mixing 0.5-1 vol.% indicator solution with 3 vol.% chitosan solution, pouring 1-2 vol.% trehalose, stirring uniformly, adding 2-5 vol.% starch into the mixed solution, stirring for 5-10 minutes, adding 1.5-2 vol.% glycerin dropwise, stirring, mixing, subjecting the mixed solution into a water bath medium at 80 degrees C, heating and stirring for 20-30 minutes to prepare pigment attachment liquid. The smart label is prepared by cooling the mixed solution for 10 minutes, uniformly coating the solution on polylactic acid (PLA) film with a thickness of 0.04-0.08 mm, drying at room temperature for 48 hours, maintaining the film at a constant temperature, drying at 30 degrees C and at relative humidity of 60% for 16 -24 hours and allowing the film to stand and dry for 24 hours at room temperature to become a smart label, where the natural pigments are beet red, rose red, lac red, Llithospermum, purple cabbage, Niuhuahong or purple sweet potato, the natural pigments are used as color developing agent and biodegradable material PLA is used as base material. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for utilization of smart label, which involves rapidly and timely detecting sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide content change in fruit and vegetable packaging environment; and
   (2) smart label, which comprises an upper layer of natural pigment with a thickness of 0.04-0.08 mm and a bottom PLA substrate layer, where the material of the PLA substrate layer is polybutylene adipate terephthalate or PLA modified material and the smart label is composed of components of degradable materials.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN WO2021142474-A1; AU2021205982-A1; WO2021142474-A8; CA3167046-A1; CN114981335-A; EP4087893-A1
TI Composition for atmospheric water harvesting for            e.g., heat pump, comprises porous covalent organic            framework with two- or three-dimensional topologies,            having linkage generated from combination of e.g.,            ditopic and tritopic linkers.
AU YAGHI O M
   LYLE S J
   HANIKEL N
   LYU H
   XU W
   NGUYEN H L
   LV H
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202179741S
AB    NOVELTY - A composition (I) for atmospheric water                harvesting comprises porous covalent organic                framework (COF) with two- or three-dimensional (2D                or 3D) topologies (hcb, sql, kgm, fxt, kgd, or bex                for 2D; dia, ctn, bor, pts, Ion, srs, ffc, or rra                for 3D), where crystalline framework comprises                linkage selected from imine, amide, imide,                hydrazone, azine, imidazole, benzoxazole, beta                -ketoenamine, and olefin, generated from a                combination of greater than or equal to 2 different                linkers selected from ditopic linkers, tritopic                linkers, tetratopic linkers, hexatopic linkers, and                octatopic linkers.
   USE - A composition for atmospheric water harvesting                for device, such as atmospheric water harvester,                heat pump, dehumidifier, adsorption refrigerator,                and solar cooling system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition enables development of such                materials as an energy- and cost-effective solution                for capturing carbon dioxide from air and from                post-combustion gas mixtures, and provides                solutions for integrative capture of carbon dioxide                and water at the same time where water is in                substantial shortage as well. The composition                provides increased water uptake capacity under low                and medium relative humidity levels, while                simultaneously increasing water uptake rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) making the composition (I) which involves                condensing different linkers to form the                crystalline framework;
   (2) using the composition (I) which involves                contacting the compositing with air under                conditions, where composition (I) adsorbs water                from air, preferably where air has a relative                humidity of 20-40%;
   (3) composition (II) comprising chemically and                thermally stable COF material configured and                operative as a solid adsorbent for capturing carbon                dioxide from air or a post-combustion exhaust gas                mixture, with all possible topologies (e.g.,                layered topologies of sql, hcb, hxl, kgm, bex, kgd,                tth or mtf and 3D topologies of dia, Ion, pcu, srs,                pto, pts, tbo, bor, cnt or dia-);
   (4) system for capturing carbon dioxide from                air or a post-combustion exhaust gas mixture                comprising a matrix, such as a sorption bed                containing the composition (II);
   (5) use of composition (II) as a solid                adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, and                optionally water, from air, or a post-combustion                exhaust gas mixture;
   (6) composition (III) comprising charged,                water-stable COF material configured and operative                for water sorption or harvesting from a gas, such                as air or an exhaust, and comprising a cationic or                anionic backbone forming pores, and organic or                metallic counter ions inside the pores;
   (7) system for enhanced water harvesting or                for capturing water from a gas, such as air or an                exhaust, containing the composition (III)                configured as a solid adsorbent for capturing the                water from the gas, where the composition (III)                provides increased water uptake capacity under low                and medium relative humidity levels, while                simultaneously increasing water uptake rate;                and
   (8) use of composition (III) as a solid                adsorbent for capturing water from a gas, such as                air an exhaust, where the composition (III)                provides increased water uptake capacity under low                and medium relative humidity levels, while                simultaneously increasing water uptake rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Combination is                  tetratopic and tritopic linkers. Linkage is imine                  (-CH=N-). Composition is constructed from                  tetratopic 1,1,2,2-tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)ethene                  (ETTA) and tritopic 1,3,5-triformylbenzene (TFB),                  termed COF-432 (((ETTA)3(TFB)4)imne), which                  exhibits the mtf topology. Composition (II) is                  contained in a matrix configured as a sorption                  bed, fluidized bed, coated heat exchanger, or                  membrane, in a fluid flow path configured to pass                  the air or mixture over, around, and/or through                  the matrix. Composition comprises air or                  post-combustion exhaust gas mixture, where water                  is present in the air or mixture, and the                  material is configured and is operative to                  harvest the water from the air or mixture, and                  provides facile collection of water as a second                  value-delivering function. The COF is selected                  from COF-366-F-CoCOF-316, COF-3I6-CONH2,                  COF-316-C(NOH)NH2, and COF-701.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
