PN CN111500815-A; CN111500815-B; WO2021237946-A1; US2022325368-A1
TI Dynamic controlling of bottom blowing            oxygen-carbon dioxide-calcium oxide converter steel            making process comprises e.g. dividing converter            blowing process into earlier stage, middle stage and            later stage according to decarburization rate.
AU ZHU R
   LI W
   WEI G
   DONG K
   FENG C
   LIU R
   ZHOU Y
   HAN B
   WU X
   RUAN Q
   WANG D
   ZHU Q
   HAN Z
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
   UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2020771946
AB    NOVELTY - Dynamic controlling of bottom blowing                oxygen-carbon dioxide-calcium oxide (O2-CO2-CaO)                converter steel making process comprises e.g.                dividing bottom-blowing (O2-CO2-CaO) converter                blowing process into three stages as follows early                stage, middle stage and late stage according to                decarburization rate vC, the data calculation                module is based on the total charges into the                furnace m total, the molten iron temperature T0-1                into the furnace, the carbon content (%C)0-1, the                silicon content (%Si)0-1, the scrap ratio is gamma                , and the carbon content in the scrap steel                (%C)0-2, and the silicon content is (%Si)0-2,                target carbon content (%C)f and target temperature                Tf parameters calculate the blowing oxygen                consumption, carbon dioxide ratio and lime powder                injection amount, according to the feed structure,                the heating rate of the molten pool vT and                decarburization rate vC, and the central control                system is for formulating blowing operation process                in the early stage of the blowing.
   USE - The method is useful for dynamic controlling                of bottom blowing (O2-CO2-CaO) converter steel                making process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method strengthens mixing effect of the                converter bath, promotes balance of the slag-gold                reaction, reduces carbon content and phosphorus                content of the molten steel, avoids over-oxidation                at the end of converter blowing, reduces                consumption of deoxidized alloy, reduces number of                inclusions in steel, improves quality of molten                steel, increases metal yield, reduces production                costs, and shortens smelting cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dynamic controlling of bottom blowing                (O2-CO2-CaO) converter steel making process                comprises dividing bottom-blowing (O2-CO2-CaO)                converter blowing process into three stages as                follows early stage, middle stage and late stage                according to decarburization rate vC, the data                calculation module is based on the total charges                into the furnace m total, the molten iron                temperature T0-1 into the furnace, the carbon                content (%C)0-1, the silicon content (%Si)0-1, the                scrap ratio is gamma , and the carbon content in                the scrap steel (%C)0-2, and the silicon content is                (%Si)0-2, target carbon content (%C)f and target                temperature Tf parameters calculate the blowing                oxygen consumption, carbon dioxide ratio and lime                powder injection amount, according to the feed                structure, the heating rate of the molten pool vT                and the decarburization rate vC, the central                control system is for formulating the blowing                operation process in the early stage of the                blowing, in the blowing process, calculating the                decarburization rate vC by the decarburization rate                calculation module, determining the start time node                of the middle and late stages of blowing, the CO2                calculation module calculates carbon dioxide mixing                ratio through the bottom blowing thermal point                temperature calculation model and dephosphorization                model, formulating the middle and late stages of                the blowing operation process, reducing the                temperature of the thermal point, strengthening the                stirring of the molten pool, and promoting the                reaction balance of the molten pool slag                metal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows dynamic control logic block                diagram of method for dynamic controlling of bottom                blowing (O2-CO2-CaO) converter steel making process                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the bottom-blowing                  (O2-CO2-CaO) converter steelmaking process, the                  bottom blowing gun is concentric ring seam tube,                  the center tube uses oxygen + carbon dioxide                  mixed gas as the carrier gas, directly blowing                  the lime powder into the molten pool from the                  bottom of the converter, spraying the ring seam                  with cooling protection gas, the gas is methane,                  carbon dioxide, nitrogen, argon, combining the                  furnace charges composition and flue gas                  composition, predicting the composition and                  temperature of the molten steel during the                  blowing process, according to the target steel                  composition and temperature requirements,                  calculating the amount of carbon dioxide mixed                  with the bottom blowing thermal point temperature                  calculation model and dephosphorization model,                  based on the decarburization rate of the molten                  pool, and dynamically adjusting the proportion of                  carbon dioxide in the bottom blowing gas in                  stages. In the method, the control step is as                  follows: according to the total charges m total,                  silicon content (%Si) and alkalinity R                  requirements, calculating the powder calculation                  module of the lime powder injection speed                  represented by formula as given in specification,                  calculating and adjusting the lime powder                  injection speed through the formula, according to                  the working feedback parameters of the top and                  bottom blowing equipment, calculating the                  converter gas instantaneous production So-gas,                  according to the data of the furnace gas                  composition, calculating the change of the                  decarburization rate in the converter blowing                  process, determining the blowing stage of the                  converter and corresponding molten steel                  composition, the instantaneous contents of carbon                  dioxide, carbon monoxide, oxygen, and hydrogen in                  the furnace gas is P0-CO2, P0-CO, P0-O2, P0-H2,                  respectively, the top blowing oxygen flow rate is                  QU-O2, the bottom blowing center gas is oxygen +                  carbon dioxide mixed gas, the bottom blowing ring                  seam is methane, the bottom blowing total flow                  rate, the ratio of carbon dioxide and methane is                  Qb, epsilon b-carbon dioxide, epsilon b-methane                  respectively, according to the formula 2Qb(                  epsilon b-CH4) = Sogasx Po-H2 and bottom blowing                  working parameters, calculating and approving the                  furnace gas flow Sogas, calculating the                  decarburization rate by the decarburization rate                  calculation module represented by formula as                  given in specification, and calculating                  decarburization rate in the converter blowing                  process by the formula. The method specifically                  comprises (i) obtaining the charges structure of                  the converter through the data acquisition                  system, transmitting key component data and                  target parameters to the data calculation module,                  formulating through the central control system,                  controlling the operation process of the                  converter in the early stage, (ii) in the early                  stage of blowing, according to the operation                  process established in step (i), lowering the top                  blowing oxygen lance into the furnace to blow                  oxygen, the center tube of the bottom blowing                  lance uses oxygen + carbon dioxide mixture as the                  carrier gas to blow lime powder into the molten                  pool, the bottom blowing lance blows methane into                  the annular seam as protective gas, the                  decarburization rate is represented by formula as                  given in specification from the flue gas                  composition data and decarburization rate                  calculation module, determining the end node of                  the early stage of blowing, (iii) in the middle                  of converting, determining the start time of the                  middle of converting through the decarburization                  rate is represented by formula as given in                  specification, and formulating the mid-term                  operation plan, (iv) in the late stage of                  blowing, determining the starting node of the                  late stage of blowing through the decarburization                  speed is represented by formula as given in                  specification, according to the heating rate vT,                  using the carbon dioxide calculation module for                  calculating the amount of carbon dioxide mixed in                  the later stage, and formulating the later                  operation plan, (v) at the end of blowing,                  determining the time node for the end of blowing                  according to the decarburization rate is                  represented by formula as given in specification,                  and (vi) switching the bottom blowing center hole                  gas to argon, and the flow rate is 2500-18400                  Nm3/hours, switching the bottom-blowing ring seam                  gas to argon, the flow rate is 200-1790                  Nm3/hours, reducing the stirring of the molten                  pool, accelerating the separation of slag and                  iron, and taping the furnace. In the step (ii),                  the specific operation scheme is as follows: the                  bottom lime powder injection speed is 300-900                  kg/minutes, the flow rate of top blowing oxygen                  is 10000-63000 Nm3/hours, the total flow of                  oxygen + carbon dioxide in the bottom blowing                  center hole is 3000-18900 Nm3/hours, the ratio of                  carbon dioxide is 0-100%, the flow rate of                  methane in the bottom-blowing ring seam is                  300-1890 Nm3/hours, and the end time of blowing                  is 3-6 minutes. In the step (iii), the specific                  operation plan in the middle of the blowing                  process is as follows: the bottom lime powder                  injection speed is 300-900 kg/minutes, the powder                  injection is stopped for 8-10 minutes, the flow                  rate of top blowing oxygen is 9000-62000                  Nm3/hours, the oxygen flow rate of the bottom                  blowing center hole is 3000-18900 Nm3/hours, the                  carbon dioxide ratio is 0, the bottom blowing                  ring seam methane flow rate is 300-1890                  Nm3/hours, and the middle blowing end time is                  9-13 minutes. In the step (iv), the specific                  operation scheme in the later stage of blowing is                  as follows: the bottom lime powder injection                  speed is 0 kg/minutes, the top blowing oxygen                  flow rate is 9000-62000 Nm3/hours, the total flow                  of oxygen + carbon dioxide in the bottom blowing                  center hole is 3000-18900 Nm3/hours, the carbon                  dioxide ratio is 50-100%, the methane flow rate                  of the bottom blowing ring seam is 300-1890                  Nm3/hours, and the end of blowing period is 13-18                  minutes.

PN CN210495844-U; CN111841271-A
TI Flue gas desulfurization and decarbonization denitration collaborative processing system comprises desulfurization system, carbon monoxide treatment system, and denitration system, where flue gas to be processed is connected to flue gas inlet.
AU YE H
   WEI J
   LIU C
   KANG J
AE ZHONGYE CHANGTIAN INT ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 202043099Q
AB    (CN210495844-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a flue gas desulphurization/decarburization denitration collaborative processing system, after desulfurization by using carbon monoxide in the flue gas, the carbon monoxide in the smoke after desulphurization into carbon dioxide, the heat released by the process directly for temperature rising and the flue gas after desulphurization, reduces and even save process fuel heated by external heating of the flue gas. The utility model makes full use of the carbon monoxide in the flue gas, using carbon monoxide conversion heat in the carbon dioxide process discharged reaches the heating-up temperature to facilitate the flue gas denitration treatment, even to eliminate the application of fuel saving, at the same time, processing the carbon monoxide and aldehydes in flue gas, reduce the pollution of flue gas to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a flue gas desulfurization and denitrification system utilizing carbon monoxide co-processing or a system used in the flue gas desulfurization and denitrification, which comprises desulfurization system, carbon monoxide treatment system and the denitration system, where flue gas to be treated is connected to the flue gas inlet of the desulfurization system through the original flue gas transmission pipeline (L0), and flue gas outlet is connected to the flue gas inlet of the carbon monoxide treatment system through the first delivery pipe (L1), and the flue gas outlet of the carbon monoxide treatment system is connected to the denitration system through the second delivery pipe (L2), flue gas outlet of the denitration system is connected to the net flue gas conveying pipe (L3), and carbon monoxide treatment system is a reaction system that converts carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide.
EA    NOVELTY - Performing flue gas desulfurization and denitrification using carbon monoxide co-processing, involves delivering flue gas G0 to be treated to the desulfurization system, removing sulfur oxides in the flue gas G0 to obtain desulfurized flue gas G1. The desulfurized flue gas G1 is transported to the carbon monoxide treatment system, the carbon monoxide in the desulfurized flue gas G1 reacts in the carbon monoxide treatment system and the carbon monoxide and oxygen are converted into carbon dioxide while releasing heat and flue gas G1 becomes nitrogen-containing flue gas G2. The nitrate-containing flue gas G2 is passed through the denitration system for denitration treatment. The nitrogen oxides in the nitrate-containing flue gas G2 are removed after the denitration system and the flue gas after the denitration system is clean smoke gas.
   USE - Method for performing flue gas desulfurization and denitrification using carbon monoxide co-processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform flue gas desulfurization and denitrification using carbon monoxide co-processing, which makes full use of the carbon monoxide in the flue gas, and utilizes heat released during the conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide to reach the purpose of raising the flue gas temperature to facilitate denitration treatment, eliminates use of fuel, and treats the carbon monoxide in the flue gas, and reduces flue gas's environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The oxygen-containing gas is added to the carbon monoxide treatment system, preferably air or oxygen-enriched gas, replenished fuel to the carbon monoxide treatment system or the flue gas G1 after desulfurization, preferably gas fuel. The flue gas G1 after desulfurization passes through the first heat exchange system before being transported to the carbon monoxide treatment, nitrate-containing flue gas G2 passes through the second heat exchange system and then sent to the denitration system. The flow rate of flue gas G1 is detected after desulfurization per unit time, marked as U1Nm3/hour, temperature of flue gas G1 is detected after desulfurization, marked as T1 degrees C. The CO content is detected in flue gas G1 after desulfurization and mark it as P1g/Nm3, mass flow of carbon monoxide in flue gas G1 after desulfurization per unit time is U1asteriskP1g/hour. The heat Q1kJ/hour released by the combustion of carbon monoxide in the flue gas G1 after desulfurization per unit time. The carbon monoxide is calculated in the flue gas G1 after desulfurization. The carbon monoxide treatment system is converted into carbon dioxide. The optimal denitration temperature of the denitration system is set to T degrees C denitration, if T2=T denitration, the carbon monoxide in the desulfurized flue gas G1 is burned in the carbon monoxide treatment system and the heat released makes the nitrate-containing flue gas G2 entering the denitration system and the flue gas is directly subjected to denitration treatment in the denitration system, if T2 not less than T denitrification, fuel is added to the carbon monoxide treatment system or the flue gas G1 after desulfurization, or heated through the first heat exchange system and/or the second heat exchange system to enter the denitrification, if T2 not greater than T denitration, the first heat exchange system and/or the second heat exchange system reduce the temperature of the flue gas before entering the denitrification system so that the nitrate-containing flue gas entering the denitration system G2 is reduced to T degrees C. The supplementary fuel in the treatment system or the flue gas G1 after desulfurization includes setting combustion heat of the fuel to N1kJ/g, and calculated the mass flow rate U2kg/h of the supplementary fuel. The content of O2 in the flue gas G1 after desulfurization is detected, and the content is marked as P2g/Nm3.

PN CN110885981-A
TI Magnetic molybdate intercalation zinc aluminum cerium hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor comprises e.g. magnetic corrosion inhibitor composites with iron(II,III) oxide as core and layered double hydroxides of molybdate intercalation as shell.
AU WANG J
   LIU X
   HU W
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202022416A
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic molybdate intercalation zinc aluminum cerium hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor comprises e.g. magnetic corrosion inhibitor composites with iron(II,III) oxide as core and layered double hydroxides (LDH) of molybdate intercalation as shell as follows: (1) uniformly dispersing ferric oxide in a mixed solution of methanol and water to obtain a uniform suspension, and adding sodium molybdate dihydrate to suspension and dissolving and dispersing in suspension, where n(MoO42-)/n(Fe3O4) = (0.5-3.0):1, in the process of adding ferric oxide and sodium molybdate dihydrate, maintaining inert protective gas into mixed solution for avoiding influence of carbon dioxide and oxygen, the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-10:1-5, (2) dissolving and dispersing zinc nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, and cerium(III) nitrate nonahydrate in a mixed solution of methanol and water to obtain a mixed salt solution.
   USE - Used as magnetic molybdate intercalation zinc aluminum cerium hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor: improves corrosion resistance, service life of carbon steel in seawater circulating cooling system, realizes recycling performance of corrosion inhibitor composites, and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Magnetic molybdate intercalation zinc aluminum cerium hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor comprises magnetic corrosion inhibitor composites with iron(II,III) oxide as core and layered double hydroxides (LDH) of molybdate intercalation as shell as follows: (1) uniformly dispersing ferric oxide in a mixed solution of methanol and water to obtain a uniform suspension, and adding sodium molybdate dihydrate to suspension and dissolving and dispersing in suspension, where n(MoO42-)/n(Fe3O4) = (0.5-3.0):1, in the process of adding ferric oxide and sodium molybdate dihydrate, maintaining inert protective gas into mixed solution for avoiding influence of carbon dioxide and oxygen, the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-10:1-5, (2) dissolving and dispersing zinc nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, and cerium(III) nitrate nonahydrate in a mixed solution of methanol and water to obtain a mixed salt solution, where n(Zn2+)/n(Fe3O4) = 0.25-100:1, n(Al3+)/n(Ce3+) = 3-50:1, n(Zn2+)/n((Al3+) + (Ce3+)) = 1-5:1, during process of adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, and cerium(III) nitrate nonahydrate, maintaining inert protective gas into mixed solution, avoiding effects of carbon dioxide and oxygen, the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-10:1-5, (3) dissolving and dispersing sodium hydroxide in a mixed solution of methanol and water to obtain an lye, in the process of adding sodium hydroxide, maintaining inert protective gas into the mixed solution for avoiding influence of carbon dioxide and oxygen, the volume ratio of methanol and water is 1-10:1-5, (4) adding obtained mixed salt solution and obtained lye to obtained suspension, during dropping process, maintaining inert protective gas flowing into mixed solution for avoiding carbon dioxide and the effect of oxygen and maintain mechanical stirring for dispersion, the pH of solution after the dropwise addition is 8-11, and (5) transferring obtained reaction slurry to high-pressure reaction kettle, and the high-pressure reaction kettle is aging in drying box at 60-120 degrees C to obtain black product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing magnetic molybdate intercalation zinc aluminum cerium hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), n(MoO42-)/n(Fe3O4) = 1-3:1, the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-5:1, and stirring or performing sonication at 20-60 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. In the step (2), n(Zn2+)/n(Fe3O4) = 10-50:1, n(Al3+)/n(Ce3+) = 10-30:1, n(Zn2+)/n((Al3+) + (Ce3+)) = 3-5:1, the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-5:1, stirring or performing sonication at 20-60 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. In the step (3), the volume ratio of methanol to water is 1-5:1, at 20-60 degrees C, dissolving and dispersing with stirring or ultrasound for 30-60 minutes. In the step (4), using high-speed mechanical stirring at 300-500 revolutions/minutes, adopting uniform speed dropwise addition, controlling dropwise addition time to 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-3 hours, the volume ratio of the obtained suspension, mixed salt solution and lye is 5-6:3-4:4-5, and controlling pH of solution after dropwise addition is 8-11, preferably 8-10. In the step (5), the aging temperature is 80-110 degrees C, aging time is 10-50 hours, preferably 20-40 hours, separating aged product using permanent magnet of iron-boron, washing repeatedly with ethanol and de-ionized water until neutral, and drying in drying box at 60-80 degrees C for 20-24 hours.

TI Carbon dioxide capture system for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture has packed scrubbing vessel including gas-liquid contactor unit having contactor network of flow diversion barriers with flow voids for movement of process fluids.
AU PANACCIONE C
   MEULEMAN E E B
   STAAB G A
   BROWN N R
AE ION ENG LLC (IONE-Non-standard)
   ION ENG LLC (IONE-Non-standard)
   ION ENG LLC (IONE-Non-standard)
   ION CLEAN ENERGY INC (IONC-Non-standard)
   ION CLEAN ENERGY INC (IONC-Non-standard)
GA 2018583480
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capture system comprises packed scrubbing vessel including gas inlet to receive feed stream of gas mixture to vessel with carbon dioxide for removal in internal volume of vessel; gas outlet to output treated stream from internal volume of vessel having lower carbon dioxide concentration than feed stream of gas mixture to scrubbing unit; liquid inlet to receive feed stream of scrubbing solution to contact gas mixture to remove carbon dioxide from gas mixture; liquid outlet to output effluent stream of rich scrubbing solution; gas-liquid contactor unit (200) in internal volume along a flow axis between gas inlet and gas outlet and between liquid inlet and liquid outlet and including contactor network of flow diversion barriers with flow voids for movement of process fluids including gas mixture and scrubbing solution between flow diversion barriers; and greater than or equal to 1 heat exchange feed channel (112a-112c) to deliver feed of heat exchange cooling fluid to heat exchange channels.
   USE - A carbon dioxide capture system for capturing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The provision of greater than or equal to 1 heat exchange feed channel that connects with a set of heat exchange channels at multiple feed locations spaced along the flow axis advantageously facilitates the cooling heat exchange with the process fluids along the flow axis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The flow axis (AA) extends in a direction along scrubbing vessel from gas inlet to gas outlet. The contactor unit has a set of heat exchange channels (114a-114c, 124a-124c) in flow diversion barriers to transport heat exchange cooling fluid through contactor network to cool process fluids moving through flow voids during carbon dioxide scrubbing operation. The heat exchange feed channel extends in a direction of flow axis and is fluidly connected with heat exchange channels at multiple feed locations spaced along the flow axis to input the feed of the heat exchange cooling fluid into multiple different locations in the contactor network along the flow axis. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for treating carbon dioxide-containing gas to remove carbon dioxide from the gas for capture in an amine-based scrubbing solution, which involves inputting feed stream of 1st process fluid into interior volume of process vessel, where 1st process fluid includes 1st fluid phase that is a gas phase with carbon dioxide to be transferred to 2nd fluid phase in the internal volume, where 2nd fluid phase is a liquid phase of amine-based carbon dioxide scrubbing solution, contacting process fluids including the 1st fluid phase moving through the flow voids with the flow diversion barriers and transferring a portion of the carbon dioxide from the 1st fluid phase to the 2nd fluid phase, during the contacting, heating or cooling the process fluids flowing through a portion of the flow voids by providing feed stream of heat exchange fluid to the contactor unit, delivering a portion of the heat exchange fluid from the feed stream of the heat exchange fluid as the feed of the heat exchange fluid to the heat exchange feed channel and from the heat exchange feed channel through the multiple feed locations into the heat exchange channels, and removing from the contactor unit an effluent stream of the heat exchange fluid including an effluent of the heat exchange fluid from the heat exchange channels, and outputting a process effluent stream from the interior volume of the vessel, the process effluent stream including the 2nd fluid phase including captured carbon dioxide transferred from the 1st fluid phase.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the contactor unit comprising a set of interconnectable contact modules.
   Heat exchange feed channel (112a-112c)
   Heat exchange channels (114a-114c, 124a-124c)
   Heat exchange bypass channel (128a-128c)
   Gas-liquid contactor unit (200)
   Flow axis (AA)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particles of the filler comprise a metallic material. The particles of the filler comprise carbon fibers with an aspect ratio of greater than or equal to 10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Amine-based scrubbing solution comprises greater than or equal to 1 amine compound; and feed stream of scrubbing solution is an aqueous solution and comprises 10-50 wt.% amine compound. Amine compound comprises monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, N-methylethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, aminoethoxyethanol (diglycolamine), 2-amino-2-methylpropanol, methyl diethanolamine, benzylamine, substituted benzylamine, and/or piperazine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The walls of a portion of the heat exchange channels in the flow diversion barriers are constructed of a material of construction; and the material of construction is a filled polymeric material comprising a polymeric matrix and particles of filler dispersed by the polymeric matrix, where the filler has a higher thermal conductivity than the polymeric matrix.

PN DE102020004630-A1; WO2022022849-A1
TI Producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide electrolysis using electrolysis device comprising anode space with anode, cathode space separated from anode space by ion-conducting membrane, and gas space.
AU HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2022175220
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide electrolysis using an electrolysis device comprising an anode space (10) with an anode, a cathode space (20) separated from anode space by an ion-conducting membrane, and a gas space (30) delimited from cathode space by a gas diffusion electrode. The anode compartment is operated at an anode compartment pressure, cathode compartment is operated at a cathode compartment pressure, and gas compartment is operated at a gas compartment pressure. The anode compartment contains a liquid anolyte, cathode compartment a liquid catholyte (4) and gaseous carbon dioxide (2) supplied into gas space. A first portion of carbon dioxide is converted into carbon monoxide at gas diffusion electrode, and a second part of carbon dioxide supplied into gas space passes through gas diffusion electrode into catholyte, at least partially discharged with catholyte from cathode space, removed from it, and in is returned to gas space.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide electrolysis using an electrolysis device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide electrolysis using an electrolysis device (100) comprising an anode space (10) with an anode, a cathode space (20) separated from anode space by an ion-conducting membrane, and a gas space (30) delimited from cathode space by a gas diffusion electrode. The anode compartment is operated at an anode compartment pressure, cathode compartment is operated at a cathode compartment pressure, and gas compartment is operated at a gas compartment pressure. The anode compartment contains a liquid anolyte, cathode compartment a liquid catholyte (4) and gaseous carbon dioxide (2) supplied into gas space. A first portion of carbon dioxide is converted into carbon monoxide at gas diffusion electrode, and a second part of carbon dioxide supplied into gas space passes through gas diffusion electrode into catholyte, at least partially discharged with catholyte from cathode space, removed from it, and in is returned to gas space. The gas space pressure is below 100 kPa to above 10 kPa cathode space pressure and carbon dioxide is at least partially removed from catholyte and at least partially returned to gas space without mechanical compression. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for the production of carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide electrolysis, comprising an electrolysis device having an anode compartment with an anode, a cathode compartment which is delimited from the anode compartment by an ion-conducting membrane, and a gas compartment which is delimited from the cathode space by a gas diffusion electrode, the anode compartment is set up for operation at an anode compartment pressure, the cathode compartment is set up for operation at cathode compartment pressure, and the gas compartment is set up for operation at gas compartment pressure, means are provided which are set up to feed a liquid anolyte to the anode compartment, a liquid catholyte to the cathode compartment and gaseous carbon dioxide to the gas compartment, the electrolysis device is set up to convert a first portion of the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide at the gas diffusion electrode, and the electrolysis device is also set up to allow a second part of the carbon dioxide supplied to the gas space to pass through the gas diffusion electrode into the catholyte, at least partially with the catholyte from the discharge the cathode space, remove it and return it to the gas space, means are provided which are set up to operate the electrolysis device in such a way that the gas space pressure is 100 kPa below and 10 kPa above the cathode space pressure, the electrolysis device is set up to carry out the at least partial removal of the carbon dioxide in the catholyte and its at least partial recycling into the gas space without mechanical compression.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the plant.
   Gaseous carbon dioxide (2)
   Liquid catholyte (4)
   Anode space (10)
   Cathode space (20)
   Gas space (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anolyte contains at least water. The catholyte contains at least water and/or an aqueous alkali including lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, strontium carbonate, lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, strontium hydroxide and ammonia.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The headspace pressure is below 100 kPa to below 5 kPa of the cathode compartment pressure; or below 10 kPa to above 10 kPa of the cathode compartment pressure. The anode compartment pressure is 6900 or 7000 kPa below to 10 kPa above the cathode compartment pressure. A variable characterizing the anode chamber pressure and a variable characterizing the cathode chamber pressure and/or the gas chamber pressure are recorded as input variables, the anode chamber pressure being changed by setting the control valve. The hydrogen is formed at the gas diffusion electrode and is discharged from the gas space together with the carbon monoxide as a raw gas. The headspace pressure is set to 110 kPa to 7 MPa. A non-return valve is provided for the at least partial recycling of the carbon dioxide from the catholyte into the gas space. Preferred Method: The anolyte in the anode compartment is subjected to electrolysis with molecular oxygen, which is at least partially discharged with the anolyte from the anode compartment, removed from the anolyte and as anode gas is withdrawn from the process via a control valve. The carbon dioxide electrolysis comprises low-temperature co-electrolysis, low-temperature electrolysis, low-pressure electrolysis and/or high-pressure electrolysis.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a natural gas processing                plant (3) that is provided with an acidic gas                removal facility for separating carbon dioxide                contained in natural gas and producing liquefied                natural gas. The high-level energy of                high-temperature and high-pressure carbon dioxide                fluid of the carbon dioxide cycle (2) is converted                into electrical energy or mechanical energy, and                the energy is supplied to power-consuming equipment                and energy-consuming equipment provided in the                natural gas processing plant. PT P
PN US2021061706-A1; US11034619-B2
TI Producing captured carbon dioxide involves reacting calcium carbonate with sulfur dioxide solution to produce solid calcium sulfite and gaseous carbon dioxide, and thermally decomposing calcium sulfite to produce gaseous sulfur dioxide.
AU NOVEK E J
AE NOVEK E J (NOVE-Individual)
   INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2021248360
AB    NOVELTY - Producing captured carbon dioxide involves reacting calcium carbonate with sulfur dioxide solution to produce solid calcium sulfite and gaseous carbon dioxide, thermally decomposing calcium sulfite to produce gaseous sulfur dioxide, and recovering gaseous sulfur dioxide by dissolving in solution. The gaseous carbon dioxide is captured. The reaction of calcium carbonate with sulfur dioxide solution is conducted under conditions to substantially prevent formation of sulfate salts.
   USE - Method for producing captured carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve seals and flow control, enhances reaction rate and oil recovery, and increases reaction yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The thermal decomposing is conducted under conditions to further produce cement and calcium oxide, and in mixture with calcium carbonate. The thermal decomposing is conducted in mixture comprising clay, silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, iron oxide, iron carbonate, magnesium carbonate, magnesium oxide, silicate, aluminate, shale, sand, fly ash, ash, slag and sulfur oxide. The carbonaceous fuel is combusted, and combusted sulfurous fuel. The sulfur dioxide is provided in second combustion to makeup for sulfur dioxide loss in process. The mixture comprising sulfurous fuel and nitrogenous fuel is combusted. The sulfurous fuel is combusted with diatomic oxygen-lean gas and to form diatomic oxygen-ultra-lean gas. The portion of gaseous sulfur dioxide resulting from thermal decomposing is recovered. The sulfur dioxide is adsorbed in wash. The sulfur dioxide is condensed. The portion of gaseous sulfur dioxide resulting from thermal decomposing is recovered by absorption of gaseous sulfur dioxide into sulfur dioxide-lean solution to produce sulfur dioxide-rich solution.Preferred Conditions: The low oxygen environment comprises concentration of gaseous diatomic oxygen of 20000 parts per million. The combustion gas is originated from combustion of fuel-rich mixture comprising ratio of fuel to oxidant which is equal to or greater than stoichiometric ratio of fuel to oxidant for complete combustion. The second combustion reduces diatomic oxygen concentration. The thermal decomposing further produces other gases. The sulfur dioxide reacted with calcium carbonate is in form of sulfur dioxide-rich solution. The reaction with calcium carbonate further produces sulfur dioxide-lean solution. The conditions to substantially prevent formation of sulfate salts in reaction of calcium carbonate with sulfur dioxide solution comprises gaseous diatomic oxygen concentration of 21 vol.%.Preferred Components: The hot gas comprises combustion gas. The combustion gas comprises carbonaceous fuel, sulfurous fuel, nitrogenous fuel or hydrogen fuel. The calcium carbonate comprises limestone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-X

TI Method for removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from gas, involves regenerating rich liquid absorbing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide by air oxidation and dissolving sulfur in solvent.
AU YANG J
   YU J
   ZHU Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   NCIG RES INST (NCIG-Non-standard)
GA 201929565T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dissolving organic solvent absorption liquid containing a polyvalent metal complex containing sulfur and carbon dioxide. The organic solvent absorption liquid removes hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the gas during absorption. The rich liquid absorbing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide is regenerated by air oxidation. The sulfur is dissolved in the solvent and recovers sulfur from the solvent by cooling and crystallization.
   USE - Method for removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from gas.

   USE - Liquefied natural gas (LNG) fuel cell energy                supply and cold energy utilization zero carbon                discharge system for generating electricity using                LNG cold energy for trapping and generating                additional product by using generated carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system fully uses the cold energy of the                liquefied natural gas to cool and recycle the                carbon dioxide generated by the fuel cell,                generating dry ice or liquid carbon dioxide product                with high additional value, which not only improves                the energy utilization rate, but also changes                carbon dioxide into valuable. The system designs                the heat utilization network, fully uses high                temperature solid oxide fuel cell power generation                energy supply device of heat energy, realizing the                dynamic balance of the heat demand of the                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                method.

TI Heavy metal recycling from fly ash, involves mixing fly ash with water to prepare slurry, filtering, leaching carbon dioxide at high pressure to remove carbon dioxide, washing, introducing excessive carbon dioxide gas into mixture, removing dioxin, burning, and extracting.

   XU L
   ZHANG Y
AE JIANGXI WANNIAN CSU EP IND COLLABORATIVE (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI GAIYA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202219234F
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling heavy metal from fly ash comprises (i) washing fly ash water with water, mixing fly ash with water to prepare a slurry, filtering the prepared slurry through a suction filter to obtain a water-washed liquid and a solid slag, (ii) leaching carbon dioxide at high pressure to remove carbon dioxide, washing the solid to prepare slurry and introducing excessive carbon dioxide gas into the mixture, (iii) removing dioxin, placing the secondary slag, and burning to remove dioxins, (iv) extracting valuable metals in third slag through ore dressing or acid leaching to obtain valuable metal slag and residue, and (v) extracting the valuable metal in the fourth slag by ore dressing) leaching carbon dioxide at high pressure to remove carbon dioxide, washing, introducing excessive carbon dioxide gas into the mixture, removing dioxin, extracting valuable metals in third slag through ore dressing or acid leaching, and extracting the valuable metal in the fourth slag by ore dressing.

The chemical reagent is small in use amount, and the chemical agent does not contact the external environment, part of the reagent can be recycled by subsequent reaction, or the residual reagent used for recycling. In the process, it avoids secondary pollution caused by chemical agent, it has low cost and little pollution.

AB    NOVELTY - Washing methanol at low-temperature with                semi-lean liquid absorption and high carbon dioxide                recovery, comprising absorption tower (T01) divided                into pre-washing section, desulfurizing section,                rough decarburizing section and fine decarburizing                section from bottom to top. A sulfur-rich methanol                at bottom of desulfurizing section of absorption                tower is sent to medium pressure flash tower lower                section. The rich methanol at bottom of lower                section of carbon dioxide desorption tower is sent                to upper section of nitrogen stripper. A stream of                semi-lean methanol and lean methanol are sent to                top of fine decarburizing section of the absorption                tower. The other strand is sent to upper section of                a nitrogen stripping tower (T04). The                methanol/carbon dioxide flash tank is located                between a carbon dioxide desorption tower (T03) and                the nitrogen stripping tower.
   USE - The method is useful for washing methanol at                low-temperature with semi-lean liquid absorption                and high carbon dioxide recovery .
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers 65-85% carbon dioxide,                which reduces carbon emissions and protects the                environment, adds semi-lean liquid as the main wash                methanol on the basis of the original pure lean                liquid absorption, and send back to the fine                decarburization section of the absorption tower for                recycling, which reduces the amount of lean                methanol by 30-50%, reduces the load of the thermal                regeneration tower and the amount of nitrogen                stripping, and saves energy.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas sensor comprises an                insulating substrate (3), and a gas sensing layer                (1) formed on one main surface of the insulating                substrate via an electrode (2). The gas sensing                layer comprises (a) one or more rare earth metal                oxycarbonates of formula: Ln2O2CO3 (I), where Ln is                one or more rare earth metal elements chosen from                scandium, yttrium, lanthanum, cerium, neodymium,                samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium,                dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, praseodymium,                ytterbium, and lutetium, and the main component of                the rare earth metal oxycarbonate is a hexagonal                crystal rare earth metal oxycarbonate, or (b) a                monoclinic crystal samarium dioxycarbonate.
   USE - The gas sensor is useful for detecting carbon                dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas sensor is highly stable,                cost-effective and simple in structure, and rapidly                detects carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for manufacturing the carbon                dioxide gas sensor involves aging the sensor                structure by subjecting the sensor structure to a                gas atmosphere containing 300-3000 ppm carbon                dioxide at 300-400 degrees C under a humidity of                20-90% and continuously driving heating step for 48                hours or more;
   (2) method for aging a carbon dioxide gas                sensor involves performing the method;
   (3) method for selectively producing a                crystalline polymorph of lanthanum dioxycarbonate                involves heating lanthanum acetate hydrate of                formula: La(CH3COO)3.nH2O (II), or a lanthanum                oxalate hydrate of formula: La2(C2O4)3.nH2O (III),                at 425-575 degrees C for 2-80 hours in a gas                atmosphere containing 350-500 ppm carbon dioxide,                and adjusting the heating temperature and/or the                heating time; and
   (4) method for selectively producing                monoclinic samarium dioxycarbonate involves heating                samarium acetate hydrate of formula:                Sm(CH3COO)3.nH2O (IV) or samarium oxalate hydrate                of formula: Sm2(C2O4)3.nH2O (V), at 425-575 degrees                C for 2-80 hours under gas atmosphere.

A second pipe is provided to the reheater (30), such that the carbon dioxide separated from the stripper is flowed in the condenser (41), and ejects the carbon dioxided to the upper portion of the stripper. A compressor (43) compresses the carbon dioxide by removing the moisture from the condenser. A second heat exchanger is equipped with the compressor.

PN US2021214218-A1; CN113122339-A; EP3851179-A1; IN202114000267-A
TI Separating a mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced by washing with methanol in an acid gas removal unit, involves cooling mixture in a heat exchanger, and withdrawing cooled mixture from heat exchanger.
AU LINICUS M
   DAVIDIAN B
   TEIXEIRA G
   HERNANDEZ A
   LE GULUDEC E
   LE GUDULEC E
   TEIXERA G
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   L'AIR LIQUIDE SOC ANONYME ETUD & EXPL (LAIR-Non-standard)
   GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202184266T
AB    NOVELTY - Separating a mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced by washing with methanol in an acid gas removal unit, involves cooling the mixture in a heat exchanger, and withdrawing at least a portion of the cooled mixture from the heat exchanger at an intermediate temperature of the heat exchanger between a cold end and a warm end of the heat exchanger. The at least portion of the cooled mixture is cooled by a flow of liquid methanol at a temperature below -40degrees Celsius to produce methanol enriched with carbon dioxide and a carbon dioxide depleted gas with respect to the cooled mixture. The carbon dioxide depleted gas is cooled in the heat exchanger and send the carbon dioxide depleted gas to a separation unit by methane washing and/or carbon monoxide washing and/or nitrogen washing and/or distillation and/or partial condensation to produce a carbon monoxide enriched flow.
   USE - Method for separating a mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced by washing with methanol in an acid gas removal unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoid solidification of the impurities in the exchangers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating a mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced by washing with methanol in an acid gas removal unit, involves cooling the mixture in a heat exchanger, and withdrawing at least a portion of the cooled mixture from the heat exchanger at an intermediate temperature of the heat exchanger between a cold end and a warm end of the heat exchanger. The at least portion of the cooled mixture is cooled by a flow of liquid methanol at a temperature below -40degrees Celsius to produce methanol enriched with carbon dioxide and a carbon dioxide depleted gas with respect to the cooled mixture. The carbon dioxide depleted gas is cooled in the heat exchanger and send the carbon dioxide depleted gas to a separation unit by methane washing and/or carbon monoxide washing and/or nitrogen washing and/or distillation and/or partial condensation to produce a carbon monoxide enriched flow with respect to the carbon dioxide depleted gas and a hydrogen enriched flow with respect to the carbon dioxide depleted gas. A stream selected from the group consisting of the carbon monoxide enriched stream with respect to the carbon dioxide depleted gas, the hydrogen-enriched stream with respect to the carbon dioxide depleted gas, and combinations heated in the heat exchanger by exchanging heat with the mixture and/or the carbon dioxide depleted gas or heating the stream in another heat exchanger.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for separating a mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced by washing with methanol in an acid gas removal unit, which includes a heat exchanger, and a separation unit which comprises a methane scrubbing column and/or a carbon monoxide scrubbing column and/or a nitrogen scrubbing column and/or a distillation column and/or at least one phase separator, unit for sending the mixture to cool in the heat exchanger, unit for outputting at least a portion of the cooled mixture of the heat exchanger to an intermediate point of the heat exchanger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The overhead gas is cooled to at least -170degrees Celsius upstream of the separation unit. The flow of methanol is from the acid gas removal unit which produces the mixture to be cooled in the heat exchanger. A liquid from the washing column is returned to the acid gas removal unit which produces the mixture to be cooled in the heat exchanger. The mixture has not been purified by adsorption upstream of the heat exchanger.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Laminate for packaging material, contains carbon dioxide absorption layer containing carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent and reaction accelerator, and carbon dioxide generation suppression layer containing water absorbent dispersion resin.
AU HAYASHI K
   TAKEUCHI N
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 202097982L
AB    NOVELTY - A laminate (1) comprises a base material layer (2), a carbon dioxide absorption layer (3), and a carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4). The layer (3) comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent, carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent dispersion resin, a water absorbent (a1), and a reaction accelerator. The carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent comprises carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkali metal compound and/or carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkaline-earth metal compound. The component (a1) is chosen from high water-absorbing resin, deliquescent compound, hydrophilic zeolite, and silica gel. The reaction accelerator is chosen from lithium chloride, lithium hydroxide, sodium chloride, and potassium chloride. The carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer comprises an water absorbent (a2) and water absorbent dispersion resin. The component (a2) is chosen from water-absorbing alkali metal compound, water-absorbing alkaline-earth metal compound, and hydrophilic zeolite.
   USE - Laminate used for packaging material for package e.g. valveless packaging bag and valveless packaging pouch bag for roasted coffee beans (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The laminate has excellent storage stability for long time, and suppressed generation of carbon dioxide gas from the roasted coffee beans from the packaging material, and prevents the filling of carbon dioxide gas in the package, prevents package swelling up, and reduced quantity of the carbon dioxide gas emitted when the contents packaged and preventing decarboxylation reaction, and efficiently absorbs the carbon dioxide gas, and provides valveless package having excellent durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A laminate (1) comprises a base material layer (2), a carbon dioxide absorption layer (3), and a carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4). The layer (3) comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent, carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent dispersion resin, a water absorbent (a1), and a reaction accelerator. The carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkali metal compound and/or a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkaline-earth metal compound. The water absorbent (a1) is chosen from high water-absorbing resin, deliquescent compound, hydrophilic zeolite, and silica gel. The reaction accelerator is chosen from lithium chloride, lithium hydroxide, sodium chloride, and potassium chloride. The carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer comprises an water absorbent (a2) and water absorbent dispersion resin. The water absorbent (a2) is chosen from water-absorbing alkali metal compound, water-absorbing alkaline-earth metal compound, and hydrophilic zeolite. The hydrophilic zeolite comprises silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide in a molar ratio of 1/1-20/1.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the laminate.
   Laminate (1)
   Base material layer (2)
   Carbon dioxide absorption layer (3)
   Carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas-adsorption agent dispersion resin and/or the water-absorbent dispersion resin, are polyolefin resin. The carbon dioxide-gas absorption layer and/or a carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer have heat-sealing property. The laminate further comprises a barrier layer between the layers (2), (3) and (4), a carbonic acid gas absorption layer laminated on the base material layer side rather than the layer (4), and a heat sealing layer laminated on the surface of the surface of the carbon dioxide absorption laminate opposite to the base material layer. The barrier layer comprises a barrier resin coating film, metal foil, resin film with metal-deposited layer, and resin film with metal oxide vapor deposition layer. The layer (4) is laminated on the base material layer side rather than the carbonic acid gas absorption layer. Preferred Composition: The carbonic acid gas absorption layer comprises 0.5-50 %mass carbonic acid gas adsorption agent. The layer (4) comprises 0.5-40 %mass water absorbent.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for squeezing pepper oil with                supercritical carbon dioxide, involves (a)                pre-treating pepper, screening, separating,                cleaning, drying and crushing, (b) performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the                crushed pepper powder to obtain extracts and pepper                residue, stratifying, removing the water layer to                obtain pepper oleoresin, (c) performing second                squeezing extraction to remove the prickly ash                seeds from the prickly ash residue obtained in step                (b), pressing them into tablets, performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a                crude extract of pepper oleoresin, after adding                water to leave still for stratification, removing                the water layer, and dehydrating the remaining oil                layer to obtain pepper oleoresin, (d) combining the                pepper oleoresin, adding bitterness treatment                agent, reacting, centrifuging, and taking the                supernatant to obtain pepper oleoresin, and (e)                subjecting the pepper oleoresin to molecular                distillation, and packaging.
   USE - Method for squeezing pepper oil with                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction process, so that the pepper                oleoresin obtained by extraction contains high                flavor components and taste components of pepper.                The method combines with molecular distillation                technology to separate the pepper flavor oil from                the pepper oleoresin to make the pepper oil with                rich flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for squeezing pepper oil with                supercritical carbon dioxide, involves (a)                pre-treating select fresh and ripe pepper,                screening, separating and removing the husk and                impurities of pepper, cleaning with water, drying                until the water content of the pepper is                &#8815; 20%, and crushing, (b) first squeeze                extraction by putting the crushed pepper powder                into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                to obtain extracts and pepper residue, after                allowing the extract to leave still for                stratification, removing the water layer to obtain                pepper oleoresin, (c) performing second squeezing                extraction to remove the prickly ash seeds from the                prickly ash residue obtained in step (b), pressing                them into tablets, performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction to obtain a crude extract of                pepper oleoresin, after adding water to leave still                for stratification, removing the water layer, and                dehydrating the remaining oil layer to obtain                pepper oleoresin, (d) pepper oil treatment by                combining the pepper oleoresin obtained in step (b)                and step (c), adding bitterness treatment agent,                reacting for 15-30 minutes, centrifuging for 10-12                minutes under low temperature conditions, and                taking the supernatant to obtain pepper oleoresin,                and (e) distillation and packaging the pepper                oleoresin obtained in step (d), subjecting to                molecular distillation, and packaging to obtain the                finished pepper oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the                  extraction pressure is 12-22 MPa and the                  extraction time is 3-4 hours. In step (b), the                  leaving still and layering is performed at                  32-42&#176; C for 25-45 minutes. The weight                  of the water added in step (c) is 2-3 times the                  weight of the crude extract of pepper oleoresin,                  and the leaving still time after adding water is                  25-50 minutes, and the temperature is                  30-38&#176; C. The low-temperature treatment                  in step (d) is a centrifugal treatment at                  3-5&#176; C at 5000-6000 rotation/second for                  10-12 minutes. The molecular distillation                  pressure in step (e) is 0.06-0.09 MPa, and the                  distillation temperature is 80-86&#176; C.                  Preferred Composition: The bitterness treatment                  agent in step (d) comprises adenosine                  triphosphate and sodium ascorbate in a weight                  ratio of 5:2. The added amount of the bitterness                  treatment agent in step (d) is 0.1-3% of the                  total amount of pepper oleoresin.

PN KR2194013-B1
TI Treatment of cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide emitted from cement manufacturing facility, involves carbonic acid mineralizing by injecting gas containing carbon dioxide into slurry containing chlorine-containing cement kiln dust.
AU YOUN M
   JUNG S
   LEE W H
   BAK K T
   KANG S P
   KWON S J
   KIMHAKJU
   KIM E Y
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2021007946
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide involves (i) preparing a slurry of mixing chlorine-containing cement kiln dust and water, (ii) carbonic acid mineralizing by injecting gas containing carbon dioxide into the slurry and reacting to form product containing slurry containing a carbonate-type precipitate and purge gas from which carbon dioxide is removed, (iii) separating and discharging a purified gas from the product, separating and recovering the slurry containing the carbonate-type precipitate separated by gas-liquid solid-liquid separation into a carbonate-type precipitate and a separated liquid, (iv) removing impurities contained in the separation liquid and (v) separating the separated liquid from which impurities are removed into sludge containing potassium chloride and water.
   USE - Treatment of cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide emitted from cement manufacturing facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide treatment without wastewater generation and without requiring separate collection device, and capable of easily recovering the cement kiln dust carbonate and highly concentrated potassium chloride from which chlorine and heavy metals are removed. Since high concentration of potassium chloride can be obtained and water can be recovered and reused again, there is no concern of wastewater generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide treatment device, which consists of a slurry preparation unit for preparing slurry by mixing the chlorine-containing cement kiln dust introduced from the cement kiln dust supply unit and the water introduced from the water supply unit, a carbon dioxide-containing gas supply unit, a carbonic acid mineralization unit including a reactor for mixing and reacting the carbon dioxide-containing gas introduced from the carbon dioxide-containing gas supply unit and the slurry supplied from the slurry preparation unit to generate a product including a carbonate-type precipitate and purified gas, a gas-liquid separation unit that receives a product from the carbonic acid mineralization unit and separates and discharges a purified gas from the product, a separating unit including a solid-liquid separating unit that receives the product from which the purification gas is removed from the gas-liquid separating unit and separates the solid-liquid to separate and recover a carbonate type precipitate and a separating solution, an adsorbent layer capable of receiving the separation liquid from the solid-liquid separation unit and adsorbing and removing impurities contained in the separation liquid, and an impurity removing unit including a film capable of separating the separated liquid from which excess impurities are removed into sludge and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The chlorine-containing cement kiln dust is produced from waste discharged from cement manufacturing facilities.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: In the carbonic acid mineralization unit, the operating temperature of the reactor 510 is 5-50 degrees C, and the operating pressure is 0.5-2 atm. Preferred Components: In the separation unit, the gas-liquid separation is performed with a flash drum. In the separation unit, the solid-liquid separation is performed by a screw decanter which is a centrifuge separator. The reactor is a batch reactor, a semi-batch reactor, tubular reactor, continuous stirred tank reactor, plug flow reactors, packed-bed reactors, fluidized-bed reactor, continuous flow reactors, or slurry bubble column reactor. The reactor is preferably several tubular reactors connected in series. The tubular reactor includes a tubular reaction tube having a flow path formed therein so that the slurry and the carbon dioxide-containing gas flow from one end to the other end, a fixed stirrer which is fixed and located inside the reaction tube, and formed on the central axis in the longitudinal direction of the reaction tube, in which a plate is formed facing the inner circumferential surface of the reaction tube and several stirring units having the plate twisted with respect to the central axis direction are coupled to each other and a gas filter arranged adjacent to one end of the reaction tube and configured to disperse the carbon dioxide-containing gas into the slurry in a microbubble state.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The water in step (v) is recovered and resupplied to the slurry production step by impurity removing unit. In step (iv), the separated liquid recovered in step (iii) is passed through the adsorbent layer to remove metal ions including calcium, iron and/or lead present in the separation liquid. In step (ii), the reaction is carried out in several tubular reactors connected in series. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the weight ratio of the chlorine-containing cement kiln dust and water is 1:3-18. In the step (iii), the solid-liquid separated liquid is a mixture of cement kiln dust and carbon dioxide, which is an aqueous solution containing potassium chloride. The adsorbent layer comprises adsorbent which is zeolite. The zeolite is at least one of zeolite A, zeolite X, zeolite Y, zeolite L, ZSM-5 zeolite, ZSM-8 type zeolite, ZSM-11 type zeolite, ZSM-12-type zeolite, ZSM-18-type zeolite, ZSM-23-type zeolite, ZSM-34 type zeolite, ZSM-35 type zeolite, ZSM-38 type zeolite, ZSM-48 type zeolite, faujasite, silicalite-1, mordenite, ferrierite, offretite, gmelinite, Omega -zeolite, ( beta )-zeolite and MCM-22-type zeolite. In the step (ii), the volume ratio of the slurry and the carbon dioxide-containing gas is 1:1-20 based on 25 degrees C and 1 atm. In step (iii), the pH of the slurry containing the precipitate in the form of gas-liquid separated carbonate is 5-9. The adsorbent layer is present as a packed bed of zeolite beads, pellets or granules.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrogen involves providing to a single partial oxidation (POX) reactor an oxidizing gas (2) and a gas comprising hydrocarbons (1) to obtain a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, operating partial oxidation reactor at 1000-1500 degrees C and a pressure of at least 40 bar, cooling the hot synthesis gas (3) to a temperature below 300 degrees C to obtain a cooled synthesis gas (4), providing the cooled synthesis gas and water to a reactor (R2), operating the reactor at 200-480 degrees C and comprising a catalyst that converts carbon monoxide in the presence of water into carbon dioxide (6) and hydrogen, to obtain a gas mixture comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, providing the gas mixture to a carbon dioxide removal unit to obtain a hydrogen rich gas stream (7) and a carbon dioxide rich gas stream (9) having a pressure of at least 13 bar and a carbon dioxide rich gas stream (8) having a pressure of at least 0.7 bar, providing carbon dioxide rich gases streams (8, 9) to a compression unit to obtain a carbon dioxide gas stream (10) having a pressure of at least 40 bar, and utilizing the carbon dioxide in a chemical process and/or storing the carbon dioxide in a geological reservoir.

PN WO2021084250-A1; CN114650877-A; EP4051429-A1
TI Photocatalytic material used for converting carbon            dioxide to non-carbon dioxide carbon containing            product, comprises metal nanofiber substrate and            carbon-based nano-structure, which is bound to surface            of nanofiber substrate.
AU KE X
AE UNIV HULL (UYHU-Non-standard)
   UNIV HULL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021479075
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic material comprises metal                nanofiber substrate and carbon-based                nano-structure, where the carbon-based                nano-structure is bound to surface of metal                nanofiber substrate.
   USE - The photocatalytic material is used for                converting carbon dioxide to non-carbon dioxide                carbon containing product, such as hydrocarbon                product comprising methanol and/or ethanol,                preferably mixture of methanol and ethanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material enhances the                irradiated light to dissociate molecules, has                excellent recovering and reusing ability, ensures                high total surface area, and prevents multiple                metal nanofiber substrates from aggregating to                single metal structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing photocatalytic                material, involving providing metal nanofiber                substrate, and growing from surface of metal                nanofiber substrate and carbon-based nanostructure;                and
   (2) a method for converting carbon dioxide to                at least one non-carbon dioxide containing product,                involves exposing photocatalytic material to carbon                dioxide, and irradiating photocatalytic material                with light source in presence of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalytic                  material comprises metal nanofiber substrates.                  The metal nanofiber substrate comprises metal                  comprising copper, nickel, iron, cobalt, silver,                  platinum, molybdenum and/or silver. The                  photocatalytic material comprises carbon-based                  nano-structures. The carbon-based nano-structure                  comprises carbon-based structures comprising                  carbon nanotube and graphene. The photocatalytic                  material comprises carbon nanotubes that are                  bound to surface of metal nanofiber substrate.                  The carbon nanotube is multi wall carbon                  nanotubes. The photocatalytic material comprises                  substantially more metal nanofiber material than                  carbon-based structure material, optionally the                  photocatalytic material comprises up to 40 wt.%                  carbon-based structure material. The metal                  nanofiber substrate is exposed to carbon source                  under conventional chemical vapour deposition                  techniques. The metal oxide nanofiber substrate                  is exposed to carbon source under conventional                  chemical vapour deposition (CVD) techniques to                  reduce metal oxide nanofiber substrate and                  growing carbon-based structure from surface of                  metal nanofiber substrate. Preferred Method: The                  method involves growing carbon-based                  nanostructure from surface of metal nanofiber                  substrate by exposing metal nanofiber substrate                  to carbon source at high temperature, optionally                  in absence of oxygen; and in presence of inert                  carrier gas. The method involves preparing                  photocatalytic material by providing metal oxide                  nanofiber substrate, converting metal oxide                  nanofiber substrate to metal nanofiber substrate,                  and growing from surface of metal nanofiber                  substrate and carbon-based nanostructure. The                  method involves providing metal oxide nanofiber                  substrate by forming metal oxide nanofiber                  substrate. The method involves converting metal                  oxide nanofiber substrate to metal nanofiber                  substrate in conjunction with growing process of                  carbon-based nanostructure from surface of metal                  oxide nanofiber substrate. The method involves                  providing metal nanofiber substrate by providing                  metal nanofibers, or providing metal oxide                  nanofiber by forming metal oxide nanofibers. The                  method further involves suspending photocatalytic                  material in liquid, where the liquid is water.                  The liquid for suspending photocatalytic material                  further comprises additives, where the additive                  comprises acid or a base, optionally                  triethanolamine and isopropanol. The method                  involves irradiating photocatalytic material with                  light source using light source by producing                  visible light, UV light and/or infra-red                  radiation. Preferred Conditions: The each metal                  nanofiber substrate has length and diameter                  having high aspect ratio, optionally the length                  of each metal nanofiber is at least 50 times                  diameter of the nanofiber. The each metal                  nanofiber has diameter of 5-100 nm, and length of                  0.5-30 pm. The each carbon nanotube has diameter                  of less than 50 nm. The each carbon nanotube or                  multi wall carbon nanotubes has length of less                  than 10 pm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon source                  comprises cellulose, high-density polyethylene,                  polypropylene and/or polyethylene terephthalate                  plastics.
