TI Hydrophilic composite particle for functional food, comprises a multiple of hydrophilic and poorly water-soluble child particles and a hydrophobic amino acid mother particle coated with the multiple of child particles.
AU MATSUYAMA K
   NEMOTO Y
   NISHINOMIYA T
AE AJINOMOTO KK (AJIN-C)
GA 201975676S
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrophilic composite particle comprises a multiple of hydrophilic and poorly water-soluble child particles and a hydrophobic amino acid mother particle coating with the multiple of child particles.
   USE - Hydrophilic composite particle is used for functional food(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrophilic composite particle in which hydrophobic amino acids are hydrophilized to improve solubility characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing composite particles, which involves:
   (A) introducing hydrophilic and poorly water-soluble child particles and hydrophobic amino acid mother particles into a carbon dioxide liquid;
   (B) irradiating the carbon dioxide liquid with ultrasonic waves;
   (C) transferring the carbon dioxide liquid from a liquid state of carbon dioxide to a supercritical state of carbon dioxide without passing through a gas state; and
   (D) transferring the carbon dioxide from a supercritical state of carbon dioxide to a gas state without passing through a liquid state, and removing the carbon dioxide to obtain composite particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mother particle is selected from the group consisting of leucine, isoleucine, glycine, alanine, valine, proline, methionine, phenylalanine and tryptophan. The child particle is selected from the group consisting of silica, insoluble dietary fiber, and beta starch. Preferred Conditions: The base particle has a particle size of 50-1000 micrometer. The child particle has a particle size of less than 1 micrometer. The composite particle comprises 2.0-15 wt.% of the child particles with respect to the total mass of the composite particle. The 2.0-15 wt.% of the child particles is introduced with respect to a total mass of the mother particles and the child particles. The pressure of the carbon dioxide is 4-7 megapascal, and the temperature is 278-303 kelvin. The amplitude of the ultrasonic wave is 20-40 micrometer. The ultrasonic irradiation time is 60 seconds or more. The ultrasonic irradiation is intermittent irradiation. The temperature of carbon dioxide in a supercritical state is 308-343 kelvin, and the pressure is 7.5-15 megapascal.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen matrix biofilm reactor combined                device comprises a hydrogen matrix biofilm main                reactor system, and a carbon dioxide supply system.                The main reactor system comprises a raw water tank,                a reaction tank, a water inlet pipeline system, a                water outlet pipeline system, a return pipeline                system, and a hydrogen supply system. The reaction                tank is provided with a hydrogen membrane assembly.                The hydrogen supply system is connected with the                two ports of the hydrogen membrane assembly in the                reaction tank. The reaction tank, the water inlet                pipeline system and the return pipeline system are                connected using a tee joint. The reaction tank, the                water outlet pipeline system and the return                pipeline system are connected using a tee joint.                The reaction tank, the water inlet pipeline system,                the water outlet pipeline system and the return                pipeline system form a circulation loop. The supply                system comprises a deionized water tank, a                circulation system, and a carbon dioxide membrane                module.
   USE - The hydrogen matrix biofilm reactor combined                device is useful for treatment of                nitrate-containing sewage (claimed) in water                purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen matrix biofilm reactor combined                device: establishes independent carbon dioxide                supply system; achieves the purpose of                quantitatively supplying carbon dioxide to the                hydrogen matrix biofilm main reactor system by                monitoring the carbon dioxide concentration in the                buffer tank; has excellent treatment effect, and                simple structure; accurately control the carbon                dioxide addition amount and system pH; and can                perform engineering amplification. Carbon dioxide                accurately added using the carbon dioxide supply                system simultaneously serves as an inorganic carbon                source for hydroautotrophic microorganisms and                controls the pH of the system, so that the                hydrogen-based membrane biofilm reactor can                maintain long-term efficient and stable operation                during denitrification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen matrix biofilm reactor combined                device comprises a hydrogen matrix biofilm main                reactor system, and a carbon dioxide supply system.                The main reactor system comprises a raw water tank,                a reaction tank, a water inlet pipeline system, a                water outlet pipeline system, a return pipeline                system, and a hydrogen supply system. The reaction                tank is provided with a hydrogen membrane assembly.                The hydrogen supply system is connected with the                two ports of the hydrogen membrane assembly in the                reaction tank. The reaction tank, the water inlet                pipeline system and the return pipeline system are                connected using a tee joint. The reaction tank, the                water outlet pipeline system and the return                pipeline system are connected using a tee joint.                The reaction tank, the water inlet pipeline system,                the water outlet pipeline system and the return                pipeline system form a circulation loop. The supply                system comprises a deionized water tank, a                circulation system, a carbon dioxide supply bottle                and a carbon dioxide membrane module. The bottle is                connected with the two ports of the module. The                circulation system is connected with water inlet                end and water outlet end of the module. The                deionized water tank is connected with the water                inlet end of the module, and the circulation                system. The reactor system and supply system are                connected through a buffer tank, which is arranged                on a pipeline of the circulation system, is                communicated with the water inlet pipeline system,                and is water containing carbon dioxide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for treatment                of nitrate-containing sewage using the combined                hydrogen matrix biofilm reactor device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The module comprises a                  glass tube and a hollow fiber membrane bundle,                  which is placed inside the glass tube, and                  comprises several hollow fiber membrane                  filaments.Preferred Property: The air supply pressure                  provided using the hydrogen supply system is 1-15                  pisg.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The materials of the                  hollow fiber membrane are all hydrophobic                  polypropylene materials.Preferred Properties: The hollow fiber                  membrane has a pore diameter of 0.02 &#956;m,                  an inner diameter of 200 &#956;m, and an                  outer diameter of 280 &#956;m.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for absorbing carbon dioxide using the system,                which involves: a).sending carbon dioxide absorbent                and water into the absorbent configuration tank by                absorbent tube and the water tube respectively,                preparing 25 wt.% absorbent;b).sending the gas                containing carbon dioxide to the gas cooler,                cooling the gas to below 30degrees                Celsius;c).passing the gas containing carbon                dioxide into the absorption box through the air                inlet pipe;d).sending configured absorption liquid                into the first liquid inlet pipe and the second                liquid inlet pipe by the second pressurizing                pump;e).spraying out by atomizing nozzles on the                first liquid injection pipe and the second liquid                injection pipe;f).mixing fully absorption liquid                with carbon dioxide, falling into the liquid                recovery tank, when the absorption liquid in the                liquid recovery tank is full;g).opening the first                valve, the second valve and the first pressurizing                pump;h).sending the absorption liquid into the                first liquid inlet pipe and the second liquid inlet                pipe through the return pipe, and spraying out                again to continue to absorb carbon dioxide, when                the pH value of the absorption liquid in the liquid                recovery tank is less than or equal to                7.5;i).closing the second valve;i).opening the                fifth valve;k).draining the absorption liquid into                the liquid storage tank through the liquid recovery                pipe for recycling.

AB    NOVELTY - Paving material comprises one of a carbon dioxide copolymer raw material, a carbon dioxide copolymer dressing, ethyl acetate, basalt fiber, carbon fiber, and crushed stone, where carbon dioxide copolymer dressing is a scrap in the production process of a carbon dioxide copolymer medical dressing, and the main components of the carbon dioxide copolymer dressing are a carbon dioxide copolymer and a wood fiber.
   USE - Paving material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing paving material, which involves:
   (A) addign pulverized carbon dioxide copolymer raw material and ethyl acetate to the stirred tank and stirring until the carbon dioxide copolymer raw material is completely dissolved to obtain the first mixture;
   (B) adding portion of the carbon dioxide copolymer dressing to the carbon dioxide copolymer raw material mixture, immersing and stirring, obtaining second mixture, adding the remaining carbon dioxide copolymer dressing to the second mixture, stirring until completely dissolved to obtain a third mixture; and
   (C) selecting gravel of different particle sizes and mixing with the third mixture according to the gradation ratio, and adding basalt fiber and carbon fiber and stirring to obtain paving materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of the carbon dioxide copolymer to the wood fiber is 1:1 in the carbon dioxide copolymer dressing. The particle size of the crushed stone ranges from 0.075-16 mm. The carbon dioxide copolymer feedstock has an average particle size of less than 3 cm and the carbon dioxide copolymer dressing has an average particle size of less than 5 mm. The grading mass percentage of the crushed stone is 5 wt.% of 13.2 mm graded gravel minerals, 34 wt.% of 9.5 mm graded gravel minerals, 17 wt.% of 4.75 mm graded gravel minerals, 12 wt.% 2.36mm grade gravel mineral material, 9.5 wt.% of 1.18mm grade gravel mineral material, 6.5 wt.% of 0.6mm grade gravel mineral material, 5 wt.% of 0.3mm grade gravel mineral material, 4.5wt.% of 0.15mm graded gravel minerals, 6.5 wt.% of 0.075mm graded gravel minerals. The pulverized carbon dioxide copolymer raw material and ethyl acetate are added to a stirred tank for mixing for 4-8 hours. The partial carbon dioxide copolymer dressing is added to the carbon dioxide copolymer raw material mixture for 20-28 hours. Preferred Composition: The paving dressing comprises 20-40 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer raw material, 30-60 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer dressing, 100-300 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, 1-10 pts. wt. basalt fiber, 0.5-8 pts. wt. carbon fiber and 200-600 pts. wt. gravel.

TI System for recovering carbon dioxide from by-product gas, supplies liquefied carbon dioxide and cooling gas heat-exchanged with compressed gas to vaporize and separate impurities having lower boiling point than carbon dioxide.
AU KIMSANGHYUN
   AHNKYUCHOUL
AE KIMSANGHYUN (KIMS-Individual)
   AHNKYUCHOUL (AHNK-Individual)
GA 202100789X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a heat exchange unit (600) for secondary cooling of compressed gas. A condenser produces liquefied carbon dioxide by condensing at a temperature of -70 degrees C. A rectifier (720) removes the impurities contained in liquefied carbon dioxide. A liquefied carbon dioxide tank recovers the concentrated liquefied carbon dioxide from the rectifier. A carbon dioxide recovery unit (700) includes a waste heat recovery passage (740) for recovering carbon dioxide from the condenser and the rectifier and transfers the remaining exhaust gas to cooling unit so that heat exchange is performed, and the rectifier is partially branched from front end of a liquefied carbon dioxide tank (730). The liquefied carbon dioxide heat-exchanged with the compressed gas of heat exchange unit and the cooling gas provided in expansion unit and heat-exchanged with the compressed gas from heat exchange unit are supplied to vaporize and separate impurities having a lower boiling point than carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for recovering carbon dioxide from by-product gas in the pretreatment unit for removing odors.
   ADVANTAGE - By recycling the low-temperature waste heat to cool the compressed gas, the load on the expansion unit is reduced and the production of liquefied carbon dioxide is increased.

   Carbon dioxide recovery unit (700)
   Rectifier (720)

PN CN113987851-A; CN113987851-B
TI Method for analyzing fluid conduction characteristics of carbon dioxide migration channels, involves generating analytical gauge plate for inverting carbon dioxide transport channel morphology and carbon dioxide transport capacity based on elastic characteristics of carbon dioxide transport channel.
AU WANG W
   SHAN X
   ZHANG W
   FU B
AE INST GEOLOGY & GEOPHYSICS CHINESE ACAD S (CAGG-C)
GA 202223137Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining the morphological parameter features constituting a carbon dioxide migration channel based on a fracture outcrop, a core observation data and a logging data. A statistical analysis is performed on the fracture surface morphology, fracture length, and fracture aperture to obtain carbon dioxide migration channel characteristics based on the morphological parameter characteristics. A local cubic law, a Navier Stokes equation and a Hookes law are used to obtain the elastic modulus and carbon dioxide transport capacity of the carbon dioxide transport channel through a numerical simulation. An analytical gauge plate for inverting the carbon dioxide transport channel morphology and carbon dioxide transport capacity based on the elastic characteristics of the carbon dioxide transport channel is generated based on the relationship between the elastic modulus, morphological parameters and carbon dioxide transport capacity of the carbon dioxide transport channel.
   USE - Method for analyzing fluid transmission characteristic of carbon dioxide migration channel such as underground crack in rock fluid migration field for realizing carbon neutralization.
   ADVANTAGE - The evaluation of the permeability of the underground structure can be finished. The change rule of the carbon dioxide migration ability with time can be described.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a fluid transmission characteristic analysis system of carbon dioxide migration channel; (2) an electronic device; (3) a computer readable storage medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the analysis method of fluid conductivity characteristics of the carbon dioxide migration channel. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN WO2018184123-A1; CH713676-A1
TI Method for recovering carbon dioxide produced during wet oxidation treatment from aqueous mixture of waste containing organic matter, involves providing gases comprising carbon dioxide, so that carbon dioxide is separated and collected.
AU CORREA B
AE GRANIT TECHNOLOGIES & ENG GRT SA (GRAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018781761
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an oxidizing gas into aqueous waste mixture which is preheated. The pressurized liquid carbon dioxide is separated from the non-condensable gases, and the produced liquid carbon dioxide is collected. The gases separated from the liquids are transferred to an exchanger of heat, and the pre-cooled gases are moved in a first gas, so that the liquid separator is provided for separating the gases from the oxidation treatment of the liquid residues. The separate gases are sent to a cooler under pressure for liquefying, while maintaining pressure between specific ranges. The gases comprising carbon dioxide generated by the oxidation reaction of the organic materials and the non-condensable residual gases are provided, so that the pressurized liquid carbon dioxide is separated and collected from the non-condensable gases. The liquid carbon dioxide is produced, without the use of additional energy.
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide produced during wet oxidation treatment from aqueous mixture of waste containing organic matter.
   ADVANTAGE - The pollution can be limited and the product that is a raw material with many applications in the industry can be recovered, by recovering the carbon dioxide released during the wet waste oxidation treatment. The dosing of hot residual liquid fraction separated from the gases during the oxidation treatment can be carried out by pump controlled by control unit. The cost of the installation can be reduced by removing one of the heat exchangers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The wet oxidation reaction is carried out in an oxidation reactor (102) at temperatures sufficient to oxidize oxidizable substances. The gases generated by oxidation treatment of the residual liquids resulting from the oxidation treatment are separated partially and the gases separated from the liquids are pre-cooled. The pre-cooled gases are sent to a first gas and liquid separator (104), to separate the gases from the oxidation treatment of the liquid residues. The separated gases are fed to a pressure cooler to liquefy them, and comprising carbon dioxide generated by the oxidation reaction of the organic materials and the non-condensable residual gases. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for recovering carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the process for recovering carbon dioxide.
   Apparatus for recovering carbon dioxide (100)
   Oxidation reactor (102)
   Gas and liquid separator (104)
   Pump (107)
   Container or bottle (108)

TI System for methanation of carbon dioxide, consists of catalyst tower comprising catalyst for producing methane from carbon dioxide, and device for supplying carbon dioxide-containing gas to tower.
AU TAKAHASHI E
   YOSHIKAWA K
   KANAEDA M
AE HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
GA 2020205425
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide methanation system consists of a catalyst tower (12) comprising a catalyst for producing methane from carbon dioxide, and a device (11) supplying a gas comprising carbon dioxide to the catalyst tower. The carbon dioxide methanation catalyst decomposes oxygen.
   USE - System used for methanation of carbon dioxide for preventing environmental pollution such as global warming.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is economical and contributes to energy saving.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide methanation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide supply apparatus (11)
   Catalyst tower (12)
   Hydrogen supply apparatus (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The carbon dioxide supply device is a methane fermentation device. The methanation system further consists of a hydrogen supply device (14) generating hydrogen by electrolyzing water and supplying hydrogen to the catalyst tower, a water removal device for removing water generated from the catalyst tower provided at the catalyst tower, and a water recovery/supply device for removing and recovering water generated from the catalyst tower and supplying the recovered water to the hydrogen supply device provided at the catalyst tower. The system further consists of a carbon dioxide recovery device collecting carbon dioxide generated from the catalyst tower provided at the catalyst tower, a carbon dioxide recovery/supply device recovering carbon dioxide generated from the catalyst tower and supplying the carbon dioxide to the catalyst tower again provided at the catalyst tower, a hydrogen recovery device recovering hydrogen discharged from the catalyst tower at a stage after to the catalyst tower, and a hydrogen recovery/supply device recovering hydrogen discharged from the catalyst tower and supplying the recovered hydrogen to the catalyst tower is further provided at a stage after to the catalyst tower. The system further consists of an apparatus measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide and the concentration of oxygen supplied to the catalyst tower, and a desulfurization device removing sulfur contained in the carbon dioxide-containing gas. The system further consists of a desulfurization device removing sulfur and carbon dioxide separated from the gas in the desulfurization device to the catalyst tower in the preceding stage of the catalyst tower.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methanation catalyst comprises a carrier comprising at least one component chosen from cerium, lanthanum, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, ytterbium, lutetium, and nickel. The catalyst component further comprises at least one chosen from platinum, rhodium, and palladium. The molar concentration of hydrogen calculated from the molar concentration of carbon dioxide and the molar concentration of oxygen using the formula: Molar concentration of hydrogen = 4x molar concentration of carbon dioxide + 2x molar concentration of oxygen. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide-containing gas comprises 0.1-20 mol% oxygen. The methane-containing gas discharged from the catalyst tower comprises less than 0.1 mol% oxygen. The catalyst component comprises 0.1-40 parts mass nickel with respect to 100 parts mass of active ingredient of the carrier. The carbon dioxide-containing gas further comprises 0.1-95 mol% nitrogen.

TI Low-consumption carbon dioxide capture system for low-concentration carbon dioxide-containing gas, has desorption tower reboiler whose inlet is connected to rich amine liquid outlet at lower portion of carbon dioxide desorption tower.

GA 2020A1894X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooler (5) sequentially connected with an amine liquid inlet in a middle surface of a carbon dioxide absorption tower (3). A rich amine liquid outlet at a bottom surface of the carbon dioxide absorption tower is connected to an inlet of a rich amine booster pump (6). A rich amine liquid outlet at a lower portion of the carbon dioxide desorption tower is connected to an inlet of a desorption tower reboiler (13). A lean amine liquid side outlet of the lean rich amine liquid heat exchanger, a lean amine liquid cooler (11) and an upper lean amine liquid inlet of the carbon dioxide absorption tower are sequentially connected with each other.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a low-consumption carbon dioxide capturing method for low-concentration carbon dioxide-containing gas.

PN CN110699135-A; CN110699135-B
TI Producing methanol synthesis gas by decarbonization method involves reducing temperature of crude synthesis gas, separating liquid carbon dioxide from gaseous carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and collecting liquid carbon dioxide.
AU ZANG H
   DENG Y
   LIU X
   ZHANG C
   YE B
AE LANGFANG FANHUA PETROCHEMICAL EQUIP CO (LANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020083975
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol synthesis gas by decarbonization method involves physically reducing the temperature of the crude synthesis gas by using a heat exchange method, so that carbon dioxide in the crude synthesis gas is converted into a liquid state, and maintaining carbon monoxide and hydrogen in a gaseous state. The liquid carbon dioxide is separated from the gaseous carbon monoxide and hydrogen, the gaseous carbon monoxide and hydrogen are collected as a low-temperature synthesis gas, and the liquid carbon dioxide is collected in a container for storage.
   USE - Method for producing methanol synthesis gas by decarbonization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and beneficial for reducing the cost of methanol production, and allows convenient collection and storage of carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide is transported to air-conditioning facilities to utilize its cold energy, and it saves energy and protects the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The physical cooling process includes a primary process of pre-cooling the crude syngas to form a low-temperature crude syngas, and a secondary process of continuing to cool the low-temperature crude syngas. Using the low-temperature synthesis gas to perform heat exchange with the crude synthesis gas to realize the primary treatment, the low-temperature synthesis gas forms a clean synthesis gas after the primary treatment, and the crude synthesis gas forms a low-temperature crude synthesis gas after the primary treatment. The secondary treatment involves performing heat exchange between a refrigerant and the low-temperature crude synthesis gas, and forming low-temperature gas-liquid mixture by low-temperature crude synthesis gas. The refrigerant is liquefied natural gas that forms compressed natural gas after the secondary treatment. The method utilizes the residual energy in the compressed natural gas. The compressed natural gas is used for heat exchange with the waste heat of the factory, and the compressed natural gas is heated to form medium-temperature natural gas. By utilizing the mechanical energy generated by the medium temperature natural gas expansion to perform external work, the medium temperature natural gas expands to perform external work to form normal temperature natural gas, and the normal temperature natural gas is transmitted to a user gas pipeline network. The temperature of the low-temperature crude synthesis gas after the secondary treatment is -115 to -100 degrees C. The factory waste heat comprises waste steam or waste heat gas.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Method for extracting carbon dioxide-based phase            change coal gas involves injecting carbon dioxide            liquid into carbon dioxide cracker located outside            borehole, carbon dioxide cracker is provided with            constant pressure rupture disk.
AU ZHANG D
   HE Q
   JI Y
   SONG R
   LIU H
   WANG H
   YIN S
   YU B
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
   SICHUAN COAL MINE SAFETY SUPERVISORY BUR (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0350K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting carbon dioxide                liquid into carbon dioxide cracker located outside                borehole through gas source system. The carbon                dioxide cracker is provided with constant pressure                rupture disk and release tube. The inner cavity                does not contain combustion rod, ignition head and                carbon dioxide liquid. The pressure of carbon                dioxide cracker pre-filled liquid is less than set                pressure of the constant-pressure rupture disk,                after pre-filling is completed. The gas source                system is disconnected from carbon dioxide cracker.                The injection hole is provided at tail end of                carbon dioxide cracker. The high-pressure boost                injection mechanism and high-flow medium-low                pressure injection mechanism are connected in                series through high-pressure hose.
   USE - Method for extracting carbon dioxide-based                phase change coal gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high safety, gas extraction                efficiency and convenient operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of method for extracting carbon dioxide-based phase                change coal gas. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PT P
PN US2020032704-A1; US11231224-B2
TI System used to form low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream involves splitter to divide high pressure carbon dioxide stream into two portions, first expander to cool first portion, heat exchanger to cool second portion and second expander.
AU ALLAM R J
   FORREST B A
   FETVEDT J E
AE ALLAM R J (ALLA-Individual)
   FORREST B A (FORR-Individual)
   FETVEDT J E (FETV-Individual)
   ALLAM R J (ALLA-Individual)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2020094042
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: a splitter configured for dividing a high pressure carbon dioxide stream into a first portion and a second portion; a first expander configured for expanding and cooling the first portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream; a heat exchanger for cooling the second portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream against the cooled first portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream exiting the expander; and a second expander configured for expanding the cooled second portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream so as to form the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream.
   USE - The system is useful for producing a low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes the net carbon dioxide from combustion in a liquid form that is easily transportable; and provides the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream liquid carbon dioxide with high purity, preferably with low levels of oxygen, nitrogen and noble gases (e.g. argon).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing a low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream, comprising providing a high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream at a pressure of greater than or equal to 60 bar (60 MPa), dividing the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream into a bulk portion and a cooling portion, expanding the cooling portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream to reduce the temperature of it to less than or equal to 0 degrees C, cooling the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream to a temperature of less than or equal to 5 degrees C by passing the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream through a heat exchanger against the expanded cooling portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream and expanding the cooled, bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream to a pressure that is less than or equal to 30 bar (3 MPa) but is greater than the triple point pressure of carbon dioxide so as to form the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream, where at least one of the following conditions is met the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream is at a pressure of 60 bar (6 MPa) to 400 bar (40 MPa), the cooling portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream is expanded to reduce the temperature of it to less than or equal to -20 degrees C, the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream is cooled to a temperature of less than or equal to 0 degrees C, the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream is cooled to a temperature of -55 degrees C to 0 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: after the cooling of the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream and prior to the expanding of the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream, passing the bulk portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream through a re-boiler; passing the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream through a separator effective to separate a vapor stream from it, where at least of the following conditions is met the vapor stream comprises up to 8 mass% the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream passed through the separator, the vapor stream comprises 1-75 mass% carbon dioxide and 25-99 mass% of at least one of nitrogen, oxygen and argon; passing the remaining low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream into a stripping column, and the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream exiting the stripping column has an oxygen content of no more than 25 ppm; and using the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream in an enhanced oil recovery process. The method comprises pumping the low pressure liquid carbon dioxide stream to a pressure of at least 100 bar (10 MPa). Preferred Components: The first expander is configured for cooling the first portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream to a temperature of less than or equal to -20 degrees C. The heat exchanger is configured for cooling the second portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream to a temperature of less than or equal to 5 degrees C. The second expander is configured to expand the cooled second portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream to a pressure that is about 30 bar (3 MPa) or less but is greater than the triple point pressure of carbon dioxide. The system further comprises: a combined stripping column and re-boiler. The stripping column is in line downstream from the second expander and the re-boiler is in line downstream from the heat exchanger and upstream from the second expander; a liquid/vapor separator positioned downstream from the second expander and upstream from the stripping column; a compressor configured for receiving the first portion of the high pressure carbon dioxide stream from the heat exchanger; a combustor configured for combusting a carbonaceous or hydrocarbon fuel with oxygen in a combustor in the presence of a recycle carbon dioxide stream at a pressure of greater than or equal to 100 bar (10 MPa) and a temperature of greater than or equal to 400 degrees C to form a combustor exit stream comprising carbon dioxide, a turbine configured for expanding the combustor exit stream to generate power and form a turbine exit stream comprising carbon dioxide, a further heat exchanger configured for cooling the turbine exit stream, and a pump configured for pumping carbon dioxide from the cooled turbine exit stream to form the high pressure carbon dioxide stream. The high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream is a recycle stream from a power production process. The carbon dioxide in the high pressure carbon dioxide containing stream is at least partially derived from combustion of a carbonaceous fuel in the power production process, where one of the following conditions is met the carbonaceous fuel comprises a gaseous fuel and the carbonaceous fuel comprises a solid fuel.
DC Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))
MC Q52-A02; Q52-A03

PT P
PN WO2022178439-A1; US2022267147-A1
TI Producing hydrogen from natural gas for producing            hydrogen and generating electricity, by introducing            feed natural gas, feed steam, and fuel to a reformer to            produce unshifted synthesis gas, removing carbon            dioxide, introducing carbon dioxide depleted syngas,            compressing and introducing to reformer.
AU RAVIKUMAR R
   SINGHAL S
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
   FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 2022A85612
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing hydrogen from natural                gas, involves introducing a feed natural gas, a                feed steam, and a fuel to a reformer to produce                unshifted synthesis gas (syngas), introducing the                unshifted syngas to a water gas shift unit to                produce a shifted syngas, removing carbon dioxide                from the shifted syngas to produce a carbon dioxide                depleted syngas and a carbon dioxide product,                introducing the carbon dioxide depleted syngas to a                pressure swing adsorption unit to produce a                hydrogen product and an off-gas comprising carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, unreacted methane, and                compressing and introducing at least a portion of                the off-gas to the reformer with the feed natural                gas.
   USE - The process is used for producing hydrogen                from natural gas for reforming for producing                hydrogen and generating electricity                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces carbon dioxide emissions,                and improves energy production for such                manufacturing plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a process for reforming for producing hydrogen                and generating electricity, which involves                introducing a feed comprising a hydrocarbon stream                to a reformer to produce unshifted synthesis gas                (syngas), introducing the unshifted syngas to a                water gas shift unit to produce a shifted syngas,                removing carbon dioxide from the shifted syngas to                produce a carbon dioxide depleted syngas and a                carbon dioxide product, introducing the carbon                dioxide depleted syngas to a pressure swing                adsorption unit to produce a hydrogen product and                an off-gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, unreacted methane, splitting a portion of                the hydrogen product, and providing the portion of                the hydrogen product to an electricity generator                for generating electricity for use within the                process; and
   a process for installing an electricity                generator in at reforming unit for producing                hydrogen, where the reforming unit comprises a                reformer, a water gas shift unit, a shifted syngas                cooling unit, and a pressure swing adsorption unit                comprising installing an electricity generator                downstream of the pressure swing adsorption unit to                receive at least a portion of a hydrogen                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process further                  involves splitting the hydrogen product into a                  first hydrogen portion and a second hydrogen                  portion, splitting the off-gas into a first                  off-gas portion and a second off-gas portion,                  combining the second hydrogen portion and the                  second off-gas portion to form a fuel recycle                  stream, where the fuel that is introduced to the                  reformer comprises the fuel recycle stream,                  compressing and introducing the first off-gas                  portion to the reformer with the feed natural                  gas, splitting the carbon dioxide depleted syngas                  into a first carbon dioxide depleted syngas                  portion and a second carbon dioxide depleted                  syngas portion, introducing the first carbon                  dioxide depleted syngas portion to the pressure                  swing adsorption unit to produce a hydrogen                  product and an off-gas comprising carbon                  monoxide, carbon dioxide, unreacted methane;                  introducing the second carbon dioxide depleted                  syngas portion to a membrane unit to produce a                  hydrogen permeate and a retentate, splitting the                  off-gas into a first off-gas portion and a second                  off-gas portion, and combining the hydrogen                  permeate and the second off-gas portion to form a                  fuel recycle stream, where the fuel that is                  introduced to the reformer comprises the fuel                  recycle stream. The process further involves                  sending a gas turbine exhaust to a flue gas                  treatment system, sending at least a portion of                  the electricity to power an air separation unit                  that provides oxygen to the autothermal reformer.                  The air separation unit receives air and produces                  nitrogen and oxygen, where the nitrogen is                  combined with the portion of the hydrogen product                  upstream of the electricity generator. The                  process is integrated in a hydrocarbon                  manufacturing plant and at least a portion of the                  generated electricity is provided to                  manufacturing units outside the process and                  within the hydrocarbon manufacturing plant. The                  process further involves providing air to the                  hydrogen fuel cell and sending from the hydrogen                  fuel cell a fuel cell exhaust comprising water,                  nitrogen, and oxygen to the autothermal reformer,                  cooling the fuel cell exhaust prior to sending to                  the autothermal reformer, and sending at least a                  portion of the fuel cell exhaust to an                  autothermal reformer. The process further                  involves installing an adsorption unit to receive                  an effluent from the shifted syngas cooling unit                  and provide a stream to the hydrogen pressure                  swing adsorption unit and a compressor for                  receiving at least a portion of an off-gas from                  the pressure swing adsorption unit. The process                  further involves installing a cooler downstream                  of the hydrogen fuel cell to receive a fuel cell                  exhaust, and installing a pipe to route the                  cooled fuel cell exhaust to the reformer                  comprising an autothermal reformer, and                  installing an autothermal preheater upstream the                  autothermal reformer. Preferred Components: The                  reformer comprises an autothermal reformer. The                  electricity generator comprises a gas turbine.                  The electricity generator comprises a hydrogen                  fuel cell.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN CN113648782-A; CN113648782-B
TI Carbon dioxide recovery device comprises adsorption device, gas-liquid separation device, gas storage device and liquid storage device connected by pipeline, where adsorption device is connected with exhaust port of oil displacement.
AU GUO X
   LIU X
   ZHANG X
   GAO R
   HOU Y
   CUI Y
   WANG L
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D6182Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovery device comprises adsorption device (1), gas-liquid separation device (2), gas storage device (3) and liquid storage device (4) connected by pipeline. The adsorption device is connected with the exhaust port of the oil displacement well to pass in mixed gas of oil field. The adsorption device is provided with an adsorbent (123). The adsorbent is used to absorb hydrogen sulfide in the mixed gas, and oil field mixed gas after removing the hydrogen sulfide is passed into the gas-liquid separation device. The gas-liquid separation device is used to separate the oilfield mixed gas after hydrogen sulfide is removed to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide. The gas storage device is in communication with the gas-liquid separation device, and is used to store the remaining gas after removing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide. The liquid storage device is in communication with gas-liquid separation device and is used for storing supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery device has efficiently removes hydrogen sulfide in the mixed gas of the oil field, and can also directly separate and obtain supercritical carbon dioxide, and the purity and recovery rate of carbon dioxide are high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recovering carbon dioxide, which involves: (a)using the carbon dioxide recovery device;(b)passing the oilfield mixed gas into the adsorption device, and the adsorbent in the adsorption device is adsorbing the hydrogen sulfide in the oilfield mixed gas;(c)removing hydrogen sulfide;(d)passing the oilfield mixed gas into the gas-liquid separation device;(e)controlling the pressure in the gas-liquid separation device to be 7.3-7.5 megapascal and the temperature is 32-35degrees Celsius;(f)making the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas of the oil field becomes supercritical and enters the liquid storage device, and the remaining gas enters the gas storage device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide recovery device.
   Adsorption device (1)
   Gas-liquid separating device (2)
   Gas storage device (3)
   Liquid storage device (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The adsorbent comprises 40-60 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 40-60 pts. wt. activated carbon, 60-80 pts. wt. impregnation liquid, 30-50 pts. wt. oxidizer and 80-120 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol-based activated carbon fiber. The oilfield mixed gas comprises 50-60% methane, 20-30% carbon dioxide, 8-12% nitrogen, 2-5% hydrogen sulfide, and the rest is water vapor. Preferred Process: The preparation of the adsorbent involves mixing uniformly the diatomaceous earth and activated carbon, grinding to 40-60 mesh, adding the dipping solution, soaking for 2-4 hours, the immersion temperature is 50-90degrees Celsius, drying, modifying at a temperature of 110-150degrees Celsius, the modification time is 1-3 hours, cooling to obtain mixture, mixing uniformly the obtained mixture with the oxidant, heating up to 80-120degrees Celsius, adding polyvinyl alcohol-based activated carbon fiber, grinding to 50-60 mesh, heating in a muffle furnace at a temperature of 180-220degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours, and cooling to obtain the adsorbent. Preferred Conditions: The immersion liquid includes potassium carbonate solution and sodium carbonate solution with volume ratio of (1-3):1. The oxidant comprises aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide and titanium dioxide with weight ratio of (2-4):(1-3):(1-4).

PN CN113446078-A; CN217152054-U
TI Carbon dioxide waste heat power generation and            energy storage system with deep cooling, has            high-pressure liquid pump that is installed at outlet            of carbon dioxide storage tank, and throttling device            that is installed between carbon dioxide storage tank            and cryogenic heat exchanger.
AU HE H
   ZHU C
AE BEIJING ZHONGRE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021B60863
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high-pressure liquid pump (7)                that is installed at the outlet of the carbon                dioxide storage tank (1). A second throttling                device (10) is installed between the outlet of the                carbon dioxide storage tank and the third port of                the cryogenic heat exchanger (9). The fourth port                of the cryogenic heat exchanger is in communication                with the inlet of the compressor of the terminal                compression and cooling combination. The cold                liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage                tank enters the CD channel of the cryogenic heat                exchanger after throttling. The carbon dioxide                storage tank is entered through the second port of                the cryogenic heat exchanger. The carbon dioxide in                the CD channel of the cryogenic heat exchanger                absorbs heat and enters the compressor of the                terminal compression and cooling combination                through the inlet of the compressor of the terminal                compression and cooling combination and carries out                a heat storage cycle.
   USE - Carbon dioxide waste heat power generation and                energy storage system with deep cooling.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-quality waste heat used in the                portion greatly improves the power generation                efficiency. The system makes full use of the waste                heat resources, realizes optimal coordination,                realizes multi-level energy storage and release and                flexibly shifts peaks and fills valleys. The system                is more stable and has large scale, high                efficiency, low cost and environmental protection.                The efficiency, stability and safety of the power                system are improved. The system effectively solves                the problems of wind abandonment and light                abandonment and realizes large-scale consumption of                renewable energy power generation. The heat                transfer temperature difference is reduced and the                energy storage efficiency is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                carbon dioxide waste heat power generation and                energy storage system with deep cooling. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Carbon dioxide gas storage device (2)
   High-pressure liquid pump (7)
   Cryogenic heat exchanger (9)
   Second throttling device (10)

PN US2019241242-A1; US10946944-B2
TI Carbon dioxide cycle power generation system for supplying energy for underwater and unmanned vehicle, has generator for generating electrical power, and annular region including variable internal volume to receive portion of carbon dioxide.
AU HEINEN G W
AE RAYTHEON CO (RAYT-C)
GA 2019685410
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a first carbon dioxide storage for storing a first portion of carbon dioxide. A second carbon dioxide storage stores a second portion of carbon dioxide. A generator (106) generates electrical power based on flow of the carbon dioxide between the first and second carbon dioxide storages, where the carbon dioxide cycle power generation system is configured to cycle between different underwater depths in order to employ water pressure and water temperature in creating the flow of the carbon dioxide through the generator. The second carbon dioxide storage comprises an annular region surrounding a central region. The annular region includes a variable internal volume configured to receive the second portion of the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide cycle power generation system for supplying energy for underwater and unmanned vehicles (UUVs).
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes choked-flow control of pressure equalization, so that optimal turbine operation is enabled, and realizes reliable and easy manufacturing of a compact rotary vane turbine, and higher efficiency of a topping cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for operating a carbon dioxide cycle power generation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide cycle power generation system.
   Carbon dioxide cycle power generation system (100)
   Hydraulic cylinders (101, 102)
   Transfer connection (103)
   Transfer control valve (104)
   Generator (106)

PN WO2022058585-A1
TI Producing high purity carbon dioxide product comprises combining carbon dioxide-rich stream with stream rich in methane and mixing with oxygen, forming carbon dioxide/oxygen-mixture, subjecting carbon dioxide/oxygen mixture to catalytic oxidation and removing water from purified stream.
AU DAHL P J
   GHILADI M
   SAHAI A
   SKOTTE J
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202242979D
AB    NOVELTY - A process (100) for producing a high purity carbon dioxide product comprises (i) providing a carbon dioxide-rich stream (10) containing hydrocarbons, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide, combining it with a stream rich in methane, such as a natural gas stream and mixing it with oxygen, thus forming a carbon dioxide/oxygen mixture (17) (ii) subjecting the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixture to a catalytic oxidation step, thus producing a purified stream (20) having a higher carbon dioxide and/or water concentration, i.e. higher carbon dioxide and/or water concentration than in the carbon dioxide-rich stream prior to or after combining with the stream rich in methane, or in the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixture and (iii) removing water from the purified stream for producing the high purity carbon dioxide product (24).
   USE - The process is used for producing high purity carbon dioxide product. The catox unit is used for purifying carbon dioxide-rich stream containing hydrocarbons, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide, which is derived from a process or plant for producing hydrogen, in particular from a carbon dioxide-removal section, while not increasing the carbon emission of the plant (claimed). The process is used for improving the purity of a stream rich in carbon dioxide such as carbon dioxide-rich stream containing hydrocarbons. The process is used for producing hydrogen from hydrocarbon feed and the hydrogen-rich stream is used as low carbon hydrogen fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces hydrogen while avoiding the increase of carbon dioxide emissions and maintains a carbon recovery in the plant of at least 95%. The generated carbon dioxide is captured in the carbon dioxide-rich stream separated from the low-pressure flash drum, thus increasing the flow of the carbon dioxide-rich stream and avoiding the increase of carbon dioxide emission to the atmosphere. The process enables to achieve the highest carbon dioxide purity without increasing the carbon emission to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant for producing a high purity carbon dioxide product, comprising a conduit for mixing an oxygen stream, preferably oxygen generated from an air separation unit (145) and/or a water/steam electrolysis unit, with a carbon dioxide-rich stream containing hydrocarbons, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide and a conduit for combining a stream rich in methane, such as a natural gas stream, with the carbon dioxide-rich stream, thus forming an inlet gas comprising a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen, a catalytic oxidation (catox) unit (190) arranged to receive the inlet gas comprising a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen, where the catox unit comprises an outlet for withdrawing an outlet gas as a purified stream having a higher carbon dioxide and/or water concentration, a cooling train (200) arranged to receive the outlet gas from the catox unit, the cooling train comprising one or more cooling units for cooling the outlet gas, a condensate separator (210) arranged to receive the cooled outlet gas and for removing water, thus forming an outlet product comprising high purity carbon dioxide product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a layout of an ATR-based hydrogen process and plant with further purification of carbon dioxide-rich stream.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The catalytic oxidation step is conducted in two or more steps with intermediate addition of oxygen. The step (iii) comprises passing the purified stream to a cooling train including one or more cooling units to produce a cooled purified stream, and subsequently passing the cooled purified stream to a condensing step. The cooling train includes a cooling unit using nitrogen from an air separation unit. The oxygen is generated from an air separation unit and/or a water/steam electrolysis unit. The step (iii) further comprises a drying step, preferably after conducting the condensing step. The drying step is conducted in a temperature swing adsorption unit. The carbon dioxide-rich stream of step (i) is derived from a carbon dioxide-removal section. The carbon dioxide-removal section is arranged to receive a shifted synthesis gas stream, in which the carbon dioxide-removal section is an amine wash unit and comprises a carbon dioxide-absorber, a carbon dioxide-stripper and a low-pressure flash drum, from which the carbon dioxide-rich stream is separated. The carbon dioxide-removal section comprises a high-pressure flash drum and the process further comprises adding hydrogen to the carbon dioxide- rich stream. The step (i) comprises supplying a hydrocarbon feed to a reforming section and converting it to a stream of synthesis gas, withdrawing a stream of synthesis gas from the reforming section and supplying it to a shift section, shifting the synthesis gas in a high temperature shift step, and optionally also in a medium temperature shift and/or low temperature shift step, thus providing a shifted synthesis gas stream, supplying the shifted synthesis gas stream from the shift section to a carbon dioxide removal section, suitably the amine wash unit, and separating the carbon dioxide-rich stream from the shifted synthesis gas stream to provide a hydrogen-rich stream. The reforming section comprises autothermal reforming (ATR), and optionally pre-reforming the hydrocarbon feed in one or more pre-reformer units prior to being fed to the ATR. The process further comprises preheating the hydrocarbon feed in one or more fired heaters and feeding at least a part of the hydrogen-rich stream as hydrocarbon fuel to the fired heaters. The process eliminates hydrogen purification step such as pressure swing adsorption (PSA), after the carbon dioxide-removal section.

TI Photo-thermal enhanced ultra-critical carbon            dioxide energy storage system for use in power            generation side, power grid side and user side, has            thermal circulation module for converting heat energy            in expansion module, and cold storage unit provided            with low temperature heat carrier.

   ZHANG J
   ZHANG F
AE ZHONGNENG ANRAN BEIJING ENG TECHNOLOGY (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022C2827J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compression module is for                compressing ultra-critical carbon dioxide,                releasing heat energy and obtaining low temperature                compression high pressure ultra-critical carbon                dioxide. An expansion module absorbs the heat                energy to expand to apply work based on the low                temperature compression high pressure                ultra-critical carbon dioxide. A thermal                circulation module converts the heat energy in the                compression module and the expansion module. A                reinforcing heating module supplements the heat                energy to the expansion module. A low pressure gas                storage unit stores the ultra-critical carbon                dioxide. A cold storage unit is provided with a low                temperature heat carrier.
   USE - Photo-thermal enhanced ultra-critical carbon                dioxide energy storage system for use in a power                generation side, a power grid side and a user                side.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can reduce defect that the gas                storage device in the compression ultra-critical                carbon dioxide energy storage system operated by                sliding pressure of the storage device, so that the                photovoltaic heat can be used, thus enhancing                temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide                expansion of the turbine, and hence improving                system efficiency. The system connects a heat                storage unit and a cold storage unit so as to use                the heat carried by the heat carrier in the heat                exchange device to increase the temperature of the                carbon dioxide in the low temperature compression                high pressure carbon dioxide heating process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                photo-thermal enhanced ultra-critical carbon                dioxide energy storage method.

PN US2022047987-A1
TI Method for extracting carbon dioxide from atmospheric air through pressure-swing absorption, involves transferring second mixture from turbine outlet to low-pressure vessel, and releasing carbon dioxide from working fluid to low-pressure vessel to perform collection process.
AU LEE W A
AE AEOLUS WORKS LLC (AEOL-Non-standard)
GA 202226840J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing ambient air, where the ambient air comprises carbon dioxide and secondary gases with a working fluid to generate a first mixture, and the first mixture comprises a volume of ambient air passed throughout the working fluid. The first mixture is conveyed to pressurize the first mixture within first pressure range through a compressor (120). Absorption of the carbon dioxide present in a volume of air is promoted into the working fluid. The first mixture is transferred from a compressor outlet into a high-pressure vessel. A second mixture is conveyed from the high-pressure vessel. The second mixture is transferred from a turbine outlet to a low-pressure vessel (130) to promote separation of the carbon dioxide from the working fluid. The carbon dioxide is released from the working fluid to the low-pressure vessel to perform collection process. A collection outlet (160) is utilized to release a volume of carbon dioxide from the low-pressure vessel.
   USE - Method for extracting carbon dioxide from atmospheric air through pressure-swing absorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively performing pressure-swing absorption process to the carbon dioxide, so that field of carbon sequestration can be effectively determined. The method enables facilitating faster absorption of the carbon dioxide into the working fluid, and minimizing required energy to compress air in the first mixture required by using the compressor, thus reducing operating costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for extracting carbon dioxide from atmospheric air through pressure-swing absorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a system for extracting carbon dioxide from atmospheric air through pressure-swing absorption.

PN KR2021092935-A; KR2323843-B1
TI Low-temperature sludge dewatering and drying device using supercritical carbon dioxide, has gas-liquid separation device that separates mixture of gas and liquid into carbon dioxide gas and water, and deodorizing device that removes odor component.
AU BAK K T
AE BAK K T (BAKK-Individual)
GA 202187478E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a sludge supply device (110) for supplying the sludge to the sludge dehydration tank. A sludge dewatering reaction tank (120) provides a space for the dewatering process of sludge. A carbon dioxide supply device (130) stores gaseous carbon dioxide and supplies carbon dioxide to a pressurization device (140) which selectively applies pressure to the internal space of the sludge dewatering tank while injecting carbon dioxide supplied from the carbon dioxide supply device into the sludge dewatering tank. A constant temperature device (150) maintains the internal space of the sludge dewatering tank at a constant temperature. A gas-liquid separation device (160) separates a mixture of gas and liquid discharged from the sludge dewatering reactor into carbon dioxide gas and water after the sludge dewatering process is completed. A deodorizing device (170) removes odor components contained in moisture for the moisture separated and discharged through the gas-liquid separator.
   USE - Low-temperature dewatering and drying apparatus for sludge generated during sewage treatment process and food treatment process, using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The water contained in the sludge can be easily dehydrated through the excellent dissolution and penetration properties of supercritical carbon dioxide. The solid-liquid separation is possible not only for the moisture mixed in sludge, but also for moisture contained in microorganisms or fibers, so that the dewatering efficiency of sludge is improved. The odor generation is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a low-temperature sludge dewatering and drying method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the low-temperature sludge dewatering and drying apparatus using supercritical carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Sludge supply device (110)
   Sludge dewatering reaction tank (120)
   Carbon dioxide supply device (130)
   Pressurization device (140)
   Constant temperature device (150)
   Gas-liquid separation device (160)
   Deodorizing device (170)

PN RU2723283-C1
TI LUBRICATION SYSTEM AND METHOD OF LUBRICATING BEARINGS OF A PLANT FOR GENERATING HEAT AND MECHANICAL ENERGY.
AU DATSENKO V V
   KOSOI A A
AE AS RUSSIA HIGH TEMP JOINT INST (ARUS-Non-standard)
GA 202056067U
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to heat engineering, in particular to systems for lubrication of rotating elements of power plants, for example, steam and gas unit for generation of heat and mechanical energy. System for lubrication of bearings of an installation for generating heat and mechanical energy comprises water accumulator (6), the output of which is connected to the input of circulating pump (9) of the water circuit, which is configured to supply water to first (10) and second (11) bearing assemblies of steam-gas turbine (2). Besides, the second bearing assembly (11) of steam-gas turbine (2) is connected to unit (4) of heat and water recycling, which is connected to the input of water accumulator (6). Besides, the lubrication system contains accumulator (8) of liquid carbon dioxide, the output of which is connected to circulating pump (12) of the carbon dioxide circuit, which is made with the possibility of supply of liquid carbon dioxide to first (13) and second (14) bearing assemblies of the carbon dioxide compressor (3). Second bearing assembly (14) of carbon dioxide compressor (3) is connected to tank-separator (7) so that carbon dioxide can be supplied into it, and separator tank (7) contains two outputs and is made so, to enable supply of liquid carbon dioxide through first outlet and pump (15) for discharging liquid carbon dioxide from separator tank (7) to unit (5) for recycling heat and carbon dioxide, and is also configured to feed gaseous carbon dioxide through a second outlet and a line for discharging gaseous carbon dioxide (16) into a gas outlet system of the plant for generating thermal and mechanical energy before carbon dioxide compressor (3). Each bearing assembly comprises at least one bearing. Also disclosed is a method of lubricating bearings of an apparatus for generating heat and mechanical energy.
   USE - Heat power engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in reduction of heat stress of moving elements of power plant structure, increase in efficiency of using media circulating in plant, as well as increase in reliability of installation.

A function accepting portion is provided for storing carbonated water which is constructed so as to be openable and closable by a lid equipped with a check valve, and configured to inject high-pressure carbon dioxide transferred from the water. A function of generating carbon dioxide through a chemical reaction is installed, such that the carbonated water is produced.

The aqueous sodium citrate solution produced as a by-product is used as a disinfectant for cleaning the bathroom or a natural detergent for laundry, which is more economical.

PN CN115028614-A
TI Extracting cannabidiol from industrial hemp useful            in cosmetic, health care product and medical field,            comprises processing industrial hemp, dissolving            supercritical carbon dioxide liquid in extraction            kettle, separating plant extract from carbon dioxide            liquid, and returning carbon dioxide gas.
AU HU Z
   LI Y
   MO J
   ZHOU Y
AE SHENZHEN TIANCHENG MINGDAO MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022C1734Y
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting cannabidiol from industrial hemp                comprises (i) converting carbon dioxide gas into                liquid to reach a supercritical state, (ii)                processing the raw material industrial hemp to                reach the required quality state, (iii) dissolving                the supercritical carbon dioxide liquid in the                extraction kettle and collecting industrial hemp                extract, and extracting, (iv) automatically                separating the plant extract from the carbon                dioxide liquid, where the supercritical fluid that                extracts the solute at a predetermined temperature                and pressure enters the separation tank through the                expansion valve, and reducing the pressure to make                the carbon dioxide liquid into a gas, and quickly                forming a gas and liquid separation from the                extract, and (v) returning carbon dioxide gas to                the main storage tank, the extract, i.e.                cannabidiol oil, releasing from the separator under                reduced pressure, and returning carbon dioxide gas                to the extraction kettle through the compressor for                recycling.
   USE - The method for extracting cannabidiol from                industrial hemp useful in cosmetic, health care                product and medical field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a cannabidiol extraction rate                of more than 90% and a cannabidiol content of                10-20%, is scientific and has good craftsmanship,                reduces product environmental pollution and                achieves the purpose of organic green biological                extraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting cannabidiol from industrial hemp                comprises (i) converting carbon dioxide gas into                liquid to reach a supercritical state by (a)                preparing carbon dioxide in the compression                chamber, cooling to less than -56&#8451;,                increasing the pressure to more than 75 Mpa, and                converting the carbon dioxide gas into liquid, and                (b) increasing the temperature and pressure so the                temperature exceeds 31&#8451; and the pressure                exceeds 7.38 Mpa until the carbon dioxide liquid                becomes a supercritical state, where the                supercritical carbon dioxide liquid is capable of                filling the container while maintaining its                density, i.e. liquid state, when the carbon dioxide                liquid is in a supercritical state, it is the most                ideal choice for chemical extraction, because it                will not cause denaturation and damage, (ii)                processing the raw material industrial hemp to                reach the required quality state by (c) using the                industrial hemp mosaic leaves in the full bloom                period, and drying and roasting so the moisture                content is within the specified range, and the lime                soil content is below the required regulations,                after vacuum drying, the temperature and time are                between the required regulations, so the moisture                of industrial hemp mosaic leaves is within the                required range, and (d) crushing the dried                industrial hemp leaves with a crusher to achieve                the required shredding, (iii) dissolving the                supercritical carbon dioxide liquid in the                extraction kettle and collecting industrial hemp                extract, and extracting by separation kettle and                rectification column to separate the extract by (e)                adding the pretreated industrial hemp leaves into                the extraction kettle whose carbon dioxide liquid                is in a supercritical state, (f) because the carbon                dioxide liquid is in a supercritical state and is                fully in contact with the industrial hemp material,                extracting in the extraction kettle under the                specified temperature and pressure to form an                extraction liquid, (g) pumping required amount of                ethanol into the extraction kettle through the                solvent pump to increase the solubility of the                extract and improve the extraction rate, (h) adding                extraction liquid into the separation kettle                through valve control, and adjusting pressure and                temperature of the separation kettle equipment, so                the extraction liquid is separated in the                separation kettle, (i) adding extraction liquid                into separation kettle, i.e. the extraction liquid,                controlling by a valve and entering rectifying                column, and adjusting required pressure and                temperature in the rectifying column for further                enrichment to improve the extraction rate of                cannabidiol, (iv) when carbon dioxide that becomes                a liquid is reversed into the low-pressure                separator through the valve control, and                automatically separating the plant extract from the                carbon dioxide liquid, where the supercritical                fluid that extracts the solute at a predetermined                temperature and pressure enters the separation tank                through the expansion valve, and reducing the                pressure to make the carbon dioxide liquid into a                gas, and quickly forming a gas and liquid                separation from the extract, and (v) returning                carbon dioxide gas to the main storage tank, the                extract, i.e. cannabidiol oil, releasing from the                separator under reduced pressure, and returning                carbon dioxide gas to the extraction kettle through                the compressor for recycling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method                for extracting cannabidiol (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (c), in                  the industrial hemp mosaic, after drying and                  roasting, the moisture content is 15-20%, and the                  lime soil content is less than 5%, and vacuum                  drying at 150-180&#8451; for 20-40 minutes,                  and keeping the moisture content of industrial                  hemp leaves to 5-8%. In the step (d), during                  crushing the dried industrial hemp leaves with                  crusher, the crushing degree of the crushing is                  10-15 meshes.

AE SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)

GA 201835211M
AB    NOVELTY - The method of separating carbon dioxide, involves (A) passing the carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas into absorption tower, (B) contacting with absorption liquid to obtain purified gas and carbon dioxide rich liquid, (C) heating carbon dioxide rich liquid to obtain thermal transfer rich liquid, depressurizing the thermal transfer rich liquid, passing into low pressure flash tank for gas-liquid separation to obtain flash gas and flash product, (D) passing flash product into desorption tower for regeneration of absorption liquid to obtain regeneration gas and carbon dioxide lean solution, cooling the lean solution, absorbing carbon dioxide, removing carbon dioxide from middle of desorption column, cooling the semi-lean solution, carrying out heat exchange, returning lean liquid to desorption tower and regenerating absorbent.
   USE - Method of separating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating carbon dioxide with high purity by simple, highly efficient and economical process.
