TI Producing synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide from gases containing carbon dioxide, comprises subjecting gases containing carbon dioxide to scrubbing, and chemically reacting stripping gas with desorbed carbon dioxide.
AU MAASS H
   MACHHAMMER O
   MOCKEL H
AE MAASS H (MAAS-Individual)
GA 202074056G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from gases containing carbon dioxide, comprises subjecting the gases containing carbon dioxide to scrubbing, where the carbon dioxide is absorbed by a detergent and then desorbed by stripping, and chemically reacting the stripping gas containing components with the desorbed carbon dioxide in a subsequent process step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents the climate-damaging carbon dioxide from escaping into the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stripping gas comprises hydrogen and/or hydrogen-containing gases. The stripping gas has methane and/or gases containing methane. Preferred Method: The hydrogen-containing stripping gas is obtained by pyrolysis and/or a steam-methane reforming process from hydrocarbon-containing components. The hydrogen-containing stripping gas is obtained by electrolysis of water. The following process step comprises a dry reforming process to generate synthesis gas. The carbon dioxide absorbed by the detergent is desorbed in a first step by increasing the temperature of the detergent and in a second step by the stripping gas. The feed gas consisting of methane and/or hydrocarbon-containing gases is passed over hot coke from a carbonization before use in the stripping process and the hydrocarbons are partially pyrolyzed to carbon and hydrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202241130G

The flue gas and oxygen/oxygen-rich in carbon-based gas mixer (5) is circulated to mix uniformly to obtain the carbon-based gas. The carbon-based gas is introduced into gas heat exchanger (6) to be heated and sent to coal burning boiler after waste heat. The hydrogen introduced into gas heat exchanger prepared by water electrolysis hydrogen production device (1) is heated by waste heat flue gas and sent to coal burning boiler (7). The rest portion of the desulfurized circulating flue gas is waste heated, dehydrated and introduced into carbon dioxide purifying device (13). The carbon dioxide compressor (9) is used to pressurize and deliver the carbon dioxide product to downstream carbon dioxide using device. The nitrogen prepared by oxygen/oxygen enriched preparation device (2) is pressurized and conveyed to downstream nitrogen utilization device by nitrogen compressor (10).

PN CN111440303-A
TI Preparing polycarbonate by circulating loop type gas-liquid contact process, comprises e.g. polymerizing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide under carbon dioxide pressure and in presence of bi-functional catalyst.
AU REN W
   LV X
   LI M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2020717228
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polycarbonate by circulating loop type gas-liquid contact process, comprises taking circulating loop reactor having functionalized tetradentate Schiff alkali metal complex as bi-functional catalyst, and polymerizing under carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-5.5 MPa at 20-120 degrees C with carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw materials to obtain polycarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polycarbonate by circulating loop type gas-liquid contact process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves defect of reaction rate drops sharply after the alkylene oxide conversion rate, compared existing method; realizes product synthesis under low carbon dioxide pressure; improves efficiency of the polymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polycarbonate by circulating loop type gas-liquid contact process, comprises taking circulating loop reactor having functionalized tetradentate Schiff alkali metal complex as bi-functional catalyst, and polymerizing under carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-5.5 MPa at 20-120 degrees C with carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw materials to obtain polycarbonate. The specific reaction process are by adding initial circulating material containing bi-functional catalyst into circulating loop reactor where the initial circulating material may or may not contain the initiator, then heating initial circulating material to the reaction temperature through heat exchanger after the circulation is turned on, and entering carbon dioxide to the reaction pressure, then passing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into the circulating loop reactor to continue the reaction and maintaining the reaction pressure unchanged after the addition of alkylene oxide is completed and the reaction reaches the expected carbon dioxide consumption, then transferring the reaction material to flash tank, recovering unreacted alkylene oxide, and vacuum drying the product to constant weight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bi-functional catalyst is tetradentate Schiff alkali metal complex and contains quaternary ammonium salt or quaternary phosphonium salt group in molecule, where the tetradentate Schiff alkali metal complex is of formulae (I)-(III). The initiator is monohydric, dihydric, polyhydric alcohol or large molecular weight alcohol, and the molecular weight of the large molecular weight alcohol is 34-2000. The circulation loop is circulating spray or cycle injection. The circulation loop is circulating spray or cycle injection. The molar ratio between bi-functional catalyst and alkylene oxide is 1:1000-1:200000, preferebly 1:1000-1:200000. The alkylene oxide is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide or cyclohexane oxide.M = Fe3+, Co3+, Ni3+, Cr3+, Mn3+ or Al3+;R1-R2 = H, CH3, CH2CH3 or phenyl;R3 = H, 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, Cl, Br or NO2 group;R4-R5 = H or 1-6C alkyl;R6 = 1-6C alkyl or phenyl;n = 0-10;Z = nitrogen or phosphorus; andX and X1 = F-1, Cl-1, Br-1, I-1, NO3-1, CH3COO-1, CCl3COO-1, CF3COO-1, ClO4-1, BF4-1, BPh4-1, N3-1, p-toluate, p-toluenesulfonate, o-nitrophenoloxy, p-nitrophenoloxy, m-nitrophenoloxy, 2,4-dinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dinitrophenoloxy, 2,4,6-trinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dichlorophenoloxy, 3,5-difluorophenoloxy, 3,5-di-trifluoromethylphenoloxy or pentafluorophenoloxy anion.

AB    NOVELTY - Converting gas comprising carbon dioxide using                a cathode of an electrolysis cell, comprises                converting gas comprising carbon dioxide,                preferably comprising product gas comprising carbon                monoxide. The gas comprising hydrogen is converted                into a product comprising protons at an anode of                the electrolytic cell. The cathode comprises a                metal including silver, gold, zinc, lead, copper                and/or alloys.
   USE - The method is useful for producing product gas                comprising carbon monoxide from gas comprising                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for converting gas comprising carbon dioxide                into product gas, comprising a first electrolytic                cell having a first cathode compartment with a                first cathode which comprises a metal including                silver, gold, zinc, lead, copper and/or alloys and                which is designed into convert a gas comprising                carbon dioxide, preferably into a product gas                comprising carbon monoxide, and a first anode space                comprising a first anode to convert a gas                comprising hydrogen to a product comprising                protons, a first supply device (1) for the gas                comprising carbon dioxide to supply the gas                comprising carbon dioxide to the first cathode                compartment of the first electrolytic cell, a                second supply device (3) for the gas comprising                hydrogen to supply the gas comprising hydrogen to                the first anode space of the first electrolytic                cell, a first discharge device (2) for the product                gas, preferably the product gas comprising carbon                monoxide to discharge the product gas, preferably                the product gas comprising carbon monoxide from the                first cathode compartment, and a second discharge                device (4) for a gas comprising carbon dioxide to                discharge a gas comprising carbon dioxide from the                first anode space.

   First supply device (1)
   First discharge device (2)
   Second supply device (3)
   Second discharge device (4)
   Electrolyte reservoir (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode comprises a                  metal including lead, platinum, nickel,                  ruthenium, rhodium, iron, copper, cobalt,                  titanium, chromium and/or molybdenum, and oxides,                  alloys and/or organometallic systems including                  porphyrin, preferably lead, platinum, nickel,                  ruthenium, rhodium, iron, copper, cobalt,                  titanium and/or chromium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further                  comprises a second electrolytic cell to produce                  the gas comprising hydrogen from water, and a                  third discharge device to discharge the gas                  comprising hydrogen from the second electrolytic                  cell. The third discharge device is connected to                  a second supply device. The first cathode and/or                  the first anode are designed as a gas diffusion                  electrode. The first anode and the first cathode                  are separated by a first membrane, preferably an                  anion exchange membrane. The first anode and the                  first cathode abut the first membrane. The second                  discharge device is connected to the first feed                  device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate ions                  and/or hydrogen carbonate ions are formed on the                  cathode, which react with the protons of the                  anode to form water and carbon dioxide. The gas                  comprising hydrogen reacted at the anode is                  produced by water electrolysis. The cathode                  and/or anode are designed as a gas diffusion                  electrode. The anode and the cathode are                  separated by a membrane, preferably an anion                  exchange membrane. The anode and the cathode                  abutted the membrane. An excess of hydrogen                  compared to the conversion rate of carbon dioxide                  at the cathode is added into the anode and carbon                  dioxide produced at the anode is fed to the                  cathode with the remaining hydrogen, where a gas                  comprising synthesis gas is formed on the                  cathode.

   USE - Agent is used for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed) during manufacture of semiconductor.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capture agent enables capture of carbon dioxide with high carbon dioxide capture efficiency, even when carbon dioxide partial pressure is low, by simple and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for capturing carbon dioxide, which involves using the carbon dioxide capture agent in an atmosphere of 300-650 degrees C.

PT P
PN WO2022032338-A1
TI Silica-based microporous aerogel used in formation of aerogel composite used for adsorbing carbon dioxide from gaseous stream or atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, comprises pores having preset diameter.
AU KONSTAS K
   MAURDEV G
   WANG X
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 202226599P
AB    NOVELTY - A silica-based microporous aerogel comprises pores, where at least 50% of the pores have a diameter of 2 nm or less.
   USE - Silica-based microporous aerogel used in formation of aerogel composite used for adsorbing carbon dioxide from gaseous stream or atmosphere containing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The silica-based microporous aerogel provides aerogel composite with excellent carbon dioxide absorption efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an aerogel composite, which comprises the microporous aerogel, 5-35 wt.% additive, lubricant, and a solvent;
   (2) preparation of the silica-based microporous aerogel, which involves mixing an aqueous solution comprising at least one amino-substituted silane, at least one silicate, at least one alkyl substituted silane, a buffer, one or more additives, and a solvent system, to form a wet gel matrix, and drying the wet-gel matrix to provide a dried silica-based aerogel, where drying does not involve supercritical carbon dioxide;
   (3) capturing of carbon dioxide from a gaseous stream or atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the gaseous stream or atmosphere with an aerogel for capturing at least some carbon dioxide from the gaseous stream or atmosphere; and
   (4) carbon dioxide adsorption apparatus, which comprises a chamber enclosing at least one silica-based aerogel, an inlet through which gaseous stream can flow to the silica-based aerogel and an outlet through which the effluent gaseous stream can flow out from the silica-based aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method further involves rinsing the wet-gel matrix. The process is a sol gel process. The drying process involves heating the wet gel matrix to obtain a gel, applying wet gel matrix to a substrate to form a wet-gel film coating the substrate, and drying the wet-gel film by solvent evaporation and/or heat treatment to provide a dried silica-based aerogel. The drying involves vacuum drying or freeze drying. The process further involves preparing an aerogel composite by pressing a mixture consisting of an aerogel or their combination, additives and a lubricant into a pellet, and liquid extruding the mixture to provide a viscous paste, and activating. The capturing of carbon dioxide from a gaseous stream or atmosphere containing carbon dioxide further involves regeneration recovery process by heating the aerogel to 60-140 degrees C to desorb the absorbed carbon dioxide from the aerogel under reduced pressure flow of a gas with low carbon dioxide. The aerogel is heated by contacting the aerogel with steam. Preferred Apparatus: The silica-based aerogel is located between the inlet and outlet of the chamber and the inlet and outlet are at opposite ends of the chamber. The chamber comprises silica based aerogel in the form of a pellet. The chamber comprises a substrate coated with the silica based aerogel located or packed within the chamber. The apparatus comprises two or more chambers enclosing at least one silica-based aerogel connected in parallel to the gaseous stream. The apparatus comprises at least three chambers enclosing at least one silane-based aerogel in each chamber, where each chamber is connected in parallel to the gaseous stream. The silica-based aerogel enclosed within the at least three chambers is operated in different sections of the adsorption and regeneration cycle to produce a continuous flow of the effluent gaseous stream. The effluent gaseous stream from the outlet flows to a variety of secondary processes. Preferred Components: The aerogel comprises a reaction product of at least one amino substituted silane, at least one silicate, and optionally at least one alkyl substituted silane. The amino substituted silane is of formula: R1Si(OR2)nL-NH2 (I). The alkyl substituted silane is of formula: R5mSi(OR6)n (II). The microporous aerogel further comprises the reaction product of at least one silicate of formula: Si(OR7)4 (III). The aerogel is a self-supporting aerogel. The aerogel comprises one or more additives chosen from a buffer, a binder, optionally a metal organic framework (MOF), and a nanoparticle. The reaction product is prepared through a one-pot synthesis. The aerogel is in the form of a composite. The composite further comprises one or more additives, and a lubricant. The composite is self-supporting in the form of a pellet, bead, sheet or granule. The composite is applied as a coating composition, paste or film on a substrate. The binder is chosen from cellulose-based polymers, silane-based polymers, cellulose-siloxane-based polymers, polyglycol-based polymers, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene imine, bentonite, graphite, or their combinations. The solvent is water, a non-aqueous solvent, or their combination.R1 = optionally 1-6C alkyl;R2 = 1-6C alkyl;L = -(1-6C)alkyl-linker group between Si and N;n = 2 or 3;R5,R6 = 1-6C alkyl;m = 1;andR7 = 1-6C alkyl.Preferred Composition: The aerogel adsorbs carbon dioxide from the air in environments with a carbon dioxide concentration of 10 vol.% or less, preferably 0.04 vol.% or less. The carbon dioxide adsorption of the aerogel is at least 0.47 mmol/g. The loading amount of amino substituted silane is at least 40 mol%, preferably 60 mol% or less. The loading amount of alkyl substituted silane is at least 10 mol%, preferably 40 mol% or less. The loading amount of silicate is at least 20 mol%, preferably 40 mol% or less. The content of amino silane group is effective for providing free amine groups for facilitating carbon dioxide capture is 60 mol% or less. The amino substituted silane comprises 40-60 mol% 3-aminopropyl(diethoxy)methylsilane and 40-60 mol%3-aminopropyl(triethoxy)silane. The amino substituted silane comprises 40 mol% 3-aminopropyl(triethoxy)silane. The alkyl substituted silane is 40 mol% triethoxymethylsilane. The silicate is 20 mol% tetraethyl orthosilicate. The content of the additive is 5-35 wt.% based on the total weight of the aerogel. The gaseous stream or atmosphere has a carbon dioxide concentration of 150000 ppm or less, preferably 2000 ppm or less. Preferred Properties: The density of the aerogel is 0.02-0.6 g/cm3, preferably 0.2-0.6 g/cm3. The aerogel has a surface area of 0.1-500 m2/g, preferably 2-200 m2/g, pore size of 0.1-2 nm.

PN CN113385141-A; CN113385141-B
TI Increasing carbon dioxide concentration of plant cultivation facilities and growth of crops by applying ZSM-5 molecular sieve, by pretreating ZSM-5 molecular sieve, and transferring pretreated ZSM-5 molecular sieve to facility cultivation environment.
AU SHI K
   WANG A
   WANG L
   WANG H
   LI J
   QI Z
   YU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021A8642S
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of plant cultivation facilities and the growth of crops by applying ZSM-5 molecular sieve, comprises (1) pretreating ZSM-5 molecular sieve to make it fully absorb carbon dioxide, and (2) transferring the pretreated ZSM-5 molecular sieve to a facility cultivation environment where the carbon dioxide concentration is less than or equal to 480 ppm, and allowing to stand the ZSM-5 molecular sieve to release carbon dioxide, increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in the facility cultivation environment and promoting the growth of crops.
   USE - The method is used for increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of plant cultivation facilities and the growth of crops by applying ZSM-5 molecular sieve, where the crop is tomato (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The enrichment of carbon dioxide concentration in the facility cultivation environment can be achieved under normal temperature and normal pressure, where the facility cultivation environment using pre-treated ZSM-5 molecular sieve has better crop growth, increased stem thickness and increased fresh weight. The method is simple and safe to operate, environmentally-friendly, and low in energy consumption. The ZSM-5 molecular sieve can be reused, and after reuse, it still maintains a relatively stable adsorption and desorption capacity for carbon dioxide, where, compared with other carbon dioxide enrichment methods, such as combustion gas method and chemical reaction method, it is more in line with the needs of low-carbon agriculture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (1) comprises filling the enclosed space with ZSM-5 molecular sieve with carbon dioxide, and leaving still for 0.1-12 hours. The pretreatment method comprises charging carbon dioxide into a closed space equipped with ZSM-5 molecular sieve and leaving still in a sealed state for 1-4 hours. In step (2), after the ZSM-5 molecular sieve is allowed to stand for at least 3 hours to release carbon dioxide, it is taken out and returned to step (1), and the process is repeated for 1-10 times. The ZSM-5 molecular sieve is regenerated by high-temperature drying after repeated use for 3 times, and then used continuously. In step (1), the pretreatment method comprising filling carbon dioxide into a closed space equipped with ZSM-5 molecular sieve, and sealing and leaving still for 1-4 hours. Preferred Condition: The high-temperature drying and regeneration conditions are 100-300 degrees C for 1-48 hours. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of the ZSM-5 molecular sieve to the carbon dioxide charging amount is (0.01-100):1000. The volume ratio of the ZSM-5 molecular sieve to the amount of carbon dioxide charged is 0.1-4:1000. In step (2), the dosage of ZSM-5 molecular sieve is 30-100 cm3 per cubic meter of facility cultivation environment. The volume ratio of the ZSM-5 molecular sieve to the carbon dioxide charge is (1-4):1000. In step (2), the amount of ZSM-5 molecular sieve is 40-90 cm3 per cubic meter of facility cultivation environment.

GA 2020C1365B

One end of carbon dioxide heat pump is respectively connected to upper portion of hot water electric valve (5), heating electric valve (6) and heating water tank (3). One end of heating electric valve is further connected with heating radiator through heating pipeline. One end of hot water electric valve is further connected with lower portion of heating water tank. One end of heating radiator is connected with carbon dioxide heat pump by heating circulating pump, soft water device and hot water electromagnetic valve. The demineralized water device is further connected with lower portion of hot water tank.

PT P
PN WO2018182506-A1; AU2018247175-A1; SG11201908958-A1; SG10202110648-A
TI Converting carbon dioxide into a solid carbonate, comprises contacting aqueous ammonia with carbon dioxide and reacting ammonium carbonate solution with ultramafic rock material to form solid carbonate and regenerate aqueous ammonia.
AU BU J
   YEO T Y
AE AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
   AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
GA 201877569R
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide into a solid carbonate, comprises: contacting aqueous ammonia with carbon dioxide to form an ammonium carbonate solution; and reacting the ammonium carbonate solution with an ultramafic rock material to form the solid carbonate and regenerate aqueous ammonia, where the ultramafic rock material is thermally activated prior to the reacting step.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon dioxide into a solid carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is conducted at mild conditions such as but not restricted to, room temperature and atmospheric pressure, by reacting heat activated or thermally activated ultramafic minerals with ammonium carbonate; entails the formation of a solid carbonate/silica mixture, as well as regeneration of the aqueous ammonia that is recycled for further carbon dioxide capture i.e. allows the recovery of the aqueous ammonia that is not lost through absorption or other means into the product stream; avoids the use of additives such as sodium-based additives, that are non-regenerating, and require much harsher reaction conditions to carry out the carbonation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for converting carbon dioxide into a solid carbonate, comprising carbon dioxide scrubber configured to contact aqueous ammonia with carbon dioxide to form ammonium carbonate solution and reactor in fluid communication with the carbon dioxide scrubber, where the reactor is configured to react the ammonium carbonate solution with an ultramafic rock material to form the solid carbonate and regenerate aqueous ammonia, where the ultramafic rock material is thermally activated prior to the reacting step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reacting step is carried out at 20-80 degrees C. The reacting step is earned out at room temperature. The reacting step is carried out at 1-5 atmosphere. The reacting step is carried out at atmospheric pressure. The ultramafic rock material is grounded prior to the reacting step. The ultramafic rock material is grounded to 50-1000 mu (preferably 100-600) prior to the reacting step. The ultramafic rock material is thermally activated by heating at 500-1000 degrees C (preferably 550-750 degrees C) prior to the reacting step. The ultramafic rock material is thermally activated by heating for 3-8 hours preferably 4-6 hours. The method further comprises stirring the ammonium carbonate solution with the thermally activated ultramafic rock material during the reacting step and separating the solid carbonate from the regenerated aqueous ammonia after the formation. The solid carbonate is separated by filtration. The regenerated aqueous ammonia is collected for reuse. Preferred Components: The ultramafic rock material comprises serpentine (preferred), tremolite, talc, olivine, orthopyroxene, clinopyroxene and/or any other types of mineral with high alkaline earth metal oxide content (more than 20% magnesium oxide or calcium oxide) available for reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises furnace in fluid communication with the reactor, where the furnace is configured to heat up the ultramafic rock material at 500-1000 degrees C, grinder or mill to grind the ultramafic rock material to 50-1000 mu , and separator in fluid communication with the reactor, where the separator is configured to separate the solid carbonate from the regenerated aqueous ammonia after formation. The separator is in fluid communication with the carbon dioxide scrubber to recycle the regenerated aqueous ammonia.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN JP2018130386-A; JP7003416-B2
TI Hot-water supply apparatus has carbon-dioxide production apparatus which comprises adsorption unit for adsorbing in-air carbon dioxide, and heating unit which heats adsorption unit so that adsorbed carbon dioxide is released.
AU FURUHASHI T
   TAKEUCHI S
   NAKAKITA M
AE MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
   MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
GA 201865418V
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1A) has a gas-liquid mixing apparatus (12) which mixes gas supplied through an intake passage (13) with water. A carbon-dioxide production apparatus (14) is capable of supplying carbon dioxide into the intake passage. The carbon-dioxide production apparatus comprises an adsorption unit for adsorbing in-air carbon dioxide. A heating unit heats the adsorption unit so that carbon dioxide adsorbed by the adsorption unit is released. The adsorption unit comprises an air filter containing an amine compound. The adsorption unit comprises the air filter containing an alkylamine derivative. At least a portion of the air filter has a honeycomb form or a corrugated shape. The temperature of the adsorption unit when heated by the heating unit is within the range of 40 degrees C to 100 degrees C. The gas-liquid mixing apparatus is arranged in a circular flow path connected to a bathtub. The circular flow path includes a reheating heat exchanger (4a) for heating bath water.
   USE - Hot-water supply apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The at least one of low noise, space saving, and reduction in maintenance in the hot-water supply apparatus that can mix carbon dioxide into hot/cold water can be achieved. The warm bath effect can be improved, since foam adheres to the skin of the bather in the bathtub easily.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the hot-water supply apparatus.
   Hot-water supply apparatus (1A)
   Reheating heat exchanger (4a)
   Gas-liquid mixing apparatus (12)
   Intake passage (13)
   Carbon-dioxide production apparatus (14)

PT P
PN KR2022042880-A
TI Producing methanol, comprises e.g. supplying raw landfill gas to methane/carbon-dioxide separation process, supplying carbon-dioxide stream and syngas to carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process in a combined state to produce methanol.
AU KIM J Y
AE UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
GA 202248520P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol, comprises supplying raw landfill gas to a methane/carbon-dioxide separation process; directly feeding carbon-dioxide stream separated in the methane/carbon-dioxide separation process to a carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process; subjecting methane stream containing nitrogen, which is another stream separated in the methane/carbon-dioxide separation process to a nitrogen removal process, and then subjecting to methane reforming (MR) process; supplying syngas including hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon-dioxide converted in the MR process to the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process; and supplying the carbon-dioxide stream and syngas to carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process in a combined state to produce methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces municipal solid waste (MSW); is environmentally-friendly and economical; utilizes only the carbon source of landfill gas (LFG) can be completely upcycled to produce methanol; has carbon and energy efficiency of the L2M process reaching upto 92% and 69%, respectively, with methanol UPC estimated at 392-440 USD/tonne, and LFG is completely upcycled in methanol, with carbon-dioxide emissions of 0.83 kg and 0.29 kg of carbon-dioxide per kg methanol of L2M-HS and L2M-SA, respectively.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises supplying additional hydrogen to the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process to produce methanol together with the carbon-dioxide stream and syngas. The syngas converted in the methane reforming process is supplied to the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation feeding process; and carbon monoxide separated in the carbon monoxide separation process is supplied to a water gas shift (WGS) device. The hydrogen and carbon-dioxide separated in the carbon monoxide separation process and hydrogen and carbon-dioxide generated in the WGS device are merged with the carbon-dioxide stream (carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process) to produce methanol. The carbon-dioxide stream is partially fed to the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process. The methanol produced in the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process is additionally supplied to the purification process. The nitrogen removal process uses a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) method. The carbon monoxide separation process uses pressure swing adsorption (PSA) method. The production of methanol in the carbon-dioxide hydrogenation process is performed with a fibrous copper/zinc/aluminum/zirconium catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PT P
PN RD700046-A
TI Removal of long-chain hydrocarbons in direct            synthesis of dimethyl ether, involves separating            carbonaceous feedstock into e.g. hydrogen, carbon            monoxide and carbon dioxide and converting into            methanol, dehydrating methanol, passing into column,            and separating unsaturated hydrocarbons.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2022A1526M
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of long-chain hydrocarbons involves                separating a carbonaceous feedstock e.g. natural                gas by appropriate technology e.g. steam reforming                into hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,                converting hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide into a synthesis gas, converting the                synthesis gas into methanol, dehydrating methanol                in the presence of a catalytic support to obtain                dimethyl ether as a main product, cooling the main                product by heat integration with a reaction feed                gas, passing into a column (c1) to obtain an                overhead stream which is a dimethyl ether-rich                condensate and a bottom stream comprising methanol                and water, gradually cooling the overhead stream                and passing into a column (c2) to obtain an                overhead stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen                and carbon monoxide and a bottom stream comprising                dimethyl ether and unsaturated hydrocarbons (6C                components), and performing liquid-liquid                separation of the bottom stream to remove                unsaturated hydrocarbons.
   USE - Removal of long-chain hydrocarbons in direct                synthesis of dimethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient removal of                long-chain hydrocarbons in direct synthesis of                dimethyl ether with increased yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removal (P1) of long-chain hydrocarbons                involves separating a carbonaceous feedstock e.g.                natural gas by appropriate technology e.g. steam                reforming into hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide, converting hydrogen, carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide into a synthesis gas, converting the                synthesis gas into methanol, dehydrating methanol                in the presence of a catalytic support to obtain                dimethyl ether as a main product and trace                components including argon, nitrogen, oxygen,                helium or other inert gases, alcohol, aldehyde,                ketone, ester, ethereal olefin, alkane and other                hydrocarbons by-products, cooling the main product                by heat integration with a reaction feed gas,                passing into a column (c1) to obtain an overhead                stream which is a dimethyl ether-rich condensate                and a bottom stream comprising methanol and water,                gradually cooling the overhead stream and passing                into a column (c2) to obtain an overhead stream                comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and carbon                monoxide and a bottom stream comprising dimethyl                ether and unsaturated hydrocarbons (6C components),                and performing liquid-liquid separation of the                bottom stream to remove unsaturated hydrocarbons.                When the ratio of difference between stoichiometric                number of hydrogen and carbon dioxide with respect                to difference between stoichiometric number of                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is 2 or more, 2                mol of carbon monoxide reacts with 4 mol of                hydrogen to give 1 mol of dimethyl ether and 1 mol                of water with a change in enthalpy during reaction                of -205 kJ/mol at 298 K, and 2 mol of carbon                dioxide reacts with 6 mol of hydrogen to give 1 mol                of dimethyl ether and 3 mol of water with a change                in enthalpy during reaction of -123 kJ/mol at 298                K. When the ratio of difference between                stoichiometric number of hydrogen and carbon                dioxide with respect to difference between                stoichiometric number of carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide is 1, 3 mol of carbon monoxide reacts with                3 mol of hydrogen to give 1 mol of dimethyl ether                and 1 mol of carbon dioxide with a change in                enthalpy during reaction of -246 kJ/mol at 298 K.                In the process (P1), carbon dioxide is separated by                chemical wash and by recycling or discarding                synthesis gas. To increase the yield of dimethyl                ether, unreacted synthesis gas is partially used as                recycling stream.

   USE - Method for recovering various gases in                catalytic dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recovering various gases in                catalytic dry gas at large scale by realizing                formation and decomposition of gas hydrates,                separation of gas mixture and purification and                recovery of gas components and high concentration                of recovered gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A various gases recovering method involves                passing catalytic dry gas from raw material tank to                first decomposition unit and first hydrate reaction                tower, reacting to obtain two streams, such that                one is a gaseous stream of hydrogen gas drawn from                top of reaction column into hydrogen-rich collector                and other is slurry formed by remaining hydrate and                unreacted aqueous solution in reaction unit,                recovering high concentration hydrogen, entering                first hydrate resolver, resolving under heating and                decompression conditions, releasing methane, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen and diatomic carbon components                and an aqueous solution, passing methane, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen and diatomic carbon gases to                lower portion of the second hydrate reaction column separated, converting the diatomic                carbon component to hydrate, mixing with aqueous                solution to form solid-liquid mixture into second                hydrate decomposer, resolving under heating and                decompression conditions, releasing the diatomic                carbon component and aqueous solution, such that                the diatomic carbon component enters diatomic                carbon collector for recovery, returning aqueous                solution to first liquid storage tank for                recycling, discharging separated methane, carbon                dioxide and nitrogen from the top of second hydrate                reaction tower into third hydrate reaction tower,                passing gas into third decomposition unit and third                hydrate reaction tower, reversing contact with                aqueous solution containing kinetic promoter from                second liquid storage tank to form hydrate,                converting hydrate-forming methane and carbon                dioxide in the gas into hydrates, mixing with                aqueous solution to form solid-liquid mixture,                discharging remaining nitrogen from top of the                third hydrate reaction column, passing into                nitrogen collector, forming slurry by hydrate and                unreacted aqueous solution in the third hydrate                reaction column, passing into third hydrate                resolver, performing depressurization, releasing                mixed slurry of methane gas and carbon dioxide                hydrate and aqueous solution, passing resulting gas                to methane collector, passing the slurry into                fourth hydrate resolver, performing separation of                carbon dioxide hydrate, entering carbon dioxide                released by fourth hydrate resolver into carbon                dioxide collector, passing aqueous solution into                second liquid storage tank and recycling. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a various                gases recovering device.

AU HAYASHI K
   TAKEUCHI N
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 2020979107
AB    NOVELTY - A laminate comprises a base material layer (2), a carbon dioxide absorption layer (3), and a carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4). The layer (3) comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent, carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent dispersion resin, a water absorbent, and a reaction accelerator. The carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkali metal compound and/or a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkaline-earth metal compound. The carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer comprises an oxygen absorbent and oxygen-absorbent dispersion resin. The oxygen absorbent is chosen from oxygen-absorbing metal oxide, oxygen-absorbing inorganic-type mixture, and oxygen-absorbing organic-type mixture. The oxygen-absorbing organic-type mixture comprises ascorbic acid, m-xylenediamine-6 nylon, ethylenically unsaturated-hydrocarbon, and cyclohexenyl group-containing resin, and transition metal catalyst comprising a late transition metal element.
   USE - Laminate used for packaging material for package e.g. valveless packaging bag and valveless packaging pouch bag for roasted coffee beans (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The laminate has excellent storage stability for long time, and suppressed generation of carbon dioxide gas from the roasted coffee beans from the packaging material, and prevents the filling of carbon dioxide gas in the package, and prevents package swelling up, and reduced quantity of the carbon dioxide gas emitted when the contents packaged and preventing decarboxylation reaction, and efficiently absorbs the carbon dioxide gas, and provides valveless package having excellent durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A laminate (1) comprises a base material layer (2), a carbon dioxide absorption layer (3), and a carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4). The layer (3) comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent, carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent dispersion resin, a water absorbent, and a reaction accelerator. The carbon dioxide-gas adsorption agent comprises a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkali metal compound and/or a carbon dioxide-gas adsorptive alkaline-earth metal compound. The water absorbent is chosen from high water-absorbing resin, deliquescent compound, hydrophilic zeolite, and silica gel. The reaction accelerator comprises lithium chloride, lithium hydroxide, sodium chloride, and potassium chloride. The carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer comprises an oxygen absorbent and oxygen-absorbent dispersion resin. The oxygen absorbent is chosen from oxygen-absorbing metal oxide, oxygen-absorbing inorganic-type mixture, and oxygen-absorbing organic-type mixture. The oxygen-absorbing metal oxide is chosen from cerium oxide, titanium oxide, and zinc oxide. The oxygen-absorbing inorganic-type mixture comprises reduced iron and halogenated metal. The oxygen-absorbing organic-type mixture comprises ascorbic acid, m-xylenediamine-6 nylon, ethylenically unsaturated-hydrocarbon, and cyclohexenyl group-containing resin, and transition metal catalyst comprising a late transition metal element.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the laminate.
   Laminate (1)
   Base material layer (2)
   Carbon dioxide absorption layer (3)
   Carbon dioxide generation suppression layer (4)

The carbon-dioxide-gas absorption layer and/or a carbon dioxide gas generation suppression layer have heat-sealing property. The laminate further comprises a barrier layer between the layers (2), (3) and (4), a carbonic acid gas absorption layer laminated on the base material layer side rather than the layer (4), and a heat sealing layer laminated on the surface of the surface of the carbon dioxide absorption laminate opposite to the base material layer. The barrier layer comprises a barriering resin coating film, metal foil, resin film with metal-deposited layer, and resin film with metal oxide vapor deposition layer. The layer (4) is laminated on the base material layer side rather than the carbonic acid gas absorption layer. Preferred Composition: The carbonic acid gas absorption layer comprises 0.5-50 %mass carbonic acid gas adsorption agent. The layer (4) comprises 0.5-40 %mass oxygen absorbent.

PN CN114409573-A
TI Improved low energy consumption carbon dioxide            stripping urea process comprises introducing part of            urea synthesis liquid to high-pressure carbon dioxide            stripper, vacuum extracting, introducing another part            of urea synthesis liquid into middle section and            performing low-pressure decomposition.
AU YU Z
   WANG J
   SUN X
   YANG Z
   XIA Y
AE CHINA WUHUAN ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2022641919
AB    NOVELTY - Improved low energy consumption carbon dioxide                stripping urea process comprises dividing the urea                synthesis liquid into two parts, introducing a part                of the urea synthesis liquid to the high-pressure                carbon dioxide stripper in the high-pressure                decomposition recovery system for high-pressure                carbon dioxide stripping, obtaining medium pressure                gas phase and medium pressure decomposed aqueous                phase, allowing the high-pressure stripping liquid                to flows out at the bottom of the high-pressure                carbon monoxide stripping tower, vacuum extracting,                respectively introducing another part of the urea                synthesis liquid into the middle section and the                upper section of the medium-pressure decomposition                tower to carry out medium-pressure gas-liquid                separation, and introducing the medium-pressure                decomposition liquid drawn from the bottom of the                medium-pressure decomposition tower to the                low-pressure decomposition tower for low-pressure                decomposition.
   USE - The method is useful for improved low energy                consumption carbon dioxide stripping urea                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to improve, and has                low operation cost and saves energy and reduces                consumption, and improves the yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improved low energy consumption carbon dioxide                stripping urea process comprises dividing the urea                synthesis liquid into two parts, introducing a part                of the urea synthesis liquid to the high-pressure                carbon dioxide stripper in the high-pressure                decomposition recovery system for high-pressure                carbon dioxide stripping, introducing another part                of the urea synthesis liquid to the medium pressure                decomposition tower of the medium pressure                decomposition recovery system for medium pressure                decomposition, obtaining medium pressure gas phase                and medium pressure decomposed aqueous phase,                allowing the high-pressure stripping liquid to                flows out at the bottom of the high-pressure carbon                monoxide stripping tower, vacuum extracting,                respectively introducing another part of the urea                synthesis liquid into the middle section and the                upper section of the medium-pressure decomposition                tower to carry out medium-pressure gas-liquid                separation, and introducing the medium-pressure                decomposition liquid drawn from the bottom of the                medium-pressure decomposition tower to the                low-pressure decomposition tower for low-pressure                decomposition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow chart of the                method for improved low energy consumption carbon                dioxide stripping urea process (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The urea solution                  decomposed by the low pressure decomposition                  tower is decompressed and introduced to the                  atmospheric flash tank for flash evaporation to                  further decompose the methylammonium in the urea                  solution and the urea solution flowing out from                  the bottom of the atmospheric flash tank is                  introduced to the heating section of the vacuum                  pre-concentrator for further heating and                  concentration, and the concentrated urea solution                  is drawn out into the downstream process. The                  flash gas discharged from the top of the                  atmospheric flash tank is condensed by the                  evaporation condenser and mixed with the                  low-pressure decomposition gas discharged from                  the top of the low-pressure decomposition tower,                  and then introduced to the subsequent                  low-pressure methylammonium condenser for                  condensation. The method comprises introducing                  the gas phase discharged from the top of the                  medium pressure decomposition tower to the                  heating section of the vacuum pre-concentrator to                  exchange heat indirectly with the urea solution,                  introducing to the medium pressure methyl                  ammonium condenser for further condensation and                  absorption, introducing to the liquid level tank                  of the medium pressure methyl ammonium condenser                  for gas-liquid separation, washing the separated                  gas by the medium pressure scrubber and                  discharged into the low pressure decomposition                  gas discharged from the top of the low pressure                  decomposition tower, pressurizing the discharged                  high-pressure methylammonium solution by a                  high-pressure methylammonium pump and introducing                  to the next process, introducing the stripping                  gas discharged from the top of the high-pressure                  carbon dioxide stripper to the high-pressure                  methylammonium condenser for condensation and                  recovery of methylammonium liquid, discharging                  process gas and methyl ammonium liquid,                  introducing the boiler feed water through the low                  pressure steam drum, and producing low pressure                  steam on the shell side of the high pressure                  methylammonium condenser to recover the heat                  energy generated during the condensation of the                  gas to form methylammonium. The medium-pressure                  steam from outside is introduced to the                  high-pressure carbon dioxide stripper for                  indirect heat exchange with the urea synthesis                  liquid to provide heat energy and the steam                  condensate from the high-pressure carbon dioxide                  stripping tower is directly or decompressed once                  and then introduced to the first medium-pressure                  decomposer for further indirect heat exchange                  with the urea methylammonium solution to provide                  heat energy. The operating temperature of the                  second medium pressure decomposer is                  125-160degrees Celsius, the operating temperature                  of the first medium pressure decomposer is                  130-155degrees Celsius, the temperature of the                  urine at the outlet of the medium pressure                  decomposition tower is 140-160degrees Celsius,                  and the urea concentration is 55-60%. The urea                  synthesis liquid is introduced to the                  medium-pressure decomposition tower accounts for                  1-45 wt.%. A part of the gas phase at the top of                  the medium-pressure decomposition tower is                  separated into the low-pressure decomposition gas                  to adjust the ammonia-carbon ratio of the                  low-pressure decomposition gas.

PN WO2020251358-A1; NL2024761-B1; AU2020292104-A1; CA3139472-A1; CN114007941-A; BR112021024180-A2; EP3983308-A1; JP2022536636-W; US2022306369-A1
TI Package e.g. controlled atmosphere package,            comprises packaging material comprising polymer film            and having preset water vapor transmission rate, carbon            dioxide transmission rate, and oxygen transmission            rate.
AU GROENEWEG B R A
   VAN DE LOO P J B
   VAN DE LOO P J B M
   LOO P J B
AE PERFO TEC BV (PERF-Non-standard)
   PERFO TEC BV (PERF-Non-standard)
   PERFOTEC BV (PERF-Non-standard)
   PERFO TEC BV (PERF-Non-standard)
   PERFO TEC BV (PERF-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7059R
AB    NOVELTY - A package (3) comprises a packaging material                (7) comprising a polymer film (11) provided with at                least one perforation enabling gas exchange with                the atmosphere surrounding the package to form the                package into a controlled atmosphere package. The                packaging material has a water vapor transmission                rate of 100-1200 ml/m2.24 hours, carbon dioxide                transmission rate of 1000 ml/m2.24 hours or more,                preferably 1000-15000 ml/m2.24 hours and oxygen                transmission rate. The ratio of carbon dioxide                transmission rate and oxygen transmission rate of                the packaging material is 4 or more, preferably                4-25. The package defines a package volume for                containing a portion of produce (9) and a package                atmosphere.
   USE - Package e.g. controlled atmosphere package                (claimed) used for preserving respiring produce                contained in package, such as species of vegetables                and fruit, herbs, and flowers. Uses include but are                not limited to blueberries, chicory, grapes,                pomegranate, peas, snap beans and mango.
   ADVANTAGE - The package has high water vapor transmission                rate, carbon dioxide transmission rate and ratio                between the transmission rates for oxygen and                carbon dioxide and can be stored with the content                kept fresh for long period of time. The packaging                material is biodegradable and compostable and                reduces the cost of manufacturing package and                reduces waste. The high water vapor transmission                rate reduces humidity build-up in the package                atmosphere, and reduces formation of water films                and/or droplets in the package atmosphere, e.g.                inside surface of the packaging material, and                reduces fungal growth and/or other decay processes.                The high carbon dioxide transmission rate                facilitates escape of carbon dioxide and reduces                elevating carbon dioxide concentration in the                package atmosphere, and reduces or prevents the                risks of anaerobic decay processes. The high oxygen                transmission rate maximizes the shelf life. The                high ratio between the transmission rates for                oxygen and carbon dioxide reduces the oxygen level                inside package by reducing the size and number of                micro perforations, without, or with reduced risks                that the carbon dioxide concentration reaches                harmful level and/or harmful effect for the packed                fresh produce.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of the package.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                apparatus for manufacturing modified atmosphere                package.
   Modified atmosphere packages (3)
   Packaging material (7)
   Produce (9)
   Packaging film (11)
   Laser (35)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The packaging material                  has a water vapor transmission rate of preferably                  900-1000 ml/m2.24 hours, carbon dioxide                  transmission rate of preferably 5000-8500                  ml/m2.24 hours or more, and oxygen transmission                  rate of 500-4000 ml/m2.24 hours, preferably                  1000-2500 ml/m2.24 hours. The ratio of carbon                  dioxide transmission rate and oxygen transmission                  rate of the packaging material is preferably                  5-7.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polymer film has                  thickness of 10-200 mu m, preferably 25-40 mu                  m.

PN CN113982713-A
TI Self-circulating carbon dioxide waste gas utilization power plant, has combustion chamber externally connected with natural gas inlet channel, where heat energy generated by combustion of natural gas heats supercritical carbon dioxide in pipeline.
AU WANG X
   GU S
   XUE D
   SUN Z
AE SINO-INDO ALLWIN BEIJING TRADING CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 202227472R
AB    NOVELTY - The plant has a carbon dioxide inlet channel (1) in which carbon dioxide working medium is entered in a supercritical state. The carbon dioxide inlet channel is located on the upper layer of a combustion boiler. The combustion boiler is provided with a natural gas burner (4). A combustion chamber (3) is connected to the natural gas into the channel, and the heat energy generated by the combustion of the natural gas in the combustion chamber of the boiler will heat the supercritical CO2 in the pipeline and make it expand.
   USE - Self-circulation carbon dioxide waste gas utilization power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulating system uses supercritical carbon dioxide as working substance to generate electricity, which can repeatedly utilize the supercritical CO2. The CO2 waste gas generated by the steam turbine generates the collected pipeline through the heat exchanger and collected to the condensed liquid pump, and then adding new cycle power generation process of the one wheel. The cooling pipeline between the condenser and the compressor has a throttle valve, the repeated flow rate of CO2 can be controlled according to the operation of the system. The compressor can compress the low-temperature CO2 fluid to 30 MPa, the CO2 is restored to the super critical state again, and the CO 2 is pumped to the combustion chamber pipeline.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the self-circulation carbon dioxide waste gas utilization power plant.
   Inlet channel (1)
   Natural gas pipeline (2)
   Combustion chamber (3)
   Burner (4)
   Gas conveying pipeline (5)
   Steam turbine (6)
   Generator (7)
   Recycling pipeline (8)
   Condensing pump (9)
   Throttle valve (10)
   Compression device (11)
   Liquid inlet pipeline (12)
   Exhaust gas removing pipe (13)
   Exhaust gas processing area (14)

PN CN113685966-A
TI Control method for air conditioner, involves determining whether carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than preset concentration value and obtaining infrared temperature value again and returning to determine whether it is greater than preset temperature value.
AU ZHANG D
   HAO B
   SUN P
   HOU H
   WANG P
   YUAN X
AE QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITION ELECTRIC CO (HIER-C)
   HAIER SMART HOME CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER CO LTD (HIER-C)
   CHONGQI HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER LLC (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2021E4209W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S1) the infrared temperature value, after the air conditioner is turned on. The infrared temperature value is determined (S2) whether the infrared temperature value is greater than the preset temperature value, and if so, obtain the carbon dioxide concentration value. The first time period is waited to re-acquire the infrared temperature value and return to determine whether it is greater than the preset temperature value, if the infrared temperature value is not greater than the preset temperature value. The carbon dioxide concentration value is determined (S3) whether the carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than the preset concentration value, after obtaining the carbon dioxide concentration value and if so, generate a fire alarm signal. The second time period is waited to obtain the infrared temperature value again and return to determine whether it is greater than the preset temperature value.
   USE - Control method for air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the infrared sensor currently installed in the air conditioner, and additionally adds a carbon dioxide concentration sensor. The dual conditions of temperature and carbon dioxide concentration are used to determine whether a fire has occurred. The judgment process is more accurate. When the judgment result is that a fire has occurred, generate fire alarm signals in time to remind surrounding users to put out fires or take fire-fighting measures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a control device; and
   (2) an air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating an air conditioner control method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Step for obtaining infrared temperature value (S1)
   Step for determining whether infrared temperature value is greater than preset temperature value (S2)
   Step for determining whether carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than preset concentration value (S3)

TI Carbon dioxide capture system for use in e.g. hot            potassium carbonate plant, has intake for carbon            dioxide-rich exhaust gas from source to compressor, and            heat exchanger for exchanging pressurized carbon            dioxide rich gas with poor gas.
AU BORSETH K E
   FLEISCHER H
   BORSETTI K E
AE KARBON CCS LTD (KARB-Non-standard)
   KARBON CCS LTD (KARB-Non-standard)
   KARBON CCS LTD (KARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021B88628
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide (C02) capture system has                intake for C02-rich exhaust gas from first source                to compressor (2) and outlet for compressed, where                first C02 rich gas under pressure to manifold to                shell encloses combustion chamber with wall. The                combustion chamber comprises burners arranged to                burn fuel and compressed air supplied under                pressure from fuel line and an air supply pipe                respectively to form a second C02 -rich gas. The                slits are provided in the wall in the combustion                chamber to let in the compressed first gas to mix                with and cool the second gas.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide (C02) for                use in gas turbine of hot potassium carbonate (HPC)                plant used for oil production and industrial                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus is easy to implement by                converting a gas turbine. The apparatus can be                included in connection with another gas turbine                which generates 60 million watts or more, at the                same time as virtually all the C02 which the gas                turbine generates can be extracted in a non-toxic                manner, so that all this C02 can be stored under                pressure in reservoirs or be used as a pressure aid                in oil production or be included as a part of the                raw material for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a C02                capture method for capturing carbon dioxide for use                in gas turbine of hot potassium carbonate                plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon                dioxide capture system for capturing carbon dioxide                for use in gas turbine of hot potassium carbonate                (HPC) plant.
   Original gas turbine (1)
   Compressor (2)
   Expander (3)
   Annular manifold (8)
   Control system (9)

GA 2021A4375E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first chamber that receives a low-temperature airflow of high-temperature gas from a first vortex tube (200,450,520) and receives a high-temperature airflow of low-temperature gas from the second vortex tube. A second chamber receives a high-temperature airflow of a high-temperature gas from the first vortex tube and receives a low-temperature airflow of a low-temperature gas from the second vortex tube. A first heat exchanger (101,410) is connected between the vortex tube and the first compressor (120,440) to cool the hot gas. A first collecting device (420) is connected between the first heat exchanger and the first compressor to collect condensed water. The chamber includes a housing. A connector connects one end of the vortex tube and the housing. An exhaust pipe is mounted on the upper portion of the housing to exhaust gas. A drain pipe is mounted below the housing to discharge liquefied carbon dioxide and in the first chamber and the second chamber.
   USE - System for reducing carbon dioxide separation for removing carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The large amount of greenhouse gases can be reduced in a short time. The efficiency of removing carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas is higher. The compression efficiency is improved through a water remover and a surge tank. The energy waste is reduced, and the efficiency is maximized.

PN CN109534945-A
TI Ethane recovery method for carbon dioxide-containing natural gas, involves requiring decarburization device if carbon dioxide content of ethane product exceeds standard.
AU JIANG H
   YANG Y
   ZHU C
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   SICHUAN MOKURUI PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201931041K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling the first and second raw material gas is cooled by the raw material gas pre-cooling heat exchangers (E11,E12). The portion of the external gas recirculation is mixed with a small amount of cryogenic separator by heat exchange of raw material gas pre-cooling heat exchanger. The mixed phase is cooled by heat exchanger (E13) and is cooled down to the top of demethanizer (T11). The remaining cryogenic separator (V11) liquid phase is depressurized and is entered into the upper portion of the demethanizer. The gas phase coming out from the top of demethanizer is heated by the heat exchanger. The deethanizer is divided into ethane products and condensate products containing propane and propane according to the product type and quality requirements, after the ethane-containing condensate from the bottom of the demethanizer is pressure-regulated. A decarburization device is required if the carbon dioxide content of the ethane product exceeds the standard.
   USE - Ethane recovery method for carbon dioxide-containing natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances the ability of the ethane recovery unit to inhibit the formation of carbon dioxide solids, increases the amount of cold in the upper part of the demethanizer increases the ethane recovery, enhances the ability of the demethanizer to inhibit the formation of carbon dioxide solids, improves the ethane recovery rate, effectively improves the economic benefit of the operation of the ethane recovery unit, and is suitable for the ethane recovery unit with a carbon dioxide content of the feed gas greater than 0.5%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of an ethane recovery method for carbon dioxide-containing natural gas.
   Air cooler (A11)
   Raw material gas pre-cooling heat exchangers (E11,E12)
   Heat exchanger (E13)
   Demethanizer (T11)
   Remaining cryogenic separator (V11)

The setting a threshold of a temperature, humidity, illuminance, nutrient solution pH, nutrient solution temperature information and nutrient solution moisture information before comparing temperature, humidity, illuminance, pH value of the nutrient solution, liquid temperature and the moisture information of nutrient solution set threshold by computer controller.

PN CN114414722-A
TI Measuring carbon dioxide content in phosphorus ore            in gas chromatographic column separation system,            involves setting gas chromatograph condition, preparing            carbon dioxide standard gas with different volume            fraction by standard gas, preparing carbon dioxide            standard curve.
AU YANG Y
   YANG J
   WEI L
   CHANG X
   FAN S
   FU B
   DU A
AE TIANJI COAL CHEM IND GROUP CO LTD (SHLU-C)
GA 2022644674
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring carbon dioxide content in phosphorus                ore involves setting gas chromatograph condition,                preparing carbon dioxide standard gas with                different volume fraction by standard gas,                preparing carbon dioxide standard curve, adding the                phosphorite sample and phosphoric acid solution                into the pre-processing device to react, preparing                the gas to be detected, injecting the gas to be                detected into the sample injector of the gas                chromatograph, obtaining the volume fraction of the                carbon dioxide and calculating the content of                carbon dioxide in the phosphorite.
   USE - Measuring carbon dioxide content in phosphorus                ore used in gas chromatographic column separation                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately controls the experiment                pressure, temperature, reaction starting time,                reduces the test difficulty and carbon dioxide                content measurement, and improves the measuring                accuracy of the content of carbon dioxide in the                phosphorite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gas chromatograph                  includes thermal conductivity detector (detector                  temperature 110degrees Celsius, hot wire                  temperature 150degrees Celsius, 156mV),                  chromatographic column (TDX-01, column                  temperature 90degrees Celsius), sample injector                  (six-way valve sampling, temperature 90degrees                  Celsius), carrier gas (hydrogen). The method of                  preparing the detection gas involves accurately                  weighing 0.1-0.5g phosphorite sample to 0.1mg in                  the sample of groove in the reaction bottle 101,                  accurately removing 10-15mL 1 + 1 phosphoric acid                  solution outside the sample groove mixing the                  phosphorite sample with the phosphoric acid                  solution and heating, when the temperature rises                  to 80degrees Celsius, keeping the solution                  micro-boiling state, keeping for 80degrees                  Celsius for two minutes, stopping heating and                  introducing the cooling water into the cover body                  for cooling, until the display value of the                  thermometer is the same as the room temperature,                  pre-extracting 40 mL air in the first piston                  amount cylinder and the second piston amount                  cylinder, pushing the first piston amount                  cylinder and the second piston amount cylinder.                  The gas in the pipeline are mixed uniformly to                  obtain gas to be detected, pushed the first                  piston amount cylinder to clear the gas volume,                  pulled the piston of the second piston amount                  cylinder, extracted the gas to be detected, after                  the piston is automatically balanced, rotated the                  faucet of the three-way valve only keeping the                  sampler and the second piston amount cylinder                  connected, pushed the piston of the second piston                  amount cylinder, and injected the gas to be                  detected into the (sampler) of the gas                  chromatograph. The content of carbon dioxide in                  the phosphorus ore includes firstly obtaining the                  gas total volume V, where the V1 is the total                  volume of the reaction bottle and the connecting                  pipeline, V2 is the dropping volume of the                  phosphoric acid solution, V3 is the volume of the                  gas to be detected in the second piston amount                  cylinder when the sampler is connected with the                  second piston cylinder, calculating the content                  of carbon dioxide in phosphorite, where the                  volume fraction of the carbon dioxide obtained by                  the chromatograph, and the volume fraction of the                  carbon dioxide obtained by the chromatograph, V                  is total volume of gas, m is mass of phosphorite                  sample, gF is correction coefficient, calculating                  the correction coefficient F when the gas is                  converted into the standard condition according                  to the laboratory environment temperature and                  atmospheric pressure, P is laboratory atmospheric                  pressure, Pw-t is degrees centigrade gas steam                  partial pressure, t is laboratory                  temperature.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

TI Removal of carbon dioxide involves exposing            sequestration material comprising salt in molten form            comprising alkali metal cation, boron and oxygen to            environment containing carbon dioxide interacting with            sequestration material.
AU HATTON T A

   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 202027609B
AB    NOVELTY - Removal (100a) of carbon dioxide involves                exposing a sequestration material (102) comprising                a salt in molten form, to an environment containing                carbon dioxide such that at least some of carbon                dioxide interacts with the sequestration material                and is sequestered from the environment. The salt                in molten form comprises at least one alkali metal                cation, boron, and oxygen.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide using sequestration                materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the rate of carbon                dioxide capture by the sequestration material,                reduces duration of a carbon dioxide capture                process, potentially reduces energy input required                in the carbon dioxide capture process, and reduces                the amount of energy required to absorb carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                sequestration material.
   Removal of carbon dioxide (100a)
   Sequestration material (102)
   Streams (104a,106a)
   Optional reactor (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The boron is in the                  form of at least one boron cation. The oxygen is                  in the form of at least one oxygen anion,                  preferably peroxide. The alkali metal cation                  comprises cationic lithium, sodium, potassium,                  rubidium and/or cesium, preferably lithium,                  sodium, and/or potassium. The boron oxide anion                  comprises anionic boron oxide of formula: BwOz                  and/or its dissociated form, where w is 1-4, and                  z is 1-9. The boron oxide anion comprises anionic                  boron trioxide, boron tetroxide, boron pentoxide                  and/or their dissociated form. The fractional                  stoichiometry of the salt in molten form is of                  formula: MxB1-xOy, where x is 0-1, preferably                  0.5-0.9, M comprises at least one alkali metal                  cation, preferably lithium and sodium, y is                  1.5-x. Preferred Composition: The environment                  comprises at least 1 mol% carbon dioxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN208935830-U
TI Thermal power generator carbon dioxide replacing device, has carbon dioxide gas bottle connected to first valve and second valve through gas manifold, where first valve and second valve are respectively connected with generator stator bore.
AU DAI D
AE HUARUN ELECTRIC POWER LIANYUAN CO LTD (CSTE-C)
GA 201952799H
AB    NOVELTY - A thermal generator carbon dioxide replacing device, comprising a container, a plurality of carbon dioxide gas, gas manifold, said carbon dioxide gas through the gas manifold are respectively connected to the first valve and the second valve, carbon dioxide gas is put in the container, first valve and second valve are respectively connected with the generator stator bore. The utility model adopts the form of packaging, to carbon dioxide gas exchange and carbon dioxide bottle gas charging single carbon dioxide gas cylinder, which avoids repeatedly connected, disconnected and gas filling operation, the thermal power generator stator bore carbon dioxide gas displacing more efficient and convenient, saves time and labour, heating device to avoid pipeline frost and icing caused by pipe blockage phenomenon, improves the replacement efficiency of carbon dioxide gas.
