PT P
PN TW201914621-A; TW681783-B1
TI Sanitary product of providing carbon dioxide gas characterized by using a carbon dioxide-providing unit to provide carbon dioxide for body surface, thereby increasing the concentration of carbon dioxide to inhibit the growth of microorganisms.
AU TSAUR G
   GARRY T
AE TSAUR G (TSAU-Individual)
   GARRY T (GARR-Individual)
GA 201957553H
AB    NOVELTY - The invention provides a sanitary product of providing carbon dioxide gas. The sanitary product includes carbon dioxide-providing unit. An outer layer of the carbon dioxide-providing unit is an unbreathable layer; and an inner layer of the carbon dioxide-providing unit is breathable and can produce carbon dioxide by contacting with the moisture of body, so as to increase the concentration of carbon dioxide gas on the body surface. The carbon dioxide-providing unit contains solid acid and solid sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) or metal carbonate. The metal carbonate includes magnesium carbonate or calcium carbonate or sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. The sanitary products include sanitary napkin, tampon, maternity bed mat, panty liner, diaper, diaper pants, eye mask, facial mask, pasties, shoe pad, disposable underwear, band-aid and wound dressing.

AU WU H
   WU J
   ZHANG K
AE CHENGDU K & H ENVIROTECH CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B7391S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an ultra-critical hydrothermal                combustion unit provided with an ultra-critical                hydrothermal burner (11) that is provided with a                material inlet and a reactant outlet (13). The                material inlet is formed with a nozzle (12). A                water-coal-slurry supply unit provides water coal                slurry to the nozzle. An oxidant supply unit                provides pre-heated predetermined oxidant to the                nozzle. A slag separation unit separates a reaction                product discharged from the reactant outlet of the                ultra-critical water heat burner. A carbon dioxide                collecting unit separates exhaust steam discharged                from a power generation unit and collects obtained                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Coal cleaning and using thermal system for use                in a coal-fired industrial boiler of a power                station.
   ADVANTAGE - The system captures carbon dioxide easily,                realizes zero discharge of carbon dioxide and clean                utilization of coal, maintains density of the                ultra-critical carbon dioxide in the mixed working                medium, realizes high energy density, reduces unit                enthalpy and improves working ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coal                cleaning and using thermal system operation                method.

PT P
PN EP3670362-A1; EP3670362-B1
TI Closed environmental compartment used for e.g. spacecraft and mobile space stations, comprises life support system configured to remove metabolic carbon dioxide from its internal atmosphere, and with work chamber.
AU JANECEK V
AE AIRBUS DEFENCE & SPACE GMBH (EADS-C)
GA 202056167T
AB    NOVELTY - Closed environmental compartment to accommodate humans comprises a life support system (14) configured to remove metabolic carbon dioxide from its internal atmosphere, and at least one work chamber (16), which is configured for a performance of an extraction and/or purification process with carbon dioxide as a solvent, where the carbon dioxide required for the extraction and/or purification process in the work chamber is supplied by the life support system. The closed environmental compartment comprises: a further work chamber which is configured for a performance of an extraction and/or purification process with carbon dioxide as a solvent, and which is directly connected to the life support system, at a substantial distance from the work chamber; and at least one transfer unit (18) configured to transferring the carbon dioxide of the life support system into a supercritical or at least near-supercritical state before being introduced into the work chamber.
   USE - The closed environmental compartment is used for spacecraft, mobile space stations such as international space station, immobile extraterrestrial space stations such as dwellings on a planet or a moon, and submarines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating the closed environmental compartment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the closed environmental compartment.
   Spacecraft (10)
   Cabin (12)
   Life support system (14)
   Work chamber (16)
   Carbon dioxide processing subsystem (28)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The life support system comprises a carbon dioxide absorbent material for carbon dioxide absorption. The carbon dioxide absorbent material is a macroporous ion-exchange resin, preferably according to DE19830470C1.

TI Carbon dioxide gas extracting device, has gas stripping tower and gas stripping pressure control valve fixedly connected with each other, and gas stripping pressure control valve fixedly connected to circulating gas pipeline.
AU CUI J

   CUI K
AE CUI J (CUIJ-Individual)
   LIU J (LIUJ-Individual)
   LI H (LIHH-Individual)
   CUI K (CUIK-Individual)
GA 201984196R
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a rich methanol pipeline (2) fixed with a rich methanol pressure reducing control valve (L1). The rich methanol pressure reducing control valve and a rich methanol flash separating tank (V) are connected with each other. The rich methanol flash separating tank is connected with a liquid level control valve (L2). The liquid level control valve and a gas stripping tower (T) are connected with a carbon dioxide pipeline (3). The gas stripping tower and a gas stripping pressure control valve (P2) are fixedly connected with each other. The gas stripping pressure control valve is fixedly connected to a circulating gas pipeline (1).
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas extracting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device absorbs methanol-rich gas in low temperature methanol washing process, and dissolves partial effective gas so as to reduce environment pollution, methanol-rich-in-effective gas during process of content, emission of carbon monoxide atmosphere and loss of effective gas, and extracts carbon dioxide gas, and increases energy-saving effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide gas extracting method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a carbon dioxide gas extracting device.

PT P
PN JP2019098519-A
TI Dry ice injection apparatus used for cleaning electronic component, has liquefied carbon dioxide supply path whose flow path length from downstream end to ejection port is less than specific length.
AU WADA Y
   AKAGI Y
   INABA O
   ISHII T
AE COOL TECHNOS CO LTD (COOL-Non-standard)
   IWATANI PLANTEC KK (IWAN-C)
GA 201952056Q
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has an ejection port (16) which ejects liquefied carbon dioxide supplied from liquefied carbon dioxide supply path (6). An expansion space (10a) expands liquefied carbon dioxide which ejected from the ejection port, and produces dry ice particles. A merging space (11) makes the carrier gas supplied from the carrier gas supply path, and the dry ice particles produced in the expansion space merge. An injection path (18) is formed until it reached an injection opening (17) from the merging space. A gas-liquid separator (15) is provided in the middle of the liquefied carbon dioxide supply path. A flow path length of the liquefied carbon dioxide supply path from downstream end of gas-liquid separator to the ejection port is less than 200 mm.
   USE - Dry ice injection apparatus used for cleaning electronic component.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry ice injection device injects dry ice particles at a stable flow volume with which the flow volume of dry ice particles to be injected is least. The liquid level height in the gas-liquid separator is adjusted by simple and low cost structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the dry ice injection apparatus.
   Dry ice injection apparatus (1)
   Liquefied carbon dioxide supply path (6)
   Expansion space (10a)
   Merging space (11)
   Gas-liquid separator (15)
   Ejection port (16)
   Injection opening (17)
   Injection path (18)

PT P
PN WO2019021157-A1
TI Carbon nitride material for adsorption of carbon dioxide, has two-dimensional structure, and/or three-dimensional structure having preset atomic nitrogen to atomic carbon ratio.
AU KIM I Y
   SCARANTO J
   ALBAHILY K
   RAVON U
   VINU A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2019122735
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon nitride material has a two-dimensional structure and/or a three-dimensional structure having atomic nitrogen to atomic carbon ratio of 1.4:1-2.35:1. The material has atomic nitrogen to atomic carbon ratio of 1.36:1-2.35:1.
   USE - Carbon nitride material used for adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed) that can be converted into hydrocarbon fuels or used as reagent in chemical reaction to produce oxygen or carbon monoxide moiety.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has large surface area, pore volumes and nitrogen content, and excellent carbon dioxide adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) synthesize of 2-dimensional or 3-dimensional material which involves contacting a 2-dimensinal or 3-dimensional template with an aqueous amino tetrazole solution to obtain a templated reaction mixture, heating the templated reaction mixture at 40-200 degrees C, preferably 80-120 degrees C and cooling the mixture to obtain a carbon nitride/template composite, heating the composite at 250-400 degrees C in a nitrogen atmosphere to form a 2-diensional or 3-dimensional carbon nitride material/template complex, and removing the template; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide capturing process which involves contacting the carbon nitride material with a carbon-dioxide containing feed source, adsorbing the carbon dioxide in or on the carbon nitride structure, and providing the carbon dioxide adsorbed material to a chemical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 3-dimensiional material is either porous or non-porous. The template is a porous silica template selected from KIT-6, KIT-5, SBA-15 or FDU-12. The 2-dimensional template is a mesoporous silica template, preferably MCM-41. Preferred Composition: The 2-dimensional structure has a nanorod morphology and a nitrogen to carbon ratio of 1.36:1-2.28:1. Preferred Property: The average pore size of the 2-dimensional material is 3-6.5 nm, and 3-dimensional material is 3-12 nm. The material has a surface area of 50-500 m2/g, preferably 200-310 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 2-dimensional and/or 3-dimensional structures comprise a tetrazole moiety and/or amino tetrazole moiety selected from 5-amino-1H-tetrazole, 1,5-diamino-1H-tetrazole, 1,5-aminomethyl tetrazole and/or 1-(3-aminophenyl)tetrazole. The template is calcined or washed with ethanol. The removal of template involves contacting the hard template with hydrogen fluoride or ethanol.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; E31-N05D; H06-X02; J01-E02B; J01-E03C
IP B01D-053/02; B01J-020/02; B01J-020/28; B01J-020/30; C01B-021/06

PN CN114525309-A
TI Converting carbon dioxide useful for preparing            porous aragonite, by embedding carbonic anhydrase in            situ by calcium divalent ion cross-linking sodium            alginate, adding sodium alginate gel beads, introducing            carbon dioxide and obtaining white precipitate.
AU LV C
   ZHANG T
   ZHAO G
   ZHANG H
   ZANG J
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 202274466C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for efficiently converting carbon                dioxide to prepare porous aragonite involves                embedding carbonic anhydrase in situ by calcium                divalent ion cross-linking sodium alginate to form                a gel, adjusting the mass concentration ratio of                calcium divalent ion, sodium alginate and carbonic                anhydrase to prepare alginic acid with carbon                dioxide hydration activity sodium gel beads,                preparing mixed solution of calcium chloride and                carboxymethyl cellulose, adding the sodium alginate                gel beads to the mixed solution to obtain a                reaction solution, adding the above reaction                solution into a glass reactor, introducing carbon                dioxide for a period of time, and obtaining a white                precipitate by suction filtration, which is                spherical vaterite after washing and drying                processes.
   USE - The method is useful for efficiently                converting carbon dioxide to prepare porous                aragonite which is used in fields of environment,                food and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and rapid to perform, and                recycles carbonic anhydrase-sodium alginate gel                beads, which effectively reduces costs and is easy                to popularize.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  uniformly mixing sodium alginate solution and the                  carbonic anhydrase solution at room temperature,                  with a magnetic stirrer at a stirring speed of                  800-1000 revolutions per minute (rpm), adding the                  mixed solution to the calcium chloride solution                  with a syringe, continuously stirring for 1-3                  hours, filtering to obtain gel beads, and                  repeatedly washing with deionized water for 3-5                  times to obtain sodium alginate gel with carbonic                  anhydrase embedded beads.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  sodium alginate and carbonic anhydrase is                  (5-10):(2-5). The mass concentration of calcium                  chloride solution is 2-5%. The concentration of                  calcium chloride is 50 mM/l, and the mass                  concentration of carboxymethyl cellulose is 2-8                  mg/ml. The pH of the mixed solution is 8.5-9.5.                  The stirring speed during the reaction is 500-800                  rpm. The viscosity of carboxymethyl cellulose is                  600-3000 MPa.second, 1500-3100 MPa.second or                  3000-5000 MPa.second. The carbon dioxide                  ventilation rate is 130-170 ml/minute, the time                  is 5-10 minutes, and the suction filtration time                  is 5-10 minutes. The drying temperature is                  60-80&#176;C and the time is 1-2 hours.

The number of the scanning circles of the electrochemical reduction is 10-20 circle. The atomic ratio of Na is 3-3.5 wt.%Preferred Method: The preparation method specifically comprises loading sodium bismuthate nanomaterials on a conductive electrode, and electrochemically reducing to obtain a bismuth nanocatalyst modified with sodium ions. The temperature of the heat treatment is 200- 220degrees Celsius, and the time of the heat treatment is 1-2 hours.

PT P
PN WO2019211675-A1
TI Preparation of mesoporous carbon nitride material used as catalyst for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves polymerizing precursor formed of ethanol-washed KIT-6 silica template, carbonizing composite, removing template and drying.
AU PARK D
   LAKHI K S
   SCARANTO J
   ALBAHILY K
   RAVON U
   VINU A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201993788F
AB    NOVELTY - Method of producing mesoporous carbon nitride material involves contacting an ethanol-washed KIT-6 silica template with an aqueous solution comprising mesoporous carbon nitride (MCN) precursor material containing amino groups, forming mixture (I), polymerizing the precursor material, forming MCN polymeric material/KIT-6 composite, carbonizing obtained composite, removing ethanol-washed KIT-6 silica template to obtain MCN material and optionally drying the obtained material.
   USE - Preparation of mesoporous carbon nitride material used as catalyst for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed) from exhaust stream discharged from fossil-fuel burning power or industrial plant, and geothermal or natural gas wells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides mesoporous carbon nitride material having high surface area, different pore size, uniform morphology, and excellent catalytic performance and gas adsorption capacity. The mesoporous carbon nitride material efficiently adsorbs carbon dioxide from atmosphere, thus preventing environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for adsorption process of carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the MCN material with a feed stream comprising carbon dioxide, where at least one portion of carbon dioxide is adsorbed by the mesoporous MCN material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: MCN has a three-dimensional MCN matrix with amino groups, average pore diameter of 3-4 nm, carbon-nitrogen ratio of 0.48, surface area of 245-250 m2/g and total pore volume of 0.26 cm3/g. MCN is capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide at 6-7 mmol/g at 0 degrees C under pressure of 1-5 MPa or 3 MPa. MCN has three-dimensional matrix having d-spacing of 8.6-9.9, preferably 9.19 or 9.81. Preferred Process: 5-6 mmol/g Carbon dioxide is adsorbed by mesoporous MCN material at 0 degrees C under pressure of 30 bar. The adsorbed carbon dioxide is activated.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The MCN precursor is chosen from amino-1,2,4-triazole, and urea or formaldehyde.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polymerizing process is carried out by heating the mixture to 100 degrees C for 6 hours, and increasing the temperature to 160 degrees C for 6 hours. The carbonizing process is carried out by heating the mesoporous MCN material/KIT-6 composite to 500 degrees C for 5 hours under inert gas atmosphere. Preferred Process: The ethanol-washed KIT-6 silica template is prepared by obtaining a solution comprising amphiphilic triblock copolymer and tetraethyl orthosilicate, heating the solution at 100-150 degrees C for 24 hours, forming non-calcined KIT-6 silica template/triblock copolymer composite, and removing the triblock copolymer from the composite. The removal process involves contacting the ethanol-washed KIT-6 silica template/triblock copolymer composite with an alcoholic solution, preferably an ethanol solution at 20-40 degrees C for 3 hours.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN109673910-A
TI Orange-flavored carbonated beverage comprises water, citric acid monohydrate, sweetening agent, food flavor, and carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN W
AE XIAN BINGFENG BEVERAGE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201940826J
AB    NOVELTY - Orange-flavored carbonated beverage comprises water, citric acid monohydrate, sweetening agent, food flavor, and carbon dioxide. The food flavor includes food flavor 1, food flavor 2, and food flavor 3.
   USE - Used as orange-flavored carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has good flavor and is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing beverage, comprising performing water treatment, using tap water as raw material, and sequentially passing sand filter and carbon at normal temperature, filtering to obtain RO water, 5 mu m fine filtration and reverse osmosis system, and using the obtained RO water for UV sterilization and is reserved, (ii) dispensing, adding auxiliary materials to RO water to make semi-finished products, (iii) filtering, (iv) mixing water and carbon dioxide to form a carbon dioxide aqueous solution, mixing filtered semi-finished product with the carbon dioxide aqueous solution to form a mixing liquid, and the water temperature of adding carbon dioxide is less than or equal to 20 degrees C, (v) filling, (vi) capping, (vii) detecting and rejecting the non-conforming product, (viii) warming the bottle to warm to normal temperature, and (ix) post-treatment, blow-drying - coding - inspection - blow drying - packaging - box coding - weighing detection - box coating - palletizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The sweetening agent comprises sucralose and cyclamate, and each liter of the carbonated beverage comprises 0.05-1.2 g citric acid monohydrate, 0.05-0.2 g sucralose, 0.1-0.35 g sodium cyclamate, 0.2-4 g food flavor, and water (remaining amount), and carbon dioxide 4-4.5 times per liter of carbonated beverage, and the weight ratio of food flavor 1, food flavor 2, food flavor 3 is 1:1:1. The food flavor 1 comprises glycerin fatty acid ester, aspartame, orange flavor, the weight ratio of the glycerin fatty acid, aspartame, orange flavor is 3: (1-1.6):(2 -3). The food flavor 2 is sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil, orange flavor, and the weight ratio of sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil and orange flavor is (0.1-0.2): (0.1-0.2): (1-1.2): (1-1.2). The food flavor 3 includes the weight ratio of sodium chloride, orange flavor, sodium chloride, and orange flavor is (4-5): (2-3). The component is further added with white granulated sugar or fructose syrup, and the sweetener is sucralose, each liter of the carbonated beverage comprises 70-98 g white sugar or 100-140 g fructose syrup, 0.05-1.2 g citric acid monohydrate, 0.05-0.24 g sucralose, 0.2-4 g food flavor, carbon dioxide 4-4.5 times, and water (remaining amount), the flavor ratio of food flavor 1, food flavor 2, food flavor 3 is 1:1:1. The food flavor 1 comprises glycerin fatty acid ester, aspartame, orange flavor, the weight ratio of the glycerin fatty acid, aspartame, orange flavor is 3: (1-1.2): ( 2-3). The food flavor 2 is sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil, orange flavor, and the weight ratio of sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil, orange flavor is (0.1-0.2): ( 0.1-0.2): (1-1.2): (1-1.2). The food flavor 3 includes and the weight ratio of sodium chloride, orange flavor, sodium chloride, and orange flavor is (4-5):(2-3). The component further added with a preservative, white granulated sugar or fructose syrup, each liter of the carbonated beverage comprises 70-98 g white sugar or 100-140 g fructose syrup, 0.05-1.2 g citric acid monohydrate, 0.05-0.25 g sucralose, 0.05-0.2 g preservative, 0.2-4 g food flavor, 3.3-3.5 times carbon dioxide, water (remaining amount), food flavor 1, food flavor 2 The weight ratio of food flavor 3 is 1:1:1. The food flavor 1 comprises glycerin fatty acid ester, aspartame, orange flavor, the weight ratio of the glycerin fatty acid, aspartame, orange flavor is 3: (1-1.2): ( 2-3). The food flavor 2 is sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil, orange flavor, and the weight ratio of sunset yellow, lemon yellow, natural orange oil, orange flavor is (0.1-0.2): ( 0.1-0.2): (1-1.2): (1-1.2). The food flavor 3 comprises the weight ratio of sodium chloride, orange flavor, sodium chloride, orange flavor is (4-5): (2-3). The preservative is potassium sorbate or sodium benzoate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the process of adding the auxiliary material comprises (i) adding the sweetener to the RO water, stirring for 5-7 minutes to completely dissolve to obtain the first intermediate product, (ii) completely dissolving citric acid monohydrate, adding to the first intermediate product, stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain a second intermediate product, (iii) sequentially adding food flavor 1, food flavor 2, food flavor 3 to the second intermediate product, adding each flavor after adding a fragrance for 5-7 minutes, adding the water to a constant volume, and allowing to stand to obtain a semi-finished product, adding carbon dioxide for 4-4.5 times the volume of the semi-finished product. The preparation process of step (ii) further comprises a sugar dissolving process, which comprises dissolving white sugar or fructose syrup at 85 plus minus 2 degrees C to form a concentrated syrup, and continuing at 87 plus minus 2 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, and storing after filtration, adding concentrated syrup to RO water, and adding auxiliary materials to make semi-finished products and adding the carbon dioxide for 4-4.5 times the volume of the semi-finished product. In the step (ii), the process of adding the auxiliary material comprises (i) completely dissolving the preservative at 60-70 degrees C, adding the syrup, stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain the first intermediate product, (ii) dissolving the sucralose completely, adding to the first intermediate product, and stirring for 5 minutes to obtain a second intermediate product, (iii) completely dissolving citric acid monohydrate, adding to the second intermediate product, stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain a third intermediate product, and (iv) sequentially adding food flavor 1, food flavor 2, food flavor 3 to the third intermediate product, adding each flavor after adding a flavor for 5-7 minutes, adding the water to a constant volume, and allowing to stand still to obtain a semi-finished product, (iv) mixing water and carbon dioxide to form a carbon dioxide aqueous solution, mixing the filtered semi-finished product with a carbon dioxide aqueous solution to form a mixing liquid, and the water temperature of adding carbon dioxide is less than or equal to 20 degrees C.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN WO2022217521-A1
TI Titanium dioxide-covalently bonded triazine            framework heterojunction material having improved            photocatalytic activity useful in photocatalytic carbon            dioxide reduction reaction, comprises titanium dioxide            nanosheets and triazine frame.
AU LI H
   XU Z
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2022D2237K
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium dioxide-covalently bonded triazine                framework (CTF-Py) heterojunction material                comprises titanium dioxide nanosheets and triazine                frame, where the titanium dioxide nanosheets are                wrapped inside the triazine frame. The titanium                dioxide nanosheets form covalent bonds with the                triazine frame.
   USE - The heterojunction material is useful in                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterojunction material has improved                photocatalytic activity; high nitrogen content; and                high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing titanium dioxide-covalently bonded                triazine framework heterojunction material,                comprising adding and reacting amine-titanium                dioxide, 2,6-pyridinedimethanol, terephthalamide,                and cesium carbonate into organic solvent;                and
   method for photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction reaction, comprising mixing titanium                dioxide-covalently bonded triazine framework                heterojunction material, metal salt, and water                under illumination reaction in carbon dioxide                atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt is                  water-soluble metal chloride salt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  dimethyl sulfoxide. The amine-titanium dioxide is                  titanium dioxide with amino groups on the                  surface. The amine-titanium dioxide,                  2,6-pyridinedimethanol, terephthalamide, and                  cesium carbonate are included in ratio of 20-100                  mg: 0.5 mmol: 1 mmol: 2.2 mmol. Preferred Method:                  In the method, amine-titanium dioxide is added to                  the organic solvent, and part of                  2,6-pyridinedimethanol, terephthalamide and                  cesium carbonate are added for first reaction,                  and then remaining of amount of                  2,6-pyridinedimethanol, terephthalamide, and                  cesium carbonate are added for the second                  reaction to obtain titanium dioxide-covalently                  bonded triazine framework heterojunction                  material, where the part is 1/5 of the reaction                  amount. The first reaction is carried out at                  90-110&#176; C for 10-15 hours. The second                  reaction is carried out at 115-125&#176; C                  for 10-15 hours and then at 150-170&#176; C                  for 40-55 hours. The illumination reaction is                  carried out at room temperature.
   EXAMPLE - 60 mg Amine-titanium dioxide was dispersed in                30 ml dimethyl sulfoxide solution and sonicated for                30 min to form homogeneous suspension. Then, 1/5                part of 0.1 mmol 2,6-pyridinedimethanol, 0.2 mmol                terephthalamide and 0.44 mmol cesium carbonate were                added and reacted at 100&#176; C for 12 hours                to obtain prepolymer. Then, the remaining amount of                0.4 mmol 2,6-pyridinedimethanol, 0.8 mmol                terephthalamide, and 1.76 mmol cesium carbonate                were added to the reaction system and reacted at                120&#176; C for 12 hours and then heated to                160&#176; C for 48 hours. After the reaction                was completed, centrifuged and washed three times                with dilute hydrochloric acid, water, ethanol and                tetrahydrofuran in turn, and then vacuum-dried to                obtain titanium dioxide-covalently bonded triazine                framework heterojunction material.

   USE - The carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has simple preparation process and high absorption rate, and utilizes sugar resources. The method: utilizes hydrothermal method to convert sugar into other type of high-efficiency photocatalyst having photocatalytic properties; and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.

The mass ratio between metal salt, sugar and distilled water is 0.01-10:0.2-200:10-2000 g/ml. Preferred Method: In step (i), mixing is carried out by uniformly stirring at room temperature for 5-10 minutes. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out in a reactor, and the filling rate of the reactor lining is 30-90%. In step (ii), the centrifugal rotation speed is 2000-16000 revolutions/minute, and the centrifuging time is 1-30 minutes. The washing is carried out by alternately washing with distilled water and ethanol for multiple times, and the drying is carried out by vacuum drying at 70-90 degrees C for 12-36 hours.

PN CN110756193-A; CN110756193-B
TI Preparation of electrochemical reduction carbon            dioxide catalyst for preparing gas diffusion electrode,            by dissolving copper salt and indium salt in solvent,            adding dimethylimidazole with benzenetricarboxylic            acid, and hydrothermal reacting.
AU QIAO J
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   PENG L
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 202013894V
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical reduction carbon dioxide                catalyst is prepared by dissolving copper salt and                indium salt in solvent, adding dimethylimidazole                with benzenetricarboxylic acid, uniformly mixing to                form precursor solution, transferring into reaction                kettle, hydrothermal reacting, naturally cooling,                centrifuging, washing with methanol, drying to                obtain precursor, putting into tubular furnace,                introducing nitrogen gas for 1-3 hours, and                calcining at 250-450 degrees C for 4-24                hours.
   USE - Th method is used for preparing                electrochemical reduction carbon dioxide catalyst                for preparing gas diffusion electrode                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product improves specific surface area,                increases catalytic reduction catalytic activity of                the catalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, inhibits                hydrogen evolution reaction, and controls different                anion-doped precursor anion species and doping                concentrations. It improves catalytic activity and                selectivity and current density of electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid and                carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt is                  copper nitrate. The copper nitrate is copper                  nitrate trihydrate and/or copper nitrate                  hexahydrate. The indium salt is indium nitrate or                  indium sulfate. The indium nitrate is indium                  nitrate hydrate. The indium sulfate is indium                  sulfate hydrate. The concentration of copper                  salt, indium salt, dimethylimidazole and                  benzenetricarboxylic acid is 0.01-0.05 M,                  0.01-0.05 M, 0.001-0.005 M and 0.001-0.005 M,                  respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  deionized water or methanol. TI Shortening onset time of drag reducing agent involves cutting dried block drag reducing polymer solid into pieces, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor and reacting, adding liquid carbon dioxide and grinding additive and sieving.
AU DAI X
   LIU H
   LI L
   LIU F
   WANG Y
   LI J
AE UNIV SHENGLI COLLEGE CHINA PETROLEUM (UYSH-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHENGLI COLLEGE CHINA PETROLEUM (UYSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2521A
AB    NOVELTY - Shortening onset time of drag reducing agent involves cutting dried block drag reducing polymer solid into 5 cm pieces, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor and then reacting at -10 degrees C to 30 degrees C for 30 minutes, maintaining temperature for 4-12 hours and then removing carbon dioxide, maintaining temperature for 10-30 minutes, adding liquid carbon dioxide and then processing for 5-20 minutes, crushing using crusher and then ball milling, adding grinding additive and then grinding at less than -40 degrees C, adding 20% release agent and then ball milling, sieving and then dispersing in suspension system and processing.
   USE - The method is useful for shortening onset time of drag reducing agent.

PN FR3119885-A3
TI Method for separating helium from mixture            containing helium and carbon dioxide as well as other            component, involves cooling and separating portion of            first permeate by partial condensation step to produce            carbon dioxide-enriched liquid and carbon            dioxide-depleted gas.
AU LE GULUDEC E
   MARTY P
   GAERTNER A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022A6087X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating mixture (1) by                permeation in first permeation module (M1) to form                nonpermeate gas (7) depleted in helium (15). A                first permeate (5) enriched in helium is                compressed, and the first compressed permeate (11)                is sent to second permeation module (Mn) to form                second non-permeate gas (13) depleted in helium. A                second permeate enriched in helium is compressed                and separated by adsorption to produce helium-rich                product (19). A portion (27) of the first permeate                is cooled and separated by partial condensation                step to produce carbon dioxide-enriched liquid (35)                and carbon dioxide-depleted gas (33).
   USE - Method for separating helium from mixture                containing helium and carbon dioxide as well as                other component.
   ADVANTAGE - The Separation of helium from mixture by                permeation and partial condensation is performed                effectively in improved and efficient manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the arrangement for separating helium from                mixture containing helium and carbon dioxide as                well as other component.
   Mixture (1)
   Permeate (5)
   Non-permeate gas (7,13)
   Compressed permeate (11)
   Helium (15)
   Helium-rich product (19)
   Portion of first permeate (27)

PT P
PN WO2018015828-A1
TI Hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce syngas containing composition i.e. used to produce e.g. methanol, involves contacting used chromium oxide supported catalyst with hydrogen and carbon dioxide at specific temperature and pressure.
AU MAMEDOV A
   REA C
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   REA C (REAC-Individual)
GA 201807433E
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce a syngas containing composition comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, comprises contacting a used chromium oxide supported catalyst with hydrogen and carbon dioxide at a temperature of at least 600 degrees C, a pressure greater than atmospheric pressure, and a combined gas flow rate of hydrogen and carbon dioxide of at least 54 ml/minute to produce the syngas containing composition.
   USE - The process is useful for hydrogenating the carbon dioxide to produce a syngas containing composition comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where the syngas containing composition is used as an intermediate or as feed material to form a chemical product or many chemical products, where the chemical product is methanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: achieves carbon dioxide conversion of at least 50%, preferably 99% and thus provides syngas composition containing less than 5 mol.% methane or no methane by utilizing the used chromium oxide supported catalyst at elevated temperatures and pressures, where the chromium oxide supported catalyst exhibits improved stability/life-time; and enables unreacted reactant gas to be recycled and included in the reactant feed to maximize the overall conversion of carbon dioxide to syngas, thus increasing the efficiency and commercial value of the carbon dioxide to syngas conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The temperature is 610-650 degrees C, preferably 615-620 degrees C. The pressure is at least 0.5 MPa, and where the hydrogen gas flow rate is at least 3.5 times greater than the carbon dioxide gas flow rate. The pressure is 1-3 (preferably 2-3) MPa, and where the combined gas flow rate of hydrogen and carbon dioxide comprises a hydrogen gas flow rate of 70-100 (preferably 80-90) ml/minute, and a carbon dioxide gas flow rate of 15-30 (preferably 20-25) ml/minute, or the pressure is 0.5-1.5 MPa, and where the combined gas flow rate of hydrogen and carbon dioxide comprises a hydrogen gas flow rate of 40-50 ml/minute, and a carbon dioxide gas flow rate of 10-20 ml/minute. The volume ratio of the hydrogen to carbon dioxide during contacting is 2.5:1-5:1. The process further comprises using the produced syngas mixture as an intermediate or as feed material in a subsequent synthesis to form a chemical product or many chemical products. The subsequent synthesis consists of methanol production, olefin synthesis, aromatics production, hydroformylation of olefins, carbonylation of methanol or carbonylation of olefins. Preferred Components: The catalyst contains 5-30 wt.% of chromium. The catalyst comprises 0.2-30 wt.% of at least one of lithium, potassium, cesium or strontium. The supported catalyst is a used chromium oxide/alumina catalyst that has been previously used as a catalyst for a dehydrogenation reaction. The syngas composition has a hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of 1:1-5:1, preferably 4:1. The syngas composition comprises less than 5 mol.% of an alkane, where the alkane is methane.
   EXAMPLE - 3 ml Chromia/alumina dehydrogenation catalyst which was priorly subjected to reduction at 600 degrees C under 25 vol.% hydrogen in argon for 2 hours was placed on top of inert material inside the fixed bed type reactor with a 2.5 cm inner diameter and 40 cm in length. Then the catalyst was contacted with hydrogen and carbon dioxide at a temperature of 620 degrees C, a pressure of 2.8 MPa, and hydrogen flow rate of 84 cc/minute and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 21 cc/minute to obtain syngas containing composition.

PN CN113707923-A
TI Method for fixing carbon dioxide in waste gas based on sodium-carbon dioxide battery, involves providing new cathode and new cathode, anode and solid electrolyte after recovery processing are re-assembled into new sodium-carbon dioxide battery.
AU DAI Y
   YANG B
   MA W
   XIE Z
   XU B
   LEI Y
   HOU M
   YANG X
   LIANG F
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021E1929V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a sodium-carbon dioxide battery. The sodium-carbon dioxide battery comprises a positive electrode, a solid electrolyte and a negative electrode. The anode is provided with the product is removed. The sodium carbonate and carbon in the product on the anode are separated and recycled to obtain the anode after recycling treatment. A new cathode is provided. The new cathode, the anode and the solid electrolyte after recovery processing are re-assembled into a new sodium-carbon dioxide battery. The waste gas outlet is provided for fixing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for fixing carbon dioxide in waste gas based on sodium-carbon dioxide battery, from industrial production, land transport, aviation, shipshipping and resident consumption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high efficiency, low cost, simple operation, environment-friendly, large scale application based on sodium-carbon dioxide battery fixing method of carbon dioxide in waste gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating method for fixing carbon dioxide in waste gas based on sodium-carbon dioxide battery. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The positive electrolyte is sodium salt solution including sodium acetate (CH3COONa), sodium hydroxide (NaOH), sodium chloride (NaCl), sodium perchlorate (NaClO4), sodium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide (NaFSI), and sodium sulfate (Na2SO4), and the catalytic layer includes carbon paper and a catalyst supported on carbon paper. The catalyst is one of nano-gold, platinum carbon and ruthenium carbon. The content of platinum in platinum carbon is 10-50 %mass, and the content of ruthenium in ruthenium carbon is 5-50 %mass. The negative electrode comprises a metal negative electrode and a negative electrode electrolyte, and the metal negative electrode is metal sodium. The composition of the ester electrolyte includes (0.1-1) mol/L sodium perchlorate/((ethyl carbonate+dimethyl carbonate)(1:1)+lvol% fluoroethylene carbonate) , the ether electrolyte composition includes (0.11)mol/L sodium perchlorate/((tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether+dimethoxyethane)(1:1)). The proportion of water in the electrolytic solution is not more than 6ppm. The solid electrolyte is one of sodium-zirconium-silicon-phosphorus oxide (Na3Zr2Si2PO12) sodium superionic conductor (NASICON) structure fast ion conductor, beta-alumina fast ion conductor and aluminum oxide (Al2O3) fast ion conductor. The negative electrode is sodium biphenyl solution, and the sodium biphenyl solution is formed by dissolving biphenyl and metallic sodium in ethylene glycol dimethyl ether solution. The step of reassembling the new negative electrode, the recycled positive electrode and the solid electrolyte into a new sodium-carbon dioxide battery is performed in an argon glove box with less than 0.1 ppm water and less than 0.1 ppm oxygen. The exhaust gas comprises automobile exhaust gas or industrial exhaust gas, and the industrial exhaust gas comprises electrolytic aluminum exhaust gas, blast furnace exhaust, converter exhaust gas or thermal power plant exhaust gas. The step of separating and recovering sodium carbonate and carbon in the product on the positive electrode includes using a preset liquid to clean the positive electrode with the product to remove the carbonic acid on the positive electrode sodium and carbon. The preset liquid is deionized water or distilled water.

The selection of                the individual components of the carbon dioxide                absorber ensures that the carbon dioxide can be                converted very efficiently into other valuable                substances either from the solution or after                separate separation.

GA 201833316L
AB    NOVELTY - The nozzle (100) has a fluid path block (130) fixed in a carbon dioxide inlet port to flow liquid carbon dioxide. Multiple flow line holes (131) are formed as micro-channel shape. An outlet (110) is connected in the flow line holes for passing the carbon dioxide. The flow line holes are phase-changed at snow state of liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide is included with solid particle. An exhaust plenum (120) flows liquid carbon dioxide between the carbon dioxide inlet port and the fluid path block. The outlet forms a gap as slit type between first and second blocks (101, 102).
   USE - Nozzle for injecting carbon dioxide of snow state on a cleaning object i.e. organic LED (OLED).
   ADVANTAGE - The nozzle emits solid particle of the carbon dioxide with the injection nozzle so as to prevent substrate damage by coasting from being generated on a surface of the cleaning object when the carbon dioxide is emitted. The nozzle improves jet uniformity of the carbon dioxide by utilizing the injection nozzle, forms the fluid path block in the flow line holes of the micro-channel shapes and minimizes distance between multiple flow line holes so as to prevent pressure difference of discharging forward and backward, pulverize large amount of carbon dioxide solid particle, improve cleaning efficiency, and simplify a connection structure by forming the flow line hole of the micro-channel shapes on the fluid path block. The nozzle supplies gas for preventing the freezing around the injection unit when carbon dioxide is emitted and emits snow from being surrounded up on the cleaning object while performing surrounding of discharge opening and carbon dioxide. The nozzle prevents particle from being frozen in an outlet outer surface of the injection nozzle when the carbon dioxide is discharged in the outlet of the injection nozzle.

TI Vacuum temperature swing adsorption used for separating carbon dioxide, involves supplying gas mixture into feed input end of adsorbent bed and adsorbent material comprising zeolite, passing purge gas, and recovering carbon dioxide.
AU RAVIKOVITCH P I
   SHOLL D
   PAUR C
   STROHMAIER K G

   KULKARNI A R
   AWATI R V
   KAMAKOTI P
AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 2020A6305D
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum temperature swing adsorption involves subjecting the feed gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide to an adsorption process by supplying the feed gas mixture into a feed input end of an adsorbent bed and an adsorbent material selective for adsorbing carbon dioxide, stopping the supply of feed gas mixture to the adsorbent bed before breakthrough of carbon dioxide from the product output end of the adsorbent bed, heating the adsorbent bed to a temperature (T2) higher than the temperature (T1), passing a purge gas, performing desorption of at least a portion of carbon dioxide from the adsorbent bed and recovering at least a portion of carbon dioxide. The adsorbent bed comprises a feed input end and a product output end. The adsorbent material comprises one or more of zeolite having a silicon to aluminum ratio of more than 100 with a CAS framework structure, or zeolite with a framework structure and having silicon to aluminum ratio of 1-100.
   USE - Vacuum temperature swing adsorption used for separating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes zeolite materials with improved adsorption capacity for gas contaminant, process efficiency and high desorption temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Vacuum temperature swing adsorption involves subjecting the feed gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide to an adsorption process by supplying the feed gas mixture into a feed input end of an adsorbent bed and an adsorbent material selective for adsorbing carbon dioxide, stopping the supply of feed gas mixture to the adsorbent bed before breakthrough of carbon dioxide from the product output end of the adsorbent bed, heating the adsorbent bed to a temperature (T2) higher than the temperature (T1), passing a purge gas which is free of carbon dioxide through the adsorbent bed which results in reduction of pressure in the adsorption bed to a pressure (P2), performing desorption of at least a portion of carbon dioxide from the adsorbent bed and recovering at least a portion of carbon dioxide. The adsorbent bed comprises a feed input end and a product output end. The adsorbent material comprises one or more of zeolite having a silicon to aluminum ratio of more than 100 with a CAS framework structure, or zeolite with a framework structure comprising AFT, AFX, CAS, DAC, EMT, EUO, HEU, IMF, IRR, IRY, ITH, ITT, KFI, LAU, MFS, MRE, MTT, MWW, NES, PAU, RRO, RWY, SFF, STF, STI, SZR, TER, TON, TSC, TUN and/or VFI and having silicon to aluminum ratio of 1-100 and potassium cation concentration of 0-100%. The adsorbent bed is operated at pressure (P1) and temperature (T1), in which at least a portion of the carbon dioxide in the feed gas mixture is adsorbed by the adsorbent bed and gaseous product depleted in carbon dioxide exits in the product output end of the adsorbent bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The temperature (T1) is (-20 degrees C)-(80 degrees C), preferably 0-50 degrees C. The pressure (P1) is the partial pressure of carbon dioxide of 0.5-7 bar. The temperature (T2) is (50 degrees C)-(250 degrees C), preferably 175-225 degrees C. The pressure (P2) is the partial pressure of carbon dioxide of 0.05-0.5 bar, preferably 0.09-0.4 bar. The adsorbent material has a working capacity of about 3.0-14.0 mmol/cc. The zeolite preferably has silicon to aluminum ratio of 1-20, and potassium cation concentration of 0-40%. Preferred Components: The feed gas mixture is a natural gas stream. The adsorbent bed comprises open flow channels throughout its entire length, in which the feed gas mixture is passed. The adsorbent bed is a parallel channel contactor. The adsorbent material comprises one or more of zeolite comprising framework structure containing CHA, FAU, FER, MFI, RHO and/or UFI.

PN KR2022111218-A
TI Carbon dioxide capture, carbon dioxide            supercritical fluid generation heat pump configuration            and operation method involves sucking turbine low            pressure turbine exhaust into exhaust cooler in gas            turbine, cooling and preheating.
AU YANG G W
   YANG S
AE YANG G W (YANG-Individual)
GA 2022A15771
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capture, carbon dioxide                supercritical fluid generation heat pump                configuration and operation method involves sucking                turbine low pressure turbine exhaust into the                exhaust cooler in carbon dioxide multi-stage gas                turbine, cooling using the carbon dioxide                supercritical fluid and refrigerant oxygen such                that steam is condensed, and collecting circulating                working fluid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide                generated as fuel at -13 degrees C, using oxygen                and nitrogen produced by liquefaction separation of                air as refrigerants, injecting a pure oxygen into                the combustion chamber, and pressurizing carbon                dioxide at 200 degrees C to 20 bar as a circulating                working fluid. After cooling the fluid to -20                degrees C liquid in a carbon dioxide condenser,                pressurizing to the supercritical fluid                supercritical carbon dioxide pre-heater (A),                heating at 90 degrees C to obtain a supercritical                fluid, and preheating. The oxygen is further                preheated and injected into combustion                chamber.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture, carbon dioxide                supercritical fluid generation heat pump                configuration and operation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, efficiently produces                turbine power production, and more power is                produced in the turbine due to the exhaust cooling                effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide capture, carbon dioxide                supercritical fluid generation heat pump                configuration and operation method involves sucking                turbine low pressure turbine exhaust into the                exhaust cooler in carbon dioxide multi-stage gas                turbine, cooling using the carbon dioxide                supercritical fluid and refrigerant oxygen such                that steam is condensed, and collecting circulating                working fluid carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide                generated as fuel at -13 degrees C, using oxygen                and nitrogen produced by liquefaction separation of                air as refrigerants, injecting a pure oxygen into                the combustion chamber, and pressurizing carbon                dioxide at 200 degrees C to 20 bar as a circulating                working fluid. After cooling the fluid to -20                degrees C liquid in a carbon dioxide condenser,                pressurizing to the supercritical fluid                supercritical carbon dioxide pre-heater (A),                heating at 90 degrees C to obtain a supercritical                fluid, preheating at 155 degrees C by the heating                value of refrigerant nitrogen in carbon dioxide                condenser (B), reducing the compressor load by                cooling the compressed air produced by the turbine                shaft-in-line compressor in supercritical carbon                dioxide pre-heater (C), isothermally expanding to                the operating pressure of the combustion chamber                through a diffuser, injecting and recirculating                into the combustion chamber to ensure pure oxygen                combustion, and pre-heating the oxygen pressurized                to the operating pressure of the combustion chamber                at 5 degrees C in the carbon dioxide cooler and                steam pre-heater. The oxygen is further preheated                with nitrogen refrigerant at 80 degrees C in oxygen                pre-heater (D), pre-heating at 150 degrees C in                oxygen pre-heater (E), pre-heating at 430 degrees C                in oxygen preheater (E) of the refrigerant nitrogen                liquefaction cooling system and injected into                combustion chamber such that to perform pure oxygen                combustion, exhaust cooling, and exhaust heat                recovery. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) exhaust heat recovery and carbon dioxide                capture device;

AU HANSEUNGWOO
   LEE M W
   MIN L S
   ROH S
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202244929A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring carbon dioxide emissions (S210) according to driving revolution per minute (RPM) of a car-pillar type construction equipment. A database is constructed using the obtained carbon dioxide emission (S220). The database is applied to an artificial neural network analysis to generate an algorithm for predicting the carbon dioxide emits (S230) according to the driving RPM of the construction equipment, where the database comprises a ranking for each case according to size of the emission. Difference between the emission values of the cases is calculated by adding 1.5 integrated quotient ratio (IQR) to the emission value.
   USE - Method for predicting carbon dioxide emissions of construction equipment using artificial neural network analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting emission of carbon dioxide generated per hour according to type of the construction equipment by using artificial neural network analysis, so that emission of the carbon dioxide can be minimized in planning or construction stage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart for explaining a method of predicting carbon dioxide emission of a caterpillar type construction equipment. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Acquiring carbon dioxide emissions (S210)
   Database is constructed using the obtained carbon dioxide emission (S220)
   Predicting the carbon dioxide emits (S230)
   Input unit receives one information from the driving RPM of the construction equipment (S240)
   Calculates the total carbon dioxide emission by using the predicted carbon dioxide emission (S250)

   USE - Method for prefabricating tundish protective atmosphere by carbon dioxide used in steel metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide to replace the traditional argon gas as protective casting atmosphere medium, which greatly reduces the use cost of the gas medium. The carbon dioxide density is greater than argon density, about equal to 1.5 times of air density, better for air replacement effect. At the same time, it can utilize the steel enterprise to produce carbon dioxide convenient for resource recycling with less carbon discharge.

PN KR2314939-B1
TI Lithium-carbon dioxide battery for use in Mars exploration machine, comprises negative electrode made of lithium, anode made of ruthenium nanoparticles and carbon material, and molten salt electrolyte.
AU JU K
   BAEK K
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 2021C0688J
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium-carbon dioxide battery comprises negative electrode made of lithium, anode made of ruthenium nanoparticles and carbon material, and molten salt electrolyte.
   USE - Lithium-carbon dioxide battery for use in a Mars exploration machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium-carbon dioxide battery has improved carbon dioxide capture ability by using molten salt electrolyte and ruthenium catalyst, and high-output and long-life lithium-carbon dioxide battery that is driven in a specific temperature range and low current is manufactured very easily for obtaining effect of improving productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for manufacturing positive electrode for lithium-carbon dioxide battery, which involves preparing carbon material, mixing ruthenium precursor with carbon material to form slurry, applying slurry to carbon paper, and reducing ruthenium precursor by applying electric current to carbon paper coated with slurry to form ruthenium nanoparticles; and
   (2) a method for manufacturing lithium-carbon dioxide battery, which involves preparing negative electrode and positive electrode, and preparing by injecting molten salt electrolyte into glass microfiber separator between negative electrode and positive electrode, where anode is ruthenium nanoparticles and molten salt electrolyte is nitrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The negative electrode is selected from lithium metal, lithium alloy metal, lithium carbide, and lithium oxide. The carbon material is selected from carbon black, acetylene black, furnace black, channel black, ketjen black, Denka (RTM: denka acetylene black) black, carbon paper, super P (Super P), carbon isotope (Carbon-13C), carbon fiber, carbon nanotubes, fullerenes, graphene, activated carbon, graphite, pyrolytic graphite, fluorinated carbon, glassy carbon, glassy network carbon, and carbon compounds. The molten salt electrolyte is selected from lithium nitrate, potassium nitrate, cesium nitrate, sodium nitrate, calcium nitrate, rubidium nitrate, beryllium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, strontium nitrate, radium nitrate, lithium nitrite, potassium nitrite, cesium nitrite, sodium nitrite, calcium nitrite, rubidium nitrite, beryllium nitrite, magnesium nitrite, strontium nitrite or radium nitrite. The ruthenium precursor is ruthenium compound. The carbon paper comprises copper metal. Preferred Conditions: The size of ruthenium nanoparticles is 1.0-10.0 nanometer (nm). The ruthenium nanoparticles are distributed on surface of positive electrode. The lithium-carbon dioxide battery is capable of outputting more than 30 milliwatts per square centimeter (mW/cm2) power at a temperature of 150 degrees C. The lithium-carbon dioxide battery is capable of running for more than 300 cycles at current density of 10 ampere/gram (A/g). The mass ratio of carbon material and ruthenium precursor is 2:1 wt.%. The slurry is applied at 0.1-1.0 milligram/square centimeter (mg/cm2) on the carbon paper. The carbon paper coated with slurry is dried at a high temperature in a vacuum oven. A collector for supplying an electric current is connected to the carbon paper. The collector is selected from foil, wire, sheet, matrix, and combinations. A current of 5-10 ampere/second is supplied to the carbon paper for 1-60 seconds. The ruthenium nanoparticles are distributed on the surface of the carbon paper.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN114349607-A
TI Method for selective hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize dimethoxymethane, involves taking carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methanol as raw materials, and reacting with heterogeneous copper-based catalyst.
AU SUN Y
   XIA L
   WANG H
   LIU X
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 202258040A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for selective hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize dimethoxymethane, involves taking carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methanol as raw materials, and reacting with heterogeneous copper-based catalyst.
   USE - Method for selective hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize dimethoxymethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the heterogeneous copper-based catalyst, realizes one-step synthesis of dimethoxymethane with carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methanol, improves atom utilization efficiency, simplifies separation operation, and reduces energy consumption. The method has low catalyst dosage, high catalytic efficiency, easy separation of catalyst, convenient for practical industrial operation. The raw materials of the heterogeneous copper-based catalyst are readily available, the cost is low, and the synthesis method is fast and simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heterogeneous copper-based catalyst comprises copper, a doped metal and a carrier, and the copper and the doped metal are supported on the carrier. The doping metal is selected from one or more of iron, ruthenium, osmium, cobalt, rhenium, iridium, nickel, palladium and platinum. The carrier is selected from one or more of zinc oxide, silica, aluminum oxide, titania and zirconia. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of copper is 0.1-50 wt.% based on total weight of the heterogeneous copper-based catalyst. The loading amount of the doping metal is 0.01-5 wt.% based on total weight of the heterogeneous copper-based catalyst. The molar ratio of methanol to carbon dioxide is 1:(0.01-100). The pressure ratio of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:10-10:1. The added amount of the heterogeneous copper-based catalyst is 0.01-1 wt.% based on the total weight of methanol. Preferred Process: The method further involves discharging a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen and/or performing catalyst separation process. The catalyst is separated by solid-liquid separation method.

   R5 = 1-6C alkyl (optionally substituted), preferably methyl or trifluoromethyl.

   (2) electrolytic solution comprising (I) and/or (II); and

PT P
PN JP2021159813-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation collection apparatus has separation device to selectively permeate carbon dioxide contained in a gas flowing into first space into second space by separation membrane, and sealed structure, and water separator.
AU TAGUCHI K
   KUSUYAMA T
   SUZUKI M
AE PANASONIC IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (MATU-C)
GA 2021B5545K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation collection apparatus comprises carbon dioxide separation device (1) to selectively permeate carbon dioxide contained in the carbon-dioxide-containing gas flowing into 1st space (3) into 2nd space (4) by a separation membrane (2); substantially sealed structure communicating with 2nd space; water storage unit which collects water provided in the space; pump (10) which pressure-reduces the 2nd space; and water separator (11) which separates moisture content from gas discharged from the exit of the pump.
   USE - A carbon dioxide separation collection apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation collection apparatus can supply water vapor to 2nd space of a carbon dioxide separation device at less than or equal to 100 degrees C, reducing partial pressure of carbon dioxide in 2nd space and improving partial pressure of water vapor. Moreover, when the temperature of the gas of the 2nd space to which water vapor is supplied becomes lower than the temperature of the carbon-dioxide-containing gas which flows in into 1st space, the temperature of the carbon-dioxide containing gas in the separation-membrane vicinity of the 1st space divided by the separation membrane also becomes low. The relative humidity of the carbon-dioxide containing gas of the separation-membrane vicinity also goes up, and the moisture content of a separation membrane improves. Transmission rate of carbon dioxide can improve the transmission rate of carbon dioxide in the separation membrane comprised with a high material, so that moisture content is high.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide separation collection apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Carbon dioxide separation device (1)
   Separation membrane (2)
   1st space (3)
   2nd space (4)
   Pump (10)
   Water separator (11)

TI Synthesizing MER framework type zeolite for            separating carbon dioxide from process feedstream, by            providing reaction mixture comprising faujasite (FAU)            framework type zeolite, potassium cations, hydroxide            ions and water, and crystallizing.
AU THOMPSON J A
   XIE D
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 202115012C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing a MER framework type                zeolite, involves (a) providing a reaction mixture                comprising (i) a faujasite (FAU) framework type                zeolite, (ii) a source of potassium cations, (iii)                a source of hydroxide ions, and (iv) water, and (b)                subjecting the reaction mixture to crystallization                conditions sufficient to convert the FAU framework                type zeolite to the MER framework type                zeolite.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing MER                framework type zeolite for separating carbon                dioxide from process feedstream (claimed) in                sorptive separation such as selective separation of                carbon dioxide from methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a potassium-form MER framework type                zeolite having a stick-like morphology, where the                potassium is present as potassium ion in                extra-framework locations and the zeolite is                essentially free of an extra-framework cation other                than potassium; and
   (2) a process for separating carbon dioxide                from a process feedstream, which involves (a)                contacting an adsorbent bed comprising a                potassium-form MER framework type zeolite with a                process feedstream comprising carbon dioxide and a                second component comprising methane and/or nitrogen                at a first pressure and first temperature, (b)                adsorbing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide                in the adsorbent bed, (c) producing a carbon                dioxide-lean product stream, where the carbon                dioxide-lean product stream having a lower                concentration of carbon dioxide by vol.% than the                process feedstream, and (d) producing carbon                dioxide-rich product stream at a second pressure                and second temperature, where the carbon                dioxide-rich product stream having a higher                concentration of carbon dioxide by vol.% than the                process feedstream, where the potassium-form MER                framework type zeolite has a stick-like morphology,                the potassium is present as potassium ion in                extra-framework locations, and the zeolite is                essentially free of an extra-framework cation other                than potassium, or (a) contacting a first side of a                membrane comprising a potassium-form MER framework                type zeolite with a process feedstream comprising                carbon dioxide and a second component comprising                methane and/or nitrogen at a first pressure and                first temperature, (b) collecting a first permeate                stream from a second side of the membrane at a                second pressure and a second temperature, where the                first permeate stream comprises components that                selectively permeate through the membrane and the                first permeate stream has a higher concentration of                carbon dioxide by vol.% than the process                feedstream, and (c) collecting a first retentate                stream, where the first retentate stream has a                lower concentration of carbon dioxide by vol.% than                the process feedstream, where the potassium-form                MER framework type zeolite has a stick-like                morphology, the potassium is present as potassium                in extra-framework locations, and the zeolite is                essentially free of an extra-framework cation other                than potassium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The reaction mixture                  comprises silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide at a                  molar ratio of 4-10, preferably 4-7,                  potassium/silicon dioxide at a molar ratio of                  0.30-0.80, preferably 0.50-0.70, hydroxy/silicon                  dioxide at a molar ratio of 0.30-0.80, preferably                  0.50-0.70, and water/silicon dioxide at a molar                  ratio of 5-20, preferably 5-15. The                  potassium-form MER framework type zeolite has a                  molar ratio of silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide of                  4-10. The amount of potassium on an anhydrous                  basis is 0.1-5 wt.% based on the weight of the                  zeolite. The extra-framework cation other than                  potassium is present on an anhydrous basis in an                  amount of less than 0.1 wt.% based on the weight                  of the zeolite. Preferred Components: The                  potassium-form MER framework type zeolite is in                  its as synthesized form. The process feedstream                  is a natural gas, a flue gas or a biogas.                  Preferred Conditions: The crystallization is                  carried out at 125-200 degrees C. The synthesized                  MER zeolite is at least partially dehydrated at a                  temperature of at least 150 degrees C. Preferred                  Properties: The potassium-form MER framework type                  zeolite has a mean crystal size of 1-10 pm and a                  mean aspect ratio of 2-6.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN216825562-U
TI Carbon dioxide capture system comprises carbonation device for converting carbon dioxide in air into calcium carbonate, preheating device, calcining furnace, and carbon dioxide storage device.
AU WANG H
   CAI H
   HUANG Y
   YAO G
   BAI Y
   ZHONG D
   PENG S
   ZHOU X
   AN H
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 202294010E
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims type carbon dioxide capture system, the carbon dioxide capture system comprises a carbonation device, a pre-heating device, a calcining furnace and a carbon dioxide storage device, the carbonation device is used for converting the carbon dioxide in the air into calcium carbonate, the pre-heating device is used for receiving and preheating the calcium carbonate discharged from the carbonation device, the calcining furnace is used for receiving the calcium carbonate discharged from the preheating device, and the calcium carbonate is used for heating the calcium carbonate into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, the preheating device is further used for receiving at least one of the calcium oxide and the carbon dioxide discharged from the calcining furnace to preheat the calcium carbonate, the carbon dioxide storage device communicate with the preheating device, to store the carbon dioxide discharged by the preheating device inside The carbon dioxide capturing system of the utility model type high temperature waste heat high utilization rate and low system energy consumption.

PN KR2376476-B1; KR2022031205-A
TI Method for predicting carbon dioxide emissions of construction equipment using linear regression analysis, involves acquiring carbon dioxide emission according to driving revolutions per minute of construction equipment and field variables.
AU HANSEUNGWOO
   LEE M W
   KIM Y
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 2022418020
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring carbon dioxide emissions according to driving revolution per minute (RPM) of a construction equipment and field variables for case. A database is established using the carbon dioxide emissions obtained for the case. An algorithm is generated for predicting the carbon dioxide emissions according to the driving RPM of the construction equipment by applying the database to linear regression analysis. Carbon dioxide emissions are predicted by applying input information to a prediction algorithm. Total carbon dioxide emissions are calculated using the predicted carbon dioxide emissions.
   USE - Method for predicting carbon dioxide emissions of a construction equipment using linear regression analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting amount of carbon dioxide emitted per hour according to type of the construction equipment by using linear regression analysis so as to minimize amount of the carbon dioxide in planning or construction stage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a Method for predicting carbon dioxide emissions of a construction equipment using linear regression analysis. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC S03-E14P; T01-J03; T01-J05A2C; T01-J05B4P

PN CN108526141-A; CN208680044-U
TI Substrate spraying pre-treatment carbon dioxide snow cleaning system, has filter whose inlet is connected to inlet of supercharger, and nozzle whose inlet is connected to air introducing system, where supercharger is connected with heater.
AU ZHANG C
   KONG F
   TANG X
   YAO S
   LI S
AE SCIVIC ENG CORP (CHNM-C)
   CHINA AUTOMOBILE ENG CORP (CHNM-C)
GA 201875365S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank whose lower part is stored with liquid carbon dioxide. An upper part of the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank is connected with a gaseous carbon dioxide output pipe. A lower part of a gas phase carbon dioxide check valve is connected with a liquid phase carbon dioxide output pipe. An output end of the liquid-phase carbon dioxide output pipe is connected with an inlet of a carbon dioxide filter. An inlet of the carbon dioxide filter is connected to an inlet of a supercharger. The supercharger is connected with a heater. An inlet of a nozzle is connected to a compressed air introducing system.
   USE - Substrate spraying pre-treatment carbon dioxide snow cleaning system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces energy consumption, wastage of water, and improves cleaning effect, stability and working efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a substrate spraying pre-treatment carbon dioxide snow cleaning system.
