PT P
PN EP3372298-A1; US2018257027-A1; JP2018144024-A; CN108570686-A; EP3372298-B1; US11207640-B2; CN108570686-B; JP7137317-B2
TI Electrochemical device for e.g. modifying carbon            dioxide concentration, comprises catalyst layer which            reduces carbon dioxide in feed gas to form ionic            carrier component, and layer which oxidizes carrier            component to form carbon dioxide.
AU DESAI D
   BAKER RIVEST J L
   IFTIME G
   VEDHARATHINAM V
   CHINTAPALLI M
   WEI J
   DORIS S E
   BAKER R J L
   EVTAIM G
   DORIS S M
AE PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
   PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
   PALO ALTO RES INST INC (PRCA-C)
   PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
GA 2018707147
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical device (10) comprises a                cathode chamber including an inlet which receives a                feed gas comprising carbon dioxide, a reduction                catalyst layer in the cathode chamber which reduces                carbon dioxide in the gas to form an ionic carrier                component, an anode chamber with an outlet which                outputs a gas comprising carbon dioxide, an                electrolyte membrane which spaces the anode chamber                from the cathode chamber, and comprising an ionic                liquid, an oxidation catalyst layer in the anode                chamber which oxidizes the ionic carrier component                to form carbon dioxide, and an energy source,                electrically connected with at least one of the                reduction catalyst layer and the oxidation catalyst                layer, and provides energy for the reduction and                oxidation. The membrane transports the ionic                carrier component between the cathode chamber and                the anode chamber.
   USE - Electrochemical device is used as carbon                dioxide sensor in carbon dioxide detection system,                electrolytic carbon dioxide pumping apparatus and                electrolytic carbon dioxide removal apparatus for                modifying carbon dioxide concentration in interior                space (claimed) of refrigeration unit and heating                ventilation and air conditioning system used in                building, automobile, submarine, ship, aircraft and                spacecraft. Can also be used for increasing partial                pressure of carbon dioxide in environment for                applications such as greenhouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-power and low-temperature                electrochemical device is easy to install, has high                performance, long life, and can maintain carbon                dioxide concentration to a safe level of -1000 ppm                or less, reduce the outdoor retake ventilation                portion of heating ventilation and air conditioning                system load by 60%, continuously remove carbon                dioxide from indoor air at less than 70 degrees C,                and decrease in-air recirculation in building, to                reduce the overall energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for modifying carbon dioxide                concentration in an interior space, which involves                providing the electrochemical device,                electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to ionic                carrier component in the cathode chamber, ionically                transporting the ionic carrier component through                the membrane, electrochemically oxidizing the ionic                carrier component to carbon dioxide in the anode                chamber, and supplying gas from one of the cathode                chamber and the anode chamber to the interior space                to modify the carbon dioxide concentration in an                interior space; and
   (2) method for carbon dioxide transport, which                involves electrochemical reducing carbon dioxide                gas to a mobile ionic component at an electrode                (e1), transporting the mobile ionic component                across a solid electrolyte membrane and oxidizing                the ionic component to carbon dioxide at an                electrode (e2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of an                environment in which a carbon dioxide                electrochemical device operates.
   Electrolytic carbon dioxide removal apparatus                (1)
   Electrochemical device (10)
   Indoor space (12)
   Air inlet conduit (14)
   Air outlet conduit (16)

PT P
PN US2022204899-A1
TI System for biologically generating methane and            removing carbon dioxide from it, has primary anaerobic            digester, electrochemical reactor comprising reactor            cell including anode, and hydrogenotrophic            methanogenesis bioreactor.
AU LIN Y J
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
GA 2022863377
AB    NOVELTY - System for biologically generating methane and                removing carbon dioxide from it, comprises: (a) a                primary anaerobic digester adapted and arranged to                generate a gaseous mixture comprising methane and                carbon dioxide from organic materials; (b) an                electrochemical reactor (112) comprising at least                one reactor cell including an anode spaced from a                cathode by a porous ion exchange resin wafer with a                cation exchange membrane between the anode and the                resin wafer and a bipolar ion exchange membrane                between the cathode and the resin wafer; and (c) a                hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis bioreactor (120)                adapted and arranged to convert the bicarbonate and                hydrogen from the electrochemical reactor to                methane. The primary anaerobic digester includes a                gas transfer line (110, 124). The electrochemical                reactor further includes a first gas vent line                (114); an outlet operably connected to a liquid                transfer line (116); and a hydrogen transfer line                (118).
   USE - The system is useful for biologically                generating methane and removing carbon dioxide from                it; and for biologically generating carbon                dioxide-depleted biogas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system generates high-purity renewable                methane rather than just biogas (a mixture of                methane and carbon dioxide). In addition, utilizing                electrochemically produced hydrogen and carbon                dioxide captured as aqueous bicarbonate in a                hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis bioreactor boosts                the overall methane production by at least about                40% and reduces capital and production costs by at                least about 20% compared to traditional anaerobic                digestion processes. The method can produce methane                with reduced carbon dioxide content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an electrochemical reactor for capturing                carbon dioxide and bicarbonate and generating                hydrogen; comprising reactor cells in a stack with                each reactor cell including: (a) an anode; (b) a                cathode spaced from the anode; (c) a porous ion                exchange resin wafer between the anode and the                cathode; (d) a cation exchange membrane between the                anode and the resin wafer; and (e) a bipolar ion                exchange membrane between the cathode and the resin                wafer; where: (A) the cathode of each adjacent                reactor cell is separated from the anode of the                nearest adjacent cell by an electrical insulator;                (B) the reactor cells are in fluid communication                with each other in series so that liquid and gas                flow through each resin wafer sequentially; and (C)                the electrochemical reactor includes a hydrogen                transfer line to direct hydrogen gas from the                cathodes out of the electrochemical reactor; (d)                the electrochemical reactor is adapted and arranged                to capture gaseous carbon dioxide within the resin                wafer as aqueous bicarbonate, and to                electrochemically generate hydrogen gas at the                cathodes when an aqueous liquid and a carbon                dioxide-containing gas are flowing through the                reactor cells, and a voltage is applied across the                anode and the cathode of each reactor cell;                and
   (2) biologically generating carbon                dioxide-depleted biogas which involves: (a)                generating a biogas comprising methane and carbon                dioxide by anaerobic degradation of biological                material in an anaerobic digester; (b) passing the                biogas generated in step (a) through an                electrochemical reactor comprising an anode spaced                from a cathode by a porous ion exchange resin wafer                with a cation exchange membrane between the anode                and the resin wafer and a bipolar ion exchange                membrane between the cathode and the resin wafer,                while applying a voltage across the anode and the                cathode to convert carbon dioxide in the biogas to                bicarbonate and to generate hydrogen gas by                electrochemical water splitting at the cathode; (c)                venting and collecting the methane from the biogas                that passes through the electrochemical reactor;                (d) passing the hydrogen and bicarbonate formed in                step (b) into a hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis                bioreactor; (e) generating methane from the                hydrogen and bicarbonate in the hydrogenotrophic                methanogenesis bioreactor; and (f) venting and                collecting the methane that forms in the                hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis bioreactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system                for biologically generating a methane-containing                biogas and removing carbon dioxide from the biogas                as bicarbonate.
   Gas transfer line (110, 124)
   Electrochemical reactor (112)
   Gas vent line (114)
   Liquid vent line (116)
   Hydrogen transfer line (118)
   Methanogenesis bioreactor (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Device: The primary anaerobic                  digester comprises: (i) an acidogenesis reactor                  (104) adapted and arranged to biologically                  convert organic waste and wastewater to soluble                  volatile fatty acids (VFA), methane, carbon                  dioxide, and hydrogen; and (ii) an acetoclastic                  methanogenesis reactor (106) adapted and arranged                  to biologically convert the VFA to carbon dioxide                  and methane; where the gas transfer line is in                  fluid communication with both the acidogenesis                  reactor and the acetoclastic methanogenesis                  reactor; and the acidogenesis reactor is in fluid                  communication with the acetoclastic                  methanogenesis reactor via a liquid feed line                  (108); and where in use, VFA, carbon dioxide,                  hydrogen and methane are generated in the                  acidogenesis reactor; VFA-containing liquid from                  the acidogenesis reactor is fed into the                  acetoclastic methanogenesis reactor via the                  liquid feed line; methane and carbon dioxide are                  generated in the acetoclastic methanogenesis                  reactor, and carbon dioxide, methane, and                  hydrogen are transferred from the acidogenesis                  reactor and the acetoclastic methanogenesis                  reactor into the inlet end of electrochemical                  reactor through the gas transfer line. The                  electrochemical reactor comprises at least one                  porous gas and liquid flow distributor in contact                  with the resin wafer; the flow distributor being                  adapted and arranged to laterally distribute gas                  and liquid within pores prior to entering the                  resin wafer of the electrochemical reactor. At                  least one porous gas and liquid flow distributor                  includes interconnected pores having an average                  pore size of about 100-600 micrometers, and is                  adapted and arranged to laterally distribute the                  liquid and gas bubbles having an average diameter                  of about 100-600 micrometers throughout the flow                  distributor and into the resin wafer of the                  electrochemical reactor. The electrochemical                  reactor comprises the electrochemical reactor                  cells in a stack with the cathode of each                  adjacent cell separated from the anode of the                  nearest adjacent cell by an electrical insulator;                  and the reactor cells are in fluid communication                  with each other in series so that liquid and gas                  flow through each resin wafer sequentially. The                  electrochemical reactor comprises at least one                  porous gas and liquid flow distributor in contact                  with at least one of the resin wafers in the                  stack; and the porous gas and liquid flow                  distributor is adapted and arranged to distribute                  gas within the liquid flowing through the resin                  wafers of the electrochemical reactor. The porous                  gas and liquid flow distributor includes                  interconnected pores having an average pore size                  in the range of about 100-600 micrometers, and is                  adapted and arranged to laterally distribute the                  liquid and gas bubbles having an average diameter                  in the range of about 100-600 micrometers                  throughout the flow distributor and into the                  resin wafers of the electrochemical reactor. The                  electrochemical reactor comprises 2-50 reactor                  cells. The electrochemical reactor further                  comprises a gas vent line for venting a carbon                  dioxide depleted gas from the resin wafers of the                  electrochemical reactor. Further, the                  electrochemical reactor comprises at least one                  porous gas and liquid flow distributor in contact                  with at least one of the resin wafers in the                  stack; and the porous gas and liquid flow                  distributor is adapted and arranged to distribute                  gas within the liquid flowing through the resin                  wafers of the electrochemical reactor. The                  anaerobic digester comprises: (i) an acidogenesis                  reactor adapted and arranged to biologically                  convert organic waste and wastewater to soluble                  VFA, methane, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; and                  (ii) an acetoclastic methanogenesis reactor                  adapted and arranged to biologically convert the                  VFA to carbon dioxide and methane; where in use,                  VFA, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methane are                  generated in the acidogenesis reactor;                  VFA-containing liquid from the acidogenesis                  reactor is fed into the acetoclastic                  methanogenesis reactor; methane and carbon                  dioxide are generated in the acetoclastic                  methanogenesis reactor, and carbon dioxide,                  methane, and hydrogen are transferred from the                  acidogenesis reactor and the acetoclastic                  methanogenesis reactor into electrochemical                  reactor.Preferred Parameter: Each wafer has a                  thickness of about 1-20 mm.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN KR2021040670-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction generator for electrochemically converting carbon dioxide and generating electricity simultaneously, comprises e.g. mixed aqueous solution input unit for supplying mixed aqueous solution containing carbon dioxide.
AU EOM T H
   HOON C M
   SHIM J G
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 202140231A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction generator comprises a mixed aqueous solution input unit (110) for supplying a mixed aqueous solution containing carbon dioxide; an anode electrode (120) for performing oxidation reaction; a cathode electrode (130) for supplying carbon dioxide of the mixed aqueous solution from the mixed aqueous solution input unit to undergo chemical reaction; a cation exchange membrane (140) arranged between the anode electrode and the cathode electrode; and a carbon dioxide reduction generator for discharging the reduced product generated by reduction from the cathode electrode, where the anode electrode is at least one of zinc, zirconium, aluminum, magnesium, sodium, potassium or lithium.
   USE - The carbon dioxide reduction generator is useful for converting carbon dioxide (claimed) electrochemically and generating electricity simultaneously.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reduction generator having catalyst minimizes energy use and improves reaction selectivity. The method has large-capacity production and simple, utilizes low cost materials, can drastically reduce the consumption of electric energy and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide conversion method using the carbon dioxide reduction generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide reduction generator for converting carbon dioxide electrochemically and generating electricity simultaneously.
   Mixed aqueous solution input unit (110)
   Anode electrode (120)
   Cathode electrode (130)
   Cation exchange membrane (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The catalyst of the cathode electrode comprises at least one of silver, gold, palladium, gallium, mercury, tin, lead and copper. The mixed aqueous solution comprises at least one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and lithium hydroxide.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

   BARNARD COLLEGE (BARN-Non-standard)
   UNIV COLUMBIA NEW YORK (UYCO-C)
   UNIV ICELAND (UYIC-Non-standard)
   CARBFIX (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8361X
AB    NOVELTY - Abatement of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide involves dissolving pressurized carbon                dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide-rich gas stream in                pressurized water stream by merging pressurized                water stream and carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide-rich gas stream at a depth, (H1) in                injection pipe (206), keeping dissolved carbon                dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide in solution in                water stream by transferring water stream from                depth (H1) to a depth (H1+H2) at a downward flow                velocity (v(W)), resulting from the buoyant force                on bubbles of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide gas in water stream at depth (H1+H2),                keeping the resulting pH value of pressurized water                stream containing dissolved carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide, of 2-4, preferably 3.2, and                injecting pressurized water stream comprising                dissolved carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide                into a geological reservoir comprising reactive                rocks at (H1+H2) or at a depth higher than depth                (H1+H2).
   USE - Abatement of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide from combustion of fossil fuels, power                plants, and geothermal fluids, in geological                reservoir e.g. (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is effective, economical, and                environmentally-friendly, and enables abatement of                carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide with                decreased leakage by ensuring the pressure of the                dissolved carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide                which is less than the hydraulic pressure of the                water, and ensuring carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide stays dissolved in the water, increasing                downward flow velocity of the water by simply                reducing the diameter of the pipe surrounding a                merging point of the carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide-rich gas stream and the water, and                promoting carbonation of the host rock and                facilitating long-term storage of carbon dioxide in                the subsurface by injecting carbon dioxide, and                reduces the gas emission from geothermal power                plants, without producing by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Abatement of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide involves pumping or transferring water from                a water source into an outer pipe of an injection                well (210), creating a pressurized water stream in                outer pipe (205), pumping carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide-rich gas into an injection pipe of                an injection well, creating carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide-rich gas stream comprising                pressurized carbon dioxide and/or pressurized                hydrogen sulfide-rich gas, in the injection pipe,                dissolving all of pressurized carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide-rich gas stream in pressurized                water stream by merging pressurized water stream                and carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide-rich gas                stream at a depth, ((H1) which is more than 0),                where the hydraulic pressure of water in outer pipe                (P(W)), is lower than the pressure of carbon                dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide, in injection pipe,                keeping dissolved carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide in solution in water stream by transferring                water stream from depth (H1) to a depth (H1+H2) in                which (H1+H2) is higher than depth (H1), at a                downward flow velocity (v(W)), which at depth                (H1+H2) is higher than the upward flow velocity of                carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide gas,                resulting from the buoyant force on bubbles of                carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide gas in water                stream at depth (H1+H2), keeping the resulting pH                value of pressurized water stream containing                dissolved carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide,                of 2-4, preferably 3.2, and injecting pressurized                water stream comprising dissolved carbon dioxide                and/or hydrogen sulfide into a geological reservoir                comprising reactive rocks at (H1+H2) or at a depth                higher than depth (H1+H2). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide                abatement system.

PN CN212762470-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide cooling system, has supercritical carbon dioxide output end for outputting supercritical carbon dioxide of certain target pressure, where supercritical carbon dioxide is sprayed out to cooling area through electromagnetic valve.
AU YAO Y
AE CHEN X (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202132880M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical carbon dioxide cooling system, comprising a supercritical carbon dioxide output end, a high pressure transmission pipe, an electromagnetic valve; degree sensor and a controller; the supercritical carbon dioxide output end is used for outputting supercritical carbon dioxide of a certain target pressure; the supercritical carbon dioxide output end is connected with the high pressure transmission pipe and outputs supercritical carbon dioxide to the high pressure transmission pipe; the electromagnetic valve is set on the high pressure transmission pipe and keeps the closed state; the supercritical carbon dioxide in the high pressure transmission pipe keeps the target pressure; the temperature sensor is used for measuring the actual temperature of the cooling area; the controller is connected with the supercritical carbon dioxide output end; the high pressure transmission pipe; the electromagnetic valve and the temperature sensor; the controller according to the set target temperature and the actual temperature measured by the temperature sensor sends different periods and pulse width of the electric pulse to control the opening time and degree of the electromagnetic valve, so as to control the output quantity of the supercritical carbon dioxide, supercritical carbon dioxide is sprayed to the cooling area through the electromagnetic valve.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide scrubber compartment (100)                comprises an adsorbent (1), a reaction vessel (10)                in which the adsorbent is arranged, and a water                adjustment unit (20) that supplies water to the                reaction vessel, and discharges water from the                reaction vessel. The device removes carbon dioxide                from a to-be-treated carbon dioxide-containing gas                by contacting the to-be-treated carbon                dioxide-containing gas with the adsorbent.
   USE - Scrubber compartment is used for removing                carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing gas                containing carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide and                sulfur oxide, discharged from thermal power station                and an iron mill.
   ADVANTAGE - The scrubber compartment enables efficient                removal of carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide-containing gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for recovery                of carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of adsorbent,                which involves supplying water to the vessel for                contacting water with the adsorbent, and                discharging the water from the vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide scrubber compartment.
   Adsorbent (1)
   Reaction vessel (10)
   Water adjustment unit (20)
   Water supply flow path (21)
   Water discharge flow path (22)
   Water supply amount adjustment unit                (23)
   Water discharge amount adjustment unit                (24)
   Temperature adjustment unit (25,41,51)
   Temperature detection unit (40)
   Water recovery unit (50)
   Carbon dioxide scrubber compartment                (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide                  scrubber compartment further comprises a carbon                  dioxide adsorption capacity detection unit, a                  temperature adjustment unit (25) that adjusts                  temperature of water to be supplied to the                  vessel, a temperature adjustment unit (41) that                  adjusts temperature inside the vessel, and a                  water recovery unit (50) that recovers water                  discharged from the vessel, a temperature                  adjustment unit (51) that adjusts temperature of                  water in the water recovery unit. The water                  adjustment unit comprises a water supply flow                  path (21) that supplies water to the vessel, a                  water supply amount adjustment unit (23) that                  adjusts amount of water supplied from the water                  supply flow path to the vessel, and a water                  discharge amount adjustment unit (24) that                  adjusts amount of water discharged from the                  vessel to a water discharge flow path (22). The                  water supply amount adjustment unit adjusts the                  amount of water to be supplied to the vessel                  based on carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of                  the adsorbent detected by the carbon dioxide                  adsorption capacity detection unit, and based on                  temperature of the adsorbent detected by a                  temperature detection unit (40). Preferred                  Process: The recovery of carbon dioxide                  adsorption capacity of adsorbent further involves                  supplying water to the vessel for contacting                  water with the adsorbent, detecting temperature                  of the adsorbent, and heating or cooling the                  inside of the vessel based on the detected                  temperature of the adsorbent, and discharging the                  water from the vessel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent                  comprises a metal oxide containing rare earth                  element and/or zirconium.

PT P
PN CN114436259-A
TI Recycling tail gas from ethylene oxide/ethylene            glycol device, involves recycling part of first gas            material flow obtained from top of the water washing            tower to reaction system, and introducing rest of the            first gas material flow into membrane separation            system.
AU WANG L
   SONG X
   LI J
   ZHONG L
   AN T
AE SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202266871M
AB    NOVELTY - The method for recycling tail gas from an                ethylene oxide/ethylene glycol device involves:                recycling a part of a first gas material flow                obtained from a top part of the water washing tower                (1) to a reaction system, introducing the rest of                the first gas material flow into a membrane                separation system for separation to obtain a second                gas material flow rich in carbon dioxide and a                third gas material flow poor in carbon dioxide;                introducing the second gas material flow into a                carbon dioxide absorption tower (4), obtaining a                fourth gas material flow at the tower top and a                first liquid material flow at the tower bottom;                introducing the first liquid phase material flow                into a carbon dioxide desorption tower (5),                obtaining a fifth gas material flow which can enter                a carbon dioxide refining unit (6) from the tower                top, and obtaining a second liquid phase material                flow which can be recycled to the carbon dioxide                absorption tower.
   USE - Method for recycling tail gas of ethylene                oxide/ethylene glycol device.
   ADVANTAGE - Before entering the carbon dioxide absorption                tower, the gas at the top of the water washing                tower passes through the membrane separation                system, most of the gas returns to the reaction                system again, and only a small part of the gas rich                in argon and carbon dioxide enters the carbon                dioxide absorption tower, so that the equipment                investment and the later operating cost of the                carbon dioxide absorption tower and the desorption                tower are greatly reduced. The method ensures that                the argon at the top of the tower returns to the                reaction system after being enriched and cooled and                subjected to gas-liquid separation, and can achieve                the aim of preventing the accumulation of the argon                without emptying. The carbon dioxide at the top of                the carbon dioxide desorption tower can be further                refined to obtain carbon dioxide with different                purities, so that the emission of the carbon                dioxide is reduced, the requirement of clean                production is met, and the economic benefit is                higher.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for recycling tail gas of ethylene oxide/ethylene                glycol device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of a                process for the tail gas recovery of an ethylene                oxide/ethylene glycol device.
   1Water washing tower
   2Filter
   3Membrane separation system
   4Carbon dioxide absorption tower
   5Carbon dioxide desorption tower

where the                  specifically process is carried out by, (i)                  determining the single pot stirring amount and                  stirring time T of the mixer according to prepared by stirring mill device, (ii) adding                  cement of mass M through a cement feed port of                  the stirring mill device, adding water from a                  water inlet in proportion, starting a stirring                  motor, and mixing the cement slurry, (iii)                  starting the stirring, introducing pre-stored                  carbon dioxide gas into the stirring chamber                  through an air inlet, recording the concentration                  of carbon dioxide gas in mixing chamber using a                  carbon dioxide concentration meter, and                  maintaining the carbon dioxide gas concentration,                  (iv) continuously stirring, flowing the cement                  slurry absorbing carbon dioxide into a storage                  silo through a partition plate during stirring                  process, replenishing the cement of mass M is                  every grinding time t, adding water in                  proportion, and replenishing the cement slurry in                  mixing chamber, (v) opening the discharge port of                  storage bin every stirring time T, placing the                  cement slurry into the concrete mixer to                  synchronize with the feed and recycle production,                  and (vi) using concrete mixer, and adding                  aggregate and other concrete materials to mix the                  concrete.

GA 201855229Q
AB    NOVELTY - An optimal regulation model constructing                method involves obtaining the photosynthesis data                samples of crop seedlings, analyzing the effects of                light intensity and ambient temperature on crop                photosynthetic rate to construct a right-angle                hyperbolic correction model of carbon dioxide                response and utilizing the curve curvature theory                to obtain the maximum curvature of the carbon                dioxide response curve at different temperatures                and different light intensities. PN CN113087003-A
TI Utilizing and adsorbing carbon dioxide involves contacting mixed gas containing carbon dioxide with calcium oxide and absorption liquid.
AU ZHAO X
   XIONG R
   ZHUO J
   DONG Y
   LI Y
AE CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 202180256H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing and adsorbing carbon dioxide involves contacting a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide with calcium oxide and an absorption liquid to obtain an absorption suspension containing calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is used or utilizing and adsorbing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method changes the high energy-consuming regeneration process in the carbon dioxide capture process into the on-site conversion of carbon dioxide, reduces the cost of carbon dioxide capture, produces chemicals and carbonates with certain economic benefits, saves energy, is environmentally-friendly, and has higher economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves separating the absorption suspension containing calcium carbonate to obtain calcium carbonate and the regenerated absorption liquid,and recycling the regenerated absorption liquid. The method involves contacting mixed gas and calcium oxide with absorption liquid, or contacting the mixed gas with absorption liquid first, and adding calcium oxide. Preferred Components: The mixed gas is flue gas or other gas containing carbon dioxide, where the content of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 5-100%, preferably 5-30%. The molar ratio of the calcium oxide to carbon dioxide in mixed gas is (0.5-3):1, preferably (0.8-1.5):1. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the calcium oxide is 1-20 mu m, preferably 5-15 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorption liquid comprises amino acids and/or amino acid salts, solvents and optionally organic amines. The absorption liquid comprises 5-60 %mass, preferably 10-35 %mass amino acid salt, 1-60 %mass, preferably 1-15 %mass amino acid, and/or when the absorption liquid comprises an amino acid and an amino acid salt, the mass ratio of the amino acid salt to the amino acid is (2-9):1, preferably (3-6):1. The amino acid is chosen from glycine, alanine, valine, leucine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, proline, tryptophan, serine, tyrosine, cysteine, methionine, asparagine, threonine, aspartic acid, glutamic acid, lysine, arginine and/or histidine, amino acid potassium salt, amino acid sodium salt and/or amino acid lithium salt. The amino acid salt is chosen from glycinate, alanine salt, valine salt, leucine salt, isoleucine salt, phenylalanine salt, proline salt, tryptophan salt, serine salt, tyrosine amate, cysteine, methionine, asparagine, threonine, aspartate, glutamate, lysine, arginine and/or histidine. The solvent is chosen from water, 1-6C carboxylic acid and 1-6C alcohol, water, formic acid, acetic acid, methanol and/or ethanol. The organic amine is chosen from aliphatic amines, alcohol amines, amides, alicyclic amines, aromatic amines and/or naphthalene amines. The absorbing liquid further comprises a corrosion inhibitor comprising organic phosphonic acid and its salt, polycarboxylic acid and its salt, and vanadic acid and its salt, sodium vanadate and/or potassium vanadate.

PN JP2021074657-A
TI Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus of carbon-cycle system, has NOx removing unit that removes nitrogen oxides from combustion exhaust gas, and dehumidifying unit removes water from exhaust gas supplied from combustor to recovery unit.
AU SETOYAMA N
   IMAGAWA H
   YAMAMOTO S
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 202153629Q
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorbent that adsorbs carbon dioxide. A CO2 recovery unit is supplied with the combustion exhaust gas discharged from a combustor (5) and recovers carbon dioxide from the supplied combustion exhaust gas. A NOx removing unit removes nitrogen oxides from the combustion exhaust gas supplied from the combustor to the CO2 recovery unit. A dehumidifying unit (20) removes water from the combustion exhaust gas supplied from the combustor to the CO2 recovery unit. The NOx removing unit has a NOx adsorbent that adsorbs nitrogen oxides. The dehumidifying unit removes water from the combustion exhaust gas on the upstream side of the NOx removing unit. A control unit controls switching by the upstream switching valve.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus of carbon-cycle system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fall of the recovery performance of a carbon dioxide is suppressed. Since carbon can be circulated, the emission amount of the carbon dioxide to system outside is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The combustion exhaust gas is supplied to the CO2 recovery unit through the NOx removal unit when the concentration of nitrogen oxides detected by the upstream detection unit is greater than the first predetermined value. The CO2 recovery unit does not allow the combustion exhaust gas to pass through the NOx removal unit when the concentration of nitrogen oxides detected by the upstream detection unit is equal to or less than the first predetermined value. The downstream side detection unit detects the concentration of the nitrogen oxide which is positioned in the downstream side of the NOx removal unit, and is contained in the combustion exhaust gas. A downstream switching valve is controlled to make the CO2 recovery supply unit through the remover of the other of first remover or second remover. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a hydrocarbon generation apparatus has a generator that produces a hydrocarbon compound using hydrogen and carbon dioxide recovered by the CO2 recovery unit;
   (2) a carbon-cycle system has a hydrocarbon compound that is generated by the generator is burned, and the combustor emits the combustion exhaust gas; and
   (3) a carbon-dioxide collection method, involves removing the water from combustion exhaust gas and removing nitrogen oxides from the combustion exhaust gas from which water has been removed in the dehumidification step. The carbon dioxide is recovered from the combustion exhaust gas from which nitrogen oxides have been removed in the NOx removal step using a CO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the carbon-cycle system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon-cycle system (1)
   Combustor (5)
   Methane formation apparatus (6)
   Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus (10)
   Dehumidifying unit (20)

PN CN112266546-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder composite PVC building template by dispersing Paulownia wood powder in ethanol solution, adding citric acid, heat-preserving, filtering, treating, mixing dispersant, extruding and shaping.
AU ZHU P
AE JIESHOU SHENGXIANG NEW BUILDING MATERIALS CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 202111904P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder composite PVC building template involves (i) uniformly dispersing Paulownia wood powder in ethanol solution, uniformly stirring, adding ethylenediamine and citric acid to ethanol solution, heat-preserving, stirring, filtering, washing and drying, (ii) dividing the pretreated Paulownia wood powder into two portions (a) and (b), adding portion (a) to a gasifier, passing nitrogen and feeding in carbon dioxide, naturally cooling to obtain carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder, treating, and drying to obtain fumigated Paulownia powder, (iii) mixing ethylene bis(stearamide) and stearic acid to obtain a dispersant, and (iv) adding carbon dioxide activated Paulownia wood powder, fumigated Paulownia wood powder, dispersant, and PVC resin into a mixer, uniformly mixing, moving to a twin-screw extruder, pressing into a plate, and cooling and shaping.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder composite PVC building template.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves mechanical properties of the building template, strengthens the tensile strength, and reduces the elongation at break to a small extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder composite PVC building template involves (i) uniformly dispersing Paulownia wood powder in ethanol solution, uniformly stirring, adding ethylenediamine and citric acid to ethanol solution, adjusting the temperature to 55-60 degrees C, heat-preserving in a water bath, stirring for 2 hours, filtering again, washing with clean water to neutral, and drying to constant weight to obtain pretreated Paulownia powder, (ii) dividing the pretreated Paulownia wood powder into two portions (a) and (b), adding pretreated Paulownia wood powder portion (a) to a gasifier, passing nitrogen into the gasifier to exhaust air in the gasifier, adjusting the temperature to 600-650 degrees C, maintaining at constant temperature for 10-14 minutes, adjusting and feeding in carbon dioxide, controlling the volume fraction of carbon dioxide to 10-12%, adjusting the temperature to 680-700 degrees C, continuing constant temperature for 6-8 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain carbon dioxide activated Paulownia powder, treating pretreated Paulownia powder portion (a) with steam fumigation for 30 minutes, and adding to a drying room for drying to obtain fumigated Paulownia powder, (iii) mixing ethylene bis(stearamide) and stearic acid at 70-75 degrees C with high-speed stirring to obtain a dispersant, and (iv) adding carbon dioxide activated Paulownia wood powder, fumigated Paulownia wood powder, dispersant, and polyvinyl chloride resin into a mixer, uniformly mixing, moving to a twin-screw extruder to extrude and plasticize into a rubber material, moving the rubber material to a mold in compression molding machine, pressing into a plate, and cooling and shaping to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of the ethanol solution is 20 %mass. The mixing mass ratio of the Paulownia powder and the ethanol solution is 1:(6-8). The mixing weight ratio of ethylenediamine, citric acid and ethanol solution is (1.2-2):(0.3-0.5):(45-50). The mass ratio of portion (a) of Paulownia wood powder and portion (b) of Paulownia wood powder is (1-2):1. The mixing weight ratio of the carbon dioxide activated paulownia powder, dispersant and PVC resin is (25-30):(4-7):(55-58). Preferred Conditions: The steam fumigation temperature is 105 degrees C. The drying in the drying chamber is carried out at 50 degrees C for 12 hours. The ethylene bis(stearamide) and stearic acid are mixed together in a mass ratio of 4:(1-1.5).
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)
MC A04-E02E1; A04-E03E; A08-M10; A08-R07; A08-S01; A11-A03; A11-B07; A11-B11; A11-B13; A12-R01; F05-B; P63-A30

PN CN108329947-A
TI Energy solution useful for energy and power generation, boilers, and civil energy of automobiles, trains and airplanes.
AU LU Z
AE LU Z (LUZZ-Individual)
GA 2018607246
AB    NOVELTY - Energy solution, is claimed. The energy solution obtained by reacting water with calcium carbide to produce acetylene as fuel, and produced calcium hydroxide by the reaction reacts calcium dioxide to recover carbon dioxide, reacting calcium carbide with water to form acetylene (C2H2) and calcium hydroxide (CaOH2), and releases the heat as Q1, reacting 2 moles of calcium carbide and 4 moles of water to produce 2 moles of calcium hydroxide, 2 moles calcium carbide and releasing heat as 12 moles of Q, calcium carbide molecules CaC2 plus 4 water molecules H2O, two acetylene molecules C2H2, two acetylene molecules C2H2 and five oxygen molecules O2 react, generating 4 moles of carbon dioxide molecules and 2 water moles of H2O, and releasing the heat Q2, during the combustion process, releasing two moles of C2H2 molecules and four moles of CO2, and two moles of calcium hydroxide molecules Ca(OH)2, reacting with four carbon dioxide molecules CO2.
   USE - The energy solution acetylene is useful for energy and power generation, boilers, and civil energy of automobiles, trains and airplanes, driving absorption air conditioner.(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The energy solution: is environmentally-friendly, saves energy, recovers cost of purchasing calcium carbide and, thus economical; releases calcium hydrogencarbonate, which is useful in food and feed additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy solution, is claimed. The energy solution obtained by reacting water with calcium carbide to produce acetylene as fuel, and produced calcium hydroxide by the reaction reacts calcium dioxide to recover carbon dioxide, reacting calcium carbide with water to form acetylene (C2H2) and calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2), and releases the heat as Q1, reacting 2 moles of calcium carbide and 4 moles of water to produce 2 moles of calcium hydroxide, 2 moles calcium carbide and releasing heat as 12 moles of Q, calcium carbide molecules CaC2 and 4 water molecules H2O, two acetylene molecules C2H2, two acetylene molecules C2H2 and five oxygen molecules O2 react, generating 4 moles of carbon dioxide molecules and 2 water moles of H2O, and releasing the heat Q2, during the combustion process, releasing two moles of C2H2 molecules and four moles of CO2, and two moles of calcium hydroxide molecules Ca(OH)2, reacting with four carbon dioxide molecules CO2, where the chemical equation for the reaction of sub-Ca(OH)2 with four carbon dioxide molecules CO2 is: reacting two moles of Ca(OH)2, two moles of CO2 to produce two moles of CaCO3, two moles of hydrogen peroxide-calcium carbonate, two moles of carbon dioxide and two moles of water further producing two moles of 2 calcium bicarbonate, adsorbing 4 moles of carbon dioxide molecules during the reaction, producing the carbon dioxide by the fuel during the combustion process and completely absorbing by calcium hydroxide and there is no loss carbon dioxide during the energy consumption process.

PN JP2021037455-A
TI Carbon dioxide recovery system has regenerating unit which heats absorbing solution by heating unit in state where partition plate is below liquid level of absorbing liquid, to dissipate carbon dioxide from absorbing solution in container.
AU TAKADA N
   HANDA Y
   KITAMURA H
   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
GA 202123996P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption unit in which the exhaust gas contains the gas to be processed from which the carbon dioxide is removed. The carbon dioxide is dissipated from the absorption liquid discharged from the absorption unit. A regenerating unit discharges the absorbing liquid that dissipates the carbon dioxide and the exhaust gas containing the carbon dioxide emitted from the absorbing liquid. The regenerating unit includes a container (11) for accommodating the absorbing liquid discharged from the absorbing unit and a heating unit provided in the container. The openings are provided through which the absorbent liquid in the container passes. A partition plate (14) is provided above the heating unit in the container. The regenerating unit heats the absorbing liquid by the heating unit in a state where the partition plate is below the liquid level of the absorbing solution, to dissipate the carbon dioxide from the absorbing liquid in the container.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system realizes the regenerated unit having excellent heating efficiency of absorbent liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The absorption unit brings the gas to be treated containing carbon dioxide into contact with the absorption liquid and discharges the absorption liquid that absorbed the carbon dioxide. An absorption liquid supply port (11a) supplies the absorption liquid discharged from the absorption unit into the container. An absorption liquid discharge port (11b) dissipates the carbon dioxide and discharges the absorption liquid that has passed through the opening of the partition plate. The regenerating unit is provided at a position facing the second end of the heating unit. A baffle plate (13) is provided for changing the direction of the flow of the absorbing liquid flowing in the container from the first end side to the second end side. The baffle plate is coupled with the partition plate. The partition plate is configured such that the aperture ratio of the region having the one or more openings is 10 to 90%. The partition plate has a shape that is convexly curved in a direction away from the heating unit. The heating unit has a shape extending from the first end to the second end. The regenerating unit is provided at a position between the first end and the second end. The baffle plates change the direction of the flow of the absorbing liquid flowing in the container from the first end side to the second end side. The regenerating unit includes the heating units having shape extending in the same direction as the heating unit. The regenerating unit is provided with a partition plate provided between the heating units and having openings through which the absorbing liquid passes. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating carbon dioxide recovery system, which involves absorbing the carbon dioxide by the absorption liquid by contacting the gas to be treated containing carbon dioxide with the absorption liquid. The absorption unit discharges the exhaust gas of the absorption unit containing the gas to be processed from which carbon dioxide is removed. The carbon dioxide is released from the absorption liquid discharged from the absorption unit. The absorption liquid from which the carbon dioxide is released. The regenerating unit discharges the regenerating unit exhaust gas containing the carbon dioxide emitted from the absorbing liquid. The carbon dioxide is dissipated from the absorption liquid in the container, by accommodating the absorption liquid discharged from the absorption unit in the container and heating the absorption liquid by the heating unit while the partition plate is below the liquid surface of the absorption liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional drawing which shows the structure of the reproduction unit.
   Container (11)
   Absorption liquid supply port (11a)
   Absorption liquid discharge port (11b)
   Baffle plate (13)
   Partition plate (14)

PN DE102019111058-A1; WO2020221690-A1; CN113767087-A; EP3962891-A1; US2022234999-A1; JP2022530784-W
TI Producing urea, comprises producing formamide by            reacting carbon dioxide, hydrogen and ammonia with            methyl formate or ammonium formate in catalytic            reaction, and reacting formamide with ammonia in            presence of catalyst.
AU MAKHYNYA Y
   EL-HAWARY T
   GLOTZBACH C
   TENHUMBERG N
   LEITNER W
   KLANKERMAYER J
   SCHUMACHER H
   EL HAWARY T
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 2020A6150S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing urea, comprises (a) producing                formamide by reacting carbon dioxide, hydrogen and                ammonia with methyl formate or ammonium formate as                intermediate in a catalytic reaction, and (b)                reacting formamide with ammonia in presence of                catalyst to obtain urea. The source of carbon                dioxide is a liquid loaded with chemically and/or                physically bound carbon dioxide in methanol phase                or aqueous ammonia solution which is obtained by                gas scrubbing of synthesis gas to remove carbon                dioxide using a scrubbing liquid, where (a1) the                scrubbing liquid is a methanol phase or carbon                dioxide is desorbed from the scrubbing liquid laden                with chemically and/or physically bound carbon                dioxide and absorbed into a methanol phase to                obtain a methanol phase laden with carbon dioxide,                and the methanol phase laden with carbon dioxide as                a stream containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The urea can be produced catalytically via                various routes. The method is large-scale                production of urea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing urea, comprises (a) producing                formamide by reacting carbon dioxide, hydrogen and                ammonia with methyl formate or ammonium formate as                intermediate in a catalytic reaction, and (b)                reacting formamide with ammonia in presence of                catalyst to obtain urea. The source of carbon                dioxide is a liquid loaded with chemically and/or                physically bound carbon dioxide in methanol phase                or aqueous ammonia solution which is obtained by                gas scrubbing of synthesis gas to remove carbon                dioxide using a scrubbing liquid, where (a1) the                scrubbing liquid is a methanol phase or carbon                dioxide is desorbed from the scrubbing liquid laden                with chemically and/or physically bound carbon                dioxide and absorbed into a methanol phase to                obtain a methanol phase laden with carbon dioxide,                and the methanol phase laden with carbon dioxide as                a stream containing carbon dioxide which is reacted                with a stream containing hydrogen in the presence                of a catalyst to form methyl formate and the methyl                formate formed is reacted with a stream containing                ammonia to form formamide; or (a2) the scrubbing                liquid is an aqueous ammonia solution such that                carbon dioxide is at least partially bound in the                form of carbonates in the scrubbing liquid, and                this scrubbing liquid loaded with chemically and/or                physically bound carbon dioxide is a stream                containing carbon dioxide with a stream containing                hydrogen in the presence of catalyst and optionally                organic solvent is reacted with the formation of                ammonium formate or with the formation of ammonium                formate and formamide and the ammonium formate                formed is converted into formamide by heat                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The synthesis gas is a                  synthesis gas for ammonia synthesis and/or the                  gas scrubbing is carried out on a synthesis gas                  obtained from the steam reforming and/or                  subsequent water gas shift reaction, where at                  least partially optionally processed gas includes                  coke oven gas, a blast furnace gas, converter gas                  or an exhaust gas obtained from cement works                  which is used as the synthesis gas, optionally                  mixed with a synthesis gas for the ammonia                  synthesis, where methanol or an aqueous ammonia                  solution is used as scrubbing liquid for gas                  scrubbing to remove carbon dioxide. The                  hydrogen-containing stream is a partial stream of                  the synthesis gas after the gas scrubbing, where                  the partial stream is used in methanation of the                  synthesis gas, or a partial stream of the                  synthesis gas before the gas scrubbing, where                  hydrogen obtained from the work-up of the                  products of urea synthesis is used as the                  hydrogen-containing stream. The ruthenium                  catalyst iss ruthenium-phosphine complex which is                  used as a catalyst for the conversion of                  formamide to form urea or reaction of the                  formamide with ammonia to form urea and/or for                  conversion of carbon dioxide-laden methanol phase                  and the hydrogen-containing stream to form methyl                  formate and/or for conversion of the carbon                  dioxide-laden aqueous ammonia solution and the                  hydrogen-containing stream to form ammonium                  formate or to form ammonium formate and                  formamide. The ruthenium-phosphine complex                  includes at least one monophosphine, diphosphine,                  triphosphine or a compound with more than three                  phosphine groups. The monophosphine is                  monophosphine based compound of formula (PR1R2R3)                  (I). The ruthenium-phosphine complex includes                  non-phosphine ligands including carbene, amine,                  amide, phosphite, phosphoamidite,                  phosphorus-containing ethers or esters, sulfide,                  trimethylene methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl,                  methylallyl, ethylene, cyclooctadiene,                  acetylacetonate, acetate, hydride, halide,                  phenolate and/or carbon monoxide, preferably                  trimethylene methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl,                  methylallyl, ethylene, cyclooctadiene,                  acetylacetonate, acetate, hydride, halide,                  phenolate and/or carbon monoxide. The                  ruthenium-phosphine complex is                  ruthenium-triphosphine complex, where the                  triphosphine is triphosphine based compound of                  formula (R7-C(-CH2-R5R6P)(-CH2-R3R4P)-CH2-R1R2P)                  (II), preferably                  1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane. The                  ruthenium-phosphine complex is                  1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane based                  compound of formula ((A)Ru(L)3) (III), preferably                  (ruthenium(1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane)(                  trimethylene methane)). The ruthenium-phosphine                  complex is used as catalyst, optionally in                  combination with acid, preferably Lewis acid as                  cocatalyst.R1, R2 and R3 = optionally substituted                  alkyl, aryl or heteroaryl, where R2 and R3 is                  phenyl;R1-R6 = optionally substituted aryl or                  heteroaryl, preferably optionally substituted                  phenyl;R7 = H, alkyl, cycloalkyl or aryl;A =                  1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane (II);                  andL = monodentate ligands, where at least two                  monodentate ligands L are replaced by bidentate                  ligand or three monodentate ligands L are                  replaced by a tridentate ligand, and the                  monodentate ligands are preferably trimethylene                  methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl, methylallyl,                  ethylene, cyclooctadiene, acetylacetonate,                  acetate, hydride, halide, phenolate and/or carbon                  monoxide.Preferred Method: The gas scrubbing of the                  synthesis gas to remove carbon dioxide from the                  synthesis gas using the aqueous ammonia solution                  as scrubbing liquid is carried out at less than                  or equal to 100?0C, preferably 30-70 degrees C                  under pressure of 20-50 bar or the gas scrubbing                  of the synthesis gas to remove carbon dioxide                  from the synthesis gas is carried out using                  methanol as scrubbing liquid at pressure of 20-50                  bar and/or methanol cooled to less than or equal                  to -20 degrees C. The ammonia used as the                  ammonia-containing stream and/or for aqueous                  ammonia solution is obtained from the synthesis.                  The catalytic conversion of the formamide formed                  to form urea or the catalytic conversion of the                  formamide formed with ammonia to form urea is                  carried out at 50-250 degrees C, preferably                  140-170 degrees C and/or at pressure of 150 bar,                  preferably 5-40 bar using non-polar or polar                  aprotic organic solvent, preferably 1,4-dioxane.                  The catalytic conversion of the carbon                  dioxide-laden methanol phase and the                  hydrogen-containing stream to form methyl formate                  is carried out (a1) at 20-150 degrees C,                  preferably 80-120 degrees C and/or at pressure of                  40-220 bar, preferably 80-200 bar, and/or the                  reaction of the methyl formate with ammonia to                  formamide is carried out at 20-100 degrees C,                  preferably at 60-80 degrees C, and/or at pressure                  of 70 bar, preferably 45 bar, and/or where the                  catalytic conversion of the aqueous ammonia                  solution loaded with chemically bound carbon                  dioxide and the stream containing hydrogen for                  the formation of ammonium formate or for the                  formation of ammonium formate and formamide is                  carried out (a2) at 60-180 degrees C, preferably                  90-110 degrees C, and/or at pressure of 210 bar,                  preferably 80-190 bar, and/or where the heat                  treatment of the ammonium formate to form                  formamide is carried out at 100-185 degrees C.                  The methanol formed in the reaction of methyl                  formate with the ammonia-containing stream to                  form formamide is reused for scrubbing liquid or                  the methanol-containing liquid in the process,                  where the scrubbing liquid loaded with carbon                  dioxide is used as a source of carbon dioxide.                  The ammonia synthesis comprises using steam                  reforming in a primary reformer and a downstream                  secondary reformer and/or a two-stage water-gas                  shift reaction with high-temperature and                  low-temperature shift stages.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing solid carbon dioxide flame extinguishing            material comprises pouring carbon dioxide gas into            flame retardant polyamine polymer or flame retardant            polyamine polymer aqueous solution, grinding solid            halogenated ammonium bicarbonate salt into powder,            adding film-forming agent and wrapping.
AU MIAO L
   BAI Y
   SUN S
   LIU Y
   CHEN Z
AE 718TH RES INST CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CO (CSHI-C)
GA 2022748104
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material comprises (i) pouring carbon                dioxide gas into the flame retardant polyamine                polymer or the flame retardant polyamine polymer                aqueous solution under pressure of 1.5-10 MPa,                stirring for 0.5-5 hours to obtain solid                halogenated amine bicarbonate salt, where                flame-retardant polyamine polymer is halogenated                amine-based polymer with a molecular weight of                2000-20000, halogen is fluorine or chlorine, and                the hydrogen on the carbon chain of the amine-based                polymer is substituted, the mass fraction of the                flame retardant polyamine polymer in the flame                retardant polyamine polymer aqueous solution is                more than 80%, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide                gas is 0.5-2 l/minutes, and (ii) grinding solid                halogenated ammonium bicarbonate salt into powder,                adding film-forming agent and wrapping to obtain                micro-spherical solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing solid                carbon dioxide flame extinguishing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material can be delivered by drone, which                is convenient for storage, transportation and use,                and is suitable for flame scenarios e.g. high-rise                buildings or extremely cold areas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material comprises (i) pouring carbon                dioxide gas into the flame retardant polyamine                polymer or the flame retardant polyamine polymer                aqueous solution under pressure of 1.5-10 MPa,                stirring for 0.5-5 hours to obtain solid                halogenated amine bicarbonate salt, where                flame-retardant polyamine polymer is halogenated                amine-based polymer with a molecular weight of                2000-20000, halogen is fluorine or chlorine, and                the hydrogen on the carbon chain of the amine-based                polymer is substituted, the mass fraction of the                flame retardant polyamine polymer in the flame                retardant polyamine polymer aqueous solution is                more than 80%, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide                gas is 0.5-2 l/minutes, and (ii) grinding solid                halogenated ammonium bicarbonate salt into powder,                adding film-forming agent and wrapping to obtain                micro-spherical solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material, where film-forming agent is                a concentrated dispersion of                polytetrafluoroethylene of 60 wt.%, the particle                size of the solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material is 0.1-5 &#181;m, and                the density of solid carbon dioxide flame                extinguishing material is 1-3 kg/m3.

PN ES2910147-A1
TI Preparing beer with carbon dioxide nanobubbles which is mechanical, involves recirculating beer with active yeast, extracting beer using extraction pump, subjecting beer to forced injection of carbon dioxide and reintroducing beer into tank.
AU NIZA GONZALEZ E
AE NIZA GONZALEZ E (GONZ-Individual)
GA 2022809605
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing beer with carbon dioxide nanobubbles which is mechanical, comprises: (a) recirculating beer (2) still with active yeast, starting from fermentation tank (1) itself, before end of natural fermentation process; (b) extracting beer using extraction pump (4) through conduit (5) to inlet (6) of injector generator with a certain pressure; (c) inside injector generator, subjecting the beer to a forced injection of carbon dioxide supplied from carbon dioxide tank through conduit through inlet of injector generator and subjecting beer to Venturi effects combining pressure, acceleration, friction, acceleration, specific to and determined by dilution of carbon dioxide in the beer, to obtain beer bubbles with less than 100 nm in diameter in fashion at outlet of injector generator; and (d) reintroducing the beer bubbles into the fermentation tank, incorporating the beer into beer stream saturated with gas, while maintaining it without dissolving or going to free phase, until consumption.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing beer with carbon dioxide nanobubbles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves both a homogenization of size of the carbon dioxide nanobubble in the beer, and so that the beer yeast remains inactive, with the total destruction of microorganisms with antimicrobial activity, it is recirculated as often as convenient depending on the size of the fermentation tank and the pressures and amount of carbon dioxide desired in the beer; provides beer with nanobubbles of carbon dioxide that is constituted in a homogeneous way by all its nanobubbles with a size of less than 100 nm in fashion diameter, and has inactivated or destroyed the microorganisms that cause its fermentation, polydispersity index of 0.2 plus minus 0, and average sizes and polydispersities of the carbon dioxide nanobubbles e.g. number of measurements of 5, medium viscosity of 1054 cP (centipoise), refractive index of 1.62774, dispersion angle of 173 degrees , and wavelength of 633nm at temperature, 25 degrees C; and achieves the destruction of microorganisms, avoiding unwanted fermentations in the final product and speeding up the process of obtaining the same, with certain characteristics of volume and size of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for beer with nanobubbles of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates general view of the layout of the components that put into practice the beer recirculation procedure for preparing beer with nanobubble.
   Fermentation tank (1)
   Beer (2)
   Extraction pump (4)
   Conduit (5)
   Inlet (6)
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108872145-A; CN108872145-B
TI Measurement of exothermic heat of combustion of carbon-heated cigarette heating material involves detecting content of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in flue gas of heating material, and measuring combustion heat release amount.
AU ZHANG K
   WANG J
   LEI P
   YANG J
   DENG N
   ZHANG M
   TANG J
   AI M
   WANG L
   LU D
   LI B
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
   CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   SHANGHAI NEW TOBACCO PROD RES INST (SHAN-Non-standard)
   ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 201897666P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for measuring exothermic heat of combustion of carbon-heated cigarette heating material involves detecting the content of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in flue gas of the carbon-heated cigarette heating material during the mouth-to-mouth suction process, by tunable diode laser absorption spectroscopy, calculating the consumed amount of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen, combining low calorific value and elemental analysis data, performing chemometric derivation and mathematical operations, measuring the combustion heat release amount of the heating material using oxygen bomb calorimeter, calibrating the cigarette smoke gas phase monitoring device to obtain a working curve of oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, measuring the concentrations of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in the flue gas, and evaluating the difference in the concentrations of oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide before and after mouth-to-mouth suction process.
   USE - Measurement of exothermic heat of combustion of carbon-heated cigarette heating material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient measurement of exothermic heat of combustion of carbon-heated cigarette heating material with reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for measuring exothermic heat of combustion of carbon-heated cigarette heating material (CH2mOn) involves detecting the content of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in flue gas of the carbon-heated cigarette heating material during the mouth-to-mouth suction process, by tunable diode laser absorption spectroscopy, calculating the consumed amount of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen, combining the low calorific value and elemental analysis data of the heating material, performing chemometric derivation and mathematical operations, measuring the combustion heat release amount (( Delta )H1 in kJ/g) of the heating material during the pumping process using oxygen bomb calorimeter, calibrating the cigarette smoke gas phase monitoring device with a series of standard gases of known concentrations of oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to obtain a working curve of oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, measuring the concentrations of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in the flue gas, and evaluating the difference in the concentrations of oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide before and after mouth-to-mouth suction process. The combustion heat release amount (( Delta )H1) is calculated based on volumes (V1) and (V2) of inlet and outlet gases, respectively, concentration of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen and water in the atmosphere, reaction enthalpy, standard mass formation enthalpy, and standard molar generation enthalpy.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
