PT P
PN WO2021259760-A1
TI Separating gaseous carbon dioxide from gas mixture            involves contacting gas mixture with sorbent material            to allow gaseous carbon dioxide to adsorb on sorbent            material by flow-through through unit under ambient            atmospheric pressure condition.
AU VARGAS A
   GEBALD C
   ALBANI D
   NIEBEL T
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0767A
AB    NOVELTY - Separating gaseous carbon dioxide from gas                mixture involves contacting gas mixture, such as                ambient atmospheric air or flue gas and biogas                containing gaseous carbon dioxide and gasses                different from gaseous carbon dioxide by cyclic                adsorption/desorption with sorbent material to                allow gaseous carbon dioxide to adsorb on sorbent                material by flow-through through unit under ambient                atmospheric pressure conditions and ambient                atmospheric temperature conditions in an                adsorption, and isolating sorbent material with                adsorbed carbon dioxide in unit from flow-through                during maintaining temperature in sorbent.
   USE - Method for separating gaseous carbon dioxide                from gas mixture, such as ambient atmospheric air                or flue gas and biogas containing gaseous carbon                dioxide and gases different from gaseous carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to effectively separate the                gaseous carbon dioxide from the gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating gaseous carbon dioxide from gas                mixture involves contacting gas mixture, such as                ambient atmospheric air or flue gas and biogas                containing gaseous carbon dioxide and gasses                different from gaseous carbon dioxide by cyclic                adsorption/desorption with sorbent material to                allow gaseous carbon dioxide to adsorb on sorbent                material by flow-through through unit under ambient                atmospheric pressure conditions and ambient                atmospheric temperature conditions in an                adsorption, and isolating sorbent material with                adsorbed carbon dioxide in unit from flow-through                during maintaining temperature in sorbent. A stream                of saturated or super-heated steam is injected by                flow-through through unit, increase of temperature                of sorbent material is induced to a temperature                between 60-110 degrees C, and desorption of carbon                dioxide is started. The desorbed gaseous carbon                dioxide is extracted from unit and gaseous carbon                dioxide is separated from steam by condensation                downstream of the unit. The sorbent material is                brought to ambient atmospheric temperature                conditions, where sorbent material is a solid                inorganic or organic, non-polymeric or polymeric                support material functionalized on surface with                amino functionalities capable of reversibly binding                carbon dioxide with a specific Brunauer, Emmett and                Teller (BET) surface area in the range of 1-20                square meter/gram (m2/g). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for:
   (1) an application of sorbent material, which                is used for separating gaseous carbon dioxide from                a gas mixture, such as ambient atmospheric air,                flue gas and biogas, specifically for direct air                capture using a temperature, vacuum, or                temperature/vacuum swing process, specifically                using a process in which injecting a stream of                saturated or super-heated steam by flow-through is                used for inducing increase of temperature of                sorbent material to a temperature between 60 and                110 degrees C, and starting the desorption of                carbon dioxide; and
   (2) an unit for separating gaseous carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture, which comprises reactor                unit containing sorbent material suitable and                adapted for flow-through gas mixture, where reactor                unit comprises inlet for ambient air, and an outlet                for ambient air during adsorption, adsorber element                comprising support layer and sorbent material                layer, minimum one device, specifically condenser                for separating carbon dioxide from water, where gas                outlet side of device for separating carbon dioxide                from water, specifically condenser is both of a                carbon dioxide concentration sensor and a gas flow                sensor for controlling the desorption                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas mixture is                  ambient atmospheric air. The solid inorganic or                  organic, non-polymeric or polymeric support                  material is organic polymeric support, i.e.                  polystyrene based material, such as styrene                  divinylbenzene copolymer to form sorbent material                  surface functionalised with primary amine,                  specifically benzylamine moieties, where solid                  polymeric support material is obtained in                  emulsion polymerisation process or is a                  non-polymeric inorganic support selected from                  silica, alumina, titanium dioxide, magnesium                  oxide, clays, mixed forms, such as silicon                  dioxide-aluminum dioxide, or mixtures. Preferred                  Compositions: The sorbent material has a nitrogen                  content in the range of 9-15 wt.% or 10-12 wt.%                  in each case for dry sorbent material. Preferred                  Conditions: The sorbent material has a specific                  BET surface area is measured by nitrogen                  adsorption in the range of 4-10 m2/g. The sorbent                  material has a pore diameter distribution                  measured by mercury intrusion, such that 95% of                  pore volume is in the range of 50-250 nanometer                  (nm). The sorbent material has a total pore                  volume measured by Mercury intrusion in the range                  0.15-0.35 cubic centimeter/gram (cm3/g). The                  solid inorganic or organic, non-polymeric or                  polymeric support material is in the form of                  monolith, layer or sheet, hollow or solid fibres,                  specifically in woven or nonwoven structures,                  hollow or solid particles, or extrudates, where                  it takes the form of preferably essentially                  spherical beads with a particle size (D50) in the                  range of 0.30-1.25 mm, or where solid inorganic                  or organic, non-polymeric or polymeric support                  material is in the form of solid particles                  embedded in a porous or non-porous matrix. The                  gas mixture is passing through sorbent material                  with a relative humidity of 75%. The sorbent                  material has a water retention in the range of                  3-30%. Preferred Process: The desorbed gaseous                  carbon dioxide extracted from unit and gaseous                  carbon dioxide separated from steam by                  condensation downstream of the unit is carried                  out where still contacting the sorbent material                  with steam is extrcted by injecting or                  circulating saturated or super-heated steam into                  unit, flushing and purging both steam and carbon                  dioxide from the unit, regulating extraction or                  steam supply to essentially maintain the                  temperature in the sorbent or to essentially                  maintain the pressure in the sorbent.

PN CN114317122-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction agarwood oil extraction and separation comprises allowing supercritical carbon dioxide to enter extraction kettle to extract agarwood powder, separating supercritical carbon dioxide mixture, flowing agarwood extract flows into molecular still and refining.
AU QIU W
   QIU H
AE SHENZHEN QIANHAI TIANXIANGFU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202263087U
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction agarwood oil extraction and separation comprises conversion process including pressurizing the liquid carbon dioxide and then heating up to convert into supercritical carbon dioxide, extraction by allowing the supercritical carbon dioxide to enter the extraction kettle to extract the agarwood powder, where the extraction kettle has at least two groups of working groups and each working group alternately switches the extraction work, separating by heating supercritical carbon dioxide mixture with the extract, entering separation kettle for separation, separating the supercritical carbon dioxide mixture in the separation kettle into carbon dioxide gas and agarwood extract and refining by flowing agarwood extract flows into molecular still and refining to obtain agarwood essential oil and agarwood paste.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction agarwood oil extraction and separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly improve the extraction efficiency and reduce the waste and loss of resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises depressurizing and recovering carbon dioxide in the extraction kettle after the completion of extraction in the extraction kettle, recovering the carbon dioxide gas and reusing after being filtered and condensing. The method comprises conveying the recovered carbon dioxide into carbon dioxide storage tank for storage, arranging the recovery pump, a pressure reducing valve and an emptying valve between the carbon dioxide storage tank and the separation kettle, using the recovery pump to provide recovery power, where pressure reducing valve reduces the carbon dioxide pressure in the extraction kettle to a certain value and using the exhaust valve to recover the carbon dioxide in the extraction kettle. The temperature of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide storage tank is controlled within the range of -50degrees CelsiusC to -20degrees Celsius and control pressure within range of 2-4 MPa. The method comprises pulverizing the agarwood before the conversion step to obtain agarwood powder and respectively filling agarwood powder into each extraction kettle of each working group. In the separation step, the supercritical carbon dioxide is separated by two-stage heating and two-stage separation kettle. The method comprises insulating thermal insulation jacket by a heating device, where heating device includes a heating pump, a heating tank and a heater and heating tank is equipped with a heat exchange medium, allowing the heater to heat the heat exchange medium in the heating tank to 70-80degrees Celsius and pumping the heated heat exchange medium into the thermal insulation jacket by the heating pump. In the refining step, the molecular distiller is in a vacuum state and the vacuum pressure is 0.1-1.0 MPa.Preferred Components: The purity of the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank is greater than 99%. The extraction kettle has two groups of working groups, each of which has three extraction kettles. The outer periphery of the extraction kettle and the separation kettle is wrapped with a thermal insulation jacket, which is used for thermal insulation of the extraction kettle and the separation kettle

GA 2019508520
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus consists of through-port diameter with a source of liquid carbon dioxide comprising compressed air line which has compressed air inlet including a carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment and a carbon dioxide (II) injection capillary segment. The carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment includes a high pressure valve (A) and throttle (1) and which is terminates near the cutting tool, and the carbon dioxide (II) injection capillary segment includes a high pressure valve (B) and throttle (2) which is terminates near the compressed air inlet, and the source of liquid carbon dioxide with a high pressure valve (C). The throttle (1) is smaller than the throttle (2). The valve (c) and valve (a) are opened, and an amount of liquid carbon dioxide which corresponds to the diameter of the through-port of the cutting tool charges the carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment with liquid carbon dioxide at a predetermined charging pressure.
   USE - Apparatus used for cooling through-ported cutting tool e.g. portable drilling machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has excellent cooling lubrication characteristics and physiochemical performance properties under low temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An apparatus consists of a through-port diameter with a source of liquid carbon dioxide comprising a compressed air line which has a compressed air inlet and establishes a flow of compressed air to the cutting tool, in which the compressed air line includes a carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment and a carbon dioxide (II) injection capillary segment which are interconnected to the same source of liquid carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment includes a high pressure valve (A) and throttle (1) and which is terminates near the cutting tool, and the carbon dioxide (II) injection capillary segment has a high pressure valve (B) and throttle (2) which is terminates near the compressed air inlet, and the source of liquid carbon dioxide has a high pressure valve (C). The throttle (1) is smaller than the throttle (2). The valve (c) and valve (a) are opened, and an amount of liquid carbon dioxide which corresponds to the diameter of the through-port of the cutting tool charges the carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment with liquid carbon dioxide at a predetermined charging pressure. The valve (c) is closed, and the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment is mixed into the compressed air near the cutting tool for a predetermined period of time and to predetermined recycle pressure to form a cooling fluid (1), the valve (b) is opened, and residual carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide (I) injection capillary segment flows through the carbon dioxide (II) injection capillary segment, into the compressed air and near the compressed air inlet to form a cooling fluid (2). The flowing cooling fluids (1) and (2) are through cutting tool which is maintained within a certain temperature range.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of refrigerant cycle.
   Charging (100)
   Infusion (102)
   Recycle (106)
   Predetermining charging pressure (108)
   Infusion time (112)

PT P
PN CN113713789-A
TI Humidity-driven carbon dioxide adsorption membrane            deformable structure includes double-layer composite            membrane structure whose are fixed to each other along            width direction, where interior of double-layer            composite membrane structure is equidistantly            constrained along width direction.
AU LIU Y
   LIU Z
   WANG X
   SONG J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021E85174
AB    NOVELTY - The humidity-driven carbon dioxide adsorption                membrane deformable structure includes adsorption                material portion (1), and a polyethylene coating                (2) which is provided on one surface of the                adsorption material main portion to form                double-layer composite membrane structure with                swelling properties. The two ends of double-layer                composite membrane structure are fixed to each                other along the width direction, and the interior                of the double-layer composite membrane structure is                equidistantly constrained along the width                direction.
   USE - Humidity-driven carbon dioxide adsorption                membrane deformable structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The interior of double-layer composite                membrane structure is equidistantly constrained                along width direction, forming deformable carbon                dioxide adsorption membrane structure. When the                structure becomes three-dimensional structure,                large number of pore structures are formed, which                is convenient for gas circulation and greatly                improves the desorption efficiency of carbon                dioxide adsorption membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The humidity-driven carbon dioxide adsorption                membrane deformable structure includes adsorption                material portion, and a polyethylene coating which                is provided on one surface of the adsorption                material main portion to form double-layer                composite membrane structure with swelling                properties. The two ends of double-layer composite                membrane structure are fixed to each other along                the width direction, and the interior of the                double-layer composite membrane structure is                equidistantly constrained along the width direction                to form a deformable carbon dioxide adsorption                membrane structure. The double-layer composite                membrane structure is comprised with two                double-layer composite membrane structures which                are symmetrically arranged. A first layer of                adsorbent material portion, a first layer of                polyethylene coating, a second layer of                polyethylene coating and a second layer of                adsorbent material portion are arranged from top to                bottom. The periodic array arrangement of                double-layer composite membrane structures                constitutes three-dimensional porous structure. The                adsorption material portion is prepared by using                humidity-regenerated carbon dioxide adsorption                material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                carbon dioxide adsorption film deformable structure                in high humidity environment.
   Adsorption material main portion (1)
   Polyethylene coating (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The adsorption                  material portion is formed with rectangular                  structure, where the thickness of adsorbent                  portion is 0.5-1.0 mm. The double-layer composite                  membrane structure is comprised with several                  structures. The double-layer composite membrane                  is equidistant corrugated structure, when the                  ambient humidity is greater than 90%. The                  thickness of polyethylene coating is 0.5-1.0                  mm.

PN WO2021185869-A1; GB2593179-A; GB2593179-B
TI Producing syngas in first reactor by feeding carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and hydrocarbons into reactor, feeding oxygen, and producing syngas from carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and oxidized hydrocarbons by e.g., reverse water-gas-shift reaction.
AU RYTTER E
   HILLESTAD M
   LOVSTAD R
   OYVIND BRINGEDAL B
   BRINGEDAL B O
AE NORDIC BLUE CRUDE AS (NORD-Non-standard)
   NORDIC ELECTROFUEL AS (NORD-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0276V
AB    NOVELTY - Syngas is produced in a first reactor by feeding carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and 1st hydrocarbons into first reactor; feeding oxygen into 1st region, 1st hydrocarbons undergoing partial oxidation in the 1st region; and producing syngas from carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and oxidized 1st hydrocarbons in 2nd region, where syngas is produced at least partly by a reverse water-gas-shift reaction, and/or by steam-methane reforming. At least a portion of the hydrogen is fed into the 2nd region; at least a portion of the 1st hydrocarbons fed into the first reactor are 1st recycled hydrocarbons recycled from second hydrocarbons produced in a second reactor from the syngas; and hydrocarbons are mixed with carbon dioxide before being fed into the 1st reactor.
   USE - Production of syngas in a first reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The steam methane reformer (SMR) catalyst may work synergistically to increase the reaction rate and secure equilibrium of the rWGS reaction. Accordingly, more syngas thus may be produced ready for being converted to products. The heat of reaction for steam reforming is strongly endothermic, coming in addition to the endothermicity of the rWGS reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide and hydrogen which involves producing syngas and producing 2nd hydrocarbons from the syngas in a second reactor;
   (2) apparatus for producing syngas comprising first reactor, greater than or equal to 1 first input configured to feed carbon dioxide into the first reactor, greater than or equal to 1 second input configured to feed hydrogen into the first reactor, greater than or equal to 1 third input configured to feed 1st hydrocarbons into the first reactor, where first reactor has 1st region configured to perform partial oxidization on 1st hydrocarbons, and fourth input configured to feed oxygen into the 1st region of the first reactor, and 2nd region configured to produce syngas from carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and oxidized 1st hydrocarbons; and
   (3) system for producing hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprising the apparatus configured to produce syngas and second reactor configured to produce 2nd hydrocarbons from the syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: The method includes feeding carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and 1st hydrocarbons into the first reactor; at least partially oxidizing the 1st hydrocarbons in the 1st region of the first reactor; producing syngas from the carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and oxidized 1st hydrocarbons in a 2nd region of the first reactor. At least a portion of the 1st hydrocarbons fed into the first reactor are 1st recycled hydrocarbons recycled from 2nd hydrocarbons produced in a second reactor from the syngas. Oxygen is fed into the 1st region of the first reactor. Oxygen is mixed with steam and/or a portion of the carbon dioxide before entering the 1st region of the first reactor. A sufficient amount of oxygen is added such that no, or minimal soot, is formed. Sufficient amount of oxygen is added such that the temperature in the 2nd region of the first reactor is beneficial for the reverse water-gas-shift (rWGS) reaction to proceed without a catalyst; and temperature is greater than or equal to 800 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 1000 degrees C. Sufficient amount of oxygen is added to minimize or reduce the amount of methane leaving the 2nd region of the first reactor; and the methane on volumetric basis is less than or equal to 10 vol.%, preferably less than or equal to 2 vol.%. Hydrogen is fed through a feed ring comprising multiple hydrogen feed lines penetrating a shell of the first reactor. Hydrogen added to the 2nd region of the first reactor, with subsequent rWGS reaction, cools the gas mixture to less than or equal to 1200 degrees C, preferably less than or equal to 1100 degrees C. A major portion of the carbon dioxide is fed into the 1st region of the first reactor, and/or a major portion of the hydrogen is fed into the 2nd region of the first reactor. A minor portion of the hydrogen is fed into the 1st region of the first reactor, and/or a minor portion of the carbon dioxide is fed into the 2nd region of the first reactor. Hydrocarbons are mixed with carbon dioxide before being fed into the first reactor; and/or hydrocarbons are mixed with steam before being fed into the first reactor. Hydrocarbons are mixed with steam in a pre-reformer, where pre-reformer converts at least a portion of the 1st recycled hydrocarbons to methane and carbon dioxide. Hydrocarbons are mixed with a combustible fuel before being fed into the first reactor. Syngas is produced in the first reactor at least partly by a rWGS reaction, and/or where syngas is produced in the first reactor at least partly by steam-methane reforming. The method comprises removing carbon dioxide from the syngas produced in the first reactor and/or adding further hydrogen to the syngas produced in the first reactor. At least a portion of the 1st hydrocarbons fed into the first reactor are first recycled hydrocarbons recycled from the 2nd hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor. At least a portion of the 2nd hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor are fed back into the second reactor as 2nd recycled hydrocarbons. A portion of the 1st hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor is recycled to the first reactor as 1st hydrocarbons. The method includes removing liquid hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor, and optionally upgrading the liquid hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor. The method includes removing water from the 2nd hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor and/or removing unwanted gasses comprising one or both of nitrogen and methane from the 2nd hydrocarbons produced in the second reactor. The 1st and 2nd hydrocarbons are produced in the second reactor by a Fischer-Tropsch process, optionally using a cobalt catalyst. Preferred Device: The 1st region comprises a burner or partial oxidization reactor. The 2nd region of the first reactor comprises a solid, inert material, optionally situated at the entry to the 2nd region.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Solid, inert material comprises alumina, magnesia, calcium oxide, silica, zirconia, titania, cordierite, and/or spinel. Solid, inert material comprises a flow restriction, preferably a disk with holes, preferably one hole.

   SETOYAMA N

   TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 201878813R
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation material comprises an organic silica-type mesoporous substance (I), in which a basic functional group bonded with silicon in skeleton of the porous component. The organic silica-type mesoporous substance has specific surface area of 10 m2/g or more and X-ray diffraction pattern having one or more peaks at diffraction angles corresponding to d100 values of 1 nm or more. The ratio of organic silica-type mesoporous substance to the d100 value is more than 0.023/nm.
   USE - Material is used for separating carbon dioxide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation material has high heat resistance and effectively separates and absorbs carbon dioxide from the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide separation material comprises an organic silica-type mesoporous substance of formula: X-Si (I), in which the basic functional group bonded with the silicon in skeleton of the porous component. The organic silica-type mesoporous substance has specific surface area of 10 m2/g or more and X-ray diffraction pattern having one or more peaks at diffraction angles corresponding to d100 values of 1 nm or more. The ratio of organic silica-type mesoporous substance to the d100 value is more than 0.023/nm.
   X = R1-NH-(CH2)n-;
   R1 = H, aminoalkyl, or aminoalkyl aminoalkyl;and
   n = 1-6.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of carbon dioxide separation material, which involves mixing a silica raw material and a surfactant in a solvent to obtain a porous precursor obtained by introducing the surfactant into pores, in which the content ratio of a basic functional group in the silica raw material is adjusted, and separating the separation material by removing the surfactant contained in the porous precursor. The mixture of basic functional group containing trialkoxysilane and tetraalkoxysilane of formula: R1-NH-(CH2)n-Si(OR2)3 (II), is used as silica raw material. The alkyl trimethyl-ammonium halide of formula: CH3(CH2)mN+(CH3)3.Y'- (III), is used as surfactant.
   m = 8-25;
   Y' = halo;and
   R2 = alkyl.

GA 201878267V
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent comprises alumina, carbonate compound and 0.9-10 wt.% at least one alkali metal, where the adsorbent has a carbonate to alumina intensity ratio (R) of less than or equal to 0.15, where R is determined by Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy of a crushed sample of the adsorbent, the intensity ratio is a ratio of a peak absorbance intensity for carbonate to a peak absorbance intensity for alumina, where each peak absorbance intensity is obtained after subtracting a baseline signal intensity, the peak absorbance intensity for alumina is observed at an FTIR wave number 420-520/cm-1 and the peak absorbance intensity for carbonate is observed at an FTIR wave number 1300-1400/cm-1.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful in an adsorption unit for removing carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The alkali salt promoted activated alumina adsorbent: has carbonate to alumina intensity ratio (R) of less than or equal to 0.15; and exhibits improved carbon dioxide adsorption and desorption kinetics that in turn reduces bed size and associated capital costs, while achieving better carbon dioxide removal performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) making adsorbent particles comprising alumina, at least one alkali metal and a carbonate compound, comprising washing alumina materials comprising alkali metals and a carbonate compound with water and drying the washed alumina materials to form the adsorbent particles, where the washed and dried adsorbent particles have a carbonate to alumina intensity ratio (R2), which is smaller than R before washing and the carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is as determined by FTIR of a crushed sample of the washed and dried adsorbent particles and a crushed sample of the alumina materials comprising alkali metals and a carbonate compound before washing;
   (2) removing carbon dioxide from a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, comprising passing the gas mixture into a bed containing the particles or the adsorbent and withdrawing a carbon dioxide-depleted from the bed; and
   (3) an adsorption unit comprising a bed containing the adsorbent or adsorbent particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent comprises: 1-8 wt.% alkali metals; and 90-99 wt.% alumina. Preferred Components: The surface area of the adsorbent is 220-400 m2/g. The alkali metals are sodium and/or potassium. The amount of carbonate is lower than the amount of carbonate arithmetically necessary for stoichiometrically compensating the charge of the total amount of alkali metals, where the carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is 0.003-0.015. The water is deionized water. The gas mixture: comprises 5 ppmv to 1 mole% carbon dioxide; comprises oxygen, nitrogen and water; and is a feed to a cryogenic air separation unit. Preferred Method: The alumina materials and a carbonate compound are washed: to produce adsorbent particles having a carbonate to alumina intensity ratio, R2 less than or equal to 0.015; with water until the alumina materials have a pH in solution of less than or equal to 9.5, thus forming washed alumina materials, the pH in solution as determined by measuring the pH of an equilibrated 2 l solution of deionized water containing 100 g washed alumina materials. The alumina materials and a carbonate compound are washed with water containing less than 100 ppm total dissolved solids.

PN CN113881424-A; CN113881424-B
TI Composite carbon dioxide fracturing fluid            comprises gaseous carbon dioxide, thickening agent,            ethanol and liquid carbon dioxide, where first monomer            is N,N-diethyl-2-chloroacetamide, 4-hydroxybutyl vinyl            ether and 1,4-butane sultone, and second monomer is            9-decenal and benzylamine.
AU ZHENG Z
   LIU X
   CUI Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022117162
AB    NOVELTY - Composite carbon dioxide fracturing fluid                comprises 10-20 pts. wt. gaseous carbon dioxide,                0.1-2 pts. wt. thickening agent, 0.2-4 pts. wt.                ethanol and 80-100 pts. wt. liquid carbon dioxide.                The fracturing fluid is obtained by mixing the                gaseous carbon dioxide, ethanol and thickening                agent to form foam base fluid and adding the liquid                carbon dioxide. The thickening agent is obtained by                polymerizing the first monomer and the second                monomer, where the first monomer is obtained by the                reaction of N,N-diethyl-2-chloroacetamide,                4-hydroxybutyl vinyl ether and 1,4-butane sultone.                The second monomer is obtained by the reaction of                9-decenal and benzylamine.
   USE - Used as composite carbon dioxide fracturing                fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The fracturing fluid: has simple composition;                is convenient for on-site construction; has little                damage to the formation; has excellent solubility                in carbon dioxide and excellent sand-carrying                ability; and can significantly improve the                temperature resistance and shear resistance of                fracturing fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the fracturing fluid, comprising (1)                taking gaseous carbon dioxide, thickening agent,                ethanol and liquid carbon dioxide, (2) dispersing                the thickening agent in ethanol, adding gaseous                carbon dioxide to mix, and stirring to obtain foam                base liquid, and (3) cooling the foam base fluid to                10-35degrees Celsius, adding liquid carbon dioxide                to the foam base fluid under pressure of 6.5-7.5                MPa, and stirring and mixing to obtain the                composite carbon dioxide fracturing fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the thickening agent comprises (1) obtaining                  intermediate A by reacting                  N,N-diethyl-2-chloroacetamide and 4-hydroxybutyl                  vinyl ether in a weight ratio of (0.85-1):1, and                  reacting the intermediate A and 1,4-butanesultone                  in a weight ratio of 1:(1.3-1.5) to obtain the                  first monomer, (2) obtaining the second monomer                  after reacting 9-decenal and benzyl amine in a                  weight ratio of (1.1-1.3):1, and (3) polymerizing                  the first monomer and the second monomer in a                  weight ratio of (2-5):(1-3) for at least 5 hours                  to obtain the thickening agent. In the step (1),                  the reaction steps of the intermediate A are                  dissolving the N,N-diethyl-2-chloroacetamide in                  the first solvent to obtain first solution,                  dissolving the 4-hydroxybutyl vinyl ether in the                  second solvent to obtain second solution, adding                  the first solution dropwise to the second                  solution, carrying out the reaction in ice-water                  bath for more than 3 hours, and obtaining the                  intermediate A after purification and drying. The                  reaction steps of the first monomer are                  dissolving the intermediate A and 1,4-butane                  sultone in third solvent, and obtaining the first                  monomer after reacting at 60-100degrees Celsius                  for at least 5 hours. In the step (2), the                  reaction steps of the second monomer are                  dissolving the 9-decenal and benzylamine in the                  fourth solvent, and adding glacial acetic acid to                  react at 20-40degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours to                  obtain the second monomer. In the step (3), the                  reaction steps are dissolving the first monomer                  and the second monomer in the fifth solvent,                  raising the temperature to 80-90degrees Celsius,                  adding initiator, carrying out the reaction for                  at least 3 hours, and obtaining the thickening                  agent after purification and drying. The reaction                  steps of the foaming agent are dissolving the                  N-vinylpyrrolidone in the sixth solvent, adding                  disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, raising                  the temperature to 60-70degrees Celsius, adding                  hydrogen peroxide, performing the reaction for                  1-3 hours, continuously raising the temperature                  to 75-85degrees Celsius, and obtaining the                  foaming agent after the reaction for 2-5                  hours.Preferred Components: The weight ratio of                  the N,N-diethyl-2-chloroacetamide and                  4-hydroxybutyl vinyl ether is 0.9:1. The weight                  ratio of the intermediate A and 1,4-butanesultone                  is 1:1.5. The weight ratio of described 9-decenal                  and benzylamine is 1.2:1. The weight ratio of the                  first monomer to the second monomer is 4:1.Preferred Composition: The fracturing fluid                  further comprises 0.5-1 pts. wt. foaming agent,                  which is obtained by reacting N-vinylpyrrolidone                  and hydrogen peroxide in a weight ratio of                  1:(1.05-1.2).

PN US2020355431-A1
TI Method for producing purified pressurized liquid            carbon dioxide stream, involves withdrawing sub vapor            portion vented from sump into conduit from vaporized            portion, and introducing sub vapor portion into carbon            dioxide vapor feed stream.
AU NAUMOVITZ J
AE MESSER IND USA INC (MESS-Non-standard)
GA 2020B08257
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves withdrawing a liquid                carbon dioxide stream from a liquid carbon dioxide                supply. The liquid carbon dioxide stream is                introduced into a distillation column (B) with                packing (C) and volatile impurities are stripped                from the liquid carbon dioxide stream with the                packing. The liquid carbon dioxide stream is                vaporized in a sump (D) of the distillation column                for providing a carbon dioxide vapour. A first                vapor stream (G) vented from the distillation                column is withdrawn from a vaporized portion (F) of                carbon dioxide vapor in the sump. A second vapor                portion vented from the sump into a conduit is                withdrawn from the vaporized portion of the carbon                dioxide vapor in the sump. The second vapor portion                in the conduit is introduced into a carbon dioxide                vapor feed stream upstream of a purifying                filter.
   USE - Method for producing purified pressurized                liquid carbon dioxide stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The first and second high pressure                accumulation chambers are interconnected by conduit                without an isolation valve, so that both act                effectively as a single unit at lower cost. The                fully automated microprocessor controller                continuously monitors system operation providing                fault detection, pressure control and valve                sequencing, thus ensuring purifier reliability                while minimizing operator involvement. The                non-condensable hydrocarbons and impurities are                removed from the operative carbon dioxide liquid                stream. The liquid carbon dioxide boil-off is                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                apparatus in the system for producing purified                pressurized liquid carbon dioxide stream.
   Distillation column (B)
   Packing of distillation column (C)
   Sump of distillation column (D)
   Vaporized portion (F)
   First vapor stream (G)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and purifying fresh pepper oil resin comprises subjecting fresh pepper oil resin to dynamic counter-current extraction by a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid dynamic counter-current extraction separation system, where supercritical carbon dioxide fluid dynamic countercurrent extraction and separation system including carbon dioxide gas source unit, solid matter extraction unit, connecting with the carbon dioxide gas source unit through a pipeline, and transporting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the solid matter extraction unit through the pipeline, the liquid substance extraction unit is connected to the carbon dioxide gas source unit through a pipeline, and reversely transporting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the liquid substance extraction unit through the pipeline, and the separation unit, respectively connected to the solid matter extraction unit and the liquid matter extraction unit is separating the extracted matter from the supercritical carbon dioxide, and circulating the separated carbon dioxide through the pipeline through the carbon dioxide filter and the cooler back to the storage tank, where separation and purification process includes feed rate of 40-60 kg/hour, carbon dioxide flow rate of 400-600 l/hour, extraction temperature is 40-50degrees Celsius, extraction pressure is 15-25 MPa, separation I temperature 35-45degrees Celsius, separation I pressure of 6-11 MPa, separation temperature is 50-60degrees Celsius, and the separation II pressure is 4-6 MPa (not adjustable depending on the gas source, dynamic value automatically controlled by the system).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of separating and purifying fresh pepper oil resin, comprising fresh pepper essential oil includes fresh pepper essential oil I and fresh pepper essential oil II with different characteristic aromas, which can be applied independently or in combination.

PN WO2021029780-A1; EP4014752-A1; US2022297071-A1
TI Saturating product with carbon dioxide involves            e.g. supplying flow of liquid from pressure chamber to            nozzle, feeding nozzle liquid flow into receiving            chamber, serving wet saturated steam and carbon dioxide            into mixing chamber and mixing.
AU KOZLOV A G
AE KOZLOV A G (KOZL-Individual)
   KOZLOV A G (KOZL-Individual)
   KOZLOV A G (KOZL-Individual)
GA 202117925C
AB    NOVELTY - Saturating product with carbon dioxide                involves: supplying flow of liquid from pressure                chamber at pressure (P1) and flow rate (V1) to                nozzle; feeding nozzle liquid flow into receiving                chamber along with carbon dioxide coming via                channels and provide negative pressure at inlet of                receiving chamber, i.e. cross-sectional abrupt                pressure drop (RE), where the fluid transfer occurs                before saturation state in wet saturated vapor to                increase mass transfer between surface of the wet                saturated steam and carbon dioxide in the                10000-12000 times compared to the surface of the                mass transfer between the liquid droplets and                carbon dioxide; serving wet saturated steam and                carbon dioxide into the mixing chamber and                intensively mix the wet saturated steam with carbon                dioxide to obtain a steam-gas mixture; feeding                resulting vapor-gas mixture into condensation                chamber and vapor-gas mixture is condensed in flow                to form carbonated product; and feeding fused                carbonated product to the collecting tank.
   USE - The method is useful for saturating product                with carbon dioxide (claimed), which is used in the                beer and non-alcoholic industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maximizes flow turbulization to                increase cavitation and rupture of molecular bonds,                which have a high energy capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Saturating a product with carbon dioxide                includes: supplying a flow of liquid from the                pressure chamber at pressure (P1) and flow rate                (V1) to the nozzle, where the liquid is accelerated                to a high flow rate (V2) and discharged from the                nozzle at ultra-low pressure (P2); feeding the                nozzle liquid flow into the receiving chamber along                with carbon dioxide coming through the channels and                provide a negative pressure at the inlet of the                receiving chamber, i.e. cross-sectional abrupt                pressure drop (RE), where the fluid transfer occurs                before the saturation state in the wet saturated                vapor to increase the mass transfer between the                surface of the wet saturated steam and carbon                dioxide in the 10000-12000 times compared to the                surface of the mass transfer between the liquid                droplets and carbon dioxide; serving wet saturated                steam and carbon dioxide into the mixing chamber                and intensively mix the wet saturated steam with                carbon dioxide to obtain a steam-gas mixture;                feeding the resulting vapor-gas mixture into the                condensation chamber and the vapor-gas mixture is                condensed in the flow to obtain a carbonated                product; feeding fused carbonated product to the                collecting tank, in which carbon dioxide is                completely dissolved at given process state                parameters of pressure and temperature of the                dissolution efficiency of 100%. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for a device for saturating                the product with carbon dioxide is made in the form                of a water-air device of an ejector type, including                sequentially located a pressure chamber for                supplying a product (1), a nozzle (2), receiving                chamber (3) with four channels for supplying gas                (4), mixing chamber (5), condensation chamber and                diffuser, where the length of the receiving chamber                is 0.5-0.8 of the nozzle diameter, the diameter of                the mixing chamber is 1.07-1.2 nozzle diameter, and                the length of the mixing chamber is 6 times its                inner diameter, while the device has a condensation                chamber located between the mixing chamber and the                diffuser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a diagram of the device for                saturation of the product with carbon                dioxide.
   Product (1)
   Nozzle (2)
   Receiving chamber (3)
   Supplying gas (4)
   Mixing chamber (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The four gas supply                  channels are made in the walls of the receiving                  chamber and are located perpendicular to the axis                  of the device with an interval of 90 degrees                  relative to each other 90, 180, 270 or 360                  degrees .
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Sludge curing material based on cement-based carbon dioxide foam in sea sludge curing and CO2isolation sealing, comprises sludge, fly ash and cement-based carbon dioxide foam comprising cement, foaming agent, foam stabilizing agent.
AU FENG T
   ZHANG J
   XIE K
AE UNIV HOHAI (UYHO-C)
GA 202199280G
AB    NOVELTY - Sludge curing material comprises 23-37 wt.% sludge, 30-4 wt.% fly ash, and 31-36 wt.% cement-based carbon dioxide foam. The mass ratio of sludge and fly ash is 1: (0.81-0.85). The cement-based carbon dioxide foam comprises 20-25 wt.% cement , 0.7-0.9 wt.% foaming agent , 0.85-0.95 wt.% foam stabilizing agent, and 8-12 wt.% water.
   USE - Sludge curing material based on cement-based carbon dioxide foam in sea sludge curing and CO2isolation sealing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sludge curing material completely uses the source of the current two large environment problem, respectively is carbon dioxide greenhouse gas and dredged sediment in lake ocean, innovatively uses foaming agent and foam stabilizer to form foam by carbon dioxide, and forms composite slurry with cement and fly ash.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for sludge curing of materialbased on cement-based carbon dioxide foam, which involves:
   (A) preparingcement-based carbon dioxide foam: respectively and blending the foaming agent and foam stabilizing agentwith the concentration 3.7-4.2g/L to obtainthe pre-mixed liquid; the solvent is water; the pre-mixed liquid passes through the water-based foamgenerating device, the pressure of the water-basedfoam generating device is 0.2-0.5 MPa;
   (B) using carbondioxide as gas source for foaming; when foaming, highpressure pump speed is greater than 25 revolutions/seconds, generatingcarbon dioxide water-based foam; stirring andblending the cement and water with proper water ashmass ratio to obtain the cement slurry; mixing theprepared carbon dioxide water-based foam andcement slurry with a certain proportion; the volumeratio of the bubble slurry is (4.2-5.2): 1, stirring at aspeed of 20-22 revolutions/minute, the stirring time is 3-5 minutes,obtaining the cement-based carbon dioxide foam; steptwo, mixing sludge and fly ash to form compositepowder, then adding cement-based carbon dioxidefoam, stirring and mixing, maintaining to form; thestirring speed is 15 -20r/min, the stirring time is 3-5 minutes; and
   (C) obtaining the sludge curing material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The foaming agent is tea saponin, and the doping concentration of foaming agent is 3.7-4.2g/L. The foam stabilizer is SDBS of sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, and the doping concentration of foam stabilizer is 4.7-5.2g/L. The water content of the sludge is 46-63 wt.%. The sea sludge is thinned by a filter screen, remove thelarge particle impurities; obtaining the refined seasludge, further comprising treating the refined seasludge by soaking treatment and/or electrodialysismethod to obtain the desalting sludge. The large particle impurities comprise shells and seaweed. The ratio of the working rotating speed of thehigh pressure pump to the highest rotating speed ofthe high pressure pump is 50-70 wt.%. The density of the silt solidifying materialafter maintaining for 28 days is 1.337-1.359 g/cm3, the non-side limit compressive strength is 1149.9-1205.1 kiloPascals.

TI Direct air capture of carbon dioxide, comprises            preparing substantially continuous coating layer of            calcium sorbent material, subjecting substrate with            coating layer removing portion of calcium sorbent and            processing calcium sorbent.
AU GOFF A
   BEAUCHAMP D
   FETVEDT J E
   PALMER M R
   LU X
   RATHBONE D
   PETBETT J
   RASBORNE D
   LV X
   GOUGH A
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202163127C
AB    NOVELTY - Direct air capture of carbon dioxide,                comprises: preparing a substantially continuous                coating layer of a calcium sorbent material at a                density of less than 10 kg/square meter on at least                one substrates; subjecting the substrate with the                substantially continuous coating layer of the                calcium sorbent material to contact with air                including carbon dioxide for a time sufficient for                the calcium sorbent to react with the carbon                dioxide and thus capturing at least a portion of                the carbon dioxide from the air and convert at                least a portion of the calcium sorbent to a                carbonated form; removing at least a portion of the                calcium sorbent in the carbonated form from the                substrate; and processing the calcium sorbent in                the carbonated form such that the carbon dioxide                captured from the air is ready for sequestration or                other use.
   USE - The method is useful for direct air capture of                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides distinct advantages over                known uses of calcium sorbents for carbon dioxide                capture; provides for maximizing surface area of                calcium sorbent, and thus maximizing carbonation                efficiency of calcium sorbent, while minimizing                energy costs; provides customizing coating                thickness of sorbent on substrate to elicit most                efficient rate of transfer of carbon dioxide into                sorbent; provides specific design thus increasing                performance and decreases cost of direct air                capture system; and utilizes calcium sorbent will                be regenerated, and high throughput can increase                overall volume or mass of carbon dioxide removed                from air over a given time period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system for direct air capture of carbon dioxide,                comprising a coating system configured for                application of a liquid, calcium sorbent material                to substrate to form a substantially continuous                coating layer of the calcium sorbent material on                the substrates, a storage unit configured for                positioning of the substrate for a time where the                substrate are in contact with air carbon dioxide in                the air reacts with the calcium sorbent material to                form carbonated calcium sorbent material and a                collection unit configured for removal and                collection of the carbonated calcium sorbent                material from the substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises slaking the calcium oxide to form the                  calcium sorbent material used in preparing the                  substantially continuous coating layer. The                  method further comprises removing a portion of                  the calcium sorbent in the carbonated form prior                  to calcining and adding makeup limestone during                  calcining. The substantially continuous coating                  layer of the calcium sorbent material is at a                  density of 0.1-5 ksm. The removing step at least                  a portion of the calcium sorbent in the                  carbonated form from the substrate comprises                  subjecting the substrates to a force sufficient                  to break the substantially continuous coating                  layer of the calcium sorbent material and loosen                  the substantially continuous coating layer of the                  calcium sorbent material from the substrate. The                  processing step calcium sorbent in the carbonated                  form comprises particularizing the calcium                  sorbent in the carbonated form for sequestration                  of the calcium sorbent in the carbonated form.                  The processing step calcium sorbent in the                  carbonated form comprises further subjecting the                  calcium sorbent in the carbonated form to ambient                  air for a time sufficient to increase carbonation                  percentage. The processing step calcium sorbent                  in the carbonated form comprises: calcining the                  calcium sorbent in the carbonated form to release                  carbon dioxide and form calcium oxide; and                  capturing the carbon dioxide released from the                  calcium sorbent. The preparing step substantially                  continuous coating layer of the calcium sorbent                  material comprises dipping substrate in a                  reservoir of the calcium sorbent material. The                  preparing step substantially continuous coating                  layer of the calcium sorbent material comprises                  dripping or spraying the calcium sorbent material                  onto the substrate. Preferred Components: The                  substantially continuous coating layer of the                  calcium sorbent material has an average thickness                  on the substrate of less than 2.5 cm. The                  substantially continuous coating layer of the                  calcium sorbent material has an average thickness                  on the substrate of 0.01-2 cm. The substrates are                  configured substantially as a sheet. The                  substantially continuous coating layer of the                  calcium sorbent material is configured to exhibit                  a carbonation rate at least 25 wt.% the calcium                  sorbent material is carbonated within a time of                  less than or equal to 96 hours. The substantially                  continuous coating layer of the calcium sorbent                  material is configured to exhibit a carbonation                  rate such that at least 50 wt.% the calcium                  sorbent material is carbonated within a time of                  1-14 days. The subjecting the substrate with the                  substantially continuous coating layer of the                  calcium sorbent material to contact with air                  including carbon dioxide comprises hanging the                  substrates with the substantially continuous                  coating layer of the calcium sorbent material in                  a location where the substantially continuous                  coating layer of the calcium sorbent material is                  in contact with the air. The coating system                  comprises reservoir of the liquid, calcium                  sorbent material. The coating system further                  comprises a dipping unit configured for dipping                  of the substrates into the reservoir of the                  liquid, calcium sorbent material. The coating                  system comprises a hanging unit configured for                  retaining the substrate in a substantially                  vertical position. The coating system further                  comprises drip pipe configured for dripping                  calcium sorbent material onto the substrate. The                  system further comprises a calciner configured to                  receive the carbonated calcium sorbent material                  and convert the carbonated calcium sorbent                  material into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide.                  The system further comprises a solids separator                  configured to separate the calcium oxide from the                  carbon dioxide. The system further comprises a                  lime slaking unit configured to receive the                  calcium oxide and form calcium hydroxide for use                  as the calcium sorbent material.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN115046706-A
TI Closed circulation sealing test system for            supercritical carbon dioxide, has carbon dioxide            storage tank connected with inlet of sealing cavity            through first pipeline with static sealing element            installed in detection cavity.
AU WANG L
   QIAN J
   SONG H
   YANG K
   QIN Z
   YANG X
   LIN Z
   ZHANG N
   HU H
AE SHANGHAI MICROPOWERS LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022C0435T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide storage tank                connected with an inlet of the sealing cavity                through a first pipeline. A heater is positioned on                the first pipeline and used for heating carbon                dioxide in the first pipeline. A static sealing                element is installed in detection cavity of the                sealing cavity. A movable sealing element is                connected to a power output shaft of motor. The                movable sealing element abuts against static                sealing element. The motor is used for driving the                movable sealing element to rotate. An outlet of the                seal cavity is communicated with second pipeline. A                cooler is located on the second pipeline and used                for cooling carbon dioxide in second pipeline. A                second pipeline is connected with first pipeline.                Carbon dioxide cooled by cooler can enter first                pipeline to circularly enter sealing cavity. The                first flowmeter is installed at outlet of seal                cavity and used for measuring amount of carbon                dioxide leaked from joint of movable sealing                element and static sealing element.
   USE - Closed circulation sealing test system for                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The tightness test can be carried out under                the condition that the test environment is close to                the real working conditions, and the test results                are closer to the real situation.

A heating device is provided in the second container. The end of the conduit away from the first container is arranged adjacent to the heating device. An air vent is provided above the second container. A drain valve is provided near the bottom of the second container. The mixed liquid of citric acid and water is placed in first container, and preset amount of the mixed liquid is controlled to flow into heating device through the second control valve. The heating device is activated to heat mixed liquid to a preset temperature so that mixed liquid generates carbon dioxide and is discharged through the air outlet.

The method further involves opening the drain valve, and draining the water in the second container from the drain valve. A drain valve is provided near bottom of second container. A temperature control device is provided in the heating device. The carbon dioxide preparation device further includes an air outlet tube, which is inserted into the air outlet hole.

PN WO2020175711-A1; EP3932903-A1; JP2021502674-X; EP3932903-A4; US2022144757-A1
TI New diphenyldiazene compound used for gas-absorption/discharge agent for collecting carbon dioxide for fuel and chemical material.
AU IMAHORI T
   MOTOYAMA R
AE UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
GA 202085678X
AB    NOVELTY - A diphenyldiazene compound (I) , is new.
   USE - New diphenyldiazene compound used for gas-absorption/discharge agent for collecting carbon dioxide (claimed), vinyl greenhouse, hydrogen, and methane. Uses include but are not limited to fuel, agricultural products, electric power generation, and chemical material.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas-absorption/discharge agent containing compound (I) has excellent selectivity, repeats absorption and discharge of carbon dioxide at low environmental load by using renewable energy under normal temperature and normal pressure by using low amount of weight, and effectively and easily collects carbon dioxide from mixed gas, for converting material into chemicals, and fuels, without emitting carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A diphenyldiazene compound of formula (I), is new.
   L1,L2 = 1-20C alkyl, NH2, NHR3, NR3R4, ester group, carboxy, amido, cyano, nitro, halo, acyl, CF3, O(CH2)lOCH3, carbamato, or aryl;
   R1,R2 = 1-10C bivalent hydrocarbyl in which at least H in bivalent hydrocarbyl is optionally substituted by alkyl, aryl, ester group, carboxy, amido, cyano, nitro, halo, acyl, CF3, O(CH2)lOCH3, carbamato, or alkoxy;
   R3,R4 = alkyl, aryl, acyl, ester group, alkylsulfonyl, or arylsulfonyl;
   R5,R6 = H, 1-20C alkyl, or aryl;
   n+m = 1 or more;
   n,m = 0-5;
   p,q = 0-4;and
   l = 1 or 2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon-dioxide collection method, which involves collecting carbon dioxide using the gas-absorption/discharge agent; and
   (2) carbon dioxide collection apparatus, which consists of the carbon dioxide absorption/discharge agent, and an irradiation apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus further consists of a supply apparatus which supplies a fluid comprising carbon dioxide in the agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No general preparation is given. Preferred Process: The method further involves immersing the gas-absorbing/discharge agent in a solvent, and contacting the fluid containing carbon dioxide with the agent after the immersion, and further involves irradiating the agent with the UV-rays having wavelength at 300-400 nm, sunlight or simulated-sunlight, irradiating the agent with the visible light having wavelength of 400-570 nm, or leaving the agent in a solution at room temperature for preset time, making the agent to absorb the carbon dioxide, and discharging the absorbed carbon dioxide from agent. referred Components: The compound (I) is preferably chosen from 14 compounds, e.g. 5 compounds of formulae (II)-(VI). The solvent is chosen from chloroform, toluene, and acetonitrile.
   EXAMPLE - 150 mg (3-((1Z)-2-(5-(Hydroxymethyl)-2-methylphenyl)diazen-1-yl)-4-methylphenyl)methanol, 351.5 mg triphenylphosphine, and 197.2 mg phthalimide was added to a flask. 8 mL Dehydrated tetrahydrofuran (THF) was added to the mixture, and was cooled to 0 degrees C, 0.705 ml diisopropyl azodicarboxylate, was dripped to the obtained mixture. The temperature was raised from 0 degrees C to room temperature. The mixture was stirred for 5 hours. The precipitate solid in the stirred mixture, was collected by filtration and washed with THF to obtain 273.3 g 2-((3-((1Z)-2-(5-((1,3-dioxo-2,3-dihydro-1H-isoindol-2-yl)methyl)-2-methylphenyl)diazen-1-yl)-4-methylphenyl)methyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-isoindole-1,3-dione (DMPMDI) with yield of 92%. 288.8 mg DMPMDI was added to the 30 ml flask. 146 mu l Ethylene diamine were dripped to the flask. The obtained mixture was stirred for 3 days. The stirred mixture was transferred to separating funnel, and water was added to the obtained mixture. The obtained mixture was extracted with dichloromethane. 1N Hydrochloric acid was added to the organic layer, and an extraction operation was performed with a separating funnel. An aqueous sodium hydroxide solution was added to the obtained aqueous layer to make basic at pH of 12, transferred to a separatory funnel, extracted with dichloromethane, and dried over sodium sulfate. Sodium sulfate was filtered off, and the solvent was evaporated, the residue was subjected to column chromatography using methanol and aqueous ammonia at ratio of 25:1 to obtain 107.7 mg 1-(3-((1Z)-2-(5-(aminomethyl)-2-methylphenyl)diazen-1-yl)-4-methylphenyl)methanamine compound as orange solid with yield of 74%.

   USE - As a disinfectant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures controlled pressure and                temperature of reaction, so the sodium hypochlorite                does not decompose, and carbon dioxide does not                undergo acidification when fully reacting,                maintains system stability and improves                sterilization effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation method of the disinfectant, involving                (a) adding sodium hypochlorite solution, water and                carbon dioxide, in different storage tanks, (b)                transporting the water to the mixing tank, (c)                adding sodium hypochlorite solution and carbon                dioxide into the mixing tank, after transporting                the water, (d) carrying out a mixing reaction after                adding the sodium hypochlorite solution and carbon                dioxide to obtain a mixed solution; and (e)                filtering the mixed solution to obtain the                disinfectant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass                  concentration of sodium hypochlorite solution is                  more than 5%. The flow rate ratio of carbon dioxide                  and the flow ratio of sodium hypochlorite                  solution is 1: (12-15).Preferred Method: The flow rate of the                  sodium hypochlorite solution is 0.6-1.2 l/hours. The                  filtration process is done by membrane level                  filtering.

PN KR2022010118-A
TI Composition for carbon dioxide absorbent comprises an active ingredient comprises a mixture of potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate, support comprises a mixture of alpha-alumina, titanium dioxide, and zirconium dioxide.
AU HOON C M
   KWAK N S
   KIM U
   BAEK J I
   HYUN L J
   JANG Y S
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 202216659W
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises an active ingredient comprises a mixture of potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate, support comprises a mixture of alpha-alumina, titanium dioxide, and zirconium dioxide, inorganic binder including mixture of bentonite, pseudoboehmite, and synthetic calcium silicate.
   USE - Composition used for carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition improves the overall porosity and carbon dioxide absorption capacity and production efficiency of the solid carbon dioxide absorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing solid carbon dioxide absorbent, which involves:
   (A) preparing a slurry by using the composition for a carbon dioxide absorbent as a solid raw material and adding a solvent, stirring and homogenizing the slurry; and
   (B) spray drying the homogenized slurry to form particles and drying and calcining the particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 36-39 wt.% mixture of potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate, 15-25 wt.% alpha alumina, 5-10 wt.% titanium dioxide, 15-25 wt.% zirconium dioxide, 4-6 wt.% bentonite, 4-5 wt.% pseudoboehmite, 6-8 wt.% synthetic calcium silicate, and 2-4 wt.% activated carbon. Preferred Conditions: The mixture of potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate has a mixing ratio of potassium carbonate to potassium bicarbonate of 0.9-1.8:1. The specific surface area of the titanium dioxide is 25-200 m/g, boehmite pseudo-boehmite has a specific surface area of 100-400 m/g, and the specific surface area of zirconium dioxide is 10-300 m/g. The composition is included in an amount of 40-60 wt.% based on the total weight of the slurry. The additive is added in an amount of 0.01-5 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, and 1-5 pts. wt. organic binder based on 100 pts. wt. solid raw material. The drying is carried out at 110-150 degrees C for 2-24 hours, the sintering is performed at a temperature of 550-650 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minutes in a high-temperature kiln in an air atmosphere. The attrition index (AI) is 10% and the porosity measured by the mercury adsorption method is 18%. The abrasion index (AI, %) is (W1(g)/W0(g)) 100, where the W1 is a flow rate of 10.0 L/minutes (273.15 K, 1 bar) in an abrasion tester according to ASTM D5757-95 is the total weight (g) of the absorbent fine powder collected by conducting an abrasion test under the conditions, and W0 is the initial weight (g, nominal 50g) of the absorbent. The solid carbon dioxide absorbent has an average particle size of 60-200 micrometer, particle size distribution of 30-400 micrometer, and a packing density of 0.6-2.0 g/cm3. The carbon dioxide absorption capacity of the solid carbon dioxide absorbent is 6 wt.% (6g carbon dioxide/ 100g sorbent). The solid carbon dioxide absorbent has a spherical shape. Preferred Components: The solvent comprises water. The additive selected from dispersant, antifoaming agent and organic binder.

PN CN110721659-A
TI New photocatalyst comprises unique porous nature of donor-type covalent triazine framework.
AU ZHONG H
   WANG R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 202010610U
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst comprises unique porous nature of donor-type covalent triazine framework (DA-CTF), is new.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for carbon dioxide reduction reaction, and has carbon dioxide capture and synchronous conversion function, shortens the photo-generated electron transfer distance between DA-CTF and carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst improves the efficiency and selectivity of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, and reduction activity under visible light excitation, optimizes the electron and band structure, broadens the light absorption range of the catalyst, promotes the transfer of photogenerated electrons, and accelerates the carbon dioxide reduction reaction. This study provides molecular-level insights into the mechanism of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing nanoparticles, comprising snthesizing DA-CTF, stirring at room temperature, and high temperature sealing is not required.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The donor-type covalent triazine framework pi -conjugated skeleton consists of electron donors and acceptors arranged in an orderly cross. This binary electron donor and acceptor structure provides molecular-level exploration for the photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide and the design of organic semiconductor photocatalysts. The dosage of the catalyst is 30 mg, and the visible light (greater-than 420 nm) excitation at room temperature, the yield of carbon monoxide is 4.15 mu mol/hour and no significant change in activity after 5 cycles of the catalytic system.

TI Conversion of carbon dioxide to organic products            involves supporting precious metal on organic framework            material, adding precious metal to cold plasma            discharge reactor, and adjusting peak value of            discharge voltage.
AU ZHANG X
   XU W
   DI L
   BU D
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 201909494T
AB    NOVELTY - The conversion method of carbon dioxide to 1C                organic products involves supporting precious metal                on the organic framework material, adding precious                metal to cold plasma discharge reactor, introducing                working gas, where the gas pressure is atmospheric                pressure, adjusting the peak value of the discharge                voltage to 15-40 kV, and reducing the precious                metal, adding reduced metal organic framework                material supported precious metal to the                atmospheric pressure dielectric barrier plasma                reactor, and introducing mixed gas of carbon                dioxide and hydrogen to realize carbon dioxide                conversion.
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide to 1C organic                products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables conversion of carbon                dioxide to 1C organic products with improved                selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal organic                  framework material is zirconium-based                  metal-organic framework. The working gas is mixed                  gas of hydrogen and argon in ratio of 1:1. The                  volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is                  1:10-10:1. The flow rate of the mixed gas of                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 10-200                  ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The precious metal is                  ruthenium, rhodium, gold or palladium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1C organic product                  is methane, formic acid or formaldehyde.

AE BLUE PLANET LTD (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 2018832244
AB    NOVELTY - Producing solid carbon dioxide sequestering                carbonate material involves introducing divalent                cation source into flowing aqueous bicarbonate                and/or carbonate containing liquid under conditions                sufficient such that non-slurry solid phase carbon                dioxide sequestering carbonate material is produced                in the flowing aqueous bicarbonate and/or carbonate                containing liquid.
   USE - Method for producing solid carbon dioxide                sequestering carbonate material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce solid carbon                dioxide sequestering carbonate material that                improves stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                continuous reactor, which comprises flowing aqueous                bicarbonate containing liquid, divalent cation                introducer which is configured to introduce                divalent cations at introduction location into the                flowing aqueous bicarbonate liquid, and non-slurry                solid phase carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate                material production location.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid is a                  bicarbonate containing liquid. The bicarbonate                  containing liquid is a liquid produced from a                  carbon dioxide containing gas. The carbon dioxide                  containing gas is a multi-component gaseous                  stream. The multi-component gaseous stream is a                  flue gas. The non-slurry solid phase carbon                  dioxide sequestering carbonate material                  production location comprises seed structures.                  The seed structures comprise granules. The seed                  structures comprise carbonate material and                  non-carbonate material.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The stirring speed in the                  reactor is 240-400 rpm. The drying is carried out                  at 50-100 degrees C for 6-12 hours.
