AU YAO B
   WANG L
   WANG B
   WANG Y
   LI Y
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202232271E
AB    NOVELTY - Integrally formed nitrogen-containing bulk porous carbon material has specific surface area is 174-1639 m2/g, the total pore volume is 0.23-0.68 cm3. The volume of micropores with a pore diameter less than 2 nanometer is 0.07-0.60 cm3/g, and the volume of ultramicropore with a pore diameter less than 1 nanometer is 0.02-0.31 cm3/g. The mass content of nitrogen in the porous carbon is 1.79-6.22%.
   USE - Integrally formed nitrogen-containing bulk porous carbon material used in carbon dioxide adsorption or carbon dioxide-methane separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The integrally formed nitrogen-containing bulk porous carbon material is prepared by simple operation and cost-effective, the prepared porous carbon material is convenient to use, has good adsorption performance, and has good separation effect on carbon dioxide-methane mixed gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing an integrally molded nitrogen-containing bulk porous carbon material, which involves: (a)preparing a glucose solution;(b)immersing melamine sponge into the glucose solution, freezing;(c)drying to obtain a glucose-melamine sponge precursor;(d)heating the obtained glucose-melamine sponge precursor to a target temperature in a furnace under nitrogen atmosphere;(e)raising the temperature to the target temperature, converting nitrogen into carbon dioxide gas;(f)carrying out constant-temperature carbonization-activation treatment under the protection of the carbon dioxide gas;(g)replacing the gas with nitrogen to reduce the temperature to room temperature to obtain the integrally-formed nitrogen-containing blocky porous carbon material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an integrally-formed nitrogen-containing blocky porous carbon material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass concentration of the glucose solution is 1-6.25 wt.%, preferably, 3.23 wt.%. The mass ratio between the melamine sponge and the glucose is 0.3:1. The freeze-drying treatment is performed at temperature of lower than -45degrees Celsius, preferably, -50degrees Celsius, and the time is 24-36 hours. The target temperature is 700-900degrees Celsius. The obtained glucose-melamine sponge precursor is heating at rate of 2-5degrees Celsius/minutes. The flow rate of the nitrogen is 50-70 ml/minutes. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced in advance before being heated to the target temperature, and the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 50-70 ml/minutes.

TI Method for sealing dry gas coupled labyrinth sealed supercritical carbon dioxide turbine shaft, involves using labyrinth seal to reduce amount of carbon dioxide leakage and ensuring safe shutdown of compressors and turbines.
AU LIU G
   HUANG Y
   WANG J
AE CHINA NUCLEAR POWER DESIGN INST (CNNU-C)
GA 202084714B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adopting dry gas seal to realize the shaft end seal of compressor and turbine. The labyrinth seal is used to reduce the amount of carbon dioxide leakage when the dry gas seal fails. The safe shutdown of compressors and turbines are ensured. The control of the air intake temperature and flow of the dry gas seal is coordinated and the monitoring control of the dry gas seal leakage is initiated after the compressor and the turbine are activated. The lowest inlet temperature of the dry gas seal is calculated based on the equal enthalpy expansion and the inlet temperature and flow rate of the dry gas seal are automatically controlled.
   USE - Method for sealing dry gas coupled labyrinth sealed supercritical carbon dioxide turbine shaft.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-power high-speed supercritical carbon dioxide compressor and the turbine shaft end are efficiently sealed by using a dry gas coupling labyrinth seal. The labyrinth seal is used to reduce the leakage of the compressor and turbine when the dry gas seal fails and ensures the safe shutdown of the compressor and turbine. The safety of the unit is significantly increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a dry gas coupling labyrinth-sealed supercritical carbon dioxide turbine shaft end sealing device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a dry gas coupled labyrinth seal supercritical carbon dioxide turbine shaft end sealing device.

PN CN110357794-A; CN110357794-B
TI Preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide thickener used for ultra-low permeability and ultra-low permeability reservoir development includes adding perfluorooctyl alcohol and p-toluenesulfonyl chloride to three necked bottle, and drying.
AU ZHOU M
   SHANG H
   PENG P
   ZHANG J
   LI C
   LIAO M
   ZUO Z
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201988832A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide thickener includes adding perfluorooctyl alcohol and p-toluenesulfonyl chloride to three necked bottle, adding pyridine, mixing, reacting for 3 hours, removing unreacted p-toluenesulfonyl chloride, washing, removing pyridine with 15% dilute hydrochloric acid, washing with distilled water, drying to obtain p-toluene perfluorooctyl sulfonate, adding potassium carbonate and N, N-dimethylformamide, mixing, reacting at 60 degrees C for 4 hours, washing with distilled water, drying to obtain (1,2,3-perfluorooctyl ethoxymethylene-4-carbamate tert butyl ester) methane, adding mixture of dichloromethane and trifluoroacetic acid to three necked bottle, adding (1,2,3-perfluorooctyloxymethylene-4-carbamate tert butyl ester) methane, reacting at 50 degrees C for 2 hours, adding dichloromethane, extracting organic layer, drying, adding 1,6-hexadiisocyanate and (1,2,3-perfluorooctyloxymethylene-4-carbamate tert butyl ester) methane, washing, filtering and drying.
   USE - The preparation of supercritical carbon dioxide thickener is used for ultra-low permeability and ultra-low permeability reservoir development (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The thickener can increase the viscosity of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of supercritical carbon dioxide thickener comprises adding 1 wt.%, 2 wt.% and 3 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide thickeners to high-temperature and high-pressure closed system of the matched device, introducing 99 wt.%, 98 wt.% and 97 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide to closed system through injection valve, rotating rotor, adjusting system pressure and temperature, mixing to make thickener completely dissolved to complete the preparation of the mixing system, turning on rheometer and setting pressure at 29 MPa and temperature at 60 degrees C.

TI Power producing system for fuel cell power production, has gas separation assembly having shift reactor which converts carbon monoxide in anode exhaust into carbon dioxide and chiller assembly to cool exhaust to liquefy carbon dioxide.
AU GHEZEL-AYAGH H
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 201835890W
AB    NOVELTY - The power producing system has a fuel cell having a cathode section receiving inlet oxidant gas containing flue gas output from the flue gas generating assembly. A gas separation assembly (25) receiving the anode exhaust output from the anode section of the fuel cell. The separation assembly has a shift reactor (245) to convert carbon monoxide in the exhaust into carbon dioxide, and a chiller assembly (265) to cool the exhaust to a predetermined temperature to liquefy the carbon dioxide. A gas separation device (275) separates liquefied carbon dioxide from residual fuel gas.
   USE - Power producing system for fuel cell power production.
   ADVANTAGE - The power producing system can be integrated with a fossil fueled device, facility or installation including a power plant, boiler, or any type of combustor such as kilns in a cement factory and coke ovens in the steel industry, and includes efficient separation of gases, and in particular of high purity carbon dioxide, in fuel cell exhaust so that the separated carbon dioxide may be utilized in other industries. The chiller assembly, which is an absorption chiller, uses waste heat produced by the fuel cell system, increasing whole system operating efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a gas separation method for use in a power producing system utilizing a flue gas output from a flue gas generating assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a power producing gas separation and sequestration system.

   Power producing gas separation and sequestration system (200)

PT P
PN WO2018092065-A1; CN109963637-A; US2019255478-A1; EP3541494-A1; US10464008-B2
TI Purification of carbon dioxide feed stream used for oxidation reaction, involves cooling feed stream to condense and remove portion(s) of water, and contacting with zeolite adsorbent material to remove portion(s) of organic chloride(s).
AU SUBRAMANIAN S
   VARAPPURATH D S
   ANGADI S
   VARAPPURATH S D
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201841097Q
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of carbon dioxide feed stream (S200) comprising primarily carbon dioxide, organic chloride(s) and water, involves cooling the carbon dioxide feed stream to condense and remove at least a portion of water from the carbon dioxide feed stream to produce a cooled carbon dioxide feed stream (S202) comprising less than 0.3 wt.% water, and contacting the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream with a zeolite adsorbent material to remove at least a portion of the organic chloride(s) from the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream.
   USE - Purification of carbon dioxide feed stream from ethylene glycol plant, used as reactant for oxidation reaction (claimed). Can also be used for food production, enhanced oil recovery and methanol production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently purifies carbon dioxide feed stream. The method removes compounds that react to form hydrochloric acid from the carbon dioxide by-product gas stream prior to the carbon dioxide byproduct gas stream entering as feed into a catalytic reactor, and thus efficiently reduces corrosion by preventing and reducing the formation of hydrochloric acid. The method uses a robust system for purifying carbon dioxide feed stream. The method avoids the expense of designing and/or installing equipment to withstand hydrochloric acid corrosion, maintaining hydrochloric acid absorbers, and the operational difficulties of removing salts amines and water, when compared with the existing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for purifying carbon dioxide stream from ethylene glycol plant.
   Water adsorbent beds (203)
   Cooling water heat exchanger (205)
   Catalytic oxidation reactor (207)
   Carbon dioxide feed stream (S200)
   Cooled carbon dioxide stream (S202)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further involves contacting the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream with a silica adsorbent material to remove additional water, if present, from the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream, prior to contacting the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream with the zeolite adsorbent material, cooling the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream to 10-30 degrees C to condense and remove additional water, if present, from the cooled carbon dioxide feed stream such that the stream comprises less than 0.06 wt.% water. The method does not use hydrochloric absorber. The carbon dioxide feed stream is cooled using a cooling water heat exchanger (205) or a chiller unit. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide feed stream comprises 5-600 ppm, preferably 5-100 ppm organic chloride(s). The oxidation reaction produces 0 ppmv to less than 0.02 ppmv hydrochloric acid. The carbon dioxide feed stream, prior to purification, further comprises saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons. The amount of the carbon dioxide feed stream is 99-99.5 vol.%. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide feed stream is cooled to 35-55 degrees C. Adsorption is carried out at a temperature of 15-50 degrees C, at a pressure of 15-25 barg and at a space velocity of 1-5 hour-1. Preferred Components: The zeolite adsorbent material comprises zeolite-13X comprising silicon and aluminum in a ratio of 2 or less, or 1.5:1-2.5:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic chloride(s) comprises ethylene dichloride.

PN CN110344877-A; CN110344877-B
TI Method for storing carbon dioxide gas in goaf of porous medium filling material, involves sealing exhaust hole to continue to inject carbon dioxide gas into goaf, and closing injection tube after carbon dioxide gas is injected into goaf.
AU ZHANG Z
   CHEN H
   LV J
AE UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
   UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
GA 2019891059
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting carbon dioxide gas into a goaf (4) filled with a porous medium. The carbon dioxide gas injected into the goaf is gradually filled from an open cut (11) to the goaf from an inner side to an outer side. Original air in the goaf is driven by the carbon dioxide gas and discharged through an exhaust hole in a closed wall (6) of the goaf. The exhaust hole is sealed to continue to inject carbon dioxide gas into the goaf until gas pressure in the goaf reaches a predetermined value when concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas discharged from the exhaust hole exceeds 30%. A carbon dioxide gas injection tube (7) is closed after the carbon dioxide gas is injected into the goaf.
   USE - Method for storing carbon dioxide gas in goaf of porous medium filling material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables filling the goaf with coal mine solid waste so as to avoid surface subsidence caused by collapse of the goaf, and providing an ideal space for long-term storage of carbon dioxide gas so as to reduce greenhouse effect caused by carbon dioxide emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top perspective view of a porous medium filling material.
   Goaf (4)
   Closed wall (6)
   Carbon dioxide gas injection tube (7)
   Air compressing pump (9)
   Open cut (11)

PT P
PN DE102019135696-A1
TI Separation of turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide involves cooling exhaust gas, compressing remaining gas mixture, cooling gas mixture, condensing carbon dioxide, purifying gas mixture, expanding heated mixture, and absorbing deposited water.
AU KIRCHNER H W
   KIRCHNER W
AE KIRCHNER ENERGIETECHNIK GMBH (KIRC-Non-standard)
GA 202171293K
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide involves cooling exhaust gas and condensing and separating water vapor contained in the exhaust gas, compressing the remaining gas mixture to the pressure required for the condensation of carbon dioxide and cooled back, cooling the gas mixture to the condensation temperature of carbon dioxide in the multicomponent heat exchanger, condensing the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide condenser and separating in liquid form from the exhaust gas, purifying the gas mixture by adsorption, expanding the gas mixture, condensing the carbon dioxide component in gas mixture, using the coldness of the gas mixture in the heat exchanger component for the refrigeration requirement in the multi-component heat exchanger, expanding the heated gas mixture to atmospheric pressure, heating the cold expanded gas mixture in the self-cleaning heat exchanger component, and absorbing the deposited water.
   USE - Separation of turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is inexpensive, enables efficient separation of turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide involves cooling exhaust gas and the water vapor contained in the exhaust gas is condensed and separated, with the exception of a residual proportion of water vapor corresponding to the saturation pressure of the water vapor in the gas mixture, compressing the remaining gas mixture to the pressure required for the condensation of carbon dioxide and cooled back, cooling the gas mixture to the condensation temperature of carbon dioxide in the multicomponent heat exchanger and the remaining portion of water vapor condenses or freezes in the self-cleaning heat exchanger component and is deposited there, condensing the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide condenser and separating in liquid form from the exhaust gas, the remaining gas mixture still containing a residual proportion of carbon dioxide corresponding to the saturation pressure of the carbon dioxide in the gas mixture, purifying the gas mixture by adsorption, expanding the gas mixture while performing work so that the coldness of the expanded gas mixture is sufficient for the carbon dioxide evaporation enthalpy to be discharged in the carbon dioxide condenser, condensing the carbon dioxide component in gas mixture by using the expansion cold of the gas mixture, using the coldness of the gas mixture in the heat exchanger component for the refrigeration requirement in the multi-component heat exchanger, expanding the heated gas mixture to atmospheric pressure while performing work in gas turbine, heating the cold expanded gas mixture in the self-cleaning heat exchanger component, and absorbing the deposited water, after exiting the self-cleaning heat exchanger component. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide-separating device, which comprises component (A) in the direction of flow of the exhaust gas supplied to the device for carbon dioxide separation comprising exhaust gas cooler (a), a single-stage or multi-stage exhaust gas compressor, an exhaust gas cooler (b), a self-cleaning heat exchanger unit connected through the motor flaps on the heat-emitting side, a carbon dioxide condenser connected to the heat-emitting side, a carbon dioxide separator where the individual components are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines, a component (B) in the direction of flow of the carbon dioxide separated off in the carbon dioxide separator, a connection point for the downstream use of the separated carbon dioxide, component (C) in the direction of flow of the gas mixture separated in the carbon dioxide separator comprising a carbon dioxide adsorber, a single-stage or multi-stage expansion turbine, a carbon dioxide condenser connected on the heat-absorbing side, a heat exchanger component, an expansion turbine, a self-cleaning heat exchanger component connected through the motor flaps on the heat-absorbing side, and an outlet opening for the moist gas mixture into the atmosphere, where the individual components are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide separation device, which comprises component (A1) in the direction of flow of the gas mixture to be supplied to the device comprising a carbon dioxide condenser with the heat-emitting and a carbon dioxide separator, where the individual components are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines, and a component (B1) in the direction of flow of the pressurized gas mixture used for cooling comprising a single-stage or multi-stage expansion turbine, and a carbon dioxide condenser with the heat-absorbing side, where the individual components are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating turbo exhaust gas carbon dioxide separation with carbon dioxide extraction in liquid form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further involves removing carbon dioxide separated and feeding into the carbon dioxide high-pressure pump, transferring the liquid carbon dioxide to the level required for underground storage with the carbon dioxide high-pressure pump compressed, and heating the highly compressed carbon dioxide in heat exchanger. Preferred Apparatus: The carbon dioxide separation device further comprises a components (D) in the direction of flow of the carbon dioxide separated in the carbon dioxide separator comprising a high pressure carbon dioxide pump, a heat exchanger component for highly compressed carbon dioxide, a connection point for the forwarding of the highly compressed carbon dioxide to a downstream carbon dioxide storage or carbon dioxide utilization, where the individual components are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines. The carbon dioxide condensing device further comprises a multi-stage expansion turbine, in which the heat-absorbing side of the carbon dioxide condenser is divided into several fluidically separated heat exchanger components and each of these heat exchanger components is fluidly connected to each stage of the multi-stage expansion turbine, a component (C1) in the direction of flow of the pressurized gas mixture used for cooling between the expansion turbine and the carbon dioxide condenser comprising a control valve for conditioning the temperature of the gas mixture, and a regulated compressor for conditioning the flow rate of the gas mixture, where the individual components are fluidly connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines, and a component (D1) in the direction of flow of the pressurized gas mixture used for cooling between the expansion turbine and the carbon dioxide condenser of the refrigeration circuit with the components comprising a coolant/gas heat exchanger with the heat-emitting side, a control valve for conditioning the temperature of the refrigerant, and a regulated pump for conditioning the flow rate of the refrigerant, where the individual components of the refrigeration circuit are fluidically connected to one another in the order mentioned by pipelines.

GA 202266337R
AB    NOVELTY - Sealing method of carbon dioxide by inorganic                solid waste ceramsite mineralization concrete                comprises preparing inorganic solid waste ceramsite                filter ball, preparing aminated ceramic particle                filter ball, dipping inorganic solid waste                ceramsite filter ball using amino acid salt                solution and/or amine ion liquid, fully absorbing                carbon dioxide to obtain absorbed saturated carbon                dioxide filter ball by aminated ceramsite filter                ball, and uniformly mixing and stirring saturated                carbon dioxide filter ball with cement mortar                concrete to obtain mineralized light concrete test                piece.
   USE - Method for sealing carbon dioxide by inorganic                solid waste ceramsite mineralization                concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the inorganic solid waste                ceramsite filter ball amino acid salt solution                and/or amino ion liquid processing, which is                introduced into the factory chimney gas and other                high concentration carbon dioxide environment to                absorb saturated carbon dioxide, then directly                adding concrete as additive, the cement mixing                water, hydration heat or steam curing process, the                carbon dioxide quickly mineralizing and maintaining                the gel material, converting into the main                component calcium concrete, converting and sealing                the carbon dioxide; and utilizes the amination                ceramic particle filter ball obtained by dipping                method to realize the full adsorption of carbon                dioxide, then mixing and stirring uniformly the                adsorbent saturated carbon dioxide filter ball and                cement mortar concrete obtain the mineralized light                concrete, realizing the sequeezing of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The inorganic solid                  waste ceramsite filter ball is fly ash ceramic                  filter ball comprising 1500 pts. wt. fly ash,                  350-450 pts. wt. slag cement, 350-450 pts. wt.                  clay, 460-530 pts. wt. quicklime, 80-120 pts. wt.                  mineral soil, 50-80 pts. wt. desulfurized gypsum,                  150-200 pts. wt. foaming agent, 80-120 pts. wt.                  cationic interface modifying modifier and 400-450                  pts. wt. water. The cement mortar concrete                  comprises cement, building waste sand, common                  sand water, and anti-cracking fiber, and water                  reducing agent.

The amino                  acid in amino acid salt solution is alanine,                  glycine, sarcosine, L-ornithine, arginine and/or                  L-proline. The amino ion liquid comprises amino                  ion dissolved in anhydrous ethanol. The amino ion                  is 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazole glycine salt,                  1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazole alanine salt,                  1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazole lysine salt,                  tetramethyl ammonium glycine, tetramethyl                  ammonium lysine, 1-aminopropyl-3- methylimidazole                  hexafluorophosphate, ethylene diamine                  tetrafluoroborate, diethylene triamine                  tetrafluoroborate, triethylene tetraamine                  tetrafluoroborate, and/or tetraethylenepentamine                  tetrafluoroborate.

PN WO2022172490-A1; JP2022122251-A
TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent used for air conditioning            in, is formed in porous state and having chemical            structure in which functional groups containing amine            group which is primary amine group are bonded, and            comprising polymer compound which repeatedly adsorbs            and releases carbon dioxide.
AU MASAKI N
   AKIKO K
   YUKAKO A
   NOBUNAGA K
   KOMURA A
   AKIYAMA Y
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 2022A6425F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide adsorbent is formed in a porous                state and having a chemical structure in which                functional groups containing an amine group which                is at least a primary amine group are bonded, and                comprising a polymer compound which repeatedly                adsorbs and releases carbon dioxide.
   USE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent is used for air                conditioning in air conditioners, such as indoor                applications and other applications, such as                factory exhaust gas treatment, agricultural                facility, and vehicle-mounted applications, and an                adsorption device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent increases amount                of carbon dioxide adsorbed, regenerates carbon                dioxide adsorbent with relatively little energy,                and has improved handleability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) (1) an adsorption device comprising the                carbon dioxide adsorbent for air conditioning;                and
   (2) (2) an air conditioner comprising the                adsorption device, a blower mechanism for                generating an airflow passing through the                adsorption device, and a regeneration mechanism                that causes the carbon dioxide adsorbent in the                adsorption device to release carbon dioxide to                regenerate the carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functional group                  is amine group is located at the end of a                  branched chain in the chemical structure. The                  chemical structure comprises an aromatic ring                  directly or indirectly bonded to the amine group.                  Preferred Composition: The amount of amine                  carried is 2 mmol/g or more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The pore volume of the                  carbon dioxide adsorbent is 0.2 ml/g or more. PN CN113652219-A; CN113652219-B
TI Carbon dioxide oil displacement method, comprises e.g. injecting supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion into oil reservoir to displace crude oil to obtain produced gas and crude oil, pressurizing produced gas, then cooling, entering cooled fluid into gas-liquid separator, and post-processing.
AU GUO X
   LIU X
   ZHANG X
   GAO R
   HOU Y
   CUI Y
   WANG L
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D6434L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide oil displacement method, comprises (i) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion into a oil reservoir to displace crude oil to obtain produced gas and crude oil, (ii) pressurizing the produced gas, then cooling, and entering cooled fluid into gas-liquid separator to separate water vapor and heavy hydrocarbons (above 6 carbon number), (iii) heating un-separated liquid obtained in step (ii), then introducing into a adsorption tower, which is filled with zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent for carbon dioxide adsorption, and entering remaining gas into combustion boiler, and (iv) desorbing zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent in step (iii) to release carbon dioxide for preparing supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion, where the supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion is composed of modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate, n-butanol, water and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide oil displacement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases solubility of carbon dioxide in crude oil by injecting supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion into oil reservoir, and improves oil recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide microemulsion is prepared by mixing n-butanol and water uniformly at room temperature, and dissolving modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate in n-butanol and water, where the mass ratio between modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate, n-butanol and water is 1:2-8:40-70, then placing the mixed solution in a high-pressure vessel, introducing carbon dioxide, and stirring the mixed phases to form supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion. The modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate is obtained by subjecting alkylbenzene and chloro-terminated polyhexafluoropropene ether to catalytic reaction to obtain modified alkylbenzene, and sulfonating and neutralizing modified alkylbenzene and chlorosulfonic acid to obtain modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate. The catalyst used in the catalytic reaction is aluminum trichloride, boron trifluoride or antimony pentachloride; and catalytic reaction temperature is 50-100degreesC. The pressure in the high-pressure vessel is 3-12 MPa. In step (i): injection volume of supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion is 0.6-1.8 PV; and injection pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide micro-emulsion is 11-16 MPa; and temperature is 50-60degreesC. In step (ii): produced gas is pressurized to 1-5 MPa. In step (iii): carbon dioxide adsorption pressure is 200-700 kPa, and time is 60-300 seconds. In step (iv): carbon dioxide is desorbed from zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent by vacuuming, and desorption time is 90-120 seconds; and zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent is X-type zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent or Y-type zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent.Preferred Components: The modified sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate is of formula (I). R1 = 10-20C alkyl; R2 = 2-10C linear alkyl or hydroxy; and n = 1-20 (integer).
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); ; H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN WO2021247464-A1
TI Carbon dioxide composition used for increasing production of hydrocarbons from underground hydrocarbon containing formation, comprises major amount of carbon dioxide and at least one branched polyolefin polymer.
AU GUO B
   JACKSON M M
   FIROOZABADI A
   JACKSON J
AE LUBRIZOL CORP (LUBR-C)
GA 2021E0394J
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide composition comprises a major amount of carbon dioxide and at least one branched polyolefin polymer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide composition used for increasing production of hydrocarbons from underground hydrocarbon containing formation, and increasing viscosity of supercritical carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is efficient and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) increasing production of hydrocarbons from underground hydrocarbon containing formation, which involves injecting into the formation the composition, and recovering released hydrocarbons from the hydrocarbon containing formation; and
   (2) increasing viscosity of supercritical carbon dioxide, which involves adding to the carbon dioxide a thickener comprising at least one branched polyolefin polymer that increases the relative viscosity of the combination of at least 100% carbon dioxide and thickener compared to supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The composition has a relative viscosity at least 100% greater than supercritical carbon dioxide. The polyolefin polymer has a number average molecular weight of 140-5000 as measured by gel permeation chromatography with polystyrene standard. The viscosity is increased at a pressure of 500-10000 psi and temperatures of 30-120 degrees C. Preferred Components: The branched polyolefin polymer(s) are polymerized from 2-24C olefin or their mixtures. The branched polyolefin polymer comprises a polyisobutylene polymer. The branched polyolefin polymer comprises polydecene polymer having number average molecular weight of 1000-5000. The branching of the branched polyolefin polymer comprises at least one of aromatic hydrocarbyl group, aliphatic hydrocarbyl group, cyclic hydrocarbyl group, and their mixtures. The branching of the branched polyolefin polymer comprises a hydroxyl containing aromatic group. The branching of the branched polyolefin polymer comprises an amine containing aromatic group. The branching of the branched polyolefin polymer is free of succinimide or succinic anhydride functionality. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.01-5 wt.% the branched polyolefin polymer based on the weight of the composition.

TI Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank evaporating carbon dioxide liquefying and recycling device, has liquefying heat exchanger provided with low temperature liquid automatic adjusting valve that is arranged between gas output pipeline and gas outlet.
AU WEI B
   PEI X
   JIANG X
   TAO H
   ZHOU T
AE HARBIN LIMING GAS GROUP CO LTD (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021274071
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid carbon dioxide storage tank evaporating carbon dioxide liquefying recycling device, the utility model claims a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank evaporating carbon dioxide liquefying recycling device. PT P
PN WO2020010296-A1; US2021262103-A1
TI Method for the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide involves passing current through carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium under pressure conditions in presence of catalyst to reduce and convert carbon dioxide to one or more products.
AU BLAKEMORE J D
   LEONARD K C
   SUBRAMANIAM B
AE UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
   UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
GA 2020044961
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing a current through a carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium under a pressure greater than 0.2-7.4 MPa in the presence of a catalyst to reduce and convert carbon dioxide to one or more products, where the carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium comprises dissolved carbon dioxide, a liquid solvent, and a dissolved electrolyte. The liquid solvent and the dissolved electrolyte are selected to provide a concentration of the dissolved carbon dioxide of at least 2 M at the pressure.
   USE - Method for the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides much higher selectivities and faradaic efficiencies under moderate conditions (e.g., pressure, temperature) enabling a sustainable, commercially viable pathway to sequestering carbon dioxide and converting the pollutant to a variety of useful fuels and chemicals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid solvent is an organic liquid solvent selected from methanol, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate, toluene, propylene carbonate, ethylene carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, and combinations. The dissolved electrolyte is selected from tetrabutylammonium hexafluorophosphate, lithium perchlorate, tetraethylammonium tetrafluorob orate, potassium chloride, and combinations. The organic liquid solvent is acetonitrile and the dissolved electrolyte is tetrabutylammonium hexafluorophosphate. The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium further comprises a proton source. The proton source is selected from water, an alcohol, a salt, or combinations. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium does not comprise an ionic liquid and is not in contact with an ionic liquid. The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium does not comprise water and is not in contact with water. The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium exists as a single phase. The proton source is present at an amount of no more than 1 M. The selected liquid solvent and the dissolved electrolyte provide a faradaic efficiency of 80% and a selectivity of carbon monoxide of 100% using a pressure of 3.1 MPa, room temperature, a timescale of two hours, a gold catalyst, and an applied potential of - 2.5 V. The pressure is in a range of from 1-5 MPa. The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium is at a temperature in the range of from 20-60 degrees C. The carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium is at room temperature. The current is passed by applying a voltage across electrodes in direct contact with the carbon dioxide expanded liquid medium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

AB    NOVELTY - Method of adding carbon dioxide in membrane filtration to increase recovery rate of produced water, involves subjecting to-be-treated water to pretreatment filtration system, removing colloids and suspended particles, subjecting solution comprising bacterial microorganisms and large molecular weight organics to intermediate tank, injecting carbon dioxide, reducing pH, adding scale inhibitor to increase distribution coefficient of bicarbonate, obtaining conditional solution, processing in nanofiltration/reverse osmosis membrane element, and recovering water at the rate of 80-95%.
   USE - Method of adding carbon dioxide in membrane filtration to increase recovery rate of produced water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently increases recovery rate of produced water by adding carbon dioxide. The device used for adding carbon dioxide in membrane filtration to increase recovery rate of produced water, has compact structure, complete function, and easy operation and maintenance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of adding carbon dioxide in membrane filtration to increase recovery rate of produced water, involves storing to-be-treated salt-containing wastewater in a water tank as aqueous solution for use, subjecting aqueous solution to pretreatment filtration system under the action of a booster pump, removing colloids and suspended particles in the aqueous solution, subjecting resultant pretreatment solution comprising bacterial microorganisms and large molecular weight organics to intermediate tank, injecting carbon dioxide into intermediate tank through carbon dioxide airtight addition system to adjust the pH of the solution in the intermediate tank, reducing the pH to 8.32 or less, adding scale inhibitor to increase the distribution coefficient of bicarbonate, obtaining conditional solution in which carbonate distribution coefficient is reduced, dividing conditional solution into nanofiltration/reverse osmosis membrane element, collecting treated water, reacting in sedimentation tank of built-in heating device, maintaining, separating supernatant and precipitated liquid, recycling supernatant, mixing the bleeder water into pretreatment filtration system, and recovering water at the rate of 80-95%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for adding carbon dioxide in membrane filtration to increase recovery rate of produced water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The scale inhibitor is polyacrylic acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, acrylic acid or maleic acid copolymer.

PN EP3670705-A1; EP3670705-B1
TI Carbon dioxide conversion process involves            supplying carbon dioxide and water to an            electrocatalytic unit containing cell comprising a            cathode chamber and an anode chamber, and evacuating            target compound from cathode chamber1.
AU COMBES G
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202056167S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide conversion process involves                supplying carbon dioxide and water to an                electrocatalytic unit (W) containing at least one                cell comprising a cathode chamber (C) and an anode                chamber (A). The 1-2C target compound selected from                the group consisting of methanol, ethanol and                ethylene is produced in the cathode chamber with a                faradaic efficiency of at least 45%. The target                compound is evacuated from the cathode chamber in a                first product stream (104) consisting of water,                targetcompound and a mixture containing at least                unconverted carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Oxygen is                produced in the anode chamber, and evacuated from                the anode chamber as portion of a second product                stream (111). The target compound (107) is                extracted from the evacuated first product stream                and a depleted first stream is obtained. The first                portion of the mixture (209) of the depleted first                stream (205) and a first portion (201) of the                oxygen of the second product stream are fed to a                combustor (K).
   USE - Carbon dioxide conversion process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce carbon dioxide                emissions by the conversion of carbon dioxide to                useful compounds for closing the carbon cycle,                particularly carbon is locked away in a product, or                in an energy-carrier, such as methane, and then                used for power generation with renewed carbon                dioxide capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide conversion process involves                supplying carbon dioxide and water to an                electrocatalytic unit (W) containing at least one                cell comprising a cathode chamber (C) and an anode                chamber (A). The 1-2C target compound selected from                the group consisting of methanol, ethanol and                ethylene is produced in the cathode chamber with a                Faradaic efficiency of at least 45%. The target                compound is evacuated from the cathode chamber in a                first product stream (104) consisting of water,                target compound and a mixture containing at least                unconverted carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Oxygen is                produced in the anode chamber, and evacuated from                the anode chamber as portion of a second product                stream (111). The target compound (107) is                extracted from the evacuated first product stream                and a depleted first stream is obtained. The first                portion of the mixture (209) of the depleted first                stream (205) and a first portion (201) of the                oxygen of the second product stream are fed to a                combustor (K). The first portion of the mixture is                combusted using at least the first portion of the                oxygen as a combustion oxidant generating thermal                energy and fumes (308) consisting of at least 98                vol.% carbon dioxide and water. The thermal energy                generated in the combustor is recovered and at                least portion and preferably all of the carbon                dioxide (101) from the fumes is fed to the                electrocatalytic unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of a carbon dioxide conversion process.
   Carbon dioxide (101)
   First product stream (104)
   Target compound (107)
   Second product stream (111)
   First portion of the mixture (209)
   Thermal energy and fumes (308)
   Anode chamber (A)
   Cathode chamber (C)
   Combustor (K)
   Electrocatalytic unit (W)

PN IN201911021109-A
TI Producing compressed biogas from sugar mill filter cake, comprises e.g. anaerobic digestion of filter cake and farm yard manure to obtain mixture of methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
AU MOHAN N
   PAROHA S
   SINGH S
   CHAUHAN S
AE NAT SUGAR INST (NASU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6138U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing compressed biogas from sugar mill filter cake, comprises: anaerobic digestion of filter cake and farm yard manure at 35-40 degrees C to obtain mixture of methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide; removing carbon di oxide; and compressing methane to obtain biogas.
   USE - The method is useful for producing compressed biogas from sugar mill filter cake.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: maximize production of bio-CNG; produces biogas of sufficient quality, quantity and efficiency; and is simple in construction and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system for production of compressed biogas from sugar mill filter cake, comprising an anaerobic digester for digestion of filter cake and farm yard manure, which is connected to a gas reservoir so as to collect biogas containing methane, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide produced from the digestion, where the gas reservoir is provided in connection with carbon dioxide scrubber to remove carbon dioxide from the biogas, which is connected to another gas reservoir to collect carbon dioxide free biogas, which is connected to air compressor; and
   (2) compressed biogas comprises 92-95% of methane, biogas pressure of 6-7 kg/cm2 and 0.2-5% of hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The removal of carbon dioxide is carried out by flowing the mixture through carbon dioxide scrubber comprising a column filled with lime water solution for absorption of carbon dioxide present in the mixture. The removal of hydrogen sulfide is carried out by flowing the gas from the carbon dioxide scrubber through hydrogen sulfide scrubber comprising a column filled with 20% iron rust solution for absorption of hydrogen sulfide gas (if any) evolving pure methane gas. The pure methane gas is pressurized by a primary compressor followed by passing through a separating device, which is subjected to compression. Preferred Components: The filter cake and farm yard manure is provided in a ratio of 80:20 for the digestion in 32-37 days at 35-40 degrees C to obtain the mixture, which includes 55-60% of methane, 38-40% of carbon dioxide and 1-2% of hydrogen sulfide, in which the anaerobic digestion involves hydrolysis, acidogenesis, acetogenesis and methanogenesis.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the system: carbon dioxide free biogas may be provided in flow communication with hydrogen sulfide scrubber to remove hydrogen sulfide from the carbon dioxide free biogas and obtain pure methane collected in another gas reservoir, where the air compressor includes a primary compressor connected to separating device fitted with a measuring device to gauge quantum of clean gas in the collecting tank, in which the separating device is a specially designed high pressure along with combined directional flow device for cleaning biogas of high impurities. The air compressor compresses the pure methane gas at pressure 150-200 psi. The digester comprising of slurry agitator system for uniform mixing of the filter cake and manure to obtain slurry, in which the digester is water jacketed to control the temperature and accommodates mixed culture bacteria to convert the filter cake and manure into methane gas through the anaerobic digestion.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110550688-A; CN110550688-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide activated biochar catalyst comprises e.g. adding microcrystalline cellulose in tube furnace, heating in nitrogen atmosphere to obtain unactivated biochar after carbonization and cooling to room temperature.
AU CHEN T
   HUANG Q
   SUN C
   LI X
   YAN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019A5192R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide activated biochar catalyst comprises (1) adding the microcrystalline cellulose in a tube furnace, heating to 500 degrees C in nitrogen atmosphere, to obtain unactivated biochar after carbonization for 3 hours, (2) continuing to raise the temperature to 800-950 degrees C in an nitrogen atmosphere, using carbon dioxide gas instead, maintaining the temperature condition for 1 hour, cooling to room temperature under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain carbon dioxide-activated biochar catalyst.

PN CN110057740-A; CN110057740-B
TI High temperature and high pressure coal rock            supercritical carbon dioxide fracturing-creep-seepage            test method, involves obtaining axial deformation and            radial deformation of fractured coal and rock            samples.
AU LIANG W
   CAO M
   WU P
   GENG Y
   YANG X
   GAO X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201966901Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting supercritical                carbon dioxide in a rock sample by a fracturing                pump. A valve is closed. The supercritical carbon                dioxide gas in a pre-heater and the fracturing pump                is released. A coal rock sample of methane                penetrant gas rate is obtained after fracturing.                Shaft pressure, confining pressure and temperature                set by the rock sample are not changed. Methane                penetrant gas rate of the rock sample at different                time is calculated to obtain a supercritical carbon                dioxide fracturing coal rock sample is obtained.                Axial deformation and radial deformation of the                fractured coal and rock samples are obtained by an                axial deformation collecting device and a radial                deformation collecting device.
   USE - High temperature and high pressure coal rock                supercritical carbon dioxide                fracturing-creep-seepage test method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying process, and                effectively measuring methane permeability of a                coal before and after the fracturing without                unloading stress of the coal rock sample with high                precision and high automation degree.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of a                supercritical Carbon dioxide generator                system.

PN EP3441126-A1; US2019046924-A1; US10874980-B2
TI Gas inerting system, has carbon dioxide separation unit placed downstream of catalytic oxidation unit, where carbon dioxide separation unit comprises partial pressure differential source that is in flow communication with membrane.
AU RHEAUME J
   CORDATOS H
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
   HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 201914290C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide separation unit (140) placed downstream of a catalytic oxidation unit (130), where the carbon dioxide separation unit comprises a membrane that is selected from a group consisting of planar geometry, spiral wound, and hollow fiber. The carbon dioxide separation unit comprises a partial pressure differential source that is in flow communication with the membrane, where the partial pressure differential source is a vacuum pump, an ejector, and sweep gas. The catalytic oxidation unit is selected from a group consisting of a pressure-swing adsorption system, and a temperature-swing adsorption system.
   USE - Gas inerting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes fluid-free vacuum pumps operating without operating fluid such as oil, to reduce a risk of membrane fouling and reduce maintenance costs by obviating regular oil changes, such that water can be removed from inerting gas to stabilize or passivate a fuel tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for generating inert gas through catalytic oxidation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an inert gas generating system.
   Vent (102)
   Fuel tank (110)
   Catalytic oxidation unit (130)
   Depleted gas stream (132)
   Carbon dioxide separation unit (140)
